PT P
PN WO2020121008-A1; AU2018452624-A1; BR112021010581-A2
TI Method for treating flow of natural gas, involves obtaining flow of treated natural gas and flow of liquid and regenerating flow of liquid to obtain flow of residual gas with carbon dioxide concentration higher than specific value.
AU WEISS C
   ZHAO J
   BONNEREAU J
AE TOTAL SA (PETF-C)
   TOTAL SE (PETF-C)
   TOTAL SE (PETF-C)
GA 202054686W
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves treating first gas. The flow of flue gas (9) is reacted with first flow of liquid (47) having first carbon dioxide content in order to obtain flow of treated flue gas (29) with final carbon dioxide concentration. The second flow of liquid (49) is provided with second carbon dioxide content higher than the first carbon dioxide content. The second gas is treated. The flow of natural gas (7) is reacted with second flow of liquid. The flow of treated natural gas (17) and third flow of liquid (51) are obtained. The flow of treated natural gas is set with final carbon dioxide concentration lower than the initial carbon dioxide concentration of the flow of natural gas. The third flow of liquid is set with third carbon dioxide content higher than the second carbon dioxide content. The third flow of liquid is regenerated to obtain first flow of liquid and flow of residual gas (31) and provided with carbon dioxide at concentration higher than 90vol%.
   USE - Method for treating flow of natural gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The flow of residual gas is sequestrated in subsoil or used as relatively pure carbon dioxide. The CAPEX and OPEX costs are reduced due to the reduced number of equipment components. The risk of solvent degradation and corrosion is minimized.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a gas treatment unit for treating flow of natural gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the gas treatment unit.
   Flow of natural gas (7)
   Flow of flue gas (9)
   Flow of treated natural gas (17)
   Flow of treated flue gas (29)
   Flow residual gas (31)
   Second flowliquid (49)
   Third flow of liquid (51)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The flow of aqueous solution comprises tertiary amine which is N- methyldiethanolamine (MDEA). The first flow of liquid comprises piperazine, or 1 -(2-Hydroxyethyl)piperazine.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN US2019232215-A1; JP2019130463-A; CN110090530-A; AU2019200519-A1; GB2572668-A; AU2019200519-B2; GB2572668-B; US11235277-B2; JP7054581-B2; CN110090530-B
TI Carbon dioxide capture system has second washer that cleans combustion exhaust gas discharged from carbon dioxide capturer by using second cleaning liquid to capture amine entrained by combustion exhaust gas.
AU FUJITA K
   KITAMURA H
   MURAOKA D
   UDATSU M
   HANDA Y
   KASEDA T
   KATO Y
   UTATSU M
AE TOSHIBA KK (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA ENERGY SYSTEMS & SOLUTIONS CORP (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA KK (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA ENERGY SYSTEMS KK (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA KK (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA ENERGY SYSTEMS & SOLUTIONS CORP (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA ENERGY SYSTEMS & SOLUTIONS CORP (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA ENERGY SYSTEMS & SOLUTIONS CORP (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA CORP (TOKE-C)
GA 201966264R
AB    NOVELTY - The system (1) has a carbon dioxide capturer (20a) which causes carbon dioxide contained in combustion exhaust gas to be absorbed by absorbing liquid containing amine. A first washer (21) cleans the combustion exhaust gas (2) discharged from the carbon dioxide capturer by using first cleaning liquid (11) to capture the amine entrained by the combustion exhaust gas. The second washer (22) cleans the combustion exhaust gas discharged from the carbon dioxide capturer by using second cleaning liquid (12) to capture the amine entrained by the combustion exhaust gas. The spray provided to the first washer is spraying the first cleaning liquid supplied under first pressure. The cleaning liquid diffuser is provided to the second washer and configured to diffuse and drop the second cleaning liquid supplied under second pressure lower than the first pressure.
   USE - Carbon dioxide capture system.
   ADVANTAGE - The increased capacity of the first circulation pump can be reduced, and thus increase of operation cost can be reduced. The flow rate of the first cleaning liquid per unit area and unit time contributes to increase of the probability of physical collision with mist amine entrained by the decarbonated combustion exhaust gas, and hence increases the efficiency of cleaning the decarbonated combustion exhaust gas. The amine concentration of the first cleaning liquid can be decreased, thus preventing decrease of the amine capture performance of the first washer.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of operating a carbon dioxide capture system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the carbon dioxide capture system.
   Carbon dioxide capture system (1)
   Combustion exhaust gas (2)
   First cleaning liquid (11)
   Second cleaning liquid (12)
   Carbon dioxide capturer (20a)
   First washer (21)
   Second washer (22)
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN DE102016219986-A1; WO2018069503-A2; WO2018069503-A3; EP3526315-A2; EP3526315-B1; DE102016219986-B4
TI Producing methane, comprises mixing first mass            flow, which is hydrogen gas or gas mixture containing            hydrogen, with second mass flow containing carbon            dioxide and/or carbon monoxide, and converting the            resulting mixture into methane.
AU FULDE M
AE FULDE M (FULD-Individual)
   FULDE M (FULD-Individual)
   FLD TECHNOLOGIES GMBH (FLDT-Non-standard)
   FLD TECHNOLOGIES GMBH (FLDT-Non-standard)
GA 201834721H
AB    NOVELTY - Producing methane, comprises mixing a first                mass flow, which is hydrogen gas or a gas mixture                containing hydrogen, with a second mass flow, which                contains carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide, and                converting hydrogen and carbon dioxide and/or                carbon monoxide in the resulting mixture into                methane in a catalytic reaction. The second mass                flow is obtained by (a) carrying out a catalytic                hydromethanation, (b) carrying out a                steam-hydrogasification, or (c) generating carbon                dioxide from carbon and oxygen, with subsequent                co-electrolysis of generated carbon dioxide, with                steam.
   USE - The method is useful for producing                methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables the chemical storage of                hydrogen and has a higher storage efficiency of                power-to-gas processing, and allows efficient                application of hydrogen and hydrogen-rich gases as                waste in industrial manufacturing processes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing methane, comprises mixing a first                mass flow, which is hydrogen gas or a gas mixture                containing hydrogen, with a second mass flow, which                contains carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide, and                converting hydrogen and carbon dioxide and/or                carbon monoxide in the resulting mixture into                methane in a catalytic reaction. The second mass                flow is obtained by (a) carrying out a catalytic                hydromethanation in which carbon, steam and oxygen                are converted as educts into methane, carbon                monoxide and carbon dioxide at temperature of                400-800 degrees C in the presence of at least one                catalyst, or (b) carrying out a                steam-hydrogasification in which carbon, hydrogen,                steam and oxygen are converted as educts into                methane, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide at                temperature of 800-1100 degrees C, or (c)                generating carbon dioxide from carbon and oxygen in                a direct carbon fuel cell, with subsequent                co-electrolysis of the generated carbon dioxide,                with steam, where carbon monoxide and hydrogen are                produced.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises                  reacting carbon dioxide and water vapor in                  addition to the catalytic hydromethanization of                  (a) or in addition to the steam hydrogen                  gasification of (b), by co-electrolysis as a                  subsequent step for producing the second mass                  flow, where carbon monoxide and hydrogen are                  formed. The energy balance of the sum of the                  reactions for the production of the mass flow and                  the catalytic methanization reaction is                  positively adjusted. The pressure is 5.5-6.5 MPa                  and the temperature is 500-600 degrees C during                  the catalytic hydromethanization of (a). The                  pressure is 4.5-5.5 MPa and the temperature is                  900-1000 degrees C. The mass flow is provided by                  a water electrolysis or by a high-temperature                  steam electrolysis or the mass flow is waste                  product of another reaction. Water in the                  catalytic methanation reaction resulting is                  recycled and is used as starting material in the                  production of the second mass flow.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).)

PN WO2018165029-A1; US2020009499-A1; US11014041-B2; US2021308616-A1
TI Chemical processing system to remove carbon dioxide from gas mixture by amine-based scrubbing solution comprises carbon dioxide scrubber, liquid inlet, gas outlet, liquid outlet, fluid communicators and automated spectroscopic evaluator.
AU PANACCIONE C
   BROWN N R
   MEULEMAN E E B
AE ION ENG LLC (IONE-Non-standard)
   ION ENG LLC (IONE-Non-standard)
GA 2018716984
AB    NOVELTY - Chemical processing system for removing carbon dioxide from carbon dioxide-containing gas mixture using amine-based scrubbing solution that is a multi-component liquid composition comprising water, amine component and captured carbon dioxide, comprises: carbon dioxide scrubbing unit; a second liquid inlet to receive a feed stream to the regeneration unit; a second gas outlet to output gas effluent stream from the regeneration unit rich in carbon dioxide released from the scrubbing; a second liquid outlet to output a liquid effluent stream from the regeneration unit; a first fluid communication path between second liquid outlet of regeneration unit and first liquid inlet of scrubbing unit to conduct at least a portion of the lean scrubbing solution from the regeneration unit to the scrubbing unit; and second fluid communication path; and an automated spectroscopic evaluation system.
   USE - The chemical processing system is useful for removing carbon dioxide from a carbon dioxide-containing gas mixture using an amine-based scrubbing solution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Chemical processing system for removing carbon dioxide from a carbon dioxide-containing gas mixture using an amine-based scrubbing solution that is a multi-component liquid composition comprising water, at least one amine component for carbon dioxide capture and captured carbon dioxide, the chemical processing system comprises: a carbon dioxide scrubbing unit to contact the gas mixture with the amine-based scrubbing solution for capture of carbon dioxide from the gas mixture in the scrubbing solution, the scrubbing unit comprising a first gas inlet to receive a feed stream of the gas mixture to the scrubbing unit with carbon dioxide for removal in the scrubbing unit, a first gas outlet to output a treated stream of the gas mixture from the scrubbing unit having a lower carbon dioxide concentration than the feed stream of the gas mixture to the scrubbing unit, a first liquid inlet to receive a feed stream of the scrubbing solution for processing in the scrubbing unit to contact the gas mixture to remove carbon dioxide from the gas mixture for capture in the scrubbing solution and a first liquid outlet to output an effluent stream of rich the scrubbing solution from the scrubbing unit, the rich the scrubbing solution having a concentration of captured carbon dioxide that is larger than a concentration of captured carbon dioxide in the feed stream of the scrubbing solution; a scrubbing solution regeneration unit to remove at least a portion of the captured carbon dioxide from the rich the scrubbing solution and prepare a lean the scrubbing solution depleted in the captured carbon dioxide relative to the rich the scrubbing solution, the regeneration unit comprising a second liquid inlet to receive for processing in the regeneration unit a feed stream to the regeneration unit including at least a portion of the rich the scrubbing solution; a second gas outlet to output a gas effluent stream from the regeneration unit rich in carbon dioxide released from the scrubbing solution in the regeneration unit; and a second liquid outlet to output a liquid effluent stream from the regeneration unit including at least a portion of the lean the scrubbing solution; a first fluid communication path between the second liquid outlet of the regeneration unit and the first liquid inlet of the scrubbing unit to conduct at least a portion of the lean scrubbing solution from the regeneration unit to the scrubbing unit in the feed stream of the scrubbing solution; a second fluid communication path between the first liquid outlet of the scrubbing unit and the second liquid inlet of the regeneration unit to conduct at least a portion of the rich the scrubbing solution from the scrubbing unit to the regeneration unit; and an automated spectroscopic evaluation system, comprising a liquid contact probe configured to contact a multi-component liquid composition for spectroscopic investigation and to transmit spectroscopic stimulation energy to interact with the multi-component liquid composition for the spectroscopic investigation and resulting spectroscopic response energy away from the multi-component liquid composition, an energy source connected with the liquid contact probe to provide the spectroscopic stimulation energy for the spectroscopic investigation, a spectrometer connected with the liquid contact probe to detect the spectroscopic response energy and to output spectral data corresponding to the spectroscopic response energy, a machine learning spectral data analyzer connected to the spectrometer for evaluation of the spectral data to determine a concentration value for each one of many components of the multi-component liquid composition, many components including the water, a the amine component and the captured carbon dioxide, where the machine learning spectral data analyzer is trained for each the component of many components over a corresponding trained concentration range for the component and the liquid contact probe is positioned to contact the scrubbing solution in a location in the scrubbing unit, the regeneration unit, the first fluid communication path or the second fluid communication path. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for an amine-based chemical processing method (M2) for removing carbon dioxide from a carbon dioxide-containing gas mixture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The chemical processing system comprises: a first process vessel for contacting the gas mixture with the scrubbing solution within the scrubbing unit to remove carbon dioxide from the gas mixture for capture in the scrubbing solution, the first pressure vessel is in fluid communication within the scrubbing unit with the first gas inlet, the first gas outlet, the first liquid inlet and the first liquid outlet; a second process vessel for releasing captured carbon dioxide from the scrubbing solution in the regeneration unit, the second pressure vessel is in fluid communication within the regeneration unit with the second liquid inlet, the second gas outlet and the second liquid outlet; multiple the liquid contact probes each disposed at different locations in the chemical processing system to each contact the multi-component liquid phase at the corresponding the location; and the feed stream of the scrubbing solution flowing through the first liquid input into the carbon dioxide scrubbing unit, where at least one location is in the first fluid communication path or the second fluid communication path to contact the multi-component liquid composition moving through the corresponding the fluid communication path and a first location is in the first fluid communication path to contact the multi-component liquid composition moving through the first fluid communication path and a second the location is in the second fluid communication path to contact the multi-components liquid composition moving through the second fluid communication path. At least one the location is in the carbon dioxide scrubbing unit to contact a the multi-component liquid composition in a scrubbing vessel for contacting the gas mixture with the scrubbing solution within the scrubbing unit to remove carbon dioxide from the gas mixture for capture in the scrubbing solution. The spectroscopic stimulation energy is provided as electromagnetic radiation that spans a wave number range across at least 1000 wave numbers of infrared spectrum. The wave number range spanned by the electromagnetic radiation of the spectroscopic stimulation energy has a lower limit of 600-3000 c/m. The spectroscopic response energy comprises electromagnetic radiation in the infrared spectrum that spans a wave number range across at least 1000 wave numbers of infrared spectrum. The spectral data represents electromagnetic radiation spanning a wave number range having a lower limit of 600-3000 c/m micrometers or larger. The spectral data has a resolution across the wave number range of the spectral data of no larger than 6 c/m. The machine learning spectral data analyzer is trained for temperature effect on the spectral data over a trained temperature range to provide a temperature-compensated value for the concentration value of each the component for different temperatures across the full range of the trained temperature range. The trained temperature range spans at least 10 degrees C, where the of 30-50 degrees C or higher. The lower limit is at least 5-100 degrees C. The machine learning spectral data analyzer is trained for temperature effect on the spectral data over a trained temperature range spanning at least 10 degrees C to provide a temperature- compensated value for the concentration value of each the component for different temperatures across the full range of the trained temperature range. Preferred Method: The method (M2) comprises: carbon recovery processing of the gas mixture with an aqueous amine scrubbing solution that is a multi-component liquid composition comprising water, at least one amine component for carbon dioxide capture and captured carbon dioxide, the carbon recovery processing including scrubbing processing, comprising capturing carbon dioxide from the gas mixture in the scrubbing solution, to prepare a rich the scrubbing solution enriched in the captured carbon dioxide relative to feed of the scrubbing solution to the scrubbing processing and regeneration processing, comprising removing at least a portion of the captured carbon dioxide from at least a portion of the rich the scrubbing solution to prepare lean the scrubbing solution depleted in captured carbon dioxide relative to the rich the scrubbing solution and recycling at least a portion of the lean the scrubbing solution in the feed of the scrubbing solution to the scrubbing processing to capture additional carbon dioxide automated spectroscopic evaluation of operation of the carbon recovery processing by an automated spectroscopic evaluation system, comprising automatically performing by the spectroscopic evaluation system periodic in-line spectroscopic investigation of the scrubbing solution for at least one location in the carbon recovery processing to generate spectroscopic response spectral data indicative of properties of the scrubbing solution at the corresponding the location of the spectroscopic investigation and trained machine learning evaluation of the spectral data by a machine learning spectral data analyzer to determine a concentration value for each one of a plurality of components of the multi-component liquid composition, many components including the water, a the amine component and the captured carbon dioxide, where the machine learning spectral data analyzer is trained for each the component of many components over a corresponding trained concentration range for the component; and periodic automated evaluation of operating performance of the carbon recovery processing by the automated process evaluation system based on process evaluation parameters including the determined concentration values; and based on the evaluation of operating performance, changing at least one operating parameter of the carbon recovery processing. The changing at least one operating parameter comprises automatic generation by a process controller of control signals directed to manipulable process equipment to accomplish the changing at least one operating parameter. The manipulable process equipment includes at least one control valve. The method is performed in a chemical processing system.

PN US2022341294-A1
TI Method for performing subsurface sequestration of            carbon dioxide in geological basin, involves allowing            injected aqueous carbon dioxide solution to sink below            in situ water naturally present in basin and pool into            sequestration locations.
AU STEWART S A
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
GA 2022D2560F
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves identifying subsurface                sequestration locations in a geological basin. An                aqueous carbon dioxide solution with water and                carbon dioxide that is dissolved in the water                injected into the basin, where the solution has a                density that is greater than the density of in situ                water naturally present in the basin. The injected                solution is allowed to sink below the in-situ water                (16) and pool into the subsurface sequestration                location (14) to sequester the carbon dioxide in                the geological basin (10), and the subsurface                mapping is performed.
   USE - Method for facilitating subsurface                sequestration of carbon dioxide in a geological                basin at climate change applications such as oil,                natural gas, and water.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables sequestering carbon dioxide                in geological basins in an effective manner. The                method allows the carbon dioxide to be mixed with                water before or during injection to form a fluid                that is more dense than in situ water within the                sequestration site, so that the dense fluids do not                require traps, which are required by relatively                light, positively buoyant fluids.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation                of a method for facilitating subsurface                sequestration of carbon dioxide in a geological                basin.
   10Geological Basin
   11Sedimentary Material
   14Subsurface Sequestration Location
   16Saline Water
   18Hydrocarbon Mixture

AB    NOVELTY - The device has an intake pipe (10) for                introducing air outside a greenhouse (1) into the                chamber. A transfer pipe transfers the air inside                of the chamber to an outer side of the chamber. An                exhaust pipe (21) exhausts the air transported                through a conveying Pipe (20) to the outer side. A                control valve controls the transfer pipe to                selectively communicate with the exhaust pipe or a                supply pipe. A control unit controls selective                communication of a control valve and driving of a                control module, where temperature of a reaction                chamber is changed during desorption process                performed to desorb carbon dioxide from porous                medium.
   USE - Carbon dioxide fertilizing device for a                greenhouse.
   ADVANTAGE - The fertilizer increases carbon dioxide                concentration in the greenhouse, so that                photosynthesis and transpiration of crops can be                actively performed, reduces maintenance cost                without a separate carbon dioxide storage by                adsorbing carbon dioxide in atmosphere using the                porous medium, and desorbs adsorbed carbon dioxide                at a desired time and supplies the carbon dioxide                to an inside of the greenhouse.

PN WO2021170625-A1; AU2021226847-A1; CA3164605-A1; KR2022148838-A; IN202247054514-A
TI Co-producing methanol, ammonia and urea from            hydrocarbon feedstock, by performing steam reforming of            hydrocarbon feedstock, passing part of steam reformed            effluent to carbon dioxide removal stage, by-passing            remaining part of steam reformed effluent and adding            hydrogen to methanol synthesis gas.
AU TJAERNEHOV E A
   HAN P A
   TJARNEHOV E A
AE HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
GA 2021A1890W
AB    NOVELTY - Process for co-producing methanol, ammonia and                urea from a hydrocarbon feedstock, involves (a)                performing primary and secondary steam reforming of                a hydrocarbon feedstock, and obtaining a steam                reformed effluent comprising hydrogen, nitrogen,                carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, (b) passing a                part of the steam reformed effluent from step (a)                to a carbon dioxide removal stage to produce an                effluent, (c) by-passing the remaining part of the                steam reformed effluent the carbon dioxide removal                stage and combining the effluent withdrawn from                step (b) with the by-passed part of the steam                reformed effluent to provide a methanol synthesis                gas comprising hydrogen, nitrogen and carbon                monoxide and carbon dioxide, (d) adding hydrogen to                the methanol synthesis gas, (e) converting the                methanol synthesis gas and withdrawing a liquid                effluent, (f) converting the gas effluent withdrawn                to ammonia and (g) converting ammonia to                urea.
   USE - The process is useful for co-producing                methanol, ammonia and urea from a hydrocarbon                feedstock.
   ADVANTAGE - The process minimizes primary reformer size,                improves utilization of carbon dioxide in flue gas                form burners of reformer because of less heat                required in minimized reformer, utilizes carbon                dioxide generated in burners in preparation of                urea, which decreases carbon dioxide foot print of                process, allows flexible production of methanol,                ammonia and urea depending on actual demand of                producer, reduces amount of carbon dioxide to be                removed, when by passing part of steam reformed                effluent removal stage, is environmentally-friendly                and ensures no emissions to surroundings of carbon                dioxide captured from methanol and ammonia                synthesis gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Process for co-producing methanol, ammonia and                urea from a hydrocarbon feedstock, involves (a)                performing primary and secondary steam reforming of                a hydrocarbon feedstock, and obtaining a steam                reformed effluent comprising hydrogen, nitrogen,                carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, (b) passing a                part of the steam reformed effluent from step (a)                to a carbon dioxide removal stage to produce an                effluent with a reduced content of carbon dioxide,                (c) by-passing the remaining part of the steam                reformed effluent the carbon dioxide removal stage                and combining the effluent withdrawn from step (b)                with the by-passed part of the steam reformed                effluent to provide a methanol synthesis gas                comprising hydrogen, nitrogen and carbon monoxide                and carbon dioxide, (d) adding hydrogen recovered                from a downstream ammonia synthesis stage to the                methanol synthesis gas obtained in step (c), (e)                catalytically converting the methanol synthesis gas                in a once-through methanol synthesis step and                withdrawing a liquid effluent comprising methanol                and a gas effluent comprising nitrogen and                hydrogen, (f) catalytically converting the gas                effluent withdrawn in step (e) to ammonia in the                ammonia synthesis stage and (g) converting at least                a part of the ammonia rom step (e) to urea by                reaction with carbon dioxide removed in step (b)                together with carbon dioxide contained in flue gas                recovered from the primary steam reforming in step                (a).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The process further                  involves subjecting the steam reformed effluent                  from step (a) to a water gas shift reaction. The                  process further involves subjecting the gas                  effluent from step (d) to a methanation reaction                  upstream step (e). The amount of hydrogen added                  to the methanol synthesis gas in step (d) is                  adjusted to provide a module is at least 2.5 or                  2.5-10. The once-through methanol synthesis step                  is performed in parallel methanol production                  lines.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

TI Flotation of phosphate rock by using carbon            dioxide tail gas as by-product of phosphorus chemical            industry comprises e.g. introducing carbon dioxide tail            gas to gas treatment chamber, feeding carbon dioxide            tail gas to gas collection chamber, and introducing            into flotation device through suction fan.

   WANG M
   CAI Z
   ZHANG H
   HE H
   YANG W

GA 202288447X
AB    NOVELTY - Flotation of phosphate rock by using carbon                dioxide tail gas as a by-product of phosphorus                chemical industry comprises (1) introducing the                carbon dioxide tail gas into the gas treatment                chamber from the bottom, and spraying the top of                the gas treatment chamber with an alkaline solution                with a concentration of 1%, and the alkaline                solution washes and cools the carbon dioxide tail                gas countercurrently, (2) feeding the carbon                dioxide tail gas processed into the gas collection                chamber from the bottom, spraying the top of the                gas collection chamber with tap water, and using                tap water to perform countercurrent washing and                cooling of the carbon dioxide tail gas, and the                temperature of the obtained mixed gas is                50-70degrees Celsius, and (3) introducing the                carbon dioxide tail gas into the flotation device                through a suction fan or a compressor, as a gas                source for the flotation of phosphate rock.

   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the problem that the                greenhouse effect is exacerbated caused by the                direct evacuation of the tail gas containing CO2.                The CO2 tail gas is directly used as phosphorus ore                flotation gas source, avoiding CO 2 tail gas                emptying, greatly reducing carbon emission.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkaline solution                  is sodium hydroxide, sodium carbonate or lime                  aqeuous solution.

TI Cultivating microorganism i.e. capable of            utilizing organic feedstock, comprises e.g. cultivating            microorganism in bioreactor, and capturing carbon            dioxide from bioreactors and reducing carbon dioxide to            organic feedstock in reduction unit.
AU NOORMAN H J
AE DSM IP ASSETS BV (STAM-C)
   DSM IP ASSETS BV (STAM-C)
   DSM IP ASSETS BV (STAM-C)
GA 202095806M
AB    NOVELTY - Cultivating a microorganism i.e. capable of                utilizing an organic feedstock, comprises:                cultivating the microorganism in at least one                bioreactor (1); capturing carbon dioxide from the                bioreactors and reducing the carbon dioxide to an                organic feedstock in a reduction unit (3); and                feeding at least a part of the organic feedstock                from the reduction unit into the bioreactors.
   USE - The method is useful for: cultivating a                microorganism, where the microorganism comprises                yeast, filamentous fungi, bacteria or heterotrophic                algae (all claimed); and producing biomass and/or                the producing a compound of interest including                extracellular metabolites and/or intracellular                metabolites, and complex compounds e.g. enzymes,                lipids, polysaccharides, vitamins and                antibiotics.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: reduces carbon dioxide emission,                and implements at lower costs because the needed                equipment for carbon dioxide reduction can be                smaller: reduces the amount of sugar or other                carbon containing feedstock; and utilizes                electricity that is generated from renewable energy                including solar, wind or hydro energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                system for cultivating a microorganism capable of                utilizing an organic feedstock, comprising the                bioreactors for cultivating the microorganism, a                carbon dioxide capturing unit for capturing carbon                dioxide from the bioreactors, a carbon dioxide                reduction unit for reducing the carbon dioxide to                organic feedstock, and at least one conduit to                introduce the organic feedstock into the                bioreactors.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows the substrate sugar is                introduced via conduits into bioreactor.
   Bioreactor (1)
   Reduction unit (3)
   Electrolysis unit (4)
   Inlet (13)
   Outlet (14)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises: electrolyzing water into hydrogen and                  oxygen in an electrolysis unit (4), and feeding                  at least a part of the hydrogen into the                  reduction unit for reducing the carbon dioxide to                  the organic feedstock; feeding at least a part of                  the oxygen into the bioreactors; and feeding at                  least a part of the hydrogen into the                  bioreactors. The microorganism is capable of                  utilizing the organic feedstock comprises at                  least one polynucleotide coding for a compound of                  interest or the polynucleotide coding for the                  compound involved in the production of a compound                  of interest by the cell. The method comprises                  monitoring the fermentation process comprising:                  (a) selecting at least one process parameter of                  the fermentation process, for which current                  measured values are determined during the course                  of the fermentation process; (b) comparing a                  respective current measured value of the at least                  one selected process parameter with a                  corresponding estimated value of a model                  parameter estimated by a process model for the                  process parameter; (c) comparing a variance                  between the respective current measured value and                  the corresponding estimated value for the                  selected process parameter with a predetermined                  threshold value; and (d) changing at least one                  defined model parameter employed in the process                  model when the predetermined threshold value is                  exceeded by the variance, where step (a)-(d) with                  a respective changed model parameter are executed                  until the variance is placed below a                  predetermined threshold value, and optionally                  where after a predetermined number of repetitions                  of steps (a)-(d) the threshold value is met by                  the variance, where the method is discontinued                  and a warning is generated as output. Preferred                  Components: The organic feedstock comprises                  formic acid (preferred), methanol, ethylene,                  ethanol, 1-propanol or carbon monoxide. The                  system further comprises: an electrolysis unit                  for electrolysis of water and a conduit to                  introduce hydrogen from the electrolysis unit to                  the carbon dioxide reduction unit; the conduits                  for introducing hydrogen and/or oxygen into the                  bioreactors; at least one inlet (13) for                  introducing substrate into the bioreactors and at                  least one outlet (14) for product formed in the                  bioreactors; and a computer implemented system                  for a fermentation simulation tool for simulating                  the fermentation method, where the computer                  implemented system comprises at least one                  processor, a user interface, a control system                  interface configured to adjust the process                  parameters of the fermentation method, a memory                  comprising computer readable medium storing                  instructions for simulating the fermentation                  method, where the instructions for simulating the                  fermentation method configure the processor                  comprising the steps (a)-(d) as per se on the                  user interface.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E17 (Other aliphatics.)

PT P
PN EP3485944-A1; CN210674041-U
TI Breathing mask has carbon dioxide filter that comprises compounds which react exothermically with carbon dioxide.
AU HILBIG R
AE KONINK PHILIPS NV (PHIG-C)
   KONINK PHILIPS NV (PHIG-C)
GA 201945300L
AB    NOVELTY - The breathing mask has a mask structure (12) that covers the nose or mouth of a user (10) thus defines a mask chamber (18). An air pump (20) ventilates the mask chamber to the ambient surrounding. A particulate filter (21), airflow regulator (22) and carbon dioxide filter (26) through which a proportion of the air drawn in and out of the chamber is able to pass. The carbon dioxide filter comprises compounds, which react exothermically with carbon dioxide. The mask structure is impermeable and provided with an opening for the carbon dioxide filter and the particulate filter. A controller (24) is a manual controller physically coupled to the airflow regulator or an electronic controller electronically coupled to the airflow regulator or the air pump, and controlled by the user.
   USE - Breathing mask.
   ADVANTAGE - The heating of the inhaled air reduces condensation in the mask volume and also improves comfort for the user by preventing inhalation of cold ambient air. The inhaled air drawn through the same portions of the carbon dioxide filter as the exhaled air, thus providing direct contact between the heated portions of the carbon dioxide filter and the inhaled air to maximize efficient heat exchange. The heat is retained in the mask chamber even when the efficiency of the carbon dioxide filter is reduced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of heating air drawn into chamber of breathing mask sealed to face of user.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a mask.
   User (10)
   Mask structure (12)
   Mask chamber (18)
   Air pump (20)
   Particulate filter (21)
   Airflow regulator (22)
   Controller (24)
   Carbon dioxide filter (26)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The compound is an oxide, hydroxide or peroxide of alkali or alkali earth metal, preferably chosen from lithium hydroxide, sodium, peroxide, sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, magnesium hydroxide, calcium hydroxide and calcium oxide. The carbon dioxide filter comprises 1-99 wt.% two or more calcium hydroxide which react exothermically with carbon dioxide, 1-50 wt.% sodium hydroxide and 50-99 wt.% sodium peroxide with respect to the total amount of compound which reacts exothermically with carbon dioxide.

PN WO2018224124-A1; EP3635164-A1; EP3635164-B1
TI Method for washing fabrics using carbon dioxide,            involves transferring carbon dioxide from treatment            chamber to other by unit of carbon dioxide feed or            recovery and recirculation circuit that connects two            treatment chambers to each other.
AU SCORTEGAGNA B
AE LAFER SPA (LAFE-Non-standard)
   LAFER SPA (LAFE-Non-standard)
GA 201899641L
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing to recover and                re-circulate the carbon dioxide used in one                treatment chamber (14,15) at the end of the washing                of a fabric and to feed it into the other treatment                chamber. The carbon dioxide from one treatment                chamber to the other is transferred by unit of a                carbon dioxide feed and/or recovery and                recirculation circuit (16) that connects two                treatment chambers to each other. The                temperature/pressure conditions of the carbon                dioxide are regulated so as to allow a passage of                the carbon dioxide in a gaseous form from one                treatment chamber to the other treatment chamber                without any phase change. The method provides to                recover the carbon dioxide from treatment chambers                having a greater pressure inside it than the                pressure in the other treatment chamber and to                transfer it into the latter.
   USE - Method for washing fabrics or garments using                carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The washing apparatus provides to position two                or more treatment chambers in parallel which can                operate alternately or totally overlapping washing                cycle in such a way as to increase the productivity                of the process. The apparatus has reduced energy                consumption and the total electrical power                installed can be reduced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                apparatus for washing fabrics.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                washing apparatus.
   Treatment chambers (14,15)
   Recirculation circuit (16)
   Gas aperture (19)
   Accumulation tank (36)
   Circulation pump (38)
DC F06 (Chemical-type treatment of textiles (D06B, L, M, P, Q).)

PT P
PN US2018322968-A1; WO2018204857-A2; WO2018204857-A3; CA3062480-A1; EP3619722-A2
TI Nuclear power plant used as liquid-cooled reactor comprises reactor core having fuel columns penetrating calandria, closed-loop carbon dioxide coolant circuit and compressor to compress depressurized and discharge pressurized carbon dioxide.
AU CHEATHAM J R
   COHEN M E
   JOHNSON B C
   PETROSKI R C
   TOURAN N W
   TRUONG B H
   COHEN B
   CHEATHAM J R I
AE TERRAPOWER LLC (TEPO-C)
   TERRAPOWER LLC (TEPO-C)
GA 201886877B
AB    NOVELTY - Nuclear power plant comprises: reactor core having many fuel columns (104) penetrating calandria (106), where the calandria is configured to contain a volume of secondary coolant, each fuel column includes structural tube that forms the exterior of the fuel column, the structural tube is configured to hold a nuclear fuel; closed-loop carbon dioxide coolant circuit configured to route pressurized carbon dioxide into the fuel columns and remove a heated carbon dioxide from the fuel columns, thus removing energy from the nuclear fuel within the fuel columns, where the closed-loop carbon dioxide coolant circuit includes at least one turbine (108) configured to generate mechanical energy from the heated carbon dioxide and discharge depressurized carbon dioxide; and at least one compressor configured to compress the depressurized carbon dioxide and discharge pressurized carbon dioxide to the reactor core.
   USE - The nuclear power plant is useful as liquid-cooled reactors such as pressurized water reactors.
   ADVANTAGE - The nuclear power plant is cost-effective.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for fuel column comprising structural tube having an interior surface and an exterior surface, at least one nuclear fuel insert in the structural tube, where the nuclear fuel insert includes nuclear fuel and at least one coolant passage through the nuclear fuel insert through which a coolant can flow and an insulating layer between the interior surface of the structural tube and the nuclear fuel insert.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure illustrates schematic view of gas-cooled pressure tube nuclear reactor.
   Fuel columns (104)
   Calandria (106)
   Turbine (108)
   Coolant stream (110)
   Generator (114)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The nuclear power plant is configured to maintain the pressurized carbon dioxide in a supercritical state throughout the closed-loop carbon dioxide coolant circuit. The nuclear power plant is configured to use a moderating liquid as the secondary coolant. The secondary coolant is light water, heavy water or liquid mixtures of ammonia, and organic fluids. The nuclear power plant further comprises at least one neutron shields around the calandria. Each fuel column has a long axis, a first end, a second end opposite the first end, at least one fuel access port, at least one carbon dioxide inlet, and at least one carbon dioxide outlet. The carbon dioxide inlet is positioned at the first end and the carbon dioxide outlet is positioned at the second end. The carbon dioxide inlet and carbon dioxide outlet are at the first end. The long axes of the fuel columns are horizontally oriented. The long axes of the fuel columns are vertically oriented. The fuel access port is configured to allow the insertion and removal of the nuclear fuel. Each fuel column is provided with a fuel access port at the first end and a fuel access port at the second end allowing the nuclear fuel to be inserted via the fuel access port at the first end and removed via the fuel access port at the second end. The nuclear power plant further comprises: at least one check valve in the closed-loop carbon dioxide coolant circuit configured to prevent flow of pressurized carbon dioxide out of a fuel column via the carbon dioxide inlet or into the fuel column via the carbon dioxide outlet; at least one pressure relief valve in the closed-loop carbon dioxide coolant circuit configured to allow flow of secondary coolant out of the calandria if a pressure of the secondary coolant exceeds a preselected pressure threshold; reflood tank configured to release a reflood fluid into the calandria when secondary coolant flows out of the calandria via the pressure relief valve; cooling system configured to maintain the secondary coolant below the threshold temperature in the absence of flow or pressure in the carbon dioxide coolant circuit; and containment vessel containing the calandria and the fuel columns. The volume of secondary coolant that can be held in the calandria has sufficient heat removal capacity to prevent the temperature of the nuclear fuel in the fuel columns from rising above a threshold temperature during a loss of coolant event.

PN WO2018109476-A1; EP3554674-A1; CN110392603-A; JP2020501884-W; US2020078729-A1
TI Simultaneous removal of carbon dioxide and sulfur dioxide from flue gas, involves combusting fuel and air, compressing exhaust stream, withdrawing oxides-depleted residue stream using membrane, and separating resultant permeate stream.
AU HUANG Y
   BAKER R W
   MERKEL T C
   FREEMAN B C
AE MEMBRANE TECHNOLOGY & RES INC (MEMB-Non-standard)
   SETNA R (SETN-Individual)
   MEMBRANE TECHNOLOGY & RES INC (MEMB-Non-standard)
   MEMBRANE TECHNOLOGY & RES INC (MEMB-Non-standard)
   SETNA R (SETN-Individual)
   MEMBRANE TECHNOLOGY & RES INC (MEMB-Non-standard)
GA 201849418Q
AB    NOVELTY - Simultaneous removal of carbon dioxide and sulfur dioxide from flue gas involves combusting a fuel and air in a combustion apparatus to create an exhaust stream comprising carbon dioxide and sulfur dioxide, compressing the exhaust stream to produce a compressed gas stream, providing a membrane having a feed side and a permeate side, and is selectively permeable to carbon dioxide and sulfur dioxide over nitrogen and oxygen, passing at least a portion of the compressed gas stream across the feed side, withdrawing carbon dioxide- and sulfur dioxide-depleted residue stream from the feed side, withdrawing a permeate stream (215) enriched in carbon dioxide and sulfur dioxide from the permeate side at lower pressure than the compressed gas stream, and subjecting the permeate stream to separation process which produces stream enriched in carbon dioxide, and a stream enriched in sulfur dioxide.
   USE - Simultaneous removal of carbon dioxide and sulfur dioxide from flue gas. Uses include but are not limited to flue gas generated from coal power plant, cement plant, steel mill and municipal solid waste incinerator, and by burning petroleum, coke, catalysis regeneration in fluid catalytic cracker and refineries.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple, reliable and economical simultaneous removal of carbon dioxide and sulfur dioxide from flue gas with reduced energy consumption of compressor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view explaining the simultaneous removal of carbon dioxide and sulfur dioxide from flue gas.
   Coal feed stream (201)
   Air stream (202)
   Boiler (203)
   Flue gas (204)
   Particulate removal unit (205)
   Permeate stream (215)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The exhaust stream comprises flue gas (204) from a coal-fired power plant, and further comprises nitrogen oxide of formula: NOx (I), where x is not defined. The separation process is calcium hydroxide or sodium hydroxide scrubbing step. The membrane is selectively permeable to nitrogen oxide (I) over nitrogen and to nitrogen oxide (I) over oxygen. The stream enriched in sulfur dioxide is also enriched in nitrogen oxide (I). The exhaust stream further comprises particulate material.

PN CN114110424-A
TI System for optimizing resource to prepare            synthesis gas, comprises crude gas system carbon            dioxide/hydrogen sulfide washing tower, synthetic            ammonia methanol washing carbon dioxide washing tower,            synthetic ammonia methanol washing hydrogen sulfide            washing tower, and mixer.
AU HE P
   WU E
   WANG P
   ZHEN L
AE LUXI CHEMICAL GROUP CO LTD COAL CHEMICAL (LUXI-Non-standard)
GA 202237374P
AB    NOVELTY - A system comprises a crude gas system carbon                dioxide/hydrogen sulfide washing tower (1), a                synthetic ammonia methanol washing carbon dioxide                washing tower (3), a synthetic ammonia methanol                washing hydrogen sulfide washing tower (2), a                methanol system methanol washing carbon                dioxide/hydrogen sulfide washing tower (4), a                methanol system methanol washing tower (5) and a                mixer (16). An outlet pipeline of the crude gas                system carbon dioxide/hydrogen sulfide washing                tower and an outlet pipeline of the synthetic                ammonia methanol washing carbon dioxide washing                tower are provided with an adjusting valve and a                flow meter, and are connected with a synthesis gas                pipeline. The synthesis gas pipeline is provided                with a gas on-line analyzer (8). An outlet pipeline                of the synthetic ammonia methanol washing hydrogen                sulfide washing tower is connected with an inlet                pipeline at the lower portion of the synthetic                ammonia methanol washing carbon dioxide washing                tower.
   USE - System used for optimizing resources to                prepare synthesis gas for chemical applications                (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system selects the adjusting valve and the                flow meter according to the characteristic of the                synthesis gas preparation and gas on-line analyzer,                so that the flow rate displayed by the meter on the                pipeline adjusts the opening degree of the valve                according to a certain proportion. The system is                automated, prevents manual operation error, reduces                labor intensity of the spot operating personnel,                and utilizes the resource of the synthetic ammonia                system and the methanol system, thus ensuring                stable production of the post-process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A system comprises a crude gas system carbon                dioxide/hydrogen sulfide washing tower (1), a                synthetic ammonia methanol washing carbon dioxide                washing tower (3), a synthetic ammonia methanol                washing hydrogen sulfide washing tower (2), a                methanol system methanol washing carbon                dioxide/hydrogen sulfide washing tower (4), a                methanol system methanol washing tower (5) and a                mixer (16). An outlet pipeline of the crude gas                system carbon dioxide/hydrogen sulfide washing                tower and an outlet pipeline of the synthetic                ammonia methanol washing carbon dioxide washing                tower are provided with an adjusting valve and a                flow meter, and are connected with a synthesis gas                pipeline. The synthesis gas pipeline is provided                with a gas on-line analyzer (8). An outlet pipeline                of the synthetic ammonia methanol washing hydrogen                sulfide washing tower is connected with an inlet                pipeline at the lower portion of the synthetic                ammonia methanol washing carbon dioxide washing                tower. The branch of the outlet pipeline of the                crude gas system carbon dioxide/hydrogen sulfide                washing tower is provided with a de-mixer pipeline.                The de-mixer pipeline is connected with the mixer.                The outlet of the methanol system methanol washing                tower is provided with an adjusting valve and a                flow meter, and is connected with the mixer. The                outlet pipeline of the mixer is provided with a gas                analyzer, and is connected with the synthesis gas                pipeline. The outlet pipeline of the methanol                system methanol washing carbon dioxide/hydrogen                sulfide washing tower is connected with the inlet                pipeline at the lower portion of the methanol                system methanol washing tower. The system is used                for optimizing resources during preparation of                synthesis gas containing carbon monoxide and                hydrogen in a ratio of 1:1.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                optimizing resources to prepare synthesis                gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic flow chart of a                process for preparing synthesis gas by optimizing                resources. (Drawing includes non-English language                text)
   1Crude gas system carbon dioxide/hydrogen                sulfide washing tower
   2Synthetic ammonia methanol washing hydrogen                sulfide washing tower
   3Synthetic ammonia methanol washing carbon                dioxide washing tower
   4Methanol system methanol washing carbon                dioxide/hydrogen sulfide washing tower
   5Methanol system methanol washing                tower
   8Gas on-line analyzer
   16Mixer
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Apparatus: The gas on-line                  analyzer is a carbon monoxide and hydrogen                  on-line analyzer.

PN US2020399190-A1
TI Producing distillate boiling range products, comprises e.g. combusting coke on portion of conversion catalyst to regenerate portion of conversion catalyst and to form regeneration flue gas having carbon dioxide.
AU BEHKISH A
   KOVVALI A S
AE EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
GA 2020C8350A
AB    NOVELTY - Producing distillate boiling range products, comprises: e.g. exposing a feed (100) comprising reformable hydrocarbons and at least one recycle input to a reforming catalyst under reforming conditions to produce a reformed effluent comprising hydrogen and carbon monoxide; exposing at least a portion of reformed product to a methanol synthesis catalyst under methanol synthesis conditions to produce a synthesis effluent having methanol; exposing a conversion feed having at least a portion of methanol from synthesis effluent to a conversion catalyst under conversion conditions to form a conversion effluent having olefins and to form coke on the conversion catalyst; passing at least a portion of conversion effluent into a heat exchange stage to form a cooled conversion effluent and steam (101); exposing at least a portion of cooled conversion effluent and a recycled naphtha boiling range feed to an oligomerization catalyst under oligomerization conditions to form an oligomerized effluent.
   USE - The method is useful for producing distillate boiling range products.
   ADVANTAGE - The method yields 1-20 wt.% distillate boiling range products corresponds to distillate yield generated based on recycle of carbon dioxide as part of the recycle input, and/or recycle of carbon dioxide as part of the additional carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing distillate boiling range products, comprises: exposing a feed (100) comprising reformable hydrocarbons and at least one recycle input to a reforming catalyst under reforming conditions to produce a reformed effluent comprising hydrogen and carbon monoxide; exposing at least a portion of the reformed product to a methanol synthesis catalyst under methanol synthesis conditions to produce a synthesis effluent comprising methanol; exposing a conversion feed comprising at least a portion of the methanol from the synthesis effluent to a conversion catalyst under conversion conditions to form a conversion effluent comprising olefins and to form coke on the conversion catalyst; passing at least a portion of the conversion effluent into a heat exchange stage to form a cooled conversion effluent and steam (101); exposing at least a portion of the cooled conversion effluent and a recycled naphtha boiling range feed to an oligomerization catalyst under oligomerization conditions to form an oligomerized effluent; separating a light ends product fraction, a naphtha boiling range product fraction, and a distillate boiling range product fraction from the oligomerized effluent, the recycled naphtha boiling range feed comprising at least a portion of the naphtha boiling range product fraction; and combusting the coke on at least a portion of the conversion catalyst to regenerate the portion of the conversion catalyst and to form a regeneration flue gas comprising carbon dioxide, where the recycle input comprises at least a portion of the carbon dioxide from the regeneration flue gas, and/or at least a portion of the steam. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a system for hydrocarbon upgrading comprising a reforming reactor, a methanol synthesis stage in fluid communication with the reforming reactor, a conversion reactor comprising at least one conversion inlet, a conversion outlet, and a conversion catalyst outlet, the conversion reactor is in fluid communication with the methanol synthesis stage via the conversion inlets, a regenerator comprising a regenerator catalyst inlet and a regenerator catalyst outlet, the regenerator catalyst outlet is in fluid communication with the conversion inlets, the regenerator catalyst inlet is in fluid communication with the conversion catalyst outlet, the regenerator being further in fluid communication with at least one of the reforming reactor and the methanol synthesis stage via a regenerator recycle outlet, an oligomerization reactor comprising an oligomerization inlet and an oligomerization outlet, the oligomerization reactor is in fluid communication with the conversion outlet via the oligomerization inlet and a separation stage comprising a separator inlet and at least one separator outlet, the separation stage is in fluid communication with the oligomerization outlet via the separator inlet, the separation stage is in fluid communication with the oligomerization inlet via the separator outlets, the separation stage is further in fluid communication with the conversion inlets via the separator outlets.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a configuration for integrated upgrading of reformable hydrocarbons to distillate boiling range compounds.
   Feed (100)
   Steam (101)
   Syngas stream (102)
   Methanol-containing effluent (103)
   Olefin products effluent (104)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The exposing at least a portion of the reformed product to a methanol synthesis catalyst comprises exposing a combined methanol synthesis feed to the methanol synthesis catalyst, the combined methanol synthesis feed comprising the portion of the reformed product and additional carbon dioxide. The portion of the light ends product fraction is included in the conversion feed without compression of the light ends product fraction or the portion of the light ends product fraction. The conversion conditions comprise fluidized bed conversion conditions, and the combusting the coke on the portion of the conversion catalyst comprises: transferring the portion of the conversion catalyst from a conversion reactor to a regenerator; and combusting the coke on the portion of the conversion catalyst in the regenerator. Preferred Components: The additional carbon dioxide comprises a second recycled portion of the carbon dioxide from the regeneration flue gas. The recycle input comprises portion of the carbon dioxide from the regeneration flue gas. The module M of the reformed product is 1.7-3. The module M of the combined methanol synthesis feed is 1.7-2.3. The conversion feed further comprises at least a portion of the light ends product fraction. The conversion catalyst comprises a zeotype framework structure comprising MFI, MEL, MTW, TON, MTT, ferrierite (FER) and/or MRE. The oligomerization catalyst comprises a zeotype framework structure comprising MFI, MEL, MTW, TON, MTT, FER and/or MRE. The conversion catalyst and oligomerization catalyst comprise zeolite socony mobil (ZSM)-48, and/or the conversion catalyst and the oligomerization catalyst are the same catalyst. The reformable hydrocarbons comprise methane and/or natural gas. The reforming conditions comprise steam reforming and/or dry reforming. The light ends product fraction comprises C3- hydrocarbons, and/or the naphtha boiling range product fraction comprises 4-8C hydrocarbons. The light ends product fraction comprises C4- hydrocarbons, and/or the naphtha boiling range product fraction comprises 5-9C hydrocarbons. The system further comprises a waste heat boiler, where the conversion reactor outlet which is in indirect fluid communication with the oligomerization inlet via the waste heat boiler. The waste heat boiler comprises a steam outlet, where the steam outlet which is in fluid communication with the reforming reactor.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AB    NOVELTY - Enrichment of stem cell growth factor comprises adding fetal bovine serum and basic fibroblast growth factor into umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell culture medium minimum essential medium (MEM), immersing the umbilical cord in physiological saline containing antibiotic, rinsing with the saline, removing the external amniotic membrane and blood vessels, cutting into tissue pieces, adding culture medium, gently shaking the culture flask, culturing in a carbon dioxide incubator, adding the culture medium, changing the liquid by 1/2 liquid exchange method, discarding the medium, washing with phosphate buffer free of calcium(II) and magnesium(II) ions, digesting with trypsin-EDTA digest, collecting the cells, sub-culturing in carbon dioxide incubator, counting and measuring e.g. cluster of differentiation (CD)34- and CD44+, adding Exendin-4 to stimulate culture, washing, adding physiological saline, culturing in a hypoxic incubator, centrifuging and taking the supernatant.
   USE - The stem cell growth factor system is useful for preparing cosmetic skin care product (claimed), stimulating dermal cell repair and regeneration and replacing aging cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The stem cell growth factor system: is capable of repairing and regenerating stem cell growth factor from the deeper layers of human dermal cells; can completely repair the dermal cells; and does not cause dependency. The method: is short and simple; avoids the metabolic waste generated by the long-term normal growth of stem cells, and increases the concentration and purity of growth factors.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Enrichment of stem cell growth factor comprises (a) adding 10% fetal bovine serum and 20 mu g/l basic fibroblast growth factor into umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell culture medium minimum essential medium (MEM), (b) separation and preparation of umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells by (b1) removing the umbilical cord from an operation table, immersing in physiological saline containing antibiotic, placing on a clean bench, rinsing with physiological saline containing antibiotic, and washing the residual blood in the umbilical artery and umbilical vein, (b2) removing the external amniotic membrane and blood vessels on the surface of the umbilical cord using a sterilized surgical instrument, cutting the umbilical cord into tissue pieces having size of 1-2 mm3, (b3) adding a small amount of umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell culture medium and re-suspending, transferring to 75 cm2 ventilated cap culture flask, gently shaking the culture flask to the left and right, uniformly paving the bottom of the bottle, placing at 37 degrees C, culturing in a carbon dioxide incubator with a carbon dioxide concentration of 5%, (b4) allowing to stand for 24 hours, gently adding umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell culture medium to the culture flask up to 10 ml, taking care not to touch or rush to the adherent tissue block, (b5) changing the liquid by 1/2 liquid exchange method after 3 days, (b6) discarding the medium after 5 days, washing once with phosphate buffer free of calcium(II) and magnesium(II) ions having a pH of 7.2, digesting with trypsin-EDTA digest, collecting the cells, adjusting cell concentration and inoculating into 75 cm2 vented cap culture flask, (b6) sub-culturing in carbon dioxide incubator at 37 degrees C and carbon dioxide concentration of 5% according to the ratio of 1 bottles of 4 bottles after the cell fusion degree reaches 90%, and (b7) counting one of the bottles after 4 days of enrichment, and measuring the expression of cluster of differentiation (CD)34-, CD44+, CD45-, CD90+ and CD29+ by flow cytometry, and (c) enrichment of umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell growth factor by (c1) adding 10 nmol/l Exendin-4 to stimulate culture when the umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell fusion degree is 60-70%, (c2) discarding the medium after 28 hours, washing twice with phosphate buffer free of calcium(II) and magnesium(II) ions having a pH of 7.2 to remove residual medium, (c3) adding 0.9% physiological saline (10 ml) to the cleaned umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells, placing the culture flask in a hypoxic incubator, and culturing under hypoxia for 24 hours, and (c4) taking the supernatant, centrifuging at 2000 revolutions/minute for 5 minutes, taking the supernatant, and discarding the scattered cells and debris to obtain a solution rich in epidermal growth factor (EGF), vascular endothelial growth factor (VEGF), keratinocyte growth factor (KGF), basic fibroblast growth factor (bFGF), platelet-derived growth factor (PDGF) and transforming growth factor (TGF)- beta stem cell growth factor system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The trypsin-EDTA digestive solution is prepared by dissolving 2.5 g trypsin and 0.2 g EDTA in 1 l phosphate buffer free from calcium(II) and magnesium(II) ions and having a pH of 7.2. The enriched solution of the stem cell growth factor system can be added to the skin care ingredients of different needs and functions according to different ratios of 1-10%.

PN KR2385368-B1
TI Production and construction of cement substitute material used as construction material, involves preparing quicklime for recycling oyster shells, producing and constructing green cement, removing foreign substances, grinding and separating oyster shells, and burning oyster shell powder.
AU JUNG Y Y
   CHOO Y M
AE CHOO Y M (CHOO-Individual)
   JUNG Y Y (JUNG-Individual)
GA 202251805V
AB    NOVELTY - Production and construction of cement substitute material involves (S1) preparing quicklime for recycling oyster shells, (S2) producing green cement using the prepared quicklime, (S3) constructing the produced green cement on site, (S4) removing foreign substances, (S5) grinding and separating oyster shells from which foreign substances are removed, (S6) pulverizing the separated oyster shell powder, and (S7) burning the oyster shell powder at a high temperature to burn the powder to remove carbon dioxide. The step (S2) is performed by mixing 5-35 wt.% quicklime, 15-35 wt.% fly ash of power plants, 2-7 wt.% gypsum, and 20-45 wt.% fine powder of blast furnace slag of steel mill. The step (S3) is performed by adding a mineral component to the produced green cement, and burning at the high temperature to remove carbon dioxide to produce quicklime or calcium hydroxide.
   USE - Production and construction of cement substitute material used as construction material such as ground grouting and ground cavity filler.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables production and construction of cement substitute material capable of improving soil characteristics, effect of reducing public health and environmental pollution by solidifying various landfills and wastewater sludge due to the increase of various harmful substances due to the corona crisis.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production and construction of cement substitute material involves (S1) preparing quicklime for recycling oyster shells, (S2) producing green cement using the prepared quicklime, (S3) constructing the produced green cement on site, (S4) removing foreign substances, (S5) grinding and separating oyster shells from which foreign substances are removed, (S6) pulverizing the separated oyster shell powder, and (S7) burning the oyster shell powder at a high temperature to burn the powder to remove carbon dioxide. The step (S2) is performed by mixing 5-35 wt.% quicklime, 15-35 wt.% fly ash of power plants, 2-7 wt.% gypsum, and 20-45 wt.% fine powder of blast furnace slag of steel mill. The step (S3) is performed by adding a mineral component to the produced green cement, and burning at the high temperature to remove carbon dioxide to produce quicklime or calcium hydroxide. The step (S4) is performed by collecting and washing the oyster shells containing salt in water to remove chlorine anions, odors and dust, and then removing the moisture remaining on the surface of the oyster shell through a drying device. The step (S6) is performed by pulverizing and sieving the oyster shells to collect only the particle size of 0.2 mm or less. The step (S7) is performed by burning the oyster shell powder at high temperature for baking, where the baking temperature is 600-1000 degrees C and the temperature increase rate is less than 1-1.5 degrees C/minute, after the temperature is raised, the calcination is carried out after a holding time of 1-5 hours. The quicklime for recycling oyster shells has a fine powder density of 2.59-2.91 g/cm3, a fineness of 2400 cm2/g or more, and an ignition loss of 5% or less. The quicklime for recycling oyster shells contains 1% or less salt and 50% or more calcium oxide. The quicklime for green cement has the fine powder density of 2.59- 2.91 g/cm3, the fineness of 3000 cm2/g or more, the ignition loss of 4% or less, and a particle size of 0.04 or less. The quicklime for green cement comprises 60% or less calcium oxide. The fine powder of blast furnace slag has the density of 2.8 g/cm3 or more, the particle size of 2-16, fineness of 4000 mu m or more, and the ignition loss of 3% or less. The fine powder of blast furnace slag comprises 1% or less salt. The fly ash has fineness of 2500 cm2/g or more, ignition loss of 3% or less. The fly ash comprises 1% or less salt, 30% or more silicon dioxide, and 10% or more alumina. The gypsum has fineness of 3000 cm2/g or more, and ignition loss of 5% or less. The gypsum comprises 50% or more sulfur trioxide. The step of constructing the produced green cement by drilling and inserting a casing, installing a tube and drawing out the casing, inserting a packer, and injecting the green cement to which the mineral component is added. The uniaxial compressive strength of the produced green cement 3 days after the sewage sludge solidification treatment is 1.6. The uniaxial compressive strength of the green cement produced 7 days after the sewage sludge solidification treatment is 1.8. The water content of the produced green cement before the sewage sludge solidification treatment is 90.2. The water content of the produced green cement after 3 days after the sewage sludge solidification treatment is 40.2. The water content of the produced green cement after 7 days of sewage sludge solidification treatment is 30.0.
