PN CN112679399-A
TI Method for post-treatment of methionine crystallization mother liquor comprises e.g. preparing 5-(beta-methylthioethyl)hydantoin reaction solution and concentrating first-level crystallization mother liquor.
AU CAO H
   WU Y
   ZHANG C
   LIU B
   LIAN X
   ZENG T
   YANG Y
   ZHOU M
AE NINGXIA ZIGUANG TIANHUA METHIONINE CO (NING-Non-standard)
GA 202145826K
AB    NOVELTY - Method for post-treatment of methionine crystallization mother liquor comprises using 3-methylthiopropionaldehyde, cyanide and ammonium bicarbonate solutions as raw materials, reacting in a pipeline reactor to prepare a 5-( beta -methylthioethyl)hydantoin reaction solution, sequentially beating first-grade methionine obtained by centrifugation, centrifuging and recrystallizing to obtain the first-grade methionine product, beating liquid and the recrystallization mother liquid, mixing with 5-( beta -methylthioethyl)hydantoin solution for saponification reaction, concentrating first-level crystallization mother liquor, performing concentration treatment of mixed solution, sampling and controlling to determine concentration value of methionine in the primary crystallization mother liquor or mixed liquor, cooling to 10-50 degrees C, obtaining bicarbonate and the first-grade filtrate by filtration, subjecting crude secondary methionine to beating and centrifugation and recrystallization in sequence.
   USE - The method is useful for post-treatment of methionine crystallization mother liquor.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively reduces the cost of raw materials and energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for post-treatment of methionine crystallization mother liquor comprises (i) using 3-methylthiopropionaldehyde, cyanide and ammonium bicarbonate solutions as raw materials, reacting in a pipeline reactor to prepare a 5-( beta -methylthioethyl)hydantoin reaction solution, and removing ammonia and carbon dioxide, followed by the 5-( beta -methylthioethyl) hydantoin reaction solution for alkaline hydrolysis and saponification to obtain a saponification solution, introducing the saponification liquid through a stripping tower to remove ammonia and carbon dioxide produced in the decomposition and saponification reaction to obtain a deammoniated saponification liquid, which is acidified with carbon dioxide, and centrifuging to obtain first-class methionine and first-class crystallization mother liquor; (ii) sequentially beating the first-grade methionine obtained by centrifugation, centrifuging and recrystallizing to obtain the first-grade methionine product, beating liquid and the recrystallization mother liquid, using the beating liquid in the primary crystallization mother liquid or used for the dilution of the step (i) deammonia saponification liquid, using the mother liquor of recrystallization to dissolve methionine and continuing to recrystallize or dilute the primary filtrate for sulfuric acid acidification; (iii) passing the primary crystallization mother liquor obtained by centrifugal separation in step (i) into the dissociation tower for pyrolysis, decomposing the bicarbonate and methionine in the primary crystallization mother liquor into carbonate and methionine, and controlling and judging the hydrolysis reaction by sampling; (iv) mixing with the 5-( beta -methylthioethyl)hydantoin solution for saponification reaction when the primary crystallization mother liquor in step (iii) is completely hydrolyzed, obtaining the saponification liquid, acidifying the saponification liquid with carbon dioxide, centrifuging and recrystallizing to obtain the first-grade methionine product; (v) taking a sample to control the degree of bicarbonate decomposition when the primary crystallization mother liquor in step (iii) is not completely hydrolyzed and concentrating the first-level crystallization mother liquor when the bicarbonate decomposition degree taken out from the middle section of the dissociation tower is 0.2-0.6; (vi) adding a solution containing sulfuric acid to the primary filtrate obtained in step (v) for acidification, adjusting the pH to 6-9, centrifuging and cooling to obtain crude secondary methionine and secondary crystallization mother liquor; (vii) subjecting the crude secondary methionine to beating, centrifugation and recrystallization in sequence to obtain a secondary methionine product and washing liquid, and applying the washing liquid in the secondary crystallization mother liquor; (viii) mixing and concentrating the washing liquid and the secondary crystallization mother liquor, sampling and controlling to determine the concentration value of methionine in the mixed solution and performing heat separation to obtain sulfate and secondary filtrate when the concentration of methionine in the mixed solution reaches a certain value; and (ix) adding sulfuric acid to the secondary filtrate obtained in step (viii) for acidification treatment, adjusting the pH to 2-3, using separation equipment to separate the oil phase impurities and aqueous phase in the secondary filtrate, allowing the separated aqueous phase to enter the stripper to remove formic acid and using the primary filtrate, which is used to replace or partially replace sulfuric acid to acidify the primary filtrate. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for separation device for the post-treatment method of methionine crystallization mother liquor, comprising a shell, and a first porous partition and a second porous partition sequentially arranged inside the housing from top to bottom; the first porous partition and the second porous partition divide the inside of the housing into a first separation chamber, a second separation chamber, and a third separation chamber, respectively; the second separation chamber is provided with a aqueous phase; the first separation chamber is provided with an oil phase with a density lower than the aqueous phase; the third separation chamber is provided with an oil phase with a density greater than the aqueous phase; the left and right sides of the second separation chamber are respectively provided with a secondary filtrate inlet and a aqueous phase discharge outlet; a first oil phase impurity outlet is provided above the first separation chamber; a second oil phase impurity outlet is provided below the third separation chamber.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the post-treatment of methionine crystallization mother liquor (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The salt ion in the cyanide, bicarbonate, carbonate, methionine and sulfate is sodium or potassium. In the step (vi), the sulfuric acid-containing solution is a mixture of sulfuric acid, sulfuric acid, and the secondary filtrate from which impurities are removed in step (ix), and one of the secondary filtrate from which impurities are removed in step (ix). Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the ratio of the recrystallization mother liquor for dilution of the primary filtrate is less than 10%, and the remaining recrystallization mother liquor is used for recrystallization after dissolving methionine, the beating liquid is preferably applied to the dilution of the deammonia and saponification liquid until there is no bicarbonate solid after the carbon dioxide acidification is complete, and the remainder is applied to the primary crystallization mother liquor. In the step (iv), the saponification is carried out at 140-200?Oc for 5-20 minutes, the molar ratio of basic salt ion to 5-( beta -methylthioethyl)hydantoin and methionine in the mixed solution of the added fully hydrolyzed primary crystallization mother liquor and fully hydrolyzed bicarbonate is 1.8-4:1, the pH of the saponification liquid acidified with carbon dioxide is 5.5-8, and the acidification pressure of the saponification liquid acidified with carbon dioxide is 0.2-2 MPa. In the step (iii), the decomposition temperature of the primary crystallization mother liquor is carried out at 100-160 degrees C with decomposition pressure of 0.1-1 MPa for 30-360 minutes. The step (viii) comprises sampling and control to determine the concentration value of methionine in the mixed solution and performing thermal separation treatment to obtain sulfate and secondary filtrate under the condition of greater than 70 degrees C when the concentration of methionine in the mixed solution is more than 7 wt.%. The step (vi) comprises transferring the secondary filtrate when the ratio of the organic impurity content to the methionine content in the separated crude secondary methionine is higher than 3%, performing the acidification treatment, adjusting the pH to 2-3 with sulfuric acid with a concentration more than 30 wt.%, heat preserving to less than 40 degrees C, and using separation equipment to separate the oil phase impurities and the aquoeus phase in the secondary filtrate. The step (v) comprises additionally adding the primary crystallization mother liquor for mixing when the bicarbonate decomposition degree taken out from the middle section of the dissociation tower is more than 0.6, making the decomposition degree of bicarbonate in the mixed solution between 0.2 and 0.6, performing the concentration treatment of the mixed solution, sampling and controlling to determine the concentration value of methionine in the primary crystallization mother liquor or mixed liquor, cooling to 10-50 degrees C when the concentration value of methionine in the mixed solution is more than 20 wt.%, obtaining the bicarbonate and the first-grade filtrate by filtration, completely decomposing the bicarbonate solution at high temperature and mixed with step (iv), dividing the first-stage filtrate into two subsequent directions, adjusting the proportions of the two subsequent directions according to the quality of the primary methionine product obtained in step (i) and the chromaticity of the primary crystallization mother liquor, where the two subsequent directions include the application in step (i) deammonia saponification liquid and the acidification treatment, the volume ratio of the primary filtrate deammonia saponification solution is less than 95%.

PN CN112522322-A
TI Modifying neuropilin-1 ligand used for enhancing ability of modified T cells to inhibit tumor growth, by constructing e.g. NRP1-L lentiviral vector, culturing and expanding NRP1-L modified Jurkat cells and performing flow cytometry analysis.
AU REN L
   QI H
   DENG C
   JIANG J
   WEI T
   LI N
   YU L
   OUYANG Z
   XIA M
   ZENG C
AE SHENZHEN PEOPLES HOSPITAL (SZPH-C)
GA 202131604J
AB    NOVELTY - Method for modifying neuropilin-1 (NRP1) ligand involves (a) preparing NRP1-L lentiviral expression vector by preparing the raw materials in advance, optimizing the NRP1-L gene sequence codon, synthesizing the DNA through gene synthesis, and then assembling and cloning into the lentiviral vector pLVX (Clontech) by Gibson, (b) constructing NRP1-L virus vector, (c) transfecting and culturing NRP1-L modified Jurkat cells, (d) culturing and expanding NRP1-L modified Jurkat cells, (e) constructing a three-dimensional spheroid model by taking 1x 104 293T cells, planting in an ultra-low adsorption 96-well plate, and culturing in a 37 degrees C 5% carbon dioxide cell incubator until 24 hours, (f) preparing Jurkat cells infiltrate 3D cell spheroids, (g) analyzing the distribution of Jurkat cells inside the 3D cell sphere with high content microscope, and (h) performing flow cytometry analysis of the proportion of Jurkat cells in the 3D cell sphere.
   USE - The method is useful for modifying NRP1 ligand, which is useful for enhancing the ability of modified T cells to infiltrate the tumor microenvironment and inhibit tumor growth, and blocking angiogenesis in tumor.
   ADVANTAGE - The method modifies NRP1 ligand, which plays a synergistic role in inhibiting tumor growth, improves the therapeutic effect of adoptive T cells in solid tumors, and establishes and improves the technology platform for genetically modified T cells.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for modifying neuropilin-1 (NRP1) ligand involves (a) preparing NRP1-L lentiviral expression vector by preparing the raw materials in advance, optimizing the NRP1-L gene sequence codon, synthesizing the DNA through gene synthesis, and then assembling and cloning into the lentiviral vector pLVX (Clontech) by Gibson, (b) constructing NRP1-L virus vector by counting 3x 106 293T cells, suspending in 10 ml serum-free Dulbecco's Modified Eagle Medium (DMEM) medium, inoculating on a 10 cm Petri dish, transfecting after the cells adhere to the wall and occupy more than 50% of the total area of the Petri dish, mixing the T-cell receptor (TCR) lentiviral vector, lentiviral packing plasmid pmd2.G and pSPAX2 at a ratio of 2:1:1, transfecting into 293T cells, incubating in a 37 degrees C 5% carbon dioxide cell incubator, changing to fresh DMEM medium 24 hours after transfection, collecting the culture supernatant containing the virus in 72 hours, and freezing in aliquots, (c) transfecting and culturing NRP1-L modified Jurkat cells by counting Jurkat cells, adding 10% fetal bovine serum (FBS)/Roswell Park Memorial Institute (RPMI)-1640 medium, adjusting the cell concentration to 1x 106/ml, 1 ml/well, adding 500 mu l virus supernatant, adding 8 mu g/ml protamine sulfate, incubating in a 37 degrees C 5% carbon dioxide cell incubator for 2 hours, discarding the virus supernatant by centrifugation, adding 1 ml 10% FBS/RPMI-1640 medium, and placing in a cell incubator for 3 days, (d) culturing and expanding NRP1-L modified Jurkat cells by taking 1x 106 cells for NRP1-L antibody staining, using flow cytometry to detect the ratio of NRP1-L/GFP double-positive cells to identify the infection efficiency, simultaneously, using GFP transfected Jurkat cells as a control, transferring the cells in the culture wells to T25 culture flasks for expansion, adding 10% FBS/RPMI-1640 configuration medium to 10 ml, and placing in a cell incubator for 3 days, (e) constructing a three-dimensional spheroid model by taking 1x 104 293T cells, planting in an ultra-low adsorption 96-well plate, and culturing in a 37 degrees C 5% carbon dioxide cell incubator until 24 hours, (f) preparing Jurkat cells infiltrate 3D cell spheroids by taking 1x 104, 3x 104 and 6x 104 NRP1-L modified Jurkat cells respectively, adding to the 3D cell spheroids, and culturing for 48 hours, (g) analyzing the distribution of Jurkat cells inside the 3D cell sphere with high content microscope by after 48 hours, washing the 3D cell sphere with phosphate buffer saline (PBS) three times, scanning the distribution of green fluorescent protein (GFP) fluorescent cells in the 3D cell sphere with a high-content microscope, and calculating the overall fluorescence intensity to show that under different Jurkat cell numbers, where the fluorescence intensity of GFP inside the 3D cell spheres in the NRP1-L modified Jurkat treatment group is significantly stronger than that of the Jurkat-GFP control and wild-type Jurkat, and (h) performing flow cytometry analysis of the proportion of Jurkat cells in the 3D cell sphere by after 48 hours, washing the 3D cell sphere with PBS 3 times, adding 200 mu l trypsin to digest for 3 minutes, pipetting 5 times to disperse the 3D cell sphere into a single cell suspension, and analyzing the proportion of Jurkat cells in a single cell suspension by flow cytometry under different conditions of the number of Jurkat cells, where the proportion of cells in the 3D cell sphere of NRP1-L modified Jurkat is significantly higher than that of Jurkat-GFP control and wild-type Jurkat.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves preparing the raw materials such as (i) RPMI-1640 medium (Hyclone, Catalog number SH30809.01), (ii) DMEM high glucose medium (Hyclone, Catalog number SH30243.01), (iii) FBS (Gibco, Catalog number 26140079), (iv) double antibody (Hyclone, Catalog number SH40003.01), (v) 0.25% pancreatin (Hyclone, Catalog number SH30042.02), (vi) serum-free cell cryopreservation solution: BAMBANKER (RTM: serum-free medium for long-term freezing at -80 degrees C and preservation of valuable culture cells such as tumor cells and normal cells) (GC Lymphotec Inc, Catalog number 302-14681), (vii) NRP1-L lentiviral expression vector (Shanghai Langjing, full gene synthesis), (viii) lentiviral packaging plasmid pMD2.G and pSPAX2 (Addgene), (ix) protamine sulfate (Sigma Aldrich, Catalog number 53597-25-4), (x) 293T cells (ATCC, Catalog number CRL-3216), (xi) Jurkat cells (ATCC, Catalog number TIB-152), (xii) NRP1-L antibody (Bioss, Catalog number bs-0297G-APC), and (xiii) Ultra-low adsorption 96-well plate (corning, Catalog number 7007). The step (b) involves freezing and storing aliquots at a temperature of -80 degrees C for long-term storage. The step (c) involves when cultured in a 5% carbon dioxide cell incubator, maintaining the temperature at 37 degrees C. The step (d), when cultured in a 5% carbon dioxide cell incubator, maintaining the temperature at 37 degrees C. The step (e) involves until 24 hours later, aggregating cells into a regular spherical shape with a diameter of about 600 mu m. Preferred Biomolecule: The NRP1-L comprises a 326 amino acid sequence (SEQ ID NO: Not defined) fully defined in the specification.
   ACTIVITY - Cytostatic.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - Cell therapy.

PT P
PN WO2021168498-A1; CA3172429-A1; AU2020431154-A1
TI Removing carbon dioxide from low carbon dioxide            content gaseous stream with carbon dioxide, involves            contacting gaseous stream with hydrogel for absorbing            carbon dioxide from stream, where hydrogel comprises            cross-linked hydrophilic polymer.
AU WOOD C D
   MYERS M B
   WHITE C
AE COMMONWEALTH SCI & IND RES ORG (CSIR-C)
   COMMONWEALTH SCI & IND RES ORG (CSIR-C)
GA 2021A1935R
AB    NOVELTY - Removing carbon dioxide (CO2) from low CO2                concentration gaseous stream having CO2                concentration of less than 100,000 ppm, involves                contacting the gaseous stream with a hydrogel for                absorbing some CO2 from the gaseous stream, where                the hydrogel comprises a cross-linked hydrophilic                polymer comprising a hydrophilic polymer                cross-linked with a cross-linking agent.
   USE - Method for removing carbon dioxide from a low                carbon dioxide concentration gaseous stream, where                carbon dioxide is useful in direct air                capture.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is carried out in a cost-effective                manner, where hydrogel composition is tailored to                provide control over the CO2 absorption efficiency,                in particular, hydrogels remove CO2 from low CO2                concentration gaseous stream by absorbing CO2                within the hydrogel, thus removing it from the                gaseous stream. The absorbed CO2 is then harvested                from the hydrophilic polymer, and the regenerated                hydrogold can be reused to absorb more CO2 (e.g.                recycled). The method is also scalable for                industrial application, and may find use                particularly in direct air capture. The method                allows to remove 50 vol.%, 75 vol.%, 90 vol.%, or                95 vol.% CO2 from gaseous stream.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The hydrogel is                  provided as multiple particles. The hydrophilic                  polymer used to prepare the hydrogel has a weight                  average molecular weight (Mw) of 1,000 g/mol to                  250,000 g/mol. The liquid swelling agent has a                  boiling point of greater than 100 degrees C. The                  hydrogel is a self-supported hydrogel. The                  gaseous stream has a CO2 concentration of less                  than 1,000 ppm. The gaseous stream is an                  atmosphere, where gaseous stream is ambient air.                  The process is direct air capture (DAC). The step                  of contacting the gaseous stream with the                  hydrogel involves passing the gaseous stream                  through a bed comprising the hydrogel or                  introducing a flow of the hydrogel into the                  gaseous stream. The process further involves                  measuring the concentration of CO2 in an effluent                  gaseous stream following contact with the                  hydrogel; and carrying out regeneration recovery                  process to desorb the absorbed CO2 from the                  hydrogel. The gaseous stream has a flow rate of                  100, 1,000, 10,000, 25,000, 50,000, 75,000,                  100,000, or 200,000 seem or gaseous stream has a                  flow rate of 10,000, 15,000, 17,000, 20,000,                  30,000, 40,000, or 50,000 cubic metre per hour                  (m3/hour). The regeneration recovery process                  involves heating the hydrogel to desorb the                  absorbed CO2 from the hydrogel or by reducing                  pressure, or a combination. The hydrogel is                  heated by contacting the hydrogel with steam.                  Preferred Composition: The hydrogel comprises                  1-50 wt.% hydrophilic polymer, 10-40 wt.%                  hydrophilic polymer, 0.1-20 wt.% or 1-10 wt.%                  cross-linking agent, and 20-100 g/g or 40-99 wt.%                  liquid swelling agent. The hydrophilic polymer                  comprises 0.01-50 mol.% cross-linking agent.                  Preferred Components: The hydrophilic polymer is                  a homopolymer. The hydrophilic polymer comprises                  polyamine, linear or branched polyamine or                  copolymer, particularly hydrophilic polymer is                  polyethylenimine (PEI). The cross-linking agent                  is selected to provide alkyl cross-linker in the                  cross-linked hydrophilic polymer, where the alkyl                  is optionally substituted with one or more                  functional groups selected from halo, hydroxyl,                  or amine, and optionally interrupted with one or                  more oxygen (O), nitrogen (N), silicon (Si) or                  sulfur (S), particularly epoxide. The epoxide is                  diepoxide or triepoxide. The cross-linking agent                  is 1,3-butadiene diepoxide (BDDE) or triglycidyl                  trimethylolpropane ether (TTE). The hydrophilic                  polymer is a polyacrylamide or a copolymer or                  hydrophilic polymer is selected from                  poly(acrylamide-co-acrylic acid),                  poly(acrylamide-co-sodium acrylate), poly                  (acrylamide-co-potassium acrylate),                  poly(acrylamide-co-acrylic acid) potassium salt,                  poly( acrylamide-co-acrylic acid) sodium salt or                  poly(acrylamide-co-methylenebisacrylamide). The                  liquid swelling agent is water or a non-aqueous                  solvent, or a combination. The non-aqueous                  solvent is polar solvent. The liquid swelling                  agent is selected from water, alcohols, polyol                  compounds, glycols, alkanolamines, alkylamines,                  alky loxy amines, piperidines, piperazines,                  pyridines, pyrrolidones, water, monoethylene                  glycol, polyethyleneglycol, glycerol,                  2-methoxyethanol, 2-ethoxyethanol,                  monoethanolamine, diethanolamine,                  methyldiethanolamine, diisopropanolamine, and                  aminoethoxyethanol, or combinations. The glycol                  is selected from monoethylene glycol, diethylene                  glycol, triethylene glycol, polyethyleneglycol,                  and glycerol, or combinations.

PT P
PN CN112391190-A; CN112391190-B
TI Carbon dioxide/nitrogen switch-type dual-cycle extraction of oil-containing solid waste involves mixing oil-containing solid waste and organic acid, separating, adding polyetheramine aqueous solution, supplying carbon dioxide, and recycling.
AU DAI S
   LU H
   YUE W
   HUANG Z
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST PETROLEUM (USWP-C)
GA 202121219Q
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide/nitrogen switch-type dual-cycle extraction of oil-containing solid waste involves mixing oil-containing solid waste and organic acid, separating to obtain mixed liquid (A) and solid waste, adding polyetheramine aqueous solution to the mixed liquid (A) to separate oil phase and mixed liquid (B), supplying carbon dioxide into the mixed liquid (B) for protonation and separate organic acid and mixed liquid (C), recycling organic acid to oil-containing solid waste, supplying nitrogen into mixed liquid (C) to obtain aqueous solution of carbon dioxide and polyetheramine after deprotonation, recycling polyetheramine aqueous solution to the mixed solution (A), and carbon dioxide to the mixed liquid (B), and controlling temperature of whole extraction process to 30-70 degrees C.
   USE - Carbon dioxide/nitrogen switch-type dual-cycle extraction in cleaning of oil-containing solid waste e.g. refinery sludge (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables safe, environmentally-friendly and economical carbon dioxide/nitrogen switch-type dual-cycle extraction of oil-containing solid waste at heavy oil product recovery rate of more than 90%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The organic acid is mixture of fatty acid(s) with 10-14 carbon atoms, or mixture of enoic acid(s) with 8-16 carbon atoms.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The polyetheramine is polyetheramine D230 or polyetheramine D400. The concentration of polyetheramine in the aqueous solution of polyetheramine is 0.5-23 wt.%. The molar amount of organic acid is twice the molar amount of polyetheramine. The organic acid is n-decanoic acid, the polyetheramine is polyether amine D230, and the temperature control range of the whole extraction process is 30-38 degrees C, or the organic acid is lauric acid, the polyetheramine is polyetheramine D230, and the temperature control range of the whole extraction process is 55-67 degrees C, or the organic acid is myristic acid, the polyetheramine is polyetheramine D230, and the temperature control range of the whole extraction process is 63-70 degrees C, or the organic acid is undecylenic acid, the polyetheramine is polyetheramine D230, and the temperature control range of the whole extraction process is 33-37 degrees C.

AB    NOVELTY - Producing methane, involves reacting carbon dioxide gas and hydrogen, introducing a reaction mixed gas containing carbon dioxide and hydrogen into the space below the lowermost powder layer, circulating the ascending flow of the reaction mixed gas from the lowermost powder layer to the uppermost powder layer, producing the generated gas containing methane by contacting the reaction mixed gas with the catalyst contained in the powder layer of each stage is extracted from the space above the powder layer of the uppermost stage, supplying the inorganic powder of the uppermost powder layer, supplying the inorganic powder of the powder layer, extracting the inorganic powder from the lowermost powder layer, keeping the amount of the inorganic powder contained in the powder layer, forming a downward flow of the inorganic powder flowing down in the reactor, cooling the inorganic powder extracted from the lowermost powder layer outside the reactor, and extracting the inorganic powder.
   USE - The method is useful for producing methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method captures carbon dioxide gas, removes reaction heat, and reduces the progress and temperature of the reaction to improve percent conversion to methane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for producing methane, involves reacting carbon dioxide gas and hydrogen in the presence of a catalyst, where a tower-shaped reactor in which multiple stages of powder layers are arranged vertically, and each powder layer is made of sand, silica and magnesia, and a nuclear reactor containing at least one inorganic powder containing alumina and/or calcia and the catalyst is prepared, introducing a reaction mixed gas containing carbon dioxide and hydrogen into the space below the lowermost powder layer in the reactor, sequentially circulating the ascending flow of the reaction mixed gas from the lowermost powder layer to the uppermost powder layer, each powder layer is fluidized to form a multi-stage fluidized layer, producing the generated gas containing methane by contacting the reaction mixed gas with the catalyst contained in the powder layer of each stage is extracted from the space above the powder layer of the uppermost stage, continuously supplying the inorganic powder of the uppermost powder layer, continuously supplying the inorganic powder of the powder layer of each stage to the powder layer of the adjacent stage below, continuously supplying the inorganic powder of the powder layer of each stage down to the powder layer of the adjacent stage below, continuously extracting the inorganic powder from the lowermost powder layer, keeping the amount of the inorganic powder contained in the powder layer of each stage constant, forming a downward flow of the inorganic powder flowing down in the reactor via the stages of powder layers in sequence, in the reactor, the ascending flow of the reaction mixed gas and the descending flow of the inorganic powder are in countercurrent contact with each other, and a continuous multi-stage fluidized bed reactor having a temperature gradient in which the temperature of the stages of the powder layer gradually decreases from the lowermost stage powder layer toward the uppermost stage powder layer is configured, cooling the inorganic powder extracted from the lowermost powder layer outside the reactor, circulating and using the inorganic powder by continuously supplying it to the uppermost powder layer, where the cooling amount of the inorganic powder is adjusted so that the temperature of the inorganic powder supplied to the uppermost powder layer is 100-300 degrees C, and the composition and circulation amount of the inorganic powder are adjusted so that the temperature of the inorganic powder extracted from the lowermost powder layer is 300-600 degrees C, and the inorganic powder supplied to the uppermost powder layer contains magnesium hydroxide, magnesium carbonate or their mixture, and extracting the inorganic powder from the lowermost powder layer contains magnesium oxide produced by dehydration or decarbonation of magnesium hydroxide, magnesium carbonate or their mixture in a reactor, and the lowermost powder layer by treating the inorganic powder extracted from the above while cooling in an atmosphere in which steam or carbon dioxide gas is present, magnesium oxide contained in the inorganic powder is converted into magnesium hydroxide or magnesium carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst used in the powder layer in the lower stage contains nickel. The catalyst used in the powder layer in the upper stage contains ruthenium.

PN CN113877496-A
TI Device useful for photocatalytic synthesis of methanol using carbon dioxide and water, e.g comprises tank body whose middle and lower part provided with methanol synthesis reaction chamber which is provided with light source and partition made of activated carbon.
AU SUN H
   ZHAO Z
   WU B
   ZHAO B
   SHAO Z
   YU G
   LIU S
   YUAN L
   PENG Q
   MU X
AE SHANDONG ZHONGKE GREEN CARBON TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202217559P
AB    NOVELTY - Device for photocatalytic synthesis of methanol using carbon dioxide and water, comprises e.g. tank body (1), methanol synthesis reaction chamber (2) arranged in middle and lower part of the tank body, and is provided with A light source (3) and partition (4) made of activated carbon. The partition divides the methanol synthesis reaction chamber into chamber to be filtered (5) and methanol synthesis chamber (6), and the chamber to be filtered is connected with the air outlet (7) of the carbon dioxide gas input pipe and water outlet (8) of the water input pipe. The top of tank body is provided with a vaporized methanol recovery device (10), comprise compression chamber (11) arranged in the tank body above the methanol synthesis reaction chamber, and the compression chamber is provided with gasification separation chamber (12). The gasification methanol recovery device is located above the gasification separation chamber.
   USE - The device is useful for photocatalytic synthesis of methanol using carbon dioxide and water.
   ADVANTAGE - The device for photocatalytic synthesis of methanol using carbon dioxide and water: improves the photosynthetic reaction rate; and improves the conversion rate;
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Device for photocatalytic synthesis of methanol using carbon dioxide and water, comprises tank body (1), methanol synthesis reaction chamber (2) arranged in middle and lower part of the tank body, and is provided with A light source (3) and partition (4) made of activated carbon. The partition divides the methanol synthesis reaction chamber into chamber to be filtered (5) and methanol synthesis chamber (6), and the chamber to be filtered is connected with the air outlet (7) of the carbon dioxide gas input pipe and water outlet (8) of the water input pipe. The top of tank body is provided with a gasification methanol recovery device (10), comprise compression chamber (11) arranged in the tank body above the methanol synthesis reaction chamber, and the compression chamber is provided with gasification separation chamber (12). The gasification methanol recovery device (10) is located above the gasification separation chamber and communicates with the gasification separation chamber. Compression mechanism is arranged inside the compression chamber, and evaporation mechanism for evaporating the liquid inside the methanol synthesis chamber is arranged inside the gasification separation chamber. The piston plate (13) is in close contact with the inner wall of the compression chamber and can also slide longitudinally along the inner wall of the compression chamber. The reciprocating driving member (14) is used to reciprocately drive the piston plate to slide up and down, to generate negative pressure or positive pressure in the lower part of the compression chamber. Gas one-way conveying pipe piece one (15) is used to introduce the upper gas of methanol synthesis reaction chamber into the lower chamber of compression chamber when negative pressure is generated in the lower part of the compression chamber. Gas one-way conveying pipe piece ii (16) is used to introduce the gas in the lower chamber of the compression chamber into the lower chamber of the methanol synthesis reaction chamber when a positive pressure is generated in the lower part of the compression chamber. Liquid one-way conveying pipe piece i (17) is used to introduce the liquid in the methanol synthesis chamber (6) into the inside of the evaporation mechanism when a positive pressure is generated in the lower part of the compression chamber, liquid one-way conveying pipe piece ii (18) is used to suck the liquid inside the gasification separation chamber into the methanol synthesis reaction chamber when a positive pressure is generated in the lower part of the compression chamber.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a whole structure schematic diagram of device useful for photocatalytic synthesis of methanol using carbon dioxide and water.

PT P
PN WO2022017829-A1
TI Separation of carbon dioxide- and hydrogen-containing stream for forming e.g. methane, involves compressing recovered flue gas stream containing carbon dioxide, performing water-gas shift reaction and adsorption of carbon dioxide in same reactor and recovering hydrogen-rich stream.
AU CRAVO PEREIRA L M
   ROBLES MACIAS L A
   LACROIX M
AE TOTALENERGIES SE (TOTA-Non-standard)
GA 202216429N
AB    NOVELTY - Separation of carbon dioxide- and hydrogen-containing stream from stream of flue gas of fluid catalytic cracking regenerator involves (a) providing a fluid catalytic cracking unit (1) and having a fluid catalytic cracking regenerator (7), (b) recovering a stream (A) of flue gas (21) comprising at least carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide, from the regenerator, (c) optionally reducing the pressure from the stream of flue gas, (d) compressing the stream of flue gas, to obtain stream (E) of flue gas, (e) subjecting the gas mixture obtained at step (d) to a water-gas shift reaction to convert the carbon monoxide into carbon dioxide and hydrogen on a catalyst with the adsorption of carbon dioxide on an adsorbent and recovering a hydrogen-rich stream and (f) regenerating the adsorbent of step (e) to obtain a carbon dioxide-stream containing at least 50 mol% carbon dioxide. The water-gas shift reaction and the adsorption of carbon dioxide on an adsorbent are performed in the same reactor.
   USE - Separation of carbon dioxide- and hydrogen-containing stream from stream of flue gas of fluid catalytic cracking regenerator used for forming methane, methanol, higher hydrocarbons, ammonia and/or alcohols.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces hydrogen for combustion or synthesis purposes economically, using relatively low water to carbon monoxide ratios, with high efficiency from fluid catalytic cracking unit working in partial combustion mode and without deposition of carbon on the adsorbent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Separation of carbon dioxide- and hydrogen-containing stream from stream of flue gas of fluid catalytic cracking regenerator involves (a) providing a fluid catalytic cracking unit (1) operated in partial combustion mode and having a fluid catalytic cracking regenerator (7) operated at temperature of 650-850 degrees C and at pressure of 0.1-0.4 MPa, (b) recovering a stream (A) of flue gas (21) comprising at least 5-25 mol% carbon dioxide based on the total molar content of the stream, and 1-20 mol% carbon monoxide based on the total molar content of the stream, from the fluid catalytic cracking regenerator, (c) optionally reducing the pressure from the stream of flue gas in a turbo-expander to recover electricity, (d) compressing the stream of flue gas obtained at step (b) or step (c) to provide a gas mixture, to obtain a stream (E) of flue gas at a pressure of at least 0.4 MPa and temperature of 30-450 degrees C, (e) subjecting the gas mixture obtained at step (d) to a water-gas shift reaction to convert the carbon monoxide into carbon dioxide and hydrogen on a catalyst with the adsorption of carbon dioxide on an adsorbent at a pressure of 0.4-8.0 MPa and recovering a hydrogen-rich stream (H) containing at least 1-20 mol% hydrogen based on the total molar content of the stream on a dry basis, and (f) regenerating the adsorbent of step (e) with pressure of less than half of the pressure of step (e) to obtain a carbon dioxide-stream containing at least 50 mol% carbon dioxide based on the total molar content of the stream on a dry basis. The water-gas shift reaction and the adsorption of carbon dioxide on an adsorbent are performed in the same reactor. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an installation for forming two separate streams containing respectively carbon dioxide and hydrogen from the stream of flue gas of fluid catalytic cracking regenerator, which consists of the fluid catalytic cracking unit, a compressing unit (31) and a water-gas shift unit. The fluid catalytic cracking unit, the compressing unit and the water-gas shift unit are fluidically connected in series. The compressing unit is arranged downstream of the fluid catalytic cracking unit and upstream to the water-gas shift unit. The water-gas shift unit comprises a water-gas shift reactor (35) and a carbon dioxide-adsorption bed.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of separation of carbon dioxide and hydrogen-containing stream and installation of separation of carbon dioxide and hydrogen-containing stream.
   Fluid catalytic cracking unit (1)
   Fluid catalytic cracking regenerator (7)
   Flue gas (21)
   Waste heat recovery sub-unit (27)
   Gas pre-treatment unit (29)
   Compressing unit (31)
   Water-gas shift reactor (35)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The installation further consists of a cooling unit downstream of the fluid catalytic cracking unit (1). The cooling unit comprising a turbo-expander, a waste heat recovery sub-unit (27) and a gas pre-treatment unit (29) arranged upstream of the compressing unit (31).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The step (e) of water-gas shift reaction and/or adsorption of carbon dioxide on an adsorbent is carried out at a temperature of 200-500 degrees C. The catalyst and the adsorbent of step (e) are portion of the same or different material loaded in the same reactor. The partial combustion mode of the fluid catalytic cracking unit of step (a) is carried out with carbon oxide/oxygen molar ratio of 1.0 or more. The compression of step (d) is adiabatic. The compression of step (d) produces heat which is recovered in one or more intercoolers. Before step (f), the adsorbent of step (e) is rinsed with a rinsing gas comprising steam and/or nitrogen. The regenerating of the adsorbent of step (e) is performed by subjecting the adsorbent to a purging gas comprising steam. The step (f) is performed firstly by reducing the pressure and secondly by injecting a gas containing at least 50 mol% steam based on the total molar content of the gas. When the step (c) is carried out, the pressure is reduced to 0.1 MPa. The step (c) is followed by a step of recovering heat, in the form of steam, in a waste heat recovery sub-unit (27) by reducing the temperature of the stream (A) of flue gas (21) to a temperature of 150-350 degrees C. The step (c) is followed by a step of removing impurities from said stream (A) of flue gas (21), such as sulfur oxide, nitrogen oxide and dust particles. The stream (A) of flue gas (21) recovered in step (b) further comprises one or more components comprising hydrogen, hydrogen sulfide, nitrogen, water, sulfur oxide, hydrogen cyanide, carbonyl sulfide, carbon disulfide, nitrogen oxide and hydrochloric acid. sulphate, oxalate or carbonate. The process further involves (g) using the hydrogen-rich stream (H) recovered at step (e) in the production of chemicals and (h) using the carbon dioxide-stream recovered at step (f) in the production of chemicals. The process further involves recovering a fluid catalytic cracking catalyst exiting the fluid catalytic cracking regenerator. The fluid catalytic cracking regenerator is supplied by an oxygen-enriched gas. Preferred Components: The hydrogen-rich stream (H) obtained at step (e) comprises 1-20 mol% hydrogen based on the total molar content of the stream on a dry basis. The carbon dioxide-stream (I) obtained at step (f) comprises 60-95 mol% carbon dioxide based on the total molar content of the stream on a dry basis. The adsorbent used in step (e) comprises an inorganic oxide comprising one or more trivalent metal oxides. The trivalent metal oxide comprises metal chosen from one or more of aluminum, iron, manganese, chromium, titanium and zinc. The adsorbent used in step (e) further comprises one or more alkali metals, preferably lithium, sodium, potassium, rubidium and cesium. The molar ratio of alkali metal to the metal of the one or more trivalent metal oxides is 0.10-1.00. The adsorbent used in step (e) further comprises one or more divalent metals. The divalent metal comprises magnesium, calcium, strontium, barium, cobalt, nickel, copper, zinc, cadmium, or lead. The molar ratio of divalent metals to the metal of the one or more trivalent metal oxides is 0.25-1.50. The adsorbent used in step (e) comprises an inorganic oxide comprising metal compound of formula: (M2(1-x)Al(ax)M3((1-a)x)(OH)2)(C'(power of n))(x/n).y(H2O)zM1mA(power of m). The fluid catalytic cracking catalyst comprises 1 wt.% or more carbon based on the total weight content of the fluid catalytic cracking catalyst as measured according to the UOP Method 961. The oxygen-enriched gas comprises 10-60 vol.% oxygen based on the total volume of said oxygen-enriched gas and 1-25 vol.% carbon monoxide.M1 = Li, Na, K, Rb or Cs;M2 = Mg, Mn, Cu, Co, Fe, Cd or Cr;M3 = Fe, Cr or Mn;C' = halide, nitrate or acetate (when n is 1) or sulfate or carbonate (when n is 2);A = hydroxide, halide, nitrate or acetate (when is 1), or sulfate, oxalate or carbonate (when m is 2);x = 0.01-0.99;a = 0.5-1;y = 0-4;andz = 0.001-7.

PN CN112916866-A; CN112916866-B
TI Preparation of nano silver-copper base alloy            catalyst used for preparing working electrode for            electrocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction involves            adding metal salt to surfactant solution, stirring,            reducing, drying, and treating.
AU WANG Z
   SHI Y
AE HARBIN INST TECHNOLOGY (HAIT-C)
GA 202165778G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of nano silver-copper base alloy                catalyst comprises dissolving surfactant in                alcohol-containing organic solvent, stirring to                completely dissolve, adding metal salt under the                condition of argon or nitrogen, and stirring to                mix; reducing under inert gas protection and                naturally cooling to room temperature;                centrifuging, separating, washing with absolute                ethanol or n-hexane, and vacuum drying; and                treating under argon gas or nitrogen atmosphere and                dispersing in n-hexane.
   USE - The method is used for preparing nano                silver-copper base alloy catalyst for preparing                working electrode for electrocatalytic carbon                dioxide reduction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has good uniformity and high                catalytic performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of nano silver-copper base alloy                catalyst comprises dissolving surfactant in                alcohol-containing organic solvent, stirring to                completely dissolve, adding metal salt under the                condition of argon or nitrogen, and stirring to                mix; reducing under inert gas protection at 160-190                degrees C for 12-60 minutes and naturally cooling                to room temperature; centrifuging at 7800-12000                revolutions/minute (rpm) for 3-10 minutes,                separating, washing with absolute ethanol or                n-hexane for 3-5 times, and vacuum drying for 12-24                hours; and treating under argon gas or nitrogen                atmosphere at 473-773 K for 1-4 hours and                dispersing in n-hexane. The volume ratio of                surfactant to organic solvent containing alcohol is                (0.002-2) mmol:20 mL. The metal salt is mixture of                silver salt and copper salt and volume ratio of the                sum of the metal element in the metal salt and                organic solvent is (0.0-0.2) mmol: 20 mL. The molar                ratio of the silver element in the silver salt and                copper element in the copper salt is 1:(0.11-9).                The metal salt is mixture of silver salt, copper                salt and cadmium salt, volume ratio of the sum of                the metal element in the metal salt and the organic                solvent containing alcohol is (0.02-0.2) mmol:20                mL, molar ratio of the silver element in the silver                salt to the copper element in the copper salt is                1:(0.125-7), and molar ratio of the silver element                in the silver salt to the cadmium element in the                cadmium salt is 1:(0.125-0.9). INDEPENDENT CLAIMS                are included for:
   (1) preparation of the working electrode                comprising adding carbon black and nano                silver-copper base alloy catalyst into mixed                solution of sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene based                fluoropolymer-copolymer and absolute ethanol, and                ultrasonically mixing for 30-60 minutes, where                concentration of carbon black and alloy catalyst is                4-5 mg/mL, mass ratio of the carbon black to alloy                catalyst is (1-3):1, and volume ratio of sulfonated                tetrafluoroethylene based fluoropolymer-copolymer                to absolute ethanol is 1:(17-20); orderly using                alumina powder with particle diameter of 500 nm and                alumina powder with grain diameter of 50 nm on the                glass carbon electrode to polish to the mirror                surface on the suede, ultrasonic cleaning with                deionized water for 1-3 times and with ethanol for                1 to 3times, and drying; and pipetting dispersed                ink-like mixed droplets on the clean glassy carbon                electrode at an amount of 1-3 mu L, waiting for the                ink-like mixture on the surface of the clean glassy                carbon electrode to dry naturally, performing next                pipetting and dripping, and completely drying;                and
   (2) electrocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction                comprising using the three-electrode H-type                electrolytic cell composing cathode region and                anode region separated by ion exchange membrane and                working electrode, placing reference electrode in                the cathode region, placing anode pair electrode in                the anode region, using 0.1-1 mol/L pre-purified                potassium bicarbonate as electrolyte, placing in                the cathode and anode regions, completely immersing                electrolyte in the electrode surface, providing                magnetic stirring rotor, air inlet, air outlet,                carbon dioxide air inlet pipe, cathode air storage                tank, anode air outlet, and anode air storage tank,                sealing device, and testing the air tightness; and                controlling carbon dioxide gas flow meter at 1-50                mL/minute for 5-30 minutes, pre-saturating the                dissolved carbon dioxide in the electrolyte,                stirring, starting power supply, controlling carbon                dioxide gas flow rate to 1-30 mL/minute, stirring                at 500-1300 rpm, applying - 1.2 V to - 2.2 V                potential for electrocatalysis carbon dioxide                reduction experiment, using gas storage tank to                collect cathode and anode area gas product,                separating, and purifying electrolyte.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The silver salt is                  silver nitrate. The copper salt is copper                  nitrate, copper acetate, copper acetylacetonate,                  copper chloride, copper sulfate or cuprous                  acetate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The surfactant is                  polyvinylpyrrolidone, N-methylpyrrolidone,                  octadecyl amine hydrochloride, or secondary alkyl                  sodium sulfonate. The organic solvent containing                  alcohol is triethylene glycol, diethylene glycol,                  glycol or propanetriol. The cadmium salt is                  cadmium acetate.

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves inputting the                to-be-processed marsh gas from the lower portion of                the absorption tower into the absorption tower, and                sprays the alkali top portion of the absorption                tower. TI Method for circuit cleaning used in an electronic            device, involves e.g. mounting a printed circuit board            to-be-cleaned on a working table, passing carbon            dioxide laser, reflecting the first laser beam on the            focusing mirror, and using the second laser beam to            clean the residues.

   LV H
   CHEN G
   LI H
AE SHENZHEN HANS CNC TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SZDZ-C)
GA 2022B99379
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves mounting a printed circuit                board to-be-cleaned on a working table, passing                carbon dioxide (CO2) laser emitted by the carbon                dioxide (CO2) laser through the aperture diaphragm                to obtain the first laser beam, reflecting the                first laser beam on the focusing mirror after                passing through the galvanometer to obtain the                second laser beam, making the table and/or the                second laser beam to move, and using the second                laser beam to clean the residues between the lines                of the printed circuit board until the printed                circuit board is cleaned.
   USE - Method for circuit cleaning i.e. printed                circuit board (PCB), which is used in an electronic                device. Can also be used for cleaning an integrated                circuit (IC) and laser processing technology                field.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes the characteristics that                the printed circuit board absorbs less CO2 laser,                and the processing residue absorbs more CO2 laser,                and the CO2 laser vaporizes or peels off the                residue between the lines, produces less residue,                no loss to the printed circuit board, and no                chemical pollution; efficiently remove the                submicron adhered particles that are difficult to                remove by other cleaning methods; controls the                cleaning process and can be remotely controlled                without manual contact.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of a method                for circuit cleaning (Drawing includes non-English                language text).

PN BY23738-C1
TI Producing microcrystalline cellulose by treating            chopped triticale straw with sodium hydroxide solution            in reactor heated under action of microwave radiation            in atmosphere of carbon dioxide and decompression            carried out by rapid pressure.
AU BOLTOVSKY V S
   ZALUTSKAIA A M
   SERGEENKO L A
   GORBACHEV N M
   MAKARENKO D S
AE UNIV BELO TECHNOLOGICAL (UYBT-Soviet Institute)
GA 2022C15917
AB    NOVELTY - Producing microcrystalline cellulose comprises                treating chopped triticale straw with a 4% sodium                hydroxide solution placed in a reactor and heated                under the action of microwave radiation to a                temperature of 145-150&#176; C in an atmosphere                of carbon dioxide at a pressure of 0.3-10 MPa,                maintained for 1.5-17.5 minutes, after which                decompression is carried out by rapid pressure                release to atmospheric pressure, the resulting                product washed and dried.
   USE - The method is useful for producing                microcrystalline cellulose (claimed), which is used                as cellulose materials from renewable                lignocellulosic vegetable raw materials and pulp                and paper production, chemical, pharmaceutical,                food and other industries.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: ensures maximum removal of                impurities (mainly hemicelluloses and lignin),                limiting degree of polymerization, structure                ordering and other properties; provides cellulose                by treating fibrous waste from cotton ginning and                textile industries with mixture of 6% hydrochloric                acid solution and an oxidizing agent; reduces                duration of heating to required autohydrolysis                temperature and reduces processing temperature,                which is ensured by the implementation of process                of autohydrolysis-explosion in a carbon dioxide                environment under action of microwave energy, this                allows to reduce processing temperature by                50-60&#176; C compared with the prototype and                reduce duration of process; provides hydrolysis of                renewable lignocellulosic material in form of                agricultural waste is carried out without                introduction of acid catalysts under action of                acetic acid formed as result of deacetylation of                hemicelluloses at elevated temperatures; and                increases efficiency at specified parameters due to                hydrolysis of hemicelluloses and destruction of                lignin, which are then removed during                washing.
TF EXAMPLE - Triticale straw pre-crushed and treated with a                4% alkali solution was loaded into a sampler,                hermetically sealed with a lid and placed in a 700                W microwave unit at a pressure of 1.0 MPa to a                temperature of 145&#176; C in an atmosphere of                carbon dioxide and kept for 3, 5 minutes, after                which a rapid decompression of the plant mass was                carried out by a rapid release of pressure to                atmospheric pressure (shot). The resulting product                was washed with water until neutral and dried to                obtain microcrystalline cellulose.
DC F09 (Paper-making production of cellulose, chemical treatment of wood - including chipboard and fibre-board (D21).)
MC F05-A02A
IP D21C-001/06; D21C-003/22

PN JP2022057060-A
TI Ventilation apparatus e.g. heat exchange type ventilator for replacing indoor air and outdoor air, has control unit that controls air supply blower and exhaust blower in response to required ventilation amount calculated by calculation unit.
AU HIROKAWA M
   HIGUCHI T
   KIYOMOTO K
   HASHIMOTO T
   ISHIGURO K
AE PANASONIC INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY MANAGEME (MATU-C)
GA 202258925B
AB    NOVELTY - The ventilation apparatus has an air supply passage that conveys air from the outdoors to an indoor space. An exhaust air passage conveys air from the indoor space to the outdoors. An air supply blower (20) generates an air current in the air supply air passage. An exhaust air blower (22) generates an air flow in the exhaust air path. A carbon dioxide concentration measuring unit (30) measures the concentration of carbon dioxide contained in the air passing through the exhaust air passage. A control unit (50) controls the action of the air supply blower and the exhaust air blower. A ventilation amount calculation unit (66) calculates the required ventilation amount of the indoor space according to the number of persons in the room estimated by the presence number estimation unit of the room. TI Method for calculating, compiling and displaying energy, cost and carbon dioxide savings related to lubricants usage, involves determining savings data by calculating difference in fuel economy between M111 and M111 fuel economy test results.
AU KRAL V
   GIANASCHI A
AE SHELL INT RES MIJ BV (SHEL-C)
GA 202241265S
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves recording an oil change service that is carried out on a vehicle and recording data corresponding to the age of the vehicle. The recorded data is sent from an input device to a memory is coupled to the input device. The Savings data is determined by calculating a difference in fuel economy between a M111 fuel economy test result for a new type of oil and the M111 test result. The savings data is displayed on display devices coupled to a server, where the server is configured to receive the savings data and compile the information for any specified group of local systems.
   USE - Method for calculating, compiling and displaying energy, cost and carbon dioxide savings related to lubricants usage in a vehicle i.e. internal combustion engine (ICE) vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables ensuring that after-market oil changes are selected to provide optimal efficiency and fuel savings, thus ensuring increased fuel economy without compromising on wear protection or oil life, and delivering the latest fuel-efficient oils to deliver the same wear protection and to extend oil life as less efficient counterparts, and hence reducing friction and providing energy efficiency and carbon-di-oxide (CO2) saving.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system for carrying out the process for calculating a lubricant usage in a vehicle.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram of a method for calculating, compiling and displaying energy, cost and carbon dioxide savings related to lubricants usage in a vehicle.

PN US2022065529-A1; US11293691-B2
TI Method for producing liquefied natural gas (LNG), involves generating carbon dioxide (CO2)-rich stream by capture unit from combustion product, and conveying compressed CO2-rich stream towards sequestration site.
AU ABEGG M
   FERNANDES V
   SUBRA V
   THOMPSON A
   HEICHELBECH B
   VAN DER WALT I
AE NEXT CARBON SOLUTIONS LLC (NEXT-Non-standard)
GA 202231295L
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves converting a natural gas vapor into liquefied natural gas (LNG), and storing the LNG. A storage boil off gas (BOG) is generated from the stored LNG, and a combustion product is generated. The heat is recovered from the combustion product. A carbon dioxide (CO 2)-rich stream is generated by a capture unit from the product. The stream is compressed, and the compressed stream is conveyed towards a sequestration site. A unit of the recovered heat is supplied to the generating of the CO 2 -rich stream. The storage BOG is compressed and sent to a liquefaction unit.
   USE - Method for producing liquefied natural gas (LNG) in a facility, such as a LNG power plant. Can also be used for producing methane, nitrous oxide, hydrofluorocarbon, perfluorocarbons and sulfur hexafluoride.
   ADVANTAGE - The overall efficiency of the facility is improved and the greenhouse gas emissions are reduced. The overall emissions from the LNG facility are reduced, since the sequestration compression unit is configured to compress and convey at least one CO2-rich stream from a capture unit, towards a sequestration site. The carbon dioxide (CO2) rich stream from the products of the steam reformer is compressed and conveyed to the capture unit to provide heat for regenerating the ammonia absorbent, thus reducing the overall CO2 emissions. The capture unit includes a chilled ammonia process for absorbing CO2.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a liquefied natural gas production facility.
   Liquefaction unit (110)
   Gas turbine (122)
   Waste heat recovery unit (124)
   Capture facility (128)
   Sequestration compression unit (130)

PN CN113979460-A
TI Comprehensive utilization method of steel slag used as e.g. gel material and adsorbent, comprises adding ammonium chloride solution into steel slag to carry out leaching reaction, introducing carbon dioxide, filtering, adding silicon dioxide and reducing agent, adding additive, and annealing.
AU CHEN J
   ZHANG N
   TAN Z
   WANG J
   SUN J
   TONG Z
AE UNIV YANGTZE NORMAL (UYYN-C)
GA 202223474G
AB    NOVELTY - Comprehensive utilization of steel slag comprises (i) adding ammonium chloride solution into steel slag to carry out leaching reaction to obtain calcium dichloride-ammonium chloride ammonia-water system leaching solution and filter residue, (ii) introducing carbon dioxide into leachate, stopping reaction when pH value of leachate is 7.1, vacuum filtering, rinsing and drying to obtain calcium carbonate, and returning reaction solution to circulating leaching steel slag, (iii) adding silicon dioxide and reducing agent into filter residue, mixing and grinding, placing mixture in high-temperature furnace to carry out carbothermic reduction reaction, and separating slag and iron after melting and separating to obtain metallic iron and molten reduction slag, and (iv) adding additive into molten reduction slag, carrying out clarification and homogenization, annealing at 600degrees Celsius for 2 hours, cooling to room temperature in furnace and heat-treating base glass to obtain glass-ceramic.
   USE - Method for comprehensive utilization of steel slag used as gel material, adsorbent and fertilizer in waste water treatment, building material and soil conditioner.
   ADVANTAGE - The process has no waste water and waste gas; the ammonium chloride solution can be repeatedly recycled; there is no need to add a large amount of modifier. The process effectively improves the utilization rate of the steel slag, at the same time improves the environment problem caused by the slag and carbon dioxide, and also avoids the environment pollution and resource waste. It has great economic benefit and social benefit, and has good application prospect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Method: The leaching reaction temperature is 40-60degrees Celsius. The carbothermic reduction reaction is carried out under an inert atmosphere at 1500degrees Celsius for 0.5-2 hours. The clarification and homogenization are carried out at 1300-1450degrees Celsius for 1-4 hours. The nucleation and crystallization of the steel slag are kept at 780-820degrees Celsius for 0.5-1 hours, and at 940-980degrees Celsius for 0.5-1 hours. The additive is titanium dioxide, chromium(III) oxide or boron trioxide.Preferred Components: The reducing agent is crushed coal or carbon powder. The added amount of the silica is 20-40 wt.% of the residue. The additive is titanium dioxide, chromium(III) oxide or boron trioxide. The crystal phase of the glass-ceramic is diopside.

   Volatile organic compound processing module                (2)
   Carbon dioxide recycling module (3)
   Methanol formic acid discharge pipe                (3.4)
   First clapboard (4)
   Second clapboard (5)
   Temporary operation switch door (6)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation method of                  the titanium dioxide/porous silica aerogel                  includes (a) under the protection of inert gas,                  after mixing isopropyl titanate, glycerol and                  ethanol uniformly, reacting at 130-150degrees                  Celsius for 30-40 minutes, and removing the                  solvent by distillation under reduced pressure,                  to obtain titanium glycerol, (b) after uniformly                  mixing tetramethoxysilane, methanol and water,                  adjusting the pH to acidity, reacting for 18-24                  hours, and then distilling off the solvent under                  reduced pressure to obtain the precursor, adding                  the precursor and titanium glycerol into                  N,N-dimethylformamide, after mixing, adding                  aqueous ammonia, mixing again, and then                  performing gelation, and drying and pulverizing                  the obtained gel, to obtain gel particles, and                  (c) putting the gel particles in a closed                  container, keeping the air pressure in the                  container at 0.03-0.05 MPa, calcining at                  400-450degrees Celsius for 2-2.5 hours under the                  action of ultrasound, and leaving it to stand for                  aging to titanium dioxide/porous silica                  aerogel.Preferred Conditions: In step (b),                  phosphorus pentoxide and titanium glycerol are                  added to N,N dimethylformamide together, where                  the mass ratio of the phosphorus pentoxide to the                  precursor is (0.05-0.15):1. In step (a), the                  mass-volume ratio of the isopropyl titanate,                  glycerol, and ethanol is 1 g:(0.43-0.65 g):(10-15                  ml). In step (b), the mass ratio of the precursor                  to titanium glycerol is 1:(0.05-0.30).

AU XU P
   TAN C
   TAO F
AE UNIV SHANGHAI JIAOTONG (USJT-C)
GA 2021E49033
AB    NOVELTY - Genetically engineered strain, is new. The strains have carried out different metabolic engineering strategies on basis of Cyanobacteria Synechococcus elongatus PCC 7942, including optimized propionyl-CoA transferase promoter and polyhydroxyalkanoate synthase promoter, overexpression of acetyl-CoA synthase weakens expression of acetate kinase, and knocked down genes of key enzymes acetyl-CoA carboxylase, ketone synthase and beta-ketoacyl-ACP synthase III in fatty acid pathway.
   USE - The genetically engineered strain is useful for preparing polylactic acid (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves biomass of Cyanobacteria by transforming natural Cyanobacteria and adopting high-density fermentation strategy at the same time; solves problem of polylactic acid can only be produced from raw materials from sugar in prior art, and solve problems of plastic pollution and excessive emission of greenhouse gas carbon dioxide at same time; and has very great potential industrial application value.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (a) preparing polylactic acid using carbon dioxide, comprising (i) preparing modified D-lactate dehydrogenase gene, modified propionyl-CoA transferase gene and modified polyhydroxyalkanoate synthase gene by primers to target gene, double digesting target gene and vector, purifying, transforming competent cells of Escherichia coli to obtain recombinant plasmid, and (ii) extracting DNA of recombinant plasmid, and importing in Cyanobacteria Synechococcus elongatus PCC 7942, extracting, verifying to obtain engineering cell strain, (iii) culturing engineering cell strain in medium, introducing carbon dioxide and light conditions into medium, centrifuging to collect, freeze-drying, and recovering to obtain polylactic acid.
   (b) polylactic acid, prepared by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The PYLW01 strain is prepared by modified D-lactate dehydrogenase gene, modified propionyl-CoA transferase gene and modified polyhydroxyalkanoate synthase gene on basis of cyanobacteria Synechococcus elongatus PCC 7942; on the basis of cyanobacteria Synechococcus elongatus PCC 7942, by optimizing the promoter of propionyl-CoA transferase, promoter of polyhydroxyalkanoate synthase to obtain PYLW02 strain, PYLW03 strain and PYLW04 strain; on the basis of cyanobacteria Synechococcus elongatus PCC 7942, by overexpression of acetyl-CoA synthase to obtain PYLW05 bacterial strain; on the basis of the cyanobacteria Synechococcus elongatus PCC 7942, the PYLW06 strain is obtained by knocking down acetate kinase gene; on the basis of the cyanobacteria Synechococcus elongatus PCC 7942, the PYLW07 strain is prepared by knocking out acetyl-CoA carboxylase gene, ketone synthase gene, and beta-ketoacyl-ACP synthase III gene. The modified D-lactate dehydrogenase gene in the step (i) is derived from Lactobacillus bulgaricus, the modified propionyl-CoA transferase gene is derived from Clostridium propionicum, and the modified polyhydroxyalkanoate synthase gene is derived from Pseudomonas. The upstream sequence of primer in the step (i) comprises fully defined sequence of 31 base pairs (SEQ ID NO: 1) as given in the specification, and the downstream sequence comprises fully defined sequence of 31 base pairs (SEQ ID NO: 2) as given in the specification. The ventilation volume of carbon dioxide in the step (iii) is 1.5 vvm, and the volume ratio of feeding carbon dioxide and air is 1-10%, and the intensity of light is 125-500 mumolphotons m-2s-1. The substratum in the step (iii) includes sodium carbonate, sodium nitrate, dipotassium hydrogen phosphate, magnesium sulfate, calcium chloride, citric acid, ferric ammonium citrate, EDTA, boric acid, manganese chloride, zinc sulfate, sodium molybdate, copper sulfate and cobalt nitrate. The concentration of sodium carbonate is 0.25-1.5 g/l.Preferred Method: In the step (i), optimizing promoter of propionyl-CoA transferase and the promoter of polyhydroxyalkanoate synthase before synthetic target gene, different metabolic engineering strategies is carried out, overexpressed acetyl-CoA synthase, attenuated the expression of acetate kinase, knock down genes of key enzymes of fatty acid pathway, acetyl-CoA carboxylase, ketone synthase, and beta-ketoacyl-ACP synthase III.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the colored pencil lead, involving (i) adding washed kaolin to a reaction kettle, passing carbon dioxide fluid comprising acetic acid as an entrainer, and reacting at 40-50degrees Celsius under a pressure of 18-22 MPa for 20-40 minutes, (ii) continuously adding acid-base indicator to the reaction kettle, reacting at 40-50degrees Celsius under a pressure of 18-22 MPa for 10-20 minutes, adding emulsifier, tragacanth and natural mineral wax, and reacting at 80-90degrees Celsius under a pressure of 160-120 MPa for 20-40 minutes, (iii) adding pH regulator to the reaction kettle for color mixing, and reacting at 80-90degrees Celsius under a pressure of 60-120 MPa for 5-15 minutes to obtain a mixture, and (iv) extruding the mixture into refills and drying to obtain the product.

PT P
PN KR2021109752-A
TI Exhaust system of combustion engine, has blowing fan that is installed in exhaust pipe between economizer and branch pipe, and control unit that is provided for controlling rotational speed of blowing fan to simultaneously control flow rate of exhaust gas.
AU SUNG Y
   KIM J
   JONG P S
   LEE D H
AE SAMSUNG HEAVY IND CO LTD (SMSU-C)
GA 2021A37133
AB    NOVELTY - The exhaust system (1) has an exhaust pipe (10) that discharges exhaust gas generated from a combustion engine. A branch pipe (20) is branched from the exhaust pipe and is connected to a carbon dioxide collecting device. An economizer (30) is installed in the pipe to generate steam by exchanging heat with the exhaust gas. A blowing fan is installed between the economizer and the branch pipe. A sensor unit is installed at a rear end of the blowing fan to measure a flow rate of the gas. An on/off valve is installed on the branch pipes to control the flow of exhaust gas supplied to the device. A steam supply pipe (31) is utilized for supplying the steam.
   USE - Exhaust system for combustion engine.
   ADVANTAGE - The flow rate of the exhaust gas flowing into the economizer and the carbon dioxide collecting device can be simultaneously adjusted by controlling the rotation speed of the blowing fan. The need to operate a separate boiler for steam generation is avoided, so that the energy is saved and the generation of carbon dioxide due to the operation of the boiler is prevented. Since a collecting space is formed on the outside of exhaust pipe between blower fan and economizer, the soot contained in exhaust gas supplied to economizer can be removed.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of an exhaust device of the combustion engine.
   Exhaust system (1)
   Exhaust pipe (10)
   Branch pipe (20)
   Economizer (30)
   Steam supply pipe (31)
