PN WO2021104914-A1; AU2020393954-A1; EP4065873-A1
TI Subsea installation e.g. offshore installation,            consists of unbonded flexible pipe containing pressure            sheath, tensile armor which is located in annulus and            comprising corrosion resistant material(s), outer            sheath, and anti-bird cage layer.
AU PROCIDA I
   RUBIN A
   LYCKEGAARD A
AE NAT OILWELL VARCO DENMARK IS (NAOV-C)
   NAT OILWELL VARCO DENMARK IS (NAOV-C)
   NAT OILWELL VARCO DENMARK IS (NAOV-C)
GA 202159786N
AB    NOVELTY - A subsea installation consists of an unbonded                flexible pipe for subsea transportation of hydrogen                sulfide and/or carbon dioxide, containing fluid.                The unbonded flexible pipe comprises a pressure                sheath defining a bore for transportation of the                fluid, a tensile armor and an outer sheath. The                tensile armor is located in an annulus and                comprises corrosion resistant material(s). The                tensile armor comprises at least two of cross wound                layers of elongate armor elements, which are wound                with a long pith. The pipe further comprises an                anti-bird cage layer (11) comprising at least one                elongate element wound with a short pitch onto at                least one of the tensile armor layers (15, 16). At                least one elongate element comprises steel,                titanium and/or fibers of carbon, basalt,                polyethylene, polyvinylidene fluoride or                polyvinylidene difluoride, polyether ether ketone,                polyvinyl chloride, and liquid crystalline                polymer.
   USE - Subsea installation e.g. offshore installation                used for transportation of hydrogen sulfide and                carbon dioxide-containing fluid, such as                petrochemical fluids such as oil or gas or in                sub-sea environment.
   ADVANTAGE - The subsea installation includes the unbonded                flexible pipe having excellent durability, chemical                resistance and mechanical tensile strength.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the                unbonded flexible pipe for subsea transportation of                hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide-containing                fluid.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic, side view and                cross-sectional view of the unbonded flexible pipe                forming component.
   Bird-resistant cage layer (11)
   Armor layers (14a,14b)
   Tensile armor layers (15,16)
   Liquid impervious outer sheath (17)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The pipe fluid                  comprises 100 ppm or more, preferably 500 ppm or                  more hydrogen sulfide, and 100 ppm or more,                  preferably 500 ppm or more or 90 mol% or more                  carbon dioxide. Preferred Property: The anti-bird                  cage layer has thickness of 0.5 mm-2 cm,                  preferably 1 mm-1 cm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The elongate element                  of the anti-bird cage layer comprises fibers of                  stainless steel, titanium, carbon, basalt,                  ultra-high-molecular polyethylene, or liquid                  crystalline polymer. The fibers comprise bundles                  of twisted or untwisted continuous fibers. Each                  bundle of continuous fibers comprises at least 50                  filaments, preferably 500-1000 filaments. The                  fibers are embedded in a polymer material. The                  polymer material is polyethylene, polyvinylidene                  difluoride, polyether ether ketone, or polyvinyl                  chloride. The helically wound polymeric strips                  are strips of polyethylene, polyvinylidene                  difluoride, polyether ether ketone, polyvinyl                  chloride, and optionally comprising embedded                  fibers. The pressure sheath comprises high                  density polyethylene, polyvinylidene difluoride                  or cross-linked polyethylene. The outer sheath                  comprises polyethylene or thermoplastic                  vulcanizate. The corrosion resistant material(s)                  comprises stainless steel, titanium, and/or                  composite material. The composite material                  comprises a fiber reinforced polymer material.                  Preferred Properties: The fiber bundles are                  twisted to form threads having diameter of 0.1-3                  mm, preferably 0.5-2 mm. The fibers have                  thickness of 5-250 mu m, preferably 20-100 mu                  m.

PN CN112570013-A
TI Catalyst used for catalytic conversion of gasoline, comprises molecular sieve, noble metal containing gold, silver, ruthenium, rhodium, palladium, platinum, iridium and osmium, clay, and inorganic oxide.
AU WANG L
   TIAN H
   LIN W
   WANG P
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC RES INST PETROLEUM PROCESSING (SNPC-C)
GA 202137203B
AB    NOVELTY - A catalyst comprises 15-65 pts. wt. MCM molecular sieve, 0.5-8 pts. wt. noble metal, 15-75 pts. wt. clay, and 5-50 pts. wt. inorganic oxide. The noble metal is selected from one or more of gold, silver, ruthenium, rhodium, palladium, platinum, iridium, and osmium.
   USE - Catalyst used for catalytic conversion of gasoline (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst can realize the effective utilization of carbon dioxide under mild conditions, high propylene selectivity, and is resistant to coking.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of the catalyst, which involves (1) mixing and pulping, spray-drying the noble metal salt and/or supported noble metal, portion of the clay, portion of the inorganic oxide and/or the precursor of the inorganic oxide, and optionally the precursor of the auxiliary active component, to obtain the solid product (I), (2) mixing the MCM molecular sieve and the remaining portion of the clay, inorganic oxide and/or the inorganic oxide precursor, and spray drying to obtain the solid product (II), (3) mixing the solid product (I) and the solid product (II), and calcining; and
   (2) catalytic conversion of gasoline, which involves combining gasoline, carbon dioxide and MCM molecular sieve-containing catalyst, and performing at temperature of 390-800 degrees C, preferably 520-650 degrees C, pressure of 0.1-2 MPa, preferably 0.1-0.3 MPa, a weight ratio of carbon dioxide and gasoline is 0.22-3:1, preferably 0.25-1.5:1 and a gasoline mass space velocity of 0.3-8 hour- 1, preferably 0.5-4 hour-1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The catalyst preferably comprises 28-38 pts. wt. MCM molecular sieve, 1.5-3 pts. wt. noble metal, 30-55 pts. wt. clay, and 11-36 pts. wt. inorganic oxide. The catalyst further contains 0.5-9 pts. wt. co-active component. Preferred Components: The MCM molecular sieve is one or more of MCM-68 molecular sieve, MCM-22 molecular sieve, MCM-49 molecular sieve, and MCM-56 molecular sieve. The noble metal is preferably selected from one or more of gold, palladium and rhodium. The clay is selected from one or more of montmorillonite, diatomaceous earth, attapulgite, kaolin, sepiolite, halloysite, hydrotalcite, bentonite and rectorite. The inorganic oxide is selected from one or more of silicon oxide, aluminum oxide, aluminum oxide-silicon oxide, amorphous silicon aluminum, zirconium oxide, tungsten oxide, tungsten oxide-zirconium oxide, titanium oxide, molybdenum oxide, molybdenum oxide-zirconium oxide, molybdenum oxide-titanium oxide, boron oxide, aluminum phosphate, tungsten oxide-titanium oxide, tin oxide, zinc oxide, copper oxide, nickel oxide, cobalt oxide, vanadium oxide and niobium oxide, preferably silicon oxide, aluminum oxide and aluminum oxide-silicon oxide. The co-active component chosen from group IIA element, group IIIA element, group IVA element, group VA elements, lanthanide element, scandium element, yttrium element, chromium element, manganese element, rhenium element, ferric elements and cadmium element, and an auxiliary active component is selected from one or more of ferric element, gallium element, indium element, bismuth element, lanthanum element and manganese element. The supported precious metal comprises a carrier and 2-20 pts. wt. precious metal supported on the carrier. The carrier is selected from aluminum oxide, silicon oxide, aluminum oxide-silicon oxide, zirconium oxide, tungsten oxide, tungsten oxide-zirconia, molybdenum oxide, and molybdenum oxide -Zirconium oxide, titanium oxide, molybdenum oxide-titanium oxide, tungsten oxide-titanium oxide, tin oxide, zinc oxide, copper oxide, nickel oxide, cobalt oxide, vanadium oxide and niobium oxide. Preferred Process: The method involves oxidizing MCM-type molecular sieve, clay, precursors of inorganic oxides and/or inorganic oxides precursors, slurring and spray drying to obtain a solid product (a), loading the noble metal and optionally co-active components on the solid product, and roasting. Preferred Condition: The roasting step is carried out at430-750 degrees C for 1-8 hours.

PN KR2225612-B1
TI Heterogeneous catalyst for liquid phase reforming of biomass, comprises e.g. active component supported on support, including at least one of platinum, rhenium and composite, and first region in which platinum is present on surface.
AU HAHM H C
   LEE C H
   KIM M
   KIM S H
   SONHYUNTAE
   JEONG H
   PARK H S
   CHOI S H
   YOUNGSEOKCHO
   YUN C
   YOON S
   HAN J H
   NAM S
AE KOREA INST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (KSCT-C)
GA 2021247556
AB    NOVELTY - Heterogeneous catalyst comprises a support; and an active component supported on a support, where the active component includes at least one of platinum, rhenium and a composite, and a first region in which platinum is present on the surface, and a second region in which rhenium oxide (ReOx) is present, where the platinum is located on the outermost surface of the catalyst.
   USE - The heterogeneous catalyst is useful for liquid phase reforming of biomass.
   ADVANTAGE - The heterogeneous catalyst structure formed is excellent in breaking the CC bond of xylose and can increase the hydrogen selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) producing heterogeneous catalyst, comprising supporting the active ingredient on a support, and reducing and activating the active component, where the activating step changes the chemical properties of the surface of the heterogeneous catalyst by controlling a reduction condition including an activation temperature or an atmospheric gas composition, and the active component is platinum, rhenium and a composite; and
   (2) producing hydrogen comprising either forming carbon monoxide and hydrogen from a liquid reactant through a reaction of a heterogeneous catalyst, and reacting carbon monoxide with water and converting it into hydrogen and carbon dioxide by a water gas shift or forming a synthesis gas by reacting methane and carbon dioxide in the biogas with a heterogeneous catalyst and converting carbon monoxide contained in the synthesis gas into hydrogen and carbon dioxide by reacting with water vapor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The active ingredient comprises a complex of platinum and rhenium. The active ingredient is of formula (PtxRey) (I). The molar ratio of platinum and rhenium is 1:4-4:1. The active ingredient is in an amount of 1-10 wt.% based on the total mass of the heterogeneous catalyst. The heterogeneous catalyst is a core-shell structure comprising a core containing rhenium, and a shell comprising platinum. The surface composition of the heterogeneous catalyst comprises 70-99 at% platinum when the composition is analyzed by an EDS elemental analyzer. The support is aluminum oxide or silicon dioxide. The heterogeneous catalyst further comprises an oxide film located on the surface of the active ingredient. Preferred Method: In the supporting step, a dispersion solution and an active ingredient precursor are mixed and wet impregnated. The support is an oxide support. The activation step is performed at a temperature of 300-500 degrees C for 2-5 hours. The method for preparing the heterogeneous catalyst further comprises a step of passivating the heterogeneous catalyst performed in a gas atmosphere containing oxygen and argon. The heterogeneous catalyst is carbon monoxide and hydrogen by selectively decomposing C-C and C-OH bonds contained in a liquid reactant or biogas. The steps in production of hydrogen is performed at 200-300 degrees C and a pressure of 20-50 bar. The liquid reactant is a liquid mixture in which at least one of xylitol, xylose, glucose, or sorbitol is uniformly dissolved and conversion through a heterogeneous catalyst.x = 1-4; andy = 1-4.
   EXAMPLE - Platinum was supported by a wet impregnation method, and the amount was adjusted so that the active ingredient was about 5% of the total mass of the catalyst. 1.515 g (3 wt.%) platinum salt precursor (chloroplatinic acid hydrate), 2.564 g (10 wt.%) and 10 g silica carrier were added to 200 ml of water, and then stirred at room temperature for 24 hours. Water was slowly removed in a rotary vacuum concentrator, and then dried in an oven at 105 degrees C for 12 hours to obtain a catalyst powder. Calcined at 400 degrees C for 2 hours in an air atmosphere, the platinum/silica catalyst was activated by reducing again at 300 degrees C for 3 hours in a hydrogen flow atmosphere. After reduction, passivation treatment was performed for 2 hours while flowing argon gas containing a trace amount of oxygen at a concentration of 0.5% at room temperature.

PN CN111068733-A
TI Preparation of graphitic carbon nitride nanorolls as photocatalyst for preparing methyl formate from carbon dioxide, involves calcining nitrogen-containing precursor in air atmosphere, cooling, grinding and then dispersing in isopropanol.
AU LV J
   SUN M
   LIU J
   WANG H
AE UNIV HEBEI TECHNOLOGY (UYHT-C)
GA 202038123P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing graphitic carbon nitride nanorolls involves: (1) placing a nitrogen-containing precursor in an air atmosphere, heating at 500-580 degrees C to perform high-temperature calcination for 2-6 hours to obtain a yellow solid, then calcining under the same conditions, cooling to room temperature, and grinding to obtain a graphitic carbon nitride light yellow solid powder; (2) dispersing the light yellow powder in isopropanol for 4-8 hours and ultrasonically stripping to get a white suspension; (3) centrifuging the suspension obtained in step (2) for 3-6 minutes to get a precipitate, washing and drying with ethanol to obtain graphitic carbon nitride nanosheets; and (4) adding the graphitic carbon nitride nanosheets to isopropanol, ultrasonically dispersing to obtain a turbid liquid, then dripping into a watch glass, blast drying at 50-90 degrees C, and vacuum drying for 4-10 hours, where 0.05-0.15 g nanosheets are added to per 50-100 ml isopropanol.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing graphitic carbon nitride nanorolls, which are useful as photocatalyst for preparing methyl formate from carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of simply and rapidly preparing the graphitic carbon nitride nanorolls with multilayered reel structure, high specific surface area and catalytic activity, and low photoelectron hole recombination rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of the graphitic carbon nitride nanorolls as photocatalyst for preparing methyl formate from carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The application method of the graphitic carbon nitride nanorolls involves: (i) taking graphitic carbon nitride nanorolls as a photocatalyst in pure quartz transparent closed reactor, adding methanol to get a suspension, then passing carbon dioxide, maintaining the relative pressure of carbon dioxide in the closed container at 0.5-2 kPa, and adding 30-80 ml methanol to per 40 mg nanorolls; and (ii) placing a xenon lamp directly above the quartz reactor, irradiating the suspension at 2-8 degrees C for 4-8 hours to finally obtain methyl formate. The xenon lamp is 5-10 cm away from the reactor, and the power is 20-50W. Preferred Conditions: The heating rate in step (1) is 2-5 degrees C/minute. The rotation speed of the centrifugal separation in step (3) is 4000-7000 rpm. The vacuum drying condition in step (4) is a vacuum degree of 0.05-0.09 MPa and a constant temperature of 50-90 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nitrogen-containing precursor in step (1) is melamine, dicyandiamide or urea.

PN CN110746687-A
TI Modified plastic particle is prepared by placing polymer plastic particles in supercritical fluid carbon dioxide reactor for infiltration and swelling treatment.
AU ZHANG Y
   ZHANG F
   YANG Y
AE ANHUI DEYI STATIONERY CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 202011692H
AB    NOVELTY - Modified plastic particle is prepared by placing polymer plastic particles in a supercritical fluid carbon dioxide reactor for infiltration and swelling treatment. The treated polymer plastic particles are subjected to ultrasonic treatment with supercritical fluid carbon dioxide in a reaction kettle and then placed in a hot water bath for foaming treatment to obtain micro foamed polymer plastic particles. The mixed solution of absolute ethanol and deionized water is heated in a water bath at 55-65 degrees C with aluminum sec.-butoxide with stirring to get colorless and transparent solution, and then cooled at room temperature.
   USE - Modified plastic particle for a graphite pencil core of a plastic pencil.
   ADVANTAGE - The modified plastic particle effectively solves the problems of easy breakage and slag dropping of the pencil refills, and can also improve the writing comfort of the pencils with good market application prospects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A mixture of absolute ethanol and nitric acid is also added with stirring for 10-15 minutes to obtain an alumina sol, and then mixed with aqueous cellulose solution to obtain alumina sol solution. Ammonia water droplets are added to the composite sol solution with stirring to obtain composite gel or micro foamed polymer composite plastic particles. The obtained composite gel or micro foamed polymer composite plastic particles are added to the container with tert.-butanol solution at 30-40 degrees C in a water bath for 30-40 hours, and then freeze-dried for 30-36 hours to obtain the required modified plastic particles.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The polymer plastic particles are made of polypropylene plastic particles and polyethylene plastic particles according to 3-8:1 mass ratio. The concentration of the tert.-butanol solution is 80-95%.

A three-way valve controls communication of a heat medium line. A control unit controls the three-way valve to recirculate the heat medium to the thermal storage tank when temperature of the heat medium stored in the thermal storage tank is lower than a set value.

TI Highly stable immobilized carbonic anhydrase            useful for preparing carbon dioxide absorber, is            prepared by reacting 2-methylimidazole solution and            zinc nitrate hexahydrate solution, stirring and adding            carbonic anhydrase solution.

   DU M

   CHEN J
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
GA 201979546A
AB    NOVELTY - Highly stable immobilized carbonic anhydrase,                is claimed. The highly stable immobilized carbonic                anhydrase is prepared by reacting 1-3.46 mol/l                2-methylimidazole solution and 0.15-0.5 mol/l zinc                nitrate hexahydrate solution at 25-35 degrees C,                stirring for 0-5 minutes, adding carbonic anhydrase                solution for continue stirring, stirring for 20-30                minutes, post-treating reaction solution after                reaction is complete to obtain immobilized carbonic                anhydrase CA/ZIF-8, carbonic anhydrase solution is                prepared by dissolving carbonic anhydrase in 10                mg/ml aqueous solution of polyvinylpyrrolidone to                obtain mixture having a carbonic anhydrase                concentration of 10-30 mg/ml, ratio of amount of                2-methylimidazole in aqueous solution of zinc                nitrate hexahydrate and aqueous solution of                2-methylimidazole in aqueous solution of zinc                nitrate hexahydrate is 1:14-19, amount of carbonic                anhydrase in carbonic anhydrase solution is                2.5-17.5 mg/ml based on volume of aqueous solution                of zinc nitrate hexahydrate.
   USE - The anhydrase CA/ZIF-8 is useful for preparing                carbon dioxide absorber (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The anhydrase has good stability and                recyclability, retains 99 wt.% of the initial                activity after 3 days of storage at high                temperature and 84% of the initial activity remains                after repeated use for 4 times, which can reduce                the cost of replacing the immobilized enzyme.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use                of immobilized carbonic anhydrase CA/ZIF-8 for                preparing carbon dioxide absorber.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The concentration of                  the zinc nitrate hexahydrate aqueous solution is                  0.31 mol/l. The concentration of the aqueous                  solution of 2-methylimidazole is 1.25 mol/l. The                  ratio of the amount of 2-methylimidazole in the                  aqueous solution of zinc nitrate hexahydrate and                  2-methylimidazole in the aqueous solution of zinc                  nitrate hexahydrate is 1:14. The amount of                  carbonic anhydrase in the carbonic anhydrase                  solution is 17.5 mg/l based on the volume of the                  aqueous solution of zinc nitrate hexahydrate. The                  tertiary amine is methyldiethanolamine,                  N,N-dimethylcyclohexylamine, and                  diethylaminoethanolamine. Preferred Method: The                  carbonic anhydrase solution is added at a time of                  from 0-5 minutes. The post-treatment method of                  the reaction liquid comprises centrifuging the                  reaction liquid, collecting lower layer solid,                  washing for three times with deionized water,                  then placing the washed product in vacuum oven                  and drying at 30 degrees C for 12 hours to obtain                  immobilized carbonic anhydrase CA/ZIF-8. The                  using method comprises mixing a tertiary ammonia                  aqueous solution or 20 wt.% potassium                  carbonate-potassium hydrogencarbonate buffer                  solution with immobilized carbonic anhydrase to                  form a carbon dioxide absorbent, the                  concentration of the tertiary aqueous solution is                  1-5 mol/l, the pH of the potassium                  carbonate-potassium hydrogen carbonate buffer is                  10.3, the amount of the immobilized carbonic                  anhydrase is added by the tertiary amine aqueous                  solution or the volume of potassium                  carbonate-potassium bicarbonate buffer is 0.5-20                  g/l.

AU SIL A
AE SIL A (SILA-Individual)
   SIL A (SILA-Individual)
GA 201967461J
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has housing which includes multiple chambers placed in a pre-defined spot of a flow profile of an air emanating from the pollution source. A first inlet is arranged within the housing and a first end of the first inlet is coupled to an outlet of the pollution source. The input section of the first chamber is coupled to a second end of the first inlet. The input section of the second chamber is coupled to an output of the first chamber to receive the air flow comprising sulphur dioxide and carbon dioxide. A third chamber comprises a solution of potassium hydroxide, and a fourth chamber comprises a solution of calcium hydroxide. The input section of the fourth chamber is coupled to an output of the third chamber to receive the air flow comprising sulphur dioxide and carbon dioxide. The outlet is arranged within the housing and coupled to the outlet of the fourth chamber to release air purified toward an external environment.
   USE - Apparatus for controlling air pollution emanating from pollution source such as soot, mold, pollen, methane, fumes from spray cans carbon dioxide, and smoke from power plants, cars, and airplanes.
   ADVANTAGE - The air pollution control apparatus reduces nitrogen oxides and oxidizes mercury in flue gas discharged from a boiler by an ammonia denitrating catalyst. The air pollution control apparatus removes unburnt fuel and wasteful gases from the exhaust of the vehicles and industries.

PN US2019093967-A1; US10533812-B2
TI Method for cooling gas e.g. natural gas, involves passing carrier gas across droplets to exchange heat between carrier gas and droplets, where portion of heat transferred to droplets melts solids.
AU BAXTER L
   CHAMBERLAIN J
   DAVIS N
AE BAXTER L (BAXT-Individual)
   CHAMBERLAIN J (CHAM-Individual)
   DAVIS N (DAVI-Individual)
   HALL LABS LLC (HALL-Non-standard)
GA 2019280821
AB    NOVELTY - The method (700) involves passing (701) a slurry through a droplet generating device to produce droplets of the slurry. The slurry comprises a contact liquid and solids. A melting point of the solids is higher than a vaporization point of the contact liquid. A carrier gas is passed (702) across the droplets to exchange heat between the carrier gas and the droplets. The portion of the heat is transferred to the droplets melts the solids. The contact liquid comprises water, hydrocarbons, liquid ammonia, liquid carbon dioxide, cryogenic liquid. The contact fluid comprises a mixture of a solvent and a compound. The solvent comprising water, hydrocarbons, liquid ammonia, liquid carbon dioxide, cryogenic liquids.
   USE - Method for cooling gas such as combustion flue gas, syngas, producer gas, natural gas, steam reforming gas, light gases, refinery off-gases, acid gases, hydrogen cyanide, water, hydrocarbons, acid gases comprises carbon dioxide, nitrogen oxide, sulfur dioxide, nitrogen dioxide, sulfur trioxide and hydrogen sulfide.
   ADVANTAGE - The solids act as a reservoir that absorbs heat without changing the temperature of the contact liquid past the melting temperature of the solids until all of the solids are melted. The temperature is greatly reduced from pure water ice.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrates the method for cooling a gas.
   Method for cooling gas (700)
   Step for passing slurry through droplet generating device to produce droplets of slurry (701)
   Step for passing a carrier gas across the droplets to exchange heat between carrier droplet (702)
   Step for melting solid by transfer heat (703)

AU LIU X
AE JIANGYONG BAIRUNFU NATURAL PLANT TECHNOL (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201892089P
AB    NOVELTY - An electronic cigarette oil comprises a burley tobacco extract, sophora flower essence, trimethylpyrazine, Perilla flavonoid, compound extract and an extraction solvent, where the compound extract raw material is a couch grass root, an apple and a Camellia.
   USE - Electronic cigarette oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The electronic cigarette oil has better mouth feel and fragrance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for preparing electronic cigarette oil, which involves, taking 6-9 parts of apple to 80-120 mesh, subjecting the powdered apple to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain a apple supercritical carbon dioxide extract, taking 12-18 parts of couch grass root, 10-15 parts of Camellia to 50-80 mesh, soaking and boiling couch grass root and Camellia to obtain an extract, the filtrate is concentrated to a density of 1.1g/Ml, adding 3 volumes of 95% ethanol alcohol, filtering the supernatant to concentrate to clear the paste, mixing the apple supercritical carbon dioxide extract and the clearing paste to obtain a defatted liver and liver compound extract, mixing 0.5-2.2 parts burley tobacco extract, 0.2-1.2 parts Eucalyptus essence, 0.3-0.8 parts trimethylpyrazine, 0.1-0.3 parts Perilla flavonoids uniformly, adding 100 pts. wt. with propylene glycol, stirring well, adding the propylene glycol and glycerin to the semi-finished product of the compound extract, stirring and mixing uniformly to obtain the finished product.

PN US2018326353-A1; US10486104-B2
TI Method for reducing unwanted emissions from carbon capture process in fossil fuel-burning plants, involves reducing concentration of set of unwanted compounds in decarbonized combustion gas to another concentration in exhaust gas.
AU REDDY S
   YONKOSKI J
AE FLUOR TECHNOLOGIES CORP (FLUO-C)
   FLUOR TECHNOLOGIES CORP (FLUO-C)
GA 201895223N
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves receiving combustion gas (130) into a pathway within a carbon capture absorber (112), where the absorber includes an inlet (120) for the combustion gas and an outlet (124) for exhaust gas (116) to define the pathway. A set of radical species is provided at a point (111) of the absorber using a non-thermal plasma generator (108), where the non-thermal plasma generator is coupled with the absorber. The set of radical species is received into the pathway at the point by the absorber. A subset of the radical species is reacted with a subset of a set of unwanted compounds. First concentration of the set of unwanted compounds is reduced in decarbonized combustion gas (114) to second concentration in the exhaust gas based on the reacting of the subset of the radical species, where the point is between another point (123) of the absorber and the outlet.
   USE - Method for reducing unwanted emissions from carbon capture process in fossil fuel-burning plants.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables minimizing harmful emissions created by carbon dioxide capture processes by capturing/removing carbon dioxide from combustion gas and storing the carbon dioxide for safe disposal or use.

   Points of carbon capture absorber (111, 123)

   Combustion gases (114, 130)
   Exhaust gas (116)
   Inlet (120)
   Outlet (124)

AU CHO W
   YU H

AE UNISYS INT CO LTD (UNIS-Non-standard)
GA 2018884326
AB    NOVELTY - The system (1) has a dimethyl ether facility part for providing necessary environment for growth of crops at building type farms (100) by utilizing dimethyl ether fuel. A dimethyl ether boiler (210) burns the dimethyl ether fuel and supplies carbon dioxide and heat to the building type farms. A sensor unit measures temperature, humidity, pressure, data about flow rate and carbon dioxide concentration of the dimethyl ether fuel at the dimethyl ether facility part. An electric heater is provided with a dimethyl ether electric power generator for producing electric power from the dimethyl ether fuel and produces the heat at the building type farms. An integrated management server transmits data from the sensor unit. The dimethyl ether boiler, a dimethyl ether storage tank (220), the dimethyl ether electric power generator and the electric heater are controlled through mobile phones of a farmhouse.
   USE - Integrated energy management system for controlling a building type farm i.e. smart farm, a green house and a plastic house utilized for cultivating crops e.g. fruits and vegetables.
   ADVANTAGE - The system receives the data from a temperature/humidity/heat/carbon dioxide sensor of the smart farm so as to effectively provide plant cultivation environment and facilitates administration of each smart farm so as to increase productivity of the crops.

TI Method for preparing MAF-4 metal organic framework film for separating hydrogen/carbon dioxide, involves adding polyvinyl alcohol aqueous solution to zinc oxide followed by coating aqueous solution, and adding reacting solution.
AU ZHANG F
   LIAO P
   ZHANG J
AE UNIV SUN YAT-SEN (UYSY-C)
   UNIV SUN YAT-SEN (UYSY-C)
GA 201870113K
AB    NOVELTY - A MAF-4 metal organic framework film preparing method involves adding 2-5 mass % polyvinyl alcohol aqueous solution to zinc oxide, compressing and calcining and to obtain a zinc oxide support and the mass ratio of the aqueous polyvinyl alcohol solution to the zinc oxide is 1:(8-20), coating an aqueous solution containing MAF-4 on a zinc oxide support and the mass ratio of MAF-4 to water in the aqueous solution containing MAF-4 is (0.2-0.5):1, coating thickness is 0.2-0.5 m, adding an aqueous solution containing 2-methylimidazole to an aqueous solution containing a zinc salt, and adding the reacting solution to the reaction vessel together with the seeding support for hydrothermal reaction, and completing the reaction to obtain the finished product, where the molar ratio of 2-methylimidazole to zinc salt is (4-6):1, the molar ratio of water to zinc salt is (400-600):1, the reaction temperature is 15-50 degrees C, and the reaction time is 2-10 hours.
   USE - Method for preparing MAF-4 metal organic framework film for separating hydrogen/carbon dioxide and producing hydrogen by water gas method to improve hydrogen production efficiency (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparing MAF-4 metal organic framework film with better repeatability, stability and it can be prepared in simple manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a use of MAF-4 type metal organic framework film for separating hydrogen/carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The zinc salt is zinc nitrate hexahydrate and zinc chloride.

TI High saturation pressure reservoir carbon dioxide            mixed phase petroleum oil displacement method involves            performing drilling process of injection well, and            performing mixed-phase oil displacement process in oil            miscible state.
AU ZHANG L
   KANG J
   HAN B

   YANG R
   XU S
   REN S
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM (UYPE-C)

GA 201865238K
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves selecting (S101) a                reservoir with large inclination and saturated                pressure. A reservoir structure of a drill portion                is formed (S102) in a producing well. Natural gas                is released and formed in a gas-oil interface when                reservoir pressure is reduced to saturated pressure                from crude oil. A high portion of the reservoir                structure is formed (S103) with a top portion of                gas wells to realize natural gas production                function. The reservoir pressure is reduced to set                pressure value. Drilling process of an injection                well is performed (S104) at low portion of the                reservoir structure. Mixed-phase oil displacement                process is performed at CO2 and oil miscible state.                Different voltage amplitude and degassing phase                pressure of crude oil are determined. Descending                speed of gas-oil interface is analyzed. Gas and                water separating function is realized.
   USE - High saturation pressure reservoir carbon                dioxide mixed phase petroleum oil displacement                method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves production rate, saves                production cost and increases recovery ratio of                petroleum reservoir.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating                the high saturation pressure reservoir carbon                dioxide mixed phase petroleum oil displacement                method. (Drawing includes non-English language                text)

   Step for forming a reservoir structure of a                drill portion in a producing well (S102)
   Step for forming a high portion of the                reservoir structure with a top portion of gas wells                to realize natural gas production function                (S103)
   Step for performing drilling process of an                injection well at low portion of the reservoir                structure (S104)

PT P
PN US2018133644-A1; WO2018093482-A1; CA3043460-A1; CN110099734-A; US10569218-B2; CN110099734-B
TI Multi-stage membrane process for removing nitrogen            and/or carbon dioxide from feed gas involves contacting            feed gas with glassy polymeric membrane, contacting            feed gas with rubbery polymeric membrane, and            collecting product.
AU LIU C
AE UOP LLC (UNVO-C)
   UOP LLC (UNVO-C)
   UOP LLCUOP LLC (UNVO-C)
GA 201838111C
AB    NOVELTY - Nitrogen and/or carbon dioxide are removed                from feed gas by contacting feed gas with glassy                polymeric membrane; contacting feed gas with                rubbery polymeric membrane which is chemically                crosslinked rubbery polymeric thin film composite                (TFC) membrane comprising selective layer of                chemically crosslinked rubbery polymer or high                flux, crosslinked, fumed silica reinforced                polyorganosiloxane TFC membrane comprising                selective layer of high flux, crosslinked, fumed                silica reinforced polyorganosiloxane polymer, both                supported by porous support membrane formed from                glassy polymer; and collecting product.
   USE - A multi-stage membrane process for removing                nitrogen and/or carbon dioxide from a feed gas                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The combination of glassy polymeric membranes                with new rubbery polymeric membranes in a multiple                membrane system enhances separation of greater than                or equal to 1 component from a feed gas. The high                flux, crosslinked, fumed silica reinforced                polyorganosiloxane thin film composite (TFC)                membrane has improved permeance and selectivity                with the increase of operating time due to the                increase of plasticization of condensable olefins                on the membrane or with the decrease of operating                temperature. In addition, the fumed silica                reinforced polyorganosiloxane TFC membrane has                shown comparable selectivities but significantly                higher permeance of methane for methane/nitrogen                (CH4/N2) separation, significantly higher                permeances of olefins and paraffins for olefin, and                liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) recovery and fuel gas                conditioning applications than those of                polydimethylsiloxane rubbery membrane, thermally                crosslinked RTV615A/B silicone rubber membrane, and                UV crosslinked epoxysilicone rubbery                membrane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: Feed gas is natural gas                  or hydrogen. Preferred Processes: Feed gas is                  contacted with glassy polymeric membrane before                  contacting rubbery polymeric membrane, or feed                  gas is contacted with rubbery polymeric membrane                  before contacting glassy polymeric membrane. Feed                  gas is contacted with 2nd or more glassy                  polymeric membrane and/or 2nd or more rubbery                  polymeric membrane. The method further includes                  controlling greater than or equal to 1 parameter                  of the process by measuring a value and adjusting                  the process in response to the measured                  value.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: Glassy polymeric                  membrane is selected from cellulose acetate (CA)                  membrane, cellulose triacetate (CTA) membrane,                  blend of CA and CTA membrane, polyimide (PI)                  membrane, blend of polyethersulfone (PES) and PI                  membrane, crosslinked PI membrane, crosslinked                  blend of PI and PES membrane, polybenzoxazole                  membrane, polysulfone (PSF) membrane,                  polycarbonate membrane, fluoropolymer membrane,                  and/or polymer with intrinsic microporosity                  membrane, preferably PES, PSF, PI, blend of PES                  and PI, blend of PSF and PI, or blend of CA and                  CTA. Glassy polymeric membrane provides glassy                  polymeric membrane retentate stream and glassy                  polymeric membrane permeate stream, where one or                  both streams are contacted with 2nd glassy                  polymeric membrane and/or 2nd rubbery membrane.                  Rubbery polymeric membrane provides rubbery                  polymeric membrane retentate stream and rubbery                  polymeric membrane permeate stream, where one or                  both streams are contacted with 2nd glassy                  polymeric membrane and/or 2nd rubbery membrane.                  Preferred Process: Streams are recycled to a                  membrane located upstream.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. TI Deep water micro-algae culture system comprises e.g. culture tank and nutrient medium dispersion device and carbon dioxide dispersing device installed in culture tank, light distributing system comprising light source end and fiber group.
AU SUN Z
   TIAN F
   WEN G
AE CAFS SOUTH CHINA SEA FISHERIES RES INST (CAFS-C)
GA 201839841Y
AB    NOVELTY - Deep water micro-algae culture system comprises e.g. culture tank and nutrient medium dispersion device and carbon dioxide dispersing device installed in culture tank, light distributing system. The light distributing system comprises light source end and fiber group connected to light source end, one end of fiber group is bundled to form light entrance surface comprising all fiber end faces, at the other end of fiber group, the culture tank is set in water. The fibers located in culture tank are spaced apart from each other, each surface of optical fiber located in culture tank.
   USE - Used as deep water micro-algae culture system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system achieves uptake of substances during growth of microalgae.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Deep water micro-algae culture system comprises culture tank and nutrient medium dispersion device and carbon dioxide dispersing device installed in culture tank, light distributing system. The light distributing system comprises light source end and a fiber group connected to the light source end, one end of the fiber group is bundled to form a light entrance surface comprising all the fiber end faces, at the other end of the fiber group, the culture tank is set in the water. The fibers located in the culture tank are spaced apart from each other, each surface of the optical fiber located in the culture tank provided with an uneven pattern or point structure.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the deep water micro-algae culture system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The optical fiber provided with a concave-convex pattern or a dot structure coated on its outer periphery with a transparent protective film, and the outer peripheral surface of the protective film is smooth. The surface of the optical fiber in the cultivating pool water is provided with a spiral structure, and the groove is concave in the concave surface of the optical fiber. The multiple optical fibers in the fiber group are suspended in the water immersed in the culture pool. The optical fiber group at the lower part of the light entrance surface defines the spacing between the optical fibers through the perforated plate. The perforated plate is provided with through holes corresponding to the number of optical fibers in the optical fiber group, and each optical fiber passes through one through hole. The through holes are arranged in a matrix structure, and adjacent through holes are arranged at a certain distance. The cover body is further provided on the light entrance surface, and a fill light LED is arranged in the cover body. The light barrier is also provided immediately adjacent to the light incident surface, and multiple mutually parallel blinds are disposed on the light barrier. The multiple light meters are also set in the culture tank.

TI Electrochemical cell for use in electrochemical            reactor, has cathode endplate that allows cathodic side            of polymer electrolyte membrane to be in open fluid            communication with fluid surrounding electrode            assembly.
AU SHEEHAN S W
AE SHEEHAN S W (SHEE-Individual)
   SHEEHAN S W (SHEE-Individual)
   AIR HOLDINGS CO INC (AIRH-Non-standard)
GA 201830837S
AB    NOVELTY - The electrochemical cell has an anode endplate                (206), a cathode endplate (208), and a polymer                electrolyte membrane. The polymer electrolyte                membrane has a cathodic side and an anodic side and                disposed between the anode endplate and the cathode                endplate. The cathode endplate allows the cathodic                side of the polymer electrolyte membrane to be in                open fluid communication with a fluid surrounding                the electrode assembly. A cathode catalyst is                disposed on the cathodic side of the polymer                electrolyte membrane.
   USE - Electrochemical cell for use in                electrochemical reactor (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The electrochemical cell has a design that                improves the safety of high pressure                electrochemical carbon dioxide reduction and allows                for varied pressures to be used to optimize                reaction conditions. The reaction rates and                conditions are easily improved, by varying the                carbon dioxide pressure and chemical state from                gas, to liquid, and to supercritical fluid in a                safe and distributed manner. The gas diffusion                electrode or gas diffusion layer is disposed on                either side of the polymer electrolyte membrane to                promote mass transport and electron transport to                the catalyst, to help maintain similar partial                pressures across the membrane, and to help prevent                fouling of the membrane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the                following:
   (1) an electrochemical reactor that comprises                electrochemical cells;
   (2) a method for electrochemical reduction of                carbon dioxide using an electrode assembly;                and
   (3) a method for producing of an alcoholic                beverage.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic diagram of an                electrochemical system.
   Water feed (202)
   Anode lead (206)
   Cathode lead (208)
   Stainless steel high pressure cathode chamber                (214)

PT P
PN CN112774245-A
TI Extracting and separating olive kernel active extracts used in fields of e.g. cosmetics, by crushing olive kernel, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction and performing supercritical carbon dioxide separation in different stages.
AU ZHOU Y
   NIAN J
   LI Y
   ZHENG C
   CHEN G
   LIN Z
   YAO W
   LIU Q
   WU C
AE FUJIAN XIANZHILOU BIOLOGICAL CO LTD (FUJI-Non-standard)
   FUZHOU DASHIJIE OLIVE CO LTD (FUZH-Non-standard)
GA 2021550267
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting and separating olive kernel active extracts by using supercritical technology involves (i) crushing the olive kernel into fine particles of 10 mesh or more, (ii) performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction by adding the crushed olive kernel particles into extraction tank of the supercritical extraction device, sealing, providing carbon dioxide, and dynamically extracting under a pressure of 15-29 MPa, a temperature of 40-60 degrees C, and carbon dioxide flow rate of 100-110 l/hour, and (iii) performing supercritical carbon dioxide separation under a separation tank I separation pressure of 10-12 MPa and separation temperature of 40-45 degrees C, and separating tank II separation pressure of 6 MPa and separation temperature of 40-45 degrees C for 2 hours in each stage.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting and separating olive kernel active extracts, which is used in fields of cosmetics, health food and functional food and for exhibiting antioxidant activity.
   ADVANTAGE - The method obtains extract having 2,2-diphenyl-1-picrylhydrazine (DPPH) free radicals scavenging rate of more than 80%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for extracting and separating olive kernel active extracts by using supercritical technology involves (a) (i) crushing the olive kernel into fine particles of 10 mesh or more, (ii) performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction by adding the crushed olive kernel particles into extraction tank of supercritical extraction device, sealing, providing carbon dioxide, and dynamically extracting under a pressure of 15-29 MPa, a temperature of 40-60 degrees C, and carbon dioxide flow rate of 100-110 l/hour, and (iii) performing supercritical carbon dioxide separation under a separation tank I separation pressure of 10-12 MPa and separation temperature of 40-45 degrees C, and separating tank II separation pressure of 6 MPa and separation temperature of 40-45 degrees C for 2 hours in each stage to obtain active extracts of olive nuclei in different stages, or (b) (i) crushing the olive kernel into fine particles of 10 mesh or more, (ii) performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction by adding the crushed olive kernel particles into the extraction tank of the supercritical extraction device, sealing, providing carbon dioxide, and dynamically extracting under a pressure of 15 MPa, a temperature of 40-60 degrees C, and carbon dioxide flow rate of 100-110 l/hour for 2 hours, (iii) performing supercritical carbon dioxide separation under a separation tank I separation pressure of 10-12 MPa for 40 degrees C, (iv) increasing pressure of extraction tank to 22 MPa, and dynamically extracting under a carbon dioxide flow rate of 100-110 l/hour for 2 hours, and (v) performing supercritical carbon dioxide separation under a separation tank I separation pressure of 10-12 MPa and separation temperature of 40-60 degrees C, and collecting the material separation tank I.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The optimal parameter for crushing is pulverizing the olive nuclei into particles of 40-60 mesh. The optimal extraction parameters are extraction pressure of 22 MPa, extraction temperature of 45 degrees C, and carbon dioxide flow rate of 100-110 l/hours. The separation is performed under a separation tank I separation pressure of 12 MPa and separation temperature of 40 degrees C and separation tank II separation pressure of 6 MPa and separation temperature 45 degrees C for 2 hours. The method involves (a) crushing the olive kernel into fine particles of 40-60 mesh, (b) performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction by adding the crushed olive kernel particles into the extraction tank of the supercritical extraction device, sealing, providing carbon dioxide, and dynamically extracting under a pressure of 22 MPa, a temperature of 45 degrees C, and carbon dioxide flow rate of 100-110 l/hour, and (c) performing supercritical carbon dioxide separation under a separation tank I separation pressure of 12 MPa and separation temperature of 45 degrees C, and separating tank II separation pressure of 6 MPa and separation temperature of 45 degrees C to obtain active extracts. The method involves (i) crushing the olive kernel into fine particles of 40-60 mesh more, (ii) performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction by adding the crushed olive kernel particles into the extraction tank of the supercritical extraction device, sealing, providing carbon dioxide, and dynamically extracting under a pressure of 15 MPa, a temperature of 45 degrees C, and carbon dioxide flow rate of 100-110 l/hour for 2 hours, (iii) performing supercritical carbon dioxide separation under a separation tank I separation pressure of 12 MPa for 40 degrees C, (iv) increasing the pressure of the extraction tank to 22 MPa at 45 degrees C, and dynamically extracting under a carbon dioxide flow rate of 100-110 l/hour for 2 hours, and (v) performing supercritical carbon dioxide separation under a separation tank I separation pressure of 12 MPa and separation temperature of 40 degrees C, and collecting the material in separation tank I.

PN CN109570463-A; CN109570463-B
TI Secondary cooling method for continuous casting billet cooled by carbon dioxide-water spray, involves mixing carbon dioxide gas and water in cold discharge pipe and spraying aerosol formed by mixing onto surface of slab.
AU WEI G
   WU W
   ZHU Q
   HU S
   DONG D
AE GANSU JIU STEEL GROUP HONGXING IRON & (JGHX-C)
GA 201933596B
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves pressurizing carbon dioxide gas through compressor and storing in carbon dioxide gas cabinet. The water is pressurized by a pressure pump and stored in a high pressure water tank. The carbon dioxide gas in the carbon dioxide gas cabinet is sent to the gas distributor through the pipeline and sent to the second cold discharge pipe by the gas distributor. The water in the high-pressure water tank is conveyed to the water quantity distributor through the pipeline and sent to the second cold-discharge pipe. The carbon dioxide gas and the water are mixed in a second cold discharge pipe. The aerosol formed by the mixing is sprayed onto the surface of the slab of the second cold zone through the atomizing nozzle on the second cold discharge pipe to cool the slab.
   USE - Secondary cooling method for continuous casting billet cooled by carbon dioxide-water spray.
   ADVANTAGE - The oxide layer on the surface of the slab is reduced. The metal yield is improved. The amount of the cold water and the amount of gas is reduced.

PN WO2022221954-A1
TI Producing fuel comprises sourcing two upgraded            biogases produced by collecting biogas of methane and            carbon dioxide, capturing carbon dioxide and producing            upgraded biogas, providing two upgraded biogases as            feedstock and producing fuel.
AU FOODY P J
AE IOGEN CORP (IOGE-Non-standard)
GA 2022D3066B
AB    NOVELTY - Producing (M1) fuel comprises: sourcing at                least two upgraded biogases, where each of the                upgraded biogases produced in a process comprises                (a) collecting biogas comprising methane and carbon                dioxide, (b) capturing carbon dioxide from the                collected biogas and producing upgraded biogas, and                (c) providing the captured carbon dioxide for                storage; providing two upgraded biogases for use as                feedstock at a facility that has hydrogen                production comprising methane reforming; and                producing a fuel having renewable content in a fuel                production process that uses hydrogen produced from                the hydrogen production as a feedstock, where the                captured carbon dioxide provided in step (c) of                each of the processes is stored and reduces the                carbon intensity of the fuel.
   USE - The methods are useful for producing fuel                having renewable content comprising gasoline,                diesel, jet fuel, or aviation fuel (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The methods: do not include storing carbon                dioxide produced from the hydrogen production; and                provide transportation fuel and/or renewable                content which has lifecycle GHG emissions that are                at least 20% less than the lifecycle GHG emissions                of a gasoline baseline using environmental                protection agency methodology, preferably at least                50% or &#8804; 60%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   producing (P1) fuel having renewable content,                comprising (a1) providing at least two biogas                streams, where each of the two biogas streams                comprises methane and carbon dioxide, (b1)                processing each of the two biogas streams, where                the processing comprises carbon capture or biogas                upgrading, the processing of each of the two biogas                streams producing upgraded biogas and captured                carbon dioxide, (c1) providing the upgraded biogas                produced from the processing of the two biogas                streams for use as a feedstock at a facility that                has hydrogen production, where the hydrogen                production comprises methane reforming, the                hydrogen production producing hydrogen used as a                feedstock in a fuel production process, the fuel                production process producing fuel having renewable                content and (d1) storing the captured carbon                dioxide produced from the processing of the two                biogas streams, thus preventing the captured carbon                dioxide, or an equal quantity of carbon dioxide                displaced by the captured carbon dioxide, from                which is released to the atmosphere, and reducing a                carbon intensity of the fuel;
   producing (P2) fuel having renewable content,                comprising sourcing the two upgraded biogases,                providing the two upgraded biogases for use as                feedstock at a facility that has hydrogen                production comprising methane reforming and                producing a fuel having renewable content in a fuel                production process that uses hydrogen produced from                the hydrogen production as a feedstock and storing                the captured carbon dioxide provided in step (c) of                each of the biogas production processes, thus                reducing a carbon intensity of the fuel;
   producing (M2) at least one product,                comprising providing many upgraded biogases, where                each upgraded biogas produced in a respective                process comprises (a2) collecting biogas comprising                methane and carbon dioxide, and (b2) removing at                least a portion of the carbon dioxide from the                biogas and producing upgraded biogas, providing at                least a portion of each upgraded biogas from the                feedstock for hydrogen production comprising                methane reforming, renewable hydrogen produced from                the hydrogen production used for producing the                products and storing carbon dioxide removed in (b)                from at least two of the processes, thus preventing                the removed carbon dioxide, or an equal quantity of                carbon dioxide displaced by the removed carbon                dioxide, from which is released to the atmosphere,                the storing reducing lifecycle greenhouse gas (GHG)                emissions of the products;
   producing (M3) at least one product,                comprising providing the upgraded biogases for                producing renewable hydrogen via methane reforming,                where the renewable hydrogen for use in producing                the products, reducing lifecycle greenhouse gas                emissions of the products, where the reducing                comprises carrying out many carbon capture and                storage processes, each of the carbon capture and                storage processes in the storing at least a portion                of the carbon dioxide removed in step (b2) of one                of the biogas production processes;
   producing (M4) at least one product,                comprising producing many upgraded biogases, where                each upgraded biogas in a respective process                comprises (a3) feeding biogas comprising methane                and carbon dioxide into biogas upgrading, the                biogas produced by anaerobic digestion, the biogas                upgrading removing at least a portion of the carbon                dioxide from the biogas and producing upgraded                biogas, and (b3) processing digestate from the                anaerobic digestion, providing at least a portion                of each upgraded biogas from the feedstock for                hydrogen production, the hydrogen production                comprises methane reforming, renewable hydrogen                produced from the hydrogen production used for                producing the products and storing carbon dioxide                removed in (a3) and carbon-containing material                produced in (b3) from at least two of the biogas                production processes, the storing reducing                lifecycle GHG emissions of the products; and
   reducing (M5) a lifecycle greenhouse gas                emissions of at least one product in a production                process that consumes hydrogen in a hydrogen                production process that comprises methane                reforming, comprising providing many upgraded                biogases, where at least a portion of each of the                upgraded biogases as feedstock for the hydrogen                production or a production process comprises the                hydrogen production, each of the upgraded biogases                produced in a respective process comprising (a4)                collecting biogas produced from anaerobic digestion                of biomass, the biogas comprises methane and carbon                dioxide, and (b4) removing at least a portion of                the carbon dioxide from the biogas and producing                upgraded biogas, storing carbon-containing material                obtained or derived from at least two of the                respective processes as part at least one carbon                capture and storage processes, thus reducing the                lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions of the products,                the carbon-containing material comprising carbon                dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The fuel production                  process comprises: hydrogenating crude oil                  derived liquid hydrocarbon with the hydrogen to                  produce the fuel; and hydrogenating a renewable                  feedstock selected from renewable oil and                  renewable fat with the hydrogen to produce the                  fuel. The carbon dioxide captured from one of the                  processes is provided for storage at a separate                  location than carbon dioxide captured from                  another of the processes. The step (c) of at                  least one of the processes comprises: providing                  the captured carbon dioxide for geological                  sequestration; providing the captured carbon                  dioxide for enhanced oil recovery; providing the                  captured carbon dioxide for sequestration in                  concrete; and compressing the captured carbon                  dioxide and injecting the captured carbon dioxide                  into a carbon dioxide distribution system                  configured to transport the captured carbon                  dioxide to storage. The step (b) of at least one                  of the processes comprises: capturing the carbon                  dioxide using carbon dioxide capture comprising                  cryogenic separation, membrane separation, or                  absorption with amine solvent; or capturing at                  least 70% of the carbon dioxide originally                  present in the biogas. The two upgraded biogases                  are derived from: organic waste; manure                  comprising swine manure or dairy manure; or                  landfill gas. The two upgraded biogases are                  transported to the facility using a natural gas                  distribution system. The methane reforming                  comprises steam methane reforming, where at least                  a portion of heat for the steam methane reforming                  is produced from low carbon electricity. In step                  (a), the processes comprises collecting biogas                  from an anerobic digestion that produces                  digestate, where each of the processes further                  comprises storing carbon-containing material                  obtained or derived from the digestate. The                  carbon dioxide produced from hydrogen production                  is not provided for use as part of carbon capture                  and storage. Preferred Components: The feedstock                  for the hydrogen production comprises the two                  upgraded biogases and fossil based natural gas.                  The products comprise fuel.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of chemical                entities and greenhouse gas emission values that do                not support the claims.

PT P
PN CN111204761-A; CN111204761-B
TI Performing electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide by combining ionic liquid with nitrogen-doped porous carbon material involves mixing nitrogen-doped porous carbon material with isopropanol, and adding Nafion solution ultrasonically.
AU ZHANG X
   FENG J
   NIE Y
   DONG H
   CHEN H
   JIANG C
AE ZHENGZHOU INST EMERGING IND TECHNOLOGY (ZHEN-Non-standard)
GA 202049446P
AB    NOVELTY - Performing electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide by combining an ionic liquid with a nitrogen-doped porous carbon material involves mixing the nitrogen-doped porous carbon material with isopropanol, adding Nafion solution ultrasonically to obtain the catalyst coating liquid for 30 minutes, applying the coating liquid uniformly on the carbon cloth, and drying it naturally at room temperature to obtain the working electrode. The prepared working electrode is placed in an ionic liquid electrolyte. The carbon dioxide is continuously fed to perform constant-potential electrochemical reduction.
   USE - Method for performing electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide by combining ionic liquid with nitrogen-doped porous carbon material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide which has large surface area and high conductivity to electrochemically reduce carbon dioxide, has high carbon monoxide selectivity, high current density, and good stability, and can provide a valuable way for the carbon dioxide resource utilization.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ionic liquid is an imidazole ionic liquid. Preferred Conditions: The ionic liquid-acetonitrile solution with the concentration of the imidazole ionic liquid is 0.5-2 mole per liter. The ionic liquid is (Emim)(BF4), (Bmim)(BF4), (BMmim)(BF4), (Bzmim)(BF4) and (B3Mmim)(BF4), acetonitrile contains water with a mass fraction of 5-20 wt.%. The molar ratio of the iodine to polyaniline is 1:(0.5-2), and the calcination temperature 600-1000 degrees C, time is 1-4 hours. The potential range of the constant potential electrochemical reduction is (-)1.6-(-)2.9 volt, and the reduction time is 1 hours. The specific surface area of the nitrogen-doped porous carbon material is 500-1500 square meter per gram, and the conductivity is 10-30Scm-1. Preferred Process: The nitrogen-doped porous carbon material is prepared by using aniline as the monomer and ammonium persulfate as the oxidant to prepare polyaniline by oxidative polymerization, adding the polyaniline to the iodine-containing ethanol solution, and after continuous stirring for 24 hours, filtering, washing, and drying to obtain iodine-impregnated polyaniline. The impregnated polyaniline is calcined under the protection of nitrogen to obtain a nitrogen-doped porous carbon material.

AU HARASIN S J
   POTTS B H
   RIDDLE K R
AE COVESTRO LLC (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO LLC (CVST-C)
GA 202135042R
AB    NOVELTY - Polyol premix comprises polyol, carbon dioxide generating chemical blowing agent, ester that does not contain Zerewitinoff-active hydrogen atoms, catalyst and polydimethylsiloxane-polyalkyleneoxide copolymer having weight average molecular weight of 5000 gram/mole and silicon content of 12 wt.% based on total weight of polydimethylsiloxane-polyalkyleneoxide copolymer.
   USE - Polyol premix.
   ADVANTAGE - The polyol premix improves foam properties such as closed-cell content, compressive strength and density.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for making polyurethane foam, which involves:
   a. mixing polyol premix with polyisocyanate at isocyanate index of 90-150.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Compositions: The polyol premix comprises 20-70 wt.% aliphatic amine-initiated polyether polyol having OH number of 400-800 mg potassium hydroxide (KOH)/g and functionality of 3.5-4.5, alkanolamine-initiated polyether polyol having OH number of 500-900 mg KOH/g and functionality of 2.5-3.5, and 5-15 wt.% saccharide-initiated polyether polyol having OH number of 200-600 mg KOH/g and functionality of 4-6.Preferred Conditions: The ratio of aliphatic amine-initiated polyether polyol and alkanolamine-initiated polyether polyol is 1:5-5:1. The polydimethylsiloxane-polyalkyleneoxide copolymer has silicon content of 15 wt.%. The polydimethylsiloxane-polyalkyleneoxide copolymer has polydispersity index of 3.0. The polydimethylsiloxane-polyalkyleneoxide copolymer has OH number of 40-80 mg KOH/g.Preferred Components: The 10-50 wt.% ester that does not contain Zerewitinoff-active hydrogen atoms comprises diester. The diester comprises 2,2,4-trimethyl-1,3-pentanediol diisobutyrate. The polyisocyanate comprises methylene-bridged polyphenyl polyisocyanate and prepolymer of methylene-bridged polyphenyl polyisocyanates having average functionality of 1.8-3.5 isocyanate moieties per molecule and NCO content of 25-32 wt.%.Preferred Process: The polyurethane foam-forming reaction mixture is injected beneath portion of earth-supported structure. The polyurethane foam-forming reaction mixture is allowed to react and form closed-celled, rigid polyurethane foam beneath earth-supported structure.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
