PT P
PN CN112299453-A; CN112299453-B
TI Preparing high-purity lithium fluoride, comprises            adding ammonium fluoride into aqueous lithium            bicarbonate solution obtained by carbonizing lithium            carbonate with carbon dioxide gas.
AU TAN Y
   REN Q
AE SHENZHEN MAXCHEM TECH CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 202116389U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high-purity lithium fluoride,                comprises (i) adding ammonium fluoride into aqueous                lithium bicarbonate solution obtained by                carbonizing lithium carbonate with carbon dioxide                gas to obtain refined solution A as final product;                or (ii) adding ammonium fluoride into the refined                solution B obtained from the mixed solution of                lithium hydroxide and high-purity lithium formate                to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing high-purity                lithium fluoride.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can prepare high-purity lithium                fluoride with purity more than 99.5%; has high                conversion rate of raw material, high product                quality and low production cost.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises                  controlling the feeding speed of ammonium                  fluoride, and after the feeding is completed, by                  changing the reaction temperature and pressure                  parameters in a closed reactor, completing the                  control of the metathesis reaction process and                  the production rate of the target product lithium                  fluoride. The feeding is completed at room                  temperature, and after mixing thoroughly by                  stirring, replace the air in the reactor with                  nitrogen, nitrogen pressurizes the reaction                  kettle to between 0.01 MPa and 1 MPa; the                  temperature of the materials in the kettle is                  gradually increased, the removal rate of the gas                  generated by the reaction in the reactor is                  controlled by controlling the temperature of the                  reactor and the pressure in the reactor. The                  method comprises using the metathesis route,                  during the metathesis reaction or after the                  reaction is complete, adjusting the mass fraction                  of ammonia in the solvent system of the reaction,                  improving the solubility of lithium fluoride to                  optimize the crystal size of lithium fluoride,                  mainly achieving the former by adjusting the                  content and escape rate of ammonia in the solvent                  by changing the temperature and pressure                  parameters. The method comprises adding ammonia                  into the solvent when dissolving the raw                  materials; or generating ammonia through the                  reaction of lithium hydroxide and ammonium                  fluoride, namely: the airtight reaction kettle is                  used to react under pressure to keep the ammonia                  in the solvent to control the precipitation                  process of lithium fluoride crystals. The method                  comprises (i) dissolving industrial grade                  ammonium bifluoride in excess ammonia water to                  convert it into ammonium fluoride, and removing                  impurities by precision filtration, (ii) adding                  reagent grade oxalic acid and/or ammonium oxalate                  equivalent into 0.1-2% of the molar amount of                  ammonium fluoride, stirring evenly, and filtering                  again to remove impurities, (iii) evaporating,                  dehydrating, crystallizing, and centrifugating,                  scrubbing with a small amount of electronic grade                  organic solvent, and then drying to obtain                  refined grade ammonium fluoride; or                  recrystallizing technical grade ammonium                  bifluoride and/or technical grade ammonium                  fluoride in an organic solvent to obtain refined                  grade ammonium fluoride. The method comprises (a)                  carbonizing industrial grade lithium carbonate                  with carbon dioxide, preferably, mixing                  industrial grade lithium carbonate with pure                  water, stirring, configuring into a lithium                  carbonate slurry with a concentration of 10-30                  wt.%, and continuously introducing carbon dioxide                  gas into the slurry, carrying out carbonization                  reaction for 4-5 hours, and controlling the                  reaction temperature to be 30-40 degrees C until                  the reaction is completed to obtain suspension,                  and precision filtrating the lithium bicarbonate                  aqueous solution obtained and removing impurities                  i.e. refined solution A, (b) adding high-purity                  ammonium formate into the refined solution A at                  an equimolar ratio of 1:1 and fully dissolving,                  heating at 40-90 degrees C, removing carbon                  dioxide and ammonia in a closed container under                  negative pressure to obtain aqueous solution of                  lithium formate, and (c) adding                  tetramethylammonium hydroxide into the lithium                  formate aqueous solution, adjusting the pH to the                  range of 9-13, heating, evaporating and                  concentrating to remove part of the water to a                  saturated state, precise filtrating and removing                  impurities, cooling and crystallization,                  centrifugating, and removing liquid to obtain                  lithium formate crystal and drying to obtain high                  purity lithium formate. The refined solution B is                  prepared by first dissolving industrial-grade                  lithium hydroxide with pure water or aqueous                  ammonia to prepare saturated solution, adding                  high-purity lithium formate and fully dissolving,                  filtering and removing impurities to obtain                  refined liquid B. The step (ii) comprises (1)                  dissolving industrial-grade lithium hydroxide in                  pure water or aqueous ammonia, adding high-purity                  lithium formate and dissolving, removing                  insoluble impurities, adding the filtrate into                  the reactor, and open the reactor and stirring,                  (2) adding into ammonium fluoride at a feed rate                  of 1-10 kg per minute, (3) replacing the air in                  the reactor with nitrogen, heating, maintaining                  the temperature of the material at 0-40 degrees C                  for 1-4 hours, then heating to increase the                  temperature, controlling the heating rate per                  minute between 1-2 degrees C, gradually                  increasing the temperature of the materials in                  the reaction kettle to between 40 degrees C and                  the boiling point, and stirring for 2-24 hours                  during the heating period, and maintaining the                  pressure in the reactor at a positive pressure                  between 0.01 and 1.0 MPa, slowly reducing the air                  pressure in the kettle to normal pressure within                  30 minutes, heating the materials in the kettle                  to a boiling state, condensing the steam in the                  kettle and collecting by the condenser on the top                  of the kettle, and stop heating when the pH value                  of the reflux liquid in the condenser is less                  than 8, continue maintaining the stirring of the                  materials in the reactor, and cooling the                  materials in the reactor to 20-40 degrees C                  before discharging, (4) solid-liquid separating                  the materials discharged from the reactor,                  separating liquid-phase materials and collecting                  mother liquor, washing filter cake, and                  collecting filtrate, (5) heating and drying,                  removing the moisture and volatiles in the filter                  cake, and obtaining the high-purity lithium                  fluoride after cooling, and (6) collecting the                  mother liquor of solid-liquid separation in step                  (iv), concentrating, filtering and removing                  impurities, and crystallizing to obtain ammonium                  formate, recrystallizing in electronic grade                  anhydrous methanol to obtain high-purity ammonium                  formate, preferably the obtained high-purity                  ammonium formate is used in step (b). Preferred                  Components: The industrial grade lithium                  carbonate comprises 99 wt.% lithium carbonate,                  greater than or equal to 500 parts per million                  sodium, greater than or equal to 100 parts per                  million magnesium, greater than or equal to 50                  parts per million iron, greater than or equal to                  20 parts per million potassium, aluminum, and                  copper, greater than or equal to 200 parts per                  million calcium, and greater than or equal to                  1000 parts per million sulfate.

AU ZHANG Z
   ZHANG J
   ZHU Y
AE UNIV HENAN POLYTECHNIC (UYHP-C)
GA 202113387Y
AB    NOVELTY - Reinforced concrete composite corrosion inhibitor based on natural gluten comprises 15-40 %mass natural gluten, 50-70 %mass auxiliary material and 0-20 %mass additive.
   USE - The inhibitor is useful for slowing down or inhibiting the corrosion of steel bars in reinforced concrete structures under the erosion of chloride ions or carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The inhibitor has low toxicity, adopts simple preparation technique, easy access to gluten and renewable resource and is environmentally-friendly and suitable for reinforcing steel bar corrosion protection of concrete structure engineering.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The auxiliary material is silica fume or ground slag. The additive is alcohol amine, calcium nitrate or sulfate coagulant, where the alcohol amine is triethanolamine and the sulfate is calcium sulfate. Preferred Composition: The inhibitor is doped in the form of an additive when the concrete is stirred, where the amount of the inhibitor is 5-20% based on the mass of the cement gel material.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The natural gluten protein is one of wheat gluten, corn gluten and rice gluten. The natural gluten is alkali-soluble gluten.

PN CN112209857-A
TI Preparation of dicyandiamide involves hydrolyzing            lime nitrogen and water, introducing carbon dioxide for            decalcification reaction to form cyanamide aqueous            solution, filtering, heating, polymerizing pure            cyanamide solution and filtering.
AU SUN H
   ZHOU P
   MA Y
AE NINGXIA LANBAIHEI RECYCLING TECHNOLOGY (NING-Non-standard)
GA 202110187X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of dicyandiamide involves mixing                lime nitrogen and water, performing hydrolysis                reaction, introducing carbon dioxide for                decalcification reaction to obtain calcium                carbonate and cyanamide aqueous solution, filtering                cyanamide aqueous solution to filter out                particulate matter to obtain pure cyanamide aqueous                solution, heating and polymerizing pure cyanamide                aqueous solution to obtain dicyandiamide aqueous                solution, filtering, cooling, filtering and drying                to form dicyandiamide crystals.
   USE - Preparation of dicyandiamide.
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation method effectively improves                the product quality and effectively removes                impurities such as calcium oxide, calcium hydroxide                and calcium carbonate in dicyandiamide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The preparation involves                  mixing crushed lime nitrogen and water to                  hydrolysis tank to carry out hydrolysis reaction                  to generate the cyanamide-calcium hydrogen                  solution, feeding into decalcification reaction                  tower, passing carbon dioxide into                  decalcification reaction tower to obtain calcium                  carbonate and cyanamide aqueous solution, passing                  a portion of cyanamide aqueous solution to                  hydrolysis step as aqueous solution for                  hydrolyzing the lime nitrogen, filtering                  cyanamide aqueous solution out particles with                  particle size of more than 0.1 mm with filter                  cloth, using diatomaceous earth filter to filter                  out particles with particle size of more than                  0.01 mm in cyanamide aqueous solution under                  pressure of 1 kg to obtain pure aqueous cyanamide                  solution, heating and polymerizing the pure                  aqueous cyanamide solution at 30-70 degrees C                  under pH condition of 8-11 to obtain                  dicyandiamide aqueous solution, filtering out                  particles with particle size of more than 0.1 mm                  in dicyandiamide aqueous solution at 65-75                  degrees C using filter cloth, using diatomaceous                  earth filter to filter out particles with                  particle size of more than 0.01 mm at 65-70                  degrees C under pressure of 1 kg, cooling,                  filtering at 30-40 degrees C to obtain moist                  dicyandiamide crystals and drying moist                  dicyandiamide crystals, passing filtered                  dicyandiamide solution to hydrolysis tank to                  hydrolyze the lime nitrogen.

Camellia seed, and 20-50 pts. wt., preferably 20 pts. wt. green tea is used. Preferred Components: The mesh used in step (a) is 100-120 mesh.

PT P
PN CN111592361-A; CN111592361-B; WO2021203695-A1; EP3998246-A1; US2022274888-A1; EP3998246-A4
TI Nitride high-entropy ceramic fiber comprises            titanium, hafnium, tantalum, niobium and molybdenum            elements useful in photocatalytic carbon dioxide            production of methane.
AU ZHAO T
   LI W
   YE L
   CHEN F
   SUN Y
AE CHINESE ACAD SCI CHEM INST (CSCI-C)
   CHINESE ACAD SCI CHEM INST (CSCI-C)
   CHINESE ACAD SCI CHEM INST (CSCI-C)
GA 2020857745
AB    NOVELTY - Nitride high-entropy ceramic fiber comprises                titanium, hafnium, tantalum, niobium and molybdenum                elements. The nitride high-entropy ceramic fibers                are in a single crystal phase, and each element is                uniformly distributed at the molecular level.
   USE - The ceramic fiber is useful in photocatalytic                carbon dioxide production of methane                (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing nitride high-entropy ceramic fiber                comprises combining titanium, hafnium, tantalum,                niobium and molybdenum elements carbide with                high-entropy ceramic precursors, uniformly mixing                the spinning auxiliaries and solvents to obtain                precursor spinning solution, spinning, debinding                and nitriding to prepare final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The addition amount of                  each metal element in the high-entropy ceramic                  fiber is 5-35% the total amount of metal                  elements, preferably the amounts of the metal                  elements are the same. In the precursor spinning                  solution, the mass ratio between carbide                  high-entropy ceramic precursor, spinning                  auxiliaries and solvent is 1:0.1-1:5-20,                  preferably 1:0.2-0.5:5-10. The spinning                  auxiliaries are polymethyl methacrylate,                  polyvinyl acetate, polyvinyl butyral and/or                  polyvinylpyrrolidone; and the solvent includes                  ethanol, acetone, n-propanol, ethylene glycol                  methyl ether and/or N,N-dimethylformamide.                  Preferred Method: The high-entropy ceramic                  precursor is prepared by (i) dropping the                  complexing agent into the metal alkoxide M(OR)n                  containing the target metal element, and continue                  stirring for 0.1-5 hours to obtain the metal                  alkoxide complex, (ii) selecting prepared metal                  alkoxide complexes containing different metal                  elements, mixing uniformly, slowly dripping the                  mixed liquid of water and monoalcohol, refluxing                  for 1-5 hours after dripping, and distilling at                  normal pressure to obtain metal alkoxide                  copolymer, (iii) mixing uniformly obtained metal                  alkoxide copolymer and allyl phenolic aldehyde,                  heating at 50-90 degrees C, reacting for 0.5-4                  hours, and cooling to obtain final product. The                  nitriding step comprises: nitriding the fiber                  after debinding under ammonia atmosphere at                  600-1000 degrees C for 0.5-5 hours. The debinding                  step comprises: heating at 500-600 degrees C at a                  rate of 0.5-5 degrees C/minute under inert                  atmosphere, and maintaining the temperature for                  2-4 hours. The spinning is air spinning,                  electrostatic spinning and/or spun                  spinning.

PN US2020247724-A1; US11345644-B2
TI Concrete product comprises green unit comprises            zero-slump to near-zero-slump concrete mixture and has            molded predetermined shape of concrete masonry unit,            where green unit sequesters ambient carbon dioxide over            period of time.
AU DAVELA C
AE CONCRETE PROD GROUP LLC (CONC-Non-standard)
   CONCRETE PROD GROUP LLC (CONC-Non-standard)
GA 2020752361
AB    NOVELTY - Concrete product comprises green unit                comprises zero-slump to near-zero-slump concrete                mixture and has molded predetermined shape of                concrete masonry unit. The green unit sequesters                ambient carbon dioxide over period of time. The                concrete product sequesters carbon of 5 wt.% of the                cement over 28 day period. The concrete product is                formed from zero-slump to near-zero-slump concrete                mixture and configured to sequester greenhouse                gasses.
   USE - Concrete product.
   ADVANTAGE - The concrete product improves strength,                durability, and/or environmental impact.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The zero-slump to                  near-zero-slump concrete mixture includes                  comprises predetermined amount of water in amount                  of 100% water. The zero-slump to near-zero-slump                  concrete mixture comprises aggregates, pigment,                  water repellent mortar, cement, and predetermined                  amount of water in amount of 100% water. The                  zero-slump to near-zero-slump concrete mixture of                  the green unit maintains molded predetermined                  shape of the concrete masonry unit from being                  retained in mold for less than about 20 seconds.                  The zero-slump to near-zero-slump concrete                  mixture comprises both fine aggregates and coarse                  aggregates in ratio of fine aggregate to coarse                  aggregate in range between 100:1-1:100. The                  zero-slump to near-zero-slump concrete mixture                  comprises both fine aggregates and coarse                  aggregates in ratio of fine aggregate to coarse                  aggregate of 2:1. The zero-slump to                  near-zero-slump concrete mixture comprises                  aggregates in amount that is 85 wt.% of a total                  volume of mixture, pigment, water repellent                  mortar, cement, and water. The zero-slump to                  near-zero-slump concrete mixture comprises water                  to cementitious ratio (w/cm) of 0.9:1. The                  zero-slump to near-zero-slump concrete mixture                  has less than 0.2 inch of slump when tested                  according to ASTM International C143. The                  zero-slump to near-zero-slump concrete mixture of                  the green unit maintains molded predetermined                  shape of the concrete masonry unit from retained                  in mold for less than about 20 seconds.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC L02 (Refractories, ceramics, cement - includes manufacturing methods, limes, soil preparation for (road) building, magnesias and slags, cements, mortars, concretes, abrasives, thermal or acoustic insulation (non)oxide ceramics and ceramic composites, but not brick making, concrete mixers or casting or pottersâ€™ wheels (C04).); P64 (Working cement, clay, stone (B28).)

PN CN111154174-A; WO2021017579-A1; CN111154174-B
TI Preparing polypropylene foam material involves            blending polypropylene and polydimethylsiloxane to            obtain polypropylene/polydimethylsiloxane blend,            polypropylene/polydimethylsiloxane blend are foamed            under carbon dioxide conditions.
AU ZHAO L
   QIANG W
   HU D
   GAO X
   XIE H
AE UNIV EAST CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYEC-C)
   UNIV EAST CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYEC-C)
GA 202044925R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing polypropylene foam material involves                blending polypropylene and polydimethylsiloxane to                obtain polypropylene/polydimethylsiloxane blend.                The polypropylene/polydimethylsiloxane blend are                foamed under supercritical carbon dioxide                conditions. The number-weight molecular weight of                the polydimethylsiloxane is 2000-17000g/mol.
   USE - Method for preparing polypropylene foam                material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare polypropylene                foam material that is environmentally friendly, has                large nucleation density, small cell pore diameter,                large foaming ratio.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                polypropylene/polydimethylsiloxane blend, which                comprises 98.5-99.5 wt.% polypropylene and 0.5-1.5                wt.% polydimethylsiloxane, number-weight molecular                weight of the polydimethylsiloxane is 2000-17000                g/mol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The number-weight                  molecular weight of the polydimethylsiloxane is                  4000-17000 g/mol. The content of the                  polydimethylsiloxane is 0.5-1.5 wt.%. The                  polypropylene is ethylene-propylene copolymer of                  polyolefin and/or ethylene-propylene copolymer of                  polyolefin. The foaming under supercritical                  carbon dioxide is carried out. The                  polypropylene/polydimethylsiloxane blend is                  placed in autoclave Inside, charge carbon dioxide                  to replace the air three times. The autoclave is                  heated to the foaming temperature, after holding                  for half an hour, filled with supercritical                  carbon dioxide set pressure to saturate the                  polypropylene/polydimethylsiloxane blend.

PT P
PN US2020140744-A1
TI Performing well stimulation process involves pumping treatment fluid into well, where treatment fluid comprises carbon dioxide (CO2) and breaker, and treating one of reservoir and wellbore of well with treatment fluid.
AU GERSTNER M J
   LEE G J
AE CUDD PUMPING SERVICES INC (CUDD-Non-standard)
   CUDD PRESSURE CONTROL INC (CUDD-Non-standard)
GA 202037530L
AB    NOVELTY - Performing well stimulation process involves pumping a treatment fluid into a well, where treatment fluid comprises carbon dioxide (CO2) and a breaker, and treating one of a reservoir and a wellbore of the well with the treatment fluid.
   USE - Method for performing well stimulation process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform well stimulation process which is helps to increase production by reducing hydrocarbon viscosity and improving miscible, or partially miscible, displacement of hydrocarbons into the wellbore, and used as a first stimulation operation or any subsequent stimulation operation (re-stimulation) on a well.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The breaker is selected from the group consisting of guar breakers, friction reducer breakers, slickwater breakers, oxidative breakers, enzyme breakers, gel breakers, ammonium persulfate, polyacrylamide breakers, or combinations. The breaker is a solution. The solution comprises stabilized chlorine dioxide (ClO2). The treatment fluid comprises carbon dioxide (CO2), stabilized chlorine dioxide (ClO2), and a water block removing agent selected from the group consisting of stimulation surfactants, ethoxylated surfactants, sulfonated surfactants, citrus terpenes, alcohols, ionic surfactants, nonionic surfactants, cationic surfactants, anionic surfactants, amphoteric surfactants, flow-back enhancers, emulsifiers, dispersants, oil-wetters, water-wetters, foamers, defoamers, or combinations. The treatment fluid comprises one of clay control agents, surfactants, foaming agents, fluid loss additives, scale reducing agents, corrosion inhibitors, biocides, and pH control additives. The treatment fluid comprises a water block removing agent selected from the group consisting of stimulation surfactants, ethoxylated surfactants, sulfonated surfactants, citrus terpenes, alcohols, ionic surfactants, nonionic surfactants, cationic surfactants, anionic surfactants, amphoteric surfactants, flow-back enhancers, emulsifiers, dispersants, oil-wetters, water-wetters, foamers, defoamers, or combinations. Preferred Compositions: The water block removing agent is in a range of 20-75 wt.% total volume of the treatment fluid. The carbon di-oxide is in a range of 20-75 wt.% total volume of the treatment fluid. Preferred Conditions: The solution has a concentration of 5 vol.% stabilized chlorine dioxide. The activated chlorine dioxide is formed as a result of a lowering of pH in the treatment fluid by the carbonic acid. The lowering of pH results in the treatment fluid having a pH between 4-5. Preferred Process: The method further involves commingling carbon di-oxide with water, and forming as a result of the commingling, carbonic acid, activating the stabilized chlorine dioxide to form activated chlorine dioxide, and pumping a treated spacer into the well, shutting-in the well for a predetermined period of time, and flowing back the well after the predetermined period of time. The activated ClO2 is formed at a predetermined depth in the well based on a concentration of the CO2 and the stabilized ClO2 in the treatment fluid. The breaker is in a range of 20-75 wt.% total volume of the treatment fluid. The treated spacer comprises a diverter agent to divert the treatment fluid to a particular zone of interest. The treating includes one of mobilizing hydrocarbons in the reservoir, freeing hydrocarbons in the reservoir, lowering surface tension of residual fluids in the reservoir, and removing damage caused by residual material from previous fluids introduced into the well.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN110860289-A
TI Preparing metal monoatomic material used for            electrocatalytic oxygen reduction and carbon dioxide            reduction reaction, involves using protective agent and            additive, performing surface modification treatment,            carbonizing and post-treating.
AU LIU M
   LIN Y
   FU J
AE UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
GA 202021256G
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing metal monoatomic material                involves using protective agent, additive and                precursor coordinated with a metal atom, performing                series of surface modification treatments on the                carbon-containing compound, carbonizing in an inert                atmosphere and post-treating to remove the                protective agent to obtain the product.
   USE - The metal monoatomic material is useful for                electrocatalytic oxygen reduction reaction and                carbon dioxide reduction reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method prevents the destruction of the                catalytically active structure, has excellent                performance, universality and application                prospects, and is simple and effective.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inorganic acid                  comprises hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid and/or                  nitric acid. The precursor coordinated with a                  metal atom comprises porphyrin-based metal                  compound and/or phthalocyanine-based metal                  compound. The metal monoatomic material comprises                  iron monoatomic material, cobalt monoatomic                  material, nickel monoatomic material and copper                  monoatomic material.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The protective agent is a                  compound for forming stable structure at high                  temperature. The surface modification treatment                  involves mixing the precursor coordinated with                  metal atom and carbon-containing compound with                  solvent, stirring, evaporating the solvent, and                  adding protective agent and additive for                  grinding. The post-processing method involves                  acid-etching, washing, filtering, drying and                  grinding the carbonized material in order.                  Preferred Components: The additive comprises                  inorganic acid and/or organic acid. The                  carbon-containing compound comprises carbon                  simple substance and/or carbon-containing organic                  substance. The protective agent comprises                  tetraethyl silicate. The organic acid comprises                  formic acid and/or acetic acid. The carbon simple                  substance comprises activated carbon, nano-carbon                  spheres and/or carbon nanotubes. The                  carbon-containing organic substance comprises                  melamine, urea, sucrose and/or glucose. The                  solvent comprises tetrahydrofuran,                  N,N-dimethylformamide and/or dimethyl sulfoxide.                  The acid used in acid etching process comprises                  hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, nitric acid                  and/or hydrofluoric acid. The inert atmosphere                  comprises nitrogen and/or argon with a purity of                  99-99.99%. Preferred Conditions: The carbonizing                  process is carried out at 500-1100 degrees C in a                  heating rate of 1-20 degrees C/minute for 1-12                  hours. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of                  the precursor coordinated with a metal atom and                  carbon-containing compound is 0.5-5, and the                  volume ratio of the protective agent and additive                  is between 0.5-5.

   USE - The method is useful for improving the quality                of hot-dip zinc coating of malleable cast                iron.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: reduces the difference in coating                thickness and the average thickness of different                parts of the pretreated malleable cast iron                compared to untreated malleable cast iron; and has                broad application prospects in the galvanizing                industry.

PN CN108395923-A
TI Preparation method of modified edge functionalized            graphene lubricant additive, involves adding graphite            powder to reactor, sealing, introducing carbon dioxide            gas, starting reactor, grinding, and reacting with            oleylamine.
AU XIANG Z
   ZHAO S
   NIU M
   PENG P
   YANG F
AE UNIV BEIJING CHEM TECHNOLOGY (UYBC-C)
GA 2018652675
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation method of modified edge                functionalized graphene lubricant additive involves                adding graphite powder to a reactor, sealing the                reactor, introducing carbon dioxide gas into the                reactor, starting reactor, and grinding for a                period of time to obtain edge functionalized                graphene, and reacting the obtained edge                functionalized graphene with oleylamine.
   USE - Preparation method of modified edge                functionalized graphene lubricant additive.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of highly                dispersible modified edge functionalized graphene                lubricant additive with small structural defects,                and excellent stability, lubricating performance                and safety, by simple, economical and                environmentally-friendly process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The introduction of                  carbon dioxide gas into the reaction device is                  performed by charging the carbon dioxide gas into                  the reactor until the pressure reaches 2 atm,                  discharging the gas, repeating three times, and                  introducing carbon dioxide gas at 0.5-1.5 MPa.                  The edge functionalized graphene is reacted with                  oleylamine, washed and filtered to dry. Preferred                  Conditions: The grinding process is performed at                  speed of 100-5000 rpm for 3-72 hours. The                  obtained edge functionalized graphene is reacted                  with oleylamine at 80-135 degrees C for 8-48                  hours. Preferred Components: The graphite powder                  is natural crystalline flake graphite or earthy                  graphite. Preferred Composition: The graphite                  powder is filled in an amount of 1/3 of the total                  volume of the reactor. The ratio of the material                  of the edge functionalized graphene to oleylamine                  is 1:2-1:5. The obtained additive can be added to                  the lubricating oil in an amount of 0.01-5 wt.%                  of base oil.

TI Catalyst composition useful in carbon monoxide generating agent for generating carbon monoxide and hydrogen, comprises hierarchical hexagonal type zinc particles.
AU CHUNG J H
   KIM H J
   KOH J K
   LEE H S
   SHIN H Y
   WON D H
   WOO S I
AE KOREA ADVANCED INST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (KOAD-C)
   KOREA ADVANCED SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (KOAD-C)
GA 201821546D
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst composition comprises hierarchical hexagonal zinc particles.
   USE - The composition is useful in carbon monoxide generating agent for generating carbon monoxide and hydrogen (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition has high catalytic activity for carbon dioxide reduction, high selectivity for carbon monoxide, large surface area and long-term stability for greater than or equal to 30 hours.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) producing catalyst composition, comprising (a) preparing solution containing zinc precursor, and (b) electrodepositing the hierarchical hexagonal zinc particles on a substrate by applying potential on the obtained solution; and
   (2) producing carbon monoxide, comprising reacting the catalyst composition with carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The zinc particles have size of 400 nm to 1.5 mu m and specific capacitance of 16-20 mfcm2. The hexagonal type zinc particles generate carbon monoxide and hydrogen in a ratio of 1:0.1-1:10 adjusting the applied potential to -0.1 V to -1.1 V or -1 to -4 V. The zinc particles have front or black surface. The zinc precursor includes zinc chloride, zinc cyanide, zinc acetate, zinc bromide, zinc carbonate, zinc alkoxide and/or zinc sulfide. The electrolyte solution comprises potassium chloride, sodium chloride, potassium bicarbonate, sodium bicarbonate or sodium iodine carbonate. Preferred Method: The method uses the catalyst as an electrode to apply potential on carbon dioxide-containing electrolyte solution. Carbon dioxide is converted into carbon monoxide at a potential of -0.8 to -1.2 V with Faraday efficiency of 80-95%. The production rate of carbon monoxide is kept constant for 25-35 hours.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN109813129-A; CN109813129-B
TI Producing exhaust gas recycling-based oxygen-carbon dioxide combustion involves using chain grate-rotary kiln-circulation cooling machine as main equipment of pellet production, using nitrogen with low content of industrial coal gas.
AU ZHU R
   WU W
   WANG Y
   CHEN P
   LIU F
   HAN B
   FENG C
   JIANG J
AE UNIV BEIJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNBS-C)
   SHANDONG TAISHAN IRON & STEEL CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201948737Q
AB    NOVELTY - Producing exhaust gas recycling-based oxygen-carbon dioxide combustion involves using chain grate-rotary kiln-circulation cooling machine as main equipment of pellet production, using nitrogen with low content of industrial coal gas or coal powder as fuel for rotary kiln. The high concentration carbon dioxide gas combustion improver is obtained by purifying the industrial pure oxygen and ring cold machine annular cooler I sectionand a ring cooling section, exhaust ga or grate of drying section exhaust gas according to a certain proportion.
   USE - Method for producing exhaust gas recycling-based oxygen-carbon dioxide combustion.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce exhaust gas recycling-based oxygen-carbon dioxide combustion that improves the heat utilization efficiency, realizes near-zero pellet preparation system, and reduces generation of nitrogen oxide production and cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing exhaust gas recycling-based oxygen-carbon dioxide combustion involves using chain grate-rotary kiln-circulation cooling machine as main equipment of pellet production, using nitrogen with low content of industrial coal gas or coal powder as fuel for rotary kiln. The high concentration carbon dioxide gas combustion improver is obtained by purifying the industrial pure oxygen and ring cold machine annular cooler I sectionand a ring cooling section, exhaust ga or grate of drying section exhaust gas according to a certain proportion. The rotary kiln is produced by fuel combustion of high temperature rotary kiln exhaust gas pellet preheating process of preheating section of chain grate, grate preheating section heat exchange to generate low temperature exhaust gas, dehydrated and dust to ring cold machine annular cooler I section and cooling section, heat exchange again with finished product heat pellet ore to realize forced cooling of pellets and absorbing heat, after finished pellet ore I section and of ring cold machine annular cooling ring cooling section exhaust gas recycled to grate (b) drying section. The exhaust gas drying grate section is used for drying pellet in grate containing high-concentration carbon dioxide through the dust-removing de-watering desulfurization and denitration of low load, then introduced into carbon dioxide coarse purification device and used for preparing high-concentration carbon dioxide gas (d), high-concentration carbon dioxide gas (d) conveying to carbon dioxide the cryogenic purification device for producing industrial grade and food grade carbon dioxide gas (e).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The rotary kiln exhaust gas is introduced to produce by the rotary kiln to the preheating section of the grate machine for preheating the pellets in the grate machine. The rotary kiln exhaust gas after heat exchange is introduced into the exhaust gas internal circulation section dust removal and water removal device by the booster fan, and then introduced into the ring cooling machine ring cooling section I and ring cold section II for forced cooling of the finished pellets. The gas component detecting device I is set in the ring cooling section I and the ring cooling section II outlet pipe P10 of the ring cooler, and analyze the ring cooling section I and the ring cooling of the ring cooler. The carbon dioxide concentration in the second stage exhaust gas. The concentration of nitrogen in the ring cooling zone I and the ring cooling phase II exhaust gas (b) is less than 5%, the reversing valve II (11) acts, and a portion of the ring cooler is cooled by the section I and the ring section II. The exhaust gas (b) is introduced into the oxidant proportioning system (4) and mixed with industrial pure oxygen in a certain ratio as a combustion improver for rotary kiln fuel combustion. The remaining ring cooler ring cooling section I and ring cooling section II exhaust gas (b) are pressurized by the booster fan and then introduced into the grate dryer drying section for drying the pellets. The concentration of nitrogen in the ring cooling zone I and the ring cooling phase II exhaust gas (b) is higher than 5%. The section exhaust gas (b) is pressurized by the booster fan and introduced into the grate dryer drying section for drying the pellets. The reversing valve III operates to introduce a high concentration carbon dioxide gas (d) in the high concentration carbon dioxide gas storage cabinet to the oxidant ratio system, and maintains the state for 3 minutes according to the gas component detecting device I measured the carbon dioxide concentration in the ring cooling zone I and the ring cooling phase II exhaust gas (b). The drying section of the grate machine exhaust gas (c) is pressurized by the booster fan and then introduced into the exhaust gas desulfurization and denitration device of the external circulation section of the exhaust gas, and gas is set at the outlet of the equipment. The carbon dioxide gas concentration in the exhaust gas (c) of the grate dryer drying section is less than 75%, and directly discharged through the reversing valve. The carbon dioxide gas concentration in the exhaust gas (c) of the grate dryer drying section is higher than 75%, introduced into the carbon dioxide crude purification device to obtain high concentration of carbon dioxide gas concentration greater than 98%. The rest is sent to the carbon dioxide cryogenic liquefaction purification unit for the production of industrial grade/food grade carbon dioxide gas. The rotary kiln hearth temperature is higher than 1250 degrees C. The industrial pure oxygen in oxygen concentration is less than or equal to 99.5 vol.%.

PN WO2022210405-A1
TI Adhesive used for forming sheet, comprises            aliphatic polycarbonate resin having ether structure in            its main chain.
AU KEIKO A
   SAE H
   TAKASHI M
AE LINTEC CORP (LTEC-C)
GA 2022C7566R
AB    NOVELTY - An adhesive comprises an aliphatic                polycarbonate resin having an ether structure in                its main chain.
   USE - Adhesive used for forming sheet (claimed) for                labeling and bonding components of articles such as                containers, daily necessities, electric/electronic                equipment, machines, medical instruments, etc. Can                also be used as raw material for producing carbon                dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The adhesive has excellent biodegradable                property, and uses carbon dioxide as production raw                material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                adhesive sheet (1A), which comprises a layer (11)                made of the adhesive.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional view of the                adhesive sheet.
   1AAdhesive sheet
   11Adhesive layer
   12Release sheet
   13Substrate
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The aliphatic                  polycarbonate resin contains 0.1-99 %mass unit of                  the ether structure. Preferred Components: The                  aliphatic polycarbonate resin is of formula                  (I).R=H or hydrocarbyl;n, m=1 or more;andr=2 or more.Preferred Properties: The aliphatic                  polycarbonate resin has a number average                  molecular weight 10000-2000000, and glass                  transition temperature of -60&#176; C to                  30&#176; C.                                                                                                                              
   EXAMPLE - 1.33 g Potassium hexacyanocobaltate(III)                (K3[Co(CN)6]) was dissolved in 20 mL deionized                water, stirred at 50&#176; C, dissolved 211.42                g zinc chloride solution in a mixed solution 60 mL                deionized water and 30 mL t-butyl alcohol added                dropwise over 45 minutes. The mixture was stirred                for 60 minutes, centrifuged, resuspended the solid                in a solution of t-butyl alcohol and deionized                water with stirring for 30 minutes, and dried under                vacuum at 50&#176; C to obtain                zinc-cobalt-cyanide catalyst (Zn3(Co[CN]6)2) as a                double metal cyanide complex catalyst. To 1 mol                catalyst, 1.3x 104mol propylene oxide, which was an                epoxy monomer, was added to the pressure vessel and                stirred under an argon atmosphere. The carbon                dioxide was purged inside the pressure vessel by a                liquid-sending pump, and adjusted the pressure                inside the pressure vessel to 4 MPa. The mixture                was stirred at 60&#176; C for 18 hours to carry                out a polymerization reaction. After the reaction                was completed, chloroform was added to the contents                of the pressure vessel to form a solution, and                added the solution to methanol in drops, in which 1                M hydrochloric acid was added to perform                reprecipitation purification. The precipitate was                collected with filter paper, dissolved in                chloroform again, and removed the impurities were                removed with a membrane filter The solution was                poured into a Teflon(RTM: PTFE) petri dish and                dried in a drying oven at 50&#176; C for 20                hours to obtain an aliphatic polycarbonate resin as                polymer. The polymer had glass transition                temperature of10.8&#176; C, number average                molecular weight of 62000, degradability at 5 hours                of 81 and at 100 hours of 87. The polymer was                dissolved in ethyl acetate, and one side of a                Lumirror PET50T-60(RTM: polyethylene terephthalate                film) as a base material applied with an applicator                and dried at 100&#176; C for 1 minute to obtain                an adhesive sheet having a adhesive layer with a                thickness of 25 &#956; m.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises using                  freeze-dried natto powder as raw material and                  supercritical carbon dioxide as the extraction                  solvent, extracting raw materials by                  supercritical carbon dioxide extraction machine                  and separating to obtain natto powder and natto                  oil, repeatedly squeezing the obtained natto                  powder for 10-20 times with bearing-type wall                  breaking machine, extruding the lyophilized                  powder into flakes, crushing into small flake                  particles to obtain deodorized and easily soluble                  natto freeze-dried powder. PT P
PN CN108030712-A; CN108030712-B
TI Essential oil cosmetic comprises emulsifier, humectant, tonic and thickening agent and lavender essential oil, where lavender essential oil is prepared by taking lavender, digesting by lysozyme and extarcting liquid carbon dioxide.
AU WU M
AE YANTAI YANSHENG INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY (YANT-Non-standard)
   GUANGZHOU XIANGPULAN E-COMMERCE CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201839519H
AB    NOVELTY - Essential oil cosmetic comprises emulsifier, humectant, tonic and thickening agent and lavender essential oil, where lavender essential oil is prepared by taking lavender, digesting by lysozyme. After the enzyme is inactivated, the enzyme is inactivated and liquid carbon dioxide is extracted, separated to obtain lavender essential oil crude oil. The fresh lavender is freshly picked lavender and stored at (-20) to (-5) degrees C.
   USE - Essential oil cosmetic.
   ADVANTAGE - The essential oil cosmetic removes freckle and wrinkle, whitens skin, protects from sun and promotes impaired tissue regeneration function recovery.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Componnts: The emulsifier is polyglyceryl fatty acid ester, lauric acid monoglyceride, propylene glycol fatty acid ester, sorbitan monopalmitate, sodium stearoyl lactylate, diacetyl monoglyceride, nonyl phenol polyoxyethylene ether, polyoxyethylene oleate, polyoxyethylene stearate, sorbitan fatty acid esters, dimerized glycerol, diisooctylsuccinic acid, sodium sulfonate and fatty acid polyoxyethylene ester; the humectants are glycerol, fructose, propylene glycol, sorbitol, polyglutamic acid, nicotinamide, polyethylene glycol, and xylose. The tonic agent is honey, sodium salicylate, superoxide dismutase, vitamin A, beta glucan, vitamin E, lysine sodium, and vitamin E. The thickener is sodium chloride, sodium carboxymethylcellulose, gelatin, sodium alginate and casein. Preferred Condition: The emulsifier, moisturizer and tonic agent, thickener and lavender essential oil are added at 5 to 25 degrees C and (-0.5) to (-0.08) megaPascal, defoamed for 10-20 minutes. Preferred Composition: The essential oil cosmetic comprises 10 to 30 pts. wt. emulsifier, 5 to 20 pts. wt. moisturizer, 1 to 10 pts. wt. nourishing agent, 0 to 2 pts. wt. thickening agent, and 1 to 5 pts. wt. lavender essential oil. The lavender essential oil is a composition having lavender oil and a retaining agent in mass ratio of 50 to 99.5:0.5 to 50. Preferred Process: The lysozyme enzymatic hydrolysis further comprises water, and mass ratio of fresh lavender, lysozyme and water is 10-50:0.02-1:50-90. The enzymatic temperature is 27-55 degrees C, enzymolysis time 30-60 minutes, inactivation enzyme temperature is 80-120 degrees C, enzyme inactivation time is 5-20 minutes, inactivation enzyme pressure is 0-5 megaPascal. The carbon dioxide is liquified at 15-31.26 degrees C and 5-10 megaPascal, and has flow rate of 25-35 L/hour, and extraction time is 0.5 to 2 hours. The separation includes aprimary separation vessel separation and secondary separation vessel separation, where primary separation vessel has separation pressure of 6-8 megaPascal and separation temperature of 30-50 degrees C. The separation time of the secondary separation vessel is 0.5-3 hour, separation pressure is 4-6 megaPascal and separation temperature is 25-40 degrees C. The bottom of the separation vessel is left with the residue as a mixture; the mixture is transferred to an oil-water separator. The flocculant is added to the mixture, and allowed to stand for 20-150 minutes, the mass ratio of the mixture and the flocculant is 99-99.99:0.01-1, oil-water separation is carried out at 30-50 degrees C to obtain lavender wax and water treatment product. The water treatment product is filtered to obtain water and residue. The flocculant is activated clay, polyaluminum chloride, sodium polyacrylate and polyacrylamide. The retentive agent is added to the lavender essential oil crude oil, where mass ratio of the lavender essential oil crude oil and retaining agent is 50 to 999.5:0.5 to 50. The retaining agent is ethylene glycol, glycerin, and isopropyl alcohol. The kneading is performed by agitation or homogenization.
   ACTIVITY - Dermatological.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); D21 (Preparations for dental or toilet purposes - including filling alloys, compositions for dentures or dental impressions, anti-caries chewing gum, plaque disclosing compositions, toothpastes, cosmetics, shampoos, topical anti-sunburn compositions and toilet soaps (A61K).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.)
MC B04-A08G2; B04-A10; B04-C02A2; B04-C02D; B04-C03B; B04-C03C; B04-D01A; B04-L05; B04-N02; B05-A01B; B07-A02A; B07-D04C; B10-A07A; B10-C03; B10-C04E5; B10-E04A; B10-E04C; B14-R01; B15-A00; B15-E00; D05-C19; D08-B09A1; D08-B09A3; D08-B10; A12-V04C

AU ZHANG J
   YAN B
AE UNIV TSINGHUA (UYQI-C)
GA 2021D7736E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing copper-based spinel-type catalyst comprises reducing the catalyst precursor to obtain the finished product, where the catalyst precursor is copper-aluminum spinel loaded with metal oxide.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for preparing methanol by carbon dioxide hydrogenation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: realizes precise control of copper(I) ion ratio in the catalyst; enhances the interaction between catalyst metal supports to realize higher methanol yield; reduces the emission of carbon dioxide; realizes the resource utilization of carbon dioxide; and has high environmental and economic benefits.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) a copper-based spinel-type catalyst for preparing methanol by hydrogenation of carbon dioxide prepared by the above-mentioned method; and
   (2) a method for preparing methanol by hydrogenation of carbon dioxide, comprising reacting catalyst with carbon dioxide and hydrogen to obtain methanol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The reduction temperature is 300-700degrees Celsius, and the time is 2-4 hours. The catalyst precursor is prepared by comprises (i) mixing copper-aluminum spinel, water-soluble metal salt and water for impregnation, and then drying to obtain copper-aluminum spinel after impregnation, and (ii) calcining the impregnated copper-aluminum spinel to obtain a catalyst precursor. In the step (i), impregnating at 60-100degrees Celsius. In the step (ii), calcining in an air atmosphere at 300-500degrees Celsius for 3-6 hours. The copper-aluminum spinel is prepared by (a) mixing the mixed solution of copper salt and aluminum salt with the precipitant solution to carry out a precipitation reaction to obtain a slurry, (b) carrying out a hydrothermal reaction to the slurry so as to obtain a hydrothermal back slurry, (c) carrying out the solid-liquid separation of the slurry after the hydrothermal treatment, so as to obtain a precipitate and a waste liquid, and (d) drying and calcining the precipitate to obtain the finished product. In the step (b), hydrothermally reacting at 160-200degrees Celsius for 18-24 hours, optionally calcining at 900-1200degrees Celsius for 5-6 hours.Preferred Components: The metal oxide comprises zinc oxide, zirconium dioxide and/or cerium(IV) oxide. In the step (i), the mass ratio of the copper-aluminum spinel, water-soluble metal salt and the water is 1:0.05-0.3:20-100, optionally, the water-soluble metal salt comprises zinc nitrate hexahydrate, zirconium nitrate pentahydrate and/or cerium nitrate hexahydrate. In the step (a), in the mixed solution of copper salt and aluminum salt, the molar ratio of copper and aluminum is 1:2, optionally, the molar ratio of the total metal ions in the copper salt and the aluminum salt to the precipitating agent in the precipitating agent solution is 3:8, optionally, the total metal ion concentration of the mixed solution of the copper salt and the aluminum salt is 0.02-2 mol/l, the concentration of the precipitant solution is 0.02-2 mol/l, and optionally, the precipitant solution comprises sodium hydroxide solution, sodium carbonate solution, oxalic acid and/or aqueous ammonia.

The concentration of the metal salt in the mixed solution in step (i) is 2-10 %mass. The concentration of the small molecular carbon and nitrogen compounds in the mixed solution in step (i) is 20-30 %mass. The concentration of amino acids in the mixed solution in step (i) is 20-30 %mass. Preferred Conditions: In step (ii), the calcination is carried out at 600-1000 degrees C for 2-4 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The small molecular carbon and nitrogen compound comprises melamine, pyrrole, pyridine and/or dicyandiamide.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The additional recovery                  unit of carbon dioxide is placed directly after                  at least one carbonization column, and residual                  gas of temperature of 40-55 degrees C and under                  pressure of 1-2 bar is introduced into the                  additional recovery unit of carbon dioxide. The                  additional recovery unit of carbon dioxide is                  located in the installation of baking soda, and                  into the additional recovery unit of carbon                  dioxide residual gas containing 15-30 vol.% of                  carbon dioxide with temperature of 60-90 degrees                  C and under pressure of 1-2 bar is introduced.                  The additional recovery unit of carbon dioxide is                  placed after at least one carbonization column                  between at least one ammonia absorber (202) and                  zone of rinsing exhaust gases (201), and that                  into the additional recovery unit of carbon                  dioxide residual gas of temperature of 20-50                  degrees C and under pressure of 1-2 bar is                  introduced. The additional recovery unit of                  carbon dioxide is placed after at least one                  carbonization column, after at least one ammonia                  absorber and directly after zone of rinsing                  exhaust gases, and that into the additional                  recovery unit of carbon dioxide residual gas with                  temperature of 20-30 degrees C and under pressure                  of 1-2 bar is introduced. The additional recovery                  unit of carbon dioxide the process of extraction                  of carbon dioxide is conducted by absorption,                  where the absorption is a physical absorption or                  a chemical absorption. The absorption is a                  chemical absorption conducted under pressure of                  0.1-6 bar, and as the absorption liquid a                  solution containing ammonia, preferably a                  solution with a concentration of 1-20% is used.                  The residual gas introduced into the additional                  recovery unit of carbon dioxide contacts in                  counter-flow the stream of absorption liquid, and                  that inside the additional recovery unit of                  carbon dioxide low temperature is maintained at                  10-50 degrees C. The solution of absorption                  liquid saturated with carbon dioxide is directed                  into the desorption column, heated up, upon which                  the absorption liquid is regenerated. The                  absorption liquid is regenerated by introducing a                  stream of absorption liquid into the circulation                  of the ammonia solution in the soda process. The                  absorption liquid is 1-50% aqueous solutions                  containing amines, aqueous solutions containing                  compounds of amino acids or products of a                  reaction with amino acids, e.g. potassium                  glycinate, aqueous or non-aqueous multi-component                  solutions and aqueous or non-aqueous solutions                  containing potassium carbonate salts, e.g.                  potassium carbonate at 20-40 (preferably 30)% and                  2-10%, amines with a high constant reaction rate                  greater than or equal to 4500 dm3/molex s in 25                  degrees C, with carbon dioxide, possibly in                  combination with other inorganic salts activating                  the reaction of carbon dioxide into the                  carbamate, where particularly preferable are                  aqueous solutions containing amines, preferably                  aqueous solutions containing amines at 1-50%. The                  absorption liquid is regenerated by introducing a                  stream of absorption liquid into the desorption                  column, where, possibly prior to directing into                  the desorption column, the solution of absorption                  liquid saturated with carbon dioxide is heated                  up. The absorption liquid regenerates in the                  desorption column operating under pressure of                  0.2-6 bar, in a temperature of 70-250 degrees C,                  and the temperature in the process of                  regeneration is maintained using a heat carrier                  under pressure up to 18 bar. The stream of                  extracted carbon dioxide leaving the desorption                  column is cooled down to obtain a stream of waste                  carbon dioxide of high purity using a cooling                  agent. The cooling step is conducted up to 10-40                  degrees C in a heat exchanger. The cooling agent                  process streams originates from absorption                  coolers (221), powered by water vapor under a                  pressure up to 18 (preferably 2-4) bar, and/or                  liquid and/or gaseous reaction streams from the                  soda production process and/or liquid and/or                  gaseous reaction streams and/or water are used.                  The additional recovery unit of carbon dioxide                  contains poured or structural fillings for                  providing sufficiently developed contact surface                  for the liquid phase of the absorbent with the                  gaseous phase of the process gas.

AB    NOVELTY - Separating and culturing Leydig cells of tree                shrew comprises (i) washing testicular parenchyma,                chopping into pieces and transferring into a                centrifuge tube; introducing 1 mg/ml IV                collagenase, introducing it into a carbon dioxide                incubator, centrifuging, introducing trypsin to                digest, terminating digestion using                serum-containing medium, introducing cell                suspension into a cell mesh and filtering to                disperse it into single cells, collecting it into a                centrifuge tube, centrifuging, discarding                supernatant, introducing medium/F12 medium to                resuspendcells, and repeating the centrifugation                once, (ii) purifying by adopting percoll density                gradient centrifugation method, centrifuging                suspension, introducing culture medium containing                fetal bovine serum to the pellet, and obtaining                cell suspension by shaking well, inoculating in a                culture flask or a culture plate and culturing in a                carbon dioxide incubator, replacing for culture                medium purifying cells at passages 2 and 3 by this                procedure.
   USE - The substance is useful in the preparation of                a medicine for treating male testicular damage and                infertility caused by zika virus infection                (claimed) .
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Separating and culturing Leydig cells of tree                shrew comprises (i) washing testicular parenchyma,                chopping into pieces and transferring into a                centrifuge tube; introducing 1 mg/ml IV                collagenase, introducing it into a carbon dioxide                incubator for 30-35 minutes, centrifuging,                introducing 0.25% trypsin to digest for 10-15                minutes, terminating digestion using                serum-containing medium, mix evenly by pipetting,                introducing cell suspension into a cell mesh and                filtering to disperse it into single cells,                collecting it into a centrifuge tube, centrifuging,                discarding supernatant, introducing gibco                dulbecco's modified eagle medium/F12 medium to                resuspend the cells, and repeating the                centrifugation once, (ii) purifying by adopting                percoll density gradient centrifugation method:                introducing the cell suspension obtained by                separation to Percoll separation solutions of                different density gradients, and the density from                the bottom of the separation tube upwards is 60%,                37%, 26%, 21%, 4&#8451;, centrifuging for 3000                revolutions/minutes for 30 minutes; collecting                cells between 37% and 60%, diluting with gibco                dulbecco's modified eagle medium/F12 medium,                centrifuging suspension at 1500 revolutions/minutes                for 10 minutes, introducing culture medium                containing fetal bovine serum to the pellet, and                obtaining cell suspension by shaking well, counting                purified cell suspension, and diluting into a cell                suspension with a concentration of 1&#215;10 6                cells/ml, inoculating in a culture flask or a                culture plate and culturing in a carbon dioxide                incubator, where medium formula is gibco dulbecco's                modified eagle medium/F12 medium plus 10% fetal                bovine serum, 1% penicillin-streptomycin, where                cells also contain a small amount of sertoli cells                and other miscellaneous cells, depending on the                adherence time of stromal cells and sertoli cells,                replacing for culture medium 6 hours after passage                to remove foreign cells for purification purposes,                purifying cells at passages 2 and 3 by this                procedure.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                medicine for treating male testis injury and                infertility caused by Zikv infection comprising                substance prepared by method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: Before the separation, the                  following operations are performed: taking 3                  months of male tree shrew, intraperitoneal                  injection 0.4 ml of 3 % pentobarbital sodium                  anesthesia is dead, sterile taking testis, 75 %                  ethanol to soaking for 20 seconds, taking out and                  washing with phosphate buffer saline for two                  times to remove ethanol, moving into the                  Heraguard ECO super clean bench, removing the                  white film of the testicle, epididymis and blood                  vessel, carefully taking out the testicular                  parenchyma tissue, placing in a sterile culture                  dish filled with pre-cooling D-Hanks liquid,                  washing for 2 times, and removing blood stains.                  On the basis of percoll density gradient                  centrifugation purification, depending on                  adherence speed of sertoli cells and leydig cells                  by means of differential adherence, change medium                  for 6-12 hours after the primary cells adhere to                  the wall. This method can obtain leydig cells                  with a purity of up to 98% until the third                  generation, and can rapidly proliferate with                  basic testosterone secretion ability. In the step                  (i), the 1 mg/mLIV collagenase is introduced into                  37&#8451;, 5 % carbon dioxide incubator for                  30-35 minutes, centrifuging for 5 minutes at 1000                  revolutions/minutes, introducing 0.25 %                  pancreatin to digest for 10-15 minutes, using                  serumcontaining culture medium to stop digestion,                  straw blowing and mixing uniformly, introducing                  the cell suspension into 40-mesh cell screen to                  filter, dispersing it into a single cell;                  collecting to 15 ml centrifugal tube,                  centrifuging for 1000 revolutions/minutes for 5                  minutes, and removing the supernatant.Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the                  upper density from the bottom of the separating                  pipe is 60 sequentially, 37 %, 26 %, 21 %,                  4&#8451;, 3000 revolutions/minutes for 30                  minutes, collecting the cell between 37 % and 60                  %, then using gibco dulbecco's modified eagle                  medium/F12 culture liquid to dilute, centrifuging                  the suspension at 1500 revolutions/minutes for 10                  minutes, precipitating and introducing culture                  solution containing fetal calf serum, shaking to                  obtain cell suspension, counting the separated                  and purified cell suspension, diluting into cell                  suspension with concentration of 1 * 106 /ml,                  inoculating in culture bottle or culture plate at                  37, culturing in 5 % carbon dioxide culture box,                  the culture medium formulation DMEM/F12 culture                  medium, introducing 10 % FBS, and 1 %                  penicillinstreptomycin.

PN CN113215098-A
TI Isolating and culturing primary mouse spleen net-shaped fibroblasts in vitro by low-cost method comprises e.g. using collagenase P to digest mouse spleen fragment, adding nutrient solution and carbon dioxide, and removing non-adherent cells.
AU LI J
   LIU Y
   WEI Y
   HU L
   WU T
AE UNIV ANHUI MEDICAL FIRST AFFILIATED HOSP (AFHP-C)
GA 202195926E
AB    NOVELTY - Isolating and culturing primary mouse spleen net-shaped fibroblasts in vitro by low-cost method comprises (1) using collagenase P to digest mouse spleen fragments in one step and digesting to obtain first digestion product, (2) blowing first digestion product with suction tip, absorbing digested product from supernatant, adding pre-cooled nutrient solution, removing supernatant to obtain cell pellet, suspending cell pellet in nutrient solution, removing culture medium, washing adherent cells twice with Gibco Dulbeccos modified eagle medium, adding nutrient solution and carbon dioxide, and changing nutrient solution during culture, (3) washing twice with phosphate-buffered saline without calcium and magnesium ions, adding trypsin to digest to obtain second digestion product, transferring second digested product into second culture flask, removing medium and non-adherent cells, and (4) repeating operation for more than 4 times to gradually purify mouse spleen net-shaped fibroblast cell.
   USE - The method is useful for isolating and culturing primary mouse spleen net-shaped fibroblasts in vitro by low-cost method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple and convenient operation, and low device requirements; is economical; and does not require magnetic bead sorting and flow cytometer sorting platforms.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Isolating and culturing primary mouse spleen net-shaped fibroblasts in vitro by low-cost method comprises (1) using collagenase P to digest mouse spleen fragments in one step and digesting for 30-50 minutes to obtain first digestion product, (2) blowing the first digestion product with 5 ml suction tip for 1 minute and allowing to stand for 1 minute, absorbing the digested product from the supernatant, adding 4 degrees C pre-cooled nutrient solution in a ratio of 1:1, setting the centrifugal temperature to 4 degrees C, where the centrifugal speed to 300 g, removing the supernatant to obtain the cell pellet after centrifugation for 5 minutes, suspending the cell pellet in nutrient solution, inoculating into 25 cm2 first culture flask at 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide adhere to the wall for 12 hours, removing the culture medium, washing the adherent cells twice with Gibco Dulbeccos modified eagle medium (DMEM), adding 6 ml of nutrient solution at 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide, culturing, changing the nutrient solution every 3-7 days during the culture, and washing the paste with DMEM before changing the liquid every time, (3) washing twice with phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) without calcium and magnesium ions after the first culture flask in step S2 is overgrown with cells, adding 0.05% trypsin to digest for 30-120 seconds to obtain second digestion product, transferring the second digested product into 25 cm2 second culture flask, incubating at 37 degrees C with 5% carbon dioxide for 3-9 hours, removing the medium and non-adherent cells, and adding 6 ml nutrient solution to the second culture flask at 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide culture, and (4) repeating the operation in the step (3) for more than 4 times to gradually purify the mouse spleen net-shaped fibroblast cell until the purity of the cells identified by flow cytometry reaches more than 98%after the second culture flask in step S3 is full of cells.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: In the step (1), the specific operation comprises (1) selecting 4-week-old mice, letting die by cervical dislocation, soaking and sterilizing in 75% ethanol for 10 minutes, and taking the spleen from the back, (2) washing the spleen twice with sterile PBS at 4 degrees C, using sterile ophthalmic scissors to cut the spleen into small pieces of 1 mm3 in sterilized penicillin bottle, washing twice with sterile PBS at 4 degrees C, adding 5-10 ml of 0.2 mg/ml collagenase P digestion solution, digesting at 37 degrees C for 30-50 minutes, mixing the digestive juice and spleen fragments every ten minutes during this period, and obtaining the first digestion product after the digestion is completed.
