PN CN112592258-A
TI Process for improving methanol yield comprises combined steam methane reforming and dry methane reforming; using double carbon dioxide to feed at different positions, one feed mixes carbon dioxide with methane-rich gas and steam.
AU ZHANG Y
   ZHANG G
   LI G
   WANG Y
   ZHAO Y
   HE Y
   WU W
   LIANG Z
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 2021362518
AB    NOVELTY - Process for improving methanol yield comprises combined steam methane reforming and dry methane reforming; using double carbon dioxide to feed at different positions, where one feed mixes carbon dioxide with methane-rich gas and steam and supplies it to wet reforming SMR reactor, the other feed provides carbon dioxide to the recycle gas in methanol synthesis loop; feeding water and methane in molar ratio of 2.5-3, and significantly reducing coking when performing reforming and steam reforming at same time.
   USE - The method is useful for process for improving methanol yield.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can reduces the accumulation of water vapor and improves the conversion rate of methane and carbon dioxide and the yield of methanol.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for device for improving methanol yield, comprising gas mixer, wet reforming SMR reactor, dry reforming DMR reactor, gas separation device, gas heating device, gas cooling device, and rectification tower; the upper part of the gas mixer is provided with a gas raw material inlet, and the lower part of the gas mixer is connected with the upper part of the first heat exchanger; the lower part of the first heat exchanger is connected to the upper part of the wet reforming SMR reactor; the lower part of the wet reforming SMR reactor is connected to the other end of the first heat exchanger; then the outlet of the first heat exchanger is connected to the inlet of the second heat exchanger, the outlet of the second heat exchanger is connected to the inlet of the first cooler, and the outlet of the first cooler is connected to the inlet of the first separator, the outlet of the first separator is connected to the other inlet of the second heat exchanger, the outlet of the second heat exchanger is connected to the inlet of the third heat exchanger, and the outlet of the third heat exchanger is connected to the inlet of the dry reforming DMR reactor, the outlet of the dry reforming DMR reactor is connected to the other end of the third heat exchanger, the outlet of the third heat exchanger is connected with the inlet of the second cooler, and the outlet of the second cooler is connected with the inlet of the third cooler, the outlet of the third cooler is connected with the inlet of the second separator, and the inlet of the second separator is connected with the inlet of the second gas mixer, the outlet of the second gas mixer is connected to the inlet of the fourth heat exchanger, and the outlet of the fourth heat exchanger is connected to the methanol reactor, the outlet of the methanol reactor is connected to the other inlet of the fourth heat exchanger, and the outlet of the fourth heat exchanger is connected to the inlet of the fourth cooler, the outlet of the fourth cooler is connected to the inlet of the third separator, and the outlet of the third separator is connected to the inlet of the fourth separator, the inlet of the fourth separator is connected with the inlet of the first rectifying tower, and the outlet of the first rectifying tower is connected with the inlet of the second rectifying tower.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The process for improving methanol yield comprises (1) firstly capturing 99.6% carbon dioxide from flue gas of power plant, compressing the carbon dioxide stream to 15-20 bar and 75-80 bar respectively, mixing the carbon dioxide with steam and methane-rich gas to form a mixed gas; (2) preheating the mixed gas in the first heat exchanger, after preheating to 500-600 degrees C, entering the wet reforming SMR reactor for the reforming reaction, after the reaction occurs in the wet reforming SMR reactor, passing high-temperature gas generated by reaction into the first heat exchanger and the second heat exchanger in order to cool down, cooling to 500-510 degrees C, cooling it by first cooler, cooling to 40-50 degrees C to become a gas-liquid mixture, cooling gas-liquid mixture and separating by the first separator; (3) passing gas separated by the first separator first into the second heat exchanger for preheating, entering the third heat exchanger for heating, after heating, and passing mixed gas into dry reforming DMR reactor for secondary reforming; (4) cooling the synthesis gas generated after the secondary reforming DMR reaction by the third heat exchanger, cooling to 540-550 degrees C, passing the synthesis gas cooled by the third heat exchanger into the second cooler and the third cooler for cooling, cooling to 40-50 degrees C, and passing the cooled gas-liquid mixture into the second separator for gas-liquid separation; (5) mixing the synthesis gas separated by the second separator with the newly supplemented carbon dioxide stream in the second gas mixer, passing gas mixed with syngas and carbon dioxide into the fourth heat exchanger for preheating, passing the preheated gas into the methanol reactor for methanol synthesis reaction, the reaction state is set to 75-80 bar, 240-250 degrees C, where the reactor tube is equipped with copper/zinc oxide/aluminum oxide catalyst, removing the reaction heat by saturated water on the shell side to generate medium-pressure steam; boiling water circulates on the shell side to remove the heat of reaction, the suitable reaction temperature of the catalyst is 210-270 degrees C, and the pressure is 50-100 bar; and (6) passing the high-temperature products generated by the methanol synthesis reaction into the heat exchanger for cooling, cooling by the fourth heat exchanger, passing methanol synthesis product into the fourth cooler for cooling, passing cooled gas-liquid mixture into the third separator and the fourth separator for gas-liquid separation, sending the separated liquid to the first distillation tower, passing the heavy components after rectification in the first rectification tower into the second rectification tower for secondary rectification, and obtaining methanol after rectification in the second rectification tower. In the method, capturing the carbon dioxide from flue gas of power plants or fossil fuel combustion, compressing captured carbon dioxide into two pressures of 15-20 bar and 75-80bar respectively, mixing the carbon dioxide of 15-20 bar with methane-rich gas and steam after two reforming reactions and then converted into synthesis gas; mixing the generated synthesis gas with 75-80 bar carbon dioxide and entering the methanol reactor for methanol synthesis, and finally undergoes rectification and purification to obtain high-purity methanol. In the method, the working condition of wet reforming SMR reaction is reaction temperature is 850-900 degrees C, and the pressure is 15-20 bar, the working conditions of the dry reforming DMR reaction is reaction temperature is 900-1100 degrees C, the pressure is 15-20 bar, the catalyst in the dry reforming DMR reaction is cobalt-based carbon material catalyst. In the method, the temperature and pressure of the catalyst are 210-270 degrees C and 50-100 bar respectively, at this time, the catalyst has high selectivity and low reaction temperature for methanol synthesis. In the methanol synthesis process, the synthesis gas is compressed and pressurized before entering the methanol synthesis reactor, after being pressurized, the synthesis gas is mixed with carbon dioxide and then enters the methanol reactor. In the step (5), the rectification tower adopts double rectification tower separation process, the first rectification tower is composed of 8 theoretical stages, the reflux ratio is set to 0.5-0.7, and the second distillation column has 24 theoretical stages to separate the mixture of methanol and water. In the step (5), the rectification tower adopts double rectification tower separation process, the first distillation column is composed of 8 theoretical stages, and the reflux ratio is set to 0.5-0.7, the second distillation column has 24 theoretical stages to separate the methanol and water mixture.

PN IN201931029647-A; IN396062-B
TI Gasification process for conversion of high ash coal to syngas and production of methanol from syngas involves allowing reaction between high ash coal with gasification agent, removing the formed ash, separating and recycling fine particles.
AU CHINTALA T N
   ITTAGUNTA N
   KOTDWALA R
   DAGANI S R
   TIRUPATHI N C
   NAGAMOHAN I
AE BHARAT HEAVY ELECTRICALS LTD (BHAR-C)
GA 202113118K
AB    NOVELTY - A gasification process for conversion of high ash ( less than or equal to 45%) coal to syngas and production of methanol from syngas involves (a) allowing reaction between high ash coal with gasification agent composed of oxygen, steam and carbon dioxide generated in downstream process, in a fluidized bed reactor, to form syngas; (b) maintaining the operating temperature at 1000 degrees C by feeding enough oxygen but lower than stoichiometric value and feeding sufficient coal; (c) removing continuously the ash formed in step (a) to maintain the constant bed in the reactor; (d) separating and recycling back the fine particles in the syngas to the reactor to improve the carbon conversion; (e) cleaning the syngas with series of cyclones and candle filers to reduce the particulates concentration to less than or equal to 10 mg/nm3; and (f) passing the syngas to water scrubber or water flowing packed column to remove ammonia and alkali metals present by absorption.
   USE - A gasification process for conversion of high ash coal to syngas and production of methanol from syngas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses methanol for syngas clean up and carbon dioxide separated from syngas as fluidizing medium and for transportation of coal.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A gasification process for conversion of high ash ( less than or equal to 45%) coal to syngas and production of methanol from syngas involves (a) allowing reaction between high ash coal with gasification agent composed of oxygen, steam and carbon dioxide generated in downstream process, in a fluidized bed reactor, to form syngas; (b) maintaining the operating temperature at 1000 degrees C by feeding enough oxygen but lower than stoichiometric value and feeding sufficient coal; (c) removing continuously the ash formed in step (a) to maintain the constant bed in the reactor; (d) separating and recycling back the fine particles in the syngas to the reactor to improve the carbon conversion; (e) cleaning the syngas with series of cyclones and candle filers to reduce the particulates concentration to less than or equal to 10 mg/nm3; (f) passing the syngas to water scrubber or water flowing packed column to remove ammonia and alkali metals present by absorption; (g) passing the syngas to catalytic based sour water gas shift reactor to convert carbon monoxide into hydrogen by feeding suitable amount of steam; (h) passing the conditioned syngas to methanol based packed column to absorb carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide syngas and regeneration of spent methanol by stripping; (i) separating the carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide by employing pressure swing adsorber; (j) utilizing the separated carbon dioxide as fluidizing medium to maintain required velocity and as transporting medium for coal; (k) passing the conditioned syngas to catalytic reactor to remove remaining hydrogen sulfide present before sending to methanol loop; (l) passing the cleaned and conditioned syngas from step (j) to methanol loop to convert syngas into methanol and subsequent condensation; and (m) utilizing portion of methanol produced in step (k) for syngas cleaning, particularly as fresh solvent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The conversion of carbon monoxide to hydrogen through catalytic water gas shift reaction to meet the required hydrogen to carbon monoxide molar ratio is performed by supplying little quantity of steam. The heat available in syngas is used to generate steam that is in-turn used for fluidization, water gas shift reactor and regeneration of spent methanol solvent used in the carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide removal process. The recycled carbon dioxide that is separated from syngas is used as fluidization medium and transportation medium for the coal. The portion of methanol produced is used as a fresh solvent for the carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide removal process.

PT P
PN US2020069636-A1; US11291647-B2
TI Forming carbon dioxide extract of Azadirachta indica leaves used to treat oral and colon cancer, involves e.g. extraction of powdered leaves with supercritical carbon dioxide and further extraction with mixture of carbon dioxide and ethanol.
AU SOMAN G S
AE NISARGA BIOTECH PRIVATE LTD (NISA-Non-standard)
GA 202019347C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon dioxide (CO2) extract of Azadirachta indica leaves, comprises: (a1) powdering dried A. indica leaves; (b1) subjecting the obtained powder to extraction with supercritical CO2 at a pressure of 80-350 bar at 31-45 degrees C to obtain CO2 composition comprising CO2 extractives and residual powder; (c1) separating CO2 extractives at a pressure of 40-65 bar at temperature lower than the extraction temperature to obtain extract-A; (d1) subjecting residual powder to further extraction using a mixture of CO2 and ethanol at pressure of 80-350 bar and extraction temperature of 31-45 degrees C to obtain ethanol/CO2 composition comprising A. indica extractives; (e1) recovering A. indica extractives as extract-B by reducing a solvent pressure to 40-65 bar at a temperature lower than the extraction temperature, followed by vacuum distillation of ethanol; and (f1) combining extract-A and extract-B to obtain CO2 extract of A. indica leaves.
   USE - The process is useful for preparing CO2 extract of A. indica leaves, which is useful for treating oral and colon cancers and for inducing apoptosis. The ability of the supercritical carbon dioxide extract of A. indica leaf to treat colorectal cancer was tested in colorectal cancer HCT116 cells by measuring % cell viability using 3-(4,5-dimethylthiazol-2-yl)-2,5-diphenyltetrazolium bromide assay. The results showed that the rats treated with 15 mu g/ml the extract (comprised of nimbolide, nimbin and salannin) exhibited the cell viability of 62% after 72 hours.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: maintains integrity of phytoconstituents apart from retaining the temperature sensitive ingredients; provides the extract having no harmful extraction solvents, while retaining all beneficiary ingredients; utilizes supercritical CO2 extraction which does not leave any hazardous solvent residues; and provides herbal composition having lower levels of impurities.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing carbon dioxide (CO2) extract of Azadirachta indica leaves, comprises: (a1) powdering dried A. indica leaves having moisture content of less than 12% to obtain powder comprising particles having size of less than 0.42 mm; (b1) subjecting the obtained powder to extraction with supercritical CO2 at a pressure of 80-350 bar at 31-45 degrees C to obtain CO2 composition comprising CO2 extractives and residual powder; (c1) separating CO2 extractives from CO2 composition at a pressure of 40-65 bar at temperature lower than the extraction temperature to obtain extract-A; (d1) subjecting residual powder to further extraction using a mixture of CO2 and ethanol at pressure of 80-350 bar and extraction temperature of 31-45 degrees C to obtain ethanol/CO2 composition comprising A. indica extractives; (e1) recovering A. indica extractives from ethanol/CO2 composition as extract-B by reducing a solvent pressure to 40-65 bar at a temperature lower than the extraction temperature, followed by vacuum distillation of ethanol; and (f1) combining extract-A and extract-B to obtain CO2 extract of A. indica leaves. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for: (1) the CO2 extract of A. indica; and (2) a herbal composition comprising 50-300 mg the CO2 extract and at least one carrier or excipient.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The powder subjected for supercritical CO2 extraction is carried out at a flow rate of 10-40 kg CO2/kg raw material. The extract-A is passed through a high velocity micro-jet or nozzle to obtain particles having a particle size of 10-100 nm. The temperature in separating step (c1) and recovering step (e1) is maintained at 10-30 degrees C. The vacuum distillation of ethanol is carried out at a temperature below 45 degrees C. Preferred Components: The mixture of CO2 and ethanol contains 3-10% ethanol, based on the weight of the CO2. The CO2 extract of A. indica leaves obtained in step (f1) comprises minimum of 3 mg/g nimbolide; minimum of 130 mu g/g nimbin; and minimum of 200 mu g/g salannin. The CO2 extract of A. indica leaves further comprise a minor amount of at least one phytoconstituent comprising desacetyl nimbin, azadiradione, azadirone, nimbolin and/or nimbinene.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The carriers or excipients comprise distilled water, saline, aqueous glucose solution, alcohol (e.g. ethanol), surfactants, propylene glycol, Tween-80 (RTM: Polyoxyethylene sorbitan monooleate) and polyethylene glycol, or oily carriers including various animal and vegetable oils, white soft paraffin, paraffin, wax, glucose, fructose, sucrose, maltose, yellow dextrin, malt dextrin, white dextrin, aerosol, microcrystalline cellulose, calcium stearate, magnesium stearate, sorbitol, stevioside, maize syrup, lactose, citric acid, tartaric acid, malic acid, succinic acid, lactic acid, L-ascorbic acid, dl- alpha -tocopherol, glycerin, propylene glycol, glycerin fatty ester, poly glycerin fatty ester, sucrose fatty ester, sorbitan fatty ester, propylene glycol fatty ester, acacia, carrageenan, casein, gelatin, pectin, agar, vitamin B group, nicotinamide, calcium pantothenate, amino acids, aerated or fumed silica, calcium salts, pigments, flavors and/or preservatives.
   ACTIVITY - Apoptotic; Cytostatic.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - Nuclear factor kappa B inhibitor.
   ADMINISTRATION - Administration of the CO2 extract of A. indica leaves is by oral route (claimed). Dosage of the extract is 50-300 mg/dose.
   EXAMPLE - 1 kg Cleaned and matured Azadirachta indica leaves were taken and dried under the shade to reduce moisture content to less than 12%. The dried herb was powdered in particle size below 0.42 mm and then subjected to supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) extraction at a pressure of 80-350 Bar at a temperature of 31-45 degrees C. The CO2 was passed through the herb for 2-3 hours depending upon the size of the extractors and the quantity of herb loaded into the extractor at a time. The quantity of CO2 to be pumped through the herb was 10-40 kg of CO2/kg of herb depending upon the solubility of lipophilic compounds present in the herb. The CO2 carried extractives were collected from the separator where the pressure of CO2 was reduced to a pressure of 40-65 Bar and temperature of 10-30 degrees C to separate the solute (extract-A) and the CO2. The obtained extract-A was comprised of all the temperature sensitive ingredients present in the herb and all the other lipophilic soluble compounds. Residual powder after isolating extract-A was subjected to extraction using mixture of 90-97% of supercritical CO2 and 3-10% of ethanol. The extraction was carried out at the pressure of 80-300 Bar and temperature of 31-45 degrees C. The quantity of solvent pumped (CO2 and ethanol) was 10-40 kg/kg of herbs. The solute (extract-B1) and ethanol were separated from the CO2 on reducing the solvent pressure of 40-65 Bar and temperature of 10-30 degrees C. The ethanol laced with extract was collected from the separator. The mixture was then subjected to vacuum distillation (27-28.5 inch of mercury) keeping temperature below 45 degrees C, for separating the ethanol completely from the solute (extract-B1). The extract-A and extract-B were combined to obtain CO2 extract of A. indica leaves with 2.5-5% yield.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.)

PT P
PN JP2019156762-A; WO2019176746-A1; DE112019000757-T5; JP7061485-B2
TI Methane production apparatus has controller that controls operation of methane flow rate control device to increase methane supplied to source gas source when temperature detected by sensor exceeds predetermined temperature threshold.
AU IWAKI M
   NISHIYAMA A
   WARAYA Y
   MASAMOTO M
   WARATANI Y
AE KAWASAKI HEAVY IND LTD (KAWJ-C)
   KAWASAKI JUKOGYO KK (KAWJ-C)
   KAWASAKI HEAVY IND LTD (KAWJ-C)
GA 2019806131
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has a reactor (R) which contains a catalyst for generating methane from a source gas containing hydrogen and carbon dioxide. A source gas supply line (2) is connected to an inlet of the reactor and for supplying the source gas from a source gas source to the reactor. A product gas line (3) is connected to an outlet of the reactor and for sending a product gas containing methane exiting the reactor to the product gas tank. A methane supply line (5) supplies the product gas to the source gas source. A methane flow rate control device (81) is provided in the methane supply line and adjusts the amount of methane supplied to the source gas source. A temperature sensor (92) detects the temperature in the reactor. A controller controls the operation of the methane flow rate control device to increase the amount of methane supplied to the source gas source when the temperature detected by the temperature sensor exceeds a predetermined temperature threshold.
   USE - Methane production apparatus.
   ADVANTAGE - The temperature in the reactor can be suppressed by mixing methane into the raw material gas. The methane from a raw material gas containing mixed hydrogen and carbon dioxide is provided, while avoiding an increase in size of the compressor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The flow rate sensor detects a flow rate of the source gas flowing into the reactor, and a hydrogen flow rate control device for adjusting a hydrogen supply amount to the source gas source. A carbon dioxide flow rate control device adjusts a carbon dioxide supply amount to the source gas source. The control device detects the flow rate by the flow sensor is equal to or less than a predetermined flow rate threshold. The hydrogen flow rate control device increases the suction flow rate of a compressor in response to an increase in the flow rate of the raw material gas accompanying an increase in the methane supply amount. The control device does not increase the flow rate of the source gas when the flow rate detected by the flow rate sensor exceeds the flow rate threshold. The hydrogen supply and carbon dioxide supply are reduced to the source gas source and the methane supply is increased. The separator separates the components other than methane from the product gas provided in the product gas line. An upstream end of the methane supply line is connected to a downstream side of the product gas tank or the separation unit of the product gas line. The control device reduces the hydrogen supply amount, the carbon dioxide supply amount, and the methane supply amount to the source gas source until the pressure detected by the pressure sensor is equal to or lower than the pressure threshold.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the methane production apparatus.
   Source gas supply line (2)
   Product gas line (3)
   Methane supply line (5)
   Methane flow rate control device (81)
   Temperature sensor (92)
   Reactor (R)

PN CN110192494-A
TI Method for cultivating snow mushroom, involves utilizing cottonseed hulls, sawdust, bran, maize flour, lime and soybean meal followed by packing in polypropylene plastic bag, sterilizing, inoculating strain and managing.
AU CAI X
   GU X
AE JIANGSU YOUKANG ECOLOGICAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201978050D
AB    NOVELTY - A snow mushroom cultivation method involves utilizing cultivation substrate (water content of 62 wt.%) comprising 48 wt.% cottonseed hulls, 35 wt.% sawdust, 10 wt.% bran, 5 wt.% maize flour, 1 wt.% lime and 1 wt.% soybean meal, selecting snow mushroom strains free of mold and pest with uniform thickness and particle size, utilizing polypropylene plastic bag with size of 18 cmx 35 cm for cultivation, automatically filling the cultivation substrate to height of 18 cm, such that bag weighs to 1250 plus minus 5 g, utilizing automatic punching rod to make hole in middle of the bag, excluding cold air from the bag during sterilization, sterilizing in autoclave (100 degrees C/60 minutes, 110 degrees C/90 minutes and 121 degrees C/120 minutes), cooling the bag to 23 degrees C or less for inoculation followed by inoculating the mushroom strains, opening the bag, after obtaining mature hypha, injecting water, managing mushrooms, obtaining mature mushrooms and vacuum packing the mushrooms.
   USE - Method for cultivating snow mushroom.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables cultivating snow mushroom with excellent storage resisting property, uniform thickness of mushroom stalk and better quality mushroom cap at high yield by not undergoing yellowing of fruiting bodies.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A snow mushroom cultivation method involves utilizing cultivation substrate (water content of 62 wt.%) comprising 48 wt.% cottonseed hulls, 35 wt.% sawdust, 10 wt.% bran, 5 wt.% maize flour, 1 wt.% lime and 1 wt.% soybean meal, selecting snow mushroom strains free of mold and pest with uniform thickness and particle size, utilizing polypropylene plastic bag with size of 18 cmx 35 cm for cultivation, automatically filling the cultivation substrate to height of 18 cm, such that bag weighs to 1250 plus minus 5 g, utilizing automatic punching rod to make hole in middle of the bag, excluding cold air from the bag during sterilization, sterilizing in autoclave (100 degrees C/60 minutes, 110 degrees C/90 minutes and 121 degrees C/120 minutes), cooling the bag to 23 degrees C or less for inoculation, inoculating the mushroom strains into the bag using hand-held inoculation gun (25 ml strain per bag), transferring the bag to culture chamber for mycelial culture, maintaining carbon dioxide concentration at 3000 parts per million or less, humidity to 55-65% and temperature at 22-25 degrees C, maintaining carbon dioxide concentration at 3000-5000 parts per million or less, humidity to 60-65% and temperature at 25-27 degrees C, opening the bag, after obtaining mature hyphae, rolling the bag down to a height of 3 cm, injecting 50-100 ml clean and sterile water, maintaining hyphae at 14-15 degrees C, and humidity to 90-95% and carbon dioxide concentration to below 3000 parts per million, illuminating at 100-300 lx for 5 minutes/1 hour, maintaining carbon dioxide concentration to below 3000 parts per million, appropriately reducing humidity to 80-90%, gradually increasing humidity to 90-95% and carbon dioxide concentration to 5000-7000 parts per million, maintaining humidity at 95-100% and carbon dioxide concentration at 6000 parts per million, guiding growth of mushrooms, injecting water, before 2 or 3 days of maturation, watering every day, according to size of mushroom cap, obtaining mature mushrooms, vacuum packing the mushrooms and avoiding temperature fluctuations during management of mushrooms.

PN CN109954476-A; CN109954476-B
TI Preparing manganese-doped double-layer calcium carbonate hollow microsphere carbon dioxide absorbent, comprises e.g. performing hydrothermal crystallization reaction of sucrose solution, then reacting with metal salt, heating and roasting.
AU WANG S
   LI S
   JIANG T
   XU Z
   MA X
   ZHAO Y
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
   UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 201959920E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing manganese-doped double-layer calcium carbonate hollow microsphere carbon dioxide absorbent, comprises e.g. (i) adding sucrose solution in a crystallization kettle, carrying out hydrothermal crystallization reaction, then filtering the obtained product, alternately washing with ethanol and water, then drying, and grinding to obtain carbon spheres, (ii) adding metal salt to the solvent, stirring to dissolve to obtain metal salt solution, then adding prepared metal salt solution and mixing to obtain suspension, ultrasonically dispersing the suspension, magnetically stirring the filter cake in a water bath, filtering, washing and drying, and (iii) grinding the dried obtained product, then placing into a muffle furnace, heating to 380-420 degrees C at a first heating rate under air atmosphere, then heating to 490-510 degrees C and maintaining the temperature at a second heating rate, roasting for 1-3 hours to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing manganese-doped double-layer calcium carbonate hollow microsphere carbon dioxide absorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorbent: exhibits high adsorption capacity and excellent stability in the adsorption and desorption cycle, and optimal adsorption capacity with highest performance reaches to 0.62 g carbon dioxide/g adsorbent; and still maintains optimal adsorption capacity even after 25 cycles at 0.53 g carbon dioxide/g adsorbent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing manganese-doped double-layer calcium carbonate hollow microsphere carbon dioxide absorbent, comprises (i) adding sucrose solution in a crystallization kettle, carrying out hydrothermal crystallization reaction at 160-230 degrees C for 1.5-3 hours, then filtering the obtained product, alternately washing with ethanol and water, then drying at 60-120 degrees C for 8-16 hours, and grinding to obtain carbon spheres having diameter of 1-4 mu m, where the concentration of sucrose solution is 0.5-2 M, (ii) adding metal salt to the solvent, stirring to dissolve to obtain metal salt solution, then adding prepared metal salt solution and mixing to obtain suspension, ultrasonically dispersing the suspension for 5-30 minutes, magnetically stirring the filter cake in a water bath at 25-60 degrees C for 5-15 hours, filtering, washing and drying at 60-120 degrees C for 8-24 hours, where the metal salt is calcium nitrate or calcium nitrate and manganese acetate, the molar ratio of calcium nitrate to manganese acetate is 1:0-0.01, the solvent is water, or mixed solution of ethanol and water, the volume ratio of water to ethanol is 1:0-5, the concentration of calcium nitrate in metal salt solution is 1-4 M, the addition of 300-1200 mg carbon spheres per 30 ml of metal salt solution, and (iii) grinding the dried obtained product, then placing into a muffle furnace, heating to 380-420 degrees C at a first heating rate under air atmosphere, then heating to 490-510 degrees C and maintaining the temperature at a second heating rate, roasting for 1-3 hours to obtain final product, where in step (iii), the first heating rate is 1-10 degrees C/minute, and the second heating rate is 25-75% of the first heating rate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (ii), the solvent is a mixed solution of ethanol and water in a volume ratio of 1:1-5. In step (ii), when the metal salt is calcium nitrate and manganese acetate, the molar ratio of calcium nitrate to manganese acetate is 1:0.01-0.5.

PN US2020114306-A1; WO2020075540-A1; US10773206-B2; CA3109449-A1; AU2019356720-A1; EP3842121-A1; JP2020550401-X; EP3842121-A4; AU2019356720-B2; JP7011731-B2
TI Apparatus for recovering carbon dioxide in factory exhaust gas, has control device to adjust supply amount of cleaning liquid to cleaning device, so that concentration of absorption agent is maintained to be not greater than set value.
AU MIYAMOTO O
   KAMIJO T
   TSUJIUCHI T
AE MITSUBISHI HEAVY IND ENG LTD (MITO-C)
   MITSUBISHI HEAVY IND ENG LTD (MITO-C)
   MITSUBISHI HEAVY IND ENG LTD (MITO-C)
   MITSUBISHI HEAVY IND ENG LTD (MITO-C)
GA 202029320V
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (1) has an absorption tower (4) that removes carbon dioxide in an exhaust gas by contacting with a carbon dioxide absorption liquid containing an absorption agent. A regeneration tower (6) regenerates the carbon dioxide absorption liquid from the absorption tower. A reflux water drum (8) separates carbon dioxide gas that is released from the regeneration tower into carbon dioxide gas and condensed water. A cleaning device (52) is installed in a gas-phase portion of the regeneration tower and configured to remove the absorption agent entrained with the carbon dioxide gas from the lower portion of the regeneration tower, by using cleaning liquid containing condensed water from the reflux water drum/water obtained from the condensed water. A control device is configured to adjust a supply amount of the cleaning liquid to the cleaning device, so that concentration of the absorption agent in the condensed water is maintained to be not greater than a predetermined value.
   USE - Carbon dioxide recovery apparatus for recovering carbon dioxide from exhaust gas discharged from power generation facility or a factory.
   ADVANTAGE - The heat quantity e.g. steam required to maintain the liquid amount inside the regeneration tower, the supply amount of the condensed water to be supplied to the cleaning device of the regeneration tower as the cleaning liquid can be minimized. The evaporation amount of water in the device is increased to maintain the water balance in the device. The supply amount of the first cleaning liquid containing the condensed water to the first cleaning device is adjusted so that the concentration of the absorption agent in the condensed water is maintained to be not greater than a predetermined value, to reduce the supply amount of the first cleaning liquid to the first cleaning device while suppressing increase in the concentration of the absorption agent in the first cleaning liquid supplied to the first cleaning device and the heat required to evaporate the liquid inside the regeneration tower is reduced to improve the efficiency of carbon dioxide recovery.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a carbon dioxide recovery method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the carbon dioxide recovery apparatus.
   Carbon dioxide recovery apparatus (1)
   Absorption tower (4)
   Regeneration tower (6)
   Reflux water drum (8)
   Cleaning device (52)

PT P
PN CN113479923-A; CN215249609-U
TI Preparation device for high-purity barium carbonate for electronic industry, has reactor with jacket structure, stirring device, heat conducting oil, and air supply system with compressed carbon dioxide gas source and switch valves.
AU QIN Y
   JIN Y
   DI D
   HU S
   CAO Y
   HAN H
   YANG M
   GE X
AE BENGBU ZHONGHENG NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY (CHNB-C)
   ANHUI ZHONGCHUANG ELECTRONIC MATERIAL CO (CHNB-C)
   ANHUI ZHONGCHUANG ELECTRONICS & INFORMATION MATERIALS CO LTD (CHNB-C)
GA 2021B90425
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation device comprises reaction system and air supply system. The reaction system comprises reactor with jacket structure, and stirring device. The inner cavity of jacket is filled with heat conducting oil, and provided with heating resistance wire. The bottom of inner cavity of reactor is provided with gas distribution plate. The upper end enclosure of reactor is provided with solid feeding port, water adding port, pressure meter port, thermometer opening and air outlet. The lower end enclosure of reactor is provided with discharging port, heat conducting oil inlet and air inlet. The upper portion of reactor is provided with heat conducting oil outlet. The stirring device includes motor, speed reducer, stirring rod and stirring blade. The air supply system includes gas source, switch valves, gas check valve, liquid check valve, pressure gauge, vacuum pump and pipeline. The gas source is compressed carbon dioxide.
   USE - The device is used for preparing high-purity barium carbonate for electronic industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The device provides environment-friendly reaction, high synthesis conversion rate, high carbon dioxide utilization rate, and high product purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The preparation device comprises reaction system and air supply system. The reaction system comprises reactor with jacket structure, and stirring device. The inner cavity of jacket is filled with heat conducting oil, and provided with heating resistance wire. The bottom of inner cavity of reactor is provided with gas distribution plate. The upper end enclosure of reactor is provided with solid feeding port, water adding port, pressure meter port, thermometer opening and air outlet. The lower end enclosure of reactor is provided with discharging port, heat conducting oil inlet and air inlet. The upper portion of reactor is provided with heat conducting oil outlet. Solid feeding port, water adding port, pressure meter port, thermometer port, air outlet, discharging port and air inlet are connected with inner cavity of reactor. The heat conducting oil inlet and heat conducting oil outlet are connected with inner cavity of jacket. The stirring device includes motor, speed reducer, stirring rod and stirring blade. The speed reducer and motor are fixed on top of reactor. The stirring rod is inserted into inner cavity of reactor. The stirring blade rod is fixed on the stirring rod. The air supply system includes gas source, switch valves, gas check valve, liquid check valve, pressure gauge, vacuum pump and pipeline. The gas source is compressed carbon dioxide. The switch valve is interlocked with the pressure gauge. The two ends of pipeline are respectively connected with air outlet and air inlet of reactor. The vacuum pump is arranged on the pipeline of air supply system.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method of preparing high-purity barium carbonate comprising using barium hydroxide octahydrate, carbon dioxide and water as raw material, heating and dissolving barium hydroxide octahydrate in water, circulating carbon dioxide gas, stirring raw materials in sealed reactor to generate precipitate, dehydrating, and drying.

PN US2021121826-A1; US11247176-B2
TI Apparatus for direct air capture of components of atmospheric air, has membrane unit having outer surface and inner surface, permeate collection device, vacuum-generating device which applies vacuum to membrane unit, and delivery conduit.
AU KOLODJI B
AE KOLODJI B (KOLO-Individual)
   BLACK SWAN LLC (BLAC-Non-standard)
GA 2021425406
AB    NOVELTY - Apparatus for direct air capture of components of atmospheric air comprises a membrane unit (102) comprising an outer surface and an inner surface, where the membrane unit is configured such that as a first gas comprising a first concentration of nitrogen, a first concentration of oxygen, a first concentration of water, and a first concentration of carbon dioxide is drawn into the outer surface and passes through the membrane, a permeate stream exits the inner surface where the permeate stream comprises a second concentration of nitrogen, a second concentration of oxygen, a second concentration of water, and a second concentration of carbon dioxide; a permeate collection device which collects the permeate stream; a vacuum-generating device (108) which applies a vacuum to the membrane unit; and a delivery conduit (104) which conducts portion of the permeate stream to a sequestering facility.
   USE - The apparatus is useful for direct air capture of components of atmospheric air (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system is relatively simple and requires low power input to generate the vacuum necessary to process a feed gas through the leaf membranes. Because the disclosed systems, including the membranes, the conduits, and the vacuum generating devices can be produced at relatively low cost, the only significant detriment in utilizing a substantial number of membrane banks is the amount of area required for placement of the units. The flue gas generator may be pressurized, thus eliminating the need for downstream pressurization. The flue gas stream from the flue gas generator is reduced in volume and thus more economical to transport because ducts and permeate blower systems may be substantially reduced in size.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for direct air capture of components of atmospheric ambient air which involves exposing a membrane unit to atmospheric air, the membrane unit comprising an outer surface and an inner surface, where the membrane unit is configured such that as a first gas comprising a first concentration of oxygen, a first concentration of carbon dioxide and a first concentration of nitrogen is drawn into the outer surface and passes through the membrane unit a permeate stream exits the inner surface where the permeate stream comprises a second concentration of oxygen, a second concentration of carbon dioxide and a second concentration of nitrogen, where the second concentration of oxygen is greater than the first concentration of oxygen, the second concentration of carbon dioxide is greater than the first concentration of carbon dioxide, and the second concentration of nitrogen is less than the first concentration of nitrogen; applying a vacuum to the membrane unit through a permeate conduit; receiving the permeate stream into the permeate conduit; and delivering at least a portion of the permeate stream to a sequestering facility.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of bank or array of leaf membrane units which may be utilized for direct air capture of atmospheric air.
   Array (100)
   Membrane units (102)
   Delivery conduit (104)
   Headers (106)
   Vacuum-generating device (108)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The sequestering facility comprises photosynthetic organisms.Preferred Method: The method further involves taking the permeate stream from the permeate conduit, transmitting the permeate stream to a secondary enrichment system which produces an enriched permeate, and delivering at least a portion of the enriched permeate to the sequestering facility.

PT P
PN CN110105058-A
TI Preparation of ceramic foam useful for waste water treatment.
AU YU H
AE YU H (YUHH-Individual)
GA 201970687E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of ceramic foam comprises: mixing raw materials, grinding, ball milling and drying to obtain mixed powder, adding gelatin solution, mixing and ball milling, stirring the slurry, injecting into mold, drying and demolding, placed in an autoclave of supercritical carbon dioxide foaming device, filled with low-pressure carbon dioxide, refilling with high pressure carbon dioxide, relieving pressure quickly, placing in a muffle furnace, laid in the low-temperature plasma treatment device, processing, dissolving aluminum nitrate nonahydrate and distilled water, placing in water bath, heating, adding aluminum powder, removing bottom gray precipitate after centrifugation, obtaining colorless transparent sol-gel, adding polyethylene glycol, lanthanium nitrate and barium nitrate, dissolving into a light yellow transparent solution, immersing the modified foam ceramic into light yellow transparent solution, placing in a muffle furnace and calcining to obtain a foamed ceramic.
   USE - The ceramic foam is useful in the wastewater treatment (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The ceramic foam has good treatment effect on chemical oxygen demand and ammonia nitrogen of waste water.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of ceramic foam comprises (1) mixing diatomite, kaolin, quartz, straw biochar and alpha -aluminum oxide micro powder and grinding until the fineness is less than 0.1 mm, (2) performing ball milling until the grain diameter is less than or equal to 2 mu m and drying to obtain mixed powder, (3) adding gelatin solution to prepare slurry having solid content of 40-45%, mixing and ball milling for 5-10 minutes, (4) magnetic stirring the slurry after vacuum degassing, injecting into a mold and sealing in the drying box of 85?0C for 1 hour, (5) transferring to a drying oven of 50 degrees C for drying and demolding, (6) placing in an autoclave of a supercritical carbon dioxide foaming device, filling with low-pressure carbon dioxide to replace the air in the kettle, refilling with high pressure carbon dioxide at a temperature of 50-80 degrees C, swelling at a pressure of 10-20 MPa for 10-20 minutes, and relieving pressure quickly within 2 seconds, (7) placing in a muffle furnace, calcining at a temperature of 1300-1350 degrees C for 4 hours, (8) laying the modified foam ceramic on ground electrode in the low-temperature plasma treatment device after cooling, processing for 10-15 minutes with power supply frequency of 10-15khz, working voltage of 20 kV and discharge power of 70-80 W, (9) stirring and dissolving 200-250 parts of aluminum nitrate nonahydrate and 600-650 pts of distilled water, placing in a water bath, and heating at 90 degrees C under reflux, adding 35-40 parts of aluminum powder slowly to the reaction controlled at pH 3-4, (10) removing bottom gray precipitate after centrifugation, to obtain colorless transparent sol-gel, (11) adding 20-25 pts. wt. polyethylene glycol, 3-6 pts. wt. lanthanum nitrate and 4-8 pts. wt. barium nitrate, stirring and dissolving into a light yellow transparent solution, (12) immersing the modified foam ceramic into light yellow transparent solution in vacuum for 0.5-1 hour, providing into a drying box for drying, and (13) placing in a muffle furnace and calcining at 500-800 degrees C for 5 hours to obtain a foamed ceramic.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Method: In the step (3), the content of the gelatin in the mixed powder is 20-30 wt.%. In the step (6), the temperature is 65 degrees C, the pressure is 15 MPa and the swelling time is 15 minutes. In the step (8), the power frequency is 13 kHz and the discharge power is 75 W.

TI New microporous zinc metal-organic framework material i.e. zinc-tetracarboxylic acid-bipyridine complex useful for selectively adsorbing carbon dioxide gas, and separating carbon dioxide and methane.
AU LI J
   XIE Y
   LV J
   LIU L
   XIAO J
   LI Y
   CHEN Q
AE UNIV BEIJING TECHNOLOGY (UYBT-C)
GA 2021C9062V
AB    NOVELTY - Microporous zinc metal-organic framework material i.e. zinc-tetracarboxylic acid-bipyridine complex (I) is new.
   USE - (I) is useful for selectively adsorbing carbon dioxide gas, and separating carbon dioxide and methane (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I) has regular pore structure, high porosity, specific surface area, different adsorption force to carbon dioxide and methane, and has carbon dioxide selective adsorption performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Microporous zinc metal-organic framework material i.e. zinc-tetracarboxylic acid-bipyridine complex of formula (Zn2(TCBPE)(Bpb)) (I) is new.
   TCBPE = planar tetracarboxylic acid ligand i.e. tetra(4-(4'-carboxyphenyl)phenyl)ethylene; and
   Bpb = bipyridine ligand i.e. 1,4-bis(4-pyridyl)benzene.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparation of (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I), comprises (i) dissolving organic ligands tetra(4-(4'-carboxyphenyl)phenyl)ethylene, 1,4-bis(4-pyridyl)benzene and zinc nitrate in mixed solution of N,N-dimethylformamide and methanol, and (ii) subjecting the mixed solution obtained in step (i) into solvothermal reaction to obtain a massive single crystal, washing with N,N-dimethylformamide to obtain final product.Preferred Components: (I) belongs to monoclinic crystal system and having the space group of P2/c, and unit cell parameters are V is 4099.2(5)Angstrom3, a is 13.7413(9)Angstrom, b is 20.0591(10)Angstrom, c is 16.0569(11)Angstrom, alpha is 90degrees, beta is 112.152(8)degrees, and gamma is 90degrees. (I) has a triple-interpenetrating layered column structure, and the binuclear metal zinc and tetra(4-(4'-carboxyphenyl)phenyl)ethylene ligand form a two-dimensional layered structure, the binuclear metallic zinc in the two-dimensional layered structure is connected with the 1,4-bis(4-pyridyl)benzene to form a three-dimensional microporous structure.The molar ratio between organic ligand tetra(4-(4'-carboxyphenyl)phenyl)ethylene and 1,4-bis(4-pyridyl)benzene is 1:1-1:3, the total molar ratio between zinc nitrate and tetra(4-(4'-carboxyphenyl)phenyl)ethylene and 1,4-bis(4-pyridyl)benzene is 1:1. The volume ratio between dimethylformamide and methanol in the mixed solvent is 1:1-2:1.Preferred Method: The solvothermal reaction is carried out is 100-120degrees Celsius for 48-96 hours.

   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to manufacture dialkyl carbonate which is cost-effective, saves energy and short to process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The base comprises Cs (Triazolide), Cs (Benzotriazolide), or Cs (Amino-triazolide). The solvent comprises CH2Br2 or CH2Cl2. The first pressure is between 10-90 psi, and the second pressure is 200-600 psi. The reaction time of the dialkyl carbonate is 24 hours or more. Preferred Process: The method further involves injecting the carbon dioxide to produce a reactant, injecting carbon dioxide into the mixture at a first pressure, stirring the carbon dioxide injected mixture with the first pressure and injecting carbon dioxide into the agitated mixture at a second pressure.

   USE - The secondary battery is useful for charging and discharging function in power generation system (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The secondary battery produces hydrogen as environmentally friendly fuel with high purity using carbon dioxide in cathode during discharge, and sodium hydrogen carbonate using carbon dioxide as raw material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Secondary battery comprises a cathode unit (110) having a first aqueous solution (115) of potassium hydroxide accommodated in a first accommodation space (111) and a cathode partially immersed in the first aqueous solution of potassium hydroxide; an anode unit (150) having a second aqueous solution of potassium hydroxide contained in a second accommodation space (151), and an anode made of metal at least partially immersed in the aqueous potassium hydroxide solution; a connecting unit having a connection passage communicating the first accommodation space and the second accommodation space, and an ion exchange membrane disposed in the connection passage for blocking the movement of the first aqueous solution of potassium hydroxide and second aqueous solution of potassium hydroxide; and a carbon dioxide treatment unit having the first aqueous solution of potassium hydroxide accommodated in a third accommodation space communicating with the first accommodation space. The carbon dioxide gas is introduced into the first aqueous solution of potassium hydroxide in the third accommodation space to generate hydrogen ions and bicarbonate ions by the reaction of the water of the first aqueous potassium hydroxide solution and the carbon dioxide gas, where hydrogen ions and the electrons of the cathode are coupled to generate hydrogen gas in the cathode unit. The carbon dioxide processing unit is configured to separate the carbon dioxide gas, which is not ionized in the carbon dioxide gas introduced into the first aqueous solution of potassium hydroxide in the third accommodation space, from the aqueous potassium hydroxide solution to prevent the carbon dioxide gas from being supplied to the cathode unit. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for power generation system comprising either: secondary battery which generates hydrogen by using carbon dioxide as raw material in discharge process, and a fuel cell supplied with hydrogen generated in the secondary battery; secondary battery which generates hydrogen by using carbon dioxide as raw material in discharge process, a reformer for producing hydrogen-rich reformed gas from the hydrogen-containing fuel and generating carbon dioxide as a by-product, a fuel cell supplied with the reformed gas produced from the reformer as fuel, and a carbon dioxide supply unit for supplying carbon dioxide generated in the reformer to the secondary battery; secondary battery which generates hydrogen by using carbon dioxide as raw material in discharge process, a reformer for producing hydrogen-rich reformed gas from the hydrogen-containing fuel and generating carbon dioxide as a by-product, a fuel cell supplied with the reformed gas produced from the reformer as fuel, and a hydrogen supply unit for further supplying hydrogen generated in the secondary battery to the fuel of the fuel cell; secondary battery which generates hydrogen by using carbon dioxide as raw material in discharge process, a reformer for producing hydrogen-rich reformed gas from the hydrogen-containing fuel and generating carbon dioxide as a by-product, a fuel cell supplied with the reformed gas produced from the reformer as fuel, a carbon dioxide supply unit for supplying carbon dioxide generated in the reformer to the secondary battery and a hydrogen supply unit for further supplying hydrogen generated in the secondary battery to the fuel of the fuel cell; or secondary battery which generates hydrogen by using carbon dioxide as raw material in discharge process and hydrocarbon fuel cells which produce electricity by using hydrocarbons as fuel and generate carbon dioxide as by-product, where the carbon dioxide generated as a by-product in the hydrocarbon fuel cell is supplied to the secondary battery.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing fluorine oxide supported noble metal                catalyst involves: taking lanthanide metal nitrate                and fluoride, dissolving in deionized water, adding                sodium carbonate solution to adjust pH value,                heating and stirring to make it fully react,                filtering, washing, drying and roasting to obtain                fluorine oxide carrier; impregnating the obtained                fluorine oxide carrier with a precious metal salt                in an unequal volume, centrifuging, drying, cooling                and grinding to obtain a catalyst precursor; and                calcining the obtained catalyst precursor at a                certain temperature to obtain fluorine                oxide-supported noble metal catalyst.
   USE - Method for preparing fluorine oxide supported                noble metal catalyst that is used for catalytic                hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to produce formic                acid (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared metal catalyst ensures high                reactivity, easy separation, high recycling rate,                and high turnover number (TON) value, and maintains                high formic acid selectivity in a wide temperature                and pressure range, and can be prepared in simple                and easy manner.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Composition: The metal catalyst                  comprises fluorine oxide as a carrier, and 0.5-2                  wt.% precious metal as an active site. The                  fluorine oxide is composed of lanthanide metal                  elements, fluorine, and oxygen elements at ratio                  of 1:0.5-1:1. The lanthanide metal is lanthanum                  and cerium, the precious metal is rhodium,                  ruthenium, palladium, iridium, and                  platinum.Preferred Conditions: The calcination                  temperature is 600-900&#8451;, the                  calcination time is 3-5 hours, and the heating                  rate is 2-5&#8451;/minute.Preferred Method: The formic acid by                  catalytic hydrogenation is prepared by using                  aqueous solution of sodium bicarbonate as the                  carbon dioxide source, under hydrogen pressure of                  1-4 MPa, and reaction temperature of                  50-200&#8451;, rotating speed of the solution                  stirring of 500-1000 rpm for 5-40 hours.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (1), the                  solvent is dehydrated alcohol. PN KR2021111580-A
TI Catalyst for production of linear alpha olefins comprises catalyst carrier including zinc oxide, and catalyst particles supported on catalyst carrier and including iron compound in which sodium is dispersed on surface.
AU TAE K Y
   YANG S
   LEE S
AE KOREA RES INST CHEM TECHNOLOGY (KRIC-C)
GA 2021A63242
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst (100) comprises a catalyst carrier (120) including zinc oxide, and catalyst particles (110) supported on the catalyst carrier and including an iron compound in which sodium is dispersed on the surface. The catalyst particles includes 30-60 wt.% iron oxide and 40-70 wt. iron carbide in stabilized state.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for the production of linear alpha -olefins by simultaneous hydrogenation of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide from synthesis gas rich in carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: has increased lifespan stability due to its superior coke resistance and moisture resistance compared to conventional iron-based catalyst; and solves the problem of supplying a linear alpha -olefin compound having high added value. The method: has high productivity of linear alpha -olefin with yield of greater than or equal to 16%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing linear alpha -olefins, comprising carrying out Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction and a reverse water gas shift reaction of synthesis gas containing carbon dioxide using the catalyst.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the reaction schematic diagram of the catalyst in stabilized state (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Catalyst (100)
   Catalyst particle (110)
   Catalyst carrier (120)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The synthesis gas rich in carbon dioxide has: carbon monoxide/carbon dioxide molar ratio of less than or equal to 1; and hydrogen/(carbon monoxide+carbon dioxide) molar ratio of greater than or equal to 2. The weight ratio of iron carbide to iron oxide in catalyst particles is 0.5-3. The catalyst particles were measured by dynamic moisture adsorption analysis of 10-60% RH at the same pressure, and the water desorption mass change with respect to water adsorption mass change is 1.5-10. The linear alpha -olefin has carbon number of greater than or equal to 2C. Preferred Method: The Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction and reverse water gas shift reaction are carried out with a reaction temperature of 300-400 degrees C and a reaction pressure of 1-3 MPa.
   EXAMPLE - Distilled water was added to 50 g ferric nitrate nonahydrate in a beaker up to the mark of 121.9 ml. Distilled water was added into 50 g zinc nitrate hexahydrate in another beaker up to the mark of 121.9 ml. Two aqueous solution were added to a round bottom flask to obtain an iron-zinc precursor mixture. The pH of the iron-zinc precursor mixture was adjusted to 9 using aqueous potassium carbonate solution, stirred at 80 degrees C with a speed of 360 rotations/minute for 4 hours. The obtained product was washed with distilled water at 80 degrees C and dried to obtain a co-precipitate. The co-precipitate was calcined at 400 degrees C (10 degrees C/minute) under air atmosphere for 4 hours to obtain iron-zinc co-precipitate. 1.3479 ml aqueous sodium carbonate solution containing 0.5370 g sodium carbonate was impregnated with iron-zinc co-precipitate at room temperature for 1 hour by initial wetness. The impregnated material was dried overnight in an oven at 80 degrees C under air atmosphere and calcined at 350 degrees C for 4 hours at a rate of 10 degrees C/minute to obtain sodium/iron-zinc catalyst.

PN CN112515172-A
TI Sugar vitrification microcapsule comprises core            material comprising coenzyme Q10, vitamin D3 and            vitamin K2 and wall material comprising sodium starch            octenyl succinate, saccharide compound, pH regulator            and plasticizer.
AU LIU J
   LIN M
   WANG W
AE XIAMEN KINGDOMWAY BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (XIAM-Non-standard)
   XIAMEN KINGDOMWAY GROUP CO (XIAM-Non-standard)
GA 202133157L
AB    NOVELTY - Sugar vitrification microcapsule comprises                core material and wall material, where the core                material comprises coenzyme Q10, vitamin D3 and                vitamin K2, the wall material comprises sodium                starch octenyl succinate, saccharide compound, pH                regulator and plasticizer, the sodium starch                octenyl succinate has viscosity of 20-100                mPa/second at 25 degrees C and the saccharide                compound has dextrose equivalent (DE) value of more                than 16.
   USE - As sugar vitrification microcapsule.
   ADVANTAGE - The microcapsule has excellent water                solubility, stability and remulsibility and its                preparation method prevents formation of cavities                inside the microcapsules and appearance of cracks                and depressions on the surface.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method                for preparing sugar vitrification microcapsule,                which involves (a) uniformly mixing the core                material with the wall material to form an emulsion                and (b) passing the supercritical carbon dioxide                fluid into the emulsion, keeping the supercritical                carbon dioxide fluid in the supercritical state for                a period of time under suitable temperature and                pressure, so that the supercritical carbon dioxide                fluid is dissolved in the wall material and the                wall material expands, and then rapidly relieving                the pressure to make the wall material solidify                around the core material to obtain sugar vitrified                microcapsules.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of the                  sodium starch octenyl succinate to the saccharide                  compound is 1:(2-3). The wall material comprises                  20-25 wt.% sodium starch octenyl succinate, 55-60                  wt.% saccharide compound, 1-2 wt.% pH regulator                  and 4-8 wt.% plasticizer. The saccharide compound                  is chosen from at least one of glucose, fructose,                  sucrose, glucose syrup, maltose, lactose, xylose,                  oligofructose, solid corn syrup and maltodextrin.                  The pH regulator is chosen from at least one of                  citric acid, potassium citrate, sodium citrate,                  ascorbic acid, potassium ascorbate, sodium                  ascorbate, phytic acid, potassium phytate and                  sodium phytate. The plasticizer is chosen from at                  least one of glycerin, polyethylene glycol,                  sorbitol and maltitol. The core material                  comprises 5.5-10.5 wt.% coenzyme Q10, 0.004-0.007                  wt.% vitamin D3, 0.2-0.4 wt.% vitamin K2,                  0.02-0.05 wt.% antioxidants, 3.043-8.776 wt.%                  carrier oil and 0.2-0.5 wt.% oil phase                  emulsifier. The antioxidant is chosen from at                  least one of lecithin, natural vitamin E,                  rosemary extract and 2,6-di-tert-butyl-p-cresol.                  The carrier oil is chosen from at least one of                  caprylic acid triglyceride, caprylic acid                  triglyceride, capric acid triglyceride, caprylic                  acid/capric acid triglyceride, enanthate                  triglyceride, and lauric acid triglyceride. The                  oil phase emulsifier is chosen from at least one                  of monoglyceride compound, sucrose ester                  compound, lecithin and ascorbyl palmitate.                  Preferred Method: The step (a) involves melting                  the core material at 40-50 degrees C to obtain an                  oil phase liquid, dissolving 100 pts. wt. wall                  material in 60-80 pts. wt. water, heating at                  40-50 degrees C to obtain aqueous phase solution,                  mixing the oil phase solution with aqueous phase                  solution and shearing and emulsifying at 40-50                  degrees C and 8000-12000 revolutions per minute                  (rpm) for 10-30 minutes to obtain oil droplets                  having size D50 of less than or equal to 2 mu m                  and viscosity of 100-350 mPa/second to obtain                  emulsion. The step (b) involves maintaining the                  supercritical carbon dioxide fluid at 30-40                  degrees C and pressure of 25-40 MPa for 5-20                  minutes.

AU AKITANI K
   AOTA H
AE OMIYA KOATSU YG (OMIY-Non-standard)
GA 2020A34322
AB    NOVELTY - Rinse water comprises sodium hypochlorite having 10-199 ppm chlorine, and 1-3.5 GV carbon dioxide. The rinse water is water that meets specified water quality standards, and has a pH of 5-7.5.
   USE - The water is useful for rinsing foodstuff, packaging foodstuff, and preserving foodstuff (claimed). The foodstuff is chosen from lettuce, cabbage, Welsh onion, apple, mandarin orange, orange, and kiwi fruit.
   ADVANTAGE - The water improves long-term quality of foodstuff, and maintains a sterile state of foodstuff for a long time (a state in which growth of bacteria is suppressed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method (M1) for producing rinse water involves mixing sodium hypochlorite having 10-199 ppm chlorine, carbonated water having 1-3.5 GV carbon dioxide, and tap water;
   (2) a method (M2) for final processing foodstuff involves (a) washing and/or sterilizing foodstuff, and (b) rinsing foodstuff using rinse water;
   (3) a method (M3) for packaging foodstuff after cleaning and/or sterilizing foodstuff involves performing above-mentioned method (M2) immediately before packaging in a sterile packaging material; and
   (4) a method (M4) for preserving foodstuff involves performing above-mentioned method (M2).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Component: The rinse water has 20-180 ppm chlorine. The evaporation residue of rinse water comprises 0-50 wt.% sodium compound. The rinse water further comprises 108 pieces/ml or more nanobubble or microbubble. Preferred Method: The mixing of sodium hypochlorite, carbonated water, and tap water is such that temperature of the mixture immediately after mixing of 0-50 degrees C. The carbonated water is produced by bubbling carbon dioxide in tap water at 0-50 degrees C in a carbon dioxide atmosphere under a carbon dioxide pressure of 0.3-0.6 MPa. The method (M1) involves using nanobubble or microbubble generator to generate nanobubble or microbubble, after the bubbling. The method (M2) involves (b) rinsing foodstuff at 0-50 degrees C while simultaneously generating nanobubble or microbubble by a nanobubble or microbubble generator. In method (M2), only a washing treatment is performed before rinsing step. The step (a) of method (M2) involves using sterilized water containing sodium hypochlorite having 200-1000 ppm chlorine. The method (M3) involves performing a draining or drying treatment, after the final food processing method.

PT P
PN CN111389401-A; CN111389401-B
TI Preparing a microorganism coupled catalytic system useful for high-efficiency catalytic conversion of carbon dioxide, comprises drying, dehydrating precursor of iron-base active metal catalyst to obtain solid A and dissolving auxiliary salt.
AU ZHANG Y
   LU F
   WANG W
   LIU Y
AE UNIV BEIJING CHEM TECHNOLOGY (UYBC-C)
GA 202066803F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a microorganism coupled catalytic system comprises (i) drying and dehydrating the precursor of the iron-based active metal catalyst to obtain solid A; (ii) dissolving the auxiliary salt in the solvent to obtain solution B; (iii) immersing the solution B on solid A powder, stirring and drying to evaporate water to obtain solid C; (iv) pressing solid C to 20-40 meshes, and activating to obtain solid D; (v) grinding the solid D to obtain catalyst E; and (v) mixing catalyst E with domesticated anaerobic microorganisms under anaerobic conditions by using a shaker to mechanically and physically mix to obtain a microorganism-coupled catalytic system.
   USE - The microorganism coupled catalytic system is useful for high-efficiency catalytic conversion of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The microorganism coupled catalytic system: can couples anaerobic microorganisms and iron-based catalysts to obtain a catalytic system, which can accelerate the efficient catalytic conversion of inert carbon dioxide by anaerobic microorganisms into high value-added products, namely methanol, formic acid or acetic acid, thus realizes efficient utilization of CO2, and realizes the efficient output of high value-added products.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the assistant salt comprises trisodium citrate, tripotassium phosphate, citric acid lithium, sodium nitrate, potassium nitrate, lithium nitrate, rubidium nitrate, magnesium nitrate, copper nitrate, zinc nitrate, zinc sulfate, zirconium sulfate, gallium sulfate, manganese sulfate, manganese acetate, zinc acetate, potassium permanganate, sodium permanganate, nitrate, ruthenium nitrate, ruthenium chloride, platinum nitrate, chloroplatinic acid, palladium nitrate, tungsten nitrate, gallium nitrate, manganese nitrate, sodium sulfate, potassium sulfate, lithium sulfate, rubidium sulfate, magnesium sulfate, copper sulfate, preferably, the catalyst auxiliary agent salt comprises trisodium citrate. citric acid, tripotassium phosphate, citric acid lithium, sodium nitrate, potassium nitrate, lithium nitrate, rubidium nitrate, magnesium nitrate, copper nitrate, zinc nitrate, zinc sulfate, zirconium sulfate, gallium sulfate, manganese sulfate, manganese acetate, zinc acetate, potassium permanganate, sodium permanganate, zirconium nitrate, ruthenium nitrate, ruthenium chloride, platinum nitrate, chloroplatinic acid, palladium nitrate, more preferably, assistant salt of the catalyst comprises: trisodium citrate, tripotassium phosphate, citric acid lithium, sodium nitrate, potassium nitrate, lithium nitrate, rubidium nitrate, magnesium nitrate, copper nitrate, zinc nitrate, zinc sulfate, zirconium sulfate, gallium sulfate, manganese sulfate, manganese acetate, zinc acetate, and potassium permanganate. In the step (ii), the solvent comprises methanol, ethanol, propanol, acetone, hexane, cyclohexane, cyclohexanone, diethyl ether, propane, water, ethylene glycol, preferably, the solvent comprises ethanol, propanol, acetone, hexane, and glycol. In the step (iii), the mass fraction of the iron-based active metal catalyst in the total solid C is 10-90 wt.%, preferably, the mass fraction of the auxiliary agent in the total solid C is greater than or equal to 50 wt.%, further preferably, the mass fraction of auxiliary agent in the total amount of solid C is greater than or equal to 20 wt.% preferably, the drying temperature is 80-200 degrees C, preferably, the drying temperature is 80-150 degrees C. In the step (c), the active metal iron salt includes ferric nitrate, ferrous nitrate, and ferric chloride , Ferrous chloride, ferric citrate, ferrocene, ferric sulfate, ferrous sulfate, preferably, ferric nitrate, ferrous nitrate, ferric chloride, ferrous chloride, ferric citrate, preferably, in the step (d), the precipitating agent includes: sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate, lithium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, potassium bicarbonate, and lithium bicarbonate; preferably, the precipitating agent is: sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate, preferably, the rotation speed of the stirring blade is 100-1000 rpm, preferably, the rotation speed is 500-800 revolutions/minute. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the drying temperature of the precursor of the iron-based active metal catalyst is 80-200 degrees C, preferably, the drying temperature of the precursor of the iron-based active metal catalyst is 100-150 degrees C. In the step (iv), the activation step comprises: first reducing solid C to iron carbide, oxide, the activation temperature is 200-600 degrees C, preferably, in the activation step, the gas used for reduction includes: hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, methane, synthesis gas, preferably in the step (v), the particle size of the catalyst E obtained by grinding is 100-500 meshes, preferably, in the step (vi), the shaking time for mixing is 1-15 hours, preferably 3-5 hours. When the iron-based active metal catalyst is a ferric oxide catalyst, the method for preparing a precursor of the ferric oxide catalyst is: (a) controlling the temperature of the water bath between 50-90 degrees C, (b) heating the reactor in the water bath, (c) dissolving active metal iron salt with deionized water to obtain an active metal iron salt solution, (d) dissolving the precipitating agent with deionized water to obtain the precipitating agent solution, (e) turning on the stirring blade in the reactor, and (f) adding active metal iron salt solution and the precipitant solution in dropwise to the reactor at the same time, and the pH is adjusted to 4-10, and the obtained precipitate is the precursor of the ferric oxide catalyst. In the step (i), the iron-based catalyst, the active metal catalyst is ferroferric oxide precursor for preparing method of ferroferric oxide catalyst as: the active metal iron salt is dissolved in the solvent to obtain the active metal iron salt solution, precipitator is dissolved in the solvent to obtain precipitator solution, the active metal iron salt solution and precipitator solution into the high pressure kettle, stirring, carrying out crystallization at 100-300 degrees C in a high-pressure kettle, the obtained solid washed to obtain the precursor of ferroferric oxide catalyst, preferably, the active metal iron salt includes: ferric nitrate, ferrous nitrate, ferric chloride, ferrous chloride, ferric citrate, ferrocene, ferric sulfate, ferrous sulfate, preferably, the active metal iron salt includes: ferric nitrate, ferrous nitrate, citrate, ferrocene, ferric sulfate, ferrous sulfate, preferably, the solvent comprises: methanol, ethanol, propanol, acetone, hexane, cyclohexane, cyclohexanone, diethyl ether, propylene oxide, water, preferably, the solvent is ethanol, propanol, acetone, hexane, preferably, precipitator comprises sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate, lithium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, potassium bicarbonate, lithium bicarbonate, preferably, the precipitator is: sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate, preferably, the solvent comprises methanol, ethanol, propanol, acetone, hexane, cyclohexane, cyclohexanone, diethyl ether, propane, water, preferably, the solvent comprises ethanol, propanol, acetone, preferably, the stirring time is 10-300 minutes, preferably, the stirring time is 20-100 minutes, preferably, the crystallization time is 2-50 hours, more preferably, the crystallization time is 5-20 hours. The method for acclimatizing the anaerobic microorganism mud, comprises the nutrient is dissolved in the water to obtain the nutrient solution, placing in the nutrient solution in the anaerobic microorganism, under the temperature of 10-100 degrees C, culturing and domesticating the anaerobic microorganism, supplementing the nutrient solution to maintain anaerobic microorganism and vital sign, obtaining after domestication of anaerobic microorganism, preferably, the nutrient comprises glucose, fructose, lactose, sucrose, maltose, urea, glycine, alanine, phenylalanine, threonine, preferably, the nutrient comprises: glucose, fructose, lactose, glycin, alanine, preferably, the temperature is 20-50 degrees C, preferably, the nutrient comprises glucose, fructose, lactose, sucrose, maltose, urea, glycine, alanine, phenylalanine, threonine, preferably, the nutrient is glucose, fructose, urea, glycine, alanine and phenylalanine.

TI Continuously producing furandicarboxylic acid using furan as raw material comprises heating furan as raw material to form gaseous furan, mixing gaseous furan and carbon dioxide, fixing transition metal-supported catalyst, and reacting.

   RUAN J
   WANG S
   CHEN X
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG QUZHOU RES INST (UYZH-C)
   UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 202046559M
AB    NOVELTY - Continuously producing furandicarboxylic acid using furan as raw material comprises heating furan as raw material to form gaseous furan, mixing gaseous furan and carbon dioxide in a molar ratio of 1:3-5 to form a mixed raw material gas, entering the mixed raw material gas into fixed bed reactor from top of fixed bed reactor for reaction, fixing transition metal-supported catalyst in fixed-bed reactor, where the mass ratio of transition metal-supported catalyst and total amount of furan to be reacted is 5-10%, where reacting in fixed bed reactor at 100-120 degrees C at normal pressure and the residence time of gaseous furan in the fixed bed reactor is 100-150 minutes, discharging unreacted gaseous furan and carbon dioxide from bottom gas outlet of fixed bed reactor, mixing with mixed raw material gas, entering into fixed-bed reactor from top of fixed-bed reactor for reaction, and discharging furandicarboxylic acid produced by reaction from outlet at bottom of reactor.
   USE - The method is useful for continuously producing furandicarboxylic acid using furan as raw material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple to process, and environmentally friendly; and has high yield.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Method: The preparation method of the transition metal-supported catalyst is sequentially performing (i) dissolving the soluble salt of transition metal in water to obtain transition metal salt solution, dispersing the molecular sieve in water to obtain a dispersion, mixing transition metal salt solution and dispersion under stirring conditions for 3-6 hours, allowing to stand for 1-2 hours, where the weight ratio of the transition metal in soluble salt and molecular sieve is 1-1.6:100, and (ii) calcining the precipitate obtained in the step (i) at 400-600 degrees C for 3-5 hours to obtain the finished product. Preferred Components: The transition metals comprise nickel, copper, rhodium and palladium. The molecular sieve is 4A type, X type, Y type, ZSM-5 type, aluminum oxide, silicon dioxide and zirconium dioxide.
