TI Freeze-dried powder plant extraction process comprises e.g. mixing ionic liquid and common organic extract, passing carbon dioxide, then mixing with plant powder, microwave-assisted extracting, extracting, concentrating and freeze-drying.
AU YE D
AE DONGGUAN XUANMO BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (DONG-Non-standard)
GA 202040451Q
AB    NOVELTY - Freeze-dried powder plant extraction process comprises mixing ionic liquid and common organic extract to obtain layered synergistic extract, and pretreating the plant to prepare plant powder, passing carbon dioxide into the ionic liquid to obtain highly polar ionic liquid, then mixing with plant powder, microwave-assisted extracting, stop the flow of carbon dioxide, converting the highly polar ionic liquid back to the ionic liquid to obtain extract, and separating into organic layer and ionic liquid layer, removing the solvent in the organic layer to obtain organic matter, adding deionized water into the ionic liquid layer, shaking thoroughly, and extracting the hydrophilic substance to obtain water body fluid, concentrating to density of 1.5-2 g/cm3, concentrating water extract, freeze-drying the concentrated water extract and organic matter, and mixing the prepared lyophilized powder evenly to obtain plant-extracted lyophilized powder.
   USE - The method is useful for freeze-dried powder plant extraction.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: extracts the components of plants; maintains the excellent quality of the product; ensures faster drying rate; reduces moisture; and extends the shelf life the product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for plant extract freeze-dried powder prepared by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The common organic extract is ethyl acetate, methyl acetate, petroleum ether, cyclohexane, n-hexane, acetone and/or ether. The ionic liquid includes pair of imidazole cations and hydrophobic anion, where the diimidazole cation is of formula (I), and the hydrophobic anions are bistriflimide, hexafluorophosphate and/or triflate. The highly polar ionic liquid includes hydrophobic anion and zwitterionic bisimidazole prepared by reaction with bisimidazole cation, where the biimidazole zwitterion is of formula (I).R1 = 1-3C; andR2 = 1-20C alkyl group.Preferred Method: The pretreatment of plant comprising washing the plant, drying, crushing, sieving, immersing in petroleum ether for 0.5 hours, filtering, and naturally drying the filter residue to obtain plant powder. The microwave-assisted extraction condition are: temperature is 40-60 degrees C, microwave power is 500-1000 W, and extraction time is 1-3 hours. The method for removing the solvent in the organic layer is natural volatilization, reduced-pressure distillation, or reduced-pressure suspension distillation. The freeze-drying conditions are maintaining the temperature at -10 degrees C for 10-30 minutes, and then continuously lowering to -15 degrees C, freeze-drying for 2-5 hours, and continuously cooling to -25 degrees C, and freeze-drying for 10-15 hours. The method for converting the high-polarity synergistic extraction liquid back to the synergistic extraction liquid is heating to 40-100 degrees C.

PT P
PN CN110776923-A
TI Repairing agent useful for treating soil contaminated by heavy metals cadmium and lead, comprises limestone, water, palm fruit, salt, organic fertilizer, binder, and biomass carbon.
AU FANG T
   TAO J
   LV H
   SUN F
   SUN H
AE LAIWU VOCATIONAL & TECH COLLEGE (LAIW-Non-standard)
GA 202015695B
AB    NOVELTY - Repairing agent useful for treating soil contaminated by heavy metals cadmium and lead, comprises limestone, water, palm fruit, salt, organic fertilizer, binder, and biomass carbon.
   USE - The agent is useful for treating soil contaminated by heavy metals cadmium and lead (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The agent: improves soil pH, buffering of the soil against lead and cadmium; reduces the degree of soil compaction; increases the hydrophilic capacity of the soil.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the repairing agent, comprising using the heavy metal cadmium, where the repairing agent for lead compound contaminated soil mainly includes (i) calcining limestone at high temperature to obtain lime and carbon dioxide, (ii) preparing palm oil from palm fruit, (iii) preparing stearic acid from palm oil, (iv) preparing sodium hydroxide from common salt, and(v) using raw materials for preparing soil remediation agent, (a) mixing the obtained lime, stearic acid and sodium hydroxide, adding water, and stirring for 1-2 hours, (b) passing carbon dioxide into the mixed solution, stirring and mixing for 0.5-1.5 hours, (c) adding carbon dioxide, adding biomass carbon, organic fertilizer and binder for mixing, (d) adding mixed raw materials to a granulator, and granulating to obtain soil repairing agent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The repairing agent for lead compound contaminated soil comprises 5-10 pts. wt. limestone, 5-10 pts. wt. water, 10-20 pts. wt. palm fruit, 12-17 pts. wt. salt, 2-4 pts. wt. organic fertilizer, 1-5 pts. wt. binder, and 30-50 pts. wt. biomass carbon. The binder is acrylic resin curable hot-melt adhesive, bentonite, and/or attapulgite. In the step (a) and (c), the equipment used in the mixing operation is three-dimensional mixer. Preferred Method: In the step (i), calcining the limestone at high temperature to obtain lime and carbon dioxide mainly includes the following steps (a) crushing limestone by a crusher, and passing the crushed limestone through sieve of 100-150 meshes for sieving operation, (b) placing sieved limestone in a rotary kiln for calcining at 700-1100 degrees C for 1.5-2 hours, and then collecting lime and carbon dioxide, and sieving the lime through 100-150 mesh sieve. In the step (ii), the palm oil from palm fruit is prepared by (c) picking fresh palm fruit bunches, sending to the virgin mill and cooking at 150 degrees C for 2 hours until the fruit bunches are boiling, and(d) squeezing, filtering, and passing the filtered liquid through butterfly centrifugal separator to obtain palm oil, and refining the palm oil to obtain palm oil, then separating the fruit by bunching-up and peeling machine, and retaining the fruit, and placing the palm fruit into the screw press for pressing. In the step (iii), the stearic acid from palm oil is prepared by (e) adding palm oil, hydrolyzing agents stearic acid cresol sulfonic acid, and naphthalene sulfonic acid stearate to the hydrolysis pot, steaming, when the oil layer is transparent, adding water, heating and boiling for 7-8 hours, and then clarifying for 0.5-1 hours, then separating the lower layer of glycerin water, (f) adding hydrolyzing agent, repeating the step (e), and adding salt and water for washing and clarifying for 0.5 hour, repeating the operation for multiple times, where each clarification time increases by 0.5 hour, and taking the upper layer of mixed fatty acids, and(g) adding the mixed fatty acid to the distillation kettle for vacuum distilling, where the kettle temperature is 250-255 degrees C, the tower temperature is 210-220 degrees C, top temperature is 200-210 degrees C, and the residual pressure at the top of the tower is 0.53-0.93 kpa to obtain stearic acid. In the step (iv), the sodium hydroxide from common salt is prepared by (h) adding salt to water and uniformly stirring until the salt cannot be dissolved, removing the upper liquid to obtain saturated saline solution, and(i) electrolyzing the saturated common salt solution in a diaphragm electrolyzer to further obtain sodium hydroxide.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. PN US2020023307-A1; US11298653-B2
TI Method for separating target species e.g. carbon            dioxide from gas mixture, involves introducing gas            stream that provides target species to electrochemical            cell to bond target species to electroactive species to            produce treated gas stream.
AU VOSKIAN S
   HATTON T A
AE MASSACHUSETTS INST TECHNOLOGY (MASI-C)
   MASSACHUSETTS TECHNOLOGY INST (MASI-C)
GA 202009096V
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves applying a first potential                difference across an electrochemical cell (100).                The electrochemical cell is comprised of a porous                negative electrode (110) that comprises a first                electroactive species. A positive electrode (120)                comprises a second electroactive species. A                separator (130) is saturated with a conductive                liquid and positioned between the porous negative                electrode and the positive electrode. A gas stream                is introduced which comprises a target species to                the electrochemical cell to bond the target species                to the first electroactive species to produce a                treated gas stream. The target species comprises                carbon dioxide. The first electroactive species                comprises polyanthraquinone.
   USE - Method for separating target species e.g.                carbon dioxide from gas mixture e.g. gas                stream.
   ADVANTAGE - The risk of electrodes drying is reduced, and                the flow of gas pass the electrodes are allowed                without significant loss to evaporation or                entrainment. The doubling of gas-adsorbing area                exposed to the gas is provided. The original                pressure in the chamber is restored.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows an exploded view of the                electrochemical cell.
   Electrochemical cell (100)
   Negative electrode (110)
   Gas permeable layer (112)
   Positive electrode (120)
   Separator (130)

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN US2020010786-A1
TI Enhanced alcoholic beverage composition comprises at least one cannabis plant derived compounds infused in an alcoholic beverage.
AU DECARO M
   WEI G
AE DECARO M (DECA-Individual)
   WEI G (WEIG-Individual)
GA 202003690B
AB    NOVELTY - An enhanced alcoholic beverage composition comprises greater than or equal to 1 cannabis plant derived compounds infused in an alcoholic beverage.
   USE - An enhanced alcoholic beverage composition.
   ADVANTAGE - The extracted plant-based compounds directly react with alcoholic beverage in situ to result in enhanced alcoholic beverage compositions in a short time period. After infusing with alcoholic beverage, the extracted compounds provide respective medicinal, aroma, and flavor profiles to the alcoholic beverage compositions. At the end of the process, carbon dioxide is removed easily without leaving any unwanted/toxic residues in the enhanced alcoholic beverage compositions. The entire process is carried out in an oxygen free environment, which prevents oxidation damages to the compounds and alcoholic beverage at elevated temperature.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) method of enhanced alcoholic beverage infusion which involves pretreating cannabis plant material and contacting pretreated cannabis plant material with alcoholic beverages in the presence of subcritical/critical/supercritical carbon dioxide to obtain enhanced alcoholic beverage composition; and
   (2) method of enhanced alcoholic beverage infusion which involves pretreating cannabis plant material, pretreating oak wood, and contacting pretreated cannabis plant material and pretreated oak wood with alcoholic beverages in the presence of subcritical/critical/supercritical carbon dioxide to obtain enhanced alcoholic beverage composition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Component: Alcoholic beverage is tequila, whiskey, scotch, gin, brandy, vodka, rum, bourbon, wine, and beer. Preferred Processes: Aged alcoholic beverage is aged from 1 day to 30 years in an oak wood barrel. Pretreating cannabis plant material involves toasting or baking the cannabis plant materials at 100-300 degrees C for 10 minutes to 6 hours. Pretreating oak wood involves toasting or baking the oak wood 60-500 degrees C for 5 minutes to 96 hours. Contacting pretreated cannabis plant materials and/or oak wood with alcoholic beverages in the presence of subcritical/critical/supercritical carbon dioxide involves soaking with alcoholic beverages in subcritical/critical/supercritical carbon dioxide for a period of time; returning the subcritical/critical/supercritical carbon dioxide to a non-subcritical/critical/supercritical state; and releasing carbon dioxide. Contacting pretreated cannabis plant and/or oak wood with alcoholic beverages in the presence of subcritical/critical/supercritical carbon dioxide is repeated one or more times. Preferred Conditions: Toasting or baking the cannabis plant materials is performed in an oxygen free environment. During contacting step, carbon dioxide is in subcritical/critical/supercritical status with a pressure of 50-600 bar and temperature of 20-150 degrees C, and period of contacting time is 5 minutes to 24 hours. During contacting step, the ratio (w/v) of pretreated cannabis plant to alcoholic beverage is 1:10-1:10000. During contacting step, ratio (w/v) of pretreated wood to alcoholic beverage is 1:10-1:1000.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Cannabis plant derived compounds are cannabidiol (CBD), Delta 9-tetrahydrocannabinol (THC), and/or terpenes. Composition contains 0.00001-5% (w/v%), preferably 0.001-0.5% (w/v%) cannabis plant derived compounds with respect to alcoholic beverage. Composition further comprises 0.00005-5% (w/v%), preferably 0.005-0.5% (w/v%) oak wood derived compounds with respect to alcoholic beverages. Cannabis plant material is selected from buds, flowers, leaves, stems, stalks, seeds, and/or roots.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

TI Freshness-retaining polyester film used for forming layers of laminate, consists of polyester-based resin layer comprising ethylene terephthalate as main component, and has preset oxygen permeability and carbon dioxide permeability.
AU ISHIMARU S
   MARUYAMA H
   HASHIDA T
   HARUTA M
AE TOYOBO KK (TOYM-C)
   TOYOBO KK (TOYM-C)
   TOYOBO KK (TOYM-C)
   TOYOBO KK (TOYM-C)
   TOYO BOSEKI KK (TOYM-C)
GA 2019A61827
AB    NOVELTY - A freshness-retaining polyester film consists of a polyester-based resin layer comprising ethylene terephthalate as a main component. The freshness-retaining polyester film has an oxygen permeability of 50-1000 (cc/(m2.d.atm)), when measured in an environment having a temperature of 23 degrees C and a relative humidity of 65 %RH, a carbon dioxide permeability of 200-5000 (cc/(m2.d.atm)), when measured in an environment having a temperature of 23 degrees C, a water vapor permeability of 20-400 (g/(m2.d)), when measured in an environment having a temperature of 40 degrees C and a relative humidity of 90 %RH, and a fold retention angle of 20-80 degrees .
   USE - Freshness-retaining polyester film is used for forming layers of laminate used as cover material of package (all claimed) for industrial commodity, pharmaceutical, and foodstuff. Uses include but are not limited to broccoli, spinach, melon, cherry, octopus, squid, salmon, flatfish, pork, beef, chicken meat, mutton, etc.
   ADVANTAGE - The freshness-retaining polyester film has excellent carbon dioxide permeability, oxygen permeability, water vapor permeability, and resealability, while extending the shelf life of fruits and vegetables.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The total content of monomer components capable of forming amorphous components other than ethylene terephthalate is 12-30 mol% with respect to all monomer components contained in the polyester-based resin of the polyester film. Preferred Properties: The ratio of carbon dioxide permeability with respect to oxygen permeability of the freshness-retaining polyester film is 2-8. The freshness-retaining polyester film has a weld-cut sealing strength of 5-60 N/15 mm, a tensile elasticity modulus along longitudinal direction or width direction of 2000-4500 MPa, and a thickness of 3-70 mu m.

PN CN110404567-A; CN110404567-B
TI Photocatalytic energy conversion material useful in photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction and photocatalytic decomposition of water into hydrogen.
AU WANG Y
   HAN C
AE UNIV NAT DEFENSE TECHNOLOGY (UNDT-C)
GA 2019943706
AB    NOVELTY - Photocatalytic energy conversion material comprises silicon carbide nano-sheets, where the surface of silicon carbide nano-sheet is loaded with carbon-coated iron nanoparticle promoter, the carbon-coated iron nanoparticle promoter having core-shell nanostructure, the inside of which is iron nanoparticle, and is surrounded by carbon layer of graphene-type structure.
   USE - The energy conversion material is useful: in photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction and photocatalytic decomposition of water into hydrogen (claimed); as inexpensive and high-efficiency photocatalytic promoter instead of precious metal platinum.
   ADVANTAGE - The energy conversion material: has excellent catalytic effect, high conversion efficiency for reducing carbon dioxide and high efficiency for decomposing hydrogen from water, and excellent performance than existing platinum nano-particles/silicon carbide nano-sheets.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing photocatalytic energy conversion material, comprising (i) adding organic iron molecular material into graphene oxide solution, ultrasonically mixing uniformly, quickly cooling using liquid nitrogen, freeze-molding and drying to obtain three-dimensional aerogel material, (ii) heating the three-dimensional aerogel material under nitrogen atmosphere, performing heat preservation, and carbonization treating, (iii) placing the carbonized product in dilute sulfuric acid, stirring, pickling, and removing uncoated iron components, and (iv) placing the pickled product of silicon source in a graphite crucible, heating under argon atmosphere to cause carbon thermal conversion reaction, naturally cooling to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The number of layers of carbon layer coated around the iron nano-particles is 3-10 layers, and the overall size is 20-40 nm. In step (i), the organic iron molecular material is iron acetylacetonate, ferrocene, ferrous acetate, iron citrate and/or ferrous lactate, the concentration of graphene oxide solution is 4-6 mg/ml, preferably 5 mg/ml. In step (iii), the concentration of dilute sulfuric acid is 0.4-0.6 M, preferably 0.5 M, and the stirring reaction time is 9-11 hour, preferably 10 hour. In step (iv), the silicon source is silicon powder and/or silicon oxide powder, and the temperature of carbon thermal conversion reaction is 1300-1500 degrees C, preferably 1400 degrees C, the reaction time is 1.5-2.5 hours, preferably 1 hour. Preferred Method: In step (ii), the heating rate is 1-5 degrees C/minute, preferably 2 degrees C/minute, the carbonization temperature is 750-850 degrees C, preferably 800 degrees C, and the heat preservation time is 1.5-2.5 hours, preferably 2 hours.

AU CHEN C
AE XIAN KAIYUAN HEZHONG ELECTROMECHANICAL (XIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201954555S
AB    NOVELTY - Temperature control device comprises a rotary table, a machine body set on the rotary table, and a welding handle. A liquid carbon dioxide tank is set on one side of the machine body. A temperature measuring gun is mounted on one side of the machine body. A temperature controller is installed on one side of the machine body. The liquid carbon dioxide tank is connected to a temperature control gun hinged on a welding rod through a conveying pipe. A nozzle is welded to one side of the temperature control gun.    XING Y
AE XING Z (XING-Individual)
GA 201891457U
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon sinks per unit of forest land detecting method measuring the oxygen concentration value of the forest land according to a preset detection interval in a complete annual cycle, obtaining a difference in oxygen concentration on the day according to the measured oxygen concentration value at 0 o'clock and the oxygen concentration value at 24 o'clock, adding a difference in oxygen concentration per day during a complete year to obtain a sum of oxygen concentration differences, when the sum of oxygen concentration differences is positive, the sum of oxygen concentration differences is the total concentration of oxygen released by the plant, the total mass of oxygen according to the air volume on the unit forest area, the sum of the oxygen concentration differences and the oxygen density, the carbon dioxide quality according to total oxygen mass, carbon dioxide molecular weight and oxygen molecular weight, when the carbon dioxide mass is positive to obtain the finished product.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for preparing the plant derived complex tea beverage, which involves mixing black tea, kudzu-vine root, yam, jujube, lily, medlar and endothelium corneum gigeriae galli, soaking the mixture in water, then extracting, filtering to obtain the first extract, adding water to the residue, again extracting, filtering to obtain the second extract, mixing the first extract with the second extract, centrifuging the mixed extract, collecting the supernatant, adding 2000 pts. wt. water, adding xylitol, erythritol, citric acid, malic acid, potassium sorbate and lemon flavor to the supernatant, heating the mixture at 90 degrees C, sterilizing the mixture, cooling, feeding the solution with 2 times carbon dioxide, filling in a bottle and capping the bottle.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Product: The beverage is prepared using 40-80 pts. wt., preferably 60 pts. wt. black tea, 28-44 pts. wt., preferably 36 pts. wt. kudzu-vine root, 24-40 pts. wt., preferably 36 pts. wt. yam, 12-28 pts. wt., preferably 20 pts. wt. jujube, 4-20 pts. wt., preferably 12 pts. wt. lily, 4-20 pts. wt., preferably 12 pts. wt. medlar, 4-20 pts. wt., preferably 12 pts. wt. endothelium corneum gigeriae galli, 40-80 pts. wt., preferably 60 pts. wt. xylitol, 10-30 pts. wt., preferably 20 pts. wt. erythritol, 1-5 pts. wt., preferably 3 pts. wt. potassium sorbate, 0.2-0.6 pt. wt., preferably 0.4 pt. wt. citric acid, 0.02-0.06 pt. wt., preferably 0.04 pt. wt. malic acid, lemon flavor, 0.4-0.12 pt. wt., preferably 0.8 pt. wt. water and 2000 pts. wt. carbon dioxide, where the amount of carbon dioxide added is twice the volume of water. Preferred Method: The preparation method involves mixing black tea, kudzu-vine root, yam, jujube, lily, medlar and endothelium corneum gigeriae galli, soaking the mixture in 8 times water at 20-30 degrees C for 1 hour, then extracting at 100 degrees C under normal pressure for 2 hours, filtering through 300 mesh gauze to obtain the first extract, adding 6 times water to the residue, again extracting at 100 degrees C under normal pressure for 1.5 hours, filtering through 300 mesh gauze to obtain the second extract, mixing the first extract with the second extract, centrifuging the mixed extract at 10-30 degrees C and 15000 revolutions per minute for 10 minutes,, collecting the supernatant, adding 2000 pts. wt. water, adding xylitol, erythritol, citric acid, malic acid, potassium sorbate and lemon flavor to the supernatant, heating the mixture, sterilizing the mixture at 165 degrees C for 10 seconds, cooling, feeding the solution with carbon dioxide, filling in a bottle and capping the bottle.
   ACTIVITY - Antidiabetic.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. AE WANHUA CHEM GROUP CO LTD (WHCH-C)

GA 201853944D
AB    NOVELTY - Thermoplastic polyurethane foam particles comprise 65-90 wt.%, preferably 80-85 wt.% thermoplastic polyurethane elastomer; 2-30 wt.%, preferably 10-15 wt.% thermoplastic resin polymer; and 0-15 wt.%, preferably 5-10 wt.% compatibilizer.
   USE - Thermoplastic polyurethane foam particles.
   ADVANTAGE - The foam particles have particle size stability and do not shrink, and have full and bright surface, uniform cell diameter, controllable cell size, and good resilience.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of polyurethane foam particles by:
   (A) uniformly mixing thermoplastic polyurethane elastomer, thermoplastic resin polymer and compatibilizer, and granulating to obtain polyurethane alloy particles; adding polyurethane alloy particles to high-pressure kettle, introducing carbon dioxide in liquid or supercritical state, and impregnating polyurethane alloy particles; cooling high-pressure kettle to less than or equal to 40 degrees C and discharging kettle pressure to normal pressure to obtain non-foam polyurethane alloy particles in which carbon dioxide is dissolved; and heating and foaming non-foam particles in hot air; and
   (B) uniformly mixing thermoplastic polyurethane elastomer, thermoplastic resin polymer and compatibilizer, and granulating to obtain polyurethane alloy particles; adding polyurethane alloy particles to high-pressure kettle, introducing carbon dioxide in liquid or supercritical state, and impregnating polyurethane alloy particles; and discharging material in the high-pressure kettle to atmospheric environment by releasing pressure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The thermoplastic polyurethane elastomer has Shore hardness of 60A-90A. The thermoplastic polyurethane elastomer is polycarbonate polyurethane, polylactone polyurethane, and/or preferably polyether polyurethane and/or polyester polyurethane. The thermoplastic resin polymer is low-melting-point polyester, thermoplastic polyamide, polyethylene, and/or preferably thermoplastic polyester elastomer, polybutylene terephthalate (PBT), polypropylene, and/or polystyrene. The thermoplastic polyester elastomer has melting point of 170-230 degrees C, preferably 190-220 degrees C. The low-melting-point polyester has melting point of 120-240 degrees C, preferably 185-220 degrees C. The thermoplastic polyamide has melting point of 180-240 degrees C, preferably 190-220 degrees C. The PBT has intrinsic viscosity of 0.55- 1.5 dL/g, preferably 0.8-1 dL/g. The polyethylene is low-density polyethylene, linear low-density polyethylene, and/or preferably high-density polyethylene. The polypropylene is polypropylene homopolymer, polypropylene block copolymer, and/or preferably polypropylene random copolymer. The polystyrene is high-impact polystyrene and/or preferably general-purpose polystyrene. The compatibilizer is polypropylene grafted maleic anhydride polymer, hydrogenated styrene-butadiene-styrene (SBS) block copolymer grafted maleic anhydride polymer, SBS block copolymer grafted maleic anhydride polymer, polystyrene grafted maleic anhydride polymer, and/or preferably ethylene-octylene copolymer grafted maleic anhydride polymer, polyethylene grafted maleic anhydride polymer, and/or ethylene propylene diene rubber grafted maleic anhydride polymer. The compatibilizer has maleic anhydride content of 0.5-5 wt.%, preferably 0.8-1.5 wt.%. The particles have density of 0.06-0.4 g/cm3 and pore diameter of 10-100 mu m.

   ADVANTAGE - The polyurethane adhesive improves the water resistance and viscosity of existing waterborne polyurethane adhesives, has good abrasion resistance and adhesion, good water resistance, releases high concentrations of negative oxygen ions, improves the environment and promotes body health.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing polyurethane adhesive, comprising (a) taking carbon dioxide copolymer polyol, polyether polyol, castor oil, aliphatic diisocyanates, alicyclic diisocyanates, chain extenders, ,catalysts and aqueous hydroxy acrylic dispersions, mixing uniformly and reacting to obtain adhesive prepolymers; and (b) mixing the adhesive prepolymers with chain extender crosslinker, negative oxygen ion powder and water and reacting.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The polyurethane adhesive comprises 5-7 pts. wt. negative oxygen ion powder, 55-60 pts. wt. carbon dioxide copolymer polyol, 40-45 pts. wt. polyether polyol, 20-25 pts. wt. castor oil, 10-15 pts. wt. aliphatic diisocyanate, 40-50 pts. wt. cycloaliphatic diisocyanate, 10-20 pts. wt. chain extender, 3-8 pts. wt. chain extending crosslinker, 0.1-0.5 pts. wt. catalyst and 250-300 pts. wt. water. Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide copolymer polyol has a molecular weight of 2000-6000 g/mol, preferably 3000-4000 g/mol. The carbon dioxide copolymer polyol has a hydroxy functionality of 2-4, preferably 3-4. The carbon dioxide copolymer polyol has an intramolecular carbonate group molar fraction of 0.2-0.4, preferably 0.3-0.4. The negative oxygen ion powder comprises 30-40 wt.%, preferably 35.1 wt.% aluminum trioxide, 30-40 wt.%, preferably 34.81 wt.% silica, 8-14 wt.%, preferably 11.02 wt.% boron trioxide, 3-6 wt.%, preferably 4.7 wt.% magnesium oxide, 8-12 wt.%, preferably 10.18 wt.% iron oxide, 0.8-1.2 wt.%, preferably 0.91 wt.% sodium oxide, 0.02-0.06 wt.%, preferably 0.04 wt.% potassium oxide, 0.18-0.25 wt.%, preferably 0.22 wt.% phosphorus pentoxide, 0.25-0.3 wt.%, preferably 0.26 wt.% titanium dioxide, 1.2-1.5 wt.%, preferably 1.35 wt.% ferrous oxide and 0.01-0.05 wt.%, preferably 1.41 wt.% calcium oxide. The raw material of the aqueous negative oxygen ion polyurethane adhesive also includes 40-60 pts. wt. aqueous hydroxy acrylic dispersion, preferably, the aqueous hydroxyacrylic acid dispersion is prepared from methyl methacrylate, n-butyl acrylate, acrylic acid, hydroxyethyl methacrylate and dimethylethanolamine in a mass ratio of 1:1:2:1:4. The chain extender is sodium ethylenediamine ethanesulfonate, 1,4-butanediol-2- sulfonate, dihydroxymethyl butyrate half ester, dimethylolpropionic acid, dihydroxymethyl butyric acid, ethylene glycol, butylene glycol, diethylene glycol, dipropylene glycol, 1,6-hexanediol, 1,3-propanediol, 1,2-propanediol, 1,4-dimethylolcyclohexane and trimethylpentanedio, preferably, the chain extension crosslinking agent is di-n-butylamine, ethylenediamine, trimethylhexamethylenediamine, tetraethylene pentamine, diethylenetriamine and/or isophorone diamines, preferably, the catalyst is N-methylmorpholine, N-ethylmorpholine, N,N,N',N'-tetramethylhexamethylenediamine, 1,2-dimethylimidazole stannous acetate, stannous octoate, stannous laurate, dibutyltin dilaurate, dibutyltin dimaleate, dioctyltin diacetate, and dibutyltin dichloride. Preferred Method: In the step (a), the reaction temperature is 70-85 degrees C. In the step (b), the reaction temperature is 20-40 degrees C.

GA 201881173K
AB    NOVELTY - The container has a absorption liquid recovery port which is connected to the carbon dioxide absorption tower at a position on the downstream side of the packing material in the flowing direction of the amine compound-containing absorption liquid from the absorption liquid introduction port toward the absorption liquid discharge port. The absorption liquid supply port is connected to the absorption tower at a position on the upstream side of the packing material. The second cooler includes a liquid supply port for supplying the liquid to be contacted with the combustion exhaust gas to the absorption tower. The liquid supply port and the liquid recovery port are connected to each other. The carbon dioxide absorption device is connected to the absorption tower at a position on the downstream side of the packing material in the flowing direction of the combustion exhaust gas from the combustion exhaust gas introduction port toward the combustion exhaust gas discharge port.
   USE - Container for carbon dioxide absorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The gas cooling, dust removal and desulfurization on combustion exhaust gas is efficiently performed. The contained in the combustion exhaust gas is efficiently recovered.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The container has a absorption tower which reacts and absorbs contained in the combustion exhaust gas into an absorption liquid containing an amine compound. One cooler selected from the group consisting of a first cooler for cooling an amine compound-containing absorption liquid and a second cooler for cooling a liquid different from the amine compound-containing absorption liquid. The absorption tower comprises a combustion exhaust gas introduction port for introducing the combustion exhaust gas into the absorption tower. A filler (30) is a stationary mixer having helical porous blades. The first cooler includes an absorption liquid recovery port for recovering the amine compound-containing absorption liquid from the absorption tower and an absorption liquid supply port for supplying the amine compound-containing absorption liquid to the absorption tower. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a carbon-dioxide absorption method involves recovering a portion of the amine compound-containing absorbing solution from the absorbing column at the position downstream of the packing material in the flowing direction of the amine compound-containing absorbing solution from the absorbing liquid introducing port toward the absorbing solution discharging port. A liquid is supplied to the absorption tower at a position on the downstream side of the packing material in the flow direction of the combustion exhaust gas flowing from the combustion exhaust gas introduction port toward the combustion exhaust gas discharge port to bring it into contact with the combustion exhaust gas;
   (2) an amine compound containing absorption liquid regenerating apparatus has second heater which comprises an absorption liquid recovery port for recovering the amine compound-containing absorption liquid from the diffusion column and a steam supply port for supplying the steam to the radiation tower. The steam supply port is connected to the stripping tower at a position on the downstream side of the packing material in the flowing direction of the amine compound-containing absorption liquid;
   (3) a amine compound containing absorption liquid regenerating method involves heating recovered amine compound-containing absorbing solution. The heated amine compound-containing absorbing solution is supplied to the stripping column at a position downstream of the packing in the flowing direction of the amine compound-containing absorbing solution. A portion of the amine compound-containing absorbing solution is recovered from the stripping column at a position on the downstream side of the packing material in the flowing direction of the amine compound-containing absorbing solution. The steam is supplied to the stripping tower from the steam introduction port;
   (4) an amine compound containing absorption liquid purifier has a gas-liquid separating apparatus which includes an absorption liquid introduction port for introducing an amine compound-containing absorption liquid, which is evaporated and separated from impurities. A gas discharge port is configured for discharging the gas-liquid separated gas. The decompression device is connected to the evaporation tank through an absorption liquid introduction port of the gas-liquid separation device, a gas discharge port of the gas-liquid separation device and an absorption liquid discharge port of the evaporation tank. The superheated steam prevention device is connected to the heating jacket;
   (5) an method for purifying an absorption liquid containing an amine compound involves supplying steam to a heating jacket arranged outside the evaporation tank to heat the evaporation tank;
   (6) a combustion exhaust gas preprocessor has a combustion exhaust gas introduction port which is arranged on the upstream side of the packing material in the flow direction of the desulfurization liquid heading from the desulfurization liquid introduction port toward the desulfurization liquid discharge port;
   (7) a method for pretreatment of combustion exhaust gas involves supplying combustion exhaust gas flows from the combustion exhaust gas introduction port to the combustion exhaust gas discharge port and the desulfurization liquid flows from the desulfurization liquid introduction port to the desulfurization liquid discharge port;
   (8) a carbon dioxide recovery apparatus has a absorption liquid discharge port of the radiation tower which is connected to the absorption liquid introduction port of the absorption tower through the first heat exchanger (72) and the second heat exchanger (73); and
   (9) a carbon-dioxide collection method involves introducing an amine compound-containing absorption liquid containing impurities generated in the carbon dioxide recovery method from the absorption liquid discharge port of the radiation tower into the evaporation tank at the absorption liquid introduction port of the evaporation tank. The amine compound-containing absorption liquid from which impurities have been removed is introduced into the absorption tower from the absorption liquid inlet of the absorption tower. The heat exchange between the amine compound-containing absorption liquid directed to the absorption liquid inlet of the radiation tower and the amine compound-containing absorption liquid flowing toward the absorption liquid inlet of the absorption tower is performed.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the carbon-dioxide collection method using carbon dioxide recovery apparatus. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

PN WO2022202848-A1
TI Acidic gas adsorbent used for adsorbing acidic            gases include carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide,            carbonyl sulfide, sulfur oxides, hydrogen cyanide, and            nitrogen oxides, comprises porous body and solid amine            compound supported on surface of pores of porous            body.
AU SHOTA T
   KOSO M
   CHISATO G
AE NITTO DENKO CORP (NITL-C)
GA 2022C4397W
AB    NOVELTY - Acidic gas adsorbent comprises a porous body                and a solid amine compound supported on the surface                of the pores of the porous body.
   USE - The acidic gas adsorbent is used in acidic gas                adsorption device (claimed). The acidic gas                adsorbent is used for adsorbing acidic gases                comprise carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide, carbonyl                sulfide, sulfur oxides, hydrogen cyanide, and                nitrogen oxides, preferably carbon dioxide in the                atmosphere.
   ADVANTAGE - The acidic gas adsorbent has improved heat                resistance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) (1) a method for producing the acidic gas                adsorbent, involving mixing a porous body with a                compound group containing at least one chosen from                amine monomers and amine prepolymers, and                contacting the compound group with the surfaces of                the pores of the porous body, and forming a solid                amine compound supported on the surface of the                pores of the porous body by reacting the compound                group; and
   (2) (2) an acidic gas adsorption device                comprising an adsorption section having a gas inlet                and a gas outlet, where the adsorption section                accommodates the acidic gas adsorbent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The porous body                  comprises silica Preferred Properties: The porous                  body has a pore volume of 2 cm3/g or more. The                  coating layer calculated from the average pore                  diameter of the porous body, the pore volume of                  the porous body, and the pore volume of the                  acidic gas adsorbent, assuming that the pores of                  the porous body are spherical, where the                  thickness of acidic gas adsorbent is 5 nm or                  less. The porous body has a particulate shape.                  The adsorption amount (A1) of carbon dioxide when                  contacted with a mixed gas composed of carbon                  dioxide, nitrogen and water vapor for 15 hours is                  0.1 mmol/g or more, where the concentration of                  carbon dioxide in the mixed gas is 400 vol ppm,                  and the mixed gas has a temperature of                  23&#176; C and a humidity of 50% RH. The                  ratio of the amount of carbon dioxide adsorbed                  (A1) (mmol/g) when in contact with a mixed gas                  comprising carbon dioxide, nitrogen and water                  vapor for 15 hours, to the carbon dioxide                  adsorption amount (A2) (mmol/g) when contacted                  with the mixed gas for 30 minutes is 50% or more,                  where the concentration of carbon dioxide in the                  mixed gas is 400 volppm, and the mixed gas has a                  temperature of 23&#176; C and a humidity of                  50% RH. The porous body has an average pore                  diameter of 5-50 nm. The porous body has a                  specific surface area of 50-800 m2/g.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The acidic gas                  adsorbent comprises a coating layer which coats                  the surface, where the coating layer comprises an                  amine compound. The amine compound comprises an                  amine polymer containing a structural unit                  derived from an epoxy monomer.
   EXAMPLE - SUNSPERA L-303(RTM: Porous silica) (3 g) was                prepared as a porous material, and immersed in 60 g                methanol overnight to prepare a dispersion liquid.                0.76 g triethylenetetramine as an amine monomer,                and 0.6 g 1,7-octadiene diepoxide as an epoxy                monomer, 0.15 g                1,3-bis(N,N-diglycidylaminomethyl)cyclohexane was                prepared. These monomers were then added to the                dispersion. The compound groups                (triethylenetetramine, 1,7-octadiene diepoxide and                1,3-bis(N,N-diglycidylaminomethyl)cyclohexane) were                penetrated into the pores of the porous body and                contacted the surfaces of the pores of the porous                body. Using a rotary evaporator, the dispersion was                heated at a temperature of 60&#176; C under a                reduced pressure atmosphere. The reaction of the                compound group proceeded, and the solvent contained                in the dispersion liquid was distilled off. The                porous body was dried by heating at a temperature                of 80&#176; C in a vacuum atmosphere to obtain                acidic gas adsorbent having a solid amine compound                was obtained.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); A21 (Epoxides; aminoplasts; phenoplasts.)

PN CN114974453-A
TI Atmospheric carbon dioxide column concentration            high coverage reconstruction method, involves            subjecting all modeling data in area to standard            spatial grid processing, and using built CO2 column            concentration prediction model for prediction.
AU JIN Z
   HE J
   WANG W
AE UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
GA 2022B57840
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves analyzing the sources of                atmospheric CO2 and changing influencing factors.                The data of CO2 column concentration products and                each change influencing factor are unified to the                same resolution. The data matching is performed.                The CO2 column concentration and each change Person                correlation is calculated between impact factors.                The selected change influencing factors are used to                form a modeling data set. The random forest model                is used to model the modeling data set. The model                parameters are adjusted to make the model                verification results optimal. The CO2 column                concentration prediction model is obtained. All the                modeling data in the area are subjected to standard                spatial grid processing, and the built CO2 column                concentration prediction model is used for                prediction. The CO2 column concentration data set                of all grid points in the area is obtained every                day.
   USE - Method for reconstructing high coverage degree                of atmosphere carbon dioxide (CO2) column.
   ADVANTAGE - The method obtains the daily-scale data of the                CO2 column concentration in the whole area.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of an                atmospheric carbon dioxide column concentration                high coverage reconstruction method. (Drawing                includes non-English language text).

TI Recycling concentrated salt water, comprises e.g.            concentrating the concentrated salt water produced by            treated wastewater, performing electrodialysis to            concentrated salt water, carrying out nanofiltration of            mixed solution, and collecting the hydrochloric acid            and sodium carbonate solution.

   XIONG F
   ZHOU C
   TONG M
   YANG H
   ZHANG X
   QIU M
AE BAOWU WATER TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BAOW-Non-standard)
GA 2022A95231
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling concentrated salt water, comprises                concentrating the concentrated salt water produced                by treated wastewater, making concentration of                concentrated salt water reaches predetermined                value, removing metal ions in concentrated salt                water, performing electrodialysis to concentrated                salt water to produce hydrochloric acid and sodium                hydroxide solution, contacting the sodium hydroxide                solution with flue gas after desulfurization and                denitrification, excess carbon dioxide in flue gas                chemically reacts with sodium hydroxide solution to                obtain mixed solution of sodium bicarbonate and                sodium carbonate, carrying out nanofiltration of                mixed solution to obtain separated sodium                bicarbonate solution and sodium carbonate solution,                collecting the hydrochloric acid and sodium                carbonate solution for further use, and returning                the sodium bicarbonate solution to sodium hydroxide                solution to absorb carbon dioxide in flue                gas.
   USE - The method is useful for recycling                concentrated salt water.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the production of sodium                chloride industrial salt, reduces the cost and                sales of salt production, and recycles the                concentrated salt water to produce sodium hydroxide                solution, absorbs carbon dioxide gas in exhaust                gas, thus reducing the carbon discharge and forming                carbon neutralization.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                concentrated salt water recycling treatment                system.

AE SHELL INT RES MIJ BV (SHEL-C)
GA 202249550M
AB    NOVELTY - The invention relates to a process for the production of ethylene by oxidative dehydrogenation (ODH) of ethane, comprising: a) supplying ethane and oxygen to a first ODH zone which is formed by multiple reactor tubes containing a mixed metal oxide ODH catalyst bed; b) contacting the ethane and oxygen with the catalyst resulting in multiple effluent streams, wherein the multiple reactor tubes are cooled by a coolant; c) mixing at least a portion of the multiple effluent streams from step b) resulting in a mixture comprising ethylene, unconverted ethane, unconverted oxygen, carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide and acetylene; d) supplying at least a portion of the mixture from step c) to a second ODH zone containing a mixed metal oxide ODH catalyst bed; e) contacting at least a portion of the mixture from step c) with the catalyst in the second ODH zone resulting in a stream comprising ethylene, unconverted ethane, carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide and acetylene; f) removing carbon monoxide and acetylene from at least a portion of the stream from step e) by means of oxidation resulting in a stream comprising ethylene, unconverted ethane and carbon dioxide.

PT P
PN EP3932543-A1; EP3932543-A4
TI Synthesizing metal oxide catalyst comprising active site containing metal oxide and a support for loading active site by using supercritical carbon dioxide extraction.
AU KIM J S
   HA H P
AE KOREA INST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (KSCT-C)
   KOREA ADV SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (KOAD-C)
GA 2022017216
AB    NOVELTY - A metal oxide catalyst comprising an active site containing greater than or equal to 1 type of metal oxide and a support for loading active site is synthesized by using supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) extraction.
   USE - Synthesized of metal oxide catalyst comprising an active site containing greater than or equal to 1 type of metal oxide and a support for loading active site (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The synthesis method for a heterogeneous catalyst using supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) extraction can provide an increased rate, conversion, or selectivity to selective activation reactions of nitrogen (N)-oxygen (O)/N-hydrogen (H)/carbon (C)-O/O-H bonds inherent in reactants compared to the existing catalyst preparation method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for metal oxide catalyst comprising an active site containing greater than or equal to 1 type of metal oxide; and a support onto which the active site is loaded, where the metal oxide is an oxide of a metal selected from transition metals (atomic number 21-29, 39-47, 72-79, or 104-108), lanthanide (atomic number 57-71), post-transition metals (atomic number 13, 30-31, 48-50, 80-84, and 112), and metalloids (atomic number 14, 32-33, 51-52, and 85) in the periodic table.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method includes precipitating a precursor of metal oxide catalyst crystalline grains onto a surface of a support after dissolving the precursor of the metal oxide catalyst crystalline grains in a synthetic solvent; drying a catalyst crystalline grain precursor-support intermediate product using supercritical CO2 extraction; and calcining the dried catalyst crystalline grain precursor-support intermediate product to synthesize a metal oxide catalyst. Supercritical CO2 extraction is performed at 50-150 degrees C for 0.1-24 hours, at a flow rate of 10-5-105 mL/minute, and at a CO2 pressure of 75-150 atm. Supercritical CO2 fluid extracted by the supercritical CO2 extraction weakens an interaction between the support and the synthetic solvent. Preferred Parameters: The active site is porous and has a diameter range of 0.1 nm-500 mu m. The support has microporosity, mesoporisity, macroporosity, or hierarchical porosity. Preferred Components: The active site has a composition of 10-4 to 50 pts. wt. based on 100 pts. wt. support. The support contains greater than or equal to 1 element selected from alkaline earth metals (atomic number 4, 12, 20, 38, 56, and 88), transition metals (atomic number 21-29, 39-47, and 72-79, or 104-108), lanthanide (atomic number 57-71), post-transition metals (atomic number 13, 30-31, 48-50, 80-84, and 112), and metalloids (atomic number 14, 32-33, 51-52, and 85) in the periodic table, and carbon (C). The support contains greater than or equal to 1 oxide of the element.
   EXAMPLE - The metal oxide catalyst was synthesized by dissolving 6.9 g sulfuric acid (98% H2SO4) in 37.5 mL distilled water, heating to 50 degrees C, adding 11.25 g titanium oxygen sulfate (TiOSO4), a titanium salt, dissolving for 30 minutes, adding 75 g urea (CO(NH2)2) and 500 mL distilled water, raising the temperature of mixture to 100 degrees C, stirring the mixture for 18 hours, cooling the formed intermediate product to 25 degrees C, filtering/washing with distilled water, exposing obtained solid for 30 minutes in a supercritical carbon dioxide (99.99%) obtained under the conditions of a temperature of 60-70 degrees C and a pressure of 90-100 atm, calcining at 400 degrees C for 3 hours to obtain a titanium oxide (TiO2) with hierarchical porosity having both mesoporosity and microporosity.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

   FENG Y
   SU X
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 2021D3977J
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide responsive intelligent hydrogel                based on dynamic covalent bond comprises                polyethyleneimine and benzaldehyde polyethylene                glycol and water, where polyethyleneimine and                dibenzaldehyde polyethylene glycol are connected by                dynamic imine bonds, and the gel is transformed                from a gel state to a fluid after introducing                carbon dioxide.
   USE - The hydrogel is useful as temporary plugging                agent in hydraulic fracturing of oil reservoirs                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The hydrogel: is capable of forming a                cross-linked hydrogel in a neutral and alkaline                environment, and in an acidic environment; realizes                the reversible gel-to-fluid transformation of the                system; has reversible gel-to-fluid conversion                performance, and self-repairing ability; and solves                the contradiction between the plugging strength of                traditional temporary plugging agents and the ease                of plugging removal.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   a composition, comprising polyethyleneimine                and dibenzaldehyde polyethylene glycol.
   (1) preparing the hydrogel, comprising mixing                the polyethyleneimine aqueous solution with the                dibenzaldehyde polyethylene glycol aqueous                solution;
   (2) a temporary plugging agent, comprising                polyethyleneimine and dibenzaldehyde polyethylene                glycol; and
   (3)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of the                  polyethyleneimine and dibenzaldehyde polyethylene                  glycol is 1:2-5. The polyethyleneimine preferably                  has a molecular weight of greater than or equal                  to 10000. The molecular weight of the                  polyethylene glycol is preferably 1000, 2000 or                  4000. The amount of p-aldehydic benzoic acid is                  excessive, preferably 4 times equivalent of                  polyethylene glycol. The solid is recrystallized                  3 times from tetrahydrofuran/anhydrous                  ether.Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises dissolving polyethyleneimine and                  dibenzaldehyde polyethylene glycol in water to                  obtain solution, moving the polyethyleneimine                  solution into the mold, then injecting the                  difunctionalized-polyethylene glycol (DF-PEG)                  solution into the mold, sealing and shaking the                  mold to complete the cross-linking reaction, and                  taking out the formed gel.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The dibenzaldehyde                  polyethylene glycol is prepared by: dissolving                  polyethylene glycol, p-aldehyde benzoic acid, and                  4-dimethylaminopyridine in tetrahydrofuran,                  stirring under nitrogen atmosphere at a water                  bath temperature of 20-25degrees Celsius until                  completely dissolved to form a mixed system, then                  dissolving N,N-dicyclohexylcarbodiimide in                  tetrahydrofuran and dripping to the mixed system,                  maintaining the temperature of the water bath at                  20-25degrees Celsius, stirring and reacting for                  24 hours, filtering, adding anhydrous ether to                  the filtrate to precipitate the product, then                  filtering, dissolving the solid with                  tetrahydrofuran, adding anhydrous ether for                  recrystallization, and finally drying.

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves judging whether exhaust gas has a synchronous diffusion trend according to a correlation of smoke Plum data of multiple smoke Plum points of the exhaust gas. Linear fitting operation of an original point is performed to obtain a smoke Plum ratio of each pollutant in the other pollutants and the carbon dioxide.    USE - Method for judging exhaust gas plume data validity.

PT P
PN CN112547121-A
TI Method for synthesizing titanium silicon molecular sieve catalyst, involves putting water, silicon source, titanium source, into carbon dioxide reactor to generate titanium silicon molecular sieve raw powder, mixing, shaping and roasting.
AU YU D
   HUANG D
   XING Y
   ZHANG Y
AE HONGBAOLI GROUP TAIXING CHEM CO LTD (HONG-Non-standard)
   HONGBAOLI GROUP CO LTD (HONG-Non-standard)
GA 2021368201
AB    NOVELTY - A method for synthesizing titanium silicon molecular sieve catalyst, involves (1) putting water, silicon source, titanium source, template agent and auxiliary agent into the supercritical carbon dioxide reactor at one time, and reacting to generate titanium silicon molecular sieve raw powder, (2) mixing the raw powder of titanium silicate molecular sieve, the extrusion aid, and the adhesive, and then extruding and shaping, and roasting to obtain the molded titanium silicate molecular sieve, and (3) hydrophobizing the shaped titanium-silicon molecular sieve with a hydrophobizing agent to obtain a titanium-silicon molecular sieve catalyst.
   USE - Method for synthesizing titanium silicon molecular sieve catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The supercritical fluid has the characteristics of gas and liquid phase, which has high permeability and low viscosity of gas, but has similar density to liquid and excellent dissolving ability for substances. The solubility of each component is flexibly adjusted by changing the temperature and pressure of the system to make the reaction proceed fully. The method replaces high proportion of water and alcohol solvents, due to the liquid phase nature of supercritical carbon dioxide, thereby saving raw materials and reducing emissions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: Method for synthesizing titanium silicon molecular sieve catalyst, specifically involves (1) mixing water, silicon source, titanium source, template agent and auxiliary agent according to a set ratio and then putting into the supercritical carbon dioxide reactor at one time, maintaining the reactor pressure at 25-55MPa and the temperature at 35-90 degrees C for 3-24 hours, after completion of the reaction, returning the pressure of the supercritical carbon dioxide reactor to normal pressure and discharging the carbon dioxide, pouring nitrogen into the supercritical carbon dioxide reactor, and keeping the pressure in the supercritical carbon dioxide reactor at normal pressure, then raising the temperature in the supercritical carbon dioxide reactor to 300-700 degrees C, roasting for 8-48 hours, cooling to 35-60 degrees C, stopping the introduction of nitrogen and taking out the original titanium silicon molecular sieve powder, step (2), mixing the raw powder of titanium silicate molecular sieve, the extrusion aid and the adhesive uniformly according to the set ratio, and after extrusion molding, drying at 25-135 degrees C for 8-24 hours, and then baking at 350-630 degrees C for 8-24 hours to obtain molded titanium silicon molecular sieve, and step (3), putting the formed titanium silicon molecular sieve into the tubular reactor, and adding the hydrophobizing reagent at 90-350 degrees C, and keeping the reaction for 3-18 hours to prepare the titanium silicon molecular sieve catalyst. Preferred Composition: In step (1), the mass ratio of water, silicon source, titanium source, template agent and auxiliary agent is (0.05-0.1): 1: (0.03-0.15): (0.1-0.3): (0.05-0.1). In the step (2), the mass ratio of the original titanium silicate molecular sieve powder, the extrusion aid, and the adhesive is 1: (0.05-0.15): (2-6). In step (3), the ratio of the formed titanium silicate molecular sieve to the hydrophobizing agent is 1: (0.1-0.5). Preferred Components: In step (1), the silicon source is from at least one of tetraethyl silicate, tetramethyl silicate, propyl orthosilicate, isopropyl orthosilicate, polyethyl silicate 40 and butyl orthosilicate. The titanium source is selected from at least one of tetramethyl titanate, tetraethyl titanate, tetrapropyl titanate, tetraisopropyl titanate, tetraisooctyl titanate and tetrabutyl titanate. The adhesive is at least one of an aqueous silicon dioxide solution and a silica sol, and the mass concentration of silicon dioxide in the adhesive is 10-50%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The template is at least one of decanamine, dodecylamine, tetradecylamine, hexadecylamine and octadecylamine. The auxiliary agent is at least one of monoethanolamine, diethanolamine, triethanolamine, monoisopropanolamine, diisopropanolamine and triisopropanolamine. In step (3), the hydrophobizing agent is at least one of hexamethyldisilazane, heptamethyldisilazane, tetramethyldisilazane, trimethylchlorosilane, trimethylbromosilane and iodotrimethylsilane.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: In step (2), the extrusion aid is at least one of hydroxypropyl methylcellulose, sesame gum, polyacrylamide and polyethylene glycol 20000.
DC E32 (Compounds of Ti, Zr, Hf, Cu, Ag, Au, Zn, Cd, Hg, Ga, In, Te, Ge, Sn, Pb, As, Sb, Bi.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)
MC E07-A03A; E11-E03; E11-K03; E11-W; E31-P05A; J04-E01; J04-E04; A10-E05B; A11-C01C; A12-W11K; N03-B01; N06-B02; N06-E01; N07-C03; N07-K01
IP B01J-029/89; C01B-039/08; C07D-301/19; C07D-303/04

   JU H
AE YIXING KEXING ALLOY MATERIALS CO LTD (YIXI-Non-standard)
GA 2021257513
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves hot rolling a raw material molybdenum plate into a blank, where the blank is hot-punched into a molybdenum wafer. The surface roughness of the molybdenum mirror is less than or equal to 0.015 mu m, and the molybdenum mirror is used for the carbon dioxide laser.

PN WO2020254403-A1; CN113993841-A; IN202247001968-A; EP3986861-A1; SG11202113596-A; JP2022537757-W; US2022306574-A1
TI Preparation of D,L-methionine used in e.g.            pharmaceutical, involves neutralizing alkali            methioninate solution by mixing with carbon dioxide to            obtain neutralized process solution and crystallizing            D,L-methionine product.
AU WEFER J
   HASSELBACH H
   WINKLER T
   DE CORTE D
   DRAPAL B
   MOUSSALLEM I
   RENNER C
   RUECKRIEGEL C
   DE C D
   HASSELBACH H J
   RUCKRIEGEL C
AE EVONIK OPERATIONS GMBH (EVON-C)
   EVONIK OPERATIONS GMBH (EVON-C)
   EVONIK OPERATIONS GMBH (EVON-C)
GA 2020C96004
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of D,L-methionine involves (i)                neutralizing the alkali methioninate solution at                65-95 degrees C by turbulent mixing with carbon                dioxide to obtain neutralized process solution, and                (ii) crystallizing the D,L-methionine product in                the presence of D,L-methionine seed crystals by                cooling the process solution to 25-35 degrees C.                The alkali methioninate solution is obtained by                alkaline hydrolysis of                5-(2-methylmercaptoethyl)hydantoin.
   USE - Preparation of D,L-methionine used in                pharmaceutical, health and fitness products, and                particularly used as additives in several                feedstuffs for various livestock.
   ADVANTAGE - The process is carried out in more                concentrated manner than in the existing potassium                carbonate process in which carbonation is carried                out at low temperature. Thus increasing the yield                of methionine product after crystallization. The                process avoids energy consumption for dissolving                and cooling in the recrystallization of the crude                methionine product. The process is simple.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic view of                continuous process and its units.
   Feed to neutralization (a)
   Carbon dioxide supply (b)
   Cooling circulation (c)
   Feed carbonized solution to crystallizer loop                (d)
   Suspension with fine crystals (e)
   Supersaturated mixture (f)
   Feed of mix to the crystallizer (g)
   Product suspension (h)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The alkali                  methioninate solution comprises potassium                  methioninate solution, the potassium optionally                  replaced by up to 30% sodium. The neutralized                  process solution comprises 8-11 wt.% potassium                  concentration, 10-17 wt.% methionine                  concentration, and 5-15 wt.% D,L-methionine seed                  crystals. Preferred Conditions: The pressure is                  adjusted between 100-400 kPa and the pH is                  adjusted between 7.5-8.5 in the steps (i) and                  (ii). The cooling period applied in the step (ii)                  is for maximum 360 minutes. Preferred Properties:                  The D,L-methionine seed crystals have average                  particle sizes between 125-1000 mu m. Preferred                  Process: The process is a batch or continuous                  process.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The process solution                  further comprises 10-2000 ppm defoamer                  formulation. Preferred Components: The defoamer                  formulation comprises a silicone oil having a                  kinematic viscosity of 0.65-10000 mm2/s (measured                  at 25 degrees C in accordance with DIN 53018),                  ionic and/or non-ionic surfactants as                  emulsifiers.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); C03 (Other organic compounds, inorganic compounds and multicomponent mixtures. Polymers and proteins.); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)
MC B10-B02D; B12-M11H2; B14-S12; C10-B02D; C12-M11H2; C14-S12; D03-H01; D03-H02E; A06-A00E; A06-A00E2; A06-A00E3; A12-V01; A12-W11

PN KR2195124-B1
TI Bio three-dimensional printer has incubating gas outlet that discharges air inside printing chamber to adjust carbon dioxide concentration where carbon dioxide is supplied from tank to inside of printing chamber through incubating gas inlet.
AU YOUSEOKHWAN
AE ROKIT HEALTHCARE INC (ROKI-Non-standard)
GA 202100791W
AB    NOVELTY - The printer has a printing chamber (100) that is provided inside a case formed to be hermetically sealed, and having a chamber door (110). A temperature control unit adjusts a temperature inside the printing chamber. A humidity control unit supplies moisture into the printing chamber to control humidity. An incubating gas introduction port introduces carbon dioxide (CO2) gas into the printing chamber. An incubating gas outlet (174) discharges the air inside the printing chamber to adjust the CO2 concentration. The CO2 is supplied from the tank to the inside of the printing chamber through an incubating gas inlet (172) to create a CO2 incubating environment.
   USE - Bio three-dimensional (3D) printer.
   ADVANTAGE - The sterilization is performed while the interior of the printing chamber is isolated from the outside and printing of living tissues is possible. Multiple biomaterials in a state in which the printing chamber is sealed during outputting of a biological tissue, various applications and external contamination are prevented.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of the printing chamber provided inside bio 3D printer.
   Printing chamber (100)
   Chamber door (110)
   Transparent window (112)
   Incubating gas inlet (172)
   Incubating gas outlet (174)
DC A32 (Polymer fabrication - such as moulding, extrusion, forming, laminating, spinning.); A85 (Electrical applications.); X25 (Industrial Electric Equipment)
MC A09-D03; A11-B16; A12-V00V; X25-A08B; X25-A08M2

PN CN212142010-U
TI Device useful for recycling mixed gas in wine fermentation process, comprises a fermentation tank, a condenser connected with the fermentation tank, an organic matter membrane separator connected with the condenser, and a fragrance substance storage tank.
AU DING X
   SUN X
   ZHAO X
   DAI P
AE JIANGSU LONG MEMBRANE HI-TECH CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202100901N
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a device for recycling mixed gas in wine fermentation process, the mixed gas comprises a fragrance substance gas and carbon dioxide, wherein the device comprises a fermentation tank, a condenser connected with the fermentation tank, a transparent organic film separator connected with the condenser; a fragrance substance storage tank connected with the condenser and a carbon dioxide collecting tank connected with the organic matter permeable membrane separator; the condenser is set between the fermentation tank and the organic matter permeable membrane separator; the fragrance substance gas is condensed by the condenser to form a fragrance substance liquid and is collected in the fragrance substance storage tank; the un-condensed mixed gas enters into the organic matter-permeable membrane separator; the mixed gas not permeable to the organic matter membrane separator is collected in the carbon dioxide collecting tank. The device for recycling mixed gas in wine fermentation process of the utility model can effectively recycle the mixed gas in the wine fermentation process, while ensuring the fragrance quality of the wine, at the same time, reducing the production cost.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

TI Carbon dioxide laser engraving machine, has sliding rail fixedly installed on translation seat and provided with sliding block, where sliding block is fixedly connected with connecting plate that is provided with carbon dioxide laser.
AU WANG Y
AE SUZHOU LEILING OPTOELECTRONIC TECHNOLOGY (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 202086795X
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a carbon dioxide laser engraving machine, comprising a machine table, the machine table is equipped with two upright posts, between two upright posts is fixed with a cross beam; the machine table is further equipped with a first motor; the first motor output shaft end is equipped with a first screw rod; two sides of the first screw rod are symmetrically provided with two first guide rods; The first guide rod is installed on the machine table. The utility model through the negative pressure fan from the dust collecting hole of the carving generated debris is extracted to the dust baffle plate; when needing to take out the chip, lifting the upper shell, namely can take out the dust on the dust baffle plate; through the matching design of the convex strip and the groove, so that the upper shell and the lower shell are clamped with higher sealing property, at the same time, it is convenient for collecting the carving chips; it solves the technical problem of needing to manually clean the position of the carving table after carving in the existing technology.

PN CN211230422-U
TI Sealing system for fracturing supercritical carbon dioxide in oil and gas exploitation field, has executing mechanism installed on constant pressure mechanism for placing sample of sample, and filling supercritical carbon dioxide fracturing fluid into sample.
AU YANG Z
   FENG C
   LI X
   JIA M
   HE Y
   JIN W
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST PETROLEUM (USWP-C)
GA 202079015Q
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a sealing system for fracturing supercritical carbon dioxide, relating to the technical field of oil and gas exploitation, comprising a heat preservation mechanism, a pressure stabilizing mechanism and an executing mechanism, wherein the heat preservation mechanism is used for keeping the constant temperature experiment environment of the sample; the heat preservation mechanism comprises an upper shell, the lower shell and the sealing plate, the upper shell and the lower shell are matched with the cavity. The utility model is provided with a heat preservation mechanism, which can realize that the upper shell slides to the limiting position on the lower shell, and forms a sealed experiment environment with the fixing cover, so as to reach the constant temperature of the experiment environment; By setting the pressure stabilizing mechanism, when the sliding cover moves to the joint with the fixing cover, screwing the fixing bolt, it can realize the space of the cavity one and the cavity two to reach the sealing state, the air pressure in the inner part is kept in the range needed by the experiment.
DC H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).); Q49 (Mining (E21))
