PT P
PN CN111039773-A; WO2021129082-A1; CN111039773-B
TI Preparing alpha-acrylic acid compounds, comprises reacting alkyne compounds, palladium salt catalyst, base, bisphosphine ligand, silane reducing agent and solvent by introducing carbon dioxide gas, separating and purifying.
AU JIANG H
   XIONG W
   QI C
   CHENG R
   WANG L
   WU W
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
   UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 2020346311
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing alpha -acrylic acid compounds (A) (I) or (II), comprises adding alkyne compounds (III) or (IV), palladium salt catalyst, base, bisphosphine ligand, silane reducing agent and solvent to a reaction kettle, then introducing carbon dioxide gas, stirring and reacting at 60-120 degrees C, separating and purifying to obtain (A).
   USE - The method is useful for preparing alpha -acrylic acid compounds.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes palladium salt as catalyst, bisphosphine ligand as ligand and selective single to produces high yield product; utilizes simple raw materials, simple to operate and high atom economy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing alpha -acrylic acid compounds (A) of formulae (I) or (II), comprises adding alkyne compounds of formula (R1C(C)-R2) (III) or (IV), palladium salt catalyst, base, bisphosphine ligand, silane reducing agent and solvent to a reaction kettle, then introducing carbon dioxide gas, stirring and reacting at 60-120 degrees C, separating and purifying to obtain (A).
   R1 = alkyl, aryl-substituted alkyl, cyano-substituted alkyl, halo-substituted alkyl, phenyl, alkyl substituted phenyl, alkoxy substituted phenyl, halo substituted phenyl, alkyl substituted phenyl, aryl substituted phenyl or thiophenyl; and
   R2 and R3 = H, alkyl, phenyl, alkyl substituted phenyl, halo substituted phenyl or ester substituted phenyl.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The palladium salt is palladium acetate, bis(dibenzylideneacetone) palladium, tris(dibenzylideneacetone)dipalladium(0), palladium chloride, 1,3-bis(diphenylphosphino)propane)palladium(II) chloride, (1,2-bis(diphenylphosphino)ethane)dichloropalladium(II), (1,4-bis(diphenylphosphino)butane)palladium dichloride, (1,1'-bis(diphenylphosphino)ferrocene)palladium(II) dichloride, diethyl palladium dichloride, palladium trifluoroacetate and/or tetrakis(triphenylphosphine)palladium(0), and the molar ratio between palladium salt and alkynyl group in (III) or (IV) is 0.01-0.015:1. The bisphosphine ligand is 1,2-bis(diphenylphosphine)benzene, 1,2-bis(diphenylphosphino)ethane, 1,3-bis(diphenylphosphino)propane, 1,4-bis(diphenylphosphino)butane, 2,2'-bis-(diphenylphosphino)-1,1'-binaphthalene, bis(2-(diphenylphosphino)phenyl) ether and/or 4,5-bis(diphenylphosphino)-9,9-dimethylxanthene, and the molar ratio between amount bisphosphine ligand and alkyne group in (III) or (IV) is 0.015-0.06:1. The silane reducing agent is phenylsilane, bisphenylsilane and/or bis (trimethylsilyl), and the molar ratio between silane reducing agent and alkynyl group in (III) or (IV) is 1.3-1.5:1. The base is organic base triethylamine, pyridine, tetramethylethylenediamine, 1,5,7-triazide bicyclo(4.4.0)dec-5-ene, 1,4-diazide bicyclo(2.2.2)octane and/or inorganic alkali potassium carbonate, and the molar ratio between base and alkyne group in (III) or (IV) is 1-2:1. Preferred Method: The stirring and reaction temperature is 60-100 degrees C, and the time is 6-20 hours. The step of separating and purifying comprises acidifying the reaction liquid by hydrochloric acid, washing with water, extracting by ethyl acetate, combining organic phases, drying using anhydrous magnesium sulfate, filtering, vacuum extracting and removing organic solvent to obtain crude product, and purifying by column chromatography to obtain (A). The eluent used in purifying by the column chromatography is a mixed solvent of petroleum ether and ethyl acetate in a volume ratio of 1-5:1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent is toluene, dimethyl sulfoxide, N,N-dimethylformamide, N,N-dimethylacetamide, dichloromethane and/or tetrahydrofuran.
   Preferred Definitions:
   R1 = phenyl, 4-methylphenyl, 4-ethylphenyl, 4-methoxyphenyl, 4-tert-butylphenyl, 4-fluorophenyl, 4-chlorophenyl, 4-bromophenyl, 4-phenylphenyl, 4-trifluoromethylphenyl, 3-methoxyphenyl, 3-fluorophenyl, 2-methoxyphenyl, 2-fluorophenyl, 2-naphthylphenyl, n-butyl, n-pentyl, n-hexyl, cyclohexyl, cyclohexylmethyl, phenethyl, 3-cyano-propyl, 4-chloro-butyl or phenylthio; and
   R2 and R3 = phenyl, 4-methylphenyl, 4-ethylphenyl, 4-tert-butylphenyl, 4-fluorophenyl, 4-chlorophenyl, 4-methylphenyl, methyl or butyl.
DC A41 (Monomers and Condensants These are also included in Section E.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC A01-D00D; E10-A15B; E10-C02C1; E10-C02E; E10-C04; E11-F02B; J04-E01; J04-E04; N02-F03; N02-F04; N05-C; N07-D02B
IP C07C-051/15; C07C-057/03; C07C-057/13; C07C-057/26; C07C-057/42; C07C-057/60; C07C-059/13; C07C-057/00

   LIU X
   CHEN P
   HUANG X
   ZHOU G
   CAO Q
AE NINGBO MENOVO PHARM CO LTD (NING-Non-standard)
GA 201977144Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing 2,4-dichloro-3-cyano-5-fluorobenzoic acid (I) comprises (i) reacting 2,6-dichloro-3-fluorobenzonitrile compounds (II) as a raw material with carbon dioxide in the presence of a strong base in a low temperature environment, then quenching with water, and (ii) adjusting pH of the aqueous phase with a dilute acid, extracting the organic phase with an extractant, allowing to stand, layering, then concentrating the organic phase to obtain (I).
   USE - The method is useful for preparing 2,4-dichloro-3-cyano-5-fluorobenzoic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple preparation process, high yield of more than 85% and purity of more than 99%; and is suitable for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing 2,4-dichloro-3-cyano-5-fluorobenzoic acid of formula (I) comprises (i) reacting 2,6-dichloro-3-fluorobenzonitrile compounds of formula (II) as a raw material with carbon dioxide in the presence of a strong base in a low temperature environment, then quenching with water, and (ii) adjusting pH of the aqueous phase with a dilute acid, extracting the organic phase with an extractant, allowing to stand, layering, then concentrating the organic phase to obtain (I).
   R = H or halo.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The strong base is n-butyl lithium, sec-butyllithium, tert-butyllithium, isobutyl lithium, (trimethylsilyl)methyllithium, sodium bis(trimethylsilyl)amide, potassium bis(trimethylsilyl)amide or potassium tert-butoxide, preferably n-butyl lithium or sec-butyllithium. The organic ligand is diisopropylamine, pentamethyldiethylenediamine or hexamethyldisilazane. In step (ii), dilute acid is 2-6 N dilute hydrochloric acid, and pH value is 1-3. The extracting agent is ethyl acetate, ether or isobutanol. Preferred Method: In step (i), reaction temperature is -50 degrees C to -100 degrees C. In step (i), dissolving (II), strong base in an organic solvent, then reacting, where organic solvent is tetrahydrofuran, toluene or 2-methyltetrahydrofuran. The method comprises adding (II) into the strong base for not less than 30 minutes, passing carbon dioxide into the reaction system, reacting for not less than 30 minutes, heating at room temperature, evacuating carbon dioxide and nitrogen in the reaction system, then quenching reaction with ice water.

TI Supercritical carbon dioxide compression system starting method, involves closing final bypass valve and grading bypass valve according to degraded sequence, and increasing exhaust pressure when compressor is in normal operation state.
AU QIN F
   LIU Y
   HE W
   YANG J
   LI Q
   YAO J
   LUO Z
AE CNPC JICHAI POWER CO LTD CHENGDU COMPRES (CNPC-C)
GA 201970308L
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves starting a motor by a warming-up operation. Small circulation operation is performed. An opening and closing of a cleaning value is controlled by the small circulation operation. Intake pressure is supplemented to a rated intake pressure value in three stages. A first stage is supplemented to one-third of intake pressure. The second stage is supplemented to two- thirds of the intake pressure. The third stage is supplemented to the rated intake pressure value. Loading operation is performed after crankcase oil temperature rises to rated temperature required by the small circulation operation. A gas inlet valve is opened by the loading operation. A final bypass valve and grading bypass valve are sequentially closed according to a degraded sequence. Exhaust pressure is gradually increased when a compressor is in a normal operation state.
   USE - Supercritical carbon dioxide compression system starting method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables avoiding long cleaning time, wasting starting time and wear of precision parts of the compressor caused by starting the motor in a high- pressure environment.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating a supercritical carbon dioxide compression system starting method. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'

PN US2019185757-A1; US10392565-B2
TI Converting a biomass feedstock to product comprising liquid hydrocarbons involves combining biomass feedstock, base, and solvent, heating reaction mixture to a decarboxylation reaction temperature and reaction forms carbon dioxide byproduct.
AU XIAO X
   THOMPSON A B
   SULIC M P
   WARD P A
   ANTON D L
AE SAVANNAH RIVER NUCLEAR SOLUTIONS LLC (WESE-C)
GA 201951679J
AB    NOVELTY - Converting a biomass feedstock to a product comprising liquid hydrocarbons, comprises: combining a biomass feedstock, a base, and a solvent to form a reaction mixture; heating the reaction mixture to a decarboxylation reaction temperature, where the decarboxylation reaction forms a carbon dioxide byproduct, the base reacts with the carbon dioxide byproduct to form a carbonate, the reaction mixture comprises excess base where over the course of the entire decarboxylation reaction and the entire carbonate formation reaction, the reaction mixture is not completely vaporized and the reaction mixture has a pH of greater, than 14.
   USE - The method is useful for converting a biomass feedstock to a product comprising liquid hydrocarbons (claimed) including alcohols and phenol derivatives.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is highly efficient; is carried out with nearly 100% conversion for any type biomass feedstock and requires no expensive or complicated pretreatment; is carried out with any type of biomass feedstock and require no expensive or complicated pretreatment; is universal method for efficient use of hard-to-convert biomass i.e. lignin and cellulose, as well as the more typical and easy-to-convert biomass feedstock; carried out in mild conditions, high yield and no recycle of unconverted feeds.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The decarboxylation reaction temperature is 170-300 degrees C. The decarboxylation reaction and the carbonate formation reaction are carried out at a pressure of 300-6000 psig. The method further comprises processing the carbonate to generate carbon dioxide, where the generation of the carbon dioxide comprises acidification of a reaction effluent comprising the carbonate, electrolysis of the acidified reaction effluent, which regenerates the base, and recycling the regenerated base to the reaction mixture and recovering and recycling the solvent. The method comprises a batch treatment process or continuous treatment process. The reaction mixture comprises 1-4.5 M the base. The complete conversion of the biomass feedstock occurs in 1 hour or less following heating of the reaction mixture to the decarboxylation reaction temperature. Preferred Components: The biomass feedstock comprises whole plant biomass. The biomass feedstock comprises waste. The solvent comprises water, recycled biomass product and/or at least one organic solvent. The organic solvents comprises 1-8C organic hydrocarbons, alcohols, ethers and/o ketones. The reaction mixture as formed includes the biomass feedstock and the solvent in an amount such that a molar ratio of carbon of the biomass feedstock to the solvent is 1:1-1:10. The base comprises oxides or hydroxides of groups IA or IIA metals in the periodic table, or hydrides of groups IA, IIA, or IIIA metals, or metal-organic compounds containing groups IA, IIA, or IIIA metals. The reaction mixture as formed includes a molar ratio of carbon in the biomass feedstock to a base monovalent equivalent that is 5:1-1:2.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN WO2020159657-A1
TI Producing methanol comprises e.g. feeding catalytic partial oxidation (CPO) reactant mixture to CPO reactor, introducing first portion of first syngas and contacting portion of first hydrogen stream with second portion of first syngas.
AU RABIE A
   RAJAGOPALAN V
   ALAHMADI F
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   RABIE A (RABI-Individual)
   RAJAGOPALAN V (RAJA-Individual)
   ALAHMADI F (ALAH-Individual)
GA 202073960P
AB    NOVELTY - Producing (M1) methanol comprises: (a) feeding a catalytic partial oxidation (CPO) reactant mixture to a CPO reactor; (b) introducing a first portion of the first syngas to a first hydrogen recovery unit to produce a first hydrogen stream and a first residual gas stream; (c) contacting at least a portion of the first hydrogen stream with a second portion of the first syngas to yield a second syngas; (d) contacting at least a portion of the second syngas with a hydrogen stream to yield a third syngas; (e) feeding at least a portion of the third syngas to a methanol reactor to produce a methanol reactor effluent stream; (f) introducing at least a portion of the methanol reactor effluent stream to a separator to produce a crude methanol stream and a vapor stream; (g) introducing a first portion of vapor stream to a second hydrogen recovery unit to produce a second hydrogen stream and a second residual gas stream; and (h) recycling a second portion of the vapor stream to methanol reactor.
   USE - The processes are useful for producing methanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The processes: utilizes energy efficient unit operation that reduces unwanted carbon dioxide formation; and results in higher carbon and energy efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing (M1) methanol comprises: (a) feeding a catalytic partial oxidation (CPO) reactant mixture to a CPO reactor, where the CPO reactant mixture comprises hydrocarbons, oxygen, and optionally steam, at least a portion of the CPO reactant mixture reacts, via a CPO reaction, in the CPO reactor to produce a first syngas, the CPO reactor comprises a CPO catalyst, the first syngas comprises hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrocarbons, and the first syngas has an M ratio of the first syngas, the M ratio is a molar ratio defined as given specification; (b) introducing a first portion of the first syngas to a first hydrogen recovery unit to produce a first hydrogen stream and a first residual gas stream, where the first residual gas stream comprises carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, hydrocarbons, and optionally hydrogen; (c) contacting at least a portion of the first hydrogen stream with a second portion of the first syngas to yield a second syngas, where the second syngas comprises hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrocarbons, the second syngas has an M ratio of the second syngas, and the M ratio of the second syngas is greater than the M ratio of the first syngas; (d) contacting at least a portion of the second syngas with a hydrogen stream to yield a third syngas, where the third syngas comprises hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrocarbons, the third syngas has an M ratio of the third syngas, and the M ratio of the third syngas is greater than the M ratio of the second syngas; (e) feeding at least a portion of the third syngas to a methanol reactor to produce a methanol reactor effluent stream, where the methanol reactor effluent stream comprises methanol, water, hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrocarbons; (f) introducing at least a portion of the methanol reactor effluent stream to a separator to produce a crude methanol stream and a vapor stream, where the crude methanol stream comprises methanol and water, the vapor stream comprises hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrocarbons; (g) introducing a first portion of the vapor stream to a second hydrogen recovery unit to produce a second hydrogen stream and a second residual gas stream, where the second residual gas stream comprises carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, hydrocarbons, and optionally hydrogen, and at least a portion of the second hydrogen stream is contacted with the second syngas in step (d); and (h) recycling a second portion of the vapor stream to the methanol reactor. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) producing (M2) methanol comprising (a1) feeding the CPO reactant mixture to CPO reactor, (b1) introducing a first portion of the first syngas to a first hydrogen recovery unit to produce a first hydrogen stream and the first residual gas stream, where the first portion of the first syngas is 10-25 mol.% of the first syngas, (c1) contacting at least a portion of the first hydrogen stream with a second portion of the first syngas to yield the second syngas, where the second portion of the first syngas is 75-90 mol.% of the first syngas, (d1) contacting at least a portion of the second syngas with a hydrogen stream to yield the third syngas, (e1) optionally compressing at least a portion of the third syngas in a steam-driven compressor to yield a third compressed syngas, (f1) feeding at least a portion of the third syngas and/or at least a portion of the third compressed syngas to a methanol reactor to produce a methanol reactor effluent stream, where the methanol reactor effluent stream comprises methanol, water, hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrocarbons, (g1) introducing at least a portion of the methanol reactor effluent stream to a gas-liquid separator to produce a crude methanol stream and a vapor stream, where the crude methanol stream comprises methanol and water, and the vapor stream comprises hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrocarbons, (h1) introducing a first portion of the vapor stream to a second hydrogen recovery unit to produce a second hydrogen stream and a second residual gas stream, where the second residual gas stream comprises carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, hydrocarbons, and optionally hydrogen, the first portion of the vapor stream is 7-17 mol.% of the vapor stream, and at least a portion of the second hydrogen stream is contacted with the second syngas in step (d1), (i1) recycling a second portion of the vapor stream to the methanol reactor, where the second portion of the vapor stream is 83-93 mol.% of the vapor stream and (j1) separating at least a portion of the crude methanol stream in a distillation unit into a methanol stream and a water stream;
   (2) a system (S1) for producing methanol; and
   (3) a system (S2) for producing methanol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process (P1) further comprises: after step (a) and before step (b) recovering a CPO reactor effluent from the CPO reactor, where the CPO reactor effluent comprises hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, water and hydrocarbons; and removing at least a portion of the water from the CPO reactor effluent to produce the first syngas. The portion of the third syngas is compressed in a compressor to yield a third compressed syngas, where at least a portion of the third compressed syngas is fed to the methanol reactor in step (e). The CPO reactor has at least one CPO operational parameter of a CPO inlet temperature of 150-520 degrees C, a CPO outlet temperature of 600-1400 degrees C, a CPO pressure of 2-30 barg, a CPO contact time of 0.001 milliseconds to 5 seconds, a carbon to oxygen molar ratio in the CPO reactant mixture of 1.5:1-2.2:1, where the carbon to oxygen molar ratio refers to the total moles of carbon of hydrocarbons in the reactant mixture divided by the total moles of oxygen in the reactant mixture, and/or a steam to carbon molar ratio in the CPO reactant mixture of 0.2:1-1.5:1, where the steam to carbon molar ratio refers to the total moles of water in the reactant mixture divided by the total moles of carbon of hydrocarbons in the reactant mixture. The CPO reactor has at least one CPO operational parameter of a CPO inlet temperature of 200-400 degrees C, a CPO outlet temperature of 800-1100 degrees C, a CPO pressure of 10-25 barg, a CPO contact time of 0.001 milliseconds to 5 seconds, a carbon to oxygen molar ratio in the CPO reactant mixture of 1.5:1-1.9:1, where the carbon to oxygen molar ratio refers to the total moles of carbon of hydrocarbons in the reactant mixture divided by the total moles of oxygen in the reactant mixture, and/or a steam to carbon molar ratio in the CPO reactant mixture of 0.2:1-0.6:1, where the steam to carbon molar ratio refers to the total moles of water in the reactant mixture divided by the total moles of carbon of hydrocarbons in the reactant mixture. Preferred Components: The first portion of the first syngas is 1-25 mol.% of the first syngas, where the second portion of the first syngas is 75-99 mol.% of the first syngas. The first portion of the vapor stream is 3-20 mol.% of the vapor stream, where the second portion of the vapor stream is 80-97 mol.% of the vapor stream. The compressor is a steam-driven compressor, where at least a portion of the first residual gas stream and/or at least a portion of the second residual gas stream is used as fuel to generate steam for powering the steam-driven compressor. The M ratio of the first syngas is 1.5-1.95, where the first syngas comprises less than 10 mol% carbon dioxide, the M ratio of the second syngas is 1.7-2.2 and/or the M ratio of the third syngas is 2-13. The first hydrogen stream comprises greater than or equal to 50 mol% of the hydrogen of the first portion of the first syngas. The second hydrogen stream comprises greater than or equal to 50 mol.% of the hydrogen of the first portion of the vapor stream, where the first hydrogen stream and/or the second hydrogen stream comprise greater than or equal to 65 mol.% hydrogen. The first hydrogen recovery unit and the second hydrogen recovery unit can independently comprise a pressure swing adsorption (PSA) unit, a membrane separation unit and/or a cryogenic separation unit. The hydrocarbons comprise methane, natural gas, natural gas liquids, liquefied petroleum gas, associated gas, well head gas, enriched gas, paraffins, shale gas, shale liquids, fluid catalytic cracking off gas, refinery process gases, refinery off gases, stack gases, and/or fuel gas from a fuel gas header. The first hydrogen stream comprises 70-95 mol.% of the hydrogen of the first portion of the first syngas, and/or the second hydrogen stream comprises 70-95 mol% of the hydrogen of the first portion of the vapor stream, the M ratio of the first syngas is 1.5-1.95, the first syngas comprises less than 7 mol% carbon dioxide, the M ratio of the second syngas is 1.7-2.2 and the M ratio of the third syngas is 2-13. The system (S1) comprises (a2) a catalytic partial oxidation CPO reactor comprising a CPO catalyst and operable to produce the first syngas from the CPO reactant mixture via a CPO reaction, where at least a portion of the CPO reactant mixture reacts in the CPO reactor to produce the first syngas, (b2) a first hydrogen recovery unit operable to produce a first hydrogen stream and a first residual gas stream from the first portion of the first syngas, (c2) a methanol synthesis reactor operable to produce a methanol reactor effluent stream from at least a portion of a methanol synthesis reactor feed comprising at least a portion of the first hydrogen stream, a second portion of the first syngas, and at least a portion of a recycle vapor stream, where the methanol reactor effluent stream comprises methanol, water, hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrocarbons, (d2) a separator fluidly connected with the methanol synthesis reactor and operable to produce a crude methanol stream and a vapor stream from at least a portion of the methanol reactor effluent stream, where the crude methanol stream comprises methanol and water and the vapor stream comprises hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrocarbons and (e2) a recycle line operable to introduce a portion of the vapor stream to the methanol synthesis reactor as the recycle vapor stream of the methanol synthesis reactor feed. The system (S1) further comprises: (f2) a second hydrogen recovery unit fluidly connected with the separator and operable to produce a second hydrogen stream and a second residual gas stream from another portion of the vapor stream, where the second residual gas stream comprises carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, hydrocarbons, and optionally hydrogen; and (g2) a line fluidly connecting the second hydrogen recovery unit with the methanol synthesis reactor, where at least a portion of the second hydrogen stream can be introduced into the methanol synthesis reactor as a further component of the methanol synthesis reactor feed. The system (S2) comprises (a3) a catalytic partial oxidation CPO reactor comprising a CPO catalyst and operable to produce the first syngas from CPO reactant mixture comprising hydrocarbons, oxygen, and optionally steam via a CPO reaction, where at least a portion of the CPO reactant mixture reacts in the CPO reactor to produce the first syngas, (b3) a first hydrogen recovery unit operable to produce a first hydrogen stream and the first residual gas stream from a first portion of the first syngas, (c3) a line configured for combining at least a portion of the first hydrogen stream with a second portion of the first syngas to yield the second syngas, (d3) a line configured for carrying the third syngas comprising a combination of at least a portion of the second syngas and a hydrogen stream, where the third syngas has an M ratio of the third syngas, and the M ratio of the third syngas is greater than the M ratio of the second syngas and is 5-10, (e3) a methanol synthesis reactor fluidly connected with the line configured for carrying the third syngas and operable to produce the methanol reactor effluent stream from at least a portion of the third syngas, (f3) a separator fluidly connected with the methanol synthesis reactor and operable to produce the crude methanol stream and the vapor stream from at least a portion of the methanol reactor effluent stream, (g3) a second hydrogen recovery unit fluidly connected with the separator and operable to produce a second hydrogen stream and the second residual gas stream from a portion of the vapor stream, (h3) a line fluidly connecting the second hydrogen recovery unit and the line configured for carrying the third syngas, where the hydrogen stream of the third syngas comprises at least a portion of the second hydrogen stream and (i3) a recycle line operable to introduce another portion of the vapor stream to the methanol synthesis reactor via the line configured for carrying the third syngas stream.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN115059445-A
TI Method for sequestrating and storing carbon            dioxide geology in withered collective storage group            involves injecting extracted formation water into            permeable layer through formation water return            injection well to increase stratum fluid pressure.
AU HUANG D
   XU Q
   WU X
   YAN P
   HE Y
   YANG Y
AE UNIV CHENGDU TECHNOLOGY (UYCT-C)
GA 2022C1984X
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves selecting a depletion                reservoir with side water or bottom water according                to geological environment. A cover layer is                arranged above the depletion reservoir. A permeable                layer is provided above the cover layer. A carbon                dioxide injection well, a stratum water extraction                well, and a formation water injection well are                respectively deployed. The carbon dioxide injection                well is connected with the depleted reservoir. The                formation water extraction wells and the side water                are connected to communicate with the bottom water                portion of the depletion reservoirs. The strata                water return injection well and the permeation                layer are connected with each other to inject                carbon dioxide into the depleted reservoirs to                increase formation fluid pressure of the formation                fluid.
   USE - Method for sequestrating and storing carbon                dioxide geology in withered collective storage                group. Can also be used in sequestering carbon                dioxide in saline water layer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the problem of carbon                dioxide leakage caused by the damage to the cover                layer in the existing dry reservoir sealing,                forming potential safety hazard. It improves the                security of the cap layer, prevents the cap from                rupturing, improves the safety of the carbon                dioxide sequestrating, and avoids the                environmentally-friendly safety problem caused by                possibility. It provides low part selecting waste                well into carbon dioxide injection well in the                structure of the depletion reservoir, saving the                drilling and well completion cost. It realizes the                recycling of the formation water, not using surface                water, and saves the ground light water resource,                reducing the operation maintenance cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                carbon dioxide geology sequest/storage system in                the withered storage group.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                carbon dioxide geology sequest/storage system in                the withered storage group (Drawing includes                non-English language text).

PT P
PN RU2771380-C1
TI DEVICES FOR THE UTILIZATION OF CARBON DIOXIDE USED TO PROTECT THE ENVIRONMENT.
AU BEDNARZHEVSKII S S
AE BEDNARZHEVSKII S S (BEDN-Individual)
GA 2022651278
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to electrochemical technologies, namely to devices for the utilization of carbon dioxide, and can be used to purify the air of working premises, reduce gas emissions from industrial enterprises, protect the environment and reduce the greenhouse effect. A device for the utilization of carbon dioxide is presented, containing a working body, vertically mounted electrodes, and an electric power supply unit connected to all electrodes. Anode and cathode alternate with each other. The device contains a system for dissolving carbon dioxide in water, a system for supplying water to the working body, and a system for removing electrolysis products. It also contains a system for preparing water for dissolving carbon dioxide, connected to a system for supplying it to the working body. The system for dissolving carbon dioxide in water is located inside the working body and is combined with the electrolysis system. The device has systems for cooling water inside the working body, supplying carbon dioxide into it, removing gaseous products of electrolysis, connected to a system for releasing unutilized carbon dioxide from them, connected to a system for supplying it to the working body. The system for removing water from the working body with solid products of electrolysis is connected to a system for its purification, connected to a system for preparing water for dissolving carbon dioxide. The systems for supplying carbon dioxide to the working body and removing gaseous products of electrolysis from it are made in the form of a perforated pipe passed through holes in the lower and upper parts of the electrodes, respectively. The systems for supplying water to the working body and removing water from it with solid products of electrolysis are made in the form of a perforated pipe passed through holes in the upper and lower parts of the electrodes, respectively.
   USE - Electrochemical technologies.
   ADVANTAGE - Invention makes it possible to increase the degree of carbon dioxide utilization, to reduce the amount of water used in this case, and thereby to increase the efficiency of the device.
1 cl, 1 dwg

PN CN113813767-A; CN215842470-U
TI Preparing vaterite cementitious building material by adding flue gas to absorption tower, pumping carbon dioxide absorbent to tower for spraying to obtain carbon dioxide storage material, absorbing carbon dioxide slurry, pumping into solid-liquid separator, adding calcium hydroxide, and reacting.
AU WEI Z
   ZHANG J
AE TIANJIN ZHONGJING BUILDING MATERIALS CO (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202206846V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing vaterite cementitious building material involves: (i) pulping carbon capture agent in a pulper, diluting with water, and mixing to obtain a carbon dioxide absorbent; (ii) adding industrial flue gas containing carbon dioxide to an absorption tower (8), pumping the obtained absorbent to the tower for spraying to obtain carbon dioxide storage material, and pumping back the sprayed solution to the tower from the bottom of the tower for spraying; and (iii) absorbing the carbon dioxide slurry until the pH value is reduced to 6-7, pumping from the bottom of the tower into a solid-liquid separator, adding solid waste containing calcium oxide or calcium hydroxide alkaline mineral materials to the separator, so that the materials in the system react and crystallize in the presence of water to obtain vaterite (V-type) particles as the product, and recovering and reusing the crystallized solution for carbon dioxide capture.
   USE - Preparing vaterite cementitious building material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: provides vaterite cementitious building material having high dispersing performance and small specific gravity, effectively reduces the use amount of cement in building material, thus reducing energy and environment consumption caused by cement manufacturing, and reduces the weight of the building material and whole energy consumption; is simple in operation, and low in cost; can capture and solidify carbon dioxide in the industrial flue gas through alkaline solid waste; and can convert the carbon dioxide into inorganic high-efficiency cementitious material, which realizes the reprocessing and utilization of carbon dioxide and solid waste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for production system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic flow chart of a production system. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Main exhaust fan (2)
   Branch smoke pipeline (3)
   Chimney bypass door plate (4)
   Chimney (5)
   Absorption tower (8)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The carbon capture agent is a mixed system of ethylenediamine and 1,3-butanediol in a molar ratio of 20:1-40:1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The total mass of water in the slurry and the mass ratio of calcium oxide or calcium hydroxide in step (3) is 5:1-20:1. The mass ratio of carbon dioxide storage material to the added calcium oxide or calcium hydroxide is 10-50.Preferred Components: The solid waste containing calcium oxide or calcium hydroxide alkaline mineral material is slaked lime, lime, and/or carbide slag.

PN US2021143489-A1; WO2021096990-A1; GB2605071-A; EP4059081-A1
TI Deactivating lithium-containing battery comprises            adding lithium-containing battery, oxidizing fluid and            carbon dioxide to vessel, pressurizing vessel, heating,            reducing heat and pressure in vessel and removing            battery.
AU CRANDON L E
   SLOOP S E
AE HULICO LLC (HULI-Non-standard)
   HULICO LLC (HULI-Non-standard)
   HULICO LLC (HULI-Non-standard)
GA 202150467Q
AB    NOVELTY - Deactivating (M1) a lithium-containing                battery, comprises: adding the lithium-containing                battery to a vessel; adding an oxidizing fluid to                the vessel; adding carbon dioxide to the vessel;                pressurizing the vessel; heating the vessel to form                lithium carbonate within the lithium-containing                battery; reducing heat and pressure in the vessel;                and removing the battery from the vessel. The                method further comprises: adding the oxidizing                fluid mixed with the carbon dioxide; adding the                oxidizing fluid to the vessel separately from                adding the carbon dioxide. The oxidizing fluid is                added to the vessel before the battery is added to                the vessel. The oxidizing fluid and the carbon                dioxide are added to the vessel in a molar ratio                (carbon dioxide:oxidizing fluid) of 99:1-10:90. The                pressurizing the vessel comprises forming a                condensed carbon dioxide phase, where the condensed                carbon dioxide phase comprises a gas phase, a                liquid phase, a supercritical phase, and/or a dense                gas phase.
   USE - The methods are useful for deactivating a                lithium-containing battery.
   ADVANTAGE - The methods: prevent thermal runaway in spent                lithium-ion batteries that help ensure safe                recycling and/or its disposal, and reduce costs                involved in shipping and recycling spent                lithium-ion batteries; and utilize carbon dioxide                that provides safe, low-cost, environmentally                friendly industrial pathways to deactivate lithium                within or outside of a battery.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (a) deactivating (M2) a lithium-containing                battery, comprising adding the lithium-containing                battery to a vessel, adding water to the vessel,                suspending the battery above a liquid level of the                water, adding carbon dioxide to the vessel,                pressurizing the vessel to form a condensed carbon                dioxide phase, heating the vessel to form lithium                carbonate within the lithium-containing battery,                reducing heat and pressure in the vessel and                removing the battery from the vessel; and
   (b) a deactivated lithium-containing                battery.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The water is added to the                  vessel before the battery is added to the                  vessel.Preferred Components: The oxidizing fluid                  comprises at least one of water, hydrogen                  peroxide, ammonia, oxygen, ozone, an alcohol, a                  halogen, sulfur dioxide, and a halocarbon having                  a boiling point below 150degrees Celsius. The                  condensed carbon dioxide phase comprises a gas                  phase, a liquid phase, a supercritical phase,                  and/or a dense gas phase.

AU KIMURA K
AE KIMURA K (KIMU-Individual)
   KIMURA N (KIMU-Individual)
   SATO J (SATO-Individual)
   KIMURA S (KIMU-Individual)
GA 202239477N
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide recovery device comprises horizontal rotating cylindrical absorber connected to a smoke exhaust port cooled to 40-50 degree C, processing equipment which comprises amine as a carbon dioxide absorber contacted with the smoke in the horizontal rotating cylindrical absorber, and the amine taken out from the horizontal rotating cylindrical absorber is guided to the amine regeneration unit, and the amine is heated to 110-130 degree C in the heating section of the amine regeneration section to recover carbon dioxide, and then cooled to 40-50 degree C, and the cooled amine is circulated to a horizontal rotating cylindrical absorber.
   USE - Device used for recovering carbon dioxide during manufacture of Portland cement (all claimed), and from blast furnace and petroleum plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The device recovers carbon dioxide with excellent efficiency and reduced cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:

   (2) manufacture of Portland cement, which involves connecting fresh lime firing furnace to the front stage of the horizontal rotating cylindrical absorber to fire the Portland cement clinker, discharging the exhaust gas from the fresh lime firing furnace by cooling the horizontal rotating cylindrical absorber to 40-50 degree C, contacting amine in the processing device with the smoke in the horizontal rotary cylindrical absorber, guiding amine to the amine regeneration section, heating amine to recover carbon dioxide, cooling, and circulating cooled amine to cylindrical absorber.

TI Removing catalyst in carbon dioxide-propylene            oxide copolymer by feeding propylene oxide-carbon            dioxide copolymer to dissolving tank, mixing obtained            product with acid solution, and feeding emulsion to            oil-water separator for layering.
AU LI Y
   LOU Z
   JIANG Y
   WANG D
   LIU M
   WANG Y
   SONG L
   YING L
   NIE Q
   WANG X
   ZHOU Q
   YU T
   ZHANG Z
   DONG W
   MENG Y
   NIE T
   LI W
   HE X
   DONG Q
AE SEDIN NINGBO ENG CO LTD (CNCE-C)
GA 202089560W
AB    NOVELTY - Method for removing a catalyst in a carbon                dioxide-propylene oxide copolymer, involves (a)                feeding propylene oxide-carbon dioxide copolymer                and solvent to a polypropylene carbonate dissolving                tank, controlling the temperature in the                polypropylene carbonate dissolving tank at 45-70                degrees C, and the pressure at 0.2-0.5 MpgG, and                after dissolution, obtaining a propylene                oxide-carbon dioxide copolymer mixture, where the                solvent is at least one of methylene chloride,                chloroform, carbon tetrachloride, toluene and                xylene, (b) feeding the propylene oxide-carbon                dioxide copolymer mixture to a static mixer,                uniformly mixing with an acid solution, and feeding                to a high-speed mixing tank for further blending to                form an emulsion, and (c) feeding the emulsion to                an oil-water separator for layering, washing the                bottom oil phase obtained with deionized water to                neutrality, drying.
   USE - The method is used for removing catalyst in a                carbon dioxide-propylene oxide copolymer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides the product with low heavy                metal content and high purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for removing a catalyst in a carbon                dioxide-propylene oxide copolymer, involves (a)                feeding propylene oxide-carbon dioxide copolymer                and solvent to a polypropylene carbonate dissolving                tank, controlling the temperature in the                polypropylene carbonate dissolving tank at 45-70                degrees C, and the pressure at 0.2-0.5 MpgG, and                after dissolution, obtaining a propylene                oxide-carbon dioxide copolymer mixture, where the                solvent is at least one of methylene chloride,                chloroform, carbon tetrachloride, toluene and                xylene, (b) feeding the propylene oxide-carbon                dioxide copolymer mixture to a static mixer,                uniformly mixing with an acid solution, and feeding                to a high-speed mixing tank for further blending to                form an emulsion, where the acid solution is an                aqueous solution of at least one of sulfuric acid,                hydrochloric acid and benzenesulfonic acid, and (c)                feeding the emulsion to an oil-water separator for                layering, washing the bottom oil phase obtained                with deionized water to neutrality, drying to                obtain a propylene oxide-carbon dioxide copolymer                product with the catalyst completely removed and                the residual heavy metal content of less than 110                ppm.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The high-speed mixing                  tank described in step (b) is provided with a                  mixing stirrer, and the rotating speed of the                  mixing stirrer is 30-120 rpm. Preferred Device:                  The top of the polypropylene carbonate dissolving                  tank in step (a) is provided with a polypropylene                  carbonate polymer feed port and a solvent feed                  port, where the bottom of the polypropylene                  carbonate polymer feed port is connected with a                  porous plate, and the solvent feed port is a                  connected to the bottom of the nozzle, and the                  outer periphery of the polypropylene carbonate                  dissolving tank is provided with a hot water                  jacket, and the polypropylene carbonate                  dissolving tank is axially provided with a                  stirrer. Preferred Properties: The feed quality                  of the solvent in step (a) is 0.3-0.9 times the                  feed quality of the propylene oxide-carbon                  dioxide copolymer. The mass concentration of the                  acid component in the acid solution in step (b)                  is 0.5-5%, and the feed mass of the acid solution                  is 34-60% of the feed mass of the propylene                  oxide-carbon dioxide copolymer.

PN CN111346600-A
TI Capturing carbon dioxide based on synergistic            effect of sodium silicate and carbonate, comprises e.g.            mixing sodium metasilicate nonahydrate and sodium            hydroxide, calcining, mixing with alkali metal            carbonate powder and post processing.
AU WANG Z
   TANG Z
   WANG J
   LIU W
   LING C
   WANG Y
   CAO Y
AE SHANGHAI INST APPLIED PHYSICS CHINESE AC (CAYW-C)
GA 2020630809
AB    NOVELTY - Capturing carbon dioxide based on synergistic                effect of sodium silicate and carbonate, comprises                e.g. (i) mixing sodium metasilicate nonahydrate and                sodium hydroxide, drying at 100-140&#176; C to                obtain sodium silicate precursor and calcining at                550-650&#176; C to obtain sodium silicate                powder, (ii) mechanically mixing sodium silicate                powder and alkali metal carbonate powder to obtain                mixed adsorbent, and (iii) placing mixed adsorbent                in the environment to be processed, maintaining                sodium silicate powder in the mixed adsorbent is                solid while forming alkali metal carbonate powder                into a melt, dispersing sodium silicate powder in                alkali metal carbonate melt with excellent                fluidity, combining carbon dioxide in the                environment to be treated with alkali metal                carbonate to obtain carbonate ions to enhance                solubility of carbon dioxide in the mixed adsorbent                to synergistically capture carbon dioxide while                carbon dioxide in the environment to be treated                with sodium silicate.
   USE - The method is useful for capturing carbon                dioxide based on synergistic effect of sodium                silicate and carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has excellent adsorption effect                and low cost; and is suitable for industrial                production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Capturing carbon dioxide based on synergistic                effect of sodium silicate and carbonate, comprises                (i) mixing sodium metasilicate nonahydrate and                sodium hydroxide, drying at 100-140&#176; C to                obtain sodium silicate precursor and calcining at                550-650&#176; C to obtain sodium silicate                powder, (ii) mechanically mixing sodium silicate                powder and alkali metal carbonate powder to obtain                mixed adsorbent, where the alkali metal carbonate                powder is lithium carbonate powder, sodium                carbonate powder and/or potassium carbonate powder,                and (iii) placing mixed adsorbent in the                environment to be processed, maintaining sodium                silicate powder in the mixed adsorbent is solid                while forming alkali metal carbonate powder into a                melt, dispersing sodium silicate powder in alkali                metal carbonate melt with excellent fluidity,                combining carbon dioxide in the environment to be                treated with alkali metal carbonate to obtain                carbonate ions (C2O52-) to enhance solubility of                carbon dioxide in the mixed adsorbent to                synergistically capture carbon dioxide while carbon                dioxide in the environment to be treated with                sodium silicate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (i): the drying                  temperature is 115-125&#176; C and the                  calcination temperature is 595-605&#176; C;                  the drying time is 11-13 hours and the                  calcination time is 30-90 minutes; and the sodium                  silicate precursor is ground and sieved after                  calcining to obtain sodium silicate powder having                  a particle size of 54-74 &#956; m. In step                  (ii), the sodium silicate powder and alkali metal                  carbonate powder are ground after mechanical                  mixing to obtain mixed adsorbent. In step (iii),                  the carbon dioxide in the environment to be                  treated combines with alkali metal carbonate at                  below 400&#176; C to form carbonate ions                  (C2O52-) to enhance solubility of carbon dioxide                  in the mixed adsorbent. Preferred Components: In                  step (i), the molar ratio between sodium silicate                  nonahydrate and sodium hydroxide is 1:2. In step                  (ii), the mass ratio between sodium silicate                  powder and alkali metal carbonate powder is                  1:0.5-2.

AB    NOVELTY - A natural gas hydrate mining method involves                selecting drilling location, according to location                of hydrate deposit, drilling production well to                inside of natural gas hydrate reservoir by                low-density mud drilling method (production well                includes injection well and collection well),                lowering pressure of the collection well below                natural gas hydrate phase equilibrium pressure,                performing decomposition of natural gas hydrate,                carrying out initial mining, injecting a gas                hydrate inhibitor into the hydrate reservoir                through the collection well, promoting                decomposition of natural gas hydrates, carrying out                initial mining, injecting carbon dioxide hydrate                inhibitor into the gas hydrate reservoir through                the injection well followed by separating mixed gas                to obtain pure methane gas.

   ZHANG L
   CHEN G
   SUN C
   XIAO P
AE UNIV LIAONING SHIHUA (ULNS-C)
GA 201874262Q
AB    NOVELTY - The method for separating carbon dioxide in                carbon dioxide/methane mixed gas involves (a)                drying activated carbon and cooling to obtain a dry                activated carbon, (b) uniformly mixing                2-methylimidazole, ethylene glycol and ethanol to                obtain a mixed solution and mixing the dried                activated carbon and the mixed solution to obtain a                solid-liquid mixture, (c) leaving the solid-liquid                mixture, drying, monitoring the pore volume                occupancy of the ethanol in the activated carbon                during the drying process and completing the drying                process when the pore volume occupancy of the                ethanol in the activated carbon is reached to be 10                to obtain modified activated carbon, (d) placing                the modified activated carbon in a sapphire kettle,                connecting the sapphire kettle to a balance tank                through a pipeline with a valve, closing the valve                on a communication line between the sapphire kettle                and the balance tank, evacuating the balance tank                and the connected pipeline.
   USE - Method is useful for separating carbon dioxide                in carbon dioxide/methane mixed gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the separation effect of                activated carbon on carbon dioxide/methane                mixture.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The method for separating carbon dioxide in                carbon dioxide/methane mixed gas involves (a)                drying activated carbon at 120 degrees C under                vacuum conditions for 24 hours and cooling to room                temperature to obtain a dry activated carbon, (b)                uniformly mixing 2-methylimidazole, ethylene glycol                and ethanol in a mass ratio of 1.01:1.64:10 to                obtain a mixed solution and mixing the dried                activated carbon and the mixed solution at a mass                ratio of 3.3:12.65 to obtain a solid-liquid                mixture, (c) leaving the solid-liquid mixture under                vacuum condition for 1 week, drying at 80 degrees                C, monitoring the pore volume occupancy of the                ethanol in the activated carbon during the drying                process and completing the drying process when the                pore volume occupancy of the ethanol in the                activated carbon is reached to be 10 to obtain                modified activated carbon, and (d) placing the                modified activated carbon in a sapphire kettle,                connecting the sapphire kettle to a balance tank                through a pipeline with a valve, closing the valve                on a communication line between the sapphire kettle                and the balance tank, evacuating the balance tank                and the connected pipeline, exhausting air in the                balance tank and its connected pipeline, driving                carbon dioxide/methane mixed gas into the balance                tank to a preset pressure of 3.70 MPa, evacuating                the sapphire kettle and its connected pipeline,                opening the valve on the communication line between                the balance tank and the sapphire kettle, mixing                the carbon dioxide/methane in the balance tank,                injecting gas into sapphire kettle, where the                pressure in the sapphire kettle is stable,                indicating that the adsorption process of the                modified activated carbon in the sapphire kettle                reaches equilibrium, completing the separation of                carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide/methane mixed                gas, recording pressure readings in the equilibrium                tank and the sapphire kettle during the adsorption                equilibrium, subjecting the equilibrium gas phase                in the sapphire kettle to chromatograph to obtain                molar percentage concentration of methane and the                molar percentage of carbon dioxide in the                equilibrium gas phase in the sapphire kettle after                adsorption equilibrium, calculating the adsorption                amount of carbon dioxide in the carbon                dioxide/methane mixed gas by modified activated                carbon.

PT P
PN KR2244682-B1
TI Packaging method of processed food for vending machine, involves fusing cover element to edge/opening of container by heat energy provided to frame, and sealing opening of container by cutting extending cover element is fused to container.
AU SHIN H S
AE AUTO CHEF CO LTD (AUTO-Non-standard)
GA 2021427268
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves slidably installing the fusion frame on the second sliding shaft. The rod element protrudes from the pressure cylinder when the container (82) is arranged under the sealing bonding portion (30). The support frame (32) descends to surround the rim of the container and is seated on the rotating frame (12). The fusion frame descends and is in close contact with the rim of the container, so that the cover element (80) is moved by the thermal energy provided to the fusion frame. The cover element is fused to the edge of the opening of the container by the heat energy provided to the frame to provide a sealed space. The cutting frame descends when the rod element protrudes further. The cutting frame in the shape of a closed curve formed larger than the rim of the container descends to the outside of the container. The cover element seals the opening of the container by cutting the extending cover element is fused to the container.
   USE - Packaging method of processed food e.g. ramen and instant noodle for vending machine.
   ADVANTAGE - The storage period of the processed food stored in the vending machine is remarkably extended, so that the time and cost required for maintenance of the vending machine is reduced. The cover element is fused to the container, since the injection hole for supplying nitrogen gas and carbon dioxide gas is formed in the support frame forming the sealing bonding portion. The nitrogen gas and carbon dioxide gas are ejected through the injection hole and supplied to the space between the container and the cover element when the support frame seats the cover element on the upper surface of the container element. The packaging of the processed food is completed while forming a closed space in which nitrogen gas and carbon dioxide gas are injected while being fused to the container, so there is an advantage that the packaging can be easily completed in a state in which nitrogen gas and carbon dioxide gas are injected into the container. The operation of a tension holding unit provides a tension by which the cover element is pulled tight when the cover element is fused to the container and sealed. The malfunction in which the cover element is fused to the container in a folded state is prevented when the cover element is pulled and fused to the container.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a processed food for a vending machine.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view showing a packaging device used in a processed food packaging method.
   Rotating frame (12)
   Sealing bonding portion (30)
   Support frame (32)
   Cover element (80)
   Container (82)

PN US2020017787-A1; WO2020010431-A1; US10760024-B2; CA3102390-A1; CN112424319-A; EP3820971-A1; US2021275961-A1; EP3820971-A4
TI Upgrading of biogas e.g. renewable natural gas,            involves obtaining biogas, removing component from            biogas, in which component comprising hydrogen sulfide,            and/or carbon dioxide, transporting vessel, and            injecting renewable natural gas.
AU FOODY P J
   DECHMAN J
AE IOGEN CORP (IOGE-Non-standard)
   IOGEN CORP (IOGE-Non-standard)
   IOGEN CORP (IOGE-Non-standard)
   IOGEN CORP (IOGE-Non-standard)
GA 2020059406
AB    NOVELTY - Upgrading of biogas involves (10) obtaining a                biogas from a several of biogas sources, in which                biogas comprising a biogas (A) from a biogas source                (A') and a biogas (B) from a biogas source (B'),                (20) removing one or more components from the                biogas (A) to produce a partially purified biogas                (a) having a non-methane content of at least 10%,                in which one or more components comprising hydrogen                sulfide, and/or carbon dioxide, and components                removed using at least one stationary purification                system, (c) transporting a vessel (I) containing                the partially purified biogas (a') to a biogas                upgrading facility, (d) removing one or more                components from the biogas (B) to produce a                partially purified biogas (b'), and (e) optionally,                transporting a vessel (II) containing the partially                purified biogas (b') to the biogas upgrading                facility.
   USE - Upgrading biogas e.g. renewable natural gas                used as transportation fuel in mobile storage tank                of vehicle such as truck, rail car, or ship (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical upgrading of                biogas with improved efficiency of hub-and-spoke                configuration, capable of improving the compression                and/or transport to the centralized biogas                upgrading facility, improved flow rate, dilute                impurity, and average out variability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Upgrading of biogas involves (10) obtaining a                biogas from a several of biogas sources, in which                biogas comprising a biogas (A) from a biogas source                (A') and a biogas (B) from a biogas source (B'),                (20) removing one or more components from the                biogas (A) to produce a partially purified biogas                (a) having a non-methane content of at least 10%,                in which one or more components comprising hydrogen                sulfide, and/or carbon dioxide, and components                removed using at least one stationary purification                system, (c) transporting a vessel (I) containing                the partially purified biogas (a') to a biogas                upgrading facility, (d) removing one or more                components from the biogas (B) to produce a                partially purified biogas (b'), (e) optionally,                transporting a vessel (II) containing the partially                purified biogas (b') to the biogas upgrading                facility, (f) removing at least one component from                a gas stream comprising the partially purified                biogas (a'), and/or the partially purified biogas                (b') to produce renewable natural gas having a                heating value that is more than a heating value of                any one of the partially purified biogases (a') and                (b'), and (g) injecting the obtained renewable                natural gas into a distribution system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram of upgrading                biogas.
   Obtaining gas (10)
   Removing at least one of water, hydrogen                sulfide, and carbon dioxide from biogas (20)
   Transporting partially purified biogas                (40)
   Further purifying partially purified biogas                (50)
   Providing renewable natural gas (60)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Properties: The vessel (I) is                  filled with partially purified biogas (a') at                  pressure of at least 1000 psig. Preferred                  Components: The vessels (I) and (II) are powered                  by natural gas, and renewable natural gas.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The partially purified                  biogas (a') has heating value of at least 750-925                  BTU/scf. The biogas sources are chosen to provide                  the biogas upgrading facility with biogas at rate                  of at least 2000 SCFM, preferably at least 5000                  SCFM. Preferred Process: In the step (b),                  removing biogas (A) comprising at least 50%,                  preferably at least 85% carbon dioxide. In step                  (b), the biogas (A) from the biogas source (A')                  is subjected to a membrane separation. The method                  further involves feeding the biogas (A) into a                  stationary purification system (1) to remove                  hydrogen sulfide, and/or carbon dioxide, from the                  biogas (A) and produce a partially purified                  biogas (a') having a heating value that does not                  exceed 900 BTU/scf, feeding the biogas (B) into a                  other stationary purification system (2) to                  remove hydrogen sulfide, and/or carbon dioxide,                  from the biogas (B) and produce a partially                  purified biogas (b') having a heating value that                  does not exceed 900 BTU/scf, and feeding the                  partially purified biogases (a'), and (b') into                  one or more purification systems to remove carbon                  dioxide, nitrogen, and/or oxygen, to produce                  renewable natural gas having a heating value of                  at least 950 BTU/scf Preferred Composition: The                  partially purified biogas (a') comprises less                  than 8% carbon dioxide, and at least 5% nitrogen.                  Preferred Components: The component further                  comprises water, and hydrogen sulfide. The biogas                  upgrading facility is connected to the biogas                  source (B') by piping system, in which biogas (B)                  comprises landfill gas.

PT P
PN CN108967009-A
TI Improving selenium content of white tea by selecting soil, removing weeds, applying selenium-enriching agent, adding water, applying complex grass ash, spraying selenium-rich foliar fertilizer, and increasing carbon dioxide concentration.
AU FENG Y
   CHEN L
   LIU Y
   XIANG Q
   WANG X
   ZHAN Z
AE GUIZHOU QIANRENCHA ECOLOGICAL AGRIC TOUR (GUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 2018A1833L
AB    NOVELTY - Method for improving selenium content of white tea involves (a) selecting soil, ventilating sunny land and turning surface deeper, removing weeds and roots, applying selenium-enriching agent/acre, finely cutting the surface, adding water, draining overnight, laying the film, removing the film, turning the surface, applying complex grass ash/acre, finely leveling the surface, and opening planting ditch and draining ditch, (b) spraying selenium-rich foliar fertilizer once at the back of leaves for first 5 days of first picking of white tea and spraying selenium-enriched foliar fertilizer for second time during March and September each year, the is sprayed in the same way and (c) applying 2000-2500 kg/acre selenium-enriched farmyard manure to tea tree every year in February and November, after the white tea begins to pick tea and increasing the carbon dioxide concentration in white tea plantation to 3-5% at 11 noon in 3 days after fertilization.
   USE - The method is useful for improving selenium content of white tea.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the absorption rate of selenium in soil, reduces the storage of sulfate in soil or tea trees, promotes absorption of selenium by tea trees, increases carbon dioxide content in tea garden and improves photosynthesis of tea trees.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for improving selenium content of white tea involves (a) selecting soil having a depth of greater than or equal to 70 cm during first winter of white tea planting, ventilating the sunny land as white tea plantation, and turning surface deeper by 40-50 cm, removing weeds and roots, applying 1800-2000 kg selenium-enriching agent/acre, finely cutting the surface, adding enough water, draining overnight, and laying the film for winter, removing the film during spring of March next year, again turning the surface to 40-50 cm to remove weeds and roots, naturally ventilating for 2-3 days, adjusting the pH to 7.1-7.5, applying 350-500 kg complex grass ash/acre, finely leveling the surface, and opening planting ditch and draining ditch, (b) spraying selenium-rich foliar fertilizer once at the back of leaves for first 5 days of first picking of white tea and spraying selenium-enriched foliar fertilizer for second time during March and September each year, the is sprayed in the same way and (c) applying 2000-2500 kg/acre selenium-enriched farmyard manure to tea tree every year in February and November, after the white tea begins to pick tea and increasing the carbon dioxide concentration in white tea plantation to 3-5% at 11 noon in 3 days after fertilization.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Composition: The selenium-enriching agent comprises 300-325 pts. wt. decomposed manure with a length of less than or equal to 5cm, 30-35 pts. wt. plant ash, 25-30 pts. wt. bean cake, 15-20 pts. wt. humus, 8-12 pts. wt. red bean skin powder, 15-20 pts. wt. peanut shell powder, 7-10 pts. wt. peanut skin powder, 8-12 pts. wt. cinnamon skin powder, 7-12 pts. wt. shrimp skin powder, 3-5 pts. wt. seaweed, 1-3 pts. wt. chicken, 12-15 pts. wt. quicklime, 1-2 pts. wt. 50% carbendazim, 3-5 pts. wt. superphosphate, 0.5-1 pt. wt. cesium chloride, 0.5-1 pt. wt. potassium selenate and 0.5-1 pt. wt. sodium selenite. The selenium-enriched foliar fertilizer is a 500-fold dilution of the longan juice and further comprises 10 wt.% pineapple juice, 3 wt.% wheat germ extract, 1 wt.% calcium superphosphate, 2 wt.% potassium dihydrogen phosphate, 0.5 wt.% boric acid, 0.3 wt.% sodium selenate, 0.2 wt.% diluted potassium selenite, 0.1 wt.% barium chloride, and 0.5 wt.% carbendazim wettable powder (50%). The selenium-enriched farmyard fertilizer comprises 100-150 pts. wt. decomposed manure, 30-35 pts. wt. decomposed cow dung, 20-25 pts. wt. decomposed chicken manure, 15-20 pts. wt. decomposed sheep manure, 5-12 pts. wt. peanut skin, 5-9 pts. wt. red bean skin, 4-7 pts. wt. longan shell, 5-8 pts. wt. pineapple skin, 3-5 pts. wt. nitrogen fertilizer, 1-2 pts. wt. superphosphate, 1-1.5 pts. wt. potassium dihydrogen phosphate, 0.8-1.5 pts. wt. boric acid, 0.5-1 pt. wt. selenic acid, 0.5-1 pt. wt. selenous acid and 50-5% pts. wt. carbendazim wettable powder. Preferred Components: The complex grass ash comprises grass ash, rapeseed cake powder, barium chloride, potassium selenate and sodium selenite in a ratio of 700:200:2:1:1. Preferred Method: The surface pH is adjusted by spraying surface with 500-fold dilution of 50% sodium bicarbonate or 300-fold dilution of 80% sodium bicarbonate and plowing 10-30 cm of surface once per spray to measure the soil pH to 7.1-7.5. The selenium-enriched foliar fertilizer is applied by filtering using 325 mesh screen for 1 or 2 times during sunny day and evening. The selenium-enriched farmyard manure is applied by plowing the tea garden once, fertilizing the soil to a depth of 3-5 cm, and adding root water.

The antibacterial agent is zirconium phosphate nano silver powder, triclosan, and triclocarban. The disintegrating agent is sodium carboxymethyl starch, modified corn starch, cross-linked polyvinylpyrrolidone, and croscarmellose sodium. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing pet washing tablet comprising (1) weighing anionic surfactant, non-ionic surfactant, humectant, regulating agents, effervescing carbon dioxide source, antibacterial agent, disintegrating agent, ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid disodium salt, essence, calamine and uniformly mixing, (2) granulating by 25-mesh sieve, (3) adding effervescent agent acid source, uniformly mixing, tableting, where the tablet shape is cylindrical, the diameter is 10-25 mm, and the height is 3-10 mm, and (4) placing tablet prepared in the step (3) into the pressing workshop for 6 hours hardening to obtain the pet washing tablets.

The flow rate of the                bottom blowing gas containing carbon dioxide of the                tapping molten steel is increased to 1000-1600                Nl/minutes to perform pre-refining to obtain                product.
   USE - Pre-refining steel tapping with bottom blowing                carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has low pre-refining cost,                pre-refining desulfurizing rate of 47.1-54.7%, good                desulfurizing effect, and low cost, and is easy to                popularize.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The target carbon                  content of the tapping molten steel is 0.1-0.35%.                  The total pressure of bottom blowing is 0.3-0.8                  megaPascal. The tapping time is 3-10 minutes for                  blocking slag tapping during the tapping process.                  The 4-8kg/t of slagging agent is added for                  slagging, and making slag agent comprises 50-70                  wt.% calcium oxide, 5-10 wt.% aluminum oxide and                  rest is calcium fluoride. The pre-refining time                  is 3-4 minutes. The weight of the molten steel is                  280-320t. The carbon dioxide-containing gas is a                  mixed gas of carbon dioxide and argon, the                  tapping molten steel target N mass score is                  0.002%;. when the carbon dioxide-containing gas                  is a mixed gas of carbon dioxide and nitrogen,                  the target N mass fraction of the molten steel                  for tapping is 0.002%. Preferred Components: The                  gas containing carbon dioxide is carbon dioxide                  gas, a mixed gas of carbon dioxide and argon, a                  mixed gas of carbon dioxide and argon.
DC M24 (Metallurgy of iron and steel - including manufacture and processing, treatment of steel melts and changing the physical properties of iron and steel, control/testing methods, blast furnaces and converters. Metallurgical coking processes (C21, C10B).)

AU SHEN Z
   ZHANG Y
   WANG C
   YU Q
   HUANG X
   MIAO H
   KONG L
AE UNIV TONGJI (UYTJ-C)
GA 201865921M
AB    NOVELTY - Reducing carbon dioxide involves adding 1-80 millimole glycerol solution into stainless steel reaction kettle with polytetrafluoroethylene internal lining of 100 mL volume, adding 0.1-5 g alkali and 0.1-10 g of carbon dioxide. The 1-100 mg of catalyst Carbonylchlorohydrido(bis (2-(diphenylphosphinoethyl))amino)ruthenium is added, stirred uniformly, sealed, and placed in rotating oven for heating at 100-300 degrees C for 1-48 hours, and then quenched, and acidified to obtain product. The obtained solid catalyst is filtered, and dried.
   USE - Method for reducing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to reduce carbon dioxide that is more safe, green and sustainable, has good effect, high efficiency and high repeated utilization rate, and in cost effective manner.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The glycerol is common glycerol or biological diesel by-product acid. The carbon dioxide is dry ice, hydrogencarbonate or carbonate. The base is alkaline hydroxide or alkaline oxide. The catalyst is a ruthenium chelate containing Carbonylchlorohydrido(bis(2-(diphenylphosphinoethyl))amino)ruthenium. Preferred Process: The filtering process is carried out by filtering sand core by filter pump with filter paper is 0.22 microns. The drying temperature of 60 degrees C in air atmosphere for 2-3 minutes.

AE WACKER D (WACK-Individual)
GA 2018332017

The shut-off device does not increase the temperature of the liquid carbon dioxide.
   USE - The device is useful for producing dry ice (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device produces dry ice quickly and ensures homogeneous formation of dry ice in improved manner.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic longitudinal sectional view of the device.

   Pressure vessel (2)
   Carbon dioxide reservoir (4)

At least one extraction mandrel is provided for continuous expulsion of the dry ice produced from the pressure vessel or device is provided for endless drawing by using pistons, where extraction mandrel or device is movable via at least one eccentric drive. At least one piston with at least one passage opening is provided for passing and introducing liquid carbon dioxide into the interior of the pressure vessel, preferably into a gap between the pistons. The passage opening is arranged in the longitudinal direction of the piston. The shut-off device is provided in operative connection with the passage opening in the piston and/or in the region of at least one lateral inlet opening in a wall of inner tube of the pressure vessel, preferably in a feed line to the passage opening and/or in a feed line to inlet opening in the wall of inner tube. At least one check valve (8) is provided for preventing backflow of carbon dioxide from the pressure vessel to a carbon dioxide reservoir (4) and/or from the pressure vessel in a feed line to the carbon dioxide reservoir. The check valve is provided in the wall of inner tube, in the feed line to through hole in the piston and/or in the region of passage opening in piston. The pressure vessel comprises at least one inner tube and at least one outer tube, where the outer tube has at least one internal thread and the inner tube has at least one external thread. The thread of external thread and thread of internal thread engage with each other such that at least one thread clearance is provided between the external and internal threads for passing at least one refrigerant.
