PN WO2021194411-A1; SE202030099-A1; SE544012-C2
TI Method for mineralizing carbon dioxide, involves mixing surfactant and high-pressure air creating foam, and mixing foam with cement paste together with liquid carbon dioxide being at below freezing temperatures.
AU LARSSON E
AE AERCEMENT AB (AERC-Non-standard)
GA 2021B3677H
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves mixing cement and water with a rotating mixer screw creating a cement paste. A surfactant and high-pressure air are mixed creating a foam. The foam is mixed with the cement paste together with liquid carbon dioxide being at below freezing temperatures. The carbon dioxide is reacted with cement creating calcium carbonate. A material having a density in the range from 75 to 2000 kilogram per cubic meter (kg/m3) is obtained.
   USE - Method for mineralizing carbon dioxide for insulation and fill materials at lower densities and construction and building material at higher densities.
   ADVANTAGE - The material can be used for insulation and fill materials at lower densities and construction and building material at higher densities. It can be made in densities from 75-2000 kg/m3 depending on the ratios of the ingredients. The mechanical strength of the material increases with densities, and the material is not a concrete mix where sand or gravel is added to cement and water creating concrete. The production of this material at the higher range densities will mineralize more carbon dioxide than the carbon dioxide emissions from the production of the cement, making it carbon dioxide negative.

PN CA3150871-A1; US2022288557-A1; EP4053084-A1; US2022288556-A1
TI Treating metal carbonate salt by hydrolyzing metal            halide salt to form hydrohalic acid and hydroxide salt            of metal in halide salt and reacting hydrohalic acid            with metal carbonate salt having the salt of alkaline            earth metal or alkali metal.
AU MARTIN C L
AE EERC ENERGY & ENVIRONMENTAL RES CENT (EERC-Non-standard)
   EERC ENERGY & ENVIRONMENTAL RES CENT (EERC-Non-standard)
   EERC ENERGY & ENVIRONMENTAL RES CENT (EERC-Non-standard)
GA 2022B3839E
AB    NOVELTY - Treating a metal carbonate salt, comprises:                hydrolyzing a metal halide salt to form a                hydrohalic acid and a hydroxide salt of the metal                in the metal halide salt, the metal comprising an                alkaline earth metal or an alkali metal; and                reacting the hydrohalic acid with the metal                carbonate salt, where the metal carbonate salt is a                carbonate salt of the alkaline earth metal or                alkali metal to form carbon dioxide and the metal                halide salt, where at least some of the metal                halide salt formed from the reacting of the                hydrohalic acid with the metal carbonate salt is                recycled as at least some of the metal halide salt                in the hydrolyzing of the metal halide salt to form                the hydrohalic acid and the hydroxide salt.
   USE - The method is useful for treating metal                carbonate salt.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: treats metal carbonate salt can be                used as an alternative to the calcination of                limestone for the production of hydrated lime; is                beneficial compared to conventional limestone                calcinations is use of less energy e.g. minimum                required thermal energy can be lowered by 28%; has                benefits of require proprietary, tailored materials                that may introduce bottlenecks to future                large-scale deployment; and beneficial leverages                two benefit of calcium chloride splitting compared                to a sodium chloride-based process, resulting in                transformational improvement in ocean calcium                chloride removal.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   regenerating a used hydroxide-based carbon                dioxide-capture sorbent, comprising hydrolyzing                calcium chloride to form hydrochloric acid and                calcium hydroxide, reacting the used                hydroxide-based carbon dioxide-capture sorbent with                the calcium hydroxide to form calcium carbonate and                reacting the hydrochloric acid with the calcium                carbonate to form carbon dioxide and calcium                chloride, where at least some of the calcium                chloride formed from the reacting of the                hydrochloric acid with the calcium carbonate is                recycled as at least some of the calcium chloride                in the hydrolyzing of the calcium chloride to form                the hydrochloric acid and the calcium hydroxide;                and
   oftening water comprising hydrolyzing calcium                chloride to form hydrochloric acid and calcium                hydroxide, reacting calcium bicarbonate from a                water source with the calcium hydroxide to form                calcium carbonate and reacting the hydrochloric                acid with the calcium carbonate to form carbon                dioxide and calcium chloride, where at least some                of the calcium chloride formed from the reacting of                the hydrochloric acid with the calcium carbonate is                recycled as at least some of the calcium chloride                in the hydrolyzing of the calcium chloride to form                the hydrochloric acid and the calcium                hydroxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises: reacting a used carbon dioxide-capture                  sorbent with the hydroxide salt to provide the                  metal carbonate salt that is a carbonate salt of                  the metal in the metal halide salt; contacting a                  carbon dioxide-capture sorbent with carbon                  dioxide to form the used carbon dioxide-capture                  sorbent; and reacting a bicarbonate salt, where                  the bicarbonate salt is sodium bicarbonate,                  magnesium Bicarbonate, calcium bicarbonate,                  and/or potassium bicarbonate, with the hydroxide                  salt to provide the metal carbonate salt. The                  hydrolyzing of the metal halide salt is performed                  at a temperature of 300-500&#176; C. The                  reacting of the hydrohalic acid with the metal                  carbonate salt is performed at a pressure of                  0.1-9 MPa. The reacting of the hydrohalic acid                  with the metal carbonate salt is performed at a                  temperature of room temperature to 500&#176;                  C. The metal halide salt formed from the reacting                  of the hydrohalic acid with the metal carbonate                  salt is 80-100 wt.% the metal halide salt used in                  the hydrolyzing of the metal halide salt to form                  the hydrohalic acid and the hydroxide salt. The                  hydrolyzing of the metal halide salt and the                  reacting of the hydrohalic acid with the metal                  carbonate salt is performed together in a                  pressurized reactor. The reacting of the used                  carbon dioxide-capture sorbent with the hydroxide                  salt to form the metal carbonate salt also forms                  an unused carbon dioxide-capture sorbent, further                  comprising providing the unused carbon                  dioxide-capture sorbent for carbon dioxide                  capture. The hydroxide salt of the metal formed                  in the hydrolysis of the metal halide salt to                  form the hydrohalic acid and the hydroxide salt                  is recycled as at least some of the hydroxide                  salt used in the reacting of the used carbon                  dioxide-capture sorbent with the hydroxide salt.                  The hydroxide salt of the metal formed in the                  hydrolysis of the metal halide salt to form the                  hydrohalic acid and the hydroxide salt is                  recycled as at least some of the hydroxide salt                  used in the reacting of the bicarbonate salt with                  the hydroxide salt. Preferred Components: The                  metal carbonate salt is calcium carbonate, the                  metal halide salt is calcium chloride, the                  hydrohalic acid is hydrochloric acid, and the                  hydroxide salt is calcium hydroxide. The calcium                  carbonate is produced from a carbon                  dioxide-capture sorbent, is a calcium carbonate                  precipitate formed from water softening and/or is                  natural limestone. The hydrolyzing of the metal                  halide salt is performed at a pressure of 0.1-9                  MPa. The carbon dioxide-capture sorbent is                  calcium bicarbonate, magnesium bicarbonate,                  potassium carbonate and/or sodium carbonate. The                  bicarbonate salt is calcium bicarbonate. The                  method is a method of softening water, where the                  bicarbonate salt is from a natural water                  source.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of general                procedure that do not support the claims.

GA 202252797N
AB    NOVELTY - Producing hydrogen from natural gas with a high carbon dioxide content comprises (i) reacting the natural gas mixture with air in a methane pyrolysis reactor to obtain a product mixture comprising hydrogen, carbon and water to produce mixture of products with a high purity hydrogen content of greater than 60% for more than 2 hours of reaction time, and the used catalyst is a carbon catalyst with a composition, by 99 wt.% carbon, (ii) separating the product mixture to obtain a mixture of residual methane and hydrogen, where the resulting water is condensed into a liquid phase and separated from the gas stream, carbon is attached to the catalyst in the solid phase, and (iii) subjecting the gas mixture in step (ii) to pass through hydrogen gas purification device by a catalyst and pressure to remove the residual methane gas in the mixture to obtain hydrogen gas.
   USE - The method is useful for producing hydrogen from natural gas with a high carbon dioxide content.
   ADVANTAGE - None given.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing hydrogen from natural gas with a high carbon dioxide content comprises (i) reacting the natural gas mixture with air in a methane pyrolysis reactor to obtain a product mixture comprising hydrogen, carbon and water, where the used natural gas has a 5-30 vol.% carbon dioxide, and methane, the catalytic methane pyrolysis reactor is operated at 500-700 degrees C and pressure is 1-5 atm (101.325-506.625 KPa) to produce mixture of products with a high purity hydrogen content of greater than 60% for more than 2 hours of reaction time, and the used catalyst is a carbon catalyst with a composition, by 99 wt.% carbon, (ii) separating the product mixture to obtain a mixture of residual methane and hydrogen, where the resulting water is condensed into a liquid phase and separated from the gas stream, carbon is attached to the catalyst in the solid phase, and (iii) subjecting the gas mixture in step (ii) to pass through hydrogen gas purification device by a catalyst and pressure to remove the residual methane gas in the mixture to obtain hydrogen gas, where the resulting hydrogen is more than 90 vol.%, the refining device used is a pressure-switched absorber (PSA), the used catalyst comprises zeolite 4A, zeolite 5A, activated carbon, and aluminum oxide, and the pressure adsorption temperature and adsorption pressure are room temperature and pressure of 5 atm.

PN US2020003750-A1; EP3588083-A1; CA3047571-A1; FR3083316-A1; CN110658322-A; IN201944024922-A; BR102019013141-A2; FR3083316-B1; RU2019119596-A; US11442053-B2; EP3588083-B1
TI Method for quantifying pyritic sulfur in            sedimentary rock sample, involves heating sample in            inert atmosphere, continuously measuring amount of            hydrocarbon-based compounds followed by continuously            measuring carbon dioxide released.
AU ABOUSSOU A
   LAMOUREUX-VAR V
   PILLOT D
   KOWALEWSKI I
   GARCIA B
   WAGNER T
   MAERZ C
   MARZ C
   LAMOUREUX V V
   KOVALEVSKI I
   MARZ CH
AE IFP ENERGIES NOUVELLES (INSF-C)
   IFP ENERGIES NOUVELLES (INSF-C)
   IFP ENERGIES NOUVELLES (INSF-C)
   IFP ENERGIES NOUVELLES (INSF-C)
   IFP ENERGIES NOUVELLES (INSF-C)
GA 2020015519
AB    NOVELTY - A pyritic sulfur in a sedimentary rock sample                quantifying method involves heating sample in an                inert atmosphere. Amount of hydrocarbon-based                compounds, an amount of carbon monoxide and an                amount of carbon dioxide released during the first                temperature sequence are continuously measured.                portion of the effluents resulting from the heating                of the sample in an inert atmosphere is                continuously oxidized, an amount of sulfur dioxide                released by the oxidation of the effluents as a                function of the time of the heating in an inert                atmosphere is continuously measured. The residue of                the sample resulting from the heating in an inert                atmosphere is heated in an oxidizing atmosphere                between a third temperature of 280-320 degrees C                and a fourth temperature of greater than or equal                to 800 degrees C, while following a second                temperature sequence, and an amount of carbon                monoxide and an amount of carbon dioxide released                during the second temperature sequence are                continuously measured.
   USE - Method for quantifying pyritic sulfur in a                sedimentary rock sample, such as a marine clay rich                in organic matter.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables quantifying pyritic sulfur                in a sedimentary rock sample in simple and rapid                manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A pyritic sulfur in a sedimentary rock sample                quantifying method involves heating sample in an                inert atmosphere, between a first temperature of                80-320 degrees C and a second temperature of                600-700 degrees C. Amount of hydrocarbon-based                compounds, an amount of carbon monoxide and an                amount of carbon dioxide released during the first                temperature sequence are continuously measured.                portion of the effluents resulting from the heating                of the sample in an inert atmosphere is                continuously oxidized, an amount of sulfur dioxide                released by the oxidation of the effluents as a                function of the time of the heating in an inert                atmosphere is continuously measured, and pyrolysis                pyritic sulfur content is determined from the                amount of sulfur dioxide. The residue of the sample                resulting from the heating in an inert atmosphere                is heated in an oxidizing atmosphere between a                third temperature of 280-320 degrees C and a fourth                temperature of greater than or equal to 800 degrees                C, while following a second temperature sequence,                and an amount of carbon monoxide and an amount of                carbon dioxide released during the second                temperature sequence are continuously measured,                where pyritic sulfur content contained in the                sample is determined on the basis of expression as                given in the specification.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The sample is                  reservoir rock type, in which the first                  temperature is 100-200 degrees C, conventional                  source rock or immature shale play type, in which                  the first temperature is 280-320 degrees C,                  gas-bearing or oil-bearing shale play type, in                  which the first temperature is 80-20 degrees C.                  The rock sample is clay type, marl type, and                  limestone type.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); Q49 (Mining (E21)); S03 (Scientific Instrumentation)

   LENG A
   GUO C
   LIU G
   MA S
AE DALIAN SHIPBUILDING IND CORP (CSHI-C)
GA 2022C0614M
AB    NOVELTY - The invention claims an offshore supercritical                CO2 injection sealing system, comprising two liquid                CO2 storage tanks set in the injection platform                cabin, each storage tank is provided with a liquid                CO2 deep well pump; the liquid CO2 is transported                to the liquid CO2 main pipe through deep well pump,                and enters the current stabilizing buffer tank; the                liquid CO2 in the stable buffer tank gas-liquid                separation, the liquid CO2 pipeline into the                filling pump; after primary pressurizing by the                filling pump, entering into the pressurizing pump,                after pressurizing, reaching the injection pressure                required, and then the liquid CO2 pipeline enters                the heater to be heated to reach the supercritical                state; then injecting manifold through                supercritical CO2, supercritical CO2 injection                pipeline, injecting well to reach the salt water                layer. The CO2 supercritical process of the                invention of the invention is designed to                pressurize and then raise the temperature, the                process has the lowest energy consumption, it also                can realize supercritical CO2 safety, continuous,                efficient injection of saline water layer.

AB    NOVELTY - A transaction management system based on                virtual currency, comprising: a trading platform                for casting a plurality of ownership certificates;                a leasing platform having a first wallet for                storing the plurality of ownership certificates,                and recording the corresponding ownership                certificates The carbon dioxide absorption amount                and the profit sharing amount of the tree, select                at least one ownership certificate whose sum of                carbon dioxide absorption amount is not less than                one carbon footprint; an electronic device, which                has a second wallet, and transmits a transaction                request to the leasing platform, so that the                leasing The platform generates at least one usage                right certificate relative to the at least one                ownership certificate through the trading platform,                and transfers the at least one usage right                certificate to the second wallet, and the leasing                platform obtains a profit share relative to the at                least one usage right certificate The amount is                converted into virtual currency and sent to the                second wallet.

AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a fence device for forest pipe protection technical field comprising an upright post, two ends of the upright post are welded with a sleeve pipe, the inside of the sleeve pipe is inserted with a beam, the utility model through the photocatalyst lamp set can attract the mosquito near the photo-catalyst lamp, Because the mosquito has phototaxis, it can attract the ultraviolet light, the photocatalyst lamp uses the most favoured ultraviolet light tempting mosquito lamp, and omnidirectional light emitting, adding micro-temperature effect, and the photocatalyst lamp can react with the harmful gas in the air, releasing the carbon dioxide and gas flow, simulating carbon dioxide moist gas emitted by the human body, inducing the mosquito to fly, after the mosquito flies, the air pump can extract the gas through the air inlet hopper, so the mosquito will be extracted into the collecting box under the driving of the air flow, finally, grill to death by an electric heater, so that the fence device reaches the effect of killing mosquito.

PN CN114586977-A
TI Aerospace antioxidant lycopene microcapsule            comprises core material i.e. lycopene extracted by            supercritical carbon dioxide, and the wall material is            gum Arabic, sodium alginate and maltodextrin.
AU CHEN S
   LIU L
   ZENG P
   LIU T
   YU Y
   SHEN Q
AE LEE KUM KEE FOOD CO LTD (LEEK-Non-standard)
   SPACENTER SPACE SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (SPAC-Non-standard)
GA 202280745T
AB    NOVELTY - Aerospace antioxidant lycopene microcapsule                comprises core material i.e. lycopene extracted by                supercritical carbon dioxide, and the wall material                is gum Arabic, sodium alginate and                maltodextrin.
   USE - Used as aerospace antioxidant lycopene                microcapsule.
   ADVANTAGE - animal experiments have proved that it can                obviously improve the oxidative stress of rats                caused by simulated weightlessness, and is suitable                for use in aerospace environment .
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing Aerospace antioxidant lycopene                microcapsule comprising (1) preparing core by using                supercritical CO2 extraction technology to                preparation the lycopene from the tomato fruit, (2)                preparing material by dissolving Arabic gum,                maltodextrin, sodium alginate in pure water,                stirring into uniform suspension, (3) homogenizing                and emulsifying by mixing the core material                obtained in the step (1) and the wall material                obtained in the step (2) according to the                proportion, placing in the ultra-high pressure                micro-jet homogenizer, homogenizing and emulsifying                synchronously to obtain the mixed solution, and (4)                dry spraying the dry solution obtained in the step                (3) to obtain lycopene microcapsule.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The core material                  preparation method comprises supercritical CO2                  extraction pressure 25-40 MPa, extraction                  temperature is 35-50&#8451;, carbon dioxide                  flow is 25-35 l/hour, the ethanol volume fraction                  of the carrier agent is 80-95% and extraction                  time is 1.5-3 hours. In the step (3), the ratio                  of the core wall material is 1:5-10, the                  homogenization time of the ultra-high pressure                  micro-jet homogenizer is 3-10 minutes to obtain                  gum Arabic/maltodextrin/sodium alginate-lycopene                  microcapsule solution. In the step (4), the inlet                  air temperature of spray drying is 180-200, the                  air outlet temperature is 80-100&#8451;, and                  the feeding temperature is It is                  40-60&#8451;.

PN CN114231053-A
TI Preparing needle-shaped cluster calcium            carbonate-silver composite material antibacterial,            involves adding calcium oxide and sucrose into water,            fully mixing uniformly, reacting mixed solution in            water bath, adjusting and controlling reaction            temperature, introducing carbon-dioxide in            solution.
AU GAO L
   CAO Y
   YUAN L
   YUAN Y
   ZHANG H
   LIU Y
   WANG H
   LI X
   SUN J
   TIAN X
AE ZHONGKE LEMEI TECHNOLOGY GROUP CO LTD (ZHON-Non-standard)
GA 2022489532
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a needle-shaped cluster calcium                carbonate-silver composite material antibacterial                involves adding the calcium oxide and sucrose into                the water, fully mixing uniformly, then fully                reacting the mixed solution in the water bath for                more than 1 hours; adjusting and controlling                reaction temperature, introducing carbon-dioxide                (CO2) for more than 2 hours in the solution to                obtain calcium carbonate slurry; adding the                pre-prepared silver ammonia solution into the                calcium carbonate slurry, stirring and reacting for                more than 5 hours, finally filtering, washing,                drying to obtain calcium carbonate-silver composite                material antibacterial.
   USE - Method for preparing a needle-shaped cluster                calcium carbonate-silver composite antibacterial                material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple to purify and dry, the                powder structure and size are uniform, the                needle-shaped cluster morphology and product color                are uniform, adjustable from white to light brown                to brown, used in fields such as paper making                fillers and plastic modification.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The concentration of                  calcium oxide is 0.1-5mol/L, the concentration of                  sucrose is 0.001-0.05mol/L, the temperature of                  water bath is 100 degrees Celsius. The adjusting                  in the reaction temperature is 100 degrees                  Celsius, the speed of introducing carbon dioxide                  (CO2) is 2-30mL/min. The silver ammonia solution                  is prepared by silver nitrate and ammonia water,                  the concentration of the silver ion is                  0.001-0.2mol/L. The volume ratio of the mixed                  solution of calcium oxide and sucrose to silver                  ammonia solution is 3:1.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of chemical or                biological preparations that do not support the                claims.
DC D22 (Sterilising, bandages, dressing and skin protection agents - including sterilising agents (other than for food), sutures, plaster casts, bioactive prostheses, contact lenses, diapers, animal litter, timber, preservatives, disinfectants, bactericidal detergents, deodorants, insect repellent compounds, moth proofers, sheep dip (A61L).); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); F09 (Paper-making production of cellulose, chemical treatment of wood - including chipboard and fibre-board (D21).); G01 (Inorganic pigments and non-fibrous fillers (C09C).)

PN WO2022005770-A1
TI Preparing 2-3C hydrocarbons, preferably olefins, by introducing feed stream comprising hydrogen gas and carbon-containing gas chosen from carbon monoxide, and/or carbon dioxide to reaction zone of reactor, and converting feed to product stream comprising hydrocarbons in presence of hybrid catalyst.
AU KIRILIN A
   MILLAR D M
   CHOJECKI A
   DEWILDE J F
   POLLEFEYT G
   NIESKENS D L S
   MALEK A
AE DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
GA 202202680D
AB    NOVELTY - Process for preparing 2-3C hydrocarbons, involves introducing a feed stream comprising hydrogen gas and a carbon-containing gas chosen from carbon monoxide, and/or carbon dioxide to a reaction zone of a reactor, and converting the feed stream to a product stream comprising 2-3C hydrocarbons in the reaction zone in the presence of a hybrid catalyst, where the hybrid catalyst comprises a mixed metal oxide catalyst component, and a microporous catalyst component comprising 8-MR pore openings having a size of less than or equal to 5.1 A and a cage defining ring size of less than or equal to 7.45 A, and 2C/3C carbon molar ratio of the product stream is greater than or equal to 0.7.
   USE - The process is used for preparing 2-3C hydrocarbons, preferably olefins (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process has high productivity of the 2-3C hydrocarbons.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The microporous catalyst component comprises silicaaluminate and/or silicoaluminophosphate (SAPO). The microporous catalyst component comprises an Erionite or Levyne structure. The microporous catalyst component comprises SAPO-17, SAPO-35, LZ-220, SSZ-17, SSZ-98, SSZ-105, and/or SAPO-67. The mixed metal oxide catalyst component comprises a metal oxide support material comprising zirconia, preferably gallium supported on zirconia. Preferred Composition: The microporous catalyst component comprises a silicon dioxide/aluminum oxide at a molar ratio of less than or equal to 50. The metal component comprises 0.1-10 wt.% metal oxide catalyst component. The hybrid catalyst comprises a metal oxide catalyst component to microporous catalyst component at a weight ratio of 0.1:1-10:1. The product stream comprises greater than 40 C mol.% 2-3C hydrocarbons based on the total hydrocarbon fraction in the product stream. The 2C/3C carbon molar ratio of the product stream is greater than or equal to 1. Preferred Conditions: The reaction zone operates at 370-470 degrees C and 20-70 bar. The gas hourly space velocity (GHSV) is greater than 500 hour-1.

PT P
PN DE102020109419-A1; FR3108916-A1; WO2021198526-A1
TI Production of biological methane comprises passing            liquid through tubular reactor which has tubular            section, where liquid is obtained by converting            hydrogen and carbon dioxide into aqueous methane by            means of microorganisms.
AU DAHMEN M
AE PLANET BIOGAS GROUP GMBH (PLAN-Non-standard)
   PLANET BIOGAS GROUP GMBH (PLAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021B4971S
AB    NOVELTY - Production of biological methane comprises                passing liquid through tubular reactor (1) which                has a horizontally aligned tubular section (2),                where the liquid is obtained by converting hydrogen                and carbon dioxide into aqueous methane by means of                microorganisms with the formation of water.
   USE - The method is useful for production of                biological methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple with less energy                consumption; has little technical effort with high                degree of efficiency; and ensures that the tubular                reactor provides intensive contact between the gas                introduced and the microorganisms. The plant has                tubular reactor with circular internal                cross-section, which prevents the formation of                flow-calmed dead zones that impair the efficiency                of the tubular reactor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                plant for production of biological methane,                comprising tubular reactor which has multiple tube                sections running one above the other.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation                of the meander-shaped or serpentine-shaped tubular                reactor.
   Tubular reactor (1)
   Tubular section (2)
   Gas collector (7)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The microorganisms in                  the liquid for biological methanation is Archaea,                  where a mixture of different Archaea strains are                  used in the liquid at least at the beginning of                  the process.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The tube section runs                  upwards with a small rise angle. The tube                  sections run in a straight line and the tube                  reactor has tube bends which connect adjacent                  tube sections to one another. The tube sections                  are curved and each form a passage of a helix in                  such a way that the tubular reactor is designed                  as a helix as a whole. The tubular reactor is                  arranged inside a biogas fermenter. The biogas                  fermenter is assigned with a CHP which is                  operated with the biogas produced, and an exhaust                  pipe of the CHP being in flow connection with                  carbon dioxide inlet line opening into the                  tubular reactor. The biogas processing plant is                  assigned to the biogas fermenter, which is                  designed for the production of methane on one                  hand and carbon dioxide-containing off-gas from                  the biogas on the other hand, where a gas line                  carrying the carbon dioxide originating from the                  off-gas is in flow connection with a carbon                  dioxide inlet line opening into the tubular                  reactor. The inner side of the tube section is                  provided with a solid material which can flow                  through the tube. The liquid flowing through the                  tube section is arranged inside the tube section                  to circulate guide elements.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The liquid is passed                  through multiple tube sections which are in flow                  connection with one another. The methane gas                  produced is collected and temporarily stored in a                  gas collector (7), and the gas collector is                  emptied and the methane gas is conveyed into a                  methane gas storage device which has a larger                  storage volume than the gas collector. The liquid                  is passed from top to bottom through the tube                  section. The method is carried out under normal                  pressure and normal temperature. The carbon                  dioxide contained in the exhaust gas of a                  combined heat and power (CHP) unit is introduced                  into the tubular reactor. The methane gas                  generated in the tubular reactor is used to                  operate the CHP. The biogas generated in a biogas                  plant is processed in a biogas processing plant                  and the methane obtained from it is fed into a                  gas network, and the carbon dioxide from the                  off-gas of the processing plant is introduced                  into the tubular reactor.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

PT P
PN CN113444551-A
TI Preparing combustible gas by using dry quenching            primary dust removing ash comprises e.g. extracting            part of coke oven flue gas from coke oven, sending to            dry quenching furnace primary dust removing system,            using carbon dioxide and oxygen, and reacting with high            temperature coke powder.
AU GAO L
   WU J
   DAI C
   WANG C
   LIU F
   XIA W
   MA Y
   ZHAO H
AE ANGANG STEEL CO LTD (ANSH-C)
GA 2021B5716R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing combustible gas by using dry                quenching primary dust removing ash comprises                extracting part of the coke oven flue gas from the                coke oven, and sending to the dry quenching furnace                primary dust removing system, using the carbon                dioxide and oxygen contained in the coke oven flue                gas, reacting with the high temperature coke powder                after primary dust removing of dry quenching coke,                generating carbon monoxide content is more than                50%, the temperature is 300-700degrees Celsius of                high temperature coal gas, introducing high                temperature gas into the coke oven heating gas                pipeline, and improving the temperature of the coke                oven heating gas.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing combustible                gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves the temperature of the                coke oven heating coal gas; and reduces the heat                consumption of coke oven coking.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the process, (1)                  connecting the primary dust removal system of the                  coke oven and the dry quenching furnace, where a                  reactor is installed under the ash hopper in the                  primary dust remover of the dry quenching                  furnace, and the top of the reactor is connected                  with the ash outlet of the ash hopper, and the                  two sides of the reactor are respectively set at                  flue gas inlet and the gas outlet, the bottom of                  the reactor is equipped with a supplementary                  heating device, (2) intercepting high temperature                  coke powder by gravity sedimentation in the                  primary dry quenching dust collector falls into                  the reactor during the operation of coke ovens                  and dry quenching furnaces, turning on the valve                  and starting the exhaust fan to extract the coke                  oven flue gas from the coke oven flue and sending                  to the reactor, where the volume content of                  carbon dioxide in the coke oven flue gas is                  20-23%, and the volume content is 3-5%, and (3)                  introducing high-temperature lean gas into the                  coke oven heating gas pipe through the gas                  pipeline, as the coke oven supplementary heating                  gas, using the check valve to prevent the coke                  oven heating gas from flowing back into the                  reactor. The ash hopper is provided with 3 level                  gauges along the height direction, namely a high                  level gauge, a middle level gauge and a low level                  gauge, by adjusting the opening of the valve, the                  amount of coke oven flue gas drawn into the                  reactor is controlled, so that the material level                  of the high-temperature coke powder is controlled                  between the high material level and the medium                  material level.Preferred Components: The blast furnace                  heating gas pipeline and the lean gas pipeline                  are respectively provided with pressure gauges                  near the connection of the two. The valve is an                  electric valve, and the exhaust fan, the valve,                  the level gauge and the supplementary heating                  device are respectively connected to the control                  system.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. TI Fire fighting safety warning device, has fire alarm circuit provided with power supply, and fire power supply whose anode is connected with thermistor, where thermistor is connected with fire extinguishing device for releasing carbon dioxide.
AU GUI J
AE JIANGSU GUITAI INTERNET THINGS TECHNOLOG (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021B0599W
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model relates to the technical field of the safety, especially relates to safety warning device for fire fighting, comprising an alarm, a shell, a glass cover, a thermistor and a fire extinguishing device, the front end of the shell is completely open, the glass cover is used for completely sealing the front end of the shell, the alarm; the thermistor and the fire extinguishing device are fixedly installed in the shell; the fire extinguishing device and the thermistor are connected in series through a fire alarm circuit; the fire alarm circuit comprises a power supply; and the anode of the fire power supply is connected with the thermistor; the thermistor NTC is reduced along with the increase of the temperature; the fire extinguishing device is used for releasing carbon dioxide. The purpose of the utility model is to prolong the fire condition, the service life of the alarm device in the fire field.

PN CN113299924-A
TI Preparation of carbon nanotube/molybdenum oxide            integrated array electrode material used as            lithium-carbon dioxide battery cathode material by            preparing foamed nickel-carbon nanotube composite            material, vacuumizing, heating and adding ozone.
AU CHEN M
   LIU Y
   CHEN Q
AE UNIV HARBIN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHS-C)
GA 2021A0389B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of carbon nanotube/molybdenum                oxide integrated array electrode material comprises                cleaning foamed nickel, drying, adding to nickel                nitrate solution, soaking, taking out, vacuum                drying in oven, adding to tube furnace, introducing                mixed gas of argon and hydrogen, heating to 600-650                degrees C, passing through anhydrous ethanol                saturated steam, and insulating at 600-700 degrees                C to obtain foamed nickel-carbon nanotube composite                material; and adding to reaction chamber,                vacuumizing, heating to remove molybdenum                hexacarbonyl, introducing ozone and water vapor to                carry out cyclic deposition and grow molybdenum                oxide layer by layer on surface of carbon                nanotube.
   USE - The method is for preparation of carbon                nanotube/molybdenum oxide integrated array                electrode material is used as lithium-carbon                dioxide battery cathode material (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The electrode material exhibits very high                discharge capacity and excellent cycle stability.                The first discharge capacity reaches 30.25 mAh/cm2                at the same time, it can keep 300 cycles. The                multiplying power performance can reach to 0.25                mA/cm2.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The cleaning of foamed                  nickel is done by washing foamed nickel for 3-5                  times 3 mol/L dilute hydrochloric acid, washing                  for 3-5 times with deionized water, washing for                  3-5 times with acetone to remove oxide layer and                  organic contaminants on surface, and vacuum                  drying in oven at 50-60 degrees C for 12-14                  hours. The soaking is done at 15-25 degrees C for                  4-5 hours. The cyclic deposition is done for                  100-150 cycles at 220-240 degrees C. The program                  set for each cycle is passing through molybdenum                  hexacarbonyl for 0.35 seconds, nitrogen purging                  for 60 seconds, adding ozone for 5 seconds,                  nitrogen purging for 60 seconds, passing through                  water vapor for 0.03 seconds and nitrogen purging                  for 60 seconds. Preferred Components: The flow                  rate of argon in mixed gas is 140-160 standard                  cm3 (sccm) and flow rate of hydrogen in mixed gas                  is 10-30 sccm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The flow rate of                  anhydrous ethanol saturated steam is 50-70                  sccm.

TI Extracting of Magnolia bark extract by crushing Magnolia officinalis, carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, rectifying, separating and collecting.
AU ZHOU J

   HU Y
   XU K
   TAN Y
   XU Y
   LUO S
   HUANG S
AE KAIPING HEALTHWISE HEALTH CO LTD (KAIP-Non-standard)
GA 202191988D
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting of Magnolia bark extract with high purity comprises crushing Magnolia officinalis plant material; putting into extracting kettle of supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) extraction device, introducing supercritical CO2, controlling pressure of extracting kettle to 20-35 MPa, extracting at 30-50 degrees C and obtaining extracting material; introducing extracting material into rectifying tower to obtain rectifying material where pressure of rectifying tower is 15-18 MPa; dividing temperature of rectifying tower into three sections from bottom to top where lower area is 35 plus minus 5 degrees C, middle temperature area is 40 plus minus 5 degrees C and upper temperature area is 45 plus minus 5 degrees C; and introducing rectifying material into first stage separating kettle at 9-14 MPa and 35-55 degrees C, and collecting.
   USE - The method is used for extracting of Magnolia bark extract with high purity.
   ADVANTAGE - The process parameters of separation kettle are controlled to make high content of total phenol extract of Magnolia bark which can be directly collected in first-stage separation kettle, with high total phenol extraction rate.

PN CN113058649-A
TI Graphene phthalocyanine composite material useful            in electrochemical catalytic reduction of carbon            dioxide and photocatalytic degradation of hexavalent            chromium ions.
AU XU J
   XU Z
   QI F
   LI M
AE UNIV SOUTHERN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (USOU-C)
GA 202178487Q
AB    NOVELTY - Graphene phthalocyanine composite material is                claimed. The raw material for preparation of                graphene phthalocyanine composite material is                composed of reduced graphene and phthalocyanine                compounds in a mass ratio of 1-5:2-10. The                phthalocyanine compound is (I) or (II).
   USE - The graphene phthalocyanine composite material                is useful in electrochemical catalytic reduction of                carbon dioxide and photocatalytic degradation of                hexavalent chromium ions (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The graphene phthalocyanine composite                material: has strong bonding force of reduced                graphene and phthalocyanine compound; is easy to                prepare; utilizes low cost raw material; and                utilizes graphene and phthalocyanine can be closely                combined without additional chemical                modification.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Graphene phthalocyanine composite material is                claimed. The raw material for preparation of                graphene phthalocyanine composite material is                composed of reduced graphene and phthalocyanine                compounds in a mass ratio of 1-5:2-10. The                phthalocyanine compound is of formulae (I) or                (II).
   M1, M2 = Co2+, Zn2+, Ni2+, Fe2+, Mn2+ or Cu2+;                and
   R1, R2 = -NH2 or -NO2.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing graphene phthalocyanine composite                material, comprising mixing organic solvent with                phthalocyanine compound and reduced graphene to                obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio between                  reduced graphene and phthalocyanine compounds is                  1-2:2-5. The organic solvent is                  N,N-dimethylformamide, dimethyl sulfoxide,                  dichloromethane, chlorobenzene,                  o-dichlorobenzene, tetrahydrofuran or chloroform;                  and the concentration of the phthalocyanine                  compound in the phthalocyanine solution is 0.05-5                  mg/ml. Preferred Method: The method comprises                  dissolving phthalocyanine compound in an organic                  solvent to prepare a phthalocyanine solution, and                  mixing reduced graphene with the phthalocyanine                  solution to obtain graphene phthalocyanine                  composite material. The method further comprises                  allowing to stand for 40-80 hours, filtering and                  drying after the step of mixing the reduced                  graphene and the phthalocyanine solution; a                  dichloromethane is used to wash the filter cake                  during filtration; and the drying is natural air                  drying or vacuum drying. (I) is prepared by                  mixing 4-nitrophthalonitrile, soluble divalent                  metal salt 1 and quinoline, and carrying out a                  phthalocyanine ring-forming reaction under the                  protection of an inert gas to prepare                  tetranitro-substituted phthalocyanine compound                  (Ia) where R1 is nitro. (II) is prepared by                  mixing 4,5-dinitrophthalonitrile, soluble                  divalent metal salt 2 and quinoline, and carrying                  out the phthalocyanine ring-forming reaction                  under the protection of inert gas to prepare                  octanitro-substituted phthalocyanine compound                  (IIa), where R2 is nitro. The soluble divalent                  metal salt 1 and the soluble divalent metal salt                  2 are cobalt(II), zinc(II), nickel(II), iron(II),                  manganese(II) or copper(II) salts. (I) is                  prepared by mixing tetranitro-substituted                  phthalocyanine compound (Ia) and reducing agent,                  heating to reflux to prepare                  tetraamino-substituted phthalocyanine compound                  (Ib) where R1 is amino group. (II) is prepared by                  mixing octanitro-substituted phthalocyanine                  compound (IIa) and reducing agent, heating to                  reflux to prepare octanitro-substituted                  phthalocyanine compound (IIb) where R2 is amino                  group.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E31 (Compounds of V, Nb, Ta, Cr, Mo, W, Mn, Tc, Re, Fe, Ru, Os, Co, Rh, Ir, Ni, Pd, Pt, Pa, U and subsequent actinides.); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN CN112425786-A
TI Preparing high-peptide dietary fiber granules by enzymatically hydrolyzing corn sugar residue using alkaline protease, cutting off protein peptides with specific molecular weight, enzymatically hydrolyzing using cellulase, and granulating.
AU ZHAO Y
   WU J
   WANG X
   LI F
   KONG L
AE LUZHOU BIOLOGY TECHNOLOGY SHANDONG CO LT (LUZH-Non-standard)
   SHANDONG LUZHOU FOOD GROUP CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021230783
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing high-peptide dietary fiber granules by enzymatically hydrolyzing corn sugar residue, involves (a) drying and pulverizing the starch sugar residue, (b) degreasing the pulverized starch sugar residue, (c) adding alkaline protease to the oil-extracted materials for enzymatic hydrolysis to obtain protein peptides, and passing the material after proteolysis through a membrane separation device to cut off protein peptides with a molecular weight of 3000-5000, (d) configuring the materials obtained in step (c) according to a material-to-liquid ratio of 1:(10-15), and adding cellulase and xylanase for enzymatic hydrolysis, (e) purifying the hydrolyzate after enzymatic hydrolysis by supercritical carbon dioxide technology, and (f) drying the materials at low temperature, superfinely pulverizing, granulating, and packaging.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing high-peptide dietary fiber granules.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces granules with high peptides on the basis of the existing simple dietary fiber, efficiently utilizes corn by-products, and ensures strong physiological activity of the product by adopting supercritical carbon dioxide technology.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The step of degreasing starch sugar residue involves using vegetable oil extraction solvent No: 6 and food-grade industrial hexane as processing aids, with a material-to-liquid ratio of 1:(5-7), and extracting for 5-8 hours. The step (d) involves: either firstly adding 4 IU/g cellulase, hydrolyzing for 2 hours under the pH conditions of 4.5-5, then adding 40 IU/g xylanase, and hydrolyzing for 4 hours under the pH conditions of 6-6.8; or firstly adding 40 IU/g xylanase, hydrolyzing for 4 hours under the pH conditions of 6-6.8, then adding 4 IU/g cellulase, and hydrolyzing for 2 hours under the pH conditions of 4.5-5; or simultaneously adding 4 IU/g cellulase and 40 IU/g xylanase, and treating for 4 hours at pH of 4.6-4.8, 5.3-5.7 and 6.2-6.6, respectively. Preferred Conditions: In step (a), the drying temperature of starch sugar residue does not exceed 80 degrees C, and the moisture content of the material after drying is less than 10%. In step (c), the materials and moisture are configured according to 1:(8-12), the pH is adjusted to 9-10, and the enzymatic hydrolysis is carried out at 50-60 degrees C for 1.5-2.5 hours. In step (e), during the purification process, the extraction temperature is 40-45 degrees C, the pressure is 20-25 MPa, the time is 70-90 minutes, and the carbon dioxide flow rate is 20-25 l/hour.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PN CN112354380-A; CN112354380-B
TI Preparation of electrospun nanofiber gas separation membrane involves preparing membrane solution using e.g. poly(ethylene glycol) diacrylate, placing nanofiber mat formed by electrospinning e.g. polyacrylonitrile nanofiber and polymerizing.
AU ZHENG W
   SUN T
   HE G
   YAN X
   DAI Y
   RUAN X
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 202118287T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of electrospun nanofiber gas separation membrane involves electrospinning polyacrylonitrile nanofibers, polyvinylpyrrolidone nanofibers, polyvinyl alcohol nanofibers and polyvinylidene fluoride nanofibers with different molecular weights to prepared nanofiber mats, adding poly(ethylene glycol) diacrylate and poly(ethylene glycol) methyl ether acrylate whose mass ratio is 3:7 or more, adding photoinitiator, placing in light-proof container and mixing, obtaining membrane solution, ultrasonically degassing and cooling to room temperature, placing the nanofiber mat in membrane solution, adjusting the air pressure to 0.05-0.1 MPa, passing nitrogen to make the polymer membrane liquid pass through the nanofiber mat, obtaining a wet nanofiber mat, placing the wet nanofiber mat on the filter paper to absorb the remaining liquid, passing nitrogen to remove oxygen in device, using 308-312 nm UV lamp to irradiate in nitrogen protection for 2 minutes, and polymerizing.
   USE - Preparation of electrospun nanofiber gas separation membrane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of the gas separation membrane with high carbon dioxide permeability, high carbon dioxide/nitrogen selectivity and high mechanical strength.

PN CN111778547-A
TI Preparation of vinite type calcium carbonate            whisker used e.g. for rubber, by adjusting pH value of            calcium chloride-rich solution, mixing with ammonia            water, stirring, introducing carbon dioxide gas,            reacting, filtering washing and drying.
AU DING W
   CHEN Q
   SUN H
   PENG T
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (USWS-C)
GA 2020A3227Q
AB    NOVELTY - A vinite type calcium carbonate whisker is                prepared by taking 200 mL calcium chloride-rich                solution, adjusting pH value by ammonia water or                hydrochloric acid to 7 to obtain neutral calcium                chloride solution, mixing with ammonia water,                putting in reaction container with stirring,                introducing carbon dioxide gas, stirring at normal                pressure and low temperature for reacting for                30-180 minutes to obtain reaction product,                filtering reaction product, washing and                drying.
   USE - The method is used for preparing vinite type                calcium carbonate whisker for rubber, plastic and                chemical industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is not added with any crystal form                control agent, has wide raw material source, and                finishes whole preparation process at low                temperature and normal pressure. The product is                non-toxic and safe, has good reinforcing                performance, content of greater than or equal to                99%, diameter of 0.5-1 mu m, length of 6-30 mu m                and whiteness of greater than or equal to 95.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The calcium                  chloride-rich solution is calcium chloride-rich                  waste liquid obtained from alkali industry,                  hydrochloric acid leaching liquid of calcium                  ion-rich minerals, or hydrochloric acid leaching                  liquid of calcium ion-rich solid waste. The                  concentration of calcium ion in calcium chloride                  solution is 3-20 g/L. The adding amount of                  ammonia water is 5-20 mL/100 mL. Preferred                  Method: The flow rate of carbon dioxide gas is                  30-200 mL/minute. Stirring is done at speed of                  100-600 revolutions/minute (rpm). Reaction is                  done at 80-180 degrees C.
DC A85 (Electrical applications.)
MC A08-R; A08-R09

PT P
PN FR3093726-A1; WO2020188211-A1; FR3093726-B1; CN113597444-A; KR2021138622-A; EP3938433-A1; VN82904-A; US2022145034-A1; JP2022526273-W
TI Manufacture of polyamide block and polyether block            copolymer foam used for sports equipment, involves            mixing copolymer with additives and blowing agent            comprising mixture of dinitrogen and carbon dioxide,            and foaming obtained mixture.
AU COCQUET C
   CLIO N
AE ARKEMA FRANCE (AQOR-C)
   ARKEMA FRANCE (AQOR-C)
   ARKEMA FRANCE (AQOR-C)
GA 202091161K
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of polyamide block and polyether                block copolymer foam involves mixing a copolymer in                a molten state with one or more additives and a                blowing agent comprising a mixture of dinitrogen                and carbon dioxide, and foaming the obtained                mixture of copolymer and 0.1-10 %mass, preferably                0.2-1.5 %mass blowing agent.
   USE - Manufacture of polyamide block and polyether                block copolymer foam (claimed) used for sports                equipment. Uses include but are not limited to                soles or sole components, gloves, racquets, golf                balls, and helmets.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables manufacture of polyamide                block and polyether block copolymer foam with                improved mechanical properties, and anti-shock,                anti-vibration and anti-noise properties, and low                density.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for polyamide                block and polyether block copolymer foam, which has                a density of 0.8 g/cm3 or less, preferably 0.08-0.3                g/cm3, and an expansion ratio of 2-25, preferably                4-15.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The blowing agent                  comprises 20-95 wt.%, preferably 40-95 wt.%                  dinitrogen, and 5-80 wt.%, preferably 5-60 wt.%                  carbon dioxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Properties: The polyamide blocks                  of the copolymer have a number average molar mass                  of 600-5000 g/mol, preferably 600-4000 g/mol. The                  polyether blocks of the copolymer have a number                  average molar mass of 250-2000 g/mol, preferably                  650-2000 g/mol. The foam is non-crosslinked.                  Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of                  polyamide blocks with respect to polyether blocks                  of the copolymer is 0.3-10, preferably 0.3-3. The                  polyamide blocks of the copolymer are blocks of                  polyamide at least one chosen from polyamide 6,                  polyamide 11, polyamide 12, polyamide 5.4,                  polyamide 5.9, polyamide 5.10, polyamide 5.12,                  polyamide 5.13, polyamide 5.14, polyamide 5.16,                  polyamide 5.18, polyamide 5.36, polyamide 6.4,                  polyamide 6.9, polyamide 6.10, polyamide 6.12,                  polyamide 6.13, polyamide 6.14, polyamide 6.16,                  polyamide 6.18, polyamide 6.36, polyamide 10.4,                  polyamide 10.9, polyamide 10.10, polyamide 10.12,                  polyamide 10.13, polyamide 10.14, polyamide                  10.16, polyamide 10.18, polyamide 10.36,                  polyamide 10.T, polyamide 12.4, polyamide 12.9,                  polyamide 12.10, polyamide 12.12, polyamide                  12.13, polyamide 12.14, polyamide 12.16,                  polyamide 12.18, polyamide 12.36 and polyamide                  12.T, preferably polyamide 11, polyamide 12,                  polyamide 6 and polyamide 6.10. The polyether                  blocks are blocks of polyethylene glycol,                  propylene glycol, polytrimethylene glycol and                  polytetrahydrofuran, preferably blocks of                  polyethylene glycol or polytetrahydrofuran. The                  additives are chosen from copolymers of ethylene                  and vinyl acetate, copolymers ethylene and                  acrylate, and copolymers of ethylene and alkyl                  (meth)acrylate. Preferred Process: The method                  involves injecting the mixture of copolymer and                  blowing agent into a mold, and foaming the                  obtained mixture by opening the mold.

PT P
PN CN211060437-U
TI Super-cooling carbon dioxide refrigeration system, has temperature difference generator jointed with carbon dioxide gas cooler, and generator electrically connected with thermoelectric supercooler.
AU LIANG Y
AE CHINA SINOGY REFINERY CHEM ENG CO LTD (CHSI-Non-standard)
GA 202070750Y
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a super-cooling CO2 refrigeration system based on temperature difference power generation afterheat residual pressure drive, comprising a CO2 refrigeration cycle, a first temperature difference generator, a second temperature difference generator and a generator; the CO2 evaporator outlet is connected with the CO2 compressor inlet; the CO2 compressor outlet is connected with the CO2 gas cooler inlet; the CO2 gas cooler outlet is connected with the thermoelectric supercooler CO2 side inlet; the thermoelectric supercooler CO2 side outlet is connected with the CO2 expander inlet; the CO2 expander outlet is connected with the inlet of the CO2 evaporator; the first temperature difference generator is jointed with the CO2 compressor; the second temperature difference generator is jointed with the CO2 gas cooler. the generator is electrically connected with the thermoelectric subcooler. The utility model can deeply use the gas air cooler of the CO2 refrigeration system and the afterheat of the shell of the exhaust port of the compressor to discharge to the environment.

GA 2020497837
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of a high-temperature-resistant silicone rubber sealing ring involves adding 100 parts mass silicone rubber and 15-20 parts mass aluminum silicate fiber into a mixer, mixing at 70-80 degrees C for 40 minutes, adding 5-8 parts mass mica, 3-5 parts mass calcium carbonate powder and mixing for 30 minutes at 50-80 degrees C, adding the rubber mixture into a sealing ring mold, vulcanizing for 20-30 minutes at 160 degrees C at 15-20 MPa, and cooling to room temperature at normal pressure for 15 hours.
   USE - Preparation of high-temperature-resistant silicone rubber sealing ring.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces silicone rubber sealing ring with excellent flame retardant performance, anti-dripping performance and ceramizability of silicone rubber. The carbon dioxide released by the decomposition of calcium carbonate removes a portion of the heat energy, and the release of carbon dioxide occurs synchronously with ceramicization, preventing the polymer from being cracked.

PN US2020123580-A1; US11111510-B2
TI Producing lipid product from gaseous substrate, comprises e.g. gaseous substrate comprising carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen in first bioreactor containing culture of microorganism in liquid nutrient medium.
AU SIMPSON S D
   BERNASEK S M
   TULI D K
AE LANZATECH NEW ZEALAND LTD (LANZ-C)
   DBT-IOC CENT ADVANCED BIO-ENERGY RES (DBTI-Non-standard)
GA 202031801S
AB    NOVELTY - Producing at least one lipid product from a gaseous substrate, comprises: (i) receiving a gaseous substrate comprising carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen in a first bioreactor containing a culture of at least one microorganism in a liquid nutrient medium, and fermenting the gaseous substrate to produce acetate; (ii) passing at least a portion of the acetate to a second bioreactor containing a culture of at least one microalgae in a liquid nutrient medium, and fermenting the acetate to produce at least one lipid product, where the microalgae is Scenedesmus, Thraustochytrium, Japonochytrium, Aplanochytrium, Elina, or Labyrinthula; (iii) obtaining a fermentation broth from the second bioreactor; and (iv) passing at least a portion of the fermentation broth from the second bioreactor through an oxygen removal unit to remove oxygen and then recycling at least a portion of the resulting stream to the first bioreactor.
   USE - The method is useful for producing at least one lipid product from a gaseous substrate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The production rate of acetate in the first bioreactor is at least 10 g/l/day. The microorganism in the first bioreactor is Acetobacterium, Moorella, Clostridium, Pyrococcus, Eubacterium, Desulfobacterium, Carboxydothermus, Acetogenium, Acetoanaerobium, Butyribacterium, Peptostreptococcus, Ruminococcus, Oxobacter, and Methanosarcina. The microalgae in the second bioreactor are Thraustochytrium. The lipid product produced in stage (ii) is used to produce at least one tertiary product including hydrogenation-derived renewable diesel, fatty acid methyl esters, fatty acid ethyl esters, or biodiesel. The lipid production is increased in the second bioreactor by limiting at least one nutrient in the liquid nutrient medium. The limited nutrient is nitrogen. The lipid product is a polyunsaturated fatty acid. The polyunsaturated fatty acid is omega -3 fatty acid. The omega -3 fatty acid is at least one of alpha -linolenic acid, eicosapentaenoic acid, or docosahexaenoic acid. The first bioreactor is anaerobic. The second bioreactor is aerobic. The microorganism in the first bioreactor is an acetogenic bacterium. The microalgae is Japonochytrium, Aplanochytrium, Elina, or Labyrinthula. The liquid nutrient medium of the second bioreactor comprises: nitrogen; an acetic acid to nitrogen ratio of at least 10:1; or a carbon to nitrogen ratio of greater than or equal to 49:1.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN IN202021001440-A; IN366272-B
TI Creation and tuning of defects in nanosilica to get defected nanosilica via magnetiothermia involves treating nanosilica in sealed tube in oxygen free environment, cooling tube to room temperature and breaking and removing magnesium oxide.
AU POLSHETTIWAR V
   MISHRA A K
AE TATA FUNDAMENTAL RES INST (TTTA-C)
GA 202018554T
AB    NOVELTY - Creation and tuning of defects in nanosilica to produce defected nanosilica using magnetiothermia, comprises: treating 250 mg nanosilica with 10-50 wt.% at 675 degrees C for 12 hours in a sealed tube in an oxygen free environment; cooling the tube to room temperature and breaking to recover defected nanosilica and magnesium oxide (MgO); and removing MgO by repeated washing with dilute hydrochloric acid in water-ethanol mixture.
   USE - The process is useful for creation and tuning of defects in nanosilica to produce defected nanosilica using magnetiothermia and to convert carbon dioxide into methane (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process: generates defects (types, concentration and proximity) in nanosilica, hence the defected nanosilica is capable of catalytically converting carbon dioxide to methane with excellent productivity, selectivity and stability at industrially rates and scales, in economical manner; and avoids the use of expensive noble metal which are not stable and get deactivated in sort reaction time.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dendritic fibrous nanosilica is used. The concentration of magnesium is 25 wt.%.
   EXAMPLE - In a typical synthesis, 250 mg dendritic fibrous nanosilica and 10 wt.% magnesium powder were mixed well with a mortar and pestle. The whitish grey powder was then transferred to a tubular quartz tube, which was degassed overnight to remove atmospheric oxygen. The tube was then sealed under vacuum and heated in a furnace to 675 degrees C at a rate of 5 degrees C/minute and then held at this temperature for 12 hours. The grey powder (defected nanosilica and magnesium oxide) was collected by breaking the tube. The samples were then stirred with an acidic solution (6.8 ml hydrochloric acid, 12 M, 17 ml water, and 52 ml ethanol) for 24 hours at room temperature to dissolve magnesium oxide. The solid product was isolated by centrifugation. This step was repeated three times, and after that samples were washed by 40 ml each of water and ethanol twice. Washing was continued until all magnesium containing species such as magnesium orthosilicate, magnesium oxide and magnesium were completely absent from the defected silica. The samples were dried in an oven overnight and the defected nanosilica powder was stored under atmospheric conditions.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

AU XIAO S

   YE G
   WANG L
   LIU X
AE CHINA ATOMIC ENERGY INST (CNNU-C)
GA 202000141D
AB    NOVELTY - Palladium-containing catalyst comprises a catalytically active substance comprising metal palladium or its compound. The palladium element in the metal palladium or its compound comprises a non-radioactive isotope whose composition and/or abundance is changed from natural, where the abundance of at least one of the non-radioactive isotopes changes based on the natural abundance by more than 1/20 and not less than 20%.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for catalyzing the reaction of carbon dioxide hydrogenation to formic acid, and catalyzing the hydrogenation of o-nitrochlorobenzene to 2,2-dichlorohydroazobenzene (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has excellent catalytic performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the catalyst, involving (i) using an isotope separation method, an isotope mixing method, a nuclear reaction method, or an elemental artificial production method to prepare the catalytically active substance or its compound whose isotopic composition and/or abundance is changed, and (ii) using each catalytically active substance or its compound to obtain the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The catalyst further comprises a catalytic aid. Preferred Components: The catalytically active substance further comprises metallic gold or its compound, and metallic silver or its compound. The gold element in the metal gold or its compound is natural isotope abundance. The silver element in the metal silver or its compound comprises non-radioactive isotopes, and the composition and/or abundance of various isotopes is the same as or different from that of nature. The catalytic aid comprises co-catalyst and catalyst carrier. The co-catalyst is chosen from ruthenium, cobalt, vanadium, iridium and/or rare earth elements. The catalyst carrier is chosen from activated carbon, silicon carbide, alumina, graphene, silica and/or zeolite. Preferred Conditions: The mass ratio of the catalytically active substance to the catalytic aid is 1:(0.1-10). The mass ratio of metal palladium or its compound to metal gold or its compound is 1:(0.1-10). The mass ratio of metal palladium or its compound to metal silver or its compound is 1:(0.1-10).

PN WO2019236983-A1
TI Performing conversion of methane to ethylene involves converting by pyrolysis or partial oxidation methane-containing feed to acetylene-containing product stream, and acetylene-containing product stream is combined with hydrogenating feed.
AU MAMEDOV A
   NAIR B
   LIU Z
   PENG K Y B
   HUCKMAN M E
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   MAMEDOV A (MAME-Individual)
GA 2019A47639
AB    NOVELTY - Performing conversion of methane to ethylene involves converting by pyrolysis or partial oxidation methane-containing feed to an acetylene-containing product stream. The acetylene-containing product stream is combined with hydrogenating feed comprising ethane, hydrogen gas, and water and thermally hydrogenating acetylene of acetylene-containing product stream in absence of catalyst to form hydrogenated product stream. The hydrogenated product stream is separated into first separated stream containing residual acetylene, ethylene, ethane, and hydrogen gas and a second separated stream containing methane, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide. The A is contacted the residual acetylene of the first separated stream with a hydrogenation catalyst in catalytic hydrogenation reaction to form ethylene. The B is combining second separated stream with steam to convert by both steam reforming and carbon dioxide reforming of methane to form syngas mixture comprising carbon monoxide and hydrogen gas.
   USE - Method for performing conversion of methane to ethylene.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform conversion of methane to ethylene, with increase in ethylene production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred process: The method for performing conversion of methane to ethylene further involves separating the hydrogenated product stream into a third separated stream containing ethane, hydrogen gas, and water, a portion of the third separated stream being recycled to form at least a portion of the hydrogenating feed. Preferred Conditions: The hydrogenating feed provides a th/acetylene molar ratio of from 9 or more. The B is performed and where the syngas mixture contains less than 3 wt.% methane. The hydrogenated product stream contains from 3 wt.% or less of residual acetylene. The pyrolysis or partial oxidation of the methane-containing feed is carried out at temperature of from 1000-3000 degrees C. The acetylene-containing product stream is quenched to a temperature of from 500-950 degrees C prior to thermal hydrogenation. The hydrogenated product stream is cryogenically cooled to facilitate separating the hydrogenated product stream. The heat for the steam reforming and carbon dioxide reforming of methane is provided by at least one of the steam and the combustion of at least a portion of the carbon dioxide (CO2) and the carbon monoxide (CO) of the second separated stream. The heat for the steam reforming and carbon dioxide reforming of methane is provided by at least one of the steam and the combustion of a portion of the carbon dioxide (CO2) and the carbon monoxide (CO) of the second separated stream.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
