PN CN110540221-A
TI Preparing magnesium-aluminum hydrotalcite from            aluminum-air battery waste electrolyte slurry comprises            adding waste electrolyte slurry into container, placing            in constant temperature water tank, stirring and            reacting in reaction kettle.
AU WU X
   PAN T
   SHEN X
   CAO F
   WANG P
   LI L
AE UNIV ANHUI TECHNOLOGY (UYAT-C)
GA 2019A4386T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing magnesium-aluminum hydrotalcite from                aluminum-air battery waste electrolyte slurry                comprises (i) adding waste electrolyte slurry into                container based on the analysis of the solid                content of the waste electrolyte slurry and                aluminum and magnesium element contents and                replenishing magnesium source to make molar ratio                of the magnesium source and aluminum source to n                (magnesium):n (aluminum) of 3:1, completely                stirring, placing in constant temperature water                tank, passing carbon dioxide gas into the slurry                through bubbler, continuously stirring to react                magnesium source, aluminum source and carbon                dioxide in the waste slurry to obtain reaction                product; and (ii) moving the reaction product to                the reaction kettle to collect carbonated-type                magnesium-aluminum hydrotalcite.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                magnesium-aluminum hydrotalcite from aluminum-air                battery waste electrolyte slurry.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: achieves reuse of waste resources;                is simple, environmentally friendly, energy saving;                and reduces production cost of magnesium aluminum                hydrotalcite.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The waste electrolyte                  slurry is used as the main aluminum source,                  provided with alkaline conditions are and carbon                  dioxide is used as the main carbon source.                  Preferred Method: In the step (i), the heating                  temperature of the water bath in the constant                  temperature water tank is 40-80 degrees C and the                  stirring reaction time is 20-40 minutes. In the                  step (ii), the reaction temperature of the                  reactor is 120-180 degrees C and the reaction                  time is 6-15 hours; the collecting method is                  cooling, filtering at 80 degrees C, washing until                  pH value is 9-10 and drying for 5 hours.

PN CN109620862-A
TI Preparation of traditional Chinese medicine oral dripping pill used for relieving sore throat includes taking peppermint, Forsythia, Ligusticum striatum, balloon flower, licorice, Cicada ecdysis, boat-fruited sterculia and menthol.
AU XIA Y
   YU J
AE WUXI JIMIN KEXIN SHANHE PHARM CO LTD (WUXI-Non-standard)
GA 201938757W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of traditional Chinese medicine oral dripping pill comprises mixing peppermint, Forsythia and Ligusticum striatum, crushing into 40-80 mesh particles, extracting with supercritical carbon dioxide for 1-6 hours at 25-45 MPa, 45-65 degrees C and carbon dioxide flow rate of 25-45 L/hour and collecting volatile oil; extracting twice the balloon flower, licorice, Cicada ecdysis and boat-fruited sterculia with 2-6 times amount water for 1.5 hours for each time, mixing extract, filtering and concentrating into clear paste with relative density of 1.2-1.4; and dissolving menthol in 2-4 times amount ethanol, mixing with volatile oil, homogenizing and adding to the clear paste, adding polyethylene glycol 6000, mixing, transferring to dripping pill machine, keeping at 60-80 degrees C for 15 minutes, dropping to condensing agent, collecting formed dripping pills and degreasing.
   USE - The method is preparation of traditional Chinese medicine oral dripping pill used for opening and relieving sore throat (claimed).
TF ACTIVITY - Antiinflammatory.

TI New ion exchange resin useful for catalyzing the            addition reaction of carbon dioxide and alkylene            oxide.
AU YU F
   HE W
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC SHANGHAI RES INST PETROCHEMICAL (SNPC-C)
GA 201933614F
AB    NOVELTY - Ion exchange resin (I) are new. The ion                exchange resin is composed of gel type nano                composite resin matrix, where the gel-type                nanocomposite resin matrix is a nanogel type                copolymer obtained by in-situ copolymerization of                styrene monomer, comonomer and nano material, and                nano material is multi-walled carbon nanotubes,                single-walled carbon nanotubes, or 60C or 70C                fullerene.
   USE - (I) are useful for catalyzing the addition                reaction of carbon dioxide and alkylene oxide                (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Ion exchange resin of formula (I) are new. The                ion exchange resin is composed of gel type nano                composite resin matrix, where the gel-type                nanocomposite resin matrix is a nanogel type                copolymer obtained by in-situ copolymerization of                styrene monomer, comonomer and nano material, and                nano material is multi-walled carbon nanotubes,                single-walled carbon nanotubes, or 60C or 70C                fullerene.
   P = gel type nano composite resin                matrix;
   M- = anion;
   POSS = polyhedral oligomeric silsesquioxane                unit of formula (-SiO1.5)m;
   m = 6, 8, 10 or 12;
   core 5-membered ring = imidazolium cation                unit; and
   R = linking group between POSS and imidazolium                cation unit, alkylene or arylene group.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The method comprises                  carrying out addition reaction at 60-180 degrees                  C under the pressure of 0.1-10.0 MPa, where the                  reaction time is 1-8 hours, and the weight ratio                  between ion exchange resin and alkylene oxide is                  0.001-1:1. Preferred Components: The ion exchange                  resin comprises 5-15 wt.% polyhedral oligomeric                  silsesquioxane unit. The alkylene group is                  methylene, ethylene and/or propylene; and arylene                  group is phenylene group, naphthylene group or                  benzylene group. The styrenic monomer is styrene,                  alpha -methyl styrene or 4-butyl styrene                  (preferably styrene); and the comonomer is                  ethylene glycol methacrylate, dipropylene                  benzene, divinylphenylmethane or divinylbenzene                  (preferably divinylbenzene). The amount of                  styrene monomer is 85-95 pts. wt., the amount of                  comonomer is 2-5 pts. wt., and the amount of nano                  material is 0.1-3 pts. wt. The alkylene oxide is                  of formula (II).R1, R2, R3 and R4 = H or 1-6C alkyl                  group.
   Preferred Definitions:
   M- = fluoride ion, chloride ion, bromide ion,                iodide ion, acetate, formate, hydrogen oxalate,                bicarbonate, hydroxide, bisulfite, carboxylate,                citrate, sulfite, phosphite, tetrafluoroborate,                triflate, phosphotungstic acid, phosphomolybdate,                silicon tungstate, silicomolybdate, arsenic                tungstate, arsenic molybdate, germanium tungstate,                germanium molybdate, phospho-tungstic vanadate,                phosphomolybdate, p-toluenesulfonate,                benzenesulfonate, methanesulfonate,                tetrafluoroborate or hexafluorophosphate.

PT P
PN CN109456828-A
TI Production of egg yolk oil involves stirring whole egg and Haematococcus pluvialis, detecting astaxanthin content in egg yolk liquid, sieving and extracting, filtering, testing astaxanthin content in egg yolk oil product and storing.
AU JIAO J
   LIU Z
AE ZHONGMING BIOTECHNOLOGY SHENZHEN CO LTD (ZHON-Non-standard)
GA 201925365P
AB    NOVELTY - The production method of egg yolk oil involves (S1) taking whole egg and Haematococcus pluvialis as raw materials, (S2) processing using egg white separation equipment and then stirring and mixing to obtain egg yolk liquid, (S3) detecting astaxanthin content in egg yolk liquid using chromatographic process, (S4) drying at 40 degrees C for 8 hours under moisture content of less than 10%, (S5) sieving egg yolk pieces to obtain egg yolk powder, (S6) extracting using carbon dioxide supercritical extraction process at 40 degrees C for 2.5 hours under pressure of 32 MPa and carbon dioxide flow rate of 50 l/hour, (S7) centrifugally filtering at 7000 rpm, (S8) testing astaxanthin content in the egg yolk oil product using chromatographic process and (S9) packaging and storing.
   USE - The method is useful for production of egg yolk oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables production of egg yolk oil with high efficiency and prolonged service life. The egg yolk oil is rich in astaxanthin.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The step (S1) involves pasteurizing and sterilizing at 60 degrees C for 30 minutes. The chromatographic process is carried out using 30C liquid chromatography column and dichloromethane-acetone solution as eluent under detection wavelength of 420-450 nm, flow rate of 1-1.5 ml/minute and injection volume of 15-25 mu l. The content of astaxanthin in the egg yolk liquid needs to reach 50-100 PPM. The content of astaxanthin in the egg yolk oil product needs to reach 160-260 PPM. The sieving step is carried out using 10 mesh sieve. The storing step is carried out at room temperature. Preferred Components: Dichloromethane and acetone are used in a ratio of (1:4-1:5).
DC D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).)

PN CN109133103-A; CN109133103-B
TI Producing iron-based oxygen carrier chemical chain            ammonia involves using iron-based oxygen carrier            chemical chain, passing water vapor into hydrolysis            reactor to iron nitride and hydrolyzing to obtain pure            ammonia gas.
AU SUN C
   XIANG W
   CHEN S
   DUAN L
AE UNIV SOUTHEAST (UYSE-C)
GA 2019055344
AB    NOVELTY - Producing iron-based oxygen carrier chemical                chain ammonia involves using iron-based oxygen                carrier chemical chain, passing water vapor into a                hydrolysis reactor to iron nitride at 100-300                degrees C and hydrolyzing to obtain pure ammonia                gas, and at the same time, iron-based oxygen                carrier ferric oxide-x having the same valence                state as that of iron nitride, transporting the                iron-based oxygen carrier into the air reactor at                500-900 degrees C to obtain ferric oxide and                nitrogen. The ferric oxide produced is transported                into a nitrogen-carbon reduction reactor at                700-1000 degrees C under nitrogen atmosphere and                coke reduction. The iron oxide is reduced to iron                nitride, and carbon monoxide gas is obtained, or                carbon dioxide gas is obtained, or a mixed gas of                carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide is obtained, and                then the iron nitride is re-transferred into the                hydrolysis reactor.
   USE - Method for producing iron-based oxygen carrier                chemical chain ammonia.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The water vapor is a                  high temperature superheated water vapor. The                  nitrogen produced is also transported into the                  nitrogen carbon reduction reactor as a carrier                  oxygen reduction reaction. The raw material in                  the material provides a nitrogen source of iron                  nitride. Preferred Composition: The molar ratio                  of coke to iron oxide added is (1.5-3):1.

PT P
PN CN109095490-A
TI Modifying quicklime useful for sintering, comprises e.g. crushing untreated quicklime, adding water to crushed quicklime, uniformly stirring, adding sodium hydroxide solid to solution, stirring, heating, and mixing sodium sulfide and water.
AU WANG X
   GUO H
   ZHANG Y
   MENG H
   ZHANG L
   PENG P
AE SHANDONG TAISHAN IRON & STEEL CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201902474M
AB    NOVELTY - Modifying quicklime comprises (1) crushing untreated quicklime; (2) adding water to the crushed quicklime, uniformly stirring, and then allowing to stand for 30-40 minutes; (3) adding sodium hydroxide solid to the resulting solution, and stirring uniformly; (4) heating the resulting prepared solution to 60-70 degrees C, and continuously stirring; (5) mixing sodium sulfide and water in a mass ratio of 1:100 to prepare an aqueous solution of sodium sulfide; (6) uniformly adding the sodium sulfide aqueous solution to the solution obtained in the step (4); (7) introducing carbon dioxide into the solution prepared in the step (6), and after the solution becomes cloudy, then continuously introducing carbon dioxide, until a clear layer appears on the upper layer of the solution; and (8) filtering the resulting solution, and to obtain filtrate i.e. modified quicklime solution.
   USE - The modified quicklime is useful for sintering (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The modified quicklime has excellent mechanical properties, long service life and good stability, and improves steel quality.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (1), crushing the quicklime to a maximum particle size of not greater than 3 mm. Preferred Components: In the step (2), the mass ratio of quicklime to water is (1-3):100. In the step (3), the mass ratio of the solution to sodium hydroxide solid is 200:(1-3). In the step (6), the mass ratio of the aqueous solution of sodium sulfide to the solution obtained in the step (4) is (3-5):100.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).)

PT P
PN CN108855109-A; CN108855109-B
TI Preparing chemical looping partial oxidation            methane producing synthesis gas oxygen carrier            comprises adding iron lanthanum oxide carrier to nickel            nitrate solution, immersing and stirring uniformly,            drying and calcining.
AU ZHU Y
   WU J
   LIU R
   SHEN Q
   HUANG C
   WANG X
AE UNIV NORTHWEST (UYXB-C)
   UNIV NORTHWEST (UYXB-C)
GA 2018956939
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a chemical looping partial oxidation                methane producing synthesis gas oxygen carrier,                comprises adding the iron lanthanum oxide carrier                to a nickel nitrate solution, immersing and                stirring uniformly, then drying and calcining at                400-800 degrees C for 1-12 hours to obtain an                oxygen carrier.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing a chemical                looping partial oxidation methane producing                synthesis gas oxygen carrier used for the partial                oxidation of methane to syngas in a chemical chain                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple, reproducible, can use                its own lattice oxygen to selectively oxidize                methane to syngas in a wide temperature range (more                than 90%) and can be used in air, water vapor,                carbon dioxide, water/carbon dioxide mixture;                regenerates the carrier in various oxidizing                atmospheres and produces high value-added products                e.g. high purity hydrogen, carbon monoxide or                syngas; and maintains high reactivity and synthesis                gas selectivity of the oxygen carrier after                repeated cycles of reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation of iron                  lanthanum oxide carrier comprises: either adding                  the cerium nitrate and the ferric nitrate                  precursor to the citric acid solution at a molar                  ratio of 1:1, where the ratio of the amount of                  the citric acid substance to the total substance                  of the lanthanum and iron cations is 2-3: 1, to                  be completely dissolved, adjusting the pH to 5-8                  by using ammonia water with a mass concentration                  of 5-28%, evaporating to a gel state in a water                  bath of 60-100 degrees C, drying and finally                  calcining at 700-1400 degrees C for 1-12 hours to                  obtain the carrier; or dissolving the cerium                  nitrate and ferric nitrate in deionized water at                  30-80 degrees C and the total concentration of                  cations of lanthanum and iron is 0.1-2.5 mol/l,                  adding a solution of ammonium carbonate having a                  concentration of 0.5-5 mol/l to adjust the pH to                  8-10, stirring at 50-80 degrees C for 2-4 hours,                  allowing to stand for 2-4 hours, filtering,                  washing, drying and finally calcining at 700-1400                  degrees C for 1-12 hours to obtain the carrier.                  The drying is carried out at 60-150 degrees C for                  2-24 hours. A communicating fuel reactor and a                  regeneration reactor are employed, the oxygen                  carrier is circulated between the fuel reactor                  and the regeneration reactor, where the oxygen                  carrier is oxidized in the fuel reactor to                  methane, the temperature of methane oxidization                  is 800-950 degrees C, the oxygen carrier is                  oxidized and regenerated in the regeneration                  reactor and the temperature of the oxidation                  regeneration is 800-950 degrees C. Preferred                  Components: The concentration of the nickel                  nitrate solution is 0.344-3.44 mol/l. The oxygen                  carrier formula is n%nickel/iron lanthanum oxide,                  where n is the mass percentage content of nickel,                  i.e. 1-10. The raw material for the reaction in                  the fuel reactor is a mixture of methane and an                  inert gas. The raw material in the regeneration                  reactor is a mixture of oxygen and an inert gas,                  a mixture of water vapor and an inert gas, a                  mixture of carbon dioxide and an inert gas or                  water vapor or a mixture of carbon dioxide and an                  inert gas. The volume content of methane in the                  mixture of methane and inert gas is 1-100%, the                  volume content of oxygen in the mixture of oxygen                  and inert gas is 1-100%, the volume of the                  mixture of water vapor and inert gas is 1-100%,                  the volume content of the mixture of carbon                  dioxide and inert gas is 1-100% and the total                  volume content of the mixture of water vapor,                  carbon dioxide and inert gas is 1-100%.

TI Preparation of iron-nickel-zinc-based amorphous            alloy material, involves vacuuming raw materials,            heating, melting, adding oxide nano-powder and carbon            dioxide, cooling, vacuuming, heating, melting and            casting molten alloy solution.
AU DING H
   KE Q
   HU C
AE WUHU JUNHUA MATERIALS CO LTD (WUHU-Non-standard)
GA 201894314N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of iron-nickel-zinc-based                amorphous alloy material, involves (S1) weighing                the raw materials and putting into gas-tight medium                frequency induction melting furnace, vacuuming the                medium frequency induction melting furnace, heating                the magnetic material at 1300-1500 degrees C,                melting and mixing, (S2) weighing oxide                nano-powder, putting into vacuum medium frequency                induction melting furnace, mixing with the molten                material, adding carbon dioxide to reduce oxides                and cooling to obtain magnetic material alloy                mother ingot, (S3) putting magnetic material alloy                mother ingot into airtight medium frequency                induction melting furnace, vacuuming the medium                frequency induction melting furnace, then heating                the magnetic material alloy mother ingot at                1300-1350 degrees C, completely melting the                magnetic material alloy mother ingot and casting                the molten alloy solution on a quenching single                roll.
   USE - Preparation of iron-nickel-zinc-based                amorphous alloy material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can disperse the small material                containing the silicon element and lanthanum                element uniformly to ensure overall performance of                amorphous alloy materials.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Composition: The raw materials                  comprise 0.6 %mass silicon, 0.2 %mass bismuth,                  1.9 %mass lithium, 4.4 %mass aluminum, 16 %mass                  nickel, 3 %mass zinc, 2.8 %mass manganese and                  remaining iron. In step (S1), the raw material                  having a large proportion of combined content is                  elemental lithium, elemental aluminum, elemental                  nickel, elemental zinc, elemental manganese and                  elemental iron. In step (S2), the raw materials                  having a small proportion of combined content are                  nano silica powder and nano antimony trioxide                  powder.

PT P
PN CN108706601-A; CN108706601-B
TI Preparation of potassium feldspar powder involves crushing potassium feldspar raw ore, washing, dehydrating, carrying out first magnetic separation, primary ball milling, carrying out second magnetic separation, and mixing with water.
AU DENG P
AE HEZHOU JUNXIN MINERAL PROD CO LTD (HEZH-Non-standard)
GA 201886136V
AB    NOVELTY - A potassium feldspar powder is prepared by crushing potassium feldspar raw ore into granularity of 3-5 cm, carrying out spiral chute washing, dehydrating, carrying out first magnetic separation, primary ball milling to grain diameter of 50-80 mesh, carrying out second magnetic separation, mixing with water and capturing agent, filling carbon dioxide, stirring under 5-10 MPa at 60-65 degrees C for 20-30 minutes, continuously charging carbon dioxide to adjust pressure to 12-15 MPa, stirring for 15-20 minutes, decompressing and filtering, secondary ball milling to grain diameter of 100-120 mesh, carrying out third magnetic separation, and drying at 100-110 degrees C. The capturing agent comprises 5-10:1-4:4-8:1-5:1-5:1-3 mass ratio of sodium petroleum sulfonate, oleic acid triethanolammonium salt, octadecylamine, fatty primary amine acetate, succinic acid and linoleic acid.
   USE - Method for preparing potassium feldspar powder.
   ADVANTAGE - The product has high whiteness.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The potassium feldspar powder is prepared by crushing potassium feldspar raw ore into granularity of 3-5 cm, carrying out spiral chute washing, dehydrating, carrying out first magnetic separation at 0.5-0.8 T at rate of 100-120 revolutions/minute (rpm), primary ball milling to grain diameter of 50-80 mesh, carrying out second magnetic separation at 0.8-1.2 T at rate of 110-130 rpm, mixing with water and capturing agent according to mixing ratio of 1 Kg:6-8 Kg:0.86-0.95 g, filling carbon dioxide, stirring under 5-10 MPa at 60-65 degrees C for 20-30 minutes, continuously charging carbon dioxide to adjust pressure to 12-15 MPa, stirring for 15-20 minutes, decompressing and filtering, secondary ball milling to grain diameter of 100-120 mesh, carrying out third magnetic separation at 1.2-1.5 T at rate of 100-120 rpm, and drying at 100-110 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The capturing agent comprises 8:2:5:4:2:2 mass ratio of sodium petroleum sulfonate, oleic acid triethanolammonium salt, octadecylamine, fatty primary amine acetate, succinic acid and linoleic acid.

TI Preparation method of nano-calcium carbonate for solvent-type ink additive, involves calcining limestone, performing digestion treatment, lime milk refining, carbonizing, surface-treating, separating and drying.
AU ZHANG H
   ZHANG S
   CHEN N
   SHAO X
   MA J
AE WUHU ZHUOYUE NANO NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (WUHU-Non-standard)
GA 201879582A
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of nano-calcium carbonate involves (1) calcining limestone into calcium oxide and a kiln gas containing carbon dioxide through a lime kiln, (2) placing calcium oxide in a digestive machine, and adding water to digest to obtain calcium hydroxide emulsion, (3) coarse filtering calcium hydroxide emulsion, performing hydrocyclone separation, and sieving to obtain calcium hydroxide fine emulsion, (4) mixing calcium hydroxide fine emulsion with water, transferring calcium hydroxide fine emulsion to a carbonization tank, filling carbon dioxide, cooling to a certain temperature, and passing through kiln gas after sprayed, foamed and dedusted for carbonization to obtain cubic nano-calcium carbonate, (5) coating the cubic nano-calcium carbonate with surface treatment agent, and performing liquid phase coating process, and (6) filtering nano-calcium carbonate, drying, depolymerizing, packaging and storing.
   USE - Preparation method of nano-calcium carbonate for solvent-type ink additive.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of nano-calcium carbonate with excellent dispersion, high transparency and glossiness.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In the step (1), the obtained kiln gas containing carbon dioxide is further purified. In the step (4), after carbonization to form a certain number of crystal nucleus, adding crystal modifier to control the growth rate of each crystal, converting the crystal nucleation control into crystal growth control to achieve controllable shape, and continuously carbonizing to obtain uniformly dispersed cubic nano-calcium carbonate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The surface treatment agent in the step (5) is rosin and a coupling agent, so that the surface of the nano-calcium carbonate is uniformly coated with resin acid.

TI Manufacturing sodium hypochlorite pentahydrate crystal powder, by drying sodium hypochlorite pentahydrate crystals in wet state containing sodium hydroxide using gas and carbon dioxide gas.
AU KUWAHATA MITSUYOSHI
   KUWAHATA M
   MITSUYOSHI K
AE KANEKA CORP (KANF-C)
   KANEKA CORP (KANF-C)
   KANEKA CORP (KANF-C)
   KANEKA CORP (KANF-C)
GA 201846575L
AB    NOVELTY - Method for manufacturing sodium hypochlorite pentahydrate crystal powder, involves drying sodium hypochlorite pentahydrate crystals in a wet state containing sodium hydroxide at a concentration of 0.01 wt.% or high using a gas having a temperature of less than 25 degrees C, a dew point of -2 degrees C or less, and a carbon dioxide gas at a concentration of 250-1600 parts/million (ppm) until the moisture content of crystals reaches 2.5 wt.% or less.
   USE - The method is useful for producing manufacturing sodium hypochlorite pentahydrate crystal powder (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method manufactures sodium hypochlorite pentahydrate crystal powder with excellent storage stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) sodium hypochlorite pentahydrate crystal powder in which sodium carbonate is present on the surfaces of the sodium hypochlorite pentahydrate crystals, where the sodium carbonate has concentration of 0.1 wt.% or high and the moisture content between crystals is 2.5 wt.% or less; and
   (2) aqueous solution of sodium hypochlorite prepared using the sodium hypochlorite pentahydrate crystal powder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The drying is carried out under atmospheric pressure or under reduced pressure. The effective chlorine concentration of the sodium hypochlorite pentahydrate crystal powder is higher than the effective chlorine concentration of the wet state of sodium hypochlorite pentahydrate crystal powder. The sodium hypochlorite pentahydrate crystals in wet state are obtained by reacting an aqueous solution of sodium hydroxide with chlorine gas, filtering sodium chloride from the reaction solution, crystallizing sodium hypochlorite pentahydrate crystal from an aqueous solution of sodium hypochlorite, before crystallizing sodium hypochlorite pentahydrate crystals, and adding sodium hydroxide to an aqueous solution of sodium hypochlorite so that the molar fraction with respect to sodium hypochlorite is 0.01-0.26. The sodium hypochlorite pentahydrate crystal powder has repose angle of 40 degrees or less. The powder has freely-settled apparent density measured based on JISK7365 (1999) is 0.60 g/ml or more. The effective chlorine has concentration of 41 wt.% or more.

GA 201848393L
AB    NOVELTY - A refrigerant transport hose comprises a tubular innermost layer (1) and a rubber layer (2) provided in the outer periphery of the innermost layer.    Rubber layer (2)

AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of polyaniline cuprous oxide                composite material involves installing hydrophilic                carbon paper on platinum electrode as working                electrode, using platinum electrode as counter                electrode and saturated calomel electrode as                reference electrode, placing above electrodes in                electrolytic cell, adding mixed solution of aniline                and acid into electrolytic cell, carrying out redox                reaction by cyclic voltammetry to obtain                polyaniline template, placing polyaniline template                in mixed solution of copper salt with concentration                of 0.01-0.5 mol/L, preferably 0.05-0.2 mol/L and                acid with concentration of 0.05-0.5 mol/L,                preferably 0.05-0.2 mol/L and preparing polyaniline                cuprous oxide composite material by constant                potential electro deposition method at constant                potential of (-0.05)-(-0.3) V, preferably                (-0.05)-(-0.2) V and deposition time 150-1800                seconds, preferably 600-1800 seconds.
   USE - Preparation of polyaniline cuprous oxide                composite material used in electroreduction of                carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared polyaniline cuprous oxide                composite material has controllable morphology and                size and excellent electrocatalytic                performance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The copper salt is                  copper nitrate, copper sulfate and/or copper                  chloride. The acid is sulfuric acid, nitric acid                  and/or hydrochloric acid.

The concentration of the acid in the mixed                  solution is 0.05-2 mol/L. In step (iii), the mass ratio of the solid phase of the foamed material to the liquid phase of the foamed material obtained is 1:0.5-1.5 g/ml; the stirring time is 0.2-0.5 minutes.Preferred Method: In step (iv), the pressure of the pressurizing device is 8-12 MPa, the temperature is 40-45degreesC, and the holding time is 3-5 minutes, and foaming time is 10-60 seconds.

AB    NOVELTY - Composition comprises a porous particle, and a nanotube penetrating the porous particle, where (a) the lumen of the nanotube is substantially free of the porous particle, and/or (b) the composition further comprises a plant active, a drug, a ligand, a polymer and/or an aminated compound embedded in the porous particle.
   USE - The composition is useful for optimizing catalytic reaction, delivering plant active to plant, delivering drug to human or animal, absorbing environmental contaminants, capturing carbon dioxide and converting calcium oxide into calcium carbonate (all claimed), and used in catalyst design in chemical synthesis and petroleum processing.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for synthesizing the composition, which involves aerosolizing into a precursor solution droplets comprising a templating surfactant, a silica precursor, a solvent and nanotubes, and heating the droplets to make particles, heat-preserving the composition in a solution comprising the plant active and a solvent, and evaporating the solvent to yield the composition, or heat-preserving the composition in a solution comprising the drug and a solvent, and evaporating the solvent to yield the composition, or contacting the composition comprising a porous particle and a nanotube penetrating the porous particle with the ligand, or heat-preserving composition comprising a porous particle and a nanotube penetrating the porous particle in a solution comprising polymers and a solvent, and evaporating the solvent to yield polymers embedded in the interior of the porous particle, or aerosolizing a precursor solution comprising a salt comprising calcium, a solvent, and nanotubes into droplets and heating the droplets to make particles;
   (2) a method for optimizing a catalytic reaction, which involves contacting one or more hydrocarbon reagents with the composition;
   (3) a method for delivering a plant active to a plant, which involves spraying the plant with the composition and a carrier;
   (4) a method for delivering a drug to a human or animal, which involves administering a composition comprising the composition and a carrier to a human or animal;
   (5) a method for absorbing environmental contaminants on the composition of matter, which involves placing the composition in contact with the environmental contaminants;
   (6) a method of capturing carbon dioxide in the composition, which involves contacting the composition with carbon dioxide; and
   (7) a method of converting calcium oxide into calcium carbonate, which involves contacting the composition with carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst comprises zeolite, noble metal, transition metal cluster and/or transition metal cluster ion exchanged into zeolite, preferably zeolite. The porous particle comprises calcium oxide or silica. The porous particle is mesoporous, or microporous. The porous particle is chosen from mobil composition of matter No. 41 (MCM-41) , MCM-48, MCM-50, silica mesoporous (SBA)-11 , SBA-12, SBA-15, SBA-16, KIT-5 and COK-12, preferably MCM-41. The nanotube is ceramic or halloysite, preferably halloysite. The salt comprises calcium is calcium hydroxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The composition comprises the plant active embedded in the porous particle, drug embedded in the porous particle, ligand embedded in the porous particle, and aminated compound. The plant active comprises a fertilizer, a nutrient and/or a pesticide. The aminated compound comprises monoethanolamine, triethanolamine, diethanolamine, diethylenetriamine, tetraethylenepentamine, tetraethylenepentamine-acrylonitrile and/or 3-aminopropytriethyoxysilane. The templating surfactant comprises cetyltrimethylammonium bromide (CTAB). The silica precursor comprises tetraethyl orthosilicate (TEOS). The solvent is ethanol/water (80/20 by volume).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The step of heating the droplets further provides waste products and the method further involves separating the particles from the waste products using a filter. The method further involves grafting the ligand to the porous particle. Preferred Conditions: The heating of the droplets is carried out at 200-600 degrees C. The solvent is evaporated at more than 23 degrees C. Preferred Components: The composition further comprises (a) a catalyst, a plant active, a drug, a ligand, a polymer and/or an aminated compound embedded in the porous particle, and (b) a catalyst embedded in the porous particle. The polymer comprises poly(ethyleneimine) (PEI). The composition further comprises an environmental contaminant absorbed to the ligand, carbon dioxide adsorbed to the PEI, and carbon dioxide adsorbed to the aminated compound. The precursor solution further comprises a catalyst. The carrier is a liquid.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN111732312-A; CN111732312-B
TI Anaerobic denitrification using excess residue as            carbon source by suspending anaerobic residue, shaking            with medium, centrifuging, inoculating, adding nitrate,            sealing, adding sodium nitrate and xanthan gum and            anaerobically denitrifiying.
AU ZHANG F
   ZENG J
   DAI K
AE UNIV FUJIAN AGRIC & FORESTRY (UYFN-C)
GA 2020994094
AB    NOVELTY - Method of anaerobic denitrification using                excess residue as carbon source by taking anaerobic                residue, suspending and shaking with medium,                centrifuging, inoculating the anaerobic residue                into a culture flask, adding culture medium,                passing in excess nitrogen and carbon dioxide mixed                gas, adding nitrate and sealing, adding sodium                nitrate, sodium alginate and xanthan gum every 5                days, analyzing the changes of nitrate nitrogen and                nitrogen every day, inoculating the denitrifying                bacteria colony enriched into an anaerobic reactor,                and inoculating the denitrifying bacteria colony                enriched into an anaerobic reactor, denitrifying                bacteria colony enriched inoculated into an                anaerobic reactor, and the remaining residue,                sodium alginate and xanthan gum are used as mixed                substrates, introducing the excessive mixed gas of                nitrogen and carbon dioxide, adding sewage                containing nitrate nitrogen, and carrying out                anaerobic denitrification.
   USE - Method of anaerobic denitrification using                excess residue as carbon source.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures high denitrification                efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method of anaerobic denitrification using                excess residue as carbon source involves (i) taking                anaerobic residue, suspending and shaking with                medium to mix evenly, centrifuging to remove                supernatant, repeating the above operation several                times, (ii) inoculating the anaerobic residue                obtained in the step (i) into a culture flask,                adding culture medium, using sodium alginate,                xanthan gum, and remaining residue as mixed                substrates, passing in excess nitrogen and carbon                dioxide mixed gas, adding nitrate and sealing,                carrying out continuous cultivation at 20-25                degrees C for 25-35 days, adding sodium nitrate,                sodium alginate and xanthan gum every 5 days,                analyzing the changes of nitrate nitrogen and                nitrogen every day, when the denitrification rate                reaches 200-1000 mg nitrogen/(Lx day), the                denitrification flora will be enriched, and (iii)                inoculating the denitrifying bacteria colony                enriched in the step (ii) into an anaerobic                reactor, using remaining residue, sodium alginate                and xanthan gum as mixed substrates, and (iii)                inoculating the denitrifying bacteria colony                enriched in the step (ii) into an anaerobic                reactor, denitrifying bacteria colony enriched in                the step (ii) is inoculated into an anaerobic                reactor, and the remaining sludge, sodium alginate                and xanthan gum are used as mixed substrates,                introducing the excessive mixed gas of nitrogen and                carbon dioxide, adding sewage containing nitrate                nitrogen, and carrying out anaerobic                denitrification at 20-25 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (i) involves                  using anaerobic residue, which comes from an                  anaerobic reactor that has been running for 1                  year to degrade the remaining residue. Preferred                  Conditions: In step (i), the rotation speed of                  the centrifugation is 7000-9000 rpm, and the                  centrifugation time is 2-4 minutes. The pH of the                  medium is adjusted to 7-7.4 using acid or alkali.                  Preferred Components: The culture medium                  comprises 80-320 mg/l ammonium chloride, 48-12                  mg/l sodium sulfate, 90-110 mg/l potassium                  chloride, 3-6 mg/l calcium chloride, 45-55 mg/l                  magnesium chloride hexahydrate, 0.2-0.6 mg/l                  manganese(II) chloride tetrahydrate, 0.8-1.2 mg/l                  cobalt(II) chlorate dihydrate, 5-5.4 mg/l ferrous                  sulfate heptahydrate, 0.05-0.15 mg/l aluminum                  chloride, 0.05-0.15 mg/l sodium molybdate                  dihydrate, 0.4-0.6 mg/l nickel(II) chloride                  hexahydrate, 1.2-1.6 mg/l copper(II) chloride                  dihydrate and 2-2.4 mg/l zinc sulfate                  monohydrate. Preferred Composition: In step (ii),                  the concentrations of the excess residue, sodium                  alginate, and xanthan gum in the reaction system                  are 5-10 g/l, 1-5 g/l, and 1-5 g/l. The volume                  ratio of nitrogen and carbon dioxide in the mixed                  gas of nitrogen and carbon dioxide is 70-85:15-25                  vol.%. In step (ii), the concentration of the                  nitrate in the reaction system is 0.8-1.2 g/l. In                  step (iii), the inoculation amount of the                  denitrifying flora is 50-250 ml/l. In step (iii),                  the nitrate nitrogen content of the nitrate                  nitrogen sewage is 150-500 mg                  nitrate-nitrogen/l.

PN CN110946318-A
TI Preparing natural nicotine for electronic            cigarettes comprises using tobacco raw materials to            produce waste secondary cigarette dust, and adding            water is added of the moisture content of the tobacco            smoke.
AU ZHOU G
   WU J
   CHU G
   HU A
   JIANG J
   WANG H
   XIA Q
   XIAO W
   SHEN Y
   LU X
AE CHINA TOBACCO ZHEJIANG IND CORP (CNTA-C)
GA 202028437H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing natural nicotine comprises using                tobacco raw materials to produce waste secondary                cigarette dust, and adding water is added to 20-40                wt.% of the moisture content of the tobacco smoke.                The extraction pressure is 25-30 MPa, the                extraction temperature is 60-65 degrees C, and the                entraining agents for supercritical carbon dioxide                extraction include ethanol and propylene glycol in                the process of supercritical carbon dioxide                extraction of tobacco super extract. The ethanol                accounts for 5-10 wt.% of the raw material mass                concentration, propylene glycol accounts for 5-10                wt.% of the raw material mass concentration, static                extraction 0.5-0.75h, dynamic extraction 2.5-3.0h.                The tobacco super extract is subjected to                first-order molecular distillation, the heating                temperature is 65-70 degrees C, the condensation                temperature is 10-15 degrees C, and the water ring                pump is used. The water and ethanol is removed, and                the first-level light components discarded.
   USE - Method for preparing natural nicotine for                electronic cigarettes.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare natural                nicotine, which removes heavy components such as                wax grease and hydrocarbons.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing natural nicotine comprises using                tobacco raw materials to produce waste secondary                cigarette dust, and adding water is added to 20-40                wt.% of the moisture content of the tobacco smoke.                The extraction pressure is 25-30 MPa, the                extraction temperature is 60-65 degrees C, and the                entraining agents for supercritical carbon dioxide                extraction include ethanol and propylene glycol in                the process of supercritical carbon dioxide                extraction of tobacco super extract. The ethanol                accounts for 5-10 wt.% of the raw material mass                concentration, propylene glycol accounts for 5-10                wt.% of the raw material mass concentration, static                extraction 0.5-0.75h, dynamic extraction 2.5-3.0h.                The tobacco super extract is subjected to                first-order molecular distillation, the heating                temperature is 65-70 degrees C, the condensation                temperature is 10-15 degrees C, and the water ring                pump is used. The water and ethanol is removed, and                the first-level light components discarded. The                first-stage heavy components are subjected to                two-stage distillation, and during the                distillation, the heating temperature is 100-110                degrees C, the condensation temperature is 20-25                degrees C, and the rotary vane pump is used. The                final second-stage light component is                high-concentration nicotine excess.

PT P
PN CN110408337-A; CN110408337-B
TI Preparing electromagnetic carbon aerogel modified electromagnetic shielding tape involves adding resorcinol and nanofibers to aqueous formaldehyde solution, stirring, adding sodium carbonate catalyst to obtain aerogel precursor solution.
AU LIU B
   XIE W
AE JIANGXI BOC TECHNOLOGY GROUP CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201994283X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing electromagnetic carbon aerogel modified electromagnetic shielding tape involves adding resorcinol and nanofibers to the aqueous formaldehyde solution, stirring uniformly, adding a sodium carbonate catalyst to obtain an aerogel precursor solution, applying the aerogel precursor solution to the surface of the metal copper foil, heating and solidifying, then introducing carbon dioxide, heating and drying, and subjecting to high temperature carbonization to obtain elastic carbon aerogel modified copper foil. The resorcinol-formaldehyde aerogel precursor solution while stirring is dried in a carbon dioxide atmosphere to obtain carbon aerogel powder by high temperature carbonization. The carbon aerogel powder is dispersed in an acidic solution, boiled and purified, dried, and clacined to obtain purified carbon aerogel powder. The polyacrylate is added to 2-butanone.
   USE - Method for preparing an electromagnetic carbon aerogel modified electromagnetic shielding tape.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare an electromagnetic carbon aerogel modified electromagnetic shielding tape that has easy processing, good chemical stability, high temperature resistance, and high conductivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing electromagnetic carbon aerogel modified electromagnetic shielding tape involves adding resorcinol and nanofibers to the aqueous formaldehyde solution, stirring uniformly, adding a sodium carbonate catalyst to obtain an aerogel precursor solution, applying the aerogel precursor solution to the surface of the metal copper foil, heating and solidifying, then introducing carbon dioxide, heating and drying, and subjecting to high temperature carbonization to obtain elastic carbon aerogel modified copper foil. The resorcinol-formaldehyde aerogel precursor solution while stirring is dried in a carbon dioxide atmosphere to obtain carbon aerogel powder by high temperature carbonization. The carbon aerogel powder is dispersed in an acidic solution, boiled and purified, dried, and clacined to obtain purified carbon aerogel powder. The polyacrylate is added to 2-butanone, stirred uniformly and added purified carbon aerogel powder, a curing agent and a dispersing agent, and stirring is continued uniformly to obtain carbon-based polyacrylate conductive voltage sensitive adhesive. The surface of the conductive cloth is coated with the carbon-based polyacrylate conductive voltage sensitive adhesive, and cured by heating to obtain double-sided conductive tape. The double-sided conductive tape is placed on the surface of the release film, an elastic carbon aerogel-modified copper foil, a double-sided conductive tape. The elastic carbon aerogel-modified copper foil and the double-sided conductive tape prepared are pressurized laminated to prepare an electromagnetic shielding tape modified with an elastic carbon aerogel.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The nanofiber is nano cellulose whiskers, carbon nanofibers or polyacrylonitrile nanofibers. The acidic solution is a mixed solution of nitric acid and hydrochloric acid in a volume ratio of 1:3. Preferred Conditions: The high temperature carbonization temperature is 700-800 degrees C, and the time is 2-3 hours. The content of the elastic carbon aerogel in the elastic carbon aerogel-modified copper foil is 5-20 wt.%. The content of the purified carbon aerogel powder in the carbon-based polyacrylate voltage-sensitive adhesive is 5-15 wt.%. The content of the carbon-based polyacrylate voltage-sensitive adhesive in the double-sided conductive tape is 20-35 wt.%.
DC A85 (Electrical applications.); G03 (Adhesives - excluding dispensers. Polymeric adhesives are also classified in Section A (C09H, J).); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. PT P
PN US2019292421-A1; US10759976-B2
TI Making color changeable adhesive comprises providing carbon dioxide sensitive color changeable material, pressure sensitive adhesive, adding base to color changeable material and pressure sensitive adhesive to form compositions and mixing.
AU HEACOCK G L
   MILLS A
AE SENSOR INT LLC (SENS-Non-standard)
GA 2019822403
AB    NOVELTY - Making color changeable adhesive (I) (202) comprises providing at least one carbon dioxide sensitive color changeable material comprising a color changeable pigment, dye and/or particle coated with a color changeable pigment or dye, where the color changeable material has a pKa of 7.5-9.5, adding a base to the color changeable material to produce a color changeable material composition having a pH that is equal to or 1.5 greater than the pKa of the color changeable material, providing a pressure sensitive adhesive, adding a base to the pressure sensitive adhesive to produce a pressure sensitive adhesive composition having a pH that is neutral or alkaline up to a pH equal to or 1.5 greater than the pKa of the color changeable material and mixing the color changeable material composition with the pressure sensitive adhesive composition.
   USE - The method is useful for making color changeable adhesive and which used to make color changeable indicators (all claimed) which is used in applications of food packaging and medical applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes color changeable material which maintains efficacy and stability of the indicator or sensors that provides simple, reliable, and cost effective detection comprising ammonia and carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for
   (1) the color changeable adhesive (II) comprising the pressure sensitive adhesive and color changeable material above as per se (I), and the pigment, dye or particle is a carbon dioxide indicator;
   (2) the color changeable adhesive comprising the color changeable material comprising the color changeable pigment, dye and/or particle coated with color changeable pigment or dye, where the color changeable material has a pKa of 4-6.5, and the pressure sensitive adhesive having a pH that is neutral or acidic down to a pH equal to or 1.5 less than the pKa of the color changeable material and the pigment, dye or particle is an ammonia indicator;
   (3) color changeable indicator comprising a substrate (201) and a color changeable adhesive disposed on the substrate where the color changeable adhesive as per se (II); and
   (4) producing color changeable indicator comprising providing the substrate, applying the color changeable adhesive on the substrate where the color changeable adhesive comprises a color changeable material as per se (II).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows schematic cross sectional view of the color changeable adhesive.
   Substrate (201)
   Color changeable adhesive (202)
   Diffusion layer (203)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step of producing the color changeable indicator further comprises applying a diffusion layer (203) on the color changeable adhesive. The color changeable indicator further comprises: carbon dioxide generating layer; a diffusion layer disposed on the color changeable adhesive. The diffusion layer is a release paper disposed on the color changeable adhesive. Preferred Components: The pigment, dye or particle is substantially homogenously dispersed within the adhesive. The color changeable material is a dye comprises phenol red, cresol red or rosalic acid. The pressure sensitive adhesive is tack white. The base is tetra butyl ammonium hydroxide. The color changeable material composition further comprises a polymer comprising hydroxyethylcellulose. The color changeable adhesive comprises 0.05-20 wt.% changeable material. The adhesive comprises rubber water based adhesive, acrylic/water based adhesive, rubber/solvent based adhesive, acrylic/solvent based adhesive or hot melt adhesive. The color changeable adhesive further comprises an acid.
   EXAMPLE - 0.1 g of Color changeable particles (phenol red, cresol red and rosalic acid each coated on silicon dioxide cores), 1 ml tetra butyl ammonium hydroxide as a base (40% in water), and 1 g of water were combined to form a color changeable material composition. The components were mixed together by stirring. The color changeable material composition was then combined with 4 g of tack white adhesive. These components were mixed together by stirring for at least 4 hours to give carbon dioxide sensitive color changeable adhesives were coated on clear polyethylene terephthalate film. The phenol red carbon dioxide sensitive adhesive forms a pink ink that dries to a purple color and changes to yellow when purged with carbon dioxide, simulating packaging under carbon dioxide. The adhesive returns to its original purple color within 1.5 minutes in air. The cresol red carbon dioxide sensitive adhesive forms a purple ink that dries to blue color, and changes to yellow in the presence of carbon dioxide. The adhesive returns to blue within 2 minutes in air. The rosolic acid carbon dioxide sensitive adhesive forms a pink ink that dries to pale pink and changes to beige color with carbon dioxide present. It recovers to original color within 1 minute in air.

PT P
PN WO2018220474-A1; DE112018002771-T5; CN110997559-A; US2020172397-A1
TI Nitrogen rich two dimensional hexagonal carbon            nitride type mesoporous graphitic carbon nitride            material having rod shape morphology, specified average            pore diameter, and specified nitrogen/carbon            ratio.
AU PARK D
   LAKHI K S
   RAVON U
   AL-BAHILY K
   SCARANTO J
   VINU A
   PIAO D
   ALBAHILY K
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
GA 201896944B
AB    NOVELTY - Nitrogen rich two dimensional hexagonal carbon                nitride (C3N4+) type mesoporous graphitic carbon                nitride (gMCN) material having a rod shape                morphology, an average pore diameter of 4-6 nm, and                a nitrogen/carbon ratio of 1.35-1.6, is                claimed.
   USE - Used as nitrogen rich two dimensional                hexagonal carbon nitride type mesoporous graphitic                carbon nitride material.
   ADVANTAGE - The material has a Brunauer-Emmett-Teller                surface area of 200-400 (preferably 230-300) m2/g                (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) synthesizing a two dimensional carbon                nitride material formed from a cyclic                amino-triazole precursor, comprising (a) contacting                a santa barbara amorphous-15 (SBA-15) silica                template with an aqueous cyclic amino-triazole and                hydrogen chloride (HCl) precursor solution forming                a templated reaction mixture, where the silica                template is formed by (i) adding                tetraethylorthosilicate to a mixture of P-123                surfactant and HCl forming a template reaction                mixture, (ii) incubating the template reaction                mixture at a temperature of 35-45 degrees C for 1-4                hours, (iii) heating the template reaction mixture                to 100-200 degrees C for 1-4 days forming a heated                template reaction mixture, (iv) drying the heated                template reaction mixture at 100 degrees C for 5-10                hours forming a dried template reaction mixture and                (v) washing the dried template reaction mixture                with ethanol forming the SBA-15 template, (b)                heating the templated reaction mixture to a                temperature of 40-200 degrees C, preferably 80-120                degrees C for 4-8 hours forming a first heated                reaction mixture, (c) heating the first heated                reaction mixture to a temperature of 100-200                degrees C, preferably 140-180 degrees C for 4-8                hours forming a second heated reaction mixture, (d)                carbonizing the second heated reaction mixture by                heating to 500 degrees C for 4-6 hours forming a                hard template/cyclic amino-triazole-based carbon                nitride product, and (e) removing the hard template                to form a nitrogen rich two dimensional rod type                C3N4+ gMCN; and
   (2) a carbon dioxide capture process,                comprising contacting a nitrogen rich two                dimensional C3N4+ gMCN with carbon dioxide                containing feed source forming a reactant mixture,                where carbon dioxide is absorbed in or to gMCN, and                incubating the reactant mixture forming carbon                dioxide conversion product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nitrogen/carbon                  ratio is 1.4-1.5. The gMCN: is derived from a                  cyclic amino-triazole precursor, preferably                  3-amino-1,2,4-triazole; and has a carbon dioxide                  adsorption capacity of 7.5-10 mmol/g at 273K and                  30 bar. The material has a total pore volume of                  0.4-0.7 cm3/g. Preferred Method: In the method:                  the template reaction mixture is heated at a                  temperature of 130 degrees C forming a SBA-15-130                  template; the template reaction mixture is heated                  at a temperature of 150 degrees C forming a                  SBA-15-150 template; the carbonizing is performed                  under constant nitrogen flow; the cyclic                  amino-triazole precursor is                  3-amino-1,2,4-triazole; the first heated reaction                  mixture is incubated at a temperature of 130                  degrees C; the first heated reaction mixture is                  incubated at a temperature of 150 degrees C; and                  the template is removed by treating the hard                  template/cyclic amino-triazole-based carbon                  nitride product with hydrogen fluoride or an                  ethanol wash. The method further comprises:                  crushing the second heated reaction mixture                  before the carbonizing; and bringing the second                  heated mixture to carbonization temperature using                  a ramping rate of 2-4 degrees C/minute.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN CN108853498-A; CN108853498-B
TI Preparing phthalocyanine green polymer nanoparticle used for e.g. treating tumor, by dissolving phthalocyanine green in first organic solvent, dropping second organic solvent, performing ultrasonic process and freeze-drying product.
AU CHEN A
   WANG S
   XU P
   PAN Y
AE UNIV HUAQIAO (UYHQ-C)
GA 201895730J
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing phthalocyanine green polymer nanoparticle, involves dissolving the phthalocyanine green in first organic solvent, dropping the second organic solvent to obtain mixture, where the first solvent is ethanol, methanol, hexafluoroisopropanol or dimethyl sulfoxide, and second solvent is dichloromethane, acetone or chloroform, and the volume ratio of the first solvent and the second solvent is 1-9:1-9, pumping the mixture into supercritical granulated autoclave at liquid flow rate of 0.1-4 ml/minute, where the specific parameters in the autoclave are pressure of 80-300 bar, temperature of 32-55 degrees C and carbon dioxide flow rate of 20-100 g/minute, performing supercritical carbon dioxide for 0.5-2 hours, performing ultrasonic process of the phthalocyanine green nanoparticles in polycation-containing sodium chloride solution, stirring the mixture, centrifuging the mixture, washing the precipitate with ultrapure water, and freeze-drying the product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing phthalocyanine green polymer nanoparticle which is used in preparation of drug and fluorescence imaging contrast agent for preventing and/or treating tumor (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nanoparticle has good stability, optical absorption wavelength range of near infrared wave band, and high penetrability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing phthalocyanine green polymer nanoparticle, involves dissolving the phthalocyanine green in first organic solvent, dropping the second organic solvent to obtain mixture, where the first solvent is ethanol, methanol, hexafluoroisopropanol or dimethyl sulfoxide, and second solvent is dichloromethane, acetone or chloroform, and the volume ratio of the first solvent and the second solvent is 1-9:1-9, pumping the mixture into supercritical granulated autoclave at liquid flow rate of 0.1-4 ml/minute, where the specific parameters in the autoclave are pressure of 80-300 bar, temperature of 32-55 degrees C and carbon dioxide flow rate of 20-100 g/minute, performing supercritical carbon dioxide for 0.5-2 hours to obtain phthalocyanine green nanoparticles, performing ultrasonic process of the phthalocyanine green nanoparticles in polycation-containing sodium chloride solution having a pH of 1-13, stirring the mixture for 2-24 hours, centrifuging the mixture, washing the precipitate with ultrapure water, and freeze-drying the product, where the polycation is polylysine, polyornithine or polyarginine.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The concentration of the phthalocyanine green in the mixture is 1-30 wt.%. The phthalocyanine green nanoparticles are ultrasonically dispersed at a concentration of 0.1-2 mg/ml and stirred for 2-24 hours. The sodium chloride solution has a concentration of 0.1-1 mol/l. The concentration of the polycation in the sodium chloride solution is 0.1-4 g/ml. The speed of the centrifugation is 8000-15000 revolutions per minute (rpm) and the time is 15-30 minutes. The resulting precipitate is washed with ultrapure water for 2-3 times.
   ACTIVITY - Cytostatic.

PN CN108640598-A
TI Preparing solid carbon concrete prefabricated            member involves subjecting industrial waste gas to dust            removal treatment through dust filter, and mixing            dedusted industrial waste gas with normal temperature            air.
AU WANG S
   JIANG H
   LU L
   YIN H
   ZHANG Z
AE XUZHOU INST TECHNOLOGY (XUZH-C)
GA 201881314F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing solid carbon concrete prefabricated                member involves subjecting the industrial waste gas                to dust removal treatment through a dust filter,                and mixing the dedusted industrial waste gas with                normal temperature air so that the temperature of                the mixed industrial waste gas is 30-40 degrees C.                The ratio of coarse aggregate, fine aggregate,                cement and water according to the performance                parameters required for the concrete preform is                determined, and then coarse aggregate, fine                aggregate, cement and water are mixed in a mixing                vessel to form a concrete mixture for 10-15                minutes. The industrial waste gas is then                continuously injected into the mixing container                during the mixing process until the end of the                mixing and stirring so that the concrete mixture                and the industrial waste gas are mixed uniformly.                The concrete mixture is subjected to carbonization                reaction with with carbon dioxide and industrial                waste gas, and the industrial waste gas is                collected after the carbonization reaction.
   USE - Method for preparing solid carbon concrete                prefabricated member.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare solid carbon                concrete prefabricated member which fixes the                carbon dioxide inindustrial waste gas in the                concrete pre-preparation process, reduces the                content of carbon dioxide in the industrial waste                gas, and ensures the needed strength of concrete                prefabricated member.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The concrete mixture and steel bars are placed                in the mold of the preform and the vibrating                quality required for concrete is ensured. The                preform mold is then placed in a closed concrete                carbon-fixing chamber and the treated industrial                waste gas is injected into the concrete carbon                fixation chamber, where the humidity in the                concrete carbon fixation chamber is 35-65% in the                humidifier. The carbonation reaction between the                concrete mixture and the carbon dioxide in the                industrial waste gas is continued, and industrial                waste gas is collected after carbonization                reaction, where the curing time of concrete in the                preform mold is not less than 28 days. The                industrial waste gas collected after the                carbonization reaction is mixed with the normal                temperature air and the dust-removed industrial                waste gas so that the temperature of industrial                exhaust after mixing is 30-40 degrees C, the mixed                gas is re-injected into the concrete carbon                fixation chamber, the final industrial exhaust gas                is discharged until the concrete curing time is                reached, and the finished concrete preform is taken                out from the preform mold.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The concrete                  carbon-fixing chamber is a transparent rubber                  tank, and the volume is 3-5 times the volume of                  the preform mold. The reinforcing bar of the                  concrete precast member is an anti-rust steel bar                  or an ordinary steel bar which has been subjected                  to anti-rust pretreatment. The cement is ordinary                  Portland cement.

AE UNIV HEFEI TECHNOLOGY (UYHE-C)
GA 2018409687
AB    NOVELTY - Coke oven gas system comprises air separation                unit (1), gasification unit (2), acid gas removal                unit (4), hydrogen/carbon monoxide separation unit                (5), glycol synthesis unit (6), refining glycol                unit, oxalate synthesis unit, methane dry reforming                unit, steam methane reforming unit and coke-oven                gas purification unit. The air separation unit is                provided with an air inlet, nitrogen outlet and                oxygen outlet. The gasification unit is provided                with a raw coal inlet, oxygen inlet and                gasification synthetic gas outlet. The acid gas                removal unit is provided with gasified synthesis                gas inlet, nitrogen inlet, carbon dioxide outlet                and purifying synthesis gas outlet.
   USE - Coke oven gas system for preparing ethylene                glycol (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The coke oven gas system has high utilization                value and low carbon efficiency, reduces emission                of greenhouse gas, and improves coal glycol carbon                utilization efficiency, energy efficiency and                economic benefit.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing ethylene glycol, which                involves:
   (A) pre-heating raw coal, drying in                gasification unit for gasification reaction to                generate coke-rich gasified hydrogen and carbon                dioxide gas;
   (B) entering gasified synthesis gas directly                into acid gas removal unit to separate carbon                dioxide, subjecting carbon dioxide in methane dry                reforming unit, adding rest purified synthesis gas                containing carbon monoxide and hydrogen, in                coke-oven gas purification unit, removing sulfide                and separating to obtain hydrogen and purifying                methane;
   (C) subjecting hydrogen into glycol synthesis                unit, purifying the methane into two parts,                entering one portion of methane intodry reforming                unit, separating from acid gas removal unit to                generate dry reforming synthesis gas containing                carbon monoxide and hydrogen; and
   (D) entering other portion into steam methane                reforming unit, reacting water vapor to generate                steam and hydrogen, reforming synthesis gas of                carbon monoxide, purifying dry reforming synthesis                gas generated by the acid gas removal, entering                into hydrogen/carbon monoxide separation unit,                separating to obtain carbon monoxide, entering into                oxalate synthesis unit, producing oxalate and                introducing ethylene synthesis unit, entering the                glycol with dimethyl oxalate reaction to generate                crude glycol, adding crude glycol by ethylene                glycol refining unit to obtain glycol product with                high purity.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of coke                oven gas system.
   Air separation unit (1)
   Gasification unit (2)
   Acid gas removal unit (4)
   Hydrogen/carbon monoxide separation unit                (5)
   Glycol synthesis unit (6)

The ratio of purifying methane and                  total methane of methane steam reforming unit is                  0.5-0.8:1. the ratio of hydrogen to dimethyl                  oxalate is 30-60:1. TI System for liquefying carbon dioxide used as cleaning gas in field for manufacturing e.g. semiconductors, has liquefaction line for allowing carbon dioxide to be liquefied by performing heat exchange operation with cooling water.
AU PARK S W
   KIM S
AE PARK S W (PARK-Individual)
GA 201997057B
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a cooling line for allowing a refrigerant to be circulated through a compressor (1), a condenser (2), a dryer (3), a solenoid valve (4), and an expansion valve (5) to perform heat exchange operation in a first heat exchanger (6), where cooling water stored in a cooling water storage tank (10) or non-heating fluid including specific heat equal to or higher than the water passes through a second heat exchanger (30) by driving a pump (20) such that the heat-exchanged cooling water passes through the first heat exchanger of the cooling line. A cooling water circulation line allows the cooling water to be cooled by performing the heat exchange operation with the refrigerant. A carbon dioxide liquefaction line allows carbon dioxide to be liquefied by performing the heat exchange operation with the cooling water passing through the second heat exchanger while allowing gaseous carbon dioxide to pass through the second heat exchanger.
   USE - System for liquefying carbon dioxide used as cleaning gas in a field for manufacturing semiconductors, molds and glass.
   ADVANTAGE - The system allows the cooling water including high specific heat to be heat-exchanged with the refrigerant, and vaporized carbon dioxide to be liquefied by the cooling water including small temperature change, so that liquefied carbon dioxide can be stably supplied. The system allows cleaning operation using the liquefied carbon dioxide to be continuously and stably performed since the liquefied carbon dioxide can be stably generated by heat exchange with the cooling water including the small temperature change.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a system for liquefying carbon dioxide used as cleaning gas in a field for manufacturing semiconductors, molds and glass.

   Solenoid valve (4)
   Expansion valve (5)
   Heat exchangers (6, 30)
   Cooling water storage tank (10)
   Pump (20)
