PT P
PN CN113697836-A
TI Preparing nano-coated composite calcium carbonate by adding calcium hydroxide slurry to processing vessel, adding medium containing carbon dioxide to lower liquid level of calcium hydroxide slurry, bubbling, carbonizing calcium hydroxide, extracting carbonized calcium carbonate slurry, and grinding.
AU WANG Z
   YANG C
   WU Q
   WANG Y
AE ZHEJIANG QINGDA POWDER MATERIAL CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 2021E19239
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing nano-coated composite calcium carbonate, involves (a) preparing calcium hydroxide slurry, (b) adding calcium hydroxide slurry to a processing vessel, stirring the calcium hydroxide slurry, adding the medium containing carbon dioxide to the lower liquid level of the calcium hydroxide slurry, bubbling, and carbonizing the calcium hydroxide to obtain calcium carbonate suspended with nano-light calcium carbonate slurry, (c) taking the center of the lower liquid level of the calcium carbonate slurry, extracting carbonized calcium carbonate slurry, transferring to another processing vessel, grinding calcium carbonate, adding heavy calcium carbonate to the calcium carbonate slurry, stirring, adding a medium containing carbon dioxide to the lower liquid level of the mixed material to prepare composite calcium carbonate slurry material, and (d) filtering the composite calcium carbonate slurry, drying, pulverizing and dispersing to obtain the composite calcium carbonate.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing nano-coated composite calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides nano-coated composite calcium carbonate with excellent coating effect, reliable preparation and convenient operation and stable processing.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing nano-coated composite calcium carbonate, involves (a) preparing calcium hydroxide slurry with a concentration of 18-23 %;mass, (b) adding calcium hydroxide slurry to a processing vessel, continuously stirring the calcium hydroxide slurry at a stirring processing speed of 300-600 rpm, adding the medium containing carbon dioxide to the lower liquid level of the calcium hydroxide slurry, continuously bubbling for 20-30 minutes, and carbonizing the calcium hydroxide to obtain calcium carbonate suspended with nano-light calcium carbonate slurry, (c) taking the center of the lower liquid level of the calcium carbonate slurry as the material removal point, extracting 70-80 vol.% carbonized calcium carbonate slurry, transferring to another processing vessel, followed by calcium carbonate slurry, grinding calcium carbonate, adding heavy calcium carbonate to the calcium carbonate slurry in a volume ratio of 10:(0.6-0.8), continuously stirring the mixed material for 10-30 minutes at a stirring speed of 500-700 rpm for 3-5 minutes, adding a medium containing carbon dioxide to the lower liquid level of the mixed material to prepare composite calcium carbonate slurry material, and (d) filtering the composite calcium carbonate slurry, drying, pulverizing and dispersing to obtain the composite calcium carbonate.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device for nano-coated composite calcium carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: In step (c), when adding heavy calcium carbonate, sodium carboxymethyl cellulose is added as a dispersing agent, where the number of added portion of the dispersing agent is 0.01-0.02 times that of the calcium carbonate slurry, and the pH of the composite calcium carbonate slurry is controlled to 7-7.5. In step (b) and (c), the stirring blade of the stirring device used in the stirring process is fan-shaped, and during the stirring process, it drives the mixture in the processing container to rotate and stir, while rolling from bottom to top, and mixing in a spiral shape. In step (b), the pH of the calcium carbonate slurry is controlled to be 7.5-8, and the calcium carbonate slurry in which the nano-light calcium carbonate is suspended at least contains nano-light calcium carbonate with a particle size of 40-600 nm. In step (c), the particle size of the ground calcium carbonate is 0.5-10 microns. In step (d), the solid content of the composite calcium carbonate slurry obtained by filtration is 60-70%, when it is dry, placed in a 95-105degrees Celsius environment for negative pressure drying treatment for 5-8 hours. In step (b), the medium containing carbon dioxide is air containing 25-40% carbon dioxide or a mixture of carbon dioxide and nitrogen, and the flow rate of the medium is 2.5 -3 m3/hour, and the temperature of the processing vessel is heat-preserved at 25-55degrees Celsius. In step (c), the medium containing carbon dioxide is carbon dioxide gas at a temperature of 0-20degrees Celsius or a mixture of carbon dioxide and nitrogen with a carbon dioxide content of not less than25%, and the flow rate of the medium is 1.5-2 m3/hour. In this step, the temperature of the processing vessel is dynamically maintained at 0-60degrees Celsius, and the carbon dioxide-containing medium is air containing 25-40% carbon dioxide at a temperature of 10-20degrees Celsius or a mixture of carbon dioxide and nitrogen, while adding the carbon dioxide-containing medium, 5-10 pieces of columnar dry ice with a diameter of 0.8-1.2 cm and a length of 3-5 cm are added to the lower layer of the mixed material at preset time intervals of 2-5 minutes in the liquid level, and when 70-80% of the carbonized calcium carbonate slurry by volume is pumped away, the calcium hydroxide slurry in the processing vessel is added to the initial amount and the concentration of the calcium hydroxide slurry is kept at 18-23 %mass.

TI Preparing self-contained microporous copolyimide aerogel block adsorption material used for for carbon dioxide, heavy metal, dye, involves performing under protection of nitrogen, adding aromatic self-microporous diamine.
AU WANG Y
   MA H
   LENG X
   GAO Y
   LI Y
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 2021B9969N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing self-contained microporous copolyimide aerogel block adsorption material involves performing under protection of nitrogen, adding aromatic self-microporous diamine and common aromatic diamine monomer to aprotic polar organic solvent, dissolving completely, adding aromatic dianhydride monomer to the solution, reacting for no less than 12 hours, adding aromatic polyamine-based crosslinking agent to the above solution, reacting for no less than 2 hours, adding acetic anhydride and pyridine catalyst to the solution, standing at room temperature for no less than 24 hours to obtain a block polyimide wet gel, and drying with supercritical carbon dioxide to obtain self-producing microporous copolyimide aerogel block adsorption material
   USE - Method for preparing self-contained microporous copolyimide aerogel block adsorption material used for carbon dioxide, heavy metal, dye.
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared self-contained microporous copolyimide aerogel block adsorption material has increased porosity , and the adsorption performance of carbon dioxide, heavy metal and dye is significantly improved, and it has a sponge feel, strong adsorption and reduction ability, and is a comprehensive block adsorbent material with excellent performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing self-contained microporous copolyimide aerogel block adsorption material involves performing under protection of nitrogen, adding aromatic self-microporous diamine and common aromatic diamine monomer to aprotic polar organic solvent, dissolving completely, adding aromatic dianhydride monomer to the solution, reacting for no less than 12 hours, adding aromatic polyamine-based crosslinking agent to the above solution, reacting for no less than 2 hours, adding acetic anhydride and pyridine catalyst to the solution, standing at room temperature for no less than 24 hours to obtain a block polyimide wet gel, and drying with supercritical carbon dioxide to obtain self-producing microporous copolyimide aerogel block adsorption material, where the aromatic self-containing microporous diamine includes 3,3,3',3'-tetramethyl-2,2',3,3'-tetrahydro-1, 1'-spirobis(indene)-5,5'-diamine (SBI), 9,10-dihydro-9,10(1,2)benzoanthracene-2,6-diamine (Trip), 2,8-diamino-4,10-dimethyl-6H,12H-5,11-methane dibenzo(1,5)-diazepine (TBDA), 9,9'-spirobis(fluorene)-2,2'-diamine (SBF), 5,5'-diamino-3,3,3',3'-tetramethyl-2,2',3,3'-tetrahydro-1,1'-spirobis(indene)-6,6'-diol (SBI-1), 6,15-diamino-9,10-dihydro-9,10-(1,2)benzoanthracene-2-carboxylic acid (Trip-1), 3,9-diamino-4,10-dimethyl Base-6H,12H-5,11-methane dibenzo(1,5)-diazooctane (TBDA-1), and 3,3'-diamino-9,9'-spirobi(fluorene)-2, 2'-diol (SBF-1).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The aromatic polyamine crosslinking agent is selected from 9,10-dihydro-9,10-(1,2)benzoanthracene-2,6,14-triamine, tetraphenylaminomethane, 1,3, 5-tris(4-aminophenyl)benzene, tetraanilinosilane, 1,3,5-tris(4-aminophenyl)pyridine, 1,3,5-tris(4-aminophenoxy)benzene, tetraphenylamine fundmantane, 1,3,5-tris(4-aminophenyl)triazine, 1,3,5-tris(4-aminophenylthio)benzene, triphenylaminomethane, tris(4-aminophenyl)amine, and 1,3,5-benzenetricarboxylic acid hydrazide. The aprotic polar organic solvent is selected from N-methylpyrrolidone, N,N-dimethylacetamide, N,N-dimethylformamide, and dimethylsulfoxide. The commonly used aromatic diamine is selected from p-phenylenediamine, phenylenediamine, 2,4-diaminotoluene, 4,4-diaminobiphenyl, 3,5-diaminobenzoic acid, 3,5-diamino benzonitrile, 3,5-diaminotoluene, 2,4,6-trimethyl-3,5-diaminobenzenesulfonic acid, 4,4'-diaminotriphenylmethane, 4,4'-diamino-2,2'-dimethylbiphenyl, 4,6-diaminoresorcinol, 2,2'-bis(trifluoro methyl)-4,4'- diaminobiphenyl, 3,3'-dihydroxy-4,4'-diphenyldiamine, 3,3'-Dichloro-4,4'-diaminobiphenyl, 4,4'-diaminoterphenyl, 3,3'-dimethoxybenzidine, 4,4'-diaminodiphenyl ether, 4,4-diaminodiphenyl sulfide, 4,4-diaminodiphenyl sulfone, 4,4'-diaminobenzophenone, 4,4'-diaminodiphenylmethane, 3,4'-diaminodiphenyl ether, 4,4'-diamino-3,3'-dimethyl biphenyl, 2,2'-bis(trifluoromethyl)-4,4'-diaminophenyl ether, and 9,9'-bis(4-aminophenyl)fluorene. The aromatic dianhydride is selected from pyromellitic dianhydride, biphenyl tetracarboxylic dianhydride, 4,4'-(hexafluoroisopropyl) diphthalic anhydride, 3,3',4,4'-two Phenyl ether dianhydride, 3,3',4,4'-diphenyl sulfide dianhydride, 3,3',4,4'-diphenyl sulfone dianhydride, 3,3',4,4'-benzophenone dianhydride, 3,3',4,4'-benzyl alcohol dianhydride, and 2,2'-bis(4-(3,4-dicarboxybenzene (oxy)phenyl)propane tetraacid dianhydride. Preferred Conditions: The molar ratio of the common aromatic diamine and aromatic self-microporous diamine, aromatic dianhydride, and polyamine-based crosslinking agent is 100:(101.6-110):(1.1-7.0). The solid content is 3-10%, where the solid content includes aromatic self-contained microporous diamine, commonly used aromatic diamine, aromatic dianhydride, aromatic polyamine crosslinking agent/aromatic self-owned microporous diamine, commonly used aromatic diamine, aromatic dianhydride, aromatic polyamine-based crosslinking agent, acetic anhydride, pyridine catalyst, aprotic polar organic solvent) asterisk100% The molar ratio of acetic anhydride and pyridine catalyst is 1-10:1. The molar ratio of the aromatic self-microporous diamine to the sum of the aromatic self-microporous diamine and the commonly used aromatic diamine is not less than 5%. The 5% thermal decomposition temperature of the self-contained microporous copolyimide aerogel block absorbent material is 450-550degrees Celsius, the density is not more than 0.12 g/cm3, the porosity is not less than 90%, and the shrinkage rate is not more than 4%.

PN CN112169815-A; CN112169815-B
TI Use of iron-based catalyst in carbon dioxide hydrogenation reaction to synthesis low-carbon olefins including products of low-carbon olefins with carbon numbers ranging from 2-4 reaction without the reduction and activation process.
AU HUANG Y
   LIANG B
   MA J
   ZHANG T
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 202106152S
AB    NOVELTY - Use of iron-based catalyst in carbon dioxide hydrogenation to synthesize low-carbon olefins, comprises (i) 1-90 wt.% iron oxides, (ii) 1-97 wt.% iron carbides, and (iii) lithium, sodium, potassium, rubidium, and/or cesium as 0.01-10 wt.% alkali metal oxides, is claimed.
   USE - The catalyst is useful in carbon dioxide hydrogenation reaction to synthesis low-carbon olefins including products of low-carbon olefins with carbon numbers ranging from 2-4 reaction without the reduction and activation process (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst is simple; directly converts carbon dioxide to low-carbon olefins; and reaches the conversion rate of carbon dioxide more than 40%, the selectivity of methane less than 10%, and the carbon-based selectivity of low-carbon olefins 46.6%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the iron-based catalyst, comprising (I) dissolving soluble iron salt in deionized water to obtain solution A, where the concentration of iron ions is 0.001-10 mol/l, (ii) dissolving alkali in deionized water to prepare precipitant to 0.001-10 mol/l obtain solution B, (III) mixing solution A with solution B, where the volume ratio of solution B to solution A is 0.5-10, and stirring the resulting mixture in a water bath at 25-95 degrees C for 0.5-24 hour, (IV) filtering and washing the obtained turbid liquid, drying the filter cake in the air at 50-200 degrees C for 12-48 hours, calcining the obtained solid in the air at 300-500 degrees C for 2-6 hours to obtain solid C, (V) dissolving soluble salts of lithium, sodium, potassium, rubidium, and/or cesium in deionized water to obtain solution D, where the concentration of alkali metal ions is 0.001-10 mol/l, (VI) taking solid C, dropping the prepared solution D to solid C according to the volume ratio 0.1-10 or pouring into solid C, and mixing them, (VII) immersing the mixture obtained in the step (VII) at room temperature for 0.5-48 hours, (VIII) drying the mixture obtained in the step (VII) at 60-120 degrees C for 4-24 hours, (IX) calcining the dried mixture at 350-700 degrees C for 1-8 hours to obtain solid E, and (X) carbonizing the obtained solid E in a mixture of carbon monoxide and argon gas at 300-500 degrees C for 1-12 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The iron-based catalyst comprises 4-85 wt.% iron oxides, (ii) 5-95 wt.% iron carbides, and (iii) 0.1-10 wt.% alkali metal oxides. Preferred Components: The iron oxide comprises iron(II,III) oxide and/or iron(III) oxide. The iron carbide comprises iron2carbon or iron2.2carbon or iron5carbon 2 and/or iron3carbon. The alkali metal oxide comprises sodium oxide, potassium oxide, and/or cesium oxide. In the step (I), the soluble iron salt comprises nitrate and/or chloride. In the step (II), the alkali comprises ammonia water, sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, sodium carbonate and/or potassium carbonate. In the step (V), the soluble salts of lithium, sodium, potassium, rubidium and cesium comprises nitrates. Preferred Method: In the step (IX), the calcining is carried out at 380-500 degrees C for 0.5-4 hours. In the step (X), the carbonizing is carried out in a flowing carbon monoxide and argon atmosphere according to the volume ratio of 0.5-5, and increasing the temperature from room temperature to carbonization temperature by program temperature rise, and heating rate is 1-10 degrees C/minute. The use of catalyst to a fixed bed reactor for carbon dioxide hydrogenation to synthesize low-carbon olefins under the conditions comprises the reaction gas including mixture of carbon dioxide and hydrogen, where the molar ratio hydrogen:carbon dioxide =0.5-4, the volumetric space velocity is 600-30000 ml/(gcatx hour), the reaction temperature at 200-600 degrees C, and the reaction pressure under 0.1-6 MPa.

TI Preparing cadmium metal-organic framework, comprises e.g. adding cadmium salt and ligand into solvent, then carrying out ultrasonic treatment until cadmium salt and ligand mix completely, and reacting.
AU WANG X
   LIANG L
   ZHANG X
AE UNIV SHANXI (UYSX-C)
GA 202028257A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cadmium metal-organic framework, comprises adding cadmium salt and ligand in a molar ratio of 1:0.8-1.2 into solvent, then carrying out ultrasonic treatment until the cadmium salt and ligand mix completely, and reacting at 60-100 degrees C for 1-5 days to obtain final product, where cadmium salt is cadmium nitrate nonahydrate, ligand is 2,5-di(1H-1,2,4-triazol-1-yl)terephthalic acid (I), and solvent is N,N-dimethylformamide.
   USE - The cadmium metal-organic framework is useful for: fluorescence detection of dimethyl sulfoxide; capturing carbon dioxide (all claimed); and selective adsorption of carbon dioxide-nitrogen and carbon dioxide-methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The framework: has excellent thermal stability, and strong fluorescence enhancement effect; and can maintain original frame structure after the guest molecules are removed. The method: has mild reaction conditions; utilizes easily available raw materials; and is suitable for large scale production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing cadmium metal-organic framework of formula (Cd2(H2BTTA)(BTTA)(DMF)2), comprises adding cadmium salt and ligand in a molar ratio of 1:0.8-1.2 into solvent, then carrying out ultrasonic treatment until the cadmium salt and ligand mix completely, and reacting at 60-100 degrees C for 1-5 days to obtain final product, where cadmium salt is cadmium nitrate nonahydrate, ligand is 2,5-di(1H-1,2,4-triazol-1-yl)terephthalic acid of formula (I), and solvent is N,N-dimethylformamide. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) use of cadmium metal-organic framework in fluorescence detection of dimethyl sulfoxide, comprising soaking cadmium metal-organic framework in acetone for 48-72 hours to exchange high boiling point dimethylformamide molecules in cadmium metal-organic framework channel, then vacuum drying at 80-200 degrees C for 8-48 hours to remove the acetone molecules in channel, soaking vacuum-dried cadmium metal-organic framework in an equal volume of dimethylformamide, and dispersing uniformly for half-an-hour by ultrasonic treatment to obtain cadmium metal-organic framework suspension dispersed in dimethylformamide solvent, then adding dimethyl sulfoxide test solution into cadmium metal-organic framework suspension, and performing fluorescence detection; and
   (2) use of cadmium metal-organic framework in capturing carbon dioxide, comprising soaking cadmium metal-organic framework in ethanol for 48-72 hours to exchange high boiling point dimethylformamide molecules in cadmium metal-organic framework channel, then vacuum drying at 80-200 degrees C for 10-24 hours to remove dimethylformamide molecules in the channel, and then filling the vacuum-dried cadmium metal-organic framework into a device for storing carbon dioxide adsorbent.

AB    NOVELTY - Producing medical cable, comprises (a) forming MCX cable strips one side of a piece of paper by taping paper with a carbon dioxide laser, and exposing an outer conductor from the jacket of the MCX cable, (b) bonding each of the predetermined ground bars above and below the outer conductor, (c) cutting ends of the pair of ground bars bonded up and down, (d) stripping one side of the outer conductor with a YAG laser to expose the insulator from the outer conductor, (e) stripping one side of the insulator with a carbon dioxide laser to expose the inner conductor from the insulator, and (f) performing lead dipping process on the exposed inner conductor.

AE SUQIAN LINTONG NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (SUQI-Non-standard)
GA 201985654Q
AB    NOVELTY - Producing 2-hydroxy-3-naphthoic acid comprises e.g. mixing liquid ethylene naphthol with 50% sodium hydroxide aqueous solution, heating to 125-145 degrees C, raising the pressure to 0.25 MPa, and keeping the temperature for 0.5 hour, reacting sodium hydroxide with p-naphthol to obtain ethyl naphthol-sodium salt; transferring the ethyl naphthol-sodium salt solution into a dehydration autoclave which is longitudinally located with a plurality of annular groove plates having carboxylation reaction tanks, heating and dehydrating, and drying, diluting ethyl naphthol-sodium salt and placing in a carboxylation reaction tank on each annular groove plate; and introducing carbon dioxide gas into the dehydration high-pressure kettle by enabling ethyl naphthol-sodium salt positioned on the annular groove plate to react with carbon dioxide gas to obtain 2,3-acid disodium salt; and injecting water into the dehydration high-pressure kettle to obtain a 2,3-acid mother liquor.
   USE - The method is useful for producing 2-hydroxy-3-naphthoic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the carboxylation rate of ethyl naphthol sodium salt, reduces the amount of naphthol which is neutralized and recovered by pressure filtration, and indirectly reduces the amount of naphthol which is finally mixed into the finished product of 2-hydroxy-3-naphthoic acid and improves the purity of the finished product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing 2-hydroxy-3-naphthoic acid comprises (a) mixing liquid ethylene naphthol with 50% sodium hydroxide aqueous solution, heating to 125-145 degrees C, raising the pressure to 0.25 MPa, and keeping the temperature for 0.5 hour, reacting sodium hydroxide with p-naphthol to obtain ethyl naphthol-sodium salt; (b) transferring the ethyl naphthol-sodium salt solution into a dehydration autoclave which is longitudinally located with a plurality of annular groove plates having carboxylation reaction tanks, heating and dehydrating, and drying, diluting ethyl naphthol-sodium salt and placing in a carboxylation reaction tank on each annular groove plate; (c) introducing carbon dioxide gas into the dehydration high-pressure kettle by enabling ethyl naphthol-sodium salt positioned on the annular groove plate to react with carbon dioxide gas to obtain 2,3-acid disodium salt; (d) injecting water into the dehydration high-pressure kettle to obtain a 2,3-acid mother liquor, feeding 2,3-acid mother liquor into an acidification bucket, adding sulfuric acid into the acidification bucket to carry out acid precipitation reaction of the disodium salt of 2,3 acid with sulfuric acid, and dehydrating.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (a), empty the pressure sampling analysis at the completion of the reaction, if the excess of naphthol or sodium hydroxide is not more than 0.5%, the step ends, if the excess of sodium or the excess of naphthol is greater than 0.5%, should be added with naphthol or sodium hydroxide, stirring after feeding for 0.5 hour, the sample is re-sampled until the excess of naphthol or sodium hydroxide was not greater than 0.5%. In the step (b), the dehydration autoclave is heated by heating the jacket, and the dehydration valve is slowly opened when the pressure is raised to 0.15 MPa, when the pressure is raised to 0.24 MPa, the normal dehydration is started, and the control pressure is at 0.24 plus minus 0.02 MPa, when the temperature of the material rises to 240 degrees C, the dehydration valve is quickly opened, when the pressure drops to 0.05 MPa, close the dehydration valve, open the distillation valve, and turn on the vacuum and material end temperature was 225-230 degrees C, the vacuum reached -0.098 MPa, and the drying time was 30 minutes and drying operation was completed. In the step (d), before the acid precipitation operation, adding 35-40% dilute sulfuric acid to 2,3 acid mother liquor, lowering the pH to 4.8-6.5, naphthol sodium salt remaining in the carboxylation reaction is reacted with dilute sulfuric acid to form naphthol, and then pressure-filtering to recover the naphthol.

PN CN109420485-A; CN109420485-B
TI Cadmium oxide-titanium dioxide catalyst useful for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to methanol, comprises cadmium and titanium.
AU LI C
   WANG J
   LI Z
   HAN Z
   TANG C
   LIU H
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 2019233920
AB    NOVELTY - Cadmium oxide-titanium dioxide catalyst comprises 1-50% cadmium/cadmium and titanium, preferably 3-25% cadmium/cadmium and titanium.
   USE - The cadmium oxide-titanium dioxide catalyst is useful for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to methanol (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has methanol selectivity at 5 MPa, at 300 degrees C, 24000h-1, 17.9 carbon dioxide conversion rate, 76% methanol selectivity and 1127mg/(g and h) methanol yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing the catalyst, comprising preparing cadmium and titanium metal salt mixed solution, where the concentration of metal ions is 0.01-2 mol/l, preparing an aqueous solution of the same concentration of precipitant, precipitating two in water at 20-90 degrees C, controlling pH to 6-10, precipitation method comprises dropping the aqueous solution of the precipitating agent into the metal salt solution, dropping the metal salt solution into the aqueous solution of the precipitating agent or co-precipitating, drying the precipitate at 60-130 degrees C for 4-24 hours, calcining at 400-800 degrees C for 2-24 hours to obtain cadmium oxide-titanium dioxide, dipping method comprises preparing 0.01-2 mol/l titanium metal salt solution, preparing an aqueous solution of the same concentration of precipitant, precipitating in water at 20-90 degrees C, adjusting pH to 6-10, drying precipitate at 60-130 degrees C for 4-24 hours, calcining at 400-800 degrees C for 2-24 hours to obtain titanium dioxide support, dissolving Cd(NO3)2.6H2O in deionized water, placing into cadmium nitrate aqueous solution, sonicating for 10-30 minutes to obtain suspension, stirring the mixture, evaporating at at 100-130 degrees C, calcining at 400-800 degrees C for 2-24 hours to obtain cadmium oxide-titanium dioxide catalyst, mechanical grinding method comprises preparing 0.01-2 mol/l titanium metal salt solution, preparing the same concentration of precipitant aqueous solution, precipitating in water at 20-90 degrees C, adjusting pH to 6-10, drying precipitate at 60-130 degrees C for 4-24 hours and calcining at 400-800 degrees C for 2-24 hours to obtain titanium dioxide support, replacing titanium metal salt with a cadmium metal salt and cadmium oxide is prepared in the same manner as above and mechanically grinding to obtain catalyst; and
   (2) use method of the cadmium oxide-titanium dioxide catalyst, comprising reacting hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to methanol in a pressurized fixed bed continuous flow reactor, maintaining gas pressure to 1-10 MPa, reacting at 240-400 degrees C, where the space velocity is 3000-40000 h-1 and the n(hydrogen):n(carbon dioxide) molar ratio is 1-8.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cadmium salt is nitrate, acetate, halide, and/or sulfate. The precipitating agent is ammonia water, ammonium carbonate, sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate, sodium hydrogencarbonate, potassium hydrogencarbonate, sodium hydroxide and/or potassium hydroxide. The titanium salt is nitrate, acetate, halide, sulfate, titanyl sulfate, titanium isopropoxide, or titanium ethoxide. The titanium salt is titanium alkoxide, preferably titanium isopropoxide or titanium ethoxide, the titanium alkoxide is dissolved in an organic solvent and dropped into a preset aqueous solution. The organic solvent is methanol, ethanol, benzene, toluene, pentane and/or cyclohexane. Preferred Method: The calcination process comprises static calcination or flow atmosphere calcination, and the calcination atmosphere is air, oxygen and nitrogen. The catalyst is activated by the catalyst prior to application and activating hydrogen, nitrogen, and argon at 200-400 degrees C for 0.5-12 hours. The metal oxide solid solution catalyst is applied to hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to methanol reaction, the reaction pressure is 5 MPa, the reaction temperature is 320 degrees C, the space velocity is 24000 h-1 and the raw material gas n(hydrogen):n(carbon dioxide) molar ratio is 3.

TI Preparing peanut peel fermented milk comprises e.g. boiling fresh milk, then cooling to room temperature, removing peanuts from the peanut seed coat, and performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain peanut seed coat extract.
AU XIONG F
   LIU J

AE CASH CROP RES INST GUANGXI ACAD AGRIC SC (CASH-Non-standard)
   CASH CROP RES INST GUANGXI ACAD AGRIC SC (CASH-Non-standard)
GA 201894463H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing peanut peel fermented milk comprises e.g. boiling fresh milk, then cooling to room temperature for use, removing the peanuts from the peanut seed coat, subjecting the peanut seed to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain the peanut seed coat extract, foaming peanut kernel obtained after removing the peanut seed coat for 4-5 hours, and pulping to obtain peanut pulp, mixing the peanut seed coating extract and the peanut pulp, drying the tangerine peel until the moisture content is 8-12%, crushing and sieving by 100 sieves, and placing into a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle to obtain dried tangerine peel extract, mixing the obtained milk, peanut seed coat extract, peanut and dried orange peel extract, adding sweetener to adjust saccharinity of the mixed liquid to 50-60, homogenizing, and sterilizing.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing peanut peel fermented milk.
   ADVANTAGE - The milk is white, has no whey separation, smooth and bubble-free, non-layered, has moderately sweet and sour taste, high nutritive value and good health care effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing peanut peel fermented milk comprises (i) boiling fresh milk, then cooling to room temperature for use, removing the peanuts from the peanut seed coat, subjecting the peanut seed to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain the peanut seed coat extract, foaming peanut kernel obtained after removing the peanut seed coat for 4-5 hours, and then pulping to obtain peanut pulp, mixing the peanut seed coating extract and the peanut pulp, drying the tangerine peel until the moisture content is 8-12%, crushing and sieving by a sieve of 100 sieves, then placing into a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle to obtain dried tangerine peel extract, (ii) mixing the obtained milk, peanut seed coat extract, peanut and dried orange peel extract, adding sweetener to adjust saccharinity of the mixed liquid to 50-60, (iii) homogenizing, sterilizing, using ultrasonic wave to homogenize for 1-2 minutes the step (ii) obtained mixed solution, and then carrying out pasteurization, and cooling to room temperature, and (iv) adding ferment bacteria mixed liquid in fermenting peanut peel fermented milk obtained in step (iii), where the fermenting temperature is 35-40 degrees C, the fermentation time is 18-26 hours, the fermenting bacteria is composed of Monascus, Streptococcus lactis, Lactobacillus casei and Lactobacillus bulgaricus, adding amount of the fermentation bacteria is 0.01-0.1% of the mass of the mixed solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The extraction method of the peanut seed coat extract is: crushing the peanut seed with ethanol, acetic acid mixed solution as extracting agent with mass ratio of 1-3:1 to extract to obtain primary liquid extract at 80-90 degrees C, placing primary liquid extract in the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle to extract to obtain the peanut seed coat extract, where the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction temperature is 20-24 degrees C, the extracting pressure is 50-60 MPa, the flow speed of the carbon dioxide is 14 l/minute, the extracting time is 20-30 minutes. The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction condition of the dried orange peel extract is as follows: using petroleum ether as cosolvent, extracting temperature is 40-44 degrees C, the extracting pressure is 20-30 MPa, the flow speed of the carbon dioxide is 28 l/minute, the extracting time is 20-30 minute. The sweetener is sucrose, fructose, or maltose. The mass part of the milk, peanut seed coat extract, peanut, dried orange peel extract is 80-100 pts. wt., 0.1-1 pts. wt., 10-20 pts. wt. and 1-3 pts. wt.. The fermentation bacterial strain viable count number is 1:20-30:1-3:10-20 of Monascus, Streptococcus lactis, Lactobacillus casei and Lactobacillus bulgaricus. The frequency of ultrasonic wave is 40-50 kHz, the power density is 0.8-1 W/cm2.

TI Preparing cerium-containing bio-based material comprises adding e.g. modified lignin-based bisphenol monomer, sodium alpha-alkenylsulfonate and trans-2, cis 6-decadienol into a autoclave, vacuuming and replacing the air with carbon dioxide.
AU CHEN X
   LIN Q
   WANG H
   WANG X
   XU S
   ZHANG L
AE CHEN X (CHEN-Individual)
   LIN Q (LINQ-Individual)
   WANG H (WANG-Individual)
   WANG X (WANG-Individual)
   XU S (XUSS-Individual)
   ZHANG L (ZHAN-Individual)
GA 201871297W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cerium-containing bio-based material comprises adding 20-25 pts. wt. modified lignin-based bisphenol monomer, 0.3-0.7 pts. wt. sodium alpha -alkenylsulfonate, 0.1-1 pts. wt. trans-2, cis 6-decadienol, 0.01-0.1 pts. wt. tris(cyclopentadienyl)cerium, 0.1-1 pts. wt. 6, 6,7,7,8,8,8-heptafluoro-5,5-bis(trifluoromethyl)-1,3-octadiene, 0.1-1 pts. wt. 1-heptene-1,2-diboronic acid bis(2,3-dimethyl-2,3-butanediol)ethyl ester and 50-70 pts. wt. dimethyl sulfoxide into the autoclave, vacuuming, replacing the air in the reaction vessel with carbon dioxide, introducing the carbon dioxide for three times, reacting, heating, reacting, venting the reaction system, stirring, adding 5.5-9.5 pts. wt diamine into the reaction vessel, applying a vacuum, replacing the air in the autoclave with nitrogen, refilling with nitrogen three times, maintaining the pressure in the autoclave, reacting at room temperature, adding the system into water for precipitation, filtering, washing and drying.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing cerium-containing bio-based material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing cerium -containing bio-based material comprises adding 20-25 pts. wt. modified lignin-based bisphenol monomer, 0.3-0.7 pts. wt. sodium alpha -alkenylsulfonate, 0.1-1 pts. wt. trans-2, cis 6-decadienol, 0.01-0.1 pts. wt. tris(cyanodienyl)anthracene, 0.1-1 pts. wt. 6, 6,7,7,8,8,8-heptafluoro-5,5-bis(trifluoromethyl)-1,3-octadiene, 0.1-1 pts. wt. 1-heptene-1,2-diboronic acid bis(2,3-dimethyl-2,3-butanediol)ethyl ester and 50-70 pts. wt. dimethyl sulfoxide into the autoclave, vacuuming, replacing the air in the reaction vessel with carbon dioxide, introducing the carbon dioxide for three times, maintaining the pressure in the autoclave at 1.5-2.0 MPa, reacting, heating to 130-160 degrees C, reacting for 18-24 hours, venting the reaction system, stirring at room temperature for 60-80 minutes, adding 5.5-9.5 pts. wt diamine into the reaction vessel, applying a vacuum, replacing the air in the autoclave with nitrogen, refilling with nitrogen three times, maintaining the pressure in the autoclave at 0.1-0.5 MPa, reacting at room temperature for 24-36 hours, adding the system into water for precipitation, filtering, washing and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The diamine is dodecane diamine or 1,6-hexanediamine or hydrazine hydrate or isophorone diamine. The concentration of the sodium hydroxide solution is 16-20 %. The phase transfer catalyst is benzyltributylammonium chloride or dodecyldimethylbenzylammonium chloride or tetrabutylammonium hydrogen sulfate or tributylamine. Preferred Method: The modified lignin-based bisphenol monomer is obtained by adding 120-130 pts. wt. lignin-based bisphenol, 400-420 pts. wt. epichlorohydrin and 1.0-1.5 pts. wt. phase transfer catalyst into the reaction vessel, vacuuming, replacing the air in the reaction vessel with nitrogen, repeating three times, charging with nitrogen, heating to 70-90 degrees C in the nitrogen protection falling reaction system, reacting for 6-10 hours, cooling to room temperature, adding 400-450 pts. wt. sodium hydroxide solution and 1.0-1.5 pts. wt. phase transfer catalyst, reacting at room temperature for 60-120 minutes, extracting three times with 250-300 pts. wt. ethyl acetate, drying the combined organic phases 30-50 pts. wt. anhydrous sodium sulfate, filtering and evaporating the filtrate.

   USE - Anti-hydrogen sulfide/carbon dioxide corrosion oil pipe.
   ADVANTAGE - The anti-hydrogen sulfide/carbon dioxide corrosion oil pipe has high-strength, high-toughness anti-corrosion, and excellent product performance, high production efficiency of preparation process with low cost, good economic benefit and good popularization value.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing a anti-hydrogen sulfide/carbon dioxide corrosion oil pipe, which involves:
   (A) smelting, rolling and heat treatment, where the smelting comprises hot metal pretreatment, converter smelting, LF refining, VD degassing, round billet continuous casting;
   (B) adding silicon manganese, ferromanganese, ferrochrome and ferromolybdenum to deoxidizing and alloy prior into converter end point control target having less than or equal to 0.06 wt.% carbon, less than or equal to 0.015 wt.% phosphorus;
   (C) smelting by single slag process, adding 200KG white ash slag before tapping, final slag alkalinity less than or equal to 3.0, VD deep vacuum target value less than or equal to 0.06Kpa, deep vacuum time greater than or equal to 15 minutes, soft blowing argon is not less than 15 minutes, after the end of VD, the rare earth alloy is add to the rare earth alloy;
   (D) performing continuous casting process adopts electromagnetic stirring process, the blank type is delta 210mm, the constant pulling speed is controlled at 1.5M/minute, the molten steel superheat degree delta T greater than or equal to 30 degrees C, and the stacking method is used for cooling, and the tuyere is avoid;
   (E) rolling process includes round blank, sawing, heating, centering, perforation, rolling, tension reduction, cooling, cutting head, straightening and inspection, where temperature at which the round billet is perforate is 1250-1280 degrees C; and
   (F) rolling is carried out at 1160-1180 degrees C the temperature of the constant sheet is 780-840 degrees C the heat treatment includes quenching and tempering where, the quenching temperature is 900 plus minus 10 degrees C, and the temperature is maintain for 60-70 minutes, preferably 100 minutes to obtain the desired product.

PT P
PN WO2022058435-A1
TI Smectite or vermiculite clay mineral in the form of powder and having many layers comprising one octahedral type sheet sandwiched between two tetrahedral type sheets, useful for gas separation of carbon dioxide.
AU FOSSUM J O
   BREU J
   HUNVIK K
   LOCH P
AE UNIV NORWEGIAN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYNO-Non-standard)
GA 2022429636
AB    NOVELTY - Smectite or vermiculite clay mineral in the form of a powder and having many layers comprising one octahedral type sheet sandwiched between two tetrahedral type sheets. At least every other layer of the clay mineral comprises a hydroxide species comprising a cation including nickel, magnesium, iron, manganese or zinc.
   USE - The clay mineral is useful for gas separation of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The clay mineral exhibits increased adsorption capacity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) separating (P1) carbon dioxide from a gas mixture comprising carbon dioxide, comprising (a) contacting a smectite or vermiculite clay mineral e.g. synthetic fluorohectorite or natural bentonite with an aqueous solution of cations that can form hydroxides that are attractive for carbon dioxide, e.g. nickel, magnesium, iron, manganese or zinc ions, at a pH of greater than or equal to 7, (b) drying the clay mineral prepared in the step (a) to form a powder, and (c) contacting a bed of the powder from the step (b) with the gas mixture, the gas mixture preferably being supplied to the bed under pressure;
   (2) separating (P2) carbon dioxide from a gas mixture comprising carbon dioxide, comprising (a1) contacting a smectite or vermiculite clay mineral e.g. synthetic fluorohectorite or natural bentonite with an aqueous solution of cations that can form hydroxides that are attractive for carbon dioxide, at a pH of greater than or equal to 7, (b1) drying the clay mineral prepared in the step (a1) to form a powder, (c1) contacting a bed of the powder from the step (b1) with a gas stream comprising the gas mixture, the gas mixture preferably being supplied to the bed under pressure, (d1) removing the bed from the gas stream, and (e1) treating the bed to release carbon dioxide from it;
   (3) separating (P3) carbon dioxide from a gas mixture comprising carbon dioxide comprising (a2) contacting a smectite or vermiculite clay mineral e.g. synthetic fluorohectorite or natural bentonite with an aqueous solution of nickel ions, at a pH of greater than or equal to 7, (b2) drying the clay mineral prepared in the step (a2) to form a powder, (c2) contacting a bed of the powder from the step (b2) with a gas stream comprising the gas mixture, the gas mixture preferably being supplied to the bed under pressure, (d2) removing the bed from the gas stream, and (e2) treating the bed to release carbon dioxide from it; and
   (4) separating (P4) carbon dioxide from a gas mixture comprising carbon dioxide, comprising (a3) mixing a smectite or vermiculite clay mineral e.g. synthetic fluorohectorite or natural bentonite with an aqueous solution of cations that can form hydroxides that are attractive for carbon dioxide at a pH of greater than or equal to 7, (b3) drying the clay mineral prepared in the step (a3) to form a powder, contacting a first bed of the powder from the step (b3) with a gas stream comprising the gas mixture, the gas mixture preferably being supplied to the first bed under pressure, (c3) removing the first bed from the gas stream, (d3) redirecting the gas stream to contact a second bed of powder from the step (b3), and (e3) optionally heating the first bed to release carbon dioxide from it.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process (P4) further comprises redirecting the gas stream to contact the regenerated first bed whilst the second bed is regenerated. In the process (P1)-(P4), carbon dioxide is desorbed from the powder by heating or under reduced pressure, where the gas mixture comprises methane. Preferred Components: The clay mineral: comprises a nickel hydroxide containing species; and has a pfu of 0.2-0.5. The clay is montmorillonite. The water content of the clay is 1-6 water molecules per cation. The every other layer of the clay comprises a species comprising nickel(II), hydroxide and water. The alternate layers comprise nickel(II) ions. The aqueous solution which is contacted with the clay mineral preferably has at least a 10-fold excess of cations in solution relative to the cation exchange capacity of the clay mineral, i.e. 10-30 fold excess.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of formation of an ordered interstratification upon nickel-exchange, carbon dioxide capture by nickel hydroxide interstratified in the nanolayered space of a synthetic cay mineral, and carbon dioxide adsorption enhanced by tuning the layer charge in a clay mineral that do not support the claims.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN WO2022192153-A1
TI Making sugar from carbon dioxide, comprises            electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide to form            glycolaldehyde, where glycolaldehyde then operates as            autocatalyst to promote conversion of formaldehyde to            sugar in formose reaction.
AU YANG P
   CESTELLOS-BLANCO S
   LI Y
   ROSS M B
AE UNIV CALIFORNIA (REGC-C)
GA 2022B6731W
AB    NOVELTY - Method making a sugar, comprises                electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide to form                glycolaldehyde, where the glycolaldehyde then                operates as an autocatalyst to promote conversion                of formaldehyde to the sugar in a formose                reaction.
   USE - The method is used for making a sugar from                carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method easily scales up to produce sugars,                has optimized flow design that also helps to                increase the carbon efficiency by minimizing loss                of unreacted carbon dioxide, and minimizes                side-product by less than 4% of the overall                yield.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRICAL POWER AND ENERGY - preferred Method: The method is powered by                  solar energy.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises generating the formaldehyde from carbon                  dioxide through hydrogenation of carbon dioxide                  to methanol, and oxidizing the methanol yields                  formaldehyde. The electrochemical reduction                  employs a copper nanoparticle based                  electrocatalyst as a cathode in an                  electrochemical carbon dioxide reduction                  environment. The electrochemical reduction                  employs a platinum mesh as an anode to balance                  charge by the oxygen evolution reaction. The                  electrochemical reduction employs an                  electrochemical carbon dioxide reduction                  environment provided by potassium bicarbonate                  saturated with constantly flowing pure carbon                  dioxide. The method performed in an                  extraterrestrial environment. The method further                  comprises isolating the resultant sugar. The                  method further comprises formulating the                  resultant sugar in a feedstock configured for                  Escherichia colibacteria. Preferred Components:                  The sugar is selected from tetroses, pentoses,                  hexoses, heptoses and octoses.
   EXAMPLE - None given.
DC D17 (Sugar and starch industry (C07H, C13).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E13 (Heterocyclics.); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).)

TI Resource utilization method of capturing carbon            dioxide useful in preparing building material product,            shaping the cementitious composition and curing cement            composition comprises using organic amine solution to            capture carbon dioxide, and mixing carbonate and            tributyl phosphate uniformly.
AU WANG J
   LIU H
   XIN P
   CUI S
AE UNIV BEIJING TECHNOLOGY (UYBT-C)
GA 2022B8179R
AB    NOVELTY - Resource utilization method of capturing                carbon dioxide, comprises (i) using organic amine                solution to capture carbon dioxide to prepare                absorbent; (ii) mixing absorbent, carbonate and                tributyl phosphate uniformly, adding the                cement-based material to realize the utilization                and mineralization of carbon dioxide, where dosage                of the absorbent is calculated based on the organic                amine accounting for 0.05-2% of the mass of the                cementitious material, and the dosage of the                carbonate is 0.1-10% of the mass of the cement and                the content of the tributyl phosphate is 0.01-1% of                the cement quality.
   USE - The method is useful in preparing building                material product, shaping the cementitious                composition and curing the cement                composition.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively uses the absorption                carbon dioxide organic amine as the performance of                cement-based material for improving additive, the                carbon dioxide is mineralized and sealed. The                method avoids energy consumption caused by                conventional absorbent desorption process,                realizing zero discharge and negative discharge of                carbon dioxide in the process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                cement composition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic amines are                  monoethanolamine, diethanolamine,                  triethanolamine, 2-amino-2-methyl-1-propanol,                  piperazine, N-methyldiethanolamine,                  hydroxyethylethylenediamine, benzylamine,                  methylethanolamine, diethylenetriamine and/or                  triethylenetetramine. The carbonate is sodium                  carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, potassium                  carbonate and/or potassium bicarbonate. The                  cement base material is dry powder or slurry. The                  cement base material is cement slurry, cement                  mortar or cement concrete.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. PN IN202211038895-A
TI Method for capturing carbon dioxide from flue gas            emitting from point of source, involves converting            mechanical power obtained from turbo expander to            electrical energy through alternator or coupling with            gas compressor.
AU PRASAD B
AE INDIAN INST TECHNOLOGY ROORKEE (INTE-Non-standard)
GA 202293778A
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves feeding flue gas through                blower at constant pressure to subsequent units.                Moisture of the gas is cooled and partial removed                with the help of cooling water using a heat                exchanger. The gas is filtered in cartridge                filtration module to remove fine particles. The                compressed gas is isentropic expanded in a turbo                expander for obtaining mechanical power and                simultaneous refrigeration effect. Liquid carbon                dioxide and other gases are separated in a                separator based on density difference. The                mechanical power obtained from the expander is                converted to electrical energy through alternator                or coupling with a gas compressor. The amount of                carbon dioxide in the flue gas is captured by                99%.
   USE - Method for capturing carbon dioxide from flue                gas emitting from point of source, where carbon                dioxide is carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide,                dimethyl ether and methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to separate carbon dioxide                in liquid phase from flue gases emanating from the                installations using fossil fuels for thermal and                electrical energy generation.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view                illustrating the process for capturing carbon                dioxide from flue gas emitting from point of                source.

TI Manufacturing carbon-dioxide-gas-containing            water-containing gel articles by adjusting carbon            dioxide gas concentration of water-containing gel            containing water-soluble polymer and crosslinking agent            and crosslinking water-containing gel.
AU KIMURA S
AE KAO CORP (KAOS-C)
   KAO CORP (KAOS-C)
GA 2021B6725R
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon-dioxide-gas-containing water-containing                gel articles are manufactured by (1) preparing                articles which have water-containing gel containing                (A) water-soluble polymer, crosslinking agent of                water-soluble polymer (B), and water (C) and having                loss tangent at 1 Hz in a dynamic-viscoelasticity                measurement of greater than or equal to 0.46; (2)                adjusting composition of ambient atmosphere so that                concentration of carbon dioxide gas in ambient                atmosphere of articles is greater than or equal to                10 vol.% and sealing in a package the articles                which have the water-containing gel; and (3)                allowing crosslinking of water-containing gel until                loss tangent at 1 Hz in the dynamic-viscoelasticity                measurement of water-containing gel in articles                which have the water-containing gel becomes less                than or equal to 0.46, to obtain                carbon-dioxide-gas-containing water-containing gel                articles sealed in the package.
   USE - Manufacture of carbon-dioxide-gas-containing                water-containing gel articles.
   ADVANTAGE - The retainable high water-containing gel                articles of carbon dioxide gas in a                water-containing gel can be manufactured                productively. Carbon dioxide gas is contained in                high concentration in the water-containing                gel.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Crosslinking agent                  contains aluminum ion supply crosslinking agent.                  Amount of crosslinking agent is 0.01-10 mass% in                  the water-containing gel.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Condition: Step (2) is performed                  under normal temperature and atmospheric                  pressure. Preferred Component: Water-soluble                  polymer contains anionic polymer. Preferred                  Processes: Step (1) involves applying prepared                  water-containing gel to a support body, and                  forming water-containing gel articles which have                  water-containing gel on the support body.

AB    NOVELTY - Recovery of waste-containing polyurethane                material (18) involves electrolyzing (5) of                purified carbon dioxide (4a), preferably by                performing electrochemical conversion of the carbon                dioxide (4a) on a gas diffusion electrode, while                obtaining a mixture (23) of at least carbon                monoxide, unconverted carbon dioxide and optionally                hydrogen, separating (6) of the unconverted carbon                dioxide (6a) from the mixture (23) to obtain a                mixture (6b) of carbon monoxide and optionally                hydrogen, and recycling of the unconverted carbon                dioxide (6a) into the electrolysis (5) process,                optionally separating (7) of hydrogen (7a) of                carbon monoxide and optionally hydrogen, reacting                carbon monoxide (7b) or (6b) obtained from step (g)                or step (f) with chlorine (9a) to form phosgene in                a process (10) for the production of isocyanate                (10a), and optionally reacting (16) isocyanate                (10a) with polyether (15a) and optionally                additionally with polyester to obtain a finished                polyurethane material (16a).
   USE - Recovery of waste-containing polyurethane                material used for producing material for producing                isocyanate and polyurethane. compounds, (b) optionally refining (19) of the                mixture (1c) obtained in step (a) of low-molecular                weight hydrocarbons to obtain a mixture (19a) of                gaseous and liquid hydrocarbons and a mixture (19b)                of carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide, hydrogen and                other gaseous compound as hydrocarbon compounds,                and separation of the resulting mixtures (19b) in a                gas separation (30), (c) incinerating (2) the                residue (1b) and, polyurethane material waste, with                oxygen-containing gas (3a), preferably with pure                oxygen, to obtain carbon dioxide-containing gas                (2a), (d) purifying (4) of the carbon dioxide (1a)                and (2a) and optionally from step (a) of secondary                components, preferably nitrogen oxide, sulfur                compounds, dust, water, oxygen and hydrochloric                acid, optionally by using adsorption, gas scrubbing                or catalytic treatment to obtain a purified carbon                dioxide (4a), (e) electrolyzing of the purified                carbon dioxide obtained in step (d), preferably by                performing electrochemical conversion of the carbon                dioxide (4a) on a gas diffusion electrode, while                obtaining a mixture of at least carbon monoxide,                unconverted carbon dioxide and optionally hydrogen,                (f) separating of the unconverted carbon dioxide                (6a) from the (g) optional separating                of hydrogen obtained from the mixture obtained in                step (f) of carbon monoxide and optionally                hydrogen, (h) reacting carbon monoxide PN CN114940500-A
TI Preparing fly ash-based sodium phosphide type            molecular sieve used in carbon dioxide adsorption,            comprises mixing fly ash and acid liquid, mixing acid            washing fly ash, sodium hydroxide and water, microwave            calcining, and mixing activated fly ash.
AU ZHAO Y
   TIAN L
   XIAO X
   LIU Y
   YU J
   ZHOU T
   DOU J
AE UNIV LIAONING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (ULST-C)
GA 2022B36065
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing fly ash-based sodium phosphide type                molecular sieve, comprises (1) mixing fly ash and                acid liquid and drying to obtain acid washing fly                ash; (2) mixing acid washing fly ash, sodium                hydroxide and water, and microwave calcining to                obtain activated fly ash; and (3) mixing activated                fly ash with water, and performing ultrasonic                treatment, crystallizing and drying in sequence to                obtain fly ash-based sodium phosphide type                molecular sieve.
   USE - The molecular sieve is useful in carbon                dioxide adsorption (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple technique and low                requirement, saves a lot of industrial raw                material, improves the utilization rate of the fly                ash, promotes the high added value utilization of                the fly ash, and reduces the pollution of the fly                ash to the environment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (1), the                  acid liquid is hydrochloric acid solution or                  nitric acid solution, and the concentration of                  the acid liquid is 1-3 mol/l. The mass ratio of                  the fly ash to the acid liquid is 1:2-6. In the                  step (2), the mass ratio of acid washing fly ash                  and sodium hydroxide is 1:0.5-2. In the step (3),                  the mass-volume ratio of activated fly ash and                  water is 1:2-4 (g:ml).Preferred Method: The mixing speed in step                  (1) is 50-100 revolutions/minute, the stirring                  time is 24-26 hours, and the stirring temperature                  is 20-30&#8451;. The drying temperature in                  step (1) is 100-110&#8451;, and the time is                  12-13 hours. In the step (2), the concentration                  of sodium hydroxide in the mixture obtained by                  mixing the acid washing fly ash, sodium hydroxide                  and water is 1.25-3.75 mol/l. In the step (2),                  the temperature of microwave calcination is                  700-800&#8451;, the time is 1-2 hours, and                  the frequency is 30-35 kHz. In the step (3), the                  ultrasonic frequency is 30-35 kHz, the                  temperature is 50-60&#8451;, the time is 1-4                  hours, the crystallization temperature is                  100-110&#8451;, and the time is 12-48 hours.                  In the step (3), the drying temperature is                  105-115&#8451;, the vacuum degree is -40 to                  -80 KPa, and the time is 12-13 hours.

TI Cultivating Flammulina velutipes involves            preparing culture medium, sterilizing, inoculating and            culturing mycelium, scratching, inducing bud,            inhibiting treatment, culturing and harvesting in            growth period.
AU YU Y
   LIU Y
   FENG Z
   HU W
   GE N
   LI H
   QIU Y
AE JIANGSU CHINA GREEN BIOTECH CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202268207G
AB    NOVELTY - Cultivating Flammulina velutipes involves                preparing culture medium, sterilizing, inoculating                and culturing mycelium, scratching, inducing bud,                inhibiting treatment, culturing and harvesting in                growth period. The carbon dioxide concentration in                growth chamber is increased to 6000-15000 parts per                million (ppm) to control and reduce amount of                mycelium recovery and number of mushroom buds, and                reduced number of mushroom buds, to culture bottle                after the scratching bacteria treatment, on 0-4th                day. During the 5-6th day, adjusted carbon dioxide                concentration in the growth chamber to 2000-4000                ppm to promote the germination and formation of                mushroom buds.
   USE - Method for cultivating Flammulina                velutipes
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to reduce germination                number of needle mushroom cultivation by adjusting                the mushroom growth chamber fruiting early                environment, improves mushroom roughness and                property of production efficiency and production,                and have stable and high efficiency.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Process: The culture bottle after                  bud forcing is finished is subjected to light                  inhibition and wind inhibition treatment. The                  temperature is reduced to 4-5degrees Celsius in a                  gradient manner, mushroom buds grow into mushroom                  buds, when the mushroom buds grow out of 1-2.5 cm                  of a bottle opening, the concentration of the                  carbon dioxide is increased to be more than 10000                  ppm, and the elongation of needle mushroom stems                  is promoted. The gradient cooling is carried out                  at cooling rate of 2-3degrees Celsius per                  day.

PN CN114314656-A
TI Preparing magnesia-stabilized zirconia powder involves mixing the magnesium metal powder and the zirconia powder to obtain a mixed powder, inducing to undergo a magnesium thermal reduction reaction in a carbon dioxide atmosphere to obtain a composite powder.
AU LI J
   SHUANG S
   LI Y
   YANG X
   YANG Z
   LU N
   HE G
AE TECH INST PHYSICS & CHEM CHINESE ACAD (CAPC-C)
GA 202256710V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing magnesia-stabilized zirconia powder involves mixing the magnesium metal powder and the zirconia powder in a ratio of 1:1-1:12 to obtain a mixed powder. The mixed powder is induced to undergo a magnesium thermal reduction reaction in a carbon dioxide atmosphere to obtain a composite powder. The obtained composite powder is placed in a dilute acid solution for washing, suction filtration and calcination to obtain magnesium oxide-stabilized zirconia powder.
   USE - Method for preparing magnesia-stabilized zirconia powder
   ADVANTAGE - The magnesium oxide stable zirconium oxide powder prepared by the method has high repeatability, good high temperature stability, and has no obvious phase change at 1300degrees Celsius. The method uses self-propagating combustion synthesis technology, low energy consumption, short preparation period, low production cost and simple technique process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The average particle size of the magnesium metal powder is 1-100 micrometer, and the average particle size of the zirconia powder is 0.05-100 micrometer. The dilute acid solution is the dilute hydrochloric acid or dilute sulfuric acid with concentration of 0.1-2 molar. The magnesium/zirconium (Mg/Zr) ratio in the magnesia-stabilized zirconia powder is 0.02-0.5. The magnesium thermal reduction reaction is carried out in a gas pressure assisted combustion synthesis device.

AE ANHUI JINHE IND CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 202238818G
AB    NOVELTY - A recovering method of AK sugar from AK sugar                crystallization waste liquid comprises dropping                sulfuric acid or hydrochloric acid, acidifying at                10-80degrees Celsius for 10-600 minutes,                controlling pH at 0-7, releasing carbon dioxide                gas, filtering to remove precipitated                macromolecular substances and salts, and collecting                clear acidified solution; and repeatedly extracting                acidified solution with organic solvents to obtain                AK sugar precursors, and sending to hydrolyzed                liquid extraction section to carry out                neutralization to obtain AK sugars with purity of                85-95%.
   USE - The method is for recovering AK sugar from AK                sugar crystallization waste liquid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces chemical oxygen demand of                crystallization waste liquid after treatment from                the original 200000 ppm to below 20000 ppm,                improves the yield of the AK sugar, and has reduced                cost of post-treatment of wastewater.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The crystallization                  waste liquid contains 1-20 wt.% potassium                  carbonate, 0.1-5 wt.% potassium sulfamate, 0.1-5                  wt.% potassium sulfate, 1-10 wt.% potassium                  hydroxide, and 0.1-5 wt.% acesulfame.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic solvent                  comprises dichloromethane, trichloromethane,                  dichloroethane, trichloroethane, ethyl acetate,                  isopropyl acetate and/or butyl acetate.Preferred Method: The neutralization step is                  done by neutralizing AK sugar precursor with                  alkaline substance comprising potassium hydroxide                  or potassium carbonate at molar ratio of                  0.5-1.2:1 at 0-20degrees Celsius for 1-120                  minutes.

PN CN114032622-A
TI Preparing ginger oil regenerated cellulose fiber used in textile, involves using supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process to prepare ginger essential oil, mixing ginger essential oil with sodium starch octenyl succinate.
AU XU L
   GONG H
   ZHANG L
AE SHANGHAI LUOLAI LIFE TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   LUOLAI LIFE SCI & TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (LUOL-Non-standard)
GA 2022293193
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ginger oil regenerated cellulose fiber involves using supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process to prepare ginger essential oil, mixing ginger essential oil with sodium starch octenyl succinate and deionized water, stirring, emulsifying homogeneously to obtain ginger oil emulsion, adding sodium alginate, chitosan and calcium chloride to the ginger oil emulsion to obtain ginger oil microcapsule, adding the obtained ginger oil microcapsule to the cellulose spinning solution, and spinning to obtain ginger oil regenerated cellulose fiber.
   USE - Method for preparing ginger oil regenerated cellulose fiber used in textile (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared ginger oil regenerated cellulose fiber has excellent antibacterial property, and can be applied to textile to obtain antibacterial textile with stable antibacterial performance.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of the method for preparing ginger oil regenerated cellulose fiber . (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The preparation of ginger essential oil involves using supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) extraction method to placing ginger powder in the extraction kettle, turning the carbon dioxide on to make the ginger powder and carbon dioxide fully contact for 18 hours, extracting ginger powder at a temperature of 30-50degrees Celsius for 2-4 hours to obtain ginger oleoresin after separation, where the mesh number of the ginger powder is 50-200 meshes before extraction and performing molecular distillation on the ginger oleoresin to obtain ginger essential oil. The method for homogeneous emulsification involves transferring the uniformly stirred mixture of the ginger essential oil, sodium starch octenyl succinate and deionized water into a homogenizer, emulsifying homogeneously at a rotational speed of 10,000-20,000 revolutions per minute for 5-15 minutes. The method for preparing ginger oil microcapsules involves adding 4-10 wt.% sodium alginate to the ginger oil emulsion, stirring uniformly at 40-60degrees Celsius, adding 2-3 wt.% chitosan and 0.5-2 wt.% calcium chloride to the ginger oil emulsion, stirring uniformly, filtering and drying to obtain ginger oil microcapsule. Preferred Compositions: The added amount of the ginger essential oil is 10-15 wt.% of based on the quality of the ginger oil emulsion. The added amount of the sodium starch octenyl succinate is 8-10 wt.% based on the quality of the ginger oil emulsion. The added amount of the ginger oil microcapsule is 5-10 wt.% based on the mass of the cellulose spinning solution.

   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of high-conductivity graphene composite aluminum alloy with high strength and high conductivity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The weight ratio of the graphene and the atomized aluminum powder in step (S11) is (1-3):(97-99). The weight ratio of the dry mixed powder to the molten aluminum in step (S14) is (90-110): (90-110). The weight ratio of the milling ball to the material in step (S11) is (10-15):1, the rotating speed of the ball mill is 30-50 rpm, and the ball milling is 8-15 hours when the ball mill is used for ball milling. The temperature of the vacuum drying in step (S12) is 80-90 degrees C, and the time is 1-2 hours. The volume ratio of nitrogen and carbon dioxide in the mixed gas in step (S13) is 1:(1-5), and the flow rate of the mixed gas is (35-45) m3/hour. The volume ratio of nitrogen and carbon dioxide in step (S13) is 1:(2-4), and the flow rate of the mixed gas is (35-45) m3/hour in the mixed gas. The ventilation is continued for 20-40 minutes in step (S15). Preferred Process: The method for determining the weight ratio of the grinding ball to the material, involves (S21) providing the weight ratio of the grinding ball to the material is in the range (a, b), the rotation speed of the ball mill is c, and the ball milling time is d in the ball milling step, (S22) fixing between a and b from small to large, taking three values (x1, x2, x3), preparing three mixed powder samples, and determining the percentage of the particle size of the largest portion of the particle size distribution (S23) taking (x4) as a new sample, (S24) taking the two values close to (x1, x2, and x3) as the end values of the preferred weight ratio range of the grinding ball to the material, (S25) repeating (S22-S24) until the difference between the two end values of the weight ratio range of the grinding ball to the material and the smaller end value is less than 5%, and the middle value of the end value is the final grinding ball and the material weight ratio where a greater than or equal to 3, b less than or equal to 20, c is 10-60 rpm, d is 5-20 hours, and the range of particle size distribution is not less than 3 um, 3-5 um, 5-10 um, 10-20 um, not greater than 20 um in particle size statistics. The preparation of checking the uniformity of the powder after drying, involves (S21) taking a sample each of the mixed powder with a fixed drying time and the mixed powder with a drying time of 15-25% at the same drying temperature, a not greater than 10, testing the fluidity of the powder, and marking them as vectors X1 and X2, (S22) using the specific formula to calculate the stability of the vector including the stability of the vector X1 is the stability of the vector X2, (S24) determining the fixed drying time as the drying time when it is less than 3.02, and extending the drying time by 15-25% as the new drying time when it is greater than 3.02, and repeating (S21-S22) until is less than 3.02.

PT P
PN CN112958606-A; CN112958606-B
TI Remediation of organic contaminated soil includes            crushing organic contaminated soil, adding carbon            dioxide and methanol, extracting, mixing soil with            ferrous salt solution, performing solid-liquid            separation, washing soil and screening.
AU ZHANG Q
AE CHINA ENVIRONMENT STATE INVESTMENT CHONGQING              ENVIRONMENTAL (CHEN-Non-standard)
   CHINA ENVIRONMENT STATE INVESTMENT CHONG (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 202171187A
AB    NOVELTY - Remediation of organic contaminated soil                comprises crushing organic contaminated soil,                sieving, adding carbon dioxide and methanol to                supercritical extraction tank, raising temperature                and pressure to supercritical state, extracting for                30-60 minutes to obtain soil after one treatment,                mixing soil after primary treatment with ferrous                salt solution at solid-liquid ratio of 1:50-100,                adjusting pH of reaction system to 6-8, reacting                under anaerobic condition, performing solid-liquid                separation to obtain soil after secondary                treatment, washing soil, filtering with acetic                acid, washing with water for 1-3 times to obtain                washed soil, adding stabilizer, adsorbent and                curing agent to soil, mixing, curing for 2-5 days                and screening adsorbent.
   USE - The method is for remediation of organic                contaminated soil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high repairing efficiency and                short processing time and removes each pollution                source.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ferrous salt                  solution is ferrous sulfate solution. The                  concentration in reaction system is 10-30 mmol/L.                  The pH of reaction system is adjusted with                  potassium hydroxide solution. The stabilizer is                  calcium superphosphate. The adsorbent is corncob                  biochar doped with iron element. The curing agent                  is plant ash. The weight ratio of superphosphate,                  corncob biochar doped with iron, plant ash and                  washed soil is 2-4:1-3:4-6:100. The iron salt                  comprises trivalent iron and divalent iron at                  molar ratio of 1:1-2. The concentration of iron                  salt in reaction system is 2-4 mol/L. The                  concentration of manganese salt or aluminum salt                  in reaction system is 0.05-0.1 mol/l. The inert                  gas is nitrogen.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The liquid-solid ratio                  of methanol to organic contaminated soil is                  0.03-0.05:1.
DC P43 (Sorting, cleaning, waste disposal (B06, B07, B08, B09).)

PN CN112830915-A; CN112830915-B
TI Preparing 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid involves continuously introducing reaction gas into reactor, and subjecting mixture containing 5-hydroxymethylfurfural or its derivative and organic acid in reactor to catalytic oxidation reaction.
AU MA Z
   ZHANG Y
   CHEN J
AE NINGBO INST MATERIALS TECHNOLOGY & ENG C (CANM-C)
GA 202160515J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid involves continuously introducing reaction gas into a reactor, and subjecting the mixture in the reactor to catalytic oxidation reaction at 150 degrees C or less. The mixture contains a raw material, an organic acid, a halogen, and a metal catalyst. The raw material is 5-hydroxymethylfurfural or its derivative. The reaction gas contains carbon dioxide and oxygen in a molar ratio of not less than 0.1:1.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method controls the ratio of carbon dioxide and oxygen in the reaction gas, controls the reaction conditions at 150 degrees C, effectively inhibits the combustion of solvent and the occurrence of side reactions during oxidation, ensures safety of reaction, and improves selectivity and yield of 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid of more than 91.8%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal catalyst comprises 0.4-10 %mass, preferably 7-8 %mass cobalt, manganese, iron, zirconium, cerium, selenium, copper, vanadium, ruthenium or nickel compounds, preferably cobalt and/or cerium. When cobalt or cerium element is included, the cobalt or cerium element is 40-100% of the total mass of the elements in the catalyst. When cobalt and cerium elements are included, the sum of cobalt and cerium elements is 40-100% of the total mass of elements in the catalyst. The metal catalyst is composed of cobalt compound and cerium compound. The halogen includes chlorine, bromine, fluorine or iodine. The halogen is present in the mixture in the form of hydrogen halide, sodium halide, ammonium halide or potassium halide. The mixture further contains 0.8-10 %mass, preferably 0.8-5 %mass water. Preferred Method: The reaction gas is formed by mixing carbon dioxide with oxygen, air and/or enriched oxygen. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of cobalt and cerium is 1:(0.5-1). The molar ratio of metal element to halogen is (0.8-12):1, preferably (10-12):1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The catalytic oxidation reaction is carried out at 100-145 degrees C, preferably 120-145 degrees C and 1-10 MPa. Preferred Components: The 5-hydroxymethylfurfural derivatives are 5-hydroxymethylfurfural ether derivatives and/or 5-hydroxymethylfurfural ester derivatives. The usage amount of the raw material is 10-30% of the mass of the organic acid. The organic acid is a monocarboxylic acid, preferably acetic acid, propionic acid, butyric acid or valeric acid.
