PN CN112374518-A
TI Preparing nano calcium carbonate in preparation of drug-carrying system, involves dissolving calcium chloride dihydrate in absolute ethanol to obtain an ethanol solution of calcium chloride, placing the ethanol solution of calcium chloride.
AU BAI B
   FENG X
   GU Z
   GUO Y
   YU H
   XIE H
AE UNIV NANTONG (UYNT-C)
GA 202120289J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nano calcium carbonate involves dissolving calcium chloride dihydrate in absolute ethanol to obtain an ethanol solution of calcium chloride, placing the ethanol solution of calcium chloride and ammonium bicarbonate into different containers, and placing in a vacuum drying oven together, adjusting the temperature of the vacuum drying oven to decompose ammonium bicarbonate into carbon dioxide and ammonia gas by heating. The carbon dioxide is dissolved in the ethanol solution of calcium chloride and reacts with calcium divalent ion to form calcium carbonate, centrifuged the reaction solution and cleaned the precipitate by centrifugation to obtain product.
   USE - Method for preparing nano calcium carbonate used in the preparation of a drug-carrying system (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is suitable for industrial scale production, has better dispersibility, uniform shape and large medical application prospect, in simple manner.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The method involves dissolving 150 mg calcium chloride dihydrate in 100 mL absolute ethanol. The amount of ammonium bicarbonate used is 10 g. The temperature of the vacuum drying oven is adjusted to 40 degrees C. The time for collecting the reaction solution is 24 hours.

AU ZHANG B
   TAN J
AE HUNAN LVMAN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 2020C13790
AB    NOVELTY - Method for processing isolated Momordica                grosvenori involves alternately processing isolated                immature M.grosvenori in a first environment and a                second environment, where the first environment has                conditions of high red light illumination, high                temperature and high concentration of carbon                dioxide, the first environment has total light                illuminance of 1000-10000 lux, temperature of 30-35                degrees C, and carbon dioxide concentration of                600-2000 ppm, and the second environment has low                red light illumination and low temperature                conditions, and total light illuminance of 150-1000                lux, and temperature of 1-15 degrees C.
   USE - The method is useful for processing isolated                M.grosvenori (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively reduces content of                mogroside IIe in M.grosvenori, increases content of                mogroside V, promotes maturation of M.grosvenori,                and shortens maturation and saccharification time                of mogroside.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The first environment and                  second environment further include blue light.                  The illuminance ratio of red light and blue light                  in the first environment is (4-9):1 and the                  illuminance ratio of red light and blue light in                  the second environment is (1:4)-(3:7). The first                  environment further comprises purple light, and                  the purple light intensity is 1-4% of the total                  illuminance. The wavelengths of red light, blue                  light, and violet light in the first environment                  are 600-680 nm, 430-510 nm and 305-420 nm                  respectively, and the wavelengths of red light                  and blue light in the second environment are                  600-680 nm and 430-510 nm, respectively. The red                  light, blue light, and purple light sources in                  the first environment are red light, blue light                  and purple light respectively, and the red light                  and blue light sources in the second environment                  are red light and blue light respectively. The                  single processing time of the immature                  M.grosvenori in the first environment is 5-7                  hours, and the single processing time in the                  second environment is 5-7 hours. The immature                  M.grosvenori meets at least one of the conditions                  such as (i) the shell is turquoise, (ii) the                  shell is hard and not soft when pressed, (iii)                  there is a strong green smell, (iv) bitter taste                  and (v) the juice brix in the pulp is 4 Brix or                  less. The method involves alternately processing                  immature M.grosvenori in the first environment                  and the second environment for 6-10 times.

The product is melt-spun by                conventional methods to obtain polyester fiber                grafted with aerogel. Preferred Conditions: The essential oil                  emulsion refers to the essential oil, Tween (RTM:                  Non-ionic surfactant)-80, soybean lecithin,                  deionized water is 1:1:10-3:50 mass ratio mixed                  and obtained by ultrasound. The aerogel is                  connected in series on the polymer molecular                  chain and acts as a carrier of essential oils to                  release fragrance slowly.

The heating rate in step (3) is 2-3 degrees                  C/minute.

PT P
PN CN111068096-A
TI Preparing a dECM-ketoreductase based wound repairing dressing involves preparing decellularized extracellular matrix dECM by decellularizing animal-derived cell by supercritical carbon dioxide technology, and washing it with PBS.
AU CHEN Z
   CHEN A
   WANG S
   WANG R
   FU C
   YANG L
AE UNIV HUAQIAO (UYHQ-C)
GA 202038138T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a dECM-ketoreductase based wound repairing dressing involves preparing decellularized extracellular matrix dECM by decellularizing animal-derived cell by supercritical carbon dioxide technology, washing it with PBS, DNA enzyme, PBS and freeze-drying it to obtain decellularized extracellular matrix dECM. The NO donor-modified dECM-based dressing is prepared by subjecting decellularized extracellular matrix dECM to thiol-modification and grafting it with a NO donor to obtain a NO donor chemically graft-modified dECM, which is prepared by using genipin cross-linked dECM dressing in freeze-dried gel or solid form.
   USE - Method for preparing a dECM-ketoreductase based wound repairing dressing.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare a dECM-ketoreductase based wound repairing dressing, which is easy to degrade and absorb, has simple preparation, good biocompatibility, promotes wound healing, and provides idea for subsequent application of dECM-based biological dressing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The animal-derived cell comprises pig-derived, bovine-derived and horse-derived skin, fat, and liver tissue cell. The decellularized extracellular matrix dECM is prepared by adding lyophilized product of animal-derived cell to reactor of RESS device with solvent under condition of 10-45 degrees C and 71-300 bar through supercritical carbon dioxide and solvent. The decellularize animal-derived cell is lysed for 0.5-9 hours, treated sample is rinsed for 12 hours in PBS buffer to remove solvent, immersed in PBS buffer containing 8mM magnesium chloride in 20U/mL DNAse at 10-45 degrees C and speed of 120-150 revolutions per minute, shaken at constant temperature for 24 hours, nucleus is removed from tissue, DNase-treated sample is washed with PBS buffer, and distilled water in sequence, and freeze-dried to obtain decellularized extracellular matrix dECM. The dosage ratio of lyophilized product of animal-derived cell to solvent is 3-5g:50mL. The thiol modification is performed to dissolve decellularized extracellular matrix dECM in a 0.5M acetic acid solution containing pepsin, and shakes at a constant temperature of 10-45 degrees C and 120-150 revolutions per minute for 2 days, and when it is completely dissolved then pH value is adjusted to 7.2-7.4 with 10M sodium hydroxide solution, dialyzed for 2 days and lyophilized. The lyophilized dECM and Traut's reagent are added to 100mM potassium phosphate buffer containing 0.5mM ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid and incubated at room temperature for 1 hour to obtain terminal-SH grafted dECM. The mass ratio of decellularized extracellular matrix dECM and pepsin is 100:10, and mass ratio of lyophilized dECM to Traut's reagent is 500-600:15-18. The NO donor chemical coupling modification is performed by incubating dECM grafted with SH with 15mM isoamyl nitrite at room temperature for 2 hours, and dialyzed for 2 days and then freeze-dried to obtain the NO donor chemical graft modified dECM. The dressing is shaped by dissolving dECM and then crosslinking it into a lyophilized gel form and a solid dressing form prepared by chemical coupling grafting of undissolved solid dECM. The method involves dissolving decellularized extracellular matrix dECM and NO donor chemical graft modified dECM with distilled water for dressing shaping, cross-linking it with genipin, shaping and freeze drying to obtain NO donor chemical graft modified DECM-based dressing. Preferred Components: The NO donor comprises isoamyl nitrite. The solvent comprises ethanol, dichloromethane or hexafluoroisopropanol.
   ACTIVITY - Vulnerary.
DC D22 (Sterilising, bandages, dressing and skin protection agents - including sterilising agents (other than for food), sutures, plaster casts, bioactive prostheses, contact lenses, diapers, animal litter, timber, preservatives, disinfectants, bactericidal detergents, deodorants, insect repellent compounds, moth proofers, sheep dip (A61L).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); P34 (Sterilising, syringes, electrotherapy (A61L, M, N).)

AU YANG K
   XIE S
   ZHAO X
   ZOU X
   HAN Y
   JIN J
AE CHINA TOBACCO GUIZHOU IND CO LTD (CNTA-C)
GA 202031662F
AB    NOVELTY - Production of mulberry fruit additive comprises extracting mulberry fruit juice by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction and spray drying to obtain mulberry fruit powder.
   USE - The method is for production of mulberry fruit additive used for adding in tobacco product (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method greatly reduces the content of starch and pectin in the mulberry fruit additive, thus, greatly reducing the influence of starch and pectin on cigarette smoke, reduces the requirements for supercritical equipment and supercritical solvent, and is not affected by the seasonality and periodicity of agricultural products. Because the mulberry fruit is rich in sugar and protein, the Maillard reaction can occur during the spray drying process, which makes the prepared mulberry fruit additive have a special fragrance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for mulberry fruit additive.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The mulberry fruit juice is prepared by crushing the mulberry fruit, adding water, stirring, and filtering to obtain the mulberry fruit juice. It is prepared by sending the mulberry fruit into juice extractor, extracting juice, and filtering to obtain mulberry fruit juice. The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is conducted at 30-55 degrees C and 8-35 MPa for 0.1-1 hour. The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction comprises adding entrainer selected from water, ethanol, propanediol and/or propanetriol. The amount of water in the entrainer is not greater than 40%. The spray drying inlet temperature is 140-250 degrees C.

TI Preparing amino modified cellulose aerogel            involves dissolving cellulose in a sodium            hydroxide-urea-water solvent system to obtain a clear            and transparent cellulose solution, where solution is            formed into a cellulose gel.
AU KONG Y
   ZHANG J
   SHEN X
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
GA 201970674C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing amino modified cellulose aerogel                involves dissolving cellulose in a sodium                hydroxide-urea-water solvent system at minus 20 to                minus 10 degrees C to obtain a clear and                transparent cellulose solution, and the cellulose                solution is formed into a cellulose gel by a phase                inversion method. The cellulose gel obtained is                washed with an aqueous solution of acetic acid, and                then immersed in a carbon dioxide gas-treated amino                modifier at 0 to 60 degrees C for amino                modification. The amino-modified cellulose gel                obtained is subjected to solvent replacement and                drying to obtain an amino-modified cellulose                aerogel.
   USE - Method for preparing amino modified cellulose                aerogel used for high-efficiency selective                adsorption of formaldehyde and carbon dioxide                gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare amino modified                cellulose aerogel in a simple manner.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The mass ratio of                  sodium hydroxide:urea:water in the sodium                  hydroxide-urea-water solvent system is 1:1.5 to                  2.5:10 o 15. The aqueous acetic acid solution has                  a volume concentration of 1 to 10%. The carbon                  dioxide gas has a volume concentration of 10% to                  100% and the gas flow rate is 0.1 to 1 l/min; and                  the treatment time is 10 to 60 minutes. Preferred                  Process: The phase inversion method is placing a                  mold containing the cellulose solution in a                  vessel containing a coagulation bath, and the                  coagulation bath is volatilized and diffused to                  phase change gel of cellulose solution in                  cellulose solution. The solvent replacement is                  carried out by using water, ethanol or n-hexane                  as a solvent. Preferred Components: The solvent                  in the coagulation bath is water or ethanol. The                  amino modifier is 3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane,                  3-aminopropyltrimethoxysilane, polyethylene imine                  or tetraethylenepentamine solution. The drying is                  vacuum drying, freeze drying or supercritical                  drying.

TI Treatment of wastewater involves mixing wastewater with preset amount of chlorine solution and alkaline reagent, and subjecting to single step oxidation to convert cyanide in wastewater to carbon dioxide and nitrogen.
AU GOMEZ LEIVA P
   MENENDEZ DELMIRO V
   PADILLA VIVAS B
   GOMEZ L P
   MENENDEZ D V
   PADILLA V B
   LEIVA P G
   DELMIRO V M
   VIVAS B P
AE ARCELORMITTAL (ARCM-C)
   ARCELORMITTAL (ARCM-C)
   ARCELORMITTAL INVESTIGACION Y DESARROLLO (ARCE-C)
   ARCELORMITTAL (ARCM-C)
   ARCELORMITTAL (ARCM-C)
GA 201951834A
AB    NOVELTY - Treatment of wastewater involves subjecting wastewater to single oxidation step in which cyanides are converted into carbon dioxide and nitrogen. The oxidation step involves mixing wastewater with chlorine solution and alkaline agent to obtain mixture (4). The alkaline agent is added in an amount to maintain a pH of 8.8-9.5 and the chlorine solution is added in an amount so as to maintain the oxidation reduction potential between 150-450 mV.
   USE - Treatment of wastewater derived from blast furnace gas cleaning.
   ADVANTAGE - The process efficiently transforms toxic compounds to nontoxic compounds. The process reduces the sludge generation which can be either recycled or landfilled.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a device used for the treatment.
   Device (1)
   Tank (2)
   Mixer (3)
   Mixture (4)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The wastewater initially contains 1.5-15 ppm cyanides, 0.8-3 ppm zinc, 8 ppm iron and 0.05-0.5 lead. Chlorine solution is a hypochlorite solution. The alkaline agent is lime.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E37 (Mixtures of many components; inorganic reactions and processes of general applicability.)

PN RU2691341-C1
TI METHOD OF PURIFYING NATURAL GAS FROM IMPURITIES.
AU MNUSHKIN I A
AE MNUSHKIN I A (MNUS-Individual)
GA 201951926L
AB    NOVELTY - Method of purifying natural gas from impurities relates to gas processing and can be used at gas industry enterprises, in particular, in gas preparation for liquefaction. Invention includes a step of absorption extraction from natural gas of carbon dioxide and methanol with aqueous solution of amine with subsequent regeneration of the latter to obtain regenerated absorbent and acid gas, part of which after condensation in form of acidic water is returned to regenerator, and a step for adsorption drying of purified natural gas with adsorbent regeneration and regeneration gas generation, wherein at the stage of absorption extraction from natural gas of carbon dioxide and methanol to the absorber and regenerator, water which is stripped in an additional rectification column is fed.
   USE - Oil and gas industry.
   ADVANTAGE - Method of purifying natural gas from impurities enables to stabilize complex preparation of natural gas with removal of undesirable impurities from it, providing variability of the functioning of production with simultaneous production of methanol as a commercial product.
11 cl, 2 dwg, 2 tbl

General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

TI Manufacture of polyamide for forming product for            e.g. vehicle component, involves performing anionic            ring-opening polymerization reaction of mixture            comprising lactam, alkali metal, molecular weight            modifier, and carbon dioxide as activator.

   LEE J S

AE HANWHA CHEM CORP (HANW-C)
   HANWHA CHEM CORP (HANW-C)
   HANWHA CHEM CORP (HANW-C)
   HANWHA CHEM CORP (HANW-C)
   HANWHA SOLUTIONS CORP (HANW-C)
GA 201943536M
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of polyamide involves performing                anionic ring-opening polymerization reaction of a                mixture comprising 100 pts. wt. lactam, 0.01-20                pts. wt. alkali metal as an initiator, 0.3-10 pts.                wt. molecular weight modifier, and 0.002-1.0 pts.                wt. carbon dioxide as an activator.
   USE - Manufacture of polyamide used for forming                product for e.g. vehicle component, electronic                device component, industrial pipe material,                architectural civil engineering material,                three-dimensional printer material, fiber material,                clad material, machine tool material, medical                material, solar cell material, battery material,                sports material, domestic electrical material,                domestic material, and cosmetic material (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables environmentally-friendly                manufacture of polyamide in short time, without                using solvent and catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The alkali metal                  comprises a metal hydride, a metal hydroxide,                  and/or a metal alkoxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The lactam comprises                  caprolactam, laurolactam, pyrrolidone, and/or                  piperidone. The lactam in the polymerization                  reaction has a conversion rate of 95% or more to                  polyamide. The usage amount of lactam is 50 pts.                  wt. or more. The activator is benzoyl chloride,                  N-acetyl caprolactam, N-acetyl laurolactam,                  octadecyl isocyanate, toluene diisocyanate,                  and/or hexamethylene diisocyanate. The molecular                  weight modifier is ethylene-bis(stearamide),                  amine compound, urea and/or diurea.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The polymerization                  reaction is performed at a temperature of 180-300                  degrees C for 0.5-120 minutes. Preferred                  Properties: The polyamide has a molecular weight                  distribution of 3 or less, and a weight average                  molecular weight of 20000-100000. The polyamide                  has a linear, branched, hyperbranched or                  dendritic structure.

(including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); A84 (Household and office fittings - including carpets and carbon paper.); A85 (Electrical applications.); A86 (Fancy goods, games, sports, toys.); A93 (Roads, building, construction flooring.); A95 (Transport - including vehicle parts, tyres and armaments.); ; X15 (Non-Fossil Fuel Power Generating Systems)

   LI X
AE QINGDAO RONGCHUANG NEW MATERIAL ENG RES (QING-Non-standard)
GA 2019358233
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of porous calcium hydroxide from eggshell involves stirring and pulverizing eggshell in water, rinsing off the surface organic matter and drying, subjecting the eggshell powder to vacuum negative pressure low-temperature calcination, stopping heating after calcination for 2-8 hours, stopping vacuum, supplying air or oxygen to obtain highly active porous calcium oxide powder, subjecting the obtained powder to dry superfine pulverization, placing the pulverized powder in carbon dioxide-free closed space, adjusting humidity of the closed space to 90-95% using ultrasonically atomized water, and carrying out flexible slow reaction of the porous powder for 6-24 hours.

   ADVANTAGE - The method uses vacuum negative pressure low temperature calcination which contributes to energy saving.

PN CN109456371-A; WO2020077970-A1
TI Preparation of stevioside mixture involves adding crude stevioside in sealed pressure-resistant system, dissolving in ethanol into crystallization mother liquor, passing carbon dioxide, crystallizing, filtering or centrifuging.
AU YANG W
   XIE Y
   SONG Y
AE GUILIN LAYN NATURAL INGREDIENTS CORP (GUIL-Non-standard)
   GUILIN LAYN NATURAL INGREDIENTS CORP (GUIL-Non-standard)
GA 2019253764
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of stevioside mixture involves taking crude stevioside and placing in sealed pressure-resistant system or device, adding 2 pts. wt. crude stevioside to concentration of 70-95% of ethanol to dissolve into crystallization mother liquor, passing carbon dioxide into the sealed pressure-resistant system, carrying out crystallization for 1-2 hours when the pressure in the sealed pressure-resistant system is 0.01-0.5 MPa, and the pH of the crystallization mother liquor is 4.5-5.3, separating the liquid by suction filtration or centrifugation after the crystallization is completed.
   USE - Preparation of stevioside mixture.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of high-efficiency stevioside mixture with high glycoside content and rebaudioside A content under short crystallization time, and does not need heating process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The pure stevioside crystal is obtained by taking stevioside crystal, leaving still, separating liquid by suction filtration or centrifugation. The crystallization is carried out when pressure in the sealed pressure-resistant system is 0.05-0.4 MPa, preferably 0.4 MPa and the pH of the crystallization mother liquid is 4.65-5.01, preferably 4.65. Carbon dioxide is used to increase total glucoside content of stevioside in purified or more pure stevioside crystals during crystallization from crude stevioside. The carbon dioxide is used to increase content of rebaudioside A in purified stevioside crystal during crystallization from crude stevioside.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The ethanol concentration is 80-90%. Preferred Conditions: The crystallization time is 2 hours. The centrifugation speed is greater than or equal to 200 rpm. The filtering pressure is less than or equal to -0.03 MPa.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of the selenium powder to graphene oxide is 1:20. The concentration of the europium nitrate solution is 0.1 mol/L. 25 mL acetic acid solution and 10 mL europium nitrate solution are used per gram of graphene oxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The anhydrous ethanol solution of n-butyl titanate is prepared by mixing n-butyl titanate and absolute ethanol in a mass ratio of 1:5. Preferred Components: The concentration of the acetic acid solution is 1 mol/L. The amount of n-butyl titanate is twice the mass of the graphene oxide.

AB    NOVELTY - A copper(II) m-titanium dioxide loaded photocatalyst is obtained by uniform loading of amorphous copper(II) nanoclusters on m-titanium dioxide nanospheres.
   USE - Copper(II) m-titanium dioxide loaded photocatalyst is used for gas phase photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to form methane and carbon monoxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The copper(II) m-titanium dioxide loaded photocatalyst has excellent catalytic performance and methane selectivity, and can be prepared by simple and environmentally-friendly process with reduced energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the photocatalyst, which involves hydrolyzing titanium isopropoxide as raw material using hexadecylamine as soft templating agent, subjecting to hydrothermal reaction, calcining, obtaining m-titanium dioxide nanospheres, and uniformly loading copper(II) nanoclusters on nanospheres by simple wet chemical precipitation method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The nanospheres are prepared using 1.75 g hexadecylamine, 200 ml ethanol, 1.3 ml 0.07 M potassium chloride solution, 4.32 ml triisopropylsilane, amorphous titanium dioxide, and mixed solution of 20 ml ethanol and 10 ml water. The photocatalyst is prepared using 0.04 g nanospheres, 40 ml 0.25 M sodium hydroxide solution, and 0.0077 M copper nitrate solution. The photocatalyst contains 1 mol% copper(II).

PN CN109110790-A; CN109110790-B
TI Preparation of skid-proof magnesium carbonate block by mixing magnesium hydroxide and water, introducing carbon dioxide gas, stirring until solution pH is constant, stopping reaction, conducting primary filtration and extrusion molding.
AU XING X
   YIN Q
   SUN M
   BI L
   ZHAI L
   DI L
AE HEBEI MEISHEN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HEBE-Non-standard)
GA 2019039690
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of skid-proof magnesium carbonate block comprises mixing magnesium hydroxide and water at 1:12-25 weight ratio, introducing carbon dioxide gas, stirring at 30-100 degrees C until the solution pH is constant, stopping the reaction, pouring the carbonized slurry into a filter press, conducting primary filtration, extrusion molding, obtaining filter cake having water content of 50-85%, transferring the filter cake to an oven, raising the temperature to 150-300 degrees C, drying for 5-20 hours, grinding and packaging the dried magnesium carbonate block.
   USE - Preparation of skid-proof magnesium carbonate block.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides magnesium carbonate block, which is not fragile, does not easily crack, and has excellent blockiness, handing property and powder falling property.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The weight ratio of magnesium hydroxide and water is 1:15-20. The stirring temperature is 60-98 degrees C. The flow rate of carbon dioxide is 25-45 m3/hour. The heating rate of the oven is 1-4 degrees C/minute. The oven drying comprises heating to 100 degrees C at 4 degrees C/minute, heating to 150-300 degrees C at 1-3 degrees C/minute and reducing the temperature to room temperature at 2-4 degrees C/minute.

PN EP3406593-A1; WO2018215206-A1; CN110678445-A; SG11201909686-A1; EP3630720-A1; US2020157045-A1; JP2020520897-W; US10800736-B2; EP3630720-B1; CN110678445-B; SG11201909686-B; JP7065884-B2
TI Manufacture of methionine used as e.g. animal feed additive, involves hydrolyzing methylmercaptoethyl-hydantoin in alkali solution, neutralizing with carbon dioxide and electrodialyzing with cell including anode compartment having preset pH.
AU FISCHER D
   STENNER P
   BERNHARDT S
   JAKOB H
AE EVONIK DEGUSSA GMBH (EVON-C)
   EVONIK OPERATIONS GMBH (EVON-C)
   EVONIK OPERATIONS GMBH (EVON-C)
   EVONIK OPERATIONS GMBH (EVON-C)
   EVONIK DEGUSSA GMBH (EVON-C)
   EVONIK OPERATIONS GMBH (EVON-C)
GA 201894652U
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of methionine involves performing alkaline hydrolysis of 5-(2-methylmercaptoethyl)hydantoin in an aqueous process solution containing alkali metal hydroxide and/or alkali metal bicarbonate to form alkali metal salt of methionine, precipitating methionine by neutralizing with carbon dioxide, and separating methionine from the process solution by electrodialysis with an electrodialysis cell including 2 or more chambers in which one of the chamber is an anode compartment including anode bounded by an ion-selective anion exchange membrane, and having pH of 5.8-8, and concentrating and reusing the process solution in alkaline hydrolysis. The byproduct includes formate and 1-5C organic acid anions.
   USE - Manufacture of methionine used as additive in animal feed, pharmaceuticals, healthcare product and fitness product.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient and economical manufacture of methionine.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The current density in each of the electrodialysis cell is 1-200 A/m2. Each case of the electrodialysis cell includes three chambers including a cathode compartment including a cation exchange membrane and a cathode. The electrodialysis is performed in 2 or more electrodialysis cells which are separated by a bipolar electrode. The pH of the anode compartment is adjusted using alkali metal hydroxide solution formed in the cathode compartment. The alkali metal is potassium or sodium.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The organic acid anion contains acetate and 2-hydroxy-4-methylmercaptobutanoate.

PN CN108246316-A; CN108246316-B
TI Platinum/Molybdenum disulfide nano catalyst in carbon dioxide hydrogenation reaction, comprises Molybdenum disulfide flake and platnium atoms which set on surface of Molybdenum disulfide nano-sheet, which has very high catalytic activity.
AU LI H
   PU Z
   WANG M
   ZENG J
AE UNIV SCI & TECHNOLOGY CHINA ADVANCED TEC (UYSC-Non-standard)
GA 2018546269
AB    NOVELTY - Platinum/Molybdenum disulfide nano catalyst comprises Molybdenum disulfide flake and platnium atoms which are carried on surface of Molybdenum disulfide nano-sheet.
   USE - Platinum/Molybdenum disulfide nano catalyst in carbon dioxide hydrogenation reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The platinum/Molybdenum disulfide nano catalyst has very high catalytic activity, high atom utilization ratio and strong interaction of the metal substrate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing platinum/Molybdenum disulfide nano catalyst, which involves dispersing Molybdenum disulfide nano-sheet in mixed solution of ethanol and deionized water to obtain mixed solution A, adding chloroplatinic potassium peroxydisulfate solution to obtain mixed solution B, adding to mixed solution B to stir and react, and subjecting obtained reaction product to washing treatment and drying treatment to obtain Platinum/Molybdenum disulfide nano catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The ratio of platnium atoms and Molybdenum disulfide nano sheet is 7-8:95-105. The ratio of Molybdenum disulfide, ethanol, de-ionized water and chloroplatinic solution is 95-105 (mg):30-40 (mL):12-18 (mL):40-44 (mL). The concentration of chloroplatinic potassium solution is 2.3-2.7 mg/mL. The speed of adding chloroplatinic potassium solution is 9-11 mL/hours. The stirring time of the mixed solution B is 50-70 minutes. The ratio of Ammonium heptamolybdate tetrahydrate, thiourea and de-ionized water is 1.21-1.25:1.05-1.08:34-36. The temperature of the hydrothermal reaction is 215-225 degrees C for 17-19 hours. Preferred Process: The washing treatment is carried out by using polar solvent. The washing treatment is carried out by performing centrifugal separation to reacted mixed solution B. The obtained product is centrifuged by polar solvent to carry out ultrasonic washing, then continuing centrifugal separation is performed. The obtained product is centrifuged by polar solvent to carry out ultrasonic washing for 1-2 minutes. The centrifugal separation speed is 7000-8000 revolution per minute. The time of centrifugal separation is 5-7 minutes. The Ammonium heptamolybdate tetrahydrate and thiourea are dissolved in de-ionized water, and then hydro-thermal reaction is performed, then cooled to room temperature, and obtained solid product is washed by washing treatment and drying treatment to obtain Molybdenum disulfide nano sheet.

PN CN107935000-A
TI Preparing high-purity lithium carbonate superfine powder comprises e.g. smashing primary lithium carbonate, adding into the reactor, heating the reactor, adding formic acid into the reactor and then passing carbon dioxide.
AU TAN Y
   TAN Z
AE DAYU XURI MINING TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (DAYU-Non-standard)
GA 2018327901
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high-purity lithium carbonate superfine powder comprises (i) smashing primary lithium carbonate, adding into the reactor, heating the reactor at 90-95 degrees C, adding formic acid into the reactor to obtain lithium formate solution, adding solution of carbonic acid amide into the lithium formate solution, passing carbon dioxide to obtain lithium bicarbonate solution, and (ii) adding lithium bicarbonate solution into the spray drying equipment, spray drying at 650-680 degrees C to obtain high purity lithium carbonate superfine powder.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing high-purity lithium carbonate superfine powder.
   ADVANTAGE - The superfine powder has simple preparation process, low cost, high purity, stable quality, high recovery rate and no-toxicity; and is safe, suitable for industrial production and environmentally friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method of pulverization is ball milling, air jet pulverization or ultrasonic pulverization. The heating of the reactor is a water bath heating. The addition rate of formic acid is 6-10 d/second. The temperature of the spray drying equipment is 670 degrees C.

   LEE J H
   KWAK N S
   JANG K R
AE KOREA ELECTRIC POWER CORP (KEPC-C)
GA 2018260331
AB    NOVELTY - Absorbent comprises amine compound (I), diol based compound (II), amine compound (III), and alcohol based compound (IV).
   USE - The absorbent is useful for separating acid gas including carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide, sulfur dioxide, nitrogen and carbonyl sulfide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The absorbent increases efficiency of acid gas separation process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Absorbent comprises amine compound of formula ((CH3)2-CH-NH-(CH2)2-OH) (I), diol based compound of formula ((CH3)2-CH-N-(CH2CH2-OH)2) (II), amine compound of formula (III), and alcohol based compound of formula (R5-NH-C(R6)(R7)CH(R8)(CH2)y-OH) (IV).
   R1-R4, R5 = H, 1-4C alkyl, -(CH2)a-OH or -(CH2)b-NH2;
   c, y = 0 or 1;
   a, b = 1-4; and
   R6-R8 = H or 1-4C alkyl.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The absorbent comprises 100 pts. wt. (I), 10-200 pts. wt. (II), 10-200 pts. wt. (III) and 10-200 pts. wt. (IV). (IV) include amino-2-propanol, diethanolamine, amino-2-methyl-1-propanol, 2-(methylamino)ethanol, 2-(ethylamino)ethanol, isopropylamino-2-propanol and/or propylamino-2-propanol. The total content of (I)-(IV) is 5-80 pts. wt. based on 100 pts. wt. absorbent. The weight ratio of (I)-(IV) is 1:0.1-0.5:0.5-2:0.5.

PN CN113772666-A
TI Preparation of oil-soluble fluorinated graphene            using supercritical carbon dioxide involves adding            fluorinated carbon powder, dispersant and auxiliary            intercalation agent, mixing graphite powder and            fluorinated coke, circulating carbon dioxide in            autoclave, and controlling number of layers.
AU LIN H
   WANG B
   YUAN Q
AE DALIAN YUANJING TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (DALI-Non-standard)
GA 2022251746
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of oil-soluble fluorinated                graphene using supercritical carbon dioxide                involves adding fluorinated carbon powder into the                high pressure reaction kettle, adding dispersant                and auxiliary intercalation agent into the high                pressure reaction kettle as lubricating oil base                oil, mixing graphite powder and fluorinated coke to                form fluorinated carbon powder, pumping carbon                dioxide into the autoclave (4), circulating carbon                dioxide after the gas pressure in the autoclave                reaches the preset value, relieving the pressure                after circulating carbon dioxide, reducing the                pressure quickly, so that the gas pressure in the                autoclave is reduced to normal pressure, repeating                the process so that the material in the autoclave                undergoes multiple boosting and depressurization                processes, controlling the number of layers of the                prepared fluorinated graphene by controlling the                number of boosting and depressurization, thereby                obtaining oil-soluble fluorine for graphene.
   USE - Preparation of oil-soluble fluorinated                graphene using supercritical carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is non-toxic, cheap and easy to                obtain, simple, mild, green and pollution-free, and                cost-effective, and enables preparation of                oil-soluble fluorinated graphene using                supercritical carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of oil-soluble fluorinated                graphene using supercritical carbon dioxide                involves adding fluorinated carbon powder into the                high pressure reaction kettle, adding dispersant                and auxiliary intercalation agent into the high                pressure reaction kettle as lubricating oil base                oil, mixing graphite powder and fluorinated coke to                form fluorinated carbon powder, where the                dispersant is a fluorine-containing surfactant,                pumping carbon dioxide into the autoclave after the                temperature in the autoclave reaches the preset                value, circulating carbon dioxide after the gas                pressure in the autoclave reaches the preset value,                where the gas pressure inside is 15-45 MPa,                relieving the pressure after circulating carbon                dioxide for 30-100 minutes, reducing the pressure                quickly, so that the gas pressure in the autoclave                is reduced to normal pressure, repeating the                process so that the material in the autoclave                undergoes multiple boosting and depressurization                processes, controlling the number of layers of the                prepared fluorinated graphene by controlling the                number of boosting and depressurization for 10-100                times, thereby obtaining oil-soluble fluorine for                graphene. The fluorocarbon mass ratio of the                fluorinated graphene product is 0.8-1.1. The mass                ratio of the peeling agent and the fluorinated                carbon powder is (0.3-11):1. The mass ratio of the                fluorinated carbon powder and the dispersant is                (1:0.2)-(1:10). The mass ratio of the release agent                to the lubricating base oil is 1.1.3-12.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a main view of the                oil-soluble fluorinated graphene preparing process                device.
   Carbon dioxide steel bottle (1)
   Cooling device (2)
   Carbon dioxide pump (3)
   Autoclave (4)
   Pressure control system (6)
   Pressure relief tank (7)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The fluorocarbon mass                  ratio of the fluorinated graphene product is                  0.7-1.1. The mass ratio of the peeling agent and                  the fluorinated carbon powder is (0.1-11):1. The                  mass ratio of the fluorinated carbon powder and                  the dispersant is (1:0.1)-(1:15). The mass ratio                  of the release agent to the lubricating base oil                  is 1.1.2-8.

PN CN111304672-A; CN111304672-B
TI H-type fixed-bed carbon dioxide reduction electrolytic cell comprises electrolytic cell includes an anode assembly, a cathode assembly, and an electrolyte membrane that separates the two, and anode assembly includes an anode cavity.
AU MAO Q
   CAO Z
   GUO C
   LIU S
   XU K
   MAO Y
   LI B
   ZHAO J
   HUANG Y
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 202058050C
AB    NOVELTY - H-type fixed-bed carbon dioxide reduction electrolytic cell comprises electrolytic cell includes an anode assembly, a cathode assembly, and an electrolyte membrane that separates the two. The anode assembly includes an anode cavity (9), an oxygen evolution anode (19), and connectors for anode fixation and anode chamber sealing. The cathode assembly includes a cathode cavity (8), a reference electrode (13), and a fixed bed cover (1), fluid distributor (2), fixed bed cavity (3) and fixed bed base (4), current collector rod (18), and connecting parts for sealing the cathode chamber and fixing the electrode. The side of the fixed bed cover is provided with two screw holes to connect the tube plate joints, which are respectively used for the renewal of the electrolyte solution and the input of carbon dioxide gas. The upper middle of the fixed bed cover is provided with a threaded hole for placing the current collecting rod.
   USE - H-type fixed-bed carbon dioxide reduction electrolytic cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The H-type fixed-bed carbon dioxide reduction electrolytic cell achieves the timely supply of reactant carbon dioxide and the stability of the electrode solution interface ion concentration, which has a broad application in the field of carbon dioxide RR prospect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for using H-type fixed-bed carbon dioxide reduction electrolytic cell, which comprises
   (A) using the electrolyte on the cathode side, which flows out through the position of the reference electrode fixture, is driven by the peristaltic pump, and then returns to the cathode cavity through the side threaded hole on the side of the fixed bed cover;
   (B) entering carbon dioxide by upper space of the fluid distributor in the fixed bed cavity through the threaded hole on the other side of the fixed bed cover, where the saturated cathode-side electrolyte solution flows through the cathode granular carbon dioxide RR catalyst, and the generated product gas flows out through the threaded hole at the top of the fixed bed cover and enters the chromatograph; and
   (C) using an electrochemical workstation to control the operation of the H-type fixed-bed carbon dioxide RR electrolysis cell, with the fixed-bed cathode as the working electrode, where the saturated calomel electrode is the reference electrode, and the Pt sheet is the counter electrode, driving the carbon dioxide electroreduction reaction of the H-type fixed-bed carbon dioxide RR electrolysis cell.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view of the H-type fixed-bed carbon dioxide reduction electrolytic cell.

   Fluid distributor (2)
   Fixed bed cavity (3)
   Fixed bed base (4)

   Anode cavity (9)
   Reference electrode (13)

   Oxygen evolution anode (19)

AU BYRON M
   RAJAGOPALAN N
AE LINDE AG (LINM-C)
   UNIV ILLINOIS URBANA-CHAMPAIGN (UNII-C)
GA 201993721Q
AB    NOVELTY - Method for acidifying manure involves injecting carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for acidifying manure using device (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for device, which comprises a tank for the manure, a gas cylinder for storing carbon dioxide, at least one pH probe for measuring pH of the manure, and a pH control unit that controls amount of carbon dioxide to decrease pH of the manure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Method: The carbon dioxide can be injected as gas or liquid into the manure. The carbon dioxide is injected into manure in an amount based on pH of the manure. The carbon dioxide is injected by a mat, containing finely perforated aeration hoses or nozzle which is placed in a treatment tank. The carbon dioxide and the manure are mixed with a mechanical agitation device. The carbon dioxide is injected to the manure directly before distribution to soil. The manure is acidified continuously or batch-wise. Preferred Device: The tank is covered and comprises a mat, containing finely perforated aeration hoses or nozzles, for distributing the gas and a mechanical agitation device, where the tank comprises a pump to move the acidified manure. The tank is a mobile tank.

AU JIANG S

AE UNIV SHANGHAI (USHN-C)
GA 2022054916

The diamine compound is put into a closed reaction kettle, the reaction temperature is 100-200degrees Celsius, the carbon dioxide pressure in the reaction kettle is 1-5 MPa, and the reaction is carried out for 6-12 hours to prepare amino-terminated polyurea oligomer followed by putting the five-membered cyclic carbonate compounds and the amino-terminated polyurea oligomer into the reaction flask, adding a solvent, and feeding nitrogen as protective gas to obain carbon dioxide-based polyhydroxyurethane-urea.
   USE - Method for preparing carbon dioxide-based polyhydroxyurethane-urea.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparing polyhydroxyurethane-urea synthesis using carbon dioxide as a raw material, without using isocyanate compounds, in simple and environmentally friendly manner.

The catalyst is tetrabutylammonium bromide. The diamine compound is polyetheramine D-230, polyether amine D-400, polyetheramine ED-600, polyetheramine ED-900, polyetheramine D-2000, 1,4-butanediol bis(3-aminopropyl) ether, 4,7,10-tris oxy-1,13-tridecanediamine. TI Extracting seashore mallow with supercritical carbon dioxide useful as essential oil for e.g. improving immunity, comprises crushing seashore mallow stem and leaf, subjecting to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain extracted oil and molecularly distilling seashore mallow leaf oil.
AU DONG C
   CHEN Y
   CHEN X
AE JIANGSU GUSHEN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202202962M
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting seashore mallow with supercritical carbon dioxide comprises cleaning and drying fresh seashore mallow stem and leaf, cutting the stem and leaf into 6-10 cm segments, adding into the crushing device and crushing into powder, subjecting crushed powder to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain the extracted oil, removing water in the oil by distillation to obtain the seashore mallow leaf oil, and molecularly distilling seashore mallow leaf oil to obtain seashore mallow essential oil.
   USE - The method for extracting seashore mallow with supercritical carbon dioxide useful as essential oil for resisting inflammation and tumor, lowering blood uric acid, resisting oxidative aging, and improving body immunity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for device for extracting seashore mallow with supercritical carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of the method for extracting seashore mallow with supercritical carbon dioxide (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the method, during, cleaning and drying fresh seashore mallow stem and leaf the method further comprises picking fresh seashore mallow stem and leaf to remove mixed impurities, and repeatedly cleaning seashore mallow stem and leaf, adding the cleaned seashore mallow stem and leaf in an oven at 45-55degrees Celsius for 12 hours; during cutting stem and leaf into 6-10 cm in each section, and adding into crushing device and crushing into powder, the method further comprises cutting the roasted seashore mallow stem and leaf into small sections of 6-10 cm, adding the cut stem and leaf of seashore mallow into a crushing device and crushing for 1 hour, taking out the crushed powder and passing through a 40-50 meshes sieve; during supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, loading the powder into the extraction kettle, setting temperature of the extraction kettle to be 40-50degrees Celsius, and the pressure to be 2.4-2.6 Mpa, the temperature of the separation kettle is 48-51degrees Celsius, and the pressure of the separation kettle is 5-6.5 Mpa, the pressurized carbon monoxide is input into the extraction kettle and the separation kettle, and extracted for 5-7 hours and separating kettle to obtain oil; during removing the moisture in the oil by distillation, the method further comprises, carrying out the first molecular distillation of the oil to obtain primary seashore mallow leaf essential oil, where the rotational speed of the wiper film of the molecular distillation is 200-500 revolutions/minute, the cooling water temperature is 20-45degrees Celsius, the distillation temperature is 50-120degrees Celsius, the distillation pressure is 0.05-0.25 kpa, and the material flow rate is 5-30 ml/minute, and subjecting obtained primary seashore mallow leaf essential oil is to second molecular distillation to obtain seashore mallow essential oil, where the film scraping speed of the molecular distillation is 300-600 revolutions/minute, the cooling water temperature is 30-55degrees Celsius, the distillation temperature is 50-120degrees Celsius, the distillation pressure is 0.08-0.28 kpa, and the material flow rate is 10-30 ml/minute.
   ACTIVITY - Antiinflammatory; Cytostatic; Antigout; Metabolic; Nootropic; Dermatological; Immunostimulant.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the mixed solution, the amount of phenethyl acetate corresponding to each 100 ml of water is 0.2-0.4 g, and the amount of corresponding N-alkylimidazole is 0.2-0.8 g. Preferred Method: The mixing with carbon dioxide includes passing high-concentration carbon dioxide gas into the mixed liquid, and the volume content of carbon dioxide in the high-concentration carbon dioxide gas is at least 80%. For every 100ml of mixed liquid, the flow of high-concentration carbon dioxide gas is 0.05-0.5 l/minutes, and the time is 10-60 minutes. The mixing with carbon dioxide includes adding dry ice to the mixed liquid. In the step (ii), per 100 ml of the mixed solution, the flow rate of the carbon dioxide-depleted gas gas is 0.05-0.5 l/minutes, and the time is 1-20 minutes.

AE QINGDAO HAIER AIR-CONDITIONER CO LTD (HIER-C)
   QINGDAO HAIER CO LTD (HIER-C)
   HAIER SMART HOME CO LTD (HIER-C)
   QINGDAO HAIER JIAOZHOU AIR CONDITIONER (HIER-C)
GA 2019916377
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves switching a control valve                to an open state. A concentration value of carbon                dioxide is determined by utilizing a carbon dioxide                sensor after the control valve is switched to an                open state for a predetermined period of time. PT P
PN WO2021201763-A1; CN115279869-A
TI Production of liquid biogas involves performing            inflow of crude gas comprising methane and carbon            dioxide, performing removal of trace elements like            siloxanes and volatile organic compounds, performing            dehumidification, particle purification and separation            of carbon dioxide and condensing.
AU KARLSSON R
   BENZON M
   CARLSSON R
AE BIOFRIGAS SWEDEN AB (BIOF-Non-standard)
GA 2021B8096M
AB    NOVELTY - Method for production of liquid biogas (LBG),                involves performing inflow of crude gas comprising                methane and carbon dioxide, performing removal of                trace elements like hydrogen sulfide, siloxanes and                volatile organic compounds (VOCs) from the crude                gas, performing dehumidification, performing                particle purification for the production of a                treated biogas, performing separation of carbon                dioxide from the treated biogas, and performing                condensation of the treated biogas with a low                content of carbon dioxide, for the production of                LBG with a carbon dioxide content of maximum 100                parts per million, preferably at or close to                atmospheric pressure the LBG is close to 100% pure                methane with a carbon dioxide content of maximum                100 parts per million, where the separation of                carbon dioxide from the treated biogas involves                freezing carbon dioxide in the treated                biogas.
   USE - The method is useful for production of liquid                biogas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system is arrange in a fixed volume,                installs a system unit in a simple way and is                suitable to be arranged inside a mobile unit,                preferably in a mobile container with a maximum                size of 40 foot.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) the use of a method for the production of                LBG in which the level of carbon dioxide is maximum                100 parts per million; and
   (2) a system intended for the production of                liquid biogas, comprising a removal unit for                removal of trace elements like hydrogen sulfide,                VOCs and siloxane, a unit for particle                purification, a biogas compressor, at least two                heat exchangers, a cooling water loop, a heating                water loop, at least one dryer, a cooling system                and a LBG storage tank.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises                  flowing the produced LBG to a cryogenic storage                  tank. The method involves performing separation                  of carbon dioxide from the treated biogas, and                  performing condensation of the treated biogas                  with a low content of carbon dioxide, via                  alternately heat exchanging so that the                  separation of carbon dioxide from the treated                  biogas is performed so that carbon dioxide is                  frozen and then separated off via sublimation,                  where condensed treated biogas with a low content                  of carbon dioxide is flown to a cryogenic storage                  tank for storage of LBG with a carbon dioxide                  content of maximum 100 parts per million. The                  method involves performing separation of carbon                  dioxide from the treated biogas, and performing                  condensation of the treated biogas with a low                  content of carbon dioxide, supported by heat                  exchanging in at least two heat exchangers, one                  freezing the carbon dioxide and one in                  regeneration at the same time. Preferred                  Conditions: The removal of trace elements like                  hydrogen sulfide, siloxanes and VOCs from the                  crude gas is performed through at least one                  activated carbon filter. The particle                  purification is performed by mechanical                  filtration. The dehumidification of the treated                  biogas is performed with a drying technology,                  preferably with an initial knock out drum for                  free water. The method uses a cascade cooling                  system in which compression is performed in at                  least one reciprocating compressor. The method                  uses a cooling medium being a mixed refrigerant                  in a cascade system suitable for freezing carbon                  dioxide and condensing methane, and where high                  temperature cooling in the cooling system is                  performed using water, preferably enabling heat                  recovery to be used in the process or for other                  heating purposes, such as for heating digesters,                  green houses or other heat requiring processes or                  workshops or housing areas. At least three heat                  exchangers are used so that one is for freezing                  the carbon dioxide and two are for regeneration                  at the same time. The separation of carbon                  dioxide from the treated biogas is performed by                  freezing carbon dioxide in the treated biogas and                  then filtering off carbon dioxide from the                  treated biogas. Preferred Device: The cooling                  system comprises at least one refrigeration                  compressor, at least one heat exchanger and at                  least one refrigerant loop. The cooling system                  comprises at least three heat exchangers                  comprising one heat exchanger unit connected to                  the cooling water loop and the heating water                  loop, another heat exchanger unit arranged to                  provide carbon dioxide sublimation and                  desublimation and active or subsequent removal of                  carbon dioxide from treated biogas, and one heat                  exchanger unit arranged to provide condensation                  of the treated biogas with a low content of                  carbon dioxide and production of LBG in which the                  level of carbon dioxide is maximum 100 parts per                  million. The system further comprises a carbon                  dioxide filter arranged subsequent to one or more                  heat exchanger(s) arranged for freezing carbon                  dioxide in treated biogas. The cooling system is                  a cascade cooling system in which compression is                  performed in at least one reciprocating                  compressor. The system is arranged inside a                  mobile unit, preferably in a mobile container,                  more preferably in a container with a maximum                  size of 40 foot.

TI Fixing carbon useful for preparing phosphogypsum product, comprises e.g. converting captured carbon dioxide gas into carbonic acid liquid, adding carbonic acid liquid into hemihydrate phosphogypsum, and fixing carbon dioxide in free water.
AU HE Y
   LIU Y
   ZHANG Z
   ZHANG C
   LI Y
AE HE Y (HEYY-Individual)
   LIU Y (LIUY-Individual)
   ZHANG Z (ZHAN-Individual)
   ZHANG C (ZHAN-Individual)
   LI Y (LIYY-Individual)
GA 2021A40873
AB    NOVELTY - Fixing carbon comprises converting captured carbon dioxide gas into carbonic acid liquid under action of certain temperature and pressure, directly adding carbonic acid liquid into hemihydrate phosphogypsum under action of continuous temperature and pressure, stirring at high speed to reduce hemihydrate phosphogypsum into dihydrate phosphogypsum during hydration reaction process, fixing carbon dioxide in free water of phosphogypsum in a form of carbonic acid, directly preparing generated dihydrate phosphogypsum into phosphogypsum product, and fixing carbon dioxide in phosphogypsum product in a liquid form.
   USE - The method is useful for fixing carbon, which is useful for preparing phosphogypsum product (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple and controllable; has low cost, high efficiency, and large carbon storage amount; realizes three ways of carbon sequestration in phosphogypsum product; realizes recycling of phosphogypsum; improves heat preservation, heat insulation and sound insulation function of phosphogypsum product; and can make waste slag phosphogypsum into valuable with good economic benefit and social benefit.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) phosphogypsum product, comprises liquid or gaseous carbon dioxide sealed in phosphogypsum product, where the carbon dioxide in liquid form is existed in a way of replacing free water of phosphogypsum, and the carbon dioxide in gaseous form is sealed in closed pore of phosphogypsum; and
   (2) preparing the phosphogypsum product, comprising: converting carbon dioxide gas into carbonic acid liquid under action of certain temperature and pressure, directly adding carbonic acid liquid into hemihydrate phosphogypsum under action of continuous temperature and pressure, stirring at high speed to reduce hemihydrate phosphogypsum into dihydrate phosphogypsum during hydration reaction process, fixing carbon dioxide in free water of phosphogypsum in a form of carbonic acid, and directly forming generated dihydrate phosphogypsum.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the method for fixing carbon (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the carbon fixing method, the pressure of carbon dioxide gas is 5-30 MPa, preferably 5 MPa, and temperature is 0-20 degrees C, preferably 10 degrees C. The mass ratio of carbonic acid liquid to hemihydrate phosphogypsum is 0.5-1:1, preferably 0.7:1. The high-speed stirring process is carried out under rotating speed of 1200-1600 revolutions/minute, preferably 1400 revolutions/minute. The carbon fixing method further comprises sealing carbon dioxide gas in closed pore of phosphogypsum in prepared phosphogypsum product, and sealing carbonic acid inert substance in prepared phosphogypsum product.

AU SUN C
   SUN Z
AE UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
GA 202007428D
AB    NOVELTY - Chain circulation method involves performing                methane cracking by contacting methane with                nickel-iron-calcium-iron-aluminum oxalate catalytic                oxygen carrier to obtain high-purity hydrogen,                reducing catalytic oxygen carrier with nano carbon                deposited, introducing carbon dioxide into reduced                catalytic oxygen carrier and performing carbon                dioxide reduction reaction to regenerate                nickel-iron-calcium-iron-aluminum oxalate and to                produce carbon monoxide in parallel. The                nickel-iron-calcium-iron-aluminum oxalate catalytic                oxygen carrier comprises calcium-iron-aluminum                oxalate substrate and 10-30 wt.%. nano nickel-iron                oxalate particles dispersed on the substrate.
   USE - Chain circulation method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method achieves continuous and efficient                production of high-purity hydrogen and carbon                dioxide gas emission reductions, and utilizes                catalytic oxygen carrier to regulate the occurrence                of hydrogen production reaction and carbon dioxide                reduction reaction. The method achieves high                methane conversion rate and high hydrogen                selectivity in the methane cracking stage and high                carbon dioxide conversion and high carbon monoxide                selectivity in carbon dioxide reduction                stage.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Chain circulation method involves performing                methane cracking by contacting methane with                nickel-iron-calcium-iron-aluminum oxalate of                formula: NixFe3-xO4-Ca2FeyAl2-yO5 catalytic oxygen                carrier to obtain high-purity hydrogen, reducing                catalytic oxygen carrier with nano carbon                deposited, introducing carbon dioxide into reduced                catalytic oxygen carrier and performing carbon                dioxide reduction reaction to regenerate                nickel-iron-calcium-iron-aluminum oxalate and to                produce carbon monoxide in parallel. The                nickel-iron-calcium-iron-aluminum oxalate catalytic                oxygen carrier comprises calcium-iron-aluminum                oxalate substrate of formula: Ca2FeyAl2-yO5 and                10-30 wt.%. nano nickel-iron oxalate of formula:                NixFe3-xO4 particles dispersed on the substrate,                where x is 0.2-1 and y is 0.4-2.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The                  calcium-iron-aluminum oxalate substrate has                  hexagonal crystal phase and particle size of                  40-60 nm. The nano nickel-iron oxalate particles                  have particle size of 10-30 nm. Preferred                  Component: The nano carbon is an amorphous carbon                  with D-Raman peak and G-Raman peak (IG/ID)                  intensity of 1.3 or less.

PN US2019256990-A1; US11131028-B2
TI Reducing carbon dioxide comprises e.g. receiving anolyte feed of water and hydrogen halide at anolyte region of electrochemical cell including anode, and receiving feed of carbon dioxide gas at catholyte region of cell including cathode.
AU KACZUR J J
   LAKKARAJU P
   TEAMEY K
AE AVANTIUM KNOWLEDGE CENT BV (AVAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019718512
AB    NOVELTY - Reducing carbon dioxide comprises: receiving an anolyte feed at the anolyte region of the electrochemical cell including an anode, where the anolyte includes water and a hydrogen halide; receiving a feed of carbon dioxide gas at the catholyte region of the electrochemical cell including a cathode, where the cathode includes a gas diffusion electrode; receiving a catholyte feed including carbon dioxide and an alkali metal bicarbonate at the catholyte region of the electrochemical cell; and applying an electrical potential between the anode and the cathode of the electrochemical cell sufficient to reduce the carbon dioxide to an alkali metal formate and co-produce a halogen.
   USE - The method is useful for reducing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes the catalyst which reduces the calcination temperature and improves the conversion yield of alkali metal formate to alkali metal oxalate to a greater than or equal to 50-99%, preferably greater than or equal to 70-99%; and utilizes the anolyte brine concentration which may be operated lower sodium chloride concentrations, to as low as 100-140 gm/l, which may result in a decrease in chlorine efficiency and the generation of more byproduct oxygen in the chlorine gas stream, but which may be useful in reducing the brine flow rate through the brine purification system with a reduction in brine processing costs.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) a cathode structure for reducing carbon dioxide comprising a gas diffusion electrode, where the gas diffusion electrode is in direct contact with a carbon dioxide gas stream; and
   (2) a cathode gas diffusion electrode apparatus for reducing carbon dioxide comprising a catalyst consisting of indium, tin, bismuth, lead, silver, gold, zinc or cadmium, including their binary and ternary alloys and/or intermetallics.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises: converting the alkali metal formate to an alkali metal oxalate via a thermal reaction; receiving the alkali metal oxalate at a electrochemical acidification electrolyzer; and converting the alkali metal oxalate to oxalic acid and co-producing alkali metal hydroxide, hydrogen and a halogen at the electrochemical acidification electrolyzer. Preferred Components: The hydrogen halide includes at least one of hydrogen bromide or hydrogen chloride. The cathode structure further comprises an internal gas plenum to distribute the carbon dioxide gas stream evenly into the gas diffusion electrode. The catalyst comprises indium, tin and/or bismuth. The cathode gas diffusion electrode construction further comprises a carbon cloth support structure attached to a layer containing the catalyst. The layer containing the catalyst contains the catalyst deposited onto a carbon powder base by spraying or precipitation. The cathode gas diffusion electrode apparatus further comprises a PTFE or polyvinylidene difluoride emulsion. The cathode gas diffusion electrode apparatus comprises a 3-phase structure.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC A85 (Electrical applications.); E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).)

PN KR2018046582-A
TI Incubator comprises a well plate for culturing a sample, a carbon dioxide adjustment unit for supplying carbon dioxide, humidity adjustment unit for supplying water vapor, and a control unit.
AU KIM K S
   GWAK M J
AE KOREA OPTRON CORP (KOOP-Non-standard)
GA 2018381667
AB    NOVELTY - Incubator comprises a well plate (100) for culturing a sample, a carbon dioxide adjustment unit (200) for supplying carbon dioxide to the well plate, a humidity adjustment unit (300) for supplying water vapor to the well plate, and a control unit (400) for controlling the carbon dioxide adjustment unit and the humidity adjustment unit so that the interior of the well plate maintains culture environment.
   USE - Used as incubator.
   ADVANTAGE - The incubator prevents condensation phenomenon on the well plate, can observe sample in real time, and minimizes amount of carbon dioxide consumed and amount of outflow to the outside.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Incubator comprises a well plate (100) for culturing a sample, a carbon dioxide adjustment unit (200) for supplying carbon dioxide to the well plate, a humidity adjustment unit (300) for supplying water vapor to the well plate, and a control unit (400) for controlling the carbon dioxide adjustment unit and the humidity adjustment unit so that the interior of the well plate maintains culture environment. The well plate comprises a frame having a closed structure, a heater glass installed inside the frame and made of a transparent material so that the sample can be observed, and prevents condensation phenomenon by maintaining a predetermined temperature, a sensor unit disposed inside the frame for measuring environmental information of the inside of the frame, and an accommodating chamber provided inside the frame and having multiple wells for accommodating the sample.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the incubator (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Incubator (10)
   Well plate (100)
   Carbon dioxide adjustment unit (200)
   Humidity adjustment unit (300)
   Control unit (400)
   Main body (500)

PN JP6934553-B1; JP2022084479-A
TI Information processing apparatus for use in an information processing system, comprises a storage unit that stores the reference change information regarding the increasing rate, which shows the change of the carbon dioxide levels.
AU KUSANO K
   KATO Y
AE CREATIVE JAPAN LTD (CREA-Non-standard)
   CRESCO DIGITAL TECHNOLOGIES LTD (CRES-Non-standard)
GA 2021A6720S
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (20) has a storage unit that stores reference change information regarding the rate of increase indicating the amount of change in carbon dioxide concentration per unit time when one person is in a predetermined space. A reception unit accepts the concentration of carbon dioxide measured in predetermined space. The acquisition unit acquires the rate of increase indicating the change in concentration and the rate of increase in the concentration of carbon dioxide acquired by the acquisition unit, based on the concentration of carbon dioxide in the first hour received by the reception unit and the concentration of carbon dioxide in the second hour time prior to the first hour. An estimation unit estimates the number of people in a predetermined space, by dividing by the rate of increase based on the reference change information stored in the storage unit. An output control unit controls to output the number of people estimated by the estimation unit.
   USE - Information processing apparatus e.g. computer such as desktop and laptop, server, tablet for use in an information processing system (claimed), for storing change in carbon dioxide concentration such as rate of increase in carbon dioxide as reference change.
   ADVANTAGE - The information processing apparatus can estimate the number of persons of the person who is indoors at an early stage relatively based on the concentration of a carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) information processing method; and
   (2) information processing program.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the information processing system.
   Information processing system (10)
   Information processing apparatus (20)

PN KR2218054-B1
TI High-pressure gaseous carbon dioxide production device, has measuring device provided between first pressure measuring tube and second pressure measuring tube, and water level meter measuring water level by measuring pressure difference.
AU BIN K J
   KYUM K H
   CHOI M S
   BEOM L J
AE DAESUNG IND GASES CO LTD (DAES-Non-standard)
GA 2021192539
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a low pressure tank unit (100) for storing liquid carbon dioxide at first pressure. A liquid pump unit (200) pressurizes the liquid carbon dioxide in the low pressure tank unit and supplies second pressure higher than that of the first pressure. A high pressure tank unit (300) stores the liquid carbon dioxide supplied from the liquid pump unit at a third pressure between the first pressure and the second pressure. The high pressure tank unit is provided with a dual-housing for blocking heat intrusion. A measuring device is provided between a first pressure measuring tube and a second pressure measuring tube to measure level of the liquid carbon dioxide in an inner tank. A pressure transfer pipe is provided with a diaphragm to transmit a pressure of a predetermined fluid. A water level meter measures a water level by measuring a pressure difference between a first fluid and a second fluid.
   USE - High-pressure gaseous carbon dioxide production device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device minimizes phase change in a storage tank which liquid carbon dioxide having a high pressure of 55 bar or higher is stored so as to stably measure inside level of liquid carbon dioxide storage amount.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the high-pressure gaseous carbon dioxide production device.
   Low pressure tank unit (100)
   Cooling unit (110)
   Liquid pump unit (200)
   High pressure tank unit (300)
   Vaporization unit (400)
