PT P
PN US2020277248-A1; US11078148-B2
TI Producing formate from carbon dioxide comprises contacting carbon dioxide with composition comprising substituted heterocyclic compounds, solution solvent, and acid.
AU CRONIN S P
   GRAPPERHAUS C A
   BUCHANAN R M
   STRAIN J M
   SPURGEON J M
AE UNIV LOUISVILLE RES FOUND INC (ULOU-C)
GA 202086603P
AB    NOVELTY - Producing (M1) formate from carbon dioxide, comprises contacting carbon dioxide with a composition comprising (a) substituted heterocyclic compounds (I), (b) a solution solvent, and (c) an acid.
   USE - The method is useful for producing formate from carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing (M1) formate from carbon dioxide, comprises contacting carbon dioxide with a composition comprising (a) substituted heterocyclic compounds of formula (I) or their salts, optical isomers, geometric isomers, salts of isomers, derivatives, and solvent associated complexes, (b) a solution solvent, and (c) an acid.
   R1, R2 = -NH2, -N(CH3)2, -COH, -CO2H, 1-10C alkyl, 2-10C alkenyl, 2-10C alkynyl, 1-9C alkoxy, cycloalkyl, heterocyclyl, aryl or heteroaryl (all optionally substituted with at least one of W1), monovalent H, halo, -CN, -NO2 or -OH; either
   R3, R4 = -COH, -CO2H, 1-10C alkyl, 2-10C alkenyl, 2-10C alkynyl, 1-9C alkoxy, cycloalkyl, heterocyclyl, aryl or heteroaryl (all optionally substituted with at least one of W1), monovalent H, halo, -CN or -OH; or
   R3R4 = ring, which is formed can be cycloalkyl, heterocyclyl, aryl or heteroaryl (all optionally substituted with at least one of W1);
   W1 = halo, O, -OH, -COH, -CO2H, -NO2, -NH2, -N(CH3)2, -CN, -CCH, propynyl, -SO3H, morpholinyl, -CO-morpholin-4-yl, phenyl, -CONH2, -CON(CH3)2, 1-3C alkyl, 1-3C perfluoronated alkyl, -CF3, -OCF3 or 1-3C alkoxy; and
   M = Fe, Ni, Pd, Cd, Mn, Co, Cu, Ga or Zn.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing (M2) formate from carbon dioxide, comprising contacting carbon dioxide with a first composition to provide a second composition, where the first composition comprises (I) and the solution solvent, and adding acid to the second composition to produce formate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The composition further comprises: a hydride source comprising a chemical hydride source or an electrochemical hydride source, where the chemical hydride source comprises a metal hydride, sodium borohydride or lithium aluminum hydride, and the electrochemical hydride source comprises a platinum electrode, a palladium electrode or a glassy carbon electrode; and the electrode. Preferred Components: The solution solvent comprises acetonitrile, methanol, ethanol, propanol, n-butanol, pentanol (all preferred), water, 1-8C alcohol, butanol and/or hexanol. The acid has a pKa of not greater than 14. The acid comprises acetic acid (preferred), triethyl-ammonium tetrafluoroborate, piperidinium tetrafluoroborate and/or phosphoric acid. The temperature of the composition is 0-99 degrees C. The source of carbon dioxide comprises air, from the waste gas of a powerplant, from the byproduct of a chemical reaction and/or from the byproduct of a catalytic reaction. The source of the carbon dioxide is from a gas and the concentration of carbon dioxide in the gas is 0.01-90%.
   Preferred Definitions:
   R1, R2 = H, Cl, Br, -OH, -COH, -CO2H, methyl, ethyl, n-propyl or phenyl; either
   R3, R4 = H, methyl, ethyl, n-propyl or phenyl; or

   M = Zn.

PT P
PN US2018291805-A1; US10975766-B2
TI Method of power generation using carbon dioxide            circulating fluid, involves passing recycle carbon            dioxide stream to series of turbines, and isolating            portion of carbon dioxide from cooled turbine discharge            stream.
AU ALLAM R J
   PALMER M R
   BROWN G W
   FETVEDT J E
   FORREST B A
AE 8 RIVERS CAPITAL LLC (ERIV-C)
   8 RIVERS CAPITAL LLC (ERIV-C)
GA 202138194U
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves expanding a compressed                recycle carbon dioxide stream at a pressure so as                to output from the last turbine a last turbine                discharge stream. A discharge stream is heated from                the first turbine prior to passage into the last                turbine in a combustor by combusting a hydrocarbon                or carbonaceous fuel (254) in the presence of an                oxidant and the first turbine discharge stream so                as to form a combustor exhaust stream. The last                turbine discharge stream is cooled in a recuperator                heat exchanger. A portion of carbon dioxide is                isolated from the cooled turbine discharge stream                to form the recycle carbon dioxide stream. The                recycle carbon dioxide stream is compressed. The                recycle carbon dioxide stream is passed to the                series of turbines.
   USE - Method of power generation using carbon                dioxide circulating fluid.
   ADVANTAGE - The introduction of the transpiration                substance at the right angle with respect to the                inner transpiration component facilitates or                enhances the effect of preventing the formation of                combustion fluid vortices with sufficient velocity                perpendicular to and in proximity to the inner                transpiration component. The higher energy density                increases the efficiency of the process, and                reduces the cost of the equipment needed to                implement the energy transformation from thermal                energy to electricity by reducing the size and mass                of the equipment, thus the cost of the                equipment.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view                illustrating the process of power                generation.
   Fuel mixture (200)
   Burner (220)
   Combustion chamber (222)
   Inlet portion (222A)
   Carbonaceous fuel (254)

TI Desiccant assembly for use in carbon dioxide removal system, in which removable canister assembly resides within desiccant canister capable of operating in two modes during operation of carbon dioxide removal system.
AU BIGELOW R T

   FLORES L

   RUGG J

   KRIZAN D

AE MULTIPLE ASSIGNMENTS (MULT-Non-standard)
   BIGELOW AEROSPACE LLC (BIGE-Non-standard)
GA 201830885R
AB    NOVELTY - The desiccant assembly has a removable canister assembly having removable desiccant media, a removable dust filter (7), and a heater (5). During operation of a carbon dioxide removal system, the canister assembly resides within a desiccant canister (4). The canister operates in a first mode, in which air is received by the inlet and dried prior to passing the outlet, and in a second mode, in which the outlet receives air containing water and carbon dioxide and the inlet directs the same air to a desired location.
   USE - Desiccant assembly for use in carbon dioxide removal system used in spacecraft.
   ADVANTAGE - The use of the system with multiple canisters allows for ability to maintain functionality even if one system is inoperable. The ability to remove the insert within minutes allows for easy maintenance or replacement of each canister, which leads to increase in orbit maintenance efficiency. Launch costs for replacement media, heaters, and filters are lower than for deploying the entire canister or entire removal system to the deployed spacecraft. Valve lifetime is extended due to easy clean-up or replacement of seal.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the following:
   (1) a carbon dioxide removal system;
   (2) a process for repairing a butterfly valve connected to an adsorbing canister as portion of a carbon dioxide removal system; and
   (3) a process for removing and replacing an adsorbing media that is portion of an adsorbing assembly incorporated in a carbon dioxide removal system in a spacecraft.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view of a carbon dioxide removal system.
   Solenoid (1)
   Pump (2)
   Desiccant canister (4)
   Heater (5)
   Dust filter (7)

TI New catalyst i.e. cerium metal oxide complex useful for preparing glycerol carbonate from glycerol and carbon dioxide.
AU HE D
   LIU J
AE UNIV TSINGHUA (UYQI-C)
GA 201817814N
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst i.e. cerium metal oxide complex (I) are new. The preparation method of glycerol carbonate from glycerol and carbon dioxide using catalyst comprises e.g. (i) adding (I), glycerol, water-absorbing agent and solvent to reaction kettle, (ii) sealing the reaction kettle, then adding carbon dioxide into reaction vessel, heating reaction vessel in a supporting heating furnace, and (iii) reacting with stirring, cooling and reducing high-pressure carbon dioxide emissions from kettle to atmospheric pressure, adding standard amount, mixing, taking out mixture and centrifuging.
   USE - (I) are useful for producing glycerol carbonate from glycerol and carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I): have high raw material conversion rate and target product selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Catalyst i.e. cerium metal oxide complex of formula (Ce(1-x)MxO2) (I) are new. The preparation method of glycerol carbonate from glycerol and carbon dioxide using catalyst comprises (i) adding (I), glycerol, water-absorbing agent and solvent to reaction kettle, (ii) sealing the reaction kettle, then replacing air in the reaction vessel by carbon dioxide, adding carbon dioxide into reaction vessel to certain pressure, heating reaction vessel in a supporting heating furnace to target temperature, and (iii) reacting with stirring for certain period of time, cooling the reactor to less than 5 degrees C and reducing high-pressure carbon dioxide emissions from kettle to atmospheric pressure, open the kettle cover, adding standard amount, mixing and taking out the mixture and centrifuging and quantitative analysis of liquid using gas chromatography.
   M = Zr, Zn, Mn, Pr, or Y; and
   x = 0.01-0.1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent is common organic solvent including N,N-dimethylformamide. The mass ratio between amount of catalyst and amount of glycerol is 0.370-0.565. Preferred Method: The pressurizing condition: carbon dioxide pressure of 4-5.5 MPa. The heating conditions are: reaction temperature is 130-190 degrees C. The reaction time is 5 hours.

TI Carbon dioxide collecting absorbing agent with            secondary phase change function comprises organic            alcohol amine, double-changing driving agent, and rest            is auxiliary agent, carbon dioxide absorbent can form            two liquid phase systems which are incompatible with            each other.
AU WANG S
   LONG Q
   SHEN S
AE UNIV HEBEI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYKJ-C)
GA 2022455706
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide absorbing agent with secondary                phase change function comprises 20-70 wt.% organic                alcohol amine, 20-70 wt.% double-changing driving                agent, and the rest is auxiliary agent, the carbon                dioxide absorbent can form two liquid phase systems                which are incompatible with each other and clear                phase interface in the process of absorbing wet gas                and desorption process containing carbon dioxide,                the humidity in the wet gas is moisture or water                vapor, the organic alcohol amine is mixture of                primary amine, secondary amine, the double-variable                driving agent is mixture of aliphatic alcohol                ether, aromatic alcohol ether, auxiliary agent                water, sodium metasilicate, potassium metavanadate,                sodium bisulfite, normal butanol, tertiary butyl                alcohol, hexanol, and cyclohexanol.
   USE - Carbon dioxide absorbing agent with secondary                phase change function, where the carbon dioxide is                used in coal-fired power plant, steel plant, cement                plant and other energy and industrial                departments.
   ADVANTAGE - The absorbent generates secondary phase change                separating and enriching process, reducing the                desorbing solution amount, reducing regeneration                energy consumption, at the same time, solving the                key problem of water balance regulation and control                in the trapping system, ensuring the long-term                stability of the absorbent composition and trapping                performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for using carbon dioxide absorbing agent with                secondary phase change function, inolving:
   a. introducing mixed wet gas containing carbon                dioxide into the absorption tower, for collecting                carbon dioxide absorbing agent with secondary phase                change function contact purifying gas remove                dioxide is discharged from the absorption tower,                absorbent is single phase solution before absorbing                carbon dioxide, after absorbing, generating the                first phase deformation into incompatible light and                heavy two liquid phase system, desorbing the heavy                liquid phase containing carbon dioxide absorbing                product in the desorption tower;
   b. condensing the tower top vapor phase by the                condenser to obtain purified carbon dioxide,                deforming condensed liquid obtained from the                condenser into two incompatible liquid-phase                systems for the second time, mixing light liquid                phase obtained by the first phase change, barren                liquid phase after desorbing heavy liquid phase and                poor water phase of condensed liquid in the mixing                tank and using as absorbent to recycle, where the                wet moisture control unit comprises a moisture                measuring module and discharge water control                component; and
   c. measuring the wet value of the secondary                phase change separating function absorbent before                entering absorption tower, adjusting the discharge                water flow in the collecting system, and ensuring                the stability of the absorbent composition and                performance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic alcohol                  amine is mixture of 2-amino ethanol,                  iminodiacetic ethanol, 2-(methylamino) ethanol,                  2-(ethylamino) ethanol, 2-(propylamino) ethanol,                  2-(isopropylamino) ethanol, 2-(butylamino)                  ethanol. The double-change driving agent is                  ethylene glycol monobutyl ether, diethylene                  glycol monobutyl ether, triethylene glycol                  monobutyl ether, propylene glycol monobutyl                  ether, ethylene glycol n-amyl ether, diethylene                  glycol n-amyl ether, ethylene glycol monohexyl                  ether, diethylene glycol monohexyl ether,                  triethylene glycol monohexyl ether, propylene                  glycol monohexyl ether, ethylene glycol                  monoheptyl ether, ethylene glycol phenyl ether,                  propylene glycol phenyl ether. The carbon dioxide                  absorbent is a liquid-liquid two-phase system                  formed by absorbing wet gas containing carbon                  dioxide, where the one phase is the heavy liquid                  phase enriched with carbon dioxide absorbing                  product, the other phase is the light liquid                  phase loaded with micro carbon dioxide, the                  desorption process is formed in the liquid-liquid                  two-phase system, where the one phase is enriched                  with organic alcohol amine, double-variable                  driving agent, mixture of auxiliary agent, and                  the other phase is rich in water phase. The mixed                  wet gas containing carbon dioxide comprises                  fossil fuel combustion power plant flue gas,                  natural gas, cement plant waste gas, biogas,                  chemical production exhaust gas. Preferred                  Conditions: The wet water control unit is                  installed on the mixing tank of the online                  moisture meter signal feedback to automatically                  adjust the water phase outlet of the condensed                  liquid of the desorption tower top of the                  electromagnetic valve, and realizing the water                  balance control of the trapping system. The                  absorbing temperature of the absorbing tower is                  30-60&#8451;, the absorbing pressure is                  0.1-1.0 megaPascal, the temperature of the first                  phase change separator is 10-70&#8451;, the                  temperature of the desorption tower is                  90-140&#8451;, the desorption pressure is 0.1                  megaPascal, the temperature of the second phase                  change separator is 10-50&#8451;.

TI Carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction catalyst,            a nano-catalyst, specifically nickel-copper sheet            double-layer oxide having sheet structure of nickel            oxide and copper oxide composite, or nickel layer of            nickel oxide and tin oxide sheet.
AU XU W
   LV T
   RUAN M
   SONG P
   ZHANG Y
   YANG F
AE CHANGCHUN APPLIED CHEM INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAC-C)
GA 2018837230
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction                catalyst, is claimed. The catalyst is a                nano-catalyst, specifically a nickel-copper sheet                double-layer oxide having a sheet structure of                nickel oxide and copper oxide composite, or a                nickel layer of nickel oxide and tin oxide                composite sheet-tin sheet double metal oxide                structure.
   USE - Using carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction                catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The electrochemical reduction catalyst:                provide a preparation method of the catalyst and a                gas diffusion electrode supporting the catalyst,                which can solve the problem that the reduction                current is too low, effectively improve the Faraday                efficiency in the electrochemical reduction process                of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparation of the carbon dioxide                electrochemical reduction catalyst comprising                dissolving the copper chloride or tin chloride, and                nickel chloride in deionized water, stirring,                dropwise adding aqueous ammonia to adjust the pH of                the solution to co-precipitate the above metal                ions, centrifuging, and washing with deionized                water and absolute ethanol until neutral, drying,                and carrying out heat-treatment to obtain a                nickel-copper sheet double metal oxide or nickel                tin sheet double metal oxide;
   (2) gas diffusion electrode for loading the                carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction catalyst                comprising the gas diffusion electrode having a                size of 0.4 cmx 0.4-6 cmx 6 cm, and                electrochemically charging the supported carbon                dioxide, and the weight of the reduction catalyst                is from 0.005-20 mg; and
   (3) preparation of gas diffusion electrode for                supporting a carbon dioxide electrochemical                reduction catalyst comprising dissolving the carbon                dioxide electrochemical reduction catalyst into                ethanol, adding 1-5 wt.% perfluorosulfonic acid                resin Nafion solution, stirring the ultrasonic                wave, dispersing the carbon dioxide electrochemical                reduction catalyst uniformly, and then applying the                obtained mixed gas solution to the dispersing the                electrode, drying, to obtain a gas diffusion                electrode supporting the above carbon dioxide                electrochemical reduction catalyst or mixing the                carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction catalyst                with a carbon material, dispersing the mixture into                ethanol, adding 1-5 wt.% perfluorosulfonic acid                resin Nafion solution, stirring the ultrasonic                wave, dispersing the carbon dioxide electrochemical                reduction catalyst uniformly, and then obtained                mixed solution is applying to a gas diffusion                electrode and dried to obtain a gas diffusion                electrode supporting the above carbon dioxide                electrochemical reduction catalyst. The gas                diffusion electrode is a glass carbon sheet, carbon                paper, carbon cloth or carbon felt, the amount of                the ethanol is 0.005-10 ml, the carbon material is                activated carbon VLCAN X-72, BP200, carbon                nanotube, carbon nanofiber, carbon nanocage,                graphene, graphene quantum dots or graphene oxide,                and the amount of which is from 0.01-100 mg.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the method, the pH is                  7.5-14, and the temperature of the heat treatment                  is 250-750 degrees C, and the heat treatment time                  is 0.5-4 hours. In the method, the pH is 9, the                  heat treatment temperature is 350-450 degrees C,                  and the heat treatment time is 3 hours. Preferred                  Components: The mol ratio of the nickel oxide to                  the copper oxide is 9:1-1:9, and the mol ratio of                  the nickel oxide to the tin oxide is 9:1-1:9. The                  mol ratio of the nickel oxide to the copper oxide                  is 8:2. The mol ratio of the copper chloride to                  the nickel chloride is 1:9-9:1, the mol ratio of                  tin chloride of nickel chloride to nickel                  chloride is 1:9-9:1. The mol ratio of the copper                  chloride to the nickel chloride is 2:8.

   (2) preparing the supercritical carbon dioxide                foam system, comprising: introducing carbon dioxide                into high temperature and high pressure reactor                filled with foaming liquid, stirring at                60-120degrees Celsius for 3-10 minutes under                pressure of 7.5-15 MPa and stirring speed of                500-3000 revolutions/minute, preferably at                70degrees Celsius for 5 minutes under pressure of 8                MPa and stirring speed of 2000                revolutions/minute.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amphoteric                  surfactant comprises tetradecyl hydroxyl sulfo                  betaine and/or hexadecyl hydroxy sulfo betaine.                  The hydrophilic silica sol has 10-50 wt.%,                  preferably 30 wt.% silicon dioxide particles. A                  particle diameter of the silicon dioxide                  particles in the hydrophilic silica sol is 5-30                  mum. A salinity of the salt water is 1x104                  mg/L-25x104 mg/L. The salt water comprises sodium                  chloride and calcium chloride in a mass ratio of                  5-10:1, preferably 9:1. A mass ratio of foaming                  agent, foam stabilizing agent, sodium chloride,                  calcium chloride and water is                  0.1-1:0.01-0.1:1.8-22.5:0.2-2.5:100.

PT P
PN IN201941050835-A
TI Slow release fertilizer composite used as fertilizer, comprises fertilizer and coating material that is soluble in liquid or supercritical carbon dioxide.
AU RAVEENDRAN P
   ANTONY A
AE UNIV CALICUT (UYCA-Non-standard)
GA 202169800S
AB    NOVELTY - Slow release fertilizer composite comprises: a fertilizer; and a coating material that is soluble in liquid or supercritical carbon dioxide, where the coating material forms a glassy coating and/or a crystalline coating on the fertilizer, and the fertilizer and the coating material are mixed in any molar ratio.
   USE - The composite is useful as fertilizer. No biological data given.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite: utilizes a green solvent platform for the preparation of various fertilizer composites with coating materials that are highly soluble in the carbon dioxide-solvents; and is environment friendly and economically viable.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing a slow release fertilizer composite, comprising (a) mixing a fertilizer with a coating material in a high pressure cell to obtain a mixture, (b) introducing gaseous, liquid, or supercritical carbon dioxide to the mixture obtained in step (a) to attain a desired pressure and continuing the mixing in carbon dioxide medium and (c) venting off the carbon dioxide from the carbon dioxide medium of step (b) to obtain the composite.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The composite optionally comprises carbon dioxide. The fertilizer comprises urea, ammonium nitrate, potassium nitrate, calcium nitrate and/or their other fertilizers. The coating material comprises sugar acetates ad/or their derivatives. The sugar acetates comprise sucrose octaacetate, alpha -D-glucose pentaacetate and/or their other sugar acetates. Preferred Process: The mixing of step (c) is continued for a time period for 5 minutes to few hours. The desired pressure is 60-100 bar.
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - Initially the view cell of the carbon dioxide reactor was loaded with urea and coating material (sucrose octaacetate or alpha -D-glucose pentaacetate) in the weight ratio 1:20, followed by bringing the cell to a desired temperature of 35 degrees C. Upon attaining the desired temperature, carbon dioxide was introduced into the view cell, at a controlled flow rate of 2 ml/minutes, to attain the desired pressure of 80 bar along with vigorous stirring of the contents inside the cell. Once the desired pressure was achieved, stirring was continued for another 30 minutes to ensure effective mixing of the urea and the coating material. Finally, carbon dioxide was vent off with the aid of back pressure regulator at a constant rate of 1 bar/minutes and the obtained fertilizer composite was collected for further characterizations.

PN CN108841036-A; CN108841036-B
TI Preparing carbon dioxide responsive ordered porous film material comprises e.g. dissolving polymer and carbon dioxide responsive compound in solvent to prepare uniform solution, and dripping solution to the substrate surface.
AU YIN H
   FENG Y
   ZHAN F
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 201894339U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon dioxide responsive ordered porous film material comprises (i) dissolving polymer and carbon dioxide responsive compound in the solvent to prepare the uniform solution, (ii) dripping the prepared solution to the substrate surface, placing the substrate on the spin coater rotating platform, (iii) introducing air into the coater, blowing the air flow above the solution, controlling air speed temperature and relative humidity of the coater chamber, and starting the spin and controlling the rotating speed, and (iv) fully volatilizing the solvent, stopping spin coater rotation, and taking out the substrate to obtain the ordered porous film material.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing carbon dioxide responsive ordered porous film material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, and quick, and can simply adjust preparation parameters to prepare ordered porous film material with different apertures and different carbon dioxide response degree.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for carbon dioxide responsiveness ordered porous film material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide-responsive compound is one of compounds containing at least one responsive group, and responsive group is at least one of a primary amine, a secondary amine, and a tertiary amine group. The carbon dioxide-responsive compound is 2,4,6-tri (N,N-dimethyl-1,3-propanediamine)-1,3,5-triazine. The solvent is dichloromethane, trichloromethane, vulcanized carbon, tetrahydrofuran, benzene or toluene. The substrate is glass, silicon sheet or polyester film. In step (iii), the air speed is 100-300 l/hour, the spin chamber temperature is 20-28 degrees C and the relative humidity is 50-90%, the rotating speed of the spin coater is 0-50 revolutions/minute. The polymer solution concentration in step (i) is 5-50 mg/ml, the carbon dioxide responsive compound concentration is 0.05-1.5 mg/ml, and the mass ratio of carbon dioxide response compound and polymer is 0.001-0.1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: Polymer is polylactic acid, polycaprolactone, or polystyrene. Polymer is polylactic acid.

TI Method for co-processing hydrogen-containing tail gas of various types of hydrogen production units involves mixing first retentate gas and second retentate gas and entered into second hydrogen membrane for separation, third retentate gas is continued to undergo third hydrogen membrane separation.

   DAI Y
   MI P
   YANG X
   HE G
   GUO M
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY PANJIN IND TECHNO (UYDA-Non-standard)
GA 202222172V
AB    NOVELTY - Method for co-processing hydrogen-containing tail gas of various types of hydrogen production units, involves i. carrying out first-stage carbon dioxide (CO2) membrane separation in sequence on the hydrogen production and desorption gas from natural gas to obtain the first retentate gas and the first permeate gas, and the first permeate gas is continued to undergo the second-stage CO2 membrane separation to obtain the CO2 gas and the first exhaust; ii. Subjecting reformed desorption gas to the first hydrogen membrane separation to obtain hydrogen (H2) product and the second retentate gas, and mixing the first retentate gas and the second retentate gas and then entered into the second hydrogen membrane for separation to obtain rich hydrogen gas and third retentate gas; iii. third retentate gas is continued to undergo a third hydrogen membrane separation to obtain a fuel gas product and a second permeate gas.
   USE - Method for co-processing hydrogen-containing tail gas of various types of hydrogen production units.
   ADVANTAGE - Method and the system realize the coordination of the hydrogen-containing tail gas recovery process of various hydrogen production devices through a specific process and the same treatment is carried out, so that the gas flows is mutually promoted, the purity and recovery rate of the hydrogen product and the carbon dioxide production rate are jointly improved, and meanwhile, the heat value of the fuel gas is in a reasonable heat value interval.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for system for carrying out the co-processing of hydrogen-containing tail gas of various types of hydrogen production devices has a first-stage carbon dioxide (CO2) membrane module (M-1). Second-stage CO2 membrane module (M-2), first hydrogen membrane module (M-5). The second hydrogen membrane module (M-3) and the third hydrogen membrane module (M-4). The raw material inlet of the natural gas to hydrogen desorption gas is communicated with the inlet of the first-stage CO2 membrane module (M-1). The permeate-side outlet of the first-stage CO2 membrane module (M-1) communicates with the inlet of the second-stage CO2 membrane module (M-2). The raw material inlet of the reformed analytical gas is communicated with the inlet of the first hydrogen membrane module (M-5). The retentate side outlet of the first hydrogen membrane module (M-5) is communicated with the inlet of the second hydrogen membrane module (M-3), the retentate-side outlet of the second hydrogen membrane module (M-3) communicates with the inlet of the third hydrogen membrane module (M-4).

Conditions for the first-stage CO2 membrane separation includes a temperature of 30-95 degreesC and a pressure of 1.5-3.0 megapascal; conditions for the second-stage CO2 membrane separation include: a temperature of 30-95 degreesC and a pressure of 1.5-3.0 megapascal; conditions for the first hydrogen membrane separation include: a temperature of 50-95 degreesC and a pressure of 2.0-4.0 megapascal (MPa), conditions for the second hydrogen membrane separation includes a temperature of 50-95 degreesC and a pressure of 2.0-4.0 MPa, conditions for the third hydrogen membrane separation includes a temperature of 50-95 degreesC and a pressure of 2.0-4.0 MPa. Preferred Condition: Hydrogen concentration in the hydrogen production desorption gas is 10-50 vol.%, the hydrogen concentration in the reformed desorption gas is 30-80 vol.%, the hydrogen concentration in the gas product is 1-20 vol.%, hydrogen concentration in the hydrogen-rich gas is 50-90 vol.%, hydrogen concentration in the hydrogen product is 80-99 vol.%, the carbon dioxide concentration is 90-99 wt.%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: Second-stage carbon dioxide (CO2) membrane module are rubbery polymers such as polydimethylsiloxane and/or glassy polymers such as polyimide. PN EP3819357-A1; US2021139794-A1; IN201921045807-A; ZA202006924-A
TI Conversion of residue hydrocarbon feedstock into hydrogen and light olefins for e.g. ethylene, involves e.g. cracking residue hydrocarbon feedstock in riser reactor, and sending cracked hydrocarbon containing olefin to main fractionators.
AU MUKTHIYAR S
   SAIDULU G
   BHUYAN M K
   GUPTA K
   SAU M
   KAPUR G S
   RAMAKUMAR S S V
AE INDIAN OIL CORP LTD (INOI-C)
   INDIAN OIL CORP LTD (INOI-C)
   INDIAN OIL CORP LTD (INOI-C)
   INDIAN OIL CORP LTD (INOI-C)
GA 2021505447
AB    NOVELTY - Conversion of residue hydrocarbon feedstock (1) into hydrogen and light olefins involves (a) cracking the residue hydrocarbon feedstock in a riser reactor (3) in the presence of a multifunctional microspherical catalyst from a reformer to obtain a reaction mixture comprising cracked hydrocarbon (5), hydrocarbon gas, hydrogen, and a coked catalyst, (b) separating the coked catalyst and the cracked hydrocarbons obtained from the step (a) at the end of the riser reactor and transferring the coked catalyst to a regenerator, (c) sending the cracked hydrocarbons obtained after separation in the step (b) to a main fractionator for fractionation, (d) regenerating the coked catalyst of the step (b) in upper portion of the regenerator by burning carbon, hydrogen, sulfur, metal, and nitrogen deposit on the coked catalyst in presence of oxygen containing gases to obtain a regenerated catalyst.
   USE - Conversion of residue hydrocarbon feedstock into hydrogen and light olefins such as ethylene, propylene and butylenes.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables conversion of residue hydrocarbon feedstock into hydrogen and light olefins with high yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Conversion of residue hydrocarbon feedstock (1) into hydrogen and light olefins involves (a) cracking the residue hydrocarbon feedstock in a riser reactor (3) in the presence of a multifunctional microspherical catalyst from a reformer to obtain a reaction mixture comprising cracked hydrocarbon (5), hydrocarbon gas, hydrogen, and a coked catalyst, (b) separating the coked catalyst and the cracked hydrocarbons obtained from the step (a) at the end of the riser reactor and transferring the coked catalyst to a regenerator, (c) sending the cracked hydrocarbons obtained after separation in the step (b) to a main fractionator for fractionation, (d) regenerating the coked catalyst of the step (b) in upper portion of the regenerator by burning carbon, hydrogen, sulfur, metal, and nitrogen deposit on the coked catalyst in presence of oxygen containing gases to obtain a regenerated catalyst, (e) removing entrapped flue gas from the regenerated catalyst of the step (d) at the lower portion of the regenerator using carbon dioxide as a stripping medium to obtain a stripped catalyst, (f) transferring the stripped catalyst of the step (e) to a reformer via a transporting pipe or a lift line using carbon dioxide as a lifting medium, (g) dry reforming the hydrocarbon gases to synthesis gas in the reformer using carbon dioxide and in presence of the stripped catalyst, (h) recirculating the catalyst from the reformer to the riser reactor and to the upper portion of the regenerator, (i) sending the synthesis gas from the step (g) to a shift reactor for converting carbon monoxide in the synthesis gas to carbon dioxide and hydrogen through water gas shift reaction, and sending the carbon dioxide, hydrogen and unconverted hydrocarbons to a pressure swing adsorption (PSA) unit for hydrogen separation and recycling back the off-gas from the PSA unit to the reformer. The cracked hydrocarbon comprises 20-40 wt.% 2-4C olefins and 2-6 wt.% total hydrogen on a fresh hydrocarbon feedstock basis. The feedstock has a feed CCR of more than 5 wt.%.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the conversion of a residue hydrocarbon feedstock into hydrogen and light olefin.
   Residue hydrocarbon feedstock (1)
   Riser reactor (3)
   Steam (4)
   Cracked hydrocarbon (5)
   Catalyst standpipe (6)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The multifunctional micro spherical catalyst comprises 5-15 wt.% ultra-stable Y zeolite, 2-10 wt.% pentasil zeolite which is shape selective, 1-5 wt.% active material which is bottom selective, 0.1-2 wt.% rare earth component and 60-85 wt.% support material. Preferred Process: The cracking of residue hydrocarbon feedstock in the step (a) is performed at a temperature of 550-650 degrees C, a weight hourly space velocity (WHSV) of 40-100 hour-1, the weight ratio of a catalyst with respect to hydrocarbon of 10-25, a pressure of 1-5 kg/cm2g, and the weight ratio of a steam (4) with respect to the hydrocarbon of 0.1-1.0. The dry reforming of hydrocarbon gases to synthesis gas in the step (g) is performed at a temperature of 650-800 degrees C and WHSV of 0.5-10 hour-1. The method further involves recirculating the catalyst from the reformer to riser reactor for cracking of hydrocarbons and to the upper portion of the regenerator to control the regenerator catalyst temperature, injecting the synthesis gas or fuel gas at the middle of the transporting line or lift line of the step (f) for reducing the oxidation state of metals on the catalyst, and recirculating the regenerated catalyst to the riser reactor for cracking of hydrocarbons and for stripping. The temperature of the regenerator is 50-100 degrees C which is more than the temperature in the reformer. Preferred Components: The hydrocarbon gas comprises methane, ethane, or natural gas.

TI Performing bottled industrialized cultivation of            Pleurotus nebrodensis involves preparing culture            material, bottling, inoculating, culturing,            maintaining, excavating, maintaining, cultivating and            thinning buds.
AU HUANG Q
   LIU Y
   XU X
   ZHOU W
AE SHAOGUAN STARWAY BIOTECH CO LTD (SHAO-Non-standard)
   XICHONG XINGHE BIOLOGICAL TECHNOLOGY CO (XICH-Non-standard)
GA 202038170B
AB    NOVELTY - Method for performing bottled industrialized                cultivation of Pleurotus nebrodensis involves (a)                preparing culture material and bottling, (b) 45-50                days after inoculation, culturing at 19-22 degrees                C, controlling water content after sterilizing                culture material at 65-67%, controlling the                relative humidity of the air at 70-80% and                controlling the carbon dioxide concentration at                3000-3500, (c) after the mycelium cultivation is                completed, controlling the room temperature of the                cold stimulation process at 1-4 degrees C, air                relative humidity at 70-80% and carbon dioxide                concentration at 1500-2500 ppm, where the cold                stimulation time is 7-14 days, (d) excavating                aerial mycelium and the cultivation material near                the edge of the cultivation bottle, and (e) moving                the cultivation bottle to the cultivation rack in a                growth chamber, maintaining, cultivating, thinning                buds and using a knife to cut remaining portion of                the mushrooms.
   USE - The method is useful for performing bottled                industrialized cultivation of P.nebrodensis.
   ADVANTAGE - The method cultivates P.nebrodensis with high                quality, is suitable for industrial production                under the condition of scratching a new mode and                greatly solves the annual commercial production                problem.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for performing bottled industrialized                cultivation of Pleurotus nebrodensis involves (a)                preparing culture material and bottling by using                45% corn core, 20% bran, 18% cotton seed hull, 15%                rice bran and 2% shellfish fossil to prepare                culture material, selecting cultivating bottle                capacity of 850 cc and calibre of 6.8-7.5 cm,                charging and sterilizing, (b) 45-50 days after                inoculation, culturing at 19-22 degrees C,                controlling water content after sterilizing culture                material at 65-67%, controlling the relative                humidity of the air at 70-80% and controlling the                carbon dioxide concentration at 3000-3500, (c)                after the mycelium cultivation is completed,                controlling the room temperature of the cold                stimulation process at 1-4 degrees C, air relative                humidity at 70-80% and carbon dioxide concentration                at 1500-2500 ppm, where the cold stimulation time                is 7-14 days, (d) excavating aerial mycelium and                the cultivation material near the edge of the                cultivation bottle, where the width of the                excavation area is 8-10 mm and the depth of the                excavation area is 3-5 mm, removing 1-2 mm thick                cultivation material only during the excavation                process, and when no cultivated material is                removed, maintaining the middle portion of the                material surface without any treatment, and (e)                moving the cultivation bottle to the cultivation                rack in a growth chamber, maintaining the bottle                opening horizontally placed, controlling the room                temperature at 17-19 degrees C for the entire                growth process, the relative humidity of the air at                90-95% and the concentration of carbon dioxide at                1000-1500 ppm, using a scattering light source of                200-300 Lx to illuminate for 12-18 hours/day,                cultivating for 18-22 days, thinning buds to keep a                small mushroom with a height of 10-15 mm, a round                mushroom shape and no damage in the small buds on                the surface of the material, and using a knife to                cut remaining portion of the mushrooms.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method further                  involves harvesting mushroom fruiting portion and                  screening to obtain mushrooms with round or oval                  cap, weight of more than 20 g and length of 14-17                  cm.

PN IN201721032190-A
TI Additive used in cement composition, is obtained by mixing e.g. monobasic amine and dibasic amine, bubbling mixture of carbon dioxide and oxygen gases to obtained amine mixture, and adding alcoholic solution to obtained amine solution.
AU KAMAT M K
AE MADAN K K (MADA-Individual)
GA 201926170Q
AB    NOVELTY - A cement additive is obtained by performing method (100) which involves mixing monobasic amine, dibasic amine and benzyl amine to form amine mixture, charging amine mixture in reaction kettle and bubbling mixture of carbon dioxide and oxygen gases for 3-9 hours while maintaining at 25-55 degrees C to obtain partially neutralized amine solution, adding 15-35 %w/w of alcoholic solution to amine solution and continuing bubbling of carbon dioxide and oxygen gases till completion of neutralization to produce amine mixture (A) in powder form, mixing monobasic amine and dibasic amine, charging amine mixture to reaction kettle and neutralizing amine mixture by slowly adding mixture of carboxylic acids for 50-160 minutes and adding mixture of methanol and acetone to produce amine mixture (B) in powder form, and mixing mixtures (A) and (B) and encapsulating mixture with encapsulation material which is precipitated silica, nano-titanium dioxide, coarse silica, talc, lime, barium sulfate and/or slag.
   USE - Additive is used in cement composition (claimed) e.g. concrete and mortar, for mitigating lump formation.
   ADVANTAGE - The cement additive is capable of overcoming prehydration of cement for retaining its intrinsic mechanical and chemical characteristics. The cement additive facilitates easier transport of the cement through train wagon or road. The ionized amines and their salts in the cement additive has high electron density, and are capable of repelling water vapor from cement grains thereby rendering the cement free from coagulation and lump formation in fresh condition due to moisture.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A cement additive is obtained by mixing a monobasic amine, a dibasic amine and benzyl amine, in weight ratio of 73:29:90-33:49:103 to form amine mixture, charging the amine mixture in a reaction kettle and bubbling a mixture of carbon dioxide and oxygen gases for 3-9 hours while maintaining at 25-55 degrees C to obtain a partially neutralized amine solution, adding 15-35 %w/w of alcoholic solution to the partially neutralized amine solution and continuing bubbling of carbon dioxide and oxygen gases till completion of neutralization to produce amine mixture (A) in powder form, mixing monobasic amine and dibasic amine, in weight ratio of 90:10-27:95, charging the amine mixture to a reaction kettle and neutralizing the amine mixture by slowly adding a mixture of carboxylic acids for 50-160 minutes and adding a mixture of methanol and acetone, in weight ratio of 10:1-20:5 to produce amine mixture (B) in powder form, and mixing mixtures (A) and (B) in weight ratio of 100:60 %w/w to 100:0 %w/w and encapsulating the mixture with encapsulation material which is precipitated silica, nano titania, coarse silica, talc, lime, barium sulfate and/or slag to produce a dry and free-flowing cement additive powder. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for cement composition, which is prepared by blending 99.99-99.95 wt.% cement with 0.01-0.05 wt.% cement additive.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of manufacture of the cement additive.
   Formation of cement additive (100)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mixture of amine mixture (A) and amine mixture (B) is encapsulated with encapsulation mixture of lime, nano titania, barium sulfate and slag having size of more than 9 microns, in weight ratio of 13:2:80:15-18:2:40:45, preferably mixture of precipitated silica, nano titania and coarse silica powder, in weight ratio of 75:3:5-10 to 95:5:20.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The monobasic amine is monocyclohexylamine (MCHA). The dibasic amine is dicyclohexylamine. The alcoholic solution contains methyl alcohol and ethyl alcohol in weight ratio of 22:33-10:47. The mixture of carboxylic acids contains benzoic acid, salicylic acid and cinnamic acid in weight ratio of 40:35:9-32:38:40.

PN CN114037278-A
TI Low carbonization treatment evaluation method for large industrial user, involves establishing index system, performing index property division to index in index system, where index system comprises photovoltaic power consumption ratio.
AU YANG Y

   ZHANG Y
   GUAN Y
   GAO X
AE STATE GRID CORP CHINA (SGCC-C)
   STATE GRID LIAONING ELECTRIC POWER SUPPL (SGCC-C)
GA 202235521C
AB    NOVELTY - Low carbonization treatment evaluation method involves establishing an index system, performing index property division to index in index system, where the index system comprises photovoltaic power consumption ratio, wind power generation consumption ratio, photoelectric device utilization rate, wind power device utilization rate, carbon dioxide emission rate, carbon dioxide emission reduction rate, carbon emission reduction device utilization rate, coal saving amount, unit industrial added value power consumption, unit industrial added value new energy consumption, saving fuel cost, the index property comprises positive correlation and negative correlation, determining the subjective weight of each index by using the ordinal analysis method, determining the objective weight of each index by using the variation coefficient method, using the subjective weight.
   USE - Low carbonization treatment evaluation method used for large industrial user and to a power plant, a power generation plant, an electric power generation station, a water supply, a gas distribution system and a power distribution system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method overcome the distribution problem of different large industrial users on different levels, but also can take the subjective and objective weight. Overcomes the uncertainty and fuzziness of large industrial treatment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Low carbonization treatment evaluation method involves establishing an index system, performing index property division to index in index system, where the index system comprises photovoltaic power consumption ratio, wind power generation consumption ratio, photoelectric device utilization rate, wind power device utilization rate, carbon dioxide emission rate, carbon dioxide emission reduction rate, carbon emission reduction device utilization rate, coal saving amount, unit industrial added value power consumption, unit industrial added value new energy consumption, saving fuel cost, the index property comprises positive correlation and negative correlation, determining the subjective weight of each index by using the ordinal analysis method, determining the objective weight of each index by using the variation coefficient method, using the subjective weight and objective weight to calculate the combined weight of each index and using TOPSIS comprehensive evaluation method to evaluate the evaluation object.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The method involves constructing the structure of index system hierarchical level, dividing into target layer, criterion layer and index layer, where the target layer is large industrial user low carbon treatment evaluation index system, the criterion layer is divided into three, respectively is new energy dissipation, carbon emission and enterprise electricity utilization efficiency, the index layer respectively photovoltaic power consumption ratio, wind power generation consumption ratio, photoelectric device utilization rate, wind power device utilization rate, carbon dioxide emission, carbon dioxide emission reduction rate, carbon emission reduction device utilization rate, coal saving amount, unit industrial added value power consumption, the unit industry added value energy consumption, saving fuel cost. The photovoltaic power consumption ratio, wind power generation consumption ratio, photoelectric device utilization rate, wind power device utilization rate, carbon dioxide emission reduction rate, carbon emission reduction device utilization rate, coal saving amount, saving fuel cost index property is defined as positive correlation, the carbon dioxide emission, unit industrial added value power consumption. The index property of the unit industrial added value new energy consumption is defined as negative correlation. The method involves determining the subjective weight of each index by using the ordinal analysis method, subjectively defining each index sequence relation and obtaining the index set, subjectively defining importance degree between adjacent indexes in the index set, and setting index scale rk, where the k is the subjective weight of the k-th index, using the index scale to determine the subjective weight of each index. The method involves using the evaluation object to obtain the original index data matrix, where the n represents the evaluation object number, xij represents the ith evaluation object j item evaluation index value, calculating the average value and the standard deviation of the j-th evaluation index, calculating the variation coefficient of the j-th evaluation index, performing normalization processing to the variation coefficient and obtaining objective weight of each index. The method involves setting the evaluation value of the ith evaluation object, where the n represents the evaluation object number, wj represents the j-th evaluation index of the combined weight, s'ij is the value of the standard index data matrix, calculating the combined weight, where the n represents the evaluation object number. The method involves dividing the Euclidean distance of the positive ideal solution and negative ideal solution, dividing the large industrial user evaluation treatment level into multiple horizontal levels.

The upper surface of the                middle layer is combined with the lower surface of                the first outer surface layer. The lower surface of                the middle layer is combined with the upper surface                of the second outer surface layer and the first                outer surface layer. The substrate of the middle                layer and the second outer surface layer are the                same. The middle layer is added with a hollow                microsphere (4). The main component of the hollow                microsphere comprises organic silicon. The hollow                microsphere is filled with nitrogen or CO2 gas. TI Catalyst for the catalytic conversion of gasoline, comprises natural minerals, inorganic oxides, composite oxide solid acids and noble metal and prepared by mixing noble metal salt and/or supported noble metal, composite oxide solid acid.
AU WANG L
   ZHOU Z
   SUN M
   LIN W
   TIAN H
   WANG P
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC RES INST PETROLEUM PROCESSING (SNPC-C)
GA 202150874J
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises 15-70 wt.% natural minerals, 5-60 wt.% inorganic oxides, 10-65 wt.% composite oxide solid acids and 0.1-10 wt.% noble metal.
   USE - Catalyst used for the catalytic conversion of gasoline.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has high activity, realizes the catalytic conversion of gasoline and effective utilization of carbon dioxide under very mild condition and produces low-carbon olefin.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing a catalyst, which involves mixing noble metal salt and/or supported noble metal, composite oxide solid acid, natural mineral, inorganic oxide and/or inorganic oxide precursor, spray drying, and then roasting, introducing an auxiliary agent into the catalyst, where the introducing the auxiliary agent includes mixing and crushing the auxiliary agent precursor, noble metal salt and/or supported noble metal, composite oxide solid acid, natural mineral, inorganic oxide and/or inorganic oxide precursor and/or, mixing and crushing the supported noble metals containing additives, composite oxide solid acids, natural minerals, inorganic oxides and/or inorganic oxide precursors; and
   (2) a method for catalytic conversion of gasoline, which involves contacting gasoline and carbon dioxide with a catalyst and optionally a diluent gas to react, where catalyst comprises the catalyst for catalytic conversion of gasoline, the conditions of the contact reaction include: a temperature of 350-700 degrees C, a pressure of 0.1-2 megaPascal, a weight ratio of carbon dioxide and gasoline of 0.22-3:1, and a gasoline mass space velocity of 0.3-10h-1 and the conditions of the contact reaction includes temperature of 400-600 degrees C, a pressure of 0.1-0.3 megaPascal, a weight ratio of carbon dioxide and gasoline of 0.25-2:1, and a gasoline mass space velocity of 0.5-5h-1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The catalyst comprises 20-60 wt.% natural minerals, 10-55 wt.% inorganic oxides, 10-55 wt.% composite oxide solid acids and 0.1-5 wt.% noble metal. The molar ratio of tungsten oxide and zirconium oxide is 0.05-0.5:1. The content of the auxiliary agent is 0.5-9 wt.% based on the oxide. The content of the noble metal is 0.5-20 wt.% in terms of elements. The firing conditions includes firing atmosphere is air, inert or steam atmosphere, the firing temperature is 400-800 degrees C, and the firing time is 0.5-10 hours. Preferred Components: The noble metal is selected from gold, silver, ruthenium, rhodium, palladium, platinum, iridium and osmium. The composite oxide solid acid is selected from two of transition metal oxides. The composite oxide solid acid is selected from two of tungsten oxide, zirconium oxide, molybdenum oxide, titanium oxide, tin oxide, zinc oxide, copper oxide, nickel oxide, cobalt oxide, vanadium oxide and niobium oxide. The natural minerals are selected from kaolin, montmorillonite, diatomaceous earth, attapulgite, sepiolite, halloysite, hydrotalcite bentonite and rectorite. The inorganic oxide is selected from silicon oxide, aluminum oxide, aluminum oxide-silica, zirconium oxide, titanium oxide, boron oxide, amorphous silicon aluminum, aluminum phosphate, tungsten oxide, molybdenum oxide, tin oxide, zinc oxide, copper oxide, nickel oxide, cobalt oxide, vanadium oxide and niobium oxide. The supported noble metal comprises a carrier and a noble metal supported on the carrier. The carrier is selected from aluminum oxide, silicon oxide, aluminum oxide-silicon oxide, zirconium oxide, tungsten oxide, tungsten oxide-zirconia, molybdenum oxide, molybdenum oxide-zirconia, titanium oxide, molybdenum oxide-titanium oxide, tungsten oxide-titanium oxide, tin oxide, zinc oxide, copper oxide, nickel oxide, cobalt oxide, vanadium oxide and niobium oxide. Preferred Composition: The catalyst further contains an auxiliary agent selected from IIA, IIIA, IVA, VA, and lanthanide elements, yttrium, chromium, manganese, rhenium, iron and cadmium.

PN WO2021069763-A1
TI System for generating electrical energy from gravitational force obtained through carbon dioxide pumping process, has discharge unit that is configured for emptying capsule connected to line, so that capsule operates during descent phase.
AU VEGA CAMA S R
AE VEGA CAMA S R (CAMA-Individual)
GA 2021392534
AB    NOVELTY - The system (1a) has an energy conversion unit (7) that is operatively associated with a capsule (3). The energy conversion unit is configured to convert the potential energy resulting from the raising or lowering movement of the capsule into electrical energy. The main portion of the system is adapted to be implemented in a carbon capture and storage facility. A feed line (8) is arranged at a higher level that conducts the carbon dioxide captured from the surface land towards an evacuation line (9) arranged at a lower level that injects carbon dioxide into an underground cavity (10), located under the marine mantle (11) or the terrestrial mantle. A loading unit (5) is configured for filling the capsule connected to supply line. A discharge unit (6) is configured for emptying the capsule connected to evacuation line, so that the capsule operates during its descent phase.
   USE - System for generating electrical energy from gravitational force obtained through carbon dioxide pumping process.
   ADVANTAGE - The system maintains hydrodynamic stability against marine currents, thus avoiding the occurrence of the phenomenon of vortex detachment that can lead to resonance of the assembly. The valve regulates the pressure and prevents seawater from entering the confinement cavity and comes into contact with unextracted hydrocarbons. The receptacle is arranged and spaced predetermined distance below the lower portion of the vertical duct to ensure the evacuation of the volume of water displaced by the capsule during its descent and growing urgency prevents adverse effects on the environment from occurring in an irreversible manner.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the system for generating electrical energy.
   System for generating electrical energy (1a)
   Capsule (3)
   Loading unit (5)
   Discharge unit (6)
   Energy conversion unit (7)
   Feed line (8)
   Evacuation line (9)

   Marine mantle (11)

PN CN112467108-A; CN112467108-B
TI Porous silicon-oxygen composite material used as lithium ion battery negative electrode material, has porous core-shell structure comprising inner core comprising silicon oxide and lithium silicate and coating layer covering surface of core.
AU WANG T
AE UNIV DONGGUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDU-C)
GA 2021257160
AB    NOVELTY - Porous silicon-oxygen composite material has a porous core-shell structure comprising an inner core and a first coating layer covering the surface of the inner core, where the inner core is a microporous structure, the inner core comprises a mixture or composite of nano silicon, silicon oxide and lithium silicate, the components of the lithium silicate comprises lithium orthosilicate, lithium metasilicate and lithium disilicate, the first coating layer is a composite comprising lithium metasilicate and lithium carbonate, and the first coating layer is formed by reaction between lithium orthosilicate in the core and carbon dioxide.
   USE - The porous silicon-oxygen composite material is used as lithium ion battery negative electrode material (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The porous silicon-oxygen composite material solves the application bottleneck of pulping processability while improving the magnification and cycle stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the porous silicon-oxygen composite material, which involves (i) mixing lithium source with silicon oxide or nano silicon with a carbon coating layer on the surface, and performing first calcination in an inert atmosphere or a reducing atmosphere to obtain an initial core formed by a part of lithium-doped silicon oxide, calcining the initial core for a second time in a carbon dioxide atmosphere, so that the first coating layer is formed on the surface of the initial core and the initial core forms a microporous structure, or mixing the lithium source with silicon oxide or nano silicon with a carbon coating layer on the surface, and performing third calcination in a carbon dioxide atmosphere to obtain a microporous structure core and the first coating layer covering the surface of the core, or mixing and granulating partially lithiated doped silicon oxide or nano silicon and lithium orthosilicate, and performing fourth calcination in a carbon dioxide atmosphere to obtain a microporous structure core and the first coating layer covering the surface of the core.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The silicon oxide is of formula: SiOx.x = 0.6-2.The crystal phase structure of the silicon oxide comprises an amorphous state and/or a crystalline state. The surface of the first coating layer is covered with a second coating layer. The second coating layer is a carbon source cracked product. Preferred Composition: The content of lithium metasilicate is less than 5% of the total amount of lithium silicate. The content of lithium orthosilicate before reaction is greater than 50% of the total amount of lithium silicate. In the silicon oxide of the carbon coating layer, the amount of the carbon coating layer is 0.2-10 %mass. Preferred Method: The method further involves (ii) after step (i) placing the product obtained in step (i) in a carbon source gas, or coating the surface of the product obtained in step (i) with a carbon source, increasing the temperature in an inert atmosphere to cause a cracking reaction of the carbon source, so that the surface of the product obtained in step (i) is coated with the second coating layer. Preferred Conditions: The first calcination is carried out at 300-900 degrees C. The second calcination is carried out at 400-700 degrees C. The third calcination is carried out at 400-700 degrees C. The fourth calcination is carried out at 400-700 degrees C. Preferred Properties: The particle size of the inner core is less than or equal to 20 mu m. The micropore diameter in the inner core is less than or equal to 2 mu m.

AB    NOVELTY - Practical carbon dioxide reduction membrane                electrolyzer comprises cathode flow field plate,                cathode gasket, cathode gas diffusion layer (GDL) ,                membrane electrode assembly (MEA), anode gas                diffusion layer (anode GDL), anode gasket, anode                flow field plate assembled in sequence. The MEA                includes a solid polymer electrolyte (SPE) membrane                and a cathode catalyst and an anode catalyst                located on both sides of the SPE membrane, the                cathode flow field plate adopts a serpentine flow                field, and the anode flow field plate adopts a mesh                flow field.
   USE - Used as practical carbon dioxide reduction                membrane electrolyzer.
   ADVANTAGE - The practical carbon dioxide reduction                membrane electrolyzer is simple and safe to                operate; has works efficiently and stable; reaches                higher level in the same field and low bath                pressure can reach hundreds of milliamperes of                current density and selectivity of more than                90%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing membrane electrolyzer, comprising (i)                separately mixing the cathode catalyst and anode                catalyst with the membrane solution in proportion,                and dispersing them uniformly by ultrasound to                obtain catalyst ink, and coating the obtained                catalyst ink on the SPE membrane or the                corresponding GDL to form a catalyst layer, (ii)                placing SPE film or GDL layer coated with the                catalytic layer in 1-2 mol/l potassium hydroxide                solution and exchange overnight, then washing with                pure water multiple times to remove residual lye,                and assemble in the order of cathode flow field                plate, cathode gasket, cathode GDL, MEA, anode GDL,                anode gasket, and anode flow field plate, keeping                surface contact between adjacent parts, and (iii)                connecting the assembled device to the gas-liquid                pipeline, the electrode connection line and the                temperature control device in sequence, the cathode                begins to ventilate, and adding a certain amount of                pure water to the anode water-gas separation tank,                filling the entire anode chamber with water, and                raising to the target temperature to                electrolyze.

The cathode                  catalyst is gold or silver, the anode catalyst is                  iridium, ruthenium, iron, cobalt or nickel oxide                  or platinum-ruthenium/carbon material. The ionic                  polymer material is consistent with the SPE                  membrane material used. TI Carbon dioxide refrigeration system for cooling of refrigeration load, has oil return line that delivers oil from oil separator to suction line, in which oil mixes with refrigerant in suction line before reaching compressors.
AU CHRISTENSEN K G
AE HILL PHOENIX INC (DOVE-C)
GA 201999284B
AB    NOVELTY - The carbon dioxide (CO2) refrigeration system (100) comprises multiple compressors (14) that are configured to circulate CO2 refrigerant within the CO2 refrigeration system. A suction line (17) delivers the CO2 refrigerant to the compressors. An oil separator (31) separates oil from the CO2 refrigerant. An oil return line (33) delivers the oil from the oil separator to the suction line, in which the oil mixes with the CO2 refrigerant in the suction line before reaching the compressors. The oil equalization lines (36) are provided, which each connects one of the compressors to the oil return line. The oil equalization valves (37) are each located along one of the oil equalization lines and configured to control a flow of oil through the oil equalization lines.
   USE - Carbon dioxide refrigeration system for cooling of refrigeration load.
   ADVANTAGE - The controller is configured to open the oil control valve in response to a determination that the measured amount of oil within the suction line is less than an oil threshold and keep the oil control valve closed in response to a determination that the measured amount of the oil within the suction line is greater than or equal to the oil threshold. The oil control system uses a semi-active or semi-passive oil control technique which provides the oil from the oil separate into the refrigerant suction line that feeds into multiple compressors of the CO2 refrigeration system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for operating a CO2 refrigeration system that provides cooling for a refrigeration load using carbon dioxide as a refrigerant.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the block diagram of a CO2 refrigeration system that includes an semi-passive oil control system.

   Suction line (17)

   Oil equalization valves (37)

   (1) an anesthesia inducing device
   (2) an anesthesia machine.

PT P
PN WO2019004957-A2; WO2019004957-A3
TI New enzyme having increased affinity useful for producing formic acid from carbon dioxide.
AU BINAY B
   ASLAN A S
AE UNIV GEBZE TEKNIK (UYGE-Non-standard)
GA 2019017335
AB    NOVELTY - An enzyme i.e. capable of producing formic acid from carbon dioxide, is new.
   USE - The enzyme is useful for producing formic acid from carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The enzyme has an increased affinity, and produces formic acid in a short period of time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An enzyme i.e. capable of producing formic acid from carbon dioxide, is new. The enzyme is obtained by applying RNA isolation kit directive to the Chaetomium thermophilum culture, obtaining the PCR with C.thermophilum cDNA library by using the poly-t primer to obtain cDNA library from the total obtained RNA, designing the atggaaggttttggctgttttgtacgac (SEQ ID NO: Not defined), sequence as the degenerate primer to obtain the C.thermophilum formate dehydrogenase (FDH) nucleotide piece from cDNA, ensuring to obtain the FDH gene, that the degenerate primers does not multiply the sequences not specific to itself and amplification the FDH gene through PCR tool, i.e. designed for obtaining the gene from cDNA in a shorter period of time and with less chemical consumption (obtaining the PCR product), using nucleotide piece belonging to C.thermophilum FDH, i.e. projected to encode FDH, as a mold to convert the nucleotide piece into protein, and multiply it through PCR through the primers, on the ends of which the selected cut-off areas are placed, cloning the multiplied and purified PCR sample, to sequence it with DNA sequence analysis, by adding (5'cccgaaaagtgccacctg'3 (SEQ ID NO: Not defined)) recombinant plasmid with T7 promoter, obtaining the DNA sequence analysis information, designing primer for codon improvement, categorically determining the presence of FDH protein, molecular weight and the number and weight of subunits, if any, through Western blot analysis, following the expression of the C.thermophilum FDH nucleotide piece in Escherichia coli, purifying the C.thermophilum FDH protein with the TAGzyme method, determining the kinetic constants of C.thermophilum FDH, examining the effect of temperature on the C.thermophilum FDH enzyme activity and the stability of FDH against time, and evaluating the results obtained through computational modeling in digital environment and through bio -informatics tools.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preparation (Claimed): The enzyme is obtained by applying RNA isolation kit directive to the C.thermophilum culture, obtaining the PCR with C.thermophilum cDNA library by using the poly-t primer to obtain cDNA library from the total obtained RNA, designing the atggaaggttttggctgttttgtacgac (SEQ ID NO: Not defined), sequence as the degenerate primer to obtain the C.thermophilum formate dehydrogenase (FDH) nucleotide piece from cDNA, ensuring to obtain the FDH gene, that the degenerate primers does not multiply the sequences not specific to itself and amplification the FDH gene through PCR tool, i.e. designed for obtaining the gene from cDNA in a shorter period of time and with less chemical consumption (obtaining the PCR product), using nucleotide piece belonging to C.thermophilum FDH, i.e. projected to encode FDH, as a mold to convert the nucleotide piece into protein, and multiply it through PCR through the primers, on the ends of which the selected cut-off areas are placed, cloning the multiplied and purified PCR sample, to sequence it with DNA sequence analysis, by adding (5'cccgaaaagtgccacctg'3 (SEQ ID NO: Not defined)) recombinant plasmid with T7 promoter, obtaining the DNA sequence analysis information, designing primer for codon improvement, categorically determining the presence of FDH protein, molecular weight and the number and weight of subunits, if any, through Western blot analysis, following the expression of the C.thermophilum FDH nucleotide piece in Escherichia coli, purifying the C.thermophilum FDH protein with the TAGzyme method, determining the kinetic constants of C.thermophilum FDH, examining the effect of temperature on the C.thermophilum FDH enzyme activity and the stability of FDH against time, and evaluating the results obtained through computational modeling in digital environment and through bio -informatics tools. Preferred Biomolecule: The amino acid sequence belonging to C.thermophilum FDH enzyme obtained from the C.thermophilum organism is a 384 amino acid sequence (SEQ ID NO: Not defined) fully defined in the specification.
