PT P
PN WO2018130841-A1
TI Treating soot which is useful for producing water, nitrogen and carbon dioxide and as a sink for or the sequestration of nitrous oxide, comprises oxidizing soot in the presence of nitrous oxide as oxidant.
AU DAVIES C
   GOLUNSKI S
   THOMPSON K
AE UNIV CARDIFF COLLEGE (UYCF-C)
GA 201856540R
AB    NOVELTY - Method (m1) of treating soot comprises oxidizing soot in the presence of nitrous oxide as oxidant to produce at least one of water, nitrogen and carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method: is useful for treating soot which is useful for producing at least one of water, nitrogen and carbon dioxide, and as a sink for or the sequestration of nitrous oxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes nitrous oxide which oxidizes soot, even when an internal combustion engine (e.g. a diesel engine) is operating at relatively low temperatures and when exhaust gas temperature is as low as 150-200 degrees C, and helps to counter current emission controls, thus minimizing the production of nitrous oxide hence prevent green house gas emission; exhibits synergistic effect by enabling both nitric oxides and soot to be treated in a single, integrated unit, thus potentially reducing the weight of the after-treatment system; and treats soot by converting it to gas and water, less soot and/or ash that will be retained on the diesel particulate filter, thus reducing back-pressure increases associated with soot/ash build up and reducing or even eliminating the need for active regeneration, cleaning and/or replacement of the diesel particulate filter, and leading to improved fuel economies.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) use of nitrous oxide to oxidize soot for producing at least one of water, nitrogen and carbon dioxide;
   (2) an exhaust gas after treatment system which is configured to utilize nitrous oxide as an oxidant for the treatment of soot produced from the incomplete combustion of hydrocarbons;
   (3) a method (m2) for sequestering nitrous oxide comprising contacting nitrous oxide with soot so that the nitrous oxide is sequestered within the soot; and
   (4) transportation means, plant machinery, or industrial plant comprising an exhaust gas after treatment system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The transportation means are used for transportation by land, sea or air, e.g. an automobile, boat, plane or train, where the transportation means is diesel powered, and the soot-particulate filter is a diesel particulate filter. The industrial plant is a nitric acid or adipic acid production facility.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method (m1) further comprises a catalyst; and producing the nitrous oxide in a non-selective catalytic reduction process and producing the soot by the incomplete combustion of hydrocarbons in an internal combustion engine. The nitrous oxide is contacted with the soot at not greater than 500 degrees C, preferably optionally greater than or equal to 200 degrees C. The method (m1) is conducted in an exhaust gas after-treatment system. The oxidation reaction is carried out simultaneously with the conversion of nitric oxides, carbon monoxide and fuel derived hydrocarbon to water, carbon dioxide and nitrogen. The oxidation reaction and the simultaneous conversion of nitric oxides, carbon monoxide and fuel derived hydrocarbon are carried out in a single, integrated unit. The method (m2) further comprises causing the sequestered nitrous oxide to oxidize the soot in the presence of an oxidation catalyst for producing water, nitrogen and carbon dioxide, optionally where the water is utilized in the nitric acid or adipic acid production process and/or another process, and/or the carbon dioxide is sequestered and/or stored and/or utilized in another process, and/or nitrogen is sequester and/or stored and/or flared and/or utilized in another process. Preferred Components: The catalyst is at least bifunctional and catalyzes the oxidation reaction or a non-selective catalytic reduction process. The single, integrated unit comprises the catalyst for the oxidation reaction and the simultaneous reduction of nitric oxides, carbon monoxide and fuel derived hydrocarbons, and optionally a soot-particulate filter, optionally where the soot particulate filter is treated, coated or impregnated with the catalyst. The catalyst for catalyzing oxidation reaction comprises silver dispersed on a metal oxide support, where the metal oxide support is ceria-zirconia e.g. ceria-zirconia-alumina. The catalyst for catalyzing the non-selective catalytic reduction process comprises platinum dispersed on a metal oxide support e.g. gamma -alumina. The nitrous oxide is a by-product of a non-selective catalytic reduction process conducted in the system. The system: is configured to simultaneously convert nitric oxides, carbon monoxide and fuel derived hydrocarbons and to oxidize the soot using the nitrous oxide; and further comprises the catalyst. The system comprises the single, integrated unit, in which the oxidation reaction and the simultaneous conversion of nitric oxides, carbon monoxide and fuel derived hydrocarbons are carried out in the single, integrated unit. The nitrous oxide is a by-product of a nitric acid or adipic acid production process, optionally where the soot is produced from the incomplete combustion of hydrocarbons during power generation for the nitric acid or adipic acid production process.
   EXAMPLE - 1500 ppm Dilute nitrous oxide (in nitrogen) was passed at a flow rate of 200 cm3/minute through a mixture of 0.025 g carbon black and 0.25 g powdered catalyst, while the temperature was increased from ambient to 500 degrees C. All gas concentrations shown were measured using a Fourier transform infrared gas analyzer. The catalyst was comprised of 2 mass% silver dispersed over a cerium dioxide-zirconium dioxide-alumina support material. The mixture consisted of 10 parts by mass catalyst to 1 part by mass carbon. Immediate decline in the nitrous oxide concentration was observed. Larger spike of water that peaks at 140 degrees C together with a small spike of carbon dioxide was observed. The carbon dioxide and additional water were desorption products from the catalyst. The second much larger carbon dioxide peak (coincident with another water peak), which had an onset temperature of 180 degrees C and a maximum temperature of 260 degrees C, was indicative of the catalyzed reaction of the soot with nitrous oxide.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. PN CN107585932-A
TI Preparing mineral water, involves processing tap water, filtering with coconut shell activated carbon, drying, filtering mountain spring water, sterilizing, processing, introducing gas mixture, filtering, filling and packaging product.
AU CHEN Z
   YU J
AE ANHUI YELING BEVERAGE & FOOD CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2018068845
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing mineral water, involves using Maifan stone for processing tap water, filtering the tap water with coconut shell activated carbon, taking out, drying, selecting Dabieshan mountain spring water, filtering, then using plate-and-frame filter press, removing colloidal microorganisms and iron, filtering, sterilizing using ozone, subjecting to UV disinfection, cleaning using carbon dioxide gas, processing, introducing gas mixture, filtering, aseptically filling and packaging after passing the test.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing mineral water.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares mineral water simply by adding reduced chemical additive, where the mineral water has good taste, high oxygen content and good healthcare effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing mineral water, involves using Maifan stone for processing tap water, filtering the tap water with coconut shell activated carbon, taking out, drying, selecting Dabieshan mountain spring water, filtering using the coconut shell activated carbon, subjecting to sand filtration and activated carbon adsorption, and then using plate-and-frame filter press, heating treated tap water in an ultrafiltration device to 25-30 degrees C for reverse osmosis filtration to remove colloidal microorganisms in the water, removing iron using manganese sand filter device, when the iron is aerated, introducing oxygen, then filtering through ultrafiltration device, sterilizing using 0.02 mg/l ozone, where the pressure on the water is 1.2 times standard atmospheric pressure, subjecting to UV disinfection with intensity of 120 mu W/cm2, cleaning by introducing carbon dioxide gas to 25 degrees C until the carbon dioxide content in the water reaches 55 mg/l, processing at 1.2 MPa for 15 minutes and then at 55-60 degrees C for 2 hours, introducing gas mixture into water, where the gas mixture has oxygen content of 65-70%, carbon dioxide of 28-33% and other gases (remainder), maintaining oxygen content to 65 mg/l, filtering using activated carbon, aseptically filling and packaging after passing the test.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The method involves selecting the main peak of the Dabieshan mountain spring water. The water is filtered using the coconut shell activated carbon to flow water at flow rate of 15-18 m3/hours. The activated carbon is food-grade activated carbon, made of straw particles of charcoal, where the average particle size of the activated carbon is 0.51 mm. The high-pressure reverse osmosis filtration is performed under pressure of 500-600 MPa. The iron is removed using filter with water flow speed of 15-18 m3/hours.

TI Device for measuring critical velocity of            generation and propagation of carbon dioxide foam in            petroleum field, has data collecting device obtains            inlet pressure and outlet pressure to determine            generation critical velocity and propagation critical            velocity of carbon dioxide foam in rock core.
AU ZHAO W
   BAI J
   WANG J
   LIU H
   LV X
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM BEIJING (UPEB-C)
GA 2022C19943
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a carbon dioxide gas cylinder                (1) connected with an inlet end of a rock core                holder (5) through a first pipeline. The first                pipeline is provided with an air quality flow meter                (3), where the air quality flow meter is utilized                for adjusting a first injection speed of the carbon                dioxide injected into the rock core holder. A                surface-active agent solution device is connected                with an inlet end of the core holder through a                second pipeline. The second pipeline is provided                with a displacement pump (4). A data collecting                device (7) obtains inlet pressure and outlet                pressure corresponding to the first injection speed                and a second injection speed, where the inlet                pressure and the outlet pressure determine                generation critical velocity and propagation                critical velocity of carbon dioxide foam in a rock                core.
   USE - Device for measuring critical velocity of                generation and propagation of carbon dioxide foam                in petroleum and natural gas exploitation                field.
   ADVANTAGE - The device provides theoretical basis for                oil-gas reservoir foam development, so as to                determine the optimal injection amount of carbon                dioxide foam, thus realizing maximization of                production benefit.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for measuring critical velocity of generation and                propagation of carbon dioxide foam.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                device for measuring critical velocity of                generation and propagation of carbon dioxide                foam.

PN EP4052778-A1; WO2022184743-A1
TI Method for treating steam condensate generated by            high-pressure regenerator for regenerating carbon            dioxide absorption solution, involves supplying            low-pressure steam to steam-fired reboiler for            supplying heat to regenerator.
AU SINGH U
AE YARA INT ASA (YARI-C)
   YARA INT ASA (YARI-C)
GA 2022B4013F
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves capturing carbon dioxide                in a carbon dioxide absorption unit using a carbon                dioxide absorption solution. The carbon dioxide                absorption solution is fed that comprises absorbed                carbon dioxide and generated to a high-pressure                regenerator. The low-pressure steam is supplied to                a steam-fired reboiler for supplying heat to the                high-pressure regenerator by producing a steam                condensate and a regenerated carbon dioxide                absorption solution. The steam condensate is                directly supplied to a de-aerator by producing an                aqueous solution suitable for producing steam with                an oxygen content lower than 20 ppb.
   USE - Method for treating steam condensate generated                by high-pressure regenerator for regenerating                carbon dioxide absorption solution for producing                urea, carbonate beverage, chill, freeze and package                seafood, meat, poultry, baked good, fruit and                vegetable.
   ADVANTAGE - The method allows for utilizing the complete                heat content of the steam condensate generated by                the high-pressure regenerator, at the same time as                ensuring that the equipment in the unit is not                subjected to corrosion since it is                energy-efficiently processed in the                de-aerator.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   a system for treating of a steam condensate                generated by a high-pressure regenerator for                regenerating a carbon dioxide absorption solution;                and

TI Electrolysis of carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide for producing percarboxylic acid, involves using electrolytic cell to reduce carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide at cathode, and oxidize carboxylic acid to percarboxylic acid at anode.
AU VETTER K

   MARTIC N
   REINISCH D
   VOGL C
AE SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
GA 2021D9980V
AB    NOVELTY - Electrolysis of carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide to produce 1-10C percarboxylic acid involves carrying out the electrolysis in an electrolytic cell comprising a cathode (K), an anode (A), a cathode compartment (II) adjacent to the cathode on at least one side, and an anode compartment (III) adjacent to anode on at least one side. The anode compartment comprises 1-10C carboxylic acid, and is separated from cathode compartment by a separator. The carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide is reduced on the cathode, and 1-10C carboxylic acid is oxidized to percarboxylic acid at the anode.
   USE - Electrolysis of carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide for production of 1-10C percarboxylic acid e.g. peroxyacetic acid, peroxybenzoic acid, m-chloroperoxybenzoic acid, peroxyformic acid and peroxysalicylic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical, avoids formation of oxygen at the anode, avoids formation of carbon dioxide-oxygen mixture, and ensures recycling of carboxylic acid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) electrolytic cell, which contains cathode, anode, a gas supply provided to supply the cell with a gas (X) comprising carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide, cathode compartment, anode compartment, and separator; and
   (2) use of 1-10C carboxylic acid as a starting material in the electrolysis of carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide. The carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide is reacted at the cathode and 1-10C carboxylic acid is reacted at the anode.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawings show schematic view of electrolysis method.
   Anode (A)
   Cathode (K)
   Gas supply compartment (I)
   Cathode compartment (II)
   Anode compartment (III)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: When the separator is an anion exchange membrane, the cathode compartment comprises a catholyte containing at least water. When the separator is a cation exchange membrane, the catholyte is passed through the electrolytic cell in the cathode compartment and concentrated outside the electrolytic cell after passing through the electrolytic cell. The anode is in contact with the separator. The cathode is at least partially porous, preferably a gas diffusion electrode, a porous catalyst layer, a membrane-bound porous electrode layer, a diaphragm-bound porous electrode layer or a membrane-electrode arrangement. The electrolytic cell further comprises a gas supply compartment (I) adjoining the cathode on a side opposite to the side where the cathode compartment is provided. The gas supply compartment is provided to supply the cathode with the gas (X). The gas supply supplies the gas (X) to the gas supply compartment. The cathode compartment further comprises at least one electrolyte supply which is provided to supply an electrolyte to the cathode compartment, at least one electrolyte discharge which is provided to discharge electrolyte from the cathode compartment, and a device for concentrating the electrolyte. The device is connected to the electrolyte discharge. Preferred Composition: The carboxylic acid comprises less than 50 wt.% water with respect to mixture of 1-10C carboxylic acid, preferably acetic acid and water. Alternately, the carboxylic acid comprises more than 1 wt.% water with respect to mixture of 1-10C carboxylic acid and water.

PN CN110715692-A
TI Method for measuring ventilation quantity in multi-room building based on human exhaled carbon dioxide, involves calculating amount of carbon dioxide exhaled by human body in area according to height and weight of resident and activity.
AU SUN Y
   HOU J
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 202009907Q
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves placing a carbon dioxide concentration measuring instrument, drawing a space plan, and labeling the room size. The personal information, activities and time of the resident, and sleep time are recorded while recording opening and closing of each door and window of the test space. The carbon dioxide concentration test instrument is set to work for more than 24 hours, so as to retrieve the instrument, and enter the carbon dioxide concentration data. The space is divided into several single areas. The volume of each area is calculated according to the room size obtained from the test. The amount of carbon dioxide exhaled by the human body in each area is calculated according to the height and weight of the resident and the type of activity. The carbon dioxide concentration curve is set to get the final ventilation results in each area.
   USE - Method for measuring ventilation quantity in multi-room building based on human exhaled carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The calculation of building ventilation volume can be applied to multi-room buildings, so as to solve the calculation problem of multi-room building ventilation volume, and can measure and calculate the real-time ventilation volume of multi-room buildings in simple, convenient, fast and effective manner.

   ADVANTAGE - The device allows a carbon dioxide compressor                for compressing carbon dioxide transported by                carbon capture equipment of a thermal power plant                into liquid and transport the liquid to a sealed                storage tank at a high place for storage, which                converts the excess electric energy into                gravitational potential energy for storage and                discharges the carbon dioxide at the same time to                save a certain amount of carbon emissions during                electricity generation. The device allows the                annular bearing rack to drive the full-loaded                sealed storage tank to slide down to drive the                gravity-driven generator to rotate and generate                electricity, so as to convert the stored                gravitational potential energy into electrical                energy for use, thus realizing peak regulation of                power generation through gravity storage and                utilizing carbon dioxide emitted as a counterweight                for gravity energy storage.

PN CN114959153-A
TI Process for producing direct reduced iron in            hydrogen-rich shaft furnace, involves separating            byproduct carbon dioxide gas and decarburization gas            rich in hydrogen and carbon, and reverse water gas            shift reaction to generate synthesis gas.
AU JIANG L
   DI X
   ZHENG J
   ZHANG T
   LI J
   WU K
   HE X
AE CISDI ENG CO LTD (CMEG-C)
GA 2022B5397R
AB    NOVELTY - The process involves decarburizing and                separating the top gas discharged from the top of                the shaft furnace. The by-product carbon dioxide                gas and decarburized gas rich in hydrogen and                carbon monoxide are separated. The by-product                carbon monoxide gas is mixed with supplementary                hydrogen to generate a mixed gas. The mixed gas is                heated, subjected to inverse water gas shift                reaction and heat exchange to generate synthesis                gas. A shaft furnace circulating gas is generated                after the synthesis gas is mixed with the                decarburized gas. The shaft furnace circulating gas                is mixed with supplementary hydrogen and                supplementary fossil carbon-containing gas to form                a shaft furnace reducing gas, and is passed into                the shaft furnace after being heated to produce                direct reduced iron. The direct reduced iron                produced by the shaft furnace is discharged from                the bottom of the shaft furnace, and the top gas                discharged from the top of the shaft furnace is                repeatedly circulated.
   USE - Process for producing direct reduced iron in                hydrogen-rich shaft furnace used in iron and steel                industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The process uses dry or wet decarburization                and reverse water gas shift reaction of hydrogen                and carbon dioxide to realize the conversion and                recycling of carbon in the vertical furnace                process, no carbon oxide is directly discharged,                more green and low carbon. The process system only                needs to supplement a small amount of natural gas,                coke oven gas, and other fossil carbon-containing                gas or carbon dioxide, and renewable energy to                prepare green hydrogen to maintain vertical furnace                sponge iron carburization requirement and balance                requirement of circulating reducing gas. The                process flow is simple, low energy consumption and                obvious environment-friendly benefit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart illustrating a                Process for producing direct reduced iron in                hydrogen-rich shaft furnace. (Drawing includes                non-English language text)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The main components of                  the syngas include hydrogen, carbon monoxide,                  carbon dioxide and water.

TI No-movement direct air capture system for directly capturing carbon dioxide from gas mixtures, comprises stationary chamber, porous monolith, inlet gas flow conduit, outlet gas flow conduits, storage facility and valve.
AU CHICHILNISKY G
   MOESLER F
AE GLOBAL THERMOSTAT OPERATIONS LLC (GLOB-Non-standard)
GA 202268903M
AB    NOVELTY - The no-movement system (4) has a stationary chamber at one location. A porous monolith is supported within the chamber and being formed with channels extending through it, the monolith supporting a sorbent for reversibly sorbing carbon dioxide from a flow of such gas mixtures. An inlet gas flow conduit, open at one end to the chamber containing the contact structure and at a second end to a source of the gas flow mixtures containing co2. An automated system is electronically connected to each flow conduit valve for controlling the time of opening of each valve, to permit the flow of each of the incoming gas mixture and steam.
   USE - No-movement direct air capture System for capturing carbon dioxide from ambient air and mixtures of gases including ambient air, for use in airport restrooms.
   ADVANTAGE - The system provides a no movement direct air capture system that will capture CO2 from the air or gases and will produce a greater than or equal to 95% purity CO2 product. It will result in anticipated decreased costs of DAC, and will not require the movement of capture and/or regeneration apparatus of the type known to the art. It demonstrates an expected DAC process designed to reduce anticipated costs of captured CO2, as well as the energy burden associated with the capture process. It operates at steady state, reducing the relative complexity required for physically starting and stopping a movement system. It provides enhanced reliability. It uses a novel arrangement of valves to facilitate the capture of carbon dioxide and thereafter the regeneration of the amine or other material that was used in the capture processes.

TI Preparing sheet HZSM-5 molecular sieve catalyst used in production of aromatic hydrocarbons by hydrogenation of carbon dioxide comprises e.g. adding tetrapropyl ammonium hydroxide, tetraethyl orthosilicate, aluminum isopropoxide and sodium hydroxide to deionized water, and washing to neutrality.
AU TANG X
   GUO X
   ZHA F
   HE H
   TIAN H
AE UNIV NORTHWEST NORMAL (UNWN-C)
GA 202241785B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing sheet HZSM-5 molecular sieve catalyst comprises (1) sequentially adding tetrapropyl ammonium hydroxide (TPAOH), tetraethyl orthosilicate (TEOS), aluminum isopropoxide (C9H21AlO3) and sodium hydroxide into deionized water under vigorous stirring, and continue to stirring until a mixed sol is formed, (2) adding ammonium fluoride or urea and ammonium mixture to the above mixed sol, stirring, reacting at 160-180degrees Celsius for 48-72 hours, where the molar ratio of silicon dioxide to ammonium or mixture of urea and ammonium fluoride is 1:0.8-1:3, where the molar ratio of ammonium fluoride and urea in the mixture of urea and ammonium fluoride is 1-2:1, and (3) naturally cooling to room temperature after the crystallization is completed, centrifuging, washing to neutrality, drying, and performing calcination to obtain a sheet-type HZSM-5 molecular sieve catalyst.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing sheet HZSM-5 molecular sieve catalyst, which is used in production of aromatic hydrocarbons by hydrogenation of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: has high reactivity and high selectivity of aromatic hydrocarbons; can suppress the formation rate of carbon deposition; and prolong the service life of the catalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a use method of sheet HZSM-5 molecular sieve catalyst in the production of aromatic hydrocarbons by hydrogenation of carbon dioxide comprising using flaky HZSM-5 molecular sieve catalyst and zinc oxide-zirconium dioxide in the process of producing aromatic hydrocarbons by hydrogenation of carbon monoxide (CO) after mixing and grinding at a mass ratio of 1:2, where the process conditions are the reaction pressure is 3 MPa, the reaction temperature is 320 degreesC, the space velocity is 1800 ml/g/hour, and the molar ratio of hydrogen to carbon dioxide is 3:1.

In step (3), the drying temperature is 80-100 degrees Celsius, drying time is 5-6 hours, the preparation method of sheet HZSM-5 molecular sieve catalyst. The calcination temperature is 500-600degrees Celsius, and the calcination time is 5-6 hours. In step (1), measuring the tetraethyl orthosilicate (TEOS) , aluminum isopropoxide, sodium hydroxide respectively with silicon dioxide, aluminum oxide, sodium oxide, where the mol ratio of controlling each raw material is silicon dioxide, aluminum oxide, tetra-propyl ammonium hydroxide, sodium oxide, and water is 1:1/60:0.4 : 1/60 : 35.

AU CHOI H
AE JPS CO LTD (JPSJ-Non-standard)
GA 202216644V
AB    NOVELTY - A plant for the treatment of organic waste comprises a biogas digestion unit for digesting organic waste, a mechanical separator for separating the effluent of the biogas digester into a concentrated fraction and a liquid fraction, a heating unit for heating the liquid portion, a carbon dioxide flash unit for partially removing volatile carbon dioxide and increasing the pH of the liquid unit, a pump for transferring the liquid portion discharged from the flash column and heated to a temperature of 60 degrees C or higher to ammonia removal unit, an ammonia stripping section for removing at least 98-99% of ammonia from the liquid fraction under a reduced pressure of 0.25-0.75 bar and a reverse osmosis section for separating the water fraction and the potassium fraction useful as fertilizers from the ammonia-free liquid fraction.
   USE - Plant for the treating organic waste (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The plants has increased biogas generated from liquid waste while producing fertilizers rich in phosphoric acid, fertilizers rich in ammonium sulfate, or fertilizers rich in potassium.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for treating organic waste which involves anaerobically fermenting the organic waste in a biogas digester, mechanically separating the effluent from the biogas digester into a concentrated fraction and a liquid fraction, heating the liquid portion to a temperature below the boiling point of the liquid, introducing the heated liquid portion to a flash column to partially remove volatile carbon dioxide and to raise the pH of the liquid portion, pumping the liquid portion heated to a temperature above 60 degrees C, after removing the volatile carbon dioxide, putting it into an ammonia removal device and removing ammonia from the liquid under a reduced pressure of 0.25-0.75 bar, neutralizing the liquid portion from which ammonia has been removed and separating the neutralized ammonia-free liquid fraction into a potassium fraction and a water fraction in the reverse osmosis section.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Condition: The temperature below the boiling point of the liquid is at least 70 degrees C. The method further involves adding sodium hydroxide to the heated and flashed liquid prior to removing the ammonia, sorting the waste for impurity removal, cutting, heat treatment, grinding or extrusion prior to anaerobic fermentation. The organic waste treatment plant further comprises a grinder, a pulper, an extruder, a heat treatment unit, a grinder, and a screen, a suspended solids removal unit that further removes suspended solids from the liquid portion of the mechanically separated effluent and desulfurization unit for desulfurizing the discharged biogas.

TI Preparing organic-inorganic hybrid solid solution photocatalyst useful in photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction, comprises e.g. reacting cadmium salt, manganese salt, deionized water, sulfur compound and amine organic solution and washing.
AU DAI K
   LIU L
   ZHANG J
AE UNIV HUAIBEI NORMAL (UYHU-Non-standard)
GA 2021A4086N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing organic-inorganic hybrid solid solution photocatalyst, comprises (i) adding cadmium salt and manganese salt to a beaker, adding deionized water, and stirring until transparent to obtain solution A, (ii) placing the sulfur compound into the reaction kettle, adding the amine organic solution, and stirring evenly to obtain solution B, and (iii) while stirring the resulting solution B, dropping solution A into the reactor, stirring the solution, and finally placing the reaction kettle in the oven for heat preservation and reaction, obtaining the target product by washing, centrifuging and drying, where x represents the molar amount, and x = 0-1.
   USE - The method for preparing photocatalyst is useful in photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, the prepared solid solution has higher photocatalytic performance and stability than pure cadmium sulfide and manganese sulfide, the photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction activity is high, the stability is good, and the application prospect is broad.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing organic-inorganic hybrid MnxCd1-xS solid solution photocatalyst, comprises (i) adding cadmium salt and manganese salt to a beaker, adding deionized water, and stirring until transparent to obtain solution A, (ii) placing the sulfur compound into the reaction kettle, adding the amine organic solution, and stirring evenly to obtain solution B, and (iii) while stirring the resulting solution B, dropping solution A into the reactor, stirring the solution, and finally placing the reaction kettle in the oven for heat preservation and reaction, obtaining the target product by washing, centrifuging and drying, where x represents the molar amount, and x = 0-1. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) an organic-inorganic hybrid MnxCd1-xS solid solution photocatalyst, obtained by the method as mentioned above; and
   (2) a catalyzing the reduction of carbon dioxide under visible light irradiation using organic-inorganic hybrid MnxCd1-xS solid solution as a photocatalyst, comprising generating combustible fuel, preferably, the combustible fuel is carbon monoxide, methane, methanol, formaldehyde, formic acid, ethanol and/or ethane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the molar volume ratio of cadmium salt, manganese salt, and deionized water is 0.1-1 mol: 0.1-1 mol: 15-50 ml. The molar ratio of the cadmium salt to the manganese salt is 0.5 mol: 0.3-0.6 mol. The x = 0-1, preferably 0.5.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the sulfur compound is L-cysteine, sublimed sulfur, thioacetamide, and/or potassium thiosulfate. In the step (ii), the amine organic solution is diethylenetriamine, triethylenetetramine, hexamethylenetetramine, cyclohexamine, dimethylformamide, cycloethyleneimine, ethylenediamine, tetraethylene and/or ethylenepentamine. In the step (ii), the molar volume ratio of the sulfur compound and the amine organic solution is 1-4 mol: 15-50 ml. In step (ii), the molar volume ratio of the sulfur compound and the amine organic solution is 2-4 mol: 35 ml. In step (iii), the washing liquid used for washing is deionized water and/or absolute ethanol. Preferred Method: In step (i) and step (ii), the stirring is performed for 10-60 minutes. In the step (iii), the stirring is continued for 4-8 hours, the temperature in the oven is 80-180 degrees C, and the heat preservation reaction time is 2-10 hours. In step (iii), the drying method is vacuum drying or freeze drying.

PT P
PN EP3789760-A1
TI Particle used e.g. as sensor, preferably carbon dioxide gas sensor, to produce sensor, and to detect analyte, comprises inorganic compound having buffer in saturated aqueous solution, detecting compound, and hygroscopic compound.
AU MATARANGA-POPA L
   RIECHERS D
AE PRESENS PRECISION SENSING GMBH (PRES-Non-standard)
GA 202123735U
AB    NOVELTY - Particle comprises at least one inorganic compound having a buffer capacity of 0.1-50 mmol/l at 20 degrees C in a saturated aqueous solution, at least one detecting compound, and at least one hygroscopic compound.
   USE - The particle is useful: as a sensor, preferably a carbon dioxide gas sensor; for producing a sensor; for detecting an analyte (all claimed); and as optical sensors for detecting gases e.g. sulfur dioxide, ammonia or carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The particle: provides improved sensor element with high detection sensitivity and stability against pH-influencing gases e.g. volatile organic compounds, nitrogen dioxide, hydrogen sulfide, carbon dioxide and sulfur dioxide; and increases buffer concentration that directly reduces the sensitivity of the sensor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) producing (M1) the particle comprising (a) providing a mixture comprising the detecting compound, the hygroscopic compound and at least one liquid medium, (b) applying the mixture of step (a) to the inorganic compound, and (c) optionally at least partially removing the liquid medium from the mixture obtained in step (b), where the inorganic compound has a buffer capacity of 0.1-50 mmol/l at 20 degrees C in a saturated aqueous solution;
   (2) a composition comprising particles, and at least one hydrophobic polymer or at least one precursor;
   (3) a sensor comprising the composition;
   (4) producing (M2) a sensor, comprising (a1) providing a carrier, (b1) applying the composition to the carrier, and (c1) crosslinking the composition applied in step (b1) to obtain a polymer layer arranged on the carrier; and
   (5) detecting (M3) an analyte, comprising (a2) contacting the analyte with the sensor or the composition, (b2) irradiating the sensor or the composition with radiation lambda 1 and optionally lambda 2, (c2) measuring the absorption, emission and/or fluorescence spectrum as signal, and (d2) correlating the signal obtained in step (c2) with the presence or absence of the analyte.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inorganic compound has a solubility of at most 1 g/l in water at 20 degrees C and pH 7, and is preferably comprising a carbonate salt e.g. zinc carbonate or calcium carbonate, a phosphate salt or mineral e.g. calcium phosphate or apatite, a hydrolysable metal oxide e.g. magnesium oxide or zinc oxide, and/or a layered mineral e.g. a layered double hydroxide. The detecting compound is a pH sensitive detecting compound, preferably a pH sensitive dye. Preferred Composition: The composition comprises a reference detecting compound. Preferred Method: In the method (M2), the step (b1) is performed by a coating procedure e.g. spin coating, knife coating, spray coating, dip coating, or ink jet printing.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the method (M1), the liquid medium is an organic solvent, preferably an alcohol e.g. methanol, ethanol or propanol, tetrahydrofuran, 1,4-dioxane, dichloromethane, acetone, dimethylformamide, or N-methylpyrrolidone.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The hygroscopic compound is a homopolymer or copolymer, preferably a hydrophilic homopolymer or copolymer, or preferably comprising ionizable and/or ionic functional groups. The hydrophobic polymer comprises a silicone polymer e.g. polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS) or a fluorinated silicone polymer, or a silicone copolymer e.g. a PDMS copolymer, a silicone-polycarbonate copolymer or a fluorinated silicone copolymer. The precursor comprises at least one monomer i.e. capable of forming a hydrophobic polymer and optionally an initiator, a catalyst and/or a crosslinker, where the monomer comprises e.g. vinyl terminated poly(dimethylsiloxane), vinylphenylmethyl terminated (phenylmethylsiloxane)-vinylphenylsiloxane copolymer, vinyl terminated diphenylsiloxane-dimethylsiloxane copolymers, vinyl terminated trifluoropropylmethylsiloxane-dimethylsiloxane copolymer, vinyl terminated nonafluorohexylmethylsiloxane-dimethylsiloxane copolymer, vinyl terminated diethylsiloxane dimethylsiloxane copolymers, and/or vinyl terminated ethylene-siloxane copolymer fluids.
   EXAMPLE - In a 2.5 l flask, 0.3-3 mol/l zinc chloride solution, 0.15-1 mol/l aluminum chloride, 2-5 mol/l urea solution, and 600 ml water were stirred for 18-48 hours at 100 degrees C. The reaction mixture was then cooled and centrifuged. The particulate residue was washed with 300 ml water for 3 hours under stirring to yield purified particles. The purified particles were stirred for 1 hour in 1 l of 1 mol/l sodium tetraphenylborate solution, washed with water and dried to yield inorganically modified particles. Then 60-85 wt.% inorganically modified particles, 20-30 wt.% polyvinylpyrrolidone K15, 0.1-2 wt.% 8-hydroxypyrene-1,3,6-trisulfonic acid and 100 ml water-free methanol were stirred for 1 hour at room temperature followed by removal of the liquid medium using a rotary evaporator at 337 mbar and 40 degrees C. The resulting raw material was grounded using a cryogenic mill to obtain particles with an average particle size of 10 mu m.

PT P
PN CN112408437-A
TI Recovering lithium salt, comprises e.g. subjecting            3-acetylcholesterol-7-p-toluenesulfonyl hydrazone into            dehydrazone reaction, then first cooling and carrying            out first solid-liquid separation.
AU XU J
   ZHAO G
   CHEN Y
   TANG Y
   XU Y
AE JIANGXI TIANXIN PHARM CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202127016S
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering lithium salt, comprises e.g. (i)                subjecting 3-acetylcholesterol-7-p-toluenesulfonyl                hydrazone into dehydrazone reaction, subjecting                obtained dehydrazone liquid to the first cooling                and carrying out first solid-liquid separation to                obtain mixed lithium salt containing lithium amide                and lithium p-toluenesulfinate, (ii) dissolving the                mixed lithium salt in water to obtain                lithium-containing solution, (iii) extracting the                lithium-containing solution with extracting agent,                introducing carbon dioxide into obtained raffinate,                adjusting pH value to 9-10, then mixing with                soluble carbonate and filtering obtained mixture                through the first heat to obtain crude lithium                carbonate product, (iv) dissolving crude lithium                carbonate product in water, performing second                cooling, introducing carbon dioxide to clear and                de-coloring the obtained lithium bicarbonate                solution in turn, carrying out thermal                decomposition and carrying out secondary heat                filtering to obtain lithium carbonate                product.
   USE - The method is useful for recovering lithium                salt.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: avoids the generation of phosphate                wastewater; directly obtains high-content lithium                carbonate products, and improves the recovery rate                of lithium salts.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recovering lithium salt, comprises (i)                subjecting 3-acetylcholesterol-7-p-toluenesulfonyl                hydrazone into a dehydrazone reaction, then                subjecting obtained dehydrazone liquid to the first                cooling and carrying out first solid-liquid                separation to obtain mixed lithium salt containing                lithium amide and lithium p-toluenesulfinate, (ii)                dissolving the mixed lithium salt in water to                obtain lithium-containing solution, (iii)                extracting the lithium-containing solution with                extracting agent, introducing carbon dioxide into                obtained raffinate, adjusting pH value to 9-10,                then mixing with soluble carbonate, and filtering                obtained mixture through the first heat to obtain                crude lithium carbonate product, (iv) dissolving                crude lithium carbonate product in water, then                performing second cooling, then introducing carbon                dioxide to clear and de-coloring the obtained                lithium bicarbonate solution in turn, carrying out                thermal decomposition and carrying out secondary                heat filtering to obtain lithium carbonate                product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (i): the molar                  ratio of the mixed lithium salt to the charged                  lithium amide is 0.97-0.99:1, the molar ratio of                  lithium amide to lithium p-toluenesulfinate in                  the mixed lithium salt is 0.5-5:1, preferably                  2-3:1, the first cooling temperature is 10-50                  degrees C, preferably 20-40 degrees C. In step                  (iii): the temperature of the first hot                  filtration is 60-100 degrees C, preferably 80-90                  degrees C. In step (iv): the weight ratio between                  crude lithium carbonate to water is 1:5-30,                  preferably 1:10-15, the second cooling                  temperature is 0-25 degrees C, preferably 0-10                  degrees C. In step (iv), the decolorization                  includes: contacting the lithium bicarbonate                  solution with a decolorizing agent to obtain a                  decolorizing product, the decolorizing agent is                  activated carbon, clay and/or adsorption resin,                  preferably activated carbon. The thermal                  decomposition conditions includes: a temperature                  of 60-100 degrees C, preferably 80-90 degrees C;                  a time of 1-10 hours, preferably 2-8 hours, the                  temperature of the second thermal filtration is                  60-100 degrees C, preferably 80-90 degrees C.                  Preferred Components: In step (i): the weight                  ratio of the mixed lithium salt to water is                  1:5-20, preferably 1:10-15. The extractant is an                  organic solvent, preferably toluene,                  ethylbenzene, o-xylene, m-xylene, p-xylene,                  chlorobenzene and/or dichloromethane, preferably                  the weight ratio of the extractant to the mixed                  lithium salt is 1-10:1, preferably 2-5:1. The                  molar ratio between soluble carbonate and mixed                  lithium salt is 0.1-0.5:1, the mole of the                  soluble carbonate is based on the mole of                  carbonate, and the mole of the mixed lithium salt                  is the sum of the moles of lithium amide and                  lithium p-toluenesulfinate, the soluble carbonate                  is sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate and/or                  ammonium carbonate. The weight ratio between                  lithium bicarbonate solution and decolorizing                  agent is 1:0.001-0.02, preferably 1:0.003-0.01,                  the decolorized product is subjected to a second                  solid-liquid separation before the thermal                  decomposition.

GA 202082979D
AB    NOVELTY - Simultaneous detection device comprises a signal generator set with an input end of a quantum cascade laser (QCL) controller-I and an input end of a QCL laser controller-II. The output terminal of the QCL laser controller-I is set to an input terminal of a QCL laser-I. The output terminal of the QCL laser controller-II is set to an input terminal of a QCL laser-II. A plane mirror assembly-I is arranged on an optical path in front of the QCL laser-I. A plane mirror assembly-II is arranged on the optical path in front of QCL laser-II. The laser beam emitted by the QCL laser-I is reflected by the plane mirror assembly and incident into the integrating cavity. The laser beam emitted by the QCL laser-II is reflected by the plane mirror assembly-II and incident into the integrating cavity. The laser beams emitted by the QCL laser-I and QCL laser-II are incident on the integrating cavity in different directions. A signal processing system is interactively set with a signal generator.
   USE - The device is useful for stable isotopes of carbon dioxide and nitrous oxide based on dual beam cavity enhanced spectroscopy technology.
   ADVANTAGE - The device: achieves stable and efficient dual beam cavity enhanced optical path system, enhances spectral structure using double beam cavity, avoids power fluctuations and losses caused by coupling optical components, reduces hardware cost of system, and is conducive to detection device.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for method for detecting carbon dioxide and nitrous oxide stable isotope simultaneous detection device based on dual beam cavity enhanced spectroscopy technology.

TI Multi-heat exchanger series transcritical carbon            dioxide heat pump heating system in which coolant            channel of sub air cooler, coolant channel of            condenser, and coolant channel of main air cooler            connected by pipe as water supply flow path.
AU ZHANG C

   CHENG J
   CHANG M
AE UNIV TONGJI (UYTJ-C)
GA 202054925D
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a first compressor (1), a first                air cooler (2), a second air cooler (3), a cooling                and evaporation (4), a first throttle device (5), a                low temperature evaporator (6), a second compressor                (7), a condenser (8) and a second throttle device                (9). The first compressor, the refrigerant passage                of the first air cooler, the refrigerant passage of                the second air cooler, one of the refrigerant                passages of the cooling evaporator, the refrigerant                channels of the throttling device and the                low-temperature evaporator are connected in                sequence through pipes to form a carbon dioxide                (CO2) transcritical circulation circuit. The                refrigerant channel of the cooling evaporator is                connected through pipes to form an auxiliary                circulation circuit. The coolant channel of the                second air cooler, the coolant channel of the                condenser, and the coolant channel of the first air                cooler are connected by a pipe as a water supply                flow path.
   USE - Multi-heat exchanger series transcritical                carbon dioxide (CO2) heat pump heating                system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system can improve the heat exchange                uniformity and promote carbon dioxide. The overall                energy efficiency of the heat pump heating system                is improved. The transcritical circulation loop                effectively reduces carbon monoxide at the outlet                of the air cooler. The cycle efficiency is                improved. The three-section type heat supply is                adopted so that the heat exchange uniformity of                heating hot water and refrigerant fluid is                improved. The irreversible loss of heat exchange is                reduced and the total energy efficiency of the                system is improved.

PN CN110882687-A; CN110882687-B
TI Cerium-based composite oxide coating type integral catalyst used in synthesis of dimethyl carbonate, comprises catalyst-coated cerium-based composite oxide and catalyst substrate of cordierite honeycomb ceramic.
AU CHEN Y
   YAN L
   TANG Q
   WANG J
   LIU X
   ZOU J
   PU H
   WU X
   LIANG C
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST PETROLEUM (USWP-C)
GA 202022489E
AB    NOVELTY - A cerium-based composite oxide coating type integral catalyst comprises catalyst-coated cerium-based composite oxide and a catalyst substrate of cordierite honeycomb ceramic.
   USE - Cerium-based composite oxide coating type integral catalyst used in fixed bed reactor for synthesis of dimethyl carbonate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The cerium-based composite oxide coating type integral catalyst ensures efficient synthesis of dimethyl carbonate with high yield and easy product separation and catalyst recovery.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) manufacture of cerium-based composite oxide coating type integral catalyst, which involves (S1) ball milling the cerium-based composite oxide catalyst powder and a metered amount of deionized water together to prepare a slurry with a solid content of 40-60 wt.%, (S2) soaking the cordierite honeycomb ceramic substrate in the obtained slurry, (S3) removing the substrate from the slurry, and (S4) using compressed air to blow away excess slurry in the channels of the substrate and on the outer surface, and coating the substrate with the slurry, drying and then baking; and
   (2) method of using cerium-based composite oxide coating type integral catalyst, which involves placing cerium-based composite oxide coating type monolithic catalyst in a stainless steel tube of a fixed-bed reactor, introducing carbon dioxide and feeding the liquid methanol to a preheater through a high-pressure constant-current pump to-be-preheated at 80-120 degrees C for gasification treatment after 5-10 minutes of introduction of carbon dioxide to obtain premixing of carbon dioxide and methanol in which molar ratio of methanol and carbon dioxide in the reaction gas is 2:1, controlling the reaction pressure from 1-3 MPa and controlling the reaction temperature from 100-200 degrees C, and controlling the volumetric space velocity of gaseous materials to 2880 gcat-1hour-1, and detecting the reaction mixture by gas chromatography after 4 to 6 hours of reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The manufacture of cerium-based composite oxide catalyst powder involves adding aqueous solution of at least one of magnesium salt, zinc salt, and bismuth salt to the water-soluble cerium salt solution under stirring, and then adding a metered aluminum salt solution and a metered precipitating agent at reaction temperature of 60-120 degrees C for 3-7 hours, preferably 80-100 degrees C for 4-6 hours to obtain reactant after the precipitation, filtering and washing the reaction product, then adding 10-50 wt.% polyethylene glycol solution with respect to total mass of the obtained reaction product to form slurry, and spray drying, and controlling the average particle size of the powder to 5-10 microns, vacuum-drying and calcining in atmosphere such as air or oxygen. Preferred Properties: The cerium-based composite oxide catalyst powder has specific surface area of 120 m2/g. The molar ratio of cerium to magnesium or zinc or bismuth is 0.01-0.80:0.99-0.20. Preferred Components: The cerium composite oxide of formula: Ce1-xMxO2-Al2O3 comprises 1 wt.% aluminum oxide. The cerium salt is cerium ammonium nitrate. The magnesium salt is magnesium nitrate. The zinc salt is zinc nitrate. The bismuth salt is bismuth nitrate. The aluminum salt is aluminum nitrate. The precipitating agent is a urea solution.M = magnesium, bismuth, or zinc;andx = 0,0.1,0.3,0.5,0.7,0.9, or 1.N.B: x is defined in disclosure but not in claims. Preferred Condition: The coated substrate is dried at 70-80 degrees C for 3-4 hours, and then baked at 150-200 degrees C for 1-2 hours, and then at 400-500 degrees C for 2-5 hours. The vacuum drying is performed at 80 degrees C for 12 hours.

PT P
PN US10369544-B1
TI Synthesizing titanosilicate material for e.g., carbon dioxide adsorption by adding structure directing agent to gel solution containing titanium(III) chloride solution and functionalizing detemplated material with strontium cation.
AU HERNANDEZ-MALDONADO A J
   MARCANO-GONZALEZ M E
   PRIMERA-PEDROZO J N
AE UNIV PUERTO RICO (UYPU-Non-standard)
   HERNANDEZ-MALDONADO A J (HERN-Individual)
   MARCANO-GONZALEZ M E (MARC-Individual)
   PRIMERA-PEDROZO J N (PRIM-Individual)
GA 201967621A
AB    NOVELTY - A titanosilicate material is synthesized by adding a structure directing agent selected from tetrapropylammonium (TPA+) and tetrabutylammonium (TBA+), that allows the formation of porous adsorbent material, to a gel solution containing titanium(III) chloride solution in hydrochloric acid (HCl) and stirring for 30 minutes to prepare a gel mixture and crystallizing gel mixture to form a titanosilicate porous material having structural faults introduced by structure directing agent; removing structure directing agent from titanosilicate porous material via ion exchange with ammonium ion (NH4+) to form a detemplated material; and functionalizing detemplated material with strontium cation via ion exchange to form titanosilicate porous material having a micropore area that varies as function of thermal activation temperature due to structural faults introduced by structure directing agent.
   USE - Synthesis of titanosilicate material for carbon dioxide adsorption and natural gas purification.
   ADVANTAGE - The titanosilicate material exhibits superior adsorption capacities when compared to other porous titanosilicates. Adsorption isotherms for carbon dioxide, methane and nitrogen as well as carbon dioxide isosteric heat of adsorption on strontium(II) variants are also shown.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A titanosilicate material is synthesized by adding a structure directing agent selected from tetrapropylammonium (TPA+) and tetrabutylammonium (TBA+), that allows the formation of porous adsorbent material, to a gel solution containing titanium(III) chloride solution in hydrochloric acid (HCl) and stirring for 30 minutes to prepare a gel mixture and crystallizing gel mixture to form a titanosilicate porous material having structural faults introduced by structure directing agent; removing structure directing agent from titanosilicate porous material via ion exchange with ammonium ion (NH4+) to form a detemplated material; and functionalizing detemplated material with strontium cation via ion exchange to form titanosilicate porous material having a micropore area that varies as function of thermal activation temperature due to structural faults introduced by structure directing agent, where titanosilicate material has unit cell selected from formulae (Sr2+3.0Na+3.3OH-2.31)(Si11.4Ti8.3O37):23H2O and (Sr2+2.4Na+3.9OH-1.5)(Si9.3Ti7.1O37):20H2O.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The gel mixture comprises a composition having a formula of 3.4(TBA)2O:7.3Na2O:1.2K2O:1.3TiO2:10SiO2:201.5H2O. The gel mixture comprises a composition having a formula of 3.4(TPA)2O:7.3Na2O:1.2K2O:1.3TiO2:10SiO2:201.5H2O. The functionalized detemplated material exhibits selectivity for carbon dioxide (CO2).

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN CN110047735-A; WO2020199395-A1
TI Method for processing metal structure wet process            for thin film transistor (TFT) used in display device,            involves spraying metal structure wet process solution            onto substrate to be treated including metal structure            in chamber.
AU YIN Y
AE SHENZHEN CHINA STAR OPTOELECTRONICS TECH (TCLC-C)
   SHENZHEN CHINA STAR OPTOELECTRONICS TECH (TCLC-C)
GA 201966412R
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves treating (201) a gas or a                liquid in a chamber of a wet process apparatus to                reduce an oxygen and a carbon-dioxide content in                the chamber. The metal structure wet process                solution is sprayed (202) onto the substrate to be                treated including the metal structure in the                chamber. The chamber of the wet process equipment                is vacuumed or sealed to reduce the air content in                the chamber to reduce the oxygen and carbon-dioxide                content in the chamber. The chamber is filled with                a first inert gas to form a positive pressure in                the chamber to prevent the oxygen and                carbon-dioxide in the air from entering the chamber                and dissolving in the metal structure when spraying                the solution of the metal structure wet process                solution.
   USE - Method for processing metal structure wet                process for thin film transistor (TFT) used in                display device (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - By vacuuming and sealing the chamber, and                filling the chamber with the first inert gas, a                positive pressure is formed in the chamber, and the                oxygen content in the chamber and the solution is                reduced so that the oxygen concentration battery                effect is weakened. The carbon-dioxide content is                lowered, and the potential of hydrogen (pH) of the                solution is made closer to neutral, thus, slowing                down the dissolution of the corrosion product and                achieving the purpose of weakening the galvanic                corrosion of the metal structure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method of preparing a TFT; and
   (2) a display device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows flow chart illustrating a                metal structure wet process processing method.                (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Step for treating gas or liquid in chamber of                wet process apparatus (201)
   Step for spraying metal structure wet process                solution onto substrate (202)
DC U11 (Semiconductor Materials and Processes)

TI Separation of impurities from natural gas stream            involves contacting chilled gas stream with lean            solvent, and separating impurities from            partially-treated gas stream to form fully-treated and            permeate gas streams having lower temperature.
AU MAHER D W
   RAMKUMAR S
   NORTHROP P S
   DENTON R D
AE MAHER D W (MAHE-Individual)
   RAMKUMAR S (RAMK-Individual)
   NORTHROP P S (NORT-Individual)
   DENTON R D (DENT-Individual)
   EXXONMOBIL UPSTREAM RES CO (ESSO-C)
   MAHER D W (MAHE-Individual)
   NORTHROP P S (NORT-Individual)
   RAMKUMAR S (RAMK-Individual)
   DENTON R D (DENT-Individual)
   EXXONMOBIL UPSTREAM RES CO (ESSO-C)
   EXXONMOBIL UPSTREAM RES CO (ESSO-C)
   EXXONMOBIL UPSTREAM RES CO (ESSO-C)
GA 201916879Y
AB    NOVELTY - Separation of impurities from natural gas                stream (405) involves cooling natural gas stream                through heat exchange with one or more process                streams to produce a chilled gas stream, contacting                the chilled stream with a lean solvent stream (412)                in a contactor (402) to separate hydrogen sulfide                from the gas stream, thereby producing a rich                solvent stream (420) and a partially-treated gas                stream (414), removing hydrogen sulfide and carbon                dioxide from the rich solvent stream to produce the                lean solvent stream, recirculating the lean solvent                stream to the contactor, and separating hydrogen                sulfide and carbon dioxide from the                partially-treated gas stream in a membrane                separation system (404) to create a fully-treated                gas stream (408) comprising natural gas, and a                permeate gas stream (410) comprising hydrogen                sulfide and carbon dioxide, where the fully-treated                and the permeate gas streams are at a lower                temperature than the partially-treated gas stream,                and comprise the process streams.
   USE - Separation of impurities e.g. hydrogen sulfide                and carbon dioxide from natural gas stream e.g.                sour gas stream.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently removes hydrogen                sulfide from natural gas stream by using lean                solvent stream comprising hydrogen                sulfide-selective hindered amines. It is possible                to efficiently reduce the reaction temperature of                gas stream and solvent in contactor vessels by                inherent cooling of the outputs of membrane                separation system. The entire membrane separation                system can be operated without the presence of                hydrogen sulfide, thereby improving plant                safety.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for system                (400) for separating impurities from natural gas                stream.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                natural gas separation system.
   Sour gas separation system (400)
   Cold solvent contactor (402)
   Membrane separation system (404)
   Feed gas stream (405)
   Fully-treated gas stream (408)
   Permeate gas stream (410)
   Lean solvent stream (412)
   Partially-treated gas stream (414)
   Rich solvent stream (420)

PN CN109060928-A
TI Method for quantitatively determining formation of carbonate cement and timing of hydrocarbon charge, involves collecting dense reservoir core or outcrop sample, formulating into thin sheet followed by obtaining homogenization temperature.
AU CUI J
   ZHU R
   LI S
   MAO Z
AE PETROCHINA CO LTD (CNPC-C)
GA 2019007242
AB    NOVELTY - A formation of carbonate cement and timing of hydrocarbon charge quantitative determination method involves collecting dense reservoir core or outcrop sample, formulating into thin sheet, performing cathodoluminescence to determine diagenesis period of cement on the sheet, subjecting sheet to chloroform extraction, crushing the cement in stages, obtaining powder of different periods, reacting with concentrated phosphoric acid, obtaining carbon dioxide from powder of different stages, purifying carbon dioxide, performing isotope analysis, obtaining 13C18O16O value of different fractions, calculating temperature of formation of carbonate cement, according to the 13C18O16O value and empirical formula, obtaining homogenization temperature of associated brine inclusions of hydrocarbon inclusions, combining basin simulation to establish thermal history curve and marking homogenization temperature of the carbonate cement and brine inclusion on the thermal history curve.
   USE - Method for quantitatively determining formation of carbonate cement and timing of hydrocarbon charge.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables quantitatively determining formation of carbonate cement and timing of hydrocarbon charge by accurately understanding process of forming dense oil and gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A formation of carbonate cement and timing of hydrocarbon charge quantitative determination method involves collecting dense reservoir core or outcrop sample, formulating into thin sheet, performing cathodoluminescence to determine diagenesis period of cement on the sheet, subjecting sheet to chloroform extraction, crushing the cement in stages, obtaining powder of different periods, reacting with concentrated phosphoric acid, obtaining carbon dioxide from powder of different stages, purifying carbon dioxide, performing isotope analysis using isotope ratio mass spectrometer, obtaining 13C18O16O value of different fractions, calculating temperature of formation of carbonate cement, according to the 13C18O16O value and empirical formula, obtaining homogenization temperature of associated brine inclusions of hydrocarbon inclusions, combining basin simulation to establish thermal history curve and marking homogenization temperature of the carbonate cement and brine inclusion on the thermal history curve. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a device for quantitatively determining formation of carbonate cement and timing of hydrocarbon charge.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); S03 (Scientific Instrumentation)

PT P
PN CN108552495-A
TI Preparing blood circulation phlegm removing cream involves crushing sardine and lactic acid solution, collecting, filtering, collecting filtrate, concentrating, putting tripe and hydrogen peroxide into container, and heating.
AU LONG A
   LIU F
   LIU Q
AE CHANGZHOU JINYI ADVERTISING MEDIA CO LTD (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018781014
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a blood circulation phlegm removing cream involves crushing sardine and lactic acid solution, collecting, filtering, collecting filtrate, concentrating to 55-60% volume of filtrate, putting tripe and hydrogen peroxide into container, heating at 45-50 degrees C temperature, coolling, standing, filtering, concentrating, placing substance and mixed enzyme in the container in an enzymatic hydrolysis tank, naturally ventilating at 33-38 degrees C temperature, stirring, collecting the enzymatic hydrolyzate, filtering and collecting the filtrate, taking 60-65 pts. wt. ethanol solution, 35-40 pts. wt. filtrate, 20-25 pts. wt. cortex acanthopanacis, 13-15 pts. wt. garden burnet and 10-16 pts. wt. teasel root, crushing and mixing, placing in a supercritical carbon dioxide device, and extracting with flow rate of 10 kg/hours of carbon dioxide under 7-9 MPa pressure, collecting the extract, distilling the ethanol, collecting the distillation residue, and distilling the residue.
   USE - Method used for preparing blood circulation phlegm removing cream (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing a blood circulation phlegm removing cream involves crushing sardine and lactic acid solution, collecting, filtering, collecting filtrate, concentrating to 55-60% volume of filtrate, putting tripe and hydrogen peroxide into container, heating at 45-50 degrees C temperature, coolling, standing, filtering, concentrating, placing substance and mixed enzyme in the container in an enzymatic hydrolysis tank, naturally ventilating at 33-38 degrees C temperature, stirring, collecting the enzymatic hydrolyzate, filtering and collecting the filtrate, taking 60-65 pts. wt. ethanol solution, 35-40 pts. wt. filtrate, 20-25 pts. wt. cortex acanthopanacis, 13-15 pts. wt. garden burnet and 10-16 pts. wt. teasel root, crushing and mixing, placing in a supercritical carbon dioxide device, and extracting with flow rate of 10 kg/hours of carbon dioxide under 7-9 MPa pressure, collecting the extract, distilling the ethanol, collecting the distillation residue, distilling the residue, adding with Aloe gel and Pleurotus eryngii into a ball mill for crushing, and collecting to obtain the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Compositions: Preparing a blood circulation phlegm removing cream involves crushing the sardine and lactic acid solution in 1:2-4 ratio, mixing the tripe and hydrogen peroxide in 1:3 ratio, and the concentrate, treated tripe and leek in 8:4:1 ratio. The enzyme contains pepsin, trypsinase in 2:1 ratio. The substance and mixed enzyme are added in 7:1-2 ratio. The distilled residue, Aloe gel and Pleurotus eryngii are added in 6:3:1 ratio.
   ACTIVITY - Vasotropic; Respiratory-Gen.

PT P
PN CN108102751-A; CN108102751-B
TI Energy-saving device for synthesis gas single-pass natural gas production used in power generation industry, has carbon dioxide removal device whose methane desorption gas outlet is connected to inlet of pre-heater.
AU GONG W
   YAN B
   LV J
   HOU N
   COLLODI G
AE WISON ENG CHINA CO LTD (WISO-Non-standard)
   FOSTER WHEELER ENERGIA OY (FOSX-C)
   WISON ENG CHINA CO LTD (WISO-Non-standard)
GA 201844984U
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a desulfurization device (2) which is connected to an outlet of an upstream raw synthesis gas device. An outlet of the desulfurization device is connected to a top of a first methanation reactor through a pre-heater (4). A bottom of another methanation reactor is connected to a first gas-liquid separation tank (14) after passing through a waste heat recovery unit. A top of first gas-liquid separation tank is connected to a top of another methanation reactor after passing through a heat exchanger (16). A bottom of first gas-liquid separation tank is connected to a process condensate delivery main pipe (29). A second gas-liquid separation tank (23) is connected, and a top of second gas-liquid separation tank is connected to a carbon dioxide removal device (25). A bottom of second gas-liquid separation tank is connected to process condensate delivery main pipe. A methane desorption gas outlet of carbon dioxide removal device is connected to an inlet of pre-heater.
   USE - Energy-saving device for synthesis gas single-pass natural gas production used in power generation industry, chemical industry, city gas industry, automobile fuel industry and other industries.
   ADVANTAGE - The use of a high-temperature circulating gas compressor in the methanation device is avoided, which reduces equipment investment and process energy consumption, and simplifies the process flow, makes the system easy to handle, and improves the operational safety.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an energy-saving method for synthesis gas single-pass natural gas production.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block flow diagram of the energy-saving device.
   Desulfurization device (2)
   Pre-heater (4)
   First gas-liquid separation tank (14)
   Heat exchanger (16)
   Second gas-liquid separation tank (23)
   Carbon dioxide removal device (25)
   Process condensate delivery main pipe (29)
DC H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).)
