AB    NOVELTY - Ecological cultivating Ganoderma lucidum                comprises e.g. (i) preparing culture medium and                packaging, (ii) sterilizing, inoculating,                sterilizing, naturally cooling, cultivating, and                sealing , (iii) inoculating, and cultivating,                moving the bacterial bag from the darkroom into the                imitative ecological greenhouse, continuously                planting, and cultivating G. lucidumin this                environment, and (c) adjusting the humidity in the                imitative ecological greenhouse to 68-72%, where                carbon dioxide concentration is 600-1000 ppm, and                harvesting, and stopping the powder.
   USE - The method is useful for ecological                cultivating G. lucidum.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the quality of G.                lucidum.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Ecological cultivating Ganoderma lucidum                comprises (i) preparing culture medium and                packaging to obtain G. lucidum culture medium, (ii)                sterilizing, inoculating the G. lucidumculture                medium under atmospheric-pressure, sterilizing,                naturally cooling and using sterile water to adjust                the moisture content of 55-65%, cultivating G.                lucidumculture medium strain, sterilizing the G.                lucidum, and sealing to obtain G. lucidumbags ,                (iii) G. lucidum planting management, (a)                inoculating the G. lucidum, placing in a dark room                for light culturing, controlling the temperature in                the dark room at 22-24 degrees C and the air                humidity is 67-72%, adjusting the concentration of                carbon dioxide of intermittent ventilation is                500-650 ppm, and cultivating to grow G. lucidum,                (b) moving the bacterial bag from the darkroom into                the imitative ecological greenhouse, continuously                planting, relieving the sealing of the bag,                maintaining the moisture content of the culture                medium in the bag within 55-65%, where the                temperature in the imitated ecological greenhouse                is 18-28 degrees C, the humidity in the imitative                ecological greenhouse is 83-93%, the carbon dioxide                concentration in the imitative ecological                greenhouse is 150-250 ppm, and the light intensity                in the imitative ecological greenhouse is 0-8000                lx, and cultivating G. lucidumin this environment,                (c) beginning G. lucidum to play powder, adjusting                the humidity in the imitative ecological greenhouse                to 68-72%, where carbon dioxide concentration is                600-1000 ppm, and harvesting G. lucidum, and                stopping the powder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The G. lucidum medium                  comprises 80-95 pts. wt. white mulberry branch,                  0.5-3 pts. wt. gypsum, 2-20 pts. wt. wheat bran,                  8-12 pts. wt. rotten southernwood dregs, and                  according to 0.9-1.4 kg per bag for the                  packaging. The mulberry has grain size of 15-25                  meshes and soaking in water for 20-28 hours                  before preparing the G. lucidum medium. The                  temperature in the imitative ecological                  greenhouse is adjusting according to the time                  period, the specific method is as follows                  7:00-9:00, adjusting temperature is 25-27 degrees                  C, 9:00-15:00, adjusting temperature at 26-28                  degrees C, 15:00-19:00, adjusting the temperature                  at 28-30 degrees C, 19:00- 7:00 the next day, and                  adjusting the temperature at 18-23 degrees C. The                  humidity in the imitation ecological greenhouse                  is adjusting according to the time period, the                  specific method is as follows during 7:00-9:00,                  and adjusting humidity to 90-93%, 9:00-15:00,                  adjusting humidity to 86-90% , 15:00-19:00,                  adjusting the humidity to 83-86%, and 19:00-7:00                  the next day, and adjusting humidity to 87-91%.                  The carbon dioxide concentration in the imitative                  ecological greenhouse is adjusted according to                  the time period, the specific method is as                  follows during 7:00-9:00, adjusting the carbon                  dioxide concentration 200-230 ppm, 9:00-15:00,                  adjusting the carbon dioxide concentration to                  150-180 ppm, 15:00-19:00, adjusting the carbon                  dioxide concentration to 180-200 ppm, 19:00-7:00                  the next day, and adjusting the carbon dioxide                  concentration to 230-250 ppm. The illumination in                  the imitation ecological greenhouse is adjusted                  according to the time period, the specific method                  is as follows during 7:00-9:00, adjust the light                  intensity to 3500-5000 lx, 9:00-15:00, adjusting                  light intensity for 6500-8000 lx, 15:00-19:00,                  adjusting the light intensity to 5000-6500 lx,                  19:00 -7:00 the next day, and adjusting the light                  intensity to 0-20 lx.

PN CN111333435-A; CN212199033-U
TI Carbon dioxide hydrate micro-fertilizer preparation device includes reactor unit comprising anchor frame scraper, thermal insulation jacket and temperature control system, gas supply pipeline, liquid supply pipeline, and compression unit.
AU LIU C
   HAO X
   LIU L
   MENG Q
   BU Q
AE QINGDAO INST MARINE GEOLOGY (CGSU-C)
GA 202061158X
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide hydrate micro-fertilizer preparation device includes a reactor unit, a gas supply pipeline, a liquid supply pipeline, and a compression unit. The gas supply pipeline and the liquid supply pipeline are respectively connected with the inner cavity of the reactor unit. The compression unit is located below the reactor unit, and the compression unit is in communication with the bottom of the reactor unit. The reactor unit includes reactor (2), anchor frame type wall scraper (3), thermal insulation jacket (1) and temperature control system. The reactor is provided with closed cavity. The outer wall of the reactor is provided with thermal insulation jacket and temperature control system. The anchor frame type wall scraper includes a rotating rod and wall scraper fixed at the lower portion of the rotating rod. The liquid supply pipeline includes liquid inlet (9), booster pump (25), cooling system (11) and high-pressure spray port (16).
   USE - Carbon dioxide hydrate micro-fertilizer preparation device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device enables to synthesize micro-fertilizer of carbon dioxide hydrate that has high saturation of carbon dioxide, high gas storage per unit volume, good self-protection effect, and is convenient for storage and transportation. The composition of the trace elements of carbon dioxide hydrate micro-fertilizer can be adjusted, and the product after decomposition of micro-fertilizer is carbon dioxide, water and trace elements, with no pollution and residue. The obtained product does not change the nature of the soil after application, can provide water, carbon dioxide and trace elements required for plant growth at the same time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing carbon dioxide hydrate micro-fertilizer, which involves vacuuming the reactor and exhausting the air in the reactor; pressurizing the carbon dioxide gas to 2.5-3.5 MegaPascal and delivering it to the reactor, controlling the temperature of the reactor at 3-5 degrees C; preparing the aqueous solution and spraying it into the reactor by atomization; scraping the hydrated micro-fertilizer on the inner surface of the reactor and dropping it into the material bin; compacting carbon dioxide hydrate micro-fertilizer; and quickly freezing and shaping carbon dioxide hydrate micro-fertilizer.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic diagram of carbon dioxide hydrate micro-fertilizer preparation device.
   Thermal insulation jacket (1)
   Reactor (2)
   Anchor frame type wall scraper (3)
   Liquid inlet (9)
   Cooling system (11)
   High-pressure spray port (16)
   Booster pump (25)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The carbon dioxide gas is pressurized by the gas booster pump, and is allowed to enters the reactor along the inlet pipe. The aqueous solution containing trace elements involved in the synthesis of carbon dioxide hydrate micro-fertilizer is boosted to 4-7 MegaPascal by the booster pump and cooled to 3-5 degrees C by the cooling system. It is sprayed into the reactor through the high-pressure spray port at the top of the reactor, and the atomized fine water particles and high-pressure carbon dioxide gas are in direct contact in the reactor. The synthesized micro-fertilizer is attached to the inner surface of the reactor. The obtained carbon dioxide hydrate micro-fertilizer is quickly frozen at -5 degrees C to obtain a shaped solid carbon dioxide hydrate fertilizer.

TI Recycling acetone and carbon dioxide in sodium            phenolate carboxylation reaction by resource            utilization method, comprises e.g. dissolving sodium            phenate in organic solvent acetone, carrying out first            and second carboxylation reaction, subjecting to            gas-liquid separation and recycling.
AU WANG Y
   SUN C
   HUANG Y
   XU W
AE UNIV YANGZHOU (UYYZ-C)
GA 2022A41213
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling acetone and carbon dioxide in sodium                phenolate carboxylation reaction by resource                utilization method, comprises e.g. dissolving                sodium phenate in organic solvent acetone in sodium                phenate solution preparation device, mixing sodium                phenate solution and carbon dioxide with gas-liquid                dispersion in high-efficacy gas-liquid dispersion                mixing reactor, and carrying out carboxylation                reaction of sodium phenate and carbon dioxide to                generate sodium salicylate, subjecting material to                vacuum flash operation, and obtaining solid-phase                material through vacuum flash operation i.e. sodium                salicylate, cooling and condensing gas phase                material in cooling condensation device, and                subjecting material to gas-liquid separation in                gas-liquid separation device, carrying out second                carboxylation reaction of gas-phase material, using                as reaction raw material carbon dioxide, and                conveying liquid phase material acetone to first                step solution preparation and recycling as                solvent.
   USE - The method is useful for recycling acetone and                carbon dioxide in sodium phenolate carboxylation                reaction by resource utilization method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can remove solvent acetone used in                process of carboxylation reaction of sodium phenate                and carbon dioxide to prepare sodium salicylate;                can simultaneously remove acetone and unreacted                carbon dioxide; is safe and environmentally                friendly, which meets requirements of clean                production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recycling acetone and carbon dioxide in sodium                phenolate carboxylation reaction by resource                utilization method comprises (i) dissolving sodium                phenate in organic solvent acetone in sodium                phenate solution preparation device to obtain                sodium phenate solution, where the mass ratio of                sodium phenate to acetone in solution is 1 kg:4 l                to 1 kg:10 l, (ii) mixing sodium phenate solution                and carbon dioxide with gas-liquid dispersion in                high-efficacy gas-liquid dispersion mixing reactor,                and carrying out carboxylation reaction of sodium                phenate and carbon dioxide to generate sodium                salicylate, (iii) subjecting material to vacuum                flash operation, and obtaining solid-phase material                through vacuum flash operation i.e. sodium                salicylate, (iv) cooling and condensing gas phase                material in cooling condensation device, and (v)                subjecting material to gas-liquid separation in                gas-liquid separation device, carrying out second                carboxylation reaction of gas-phase material, using                as reaction raw material carbon dioxide, and                conveying liquid phase material acetone to first                step solution preparation and recycling as                solvent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molar ratio of                  sodium phenate to carbon dioxide is 1:1.2-1:2                  during the second-step carboxylation                  reaction.Preferred Method: In the method, the                  reaction operating pressure is 0.4-0.8 MPa, and                  the reaction temperature is 80-120&#8451;. In                  the step (iii), the absolute pressure of                  operation in vacuum flash evaporation equipment                  is 0.02-0.08 MPa.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The high-efficacy                  gas-liquid dispersion mixing device in the step                  (ii) carboxylation reaction process is static                  mixer, jet mixer, or Venturi mixer. The vacuum                  flashing device in the step (iii) is flash tank,                  flash cooler or flash tower. The cooling and                  condensing device in the step (iv) of cooling and                  condensation is a casing condenser, a tubular                  condenser or a plate condenser, and the outlet                  temperature of condensate is 20-50&#8451;.                  The gas-liquid separation device in the step (v)                  is in oil-water separator, liquid-gas separator                  or Sofitel gas-water separator.

PN CN107722331-A
TI Preparing bone tissue engineering support with diplopore structure by two-step pressure expansion foaming technology of supercritical carbon dioxide comprises e.g. mixing polymer powder with additive and introducing carbon dioxide.
AU GUAN Y
   CHEN C
   PENG H
   YAO S
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 201817157C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing bone tissue engineering support with diplopore structure by two-step pressure expansion foaming technology of supercritical carbon dioxide comprises (i) mixing polymer powder with additive by physical pressing in a cylindrical mold, (ii) placing cylindrical mold in a high pressure kettle of supercritical carbon dioxide foaming system, introducing carbon dioxide, and maintaining pressure, (iii) releasing pressure in the kettle, and forming gas nucleus of large hole, (iv) balancing mold under intermediate pressure, (v) reducing pressure, and forming large pores around cylindrical mold.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing bone tissue engineering support by two-step pressure expansion foaming technology of supercritical carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has mild and simple operation condition, stable process; and does not involve use of organic solvents.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing bone tissue engineering support with diplopore structure by two-step pressure expansion foaming technology of supercritical carbon dioxide comprises (i) mixing the polymer powder with the additive by physical pressing in a cylindrical mold, (ii) placing the cylindrical mold in a high pressure kettle of supercritical carbon dioxide foaming system, introducing carbon dioxide at 35-60 degrees C under 10-30 MPa pressure, and maintaining pressure for more than 2 hours to bring the carbon dioxide to a supercritical state, where the supercritical carbon dioxide and the cylindrical mold is fully contacted and supercritical carbon dioxide is dissolved in the cylindrical mold, (iii) slowly releasing pressure in the step (i), releasing the pressure in the kettle to an intermediate pressure to 6-18 MPa, controlling decompression time at 1 minute-6 hours, and forming gas nucleus of large hole in the cylindrical mold, where the growth degree of the gas nucleus depends on the size of the intermediate pressure, (iv) balancing the mold under intermediate pressure in the high pressure kettle for a period of time, where the balancing time is more than 1 hour, (v) rapidly releasing pressure by one step, reducing the pressure in the kettle from intermediate pressure to ambient pressure, where the decompression time is less than 2 seconds, forming large pores around the cylindrical mold, where the large pore size is 100-470 mu m and tissue engineering scaffold with diplopore is of 10-75 mu m.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The supercritical carbon dioxide foaming system comprises a carbon dioxide cylinder, cooler, high pressure pump, high pressure kettle, constant-temperature water bath boiler, pressure gauge, stop valve, precision needle valve and high throughput ball valve. The carbon dioxide cylinder, cooler, high pressure pump and high pressure kettle are orderly connected with stop valve. The high-pressure kettle, constant temperature water bath boiler and high-pressure kettle is connected with the pressure meter. The precision needle valve and high-throughput ball valve are in parallel and connected with the outlet end of the stop valve.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The additive is hydroxyapatite, tricalcium phosphate, potassium phosphate and sodium phosphate, and the content is 1-10%. The additive is small molecule drug, protein and micron/sub-micron grade or nano grade protein composite microsphere, and the content is 0.1-5%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polymer is polyester polymer, the polyester polymer is polylactic acid, polyglycolic acid, poly(lactic acid-glycolic acid) copolymer and/or polycaprolactone.

(including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); A35 (Other processing and general - including vulcanisation, welding of plastics and adhesive processes. The side length of the titanium                dioxide is 20-100 nm, and the thickness is 5-7                nm.
   USE - Photo-generated electron-hot electron enhanced                plasma photocatalyst used in reducing carbon                dioxide to methane (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The photo-generated electron-hot electron                enhanced plasma photocatalyst realizes the                enrichment of electron in the bismuth tungstate                nano point, stabilizes surface plasma resonance                effect, to promote plasma thermal electron to                continuously generate, enhancement mechanism of                photo-generated electron-heat electron and                high-efficiency photo-catalytic carbon dioxide                reduction to methane.

   1. a methods for preparing photogenerated                electron-hot electron enhanced plasma                photocatalyst, which involves:
   a. preparing titanium dioxide                nanosheets;
   b. mixing bismuth nitrate, sodium tungstate,                ethanol and water, adding nitric acid to adjust the                pH to 0.1-1 to form a bismuth tungstate                precursor;
   c. dispersing the titanium dioxide nanosheets                in a mixed solution of ethanol and water to form a                titanium dioxide dispersion; d. adding bismuth                tungstate precursor to the titanium dioxide                dispersion liquid, carrying out hydrothermal                reaction, washing and drying to obtain product;                and
   2. a method for using photogenerated                electron-hot electron enhanced plasma                photocatalyst, which involves:
   mixing the photogenerated electron-hot                electron enhanced plasma photocatalyst with water                according to a mass ratio of (10-30):1, and then                using visible-near-infrared light of 400-1100 nm                for illumination, the light intensity is                200-400mW/cm2, and the lighting time is 170-190                minutes.

The hydrothermal reaction is                  then carried out at a temperature of                  180-200&#8451; for 16-24 hours. The molar                  ratio of bismuth nitrate and sodium tungstate is                  1:(1-3). The volume ratio of ethanol and water is                  1:(1-2). TI System for generating electricity by using combined solar-assisted fuel reformer and oxy-combustion film reactor, has carbon monoxide combustion system for producing carbon dioxide that powers first gas turbine, where two gas turbines are connected to two electric generators, respectively..
AU HABIB M A

AE UNIV DHAHRAN KING FAHD PETROLEUM & MINER (UYKF-C)

AB    NOVELTY - The system (5) has a solar-heated air system (13) producing first hot air (20) for heating of a fuel reformer (32) and a second hot air (18) for separation of oxygen. The fuel reformer reacts a fuel and a first steam to produce hydrogen and carbon monoxide. A hydrogen combustion system (39) produces a second steam and powers a first gas turbine (42), where the second steam is set as a steam source (47) for the fuel reformer. A carbon monoxide combustion system (44) fed carbon monoxide from the fuel reformer and fed oxygen separated from the second hot air by an ion transport film (21). The carbon monoxide combustion system produces carbon dioxide that powers a second gas turbine (48). The first gas turbine and the second gas turbine are connected to a first and second electric generator, respectively.
   USE - System for generating electricity by using a combined solar-assisted fuel reformer and an oxy-combustion film reactor.
   ADVANTAGE - The system performs oxy-combustion process to improve combustion process, reduces amount of nitric oxide emissions, and captures resultant carbon dioxide at an exit section, and increases high capacity to absorb heat so as to absorb energy and improvecombustion efficiency, A solar reflector adjust a position of the sun to reflect sunlight onto the solar collector for warming the working fluid e.g., air, in the solar collector.

AE SHANTOU SHANGYI CRAFT CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021168508
AB    NOVELTY - Complex whitening facial mask liquid comprises Bletilla striata, Angelica dahurica, stiff silkworm, radix asparagi, Chrysanthemum flower, peony, goat milk extract, and beef tallow extracting solution.
   USE - As complex whitening facial mask liquid is used for human skin, preferably nourishing human skin, activating muscles and detoxifying.
   ADVANTAGE - The complex whitening facial mask liquid has no various adverse reactions, and excellent whitening effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the complex whitening facial mask liquid, comprising (a) weighing an appropriate amount of each medicinal material, mixing and pulverizing, performing supercritical extraction of the powder with carbon dioxide, extracting with anhydrous ethanol as a solvent, and concentrating under reduced pressure to recover the ethanol to obtain an alcohol extract concentrate, adding water to decoct the medicinal residue from the previous step of supercritical carbon dioxide extraction twice, where the first time is 8-10 times the amount of water for 40 minutes, and the second time 10-14 times the amount of water for 80 minutes, after filtering, combining the filtrate and concentrating to the relative density of 1.30-1.50 at 50 degrees C, drying the extract to obtain the extract, which is enriched with a macroporous adsorption resin column to obtain a brown powder, mixing the powder with the brown-yellow viscous oily substance obtained by supercritical extraction of carbon dioxide to obtain the Chinese medicine extract, and (b) adding the required ratio of goat milk extract and avocado extract to the traditional Chinese medicine extract, and adding the mixture according to the following ratio comprises 4 pts. wt. glycerin, 3 pts. wt. 1,3-butanediol, 0.1 pt. wt. sodium sulfate, 0.1 pt. wt. preservative, 0.1 pt. wt. flavor, 5 pts. wt. Chinese medicine mixture, and 10 pts. wt. deionized water.

PN US2021025266-A1; US11236594-B2
TI In situ thermal recovering hydrocarbons from reservoir, comprises e.g. mixing oxygen-enriched mixture, fuel, feedwater and additive to direct-contact steam generator to obtain steam-based mixture, and producing produced fluid.
AU ACOSTA R H
   PUGSLEY T
AE SUNCOR ENERGY INC (SNCR-C)
GA 2021115297
AB    NOVELTY - In situ thermal recovering (M1) hydrocarbons from reservoir, comprises: providing oxygen-enriched mixture, fuel, feedwater and additive to a direct-contact steam generator (DCSG), where the additive is carried by a heat carrier gas; operating the DCSG comprising contacting the feed-water and the additive with hot combustion gas to obtain steam-based mixture comprising steam, carbon dioxide and additive, where the heat carrier gas provides stability to the additive during operation of the DCSG; injecting the steam-based mixture or stream derived from the steam-based mixture into the reservoir to mobilize the hydrocarbons in it; and producing produced fluid comprising the hydrocarbons.
   USE - The method is useful for in situ thermal recovering hydrocarbons from reservoir.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) steam-assisted gravity drainage (SAGD) recovering (M2) hydrocarbons from reservoir, comprising providing oxygen-enriched mixture, fuel, feed-water and additive to the direct-contact steam generator, the additive comprising well integrity agent chosen from the group consisting of anticorrosive agent, antifouling agent, scale inhibitor and thermally stable cement, operating the DCSG, comprising contacting feedwater and additive with hot combustion gas to obtain steam-based mixture comprising steam, carbon dioxide and additive, injecting the steam-based mixture or stream derived from the steam-based mixture into the reservoir via SAGD injection well to mobilize hydrocarbons in it, and recovering the hydrocarbons as produced fluids from a SAGD production well; and
   (2) in situ thermal recovering (M3) hydrocarbons from reservoir, comprising providing oxygen-enriched mixture, fuel, feedwater and additive in liquid state to a DCSG, operating the DCSG comprising contacting feedwater and additive with hot combustion gas to obtain steam-based mixture comprising steam, carbon dioxide and additive in gaseous state and/or dispersed state, injecting the steam-based mixture or stream derived from the steam-based mixture into the reservoir to mobilize hydrocarbons in it, and producing produced fluid comprising the hydrocarbons.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the method (M1): the additive comprises at least one of viscosity reduction agent or well integrity agent, and at least one of viscosity reduction agent, anticorrosive agent, antifouling agent, scale inhibitor or thermally stable cement; and the heat carrier gas comprises carbon dioxide or nitrogen, and steam at a lower temperature than outgoing steam. In the method (M2): the concentration of the additive in the steam-based mixture is 0.1-30 wt.%; the additive further comprises at least one of viscosity reduction agent, it is in a dispersed state; and feedwater is provided as a feedwater stream and the additive is provided as a separate additive stream to the DCSG, is contacted with the hot combustion gas for a longer time period than the additive stream, comprises at least part of produced water and fuel comprises at least part of the produced gas, and further comprises makeup water and the concentration of the makeup water in the feedwater is up to 5 wt.% of the feedwater. In the method (M3): the additive is provided as liquid in pure form to the DCSG, provided in solution in solvent comprising water, and provided in a gaseous state in the steam-based mixture; and the additive comprises at least one of ammonia, urea or volatile amine, preferably ammonia as ammonium hydroxide solution.

TI Method of packaging organic meat such as ground beef, involves flushing bag containing wrapped meat products with gas mixture comprising blend of nitrogen and carbon dioxide and being free of carbon monoxide.
AU VAN DORN R
AE SUN BEEF LLC (SUNB-Non-standard)
GA 2020A3384L
AB    NOVELTY - The method (200) involves placing (202) each of the multiple pre-proportioned organic meat products on a polyethylene tray configured to receive the meat. A film is wrapped (204) around each of the meat products and its respective polyethylene tray. Multiple wrapped meat products are positioned (206) in a bag, where the bag contains ambient air in an initial state. The ambient air is purged (208) from the bag using a first vacuum cycle and the remaining air is purged (210) from the bag using a second vacuum cycle. The bag is flushed (212) with a gas mixture which comprises a blend of nitrogen (N) and carbon dioxide (CO2), where the gas mixture is free of carbon monoxide (CO). The bag containing the gas mixture is sealed (214).
   USE - Method of packaging organic meat such as ground beef (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon-monoxide-free gas blend which includes a combination of carbon dioxide and nitrogen, extends the shelf life of the meat and preserve its natural color, while maintaining the meat's certified organic status.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a meat packaging system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the method of packaging organic meat.
   Method of packaging organic meat (200)
   Step for placing each pre-proportioned organic meat products on a polyethylene tray configured to receive the meat (202)
   Step for wrapping a film around each of the meat products and respective tray (204)
   Step for positioning the wrapped meat products in a bag (206)
   Step for purging the ambient air from the bag using a first vacuum cycle (208)
   Step for purging any remaining air from the bag using a second vacuum cycle (210)
   Step for flushing the bag with a gas mixture (212)
   Step for sealing the bag containing the gas mixture (214)

TI Method for efficient byproduct recovery and reuse            in a fuel cell system, by making amount of nitrogen            introduced to exhaust when combusting second portion of            reformed fuel with oxygen enriched gas lower than when            combusting with air.
AU PETERSON E C
   JAMES S M
AE MICROSOFT TECHNOLOGY LICENSING LLC (MICT-C)
   MICROSOFT TECHNOLOGY LICENSING LLC (MICT-C)
   MICROSOFT TECHNOLOGY LICENSING LLC (MICT-C)
GA 202043857B
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves reforming a fuel                containing methane with steam to produce a reformed                fuel having methane, carbon monoxide, and hydrogen.                The reformed fuel is introduced into an anode of a                fuel cell (101) while introducing air into a                cathode of the fuel cell. An electrochemical                reaction is performed between a first portion of                the reformed fuel introduced to the anode and                oxygen in the air introduced to the cathode of the                fuel cell to produce electricity and an exhaust                from the anode. The exhaust has carbon dioxide,                water, and a second portion of the reformed fuel.                The method also involves combusting, in a                combustion chamber (111), the second portion of the                reformed fuel in the exhaust with an oxygen                enriched gas, to convert the second portion of the                reformed fuel into additional carbon dioxide and                water with at least a reduced amount of nitrogen                introduced to the exhaust when compared to                combusting the second portion of the reformed fuel                with air.
   USE - Method for efficient byproduct recovery and                reuse in a fuel cell system (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - A degree of polluting the exhaust from the                fuel cell system is reduced by combusting the                excess fuel with an oxygen enriched gas instead of                air. By not burning the exhaust in the combustion                chamber with air, the excess fuel in the exhaust                from the primary stage may be completely converted                into carbon dioxide and steam at the one or more                additional fuel cell stages, thus, recovery and                reuse of the byproducts can be efficiently                achieved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                fuel cell system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing is a schematic diagram of a fuel                cell system.
   Fuel cell (101)
   Reformer (110)
   Combustion chamber (111)
   Heat exchanger (116)
   Oxygen enricher (117)

TI Method for monitoring metabolic rate measurements, involves determining metabolic rate value based on determined blood oxygen and blood carbon-dioxide concentration value, and outputting determined metabolic rate value at display of device.
AU MARASCO C
   PEREZ A
   EHRMAN J
   HOILETT O
AE UNIV VANDERBILT (UVAN-C)
GA 202029330D
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves receiving a first set of the measurement values derived from measurements using a first non-invasive reflectance- or transmission-based transdermal or transcutaneous sensor located on a device placed on or adjacent to a subject at each acquisition on an intermittent basis, by a processor (122). An blood oxygen concentration value is determined from the first set of measurements. A second set of measurements is received from the non-invasive reflectance- or transmission-based transdermal or transcutaneous sensors located on the device placed on the subject at each acquisition on an intermittent basis. A blood carbon-dioxide concentration value is determined from second set of measurements. A metabolic rate value is determined based on the determined blood oxygen concentration value and the determined blood carbon-dioxide concentration value on an intermittent basis. The determined metabolic rate value is outputted at a display (124) of the device by the processor.
   USE - Method for monitoring metabolic rate measurements used in sports industry, professional athletes use the information to monitor diets and health for the purpose of adjusting diets and exercise routines.
   ADVANTAGE - The use of optical sensors at a peripheral area of the body reduces the complexity of measurements. The users can track activities and the time of the day when metabolic rates are at the highest and lowest, allowing athletes to reach their peak performance naturally with the device's ability to track metabolic rates in real-time. The environmental chambers and indirect calorimeters provide high accuracy measurements and are generally only be accessible in clinical or athletic training settings.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a system for monitoring metabolic rate measurements; and
   (2) a non-transitory computer readable medium storing program for monitoring metabolic rate measurements.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the system configured to monitor metabolic rate measurements through optical sensors.
   Metabolic rate measurement system (100)
   Preamplifier circuit (118)
   Timer circuit (120)
   Processor (122)
   Display (124)

PN CN110859807-A
TI Solid dispersion micropowder used for preparing solubilized immediate-release granules of nimesulide, prepared by performing supercritical fluid crystallization using polyvinylpyrrolidone and hypromellose as dispersion carrier materials.
AU LIU G
   HONG J
   LING R
AE HONZ PHARM CO LTD (HONZ-Non-standard)
GA 202020978Q
AB    NOVELTY - Solid dispersion micropowder is prepared by performing supercritical fluid crystallization using polyvinylpyrrolidone (PVP) K-30 and hypromellose (HPMC) as dispersion carrier materials.
   USE - The solid dispersion micropowder is used for preparing solubilized immediate-release granules, and tablets, capsules or suspensions of nimesulide.
   ADVANTAGE - The solid dispersion micropowder is prepared by simple operation, has mild conditions, excellent controllability, dispersibility and stability, no solvent residue, small particle size, saturation solubility more than 5 times with pH of 6.8 of the nimesulide raw drug, and equilibrium dissolution rate which is more than 2 times that of the drug substance and nimesulide granules, and is quickly dissolved in dissolution medium with pH of 6.8.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the solid dispersion micropowder involving (i) adding dichloromethane/methanol mixed solvent containing 0.1 vol.% analytically pure hydrochloric acid to the mixture of nimesulide, PVP K-30 and HPMC, and completely dissolving, (ii) continuously introducing liquefied carbon dioxide to the high-pressure crystallization kettle of the supercritical fluid crystallization equipment system by a high-pressure pump through two symmetrical channels and solution channels at 45 degrees or 60 degrees , and controlling the temperature in the kettle at 35-50 degrees C and the pressure at 75-88 bar, (iii) passing a mixed solvent of dichloromethane/methanol containing 0.1 vol.% analytically pure hydrochloric acid through the intermediate solution channel, replacing the mixed solvent with the solution prepared in step (i) for continuous injection after the system reaches equilibrium, rapidly mixing the solvent with the supercritical carbon dioxide and discharging out of the bottom of the kettle for recovery and settling the formed solute particles at the bottom of the crystallization kettle, and (iv) introducing carbon dioxide for 10-30 minutes to remove the residual organic solvent, releasing the pressure and collecting the sample in the autoclave to obtain the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The weight ratio of nimesulide, PVP K-30 and HPMC is 10-30:20-50:30-60, preferably 20:40:40. In the step (i), the volume ratio of mixed solvent of dichloromethane and methanol is 4:1-1:1, preferably 3:1, and the solution concentration is 25-75 mg/ml, preferably 50 mg/ml. Preferred Conditions: In step (ii), the carbon dioxide flow rate is controlled at 25-200 ml/minute, preferably 100 ml/minute, the solution flow rate is controlled at 0.5-5 ml/minute, preferably 2 ml/minute, the pressure is controlled at 80 bar, and the temperature is controlled at 40 degrees C. Preferred Method: The solid dispersion micropowder is prepared into granules, tablets, capsules or suspensions by adding diluent, disintegrant, flavoring agent, lubricant and/or surfactant.

PT P
PN CN110040915-A
TI Treating high concentration organic phosphorus wastewater comprises placing high-concentration wastewater into a conditioning tank, pumping the wastewater, decomposing the organic phosphorus, oxidizing, separating and treating deeply.
AU WANG H
   LV Y
   SHEN S
AE SUZHOU WUWEI ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY CO (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 201966241N
AB    NOVELTY - Treating high concentration organic phosphorus wastewater comprises (1) placing high-concentration wastewater into a conditioning tank and adjusting the water quality to neutral; (2) pumping the wastewater from the conditioning tank to the anaerobic baffled reactor (ABR) anaerobic tank, decomposing the organic phosphorus in the wastewater, separating organic phosphorus and decomposing most of the organic matter into methane, carbon dioxide and water; (3) passing the supernatant in the ABR anaerobic tank in the MBR membrane bioreactor, oxidizing most of the secondary phosphorus and phosphorus in the wastewaterto a positive phosphorus precipitate; (4) passing the supernatant in the MBR membrane bioreactor in the crystal dephosphorizer and separating phosphorus; and (5) passing the clear liquid in the crystal dephosphorizer in the electro-flocculation dephosphorizer, treating deeply the remaining small amount of non-oxidized secondary phosphorus and phosphorus in water and discharging.
   USE - The method is useful for treating high concentration organic phosphorus wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has less dosage, less sludge production and stable phosphorus and other indicators.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Treating high concentration organic phosphorus wastewater comprises (1) placing high-concentration wastewater into a conditioning tank and adjusting the water quality to neutral; (2) pumping the wastewater from the conditioning tank to the anaerobic baffled reactor (ABR) anaerobic tank, decomposing the organic phosphorus in the wastewater, separating organic phosphorus to obtain orthophosphorus and decomposing most of the organic matter into methane, carbon dioxide and water; (3) passing the supernatant in the ABR anaerobic tank in the MBR membrane bioreactor, oxidizing most of the secondary phosphorus and phosphorus in the wastewaterto a positive phosphorus precipitate and decomposing the remaining small amount of organic matter in the wastewater into methane, carbon dioxide and water; (4) passing the supernatant in the MBR membrane bioreactor in the crystal dephosphorizer and separating phosphorus; and (5) passing the clear liquid in the crystal dephosphorizer in the electro-flocculation dephosphorizer, treating deeply the remaining small amount of non-oxidized secondary phosphorus and phosphorus in the water and discharging after reaching the standard.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: In the step (1), the high-concentration wastewater is placed into the regulating tank and the water quality is adjusted to be neutral by adding acid or alkali into the regulating tank to adjust the water quality to neutral. In the step (4), the supernatant in the MBR membrane bioreactor enters the crystal dephosphorizer to separate the phosphorus by adding calcium chloride, acid, alkali and PAM into the crystal dephosphorizer, converting the orthophosphorus into a calcium hydroxyphosphate precipitate and separating from the water. In the step (5), passing clear liquid in the electro-flocculation dephosphorizer, deeply treating a small amount of non-oxidized secondary phosphorus and phosphorus remaining in the water by adding acid, alkali, PAM and electrolyte into electro-flocculation dephosphorizer and separating unoxidized phosphorus. Preferred Components: The heating coil is arranged in the adjusting tank to adjust the temperature of the wastewater in the regulating tank to 20-35 degrees C.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)

An alarm signal automatic generation part outputs alarm to obtain operating information of the ventilation system to basis. A blower is fixed in an inner side of a duct and provides outer side air into the confined space. PN CN108707238-A; CN108707238-B
TI New metal-organic framework material cobalt-pyridine-2,5-dicarboxylate-tetra-N-phenylbenzidin useful as adsorbent material for selective adsorption of carbon dioxide.
AU ZANG S
   ZHANG Q
   LI H
AE UNIV ZHENGZHOU (UYZZ-C)
   UNIV ZHENGZHOU (UYZZ-C)
GA 201886123V
AB    NOVELTY - Metal-organic framework material cobalt-pyridine-2,5-dicarboxylate-tetra-N-phenylbenzidin (I) is new. (I) belongs to hexagonal system having space group P-62c, unit cell parameters a and b is 14.3390(2) Angstrom , c is 14.3590(3) Angstrom , and V is 2556.78(11) Angstrom 3, where H6pydc structure is 5,5',5''-(pyridine-2,4,6-triyl)triisophthalic acid (I), and TPB is 1,3,5-tri(pyridin-3-yl)benzene (II); (I) is obtained by using Co-pydc-TPB Co3( mu 3-OH)(COO)6(TPB) trinuclear cluster as secondary structural unit, and metallic cobalt exhibits a double valence state, and consists of two Co2+ and one Co3+, each of which has six coordination positions and exhibits an octahedral configuration.
   USE - (I) is useful as adsorbent material for selective adsorption of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I): has high thermal stability, excellent water resistance, alkali resistance and high carbon dioxide selective adsorption property.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Metal-organic framework material cobalt-pyridine-2,5-dicarboxylate-tetra-N-phenylbenzidine (Co-pydc-TPB) of formula (Co3( mu 3-OH)H6(pydc)(TPB)).(3dimethylformamide) (8H2O) (I) is new. (I) belongs to hexagonal system having space group P-62c, unit cell parameters a and b is 14.3390(2) Angstrom , c is 14.3590(3) Angstrom , and V is 2556.78(11) Angstrom 3, where H6pydc structure is 5,5',5''-(pyridine-2,4,6-triyl)triisophthalic acid of formula (I), and TPB is 1,3,5-tri(pyridin-3-yl)benzene of formula (II); (I) is obtained by using Co-pydc-TPB Co3( mu 3-OH)(COO)6(TPB) trinuclear cluster as secondary structural unit, and metallic cobalt exhibits a double valence state, and consists of two Co2+ and one Co3+, each of which has six coordination positions and exhibits an octahedral configuration. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparation of (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of m(I), comprises dissolving cobalt(II) nitrate hexahydrate, H6pydc and TPB in mixed solution of dimethylformamide and deionized water, ultrasonicating, adding HBF4 using a pipette, then transferring mixture to a stainless steel autoclave, reacting at constant temperature in an oven, cooling to room temperature, filtering solid and washing with dimethylformamide to obtain crystal.

PN CN108158861-A; CN108158861-B
TI Preparing essence containing peony essential oil comprises e.g. mixing fresh peony petals with water, crushing, ultrasonically treating, obtaining a gelatinous peony extract and carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide extraction.
AU CHEN Z
   SHEN Z
AE ANHUI XINYUANKANG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
   ANHUI HANBANG TECHNOLOGY CONSULTING CO (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2018493327
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing essence containing peony essential oil comprises (i) mixing fresh peony petals with water, crushing, ultrasonically treating, and separating peony juice, (ii) obtaining a gelatinous peony extract, (iii) carrying out the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction , (iv) mixing glycerol, hyaluronic acid, vitamin E, orange essential oil, Rhodiola oil, grape seed oil, water and peony essential oil, (v) obtaining an aqueous phase liquid, (vi)obtaining an oil phase liquid and (vii) adding the oil phase liquid obtained in (vi) to the aqueous phase liquid obtained in step (v) and homogenizing.
   USE - The essence is useful for whitening, reducing wrinkle, desalting splash and enhancing skin elasticity.
   ADVANTAGE - The essence has anti-aging function.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing essence containing peony essential oil comprises (i) mixing fresh peony petals with water, crushing, ultrasonically treating, and separating peony juice by centrifugation, (ii) adding a superabsorbent resin to the peony juice obtained in step (i), stirring for 10-20 minutes and allowing to stand for 20-30 minutes to obtain a gelatinous peony extract, (iii) carrying out the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of the gelatinous peony extract obtained in step (ii) to obtain peony essential oil, (iv) mixing 10-15 pts. wt. glycerol, 8-12 pts. wt. hyaluronic acid, 1-2 pts. wt. vitamin E, 5-8 pts. wt. orange essential oil, 2-4 pts. wt. Rhodiola oil, 6-9 pts. wt. grape seed oil, 20-25 pts. wt. water and 7-10 pts. wt. peony essential oil, (v) mixing glycerol, hyaluronic acid, and water and stirring uniformly at 70-80 degrees C to obtain an aqueous phase liquid, (vi) taking vitamin E, orange oil, Rhodiola oil, grape seed oil and peony perfume mix and stirring at 70-80 degrees C to obtain an oil phase liquid and (vii) adding the oil phase liquid obtained in (vi) to the aqueous phase liquid obtained in step (v) and homogenizing for 15-20 minutes to obtain an essence containing peony essential oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the raw material of the super absorbent resin comprises 12-15 pts. wt. corn starch, 65-70 pts. wt. acrylic acid, 5-8 pts. wt. corn stalk powder, 0.5-0.7 pts. wt. potassium persulfate, 0.06-0.08 pts. wt. N,N'-methylenebisacrylamide, 85-95 pts. wt. 85-95 sodium hydroxide solution and 15-17 pts. wt. water. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the superabsorbent resin is obtained by (a) adding corn starch to 70-80 degrees C water and stirring at a constant temperature for 40-50 minutes to obtain a gelatinized starch solution A, (b) adding acrylic acid to the sodium hydroxide solution, stirring for 20-30 minutes, adding corn stover powder, potassium persulfate, N, N'-methylene bis acrylamide, and stirring uniformly to obtain a mixed solution B and (iii) mixing the starch solution A obtained in the step (i) and the mixed solution B obtained in the step (ii), placing in a microwave reactor for microwave treatment for 3-5 minutes, where the microwave power is 250-300 W, drying and crushing. In the step (ii), the weight ratio of fresh peony petals to water is 1:1-1.3. In the step (ii), the adding amount of the superabsorbent resin is 0.02-0.04 times the weight of the peony juice. In the step (iii), in the super near carbon dioxide extraction, the extraction pressure is 23-35 MPa, the extraction temperature is 35-45 degrees C, the extraction time is 50-90 minutes, and a carbon dioxide flow rate is 15-18 l/hour. In the step (iii), the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device used is having two separation vessels, where the pressure in the first separating kettle is 8-10 MPa, the temperature is 40-45 degrees C, the pressure in the second separating kettle is 3-5 MPa and the temperature is 30-35 degrees C. In the (iii), the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction uses cyclohexane as an cosolvent and the flow of cyclohexane is 6-8% of the flow of carbon dioxide. In the step (i), the ultrasonic power of the ultrasonic treatment is 200-300 w, the frequency is 28-32 Hz and the ultrasonic time is 15-20 minutes. In the step (vii), the homogeneous stirring speed is 2800-3200 revolutions/minute.
   ACTIVITY - Dermatological.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. TI High simulation fishing bait comprises e.g. polypropylene carbonate, 1, 4-butanediol, methylene diphenyl diisocyanate, corn starch, filling agent, lubricant, foaming agent, carbon dioxide, citrate plasticizer, amino acid and polylactic acid.
AU MA H
AE ANHUI HONGFEI FISHING TACKLE CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 201808593J
AB    NOVELTY - High simulation fishing bait comprises 20-30 pts. wt. polypropylene carbonate (PPC), 10-15 pts. wt. 1, 4-butanediol, 30-40 pts. wt. methylene diphenyl diisocyanate (MDI), 30-40 pts. wt. corn starch, 1-3 pts. wt. filling agent, 1-5 pts. wt. lubricant, 2-4 pts. wt. foaming agent, 13-15 pts. wt. carbon dioxide, 10-13 pts. wt. citrate plasticizer, 10-13 pts. wt. amino acid, 5-9 pts. wt. polylactic acid, and 6-8 pts. wt. protein.
   USE - Used as high simulation fishing bait.
   ADVANTAGE - The high simulation fishing bait is environmentally friendly, and biodegradable.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparation of high simulation fishing bait comprising (i) mixing PPC, and 1,4-butanediol, dehydrating at 120 degrees C; then mixing acid and polylactic acid with MDI, placing the mixed raw material into the extruder hopper, in the extruder to heat, screw shearing and plasticizing, extruding at temperature of 140-220 degrees C, the material time in the extruder is 15 minutes; at the temperature of 150-200 degrees C and pressure at 20-35 MPa, adding protein and carbon dioxide, injecting it into the mold, cooling to 60-80 degrees C, opening the mold, cooling to obtain the thermoplastic polyurethane elastomer, (ii) through formulation design. selecting the PLA, corn starch modified by blending with the TPU elastomer, adding non-toxic citrate plasticizer and loading agent, lubricant and foaming agent, melting and mixing to prepare modified TPU with excellent mechanical property and degradation property of the elastic composite blend; (iii) pouring the mixed TPU elastic composite material into the mold cavity, the upper mold and the lower mold, heating the lower mold temperature of 150-200 degrees C, and (iv) reducing the temperature to 60-80 degrees C, cooling and shaping.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The high simulation fishing bait comprises 30 pts. wt. polypropylene carbonate (PPC), 15 pts. wt. 1,4-butanediol, 40 pts. wt. methylene diphenyl diisocyanate (MDI), 60 pts. wt. Polylactic Acid (PLA), 40 pts. wt. corn starch, 3 pts. wt. filling agent,5 pts. wt. lubricant, 4 pts. wt. foaming agent, 15 pts. wt. carbon dioxide, 13 pts. wt. citrate plasticizer, 13 pts. wt. amino acid, 9 pts. wt. polylactic acid, and 8 pts. wt. protein. Preferred Components: The injection port is set on the upper mold. The front mold, and mold injecting polymeric material into the mold cavity by injection. The mold is closed, then 2-5 pts. wt. the foam conditioner injected into the mold cavity through the injection port before the mold is pressed. The mold is provided with mark, and the mark on the mold to cast.

TI Cartridge for electronic vaping device (EVD), has            additive assembly whose flavor material releases            flavorant into generated vapor based on portion of heat            generated by adsorbent material.
AU KARLES G
   OGBONLOWO T M
   CRAWFORD D
   LI S
   OGBONLOVO T M
   CRAVFORD D
   LEE S
AE ALTRIA CLIENT SERVICES LLC (ALTR-C)
   PHILIP MORRIS PROD SA (PHIM-C)
   PHILIP MORRIS PROD SA (PHIM-C)
   PHILIP MORRIS PROD (PHIM-C)
GA 201803080D
AB    NOVELTY - The cartridge (70) has a vaporizer assembly                (22) which forms a generated vapor. An additive                assembly (24) comprising an adsorbent material                including adsorbed carbon dioxide, and flavor                material including the flavorant is fluidly                communicated with the vaporizer assembly. The                adsorbent material releases the carbon dioxide into                the generated vapor, and generates heat based on                the portion of the generated vapor adsorbing on the                adsorbent material. The flavor material releases                the flavorant into the generated vapor based on                portion of heat generated by adsorbent                material.
   USE - Cartridge for electronic vaping device (EVD)                (claimed) used by adult vaper.
   ADVANTAGE - The chemical reaction between the additive                eluted into the generated vapor and the elements of                the generated vapor is partially mitigated, thus                the more consistent and improved sensory experience                is realized through the flavored vapor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The adsorbent material releases the carbon                dioxide into the generated vapor based on the                portion of the generated vapor adsorbing on the                adsorbent material. The adsorbent material                comprises adsorbent beads. The flavor material                includes multiple beads each including the                flavorant. The flavor material includes one                botanical substance comprising the flavorant. The                adsorbent material includes zeolite, silica,                activated carbon, and molecular sieves. A vaporizer                assembly module is removably coupled to one                additive module, while having the vaporizer                assembly. The additive module includes the additive                assembly. The additive modules are removably                coupled together, and respectively included with                one of the adsorbent material and the flavor                material. The additive assembly includes first and                second additive structures. The first and second                additive structures include one of the adsorbent                material and the flavor material. The first and                second additive structures partially define a                boundary of one flow pathway between the first and                second additive structures. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is                included for an EVD which comprises a power supply                section configured to selectively supply power to                the vaporizer assembly.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional view of                the cartridge for EVD.
   Vaporizer assembly (22)
   Additive assembly (24)
   Space (40)
   E-vaping device (60)
   Cartridge (70)

AB    NOVELTY - Catalyzing methane-carbon dioxide reforming by                coal modification and preparing carbon nanotubes,                comprises (1) crushing and screening of coal A, (2)                taking coal particles A for modification treatment                to obtain modified coal B, (3) adding the carbon                nano-growth catalyst, obtaining mixture C, placing                into carbon nanotube preparation reactor, heating                to 900- 950degrees Celsius for 6-8 hours, and                obtaining the coal-based carbon nanotubes D, (4)                placing coal-based carbon nanotubes D into the                methane carbon dioxide reforming reactor,                performing reforming reaction, (5) stabilizing the                methane carbon dioxide reforming reactor slowly at                880-950degrees Celsius for 0.5-1.5 hours, and                passing carbon dioxide gas, (6) impregnating                coal-based carbon nanotubes E with hydrogen supply                solvent, removing residual coke, obtaining                relatively pure coal-based carbon nanotubes F, and                (7) performing 1-3 cycles to obtain purer                coal-based carbon nanotubes G.

   ADVANTAGE - The method generates carbon nano-tube while                reforming methane carbon dioxide, using carbon                dioxide to remove carbon, so as to maintain the                activity of the catalyst.

Carbon dioxide contacts the cathode electrode and                the catalyst converts electrochemically the carbon                dioxide into 1-2C liquid organic by-products and                the anions at the catholyte. Corrosion-resistant plastic support is made of                  polyethylene or polypropylene.

   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 202097640P
AB    NOVELTY - Installation comprises a carbon dioxide crystallization system (5) for continuously crystallizing carbon dioxide contained in the feed gas stream (6) for the production of a gas stream enriched in methane (7) and a liquefaction exchanger (8) for liquifying the gas stream enriched in methane leaving the crystallization system.
   USE - Installation for the production of liquid methane from a gas feed stream comprising methane and carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The liquid methane, preferably liquid biomethane, is easy to store and transport. The biogas after purification and liquefaction can be transported at a lower cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing liquid methane from a gas feed stream, which involves crystallizing carbon dioxide contained in the feed gas flow by injection of the latter into the carbon dioxide crystallization system, recovering gaseous methane in the upper portion of the crystallization system, liquefying the recovered methane using the liquefaction exchanger and recovering liquid methane at the outlet of the liquefaction exchanger.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of an installation for the production of liquid methane from a gas feed stream comprising methane and carbon dioxide.
   Exchanger (2)
   Continuously operating carbon dioxide crystallization system (5)
   Crystallization system (5)
   Gas feed stream (6)
   Gas stream enriched in methane (7)
   Liquefaction exchanger (8)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The gas feed stream comprises water. The feed gas flow is biogas. Preferred Conditions: The concentration of water included in the supply gas stream is lowered by lowering the temperature of the supply gas stream to a temperature between - 57 degrees C to - 75 degrees C. The gas stream depleted in water is then recovered. During crystallization, the ratio of the flow rate of the feed gas stream and the flow rate of the countercurrent liquid methane flow is such that the particles formed of solid carbon dioxide are entrained towards the lower bottom of the crystallization system. The mixture of liquid methane and solid carbon dioxide particles are recovered at the lower bottom of the crystallization system. The liquid and solid phases of mixture are separated to recover the solid carbon dioxide particles. The stream having a carbon dioxide content of 2-1000 ppm, preferably 5-200 ppm, is recovered at the outlet of the phase separation.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

   Catalytic conversion (31)
   Methane conversion (32)
   Carbon dioxide trap (33)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst comprises                  a heterogeneous alkaline nitrogen oxide catalyst,                  preferably heterogeneous nitrogen oxide/sodium                  hydroxide catalyst. The gaseous sample comprises                  isotopic carbon dioxide (14CO2) and isotopic                  methane (14CH4). The gaseous sample is a gaseous                  emission sample, preferably gaseous atmospheric                  sample obtained from a nuclear power plant. The                  gaseous sample is originated from biofuels, such                  as biodiesel or biogas. Preferred Process: The                  oxidation of the dinitrogen oxide is carried out                  while avoiding oxidation of any methane present                  in the sample. The detection method involves                  subjecting the gaseous sample to a cryogenic trap                  which has been cooled to a temperature below 195K                  before determination, where the isotopic carbon                  dioxide (14CO2) present in the sample solidifies                  and becomes trapped, and releasing the trapped                  carbon dioxide (14CO2) by heating the cryogenic                  trap to a temperature above 195K. The determining                  step involves measuring an infrared absorption                  spectrum of a sample released from the cryogenic                  trap by using a cavity down-ring laser                  spectroscopy. The detection method involves                  catalytically oxidizing isotopic methane (14CH4)                  to isotopic carbon dioxide (14CO2) by a catalyst                  (C2) before determination. The catalytic                  oxidation involves heating the palladium catalyst                  to a temperature of at least 300 degrees C, and                  bringing the gaseous sample into contact with the                  heated palladium catalyst. The heated palladium                  catalyst catalyses oxidation of isotopic methane                  (14CH4) present in the gaseous sample to isotopic                  carbon dioxide (14CO2). Preferred Composition:                  The concentration of methane in the sample is                  less than 1 %. Preferred Apparatus: The unit (A)                  comprises a catalyst bed (B1) comprising a                  nitrogen oxide catalyst. The apparatus comprises                  unit (A) for selectively catalytically oxidizing                  dinitrogen oxide present in a gaseous sample and                  further unit (C) for catalytically oxidizing                  isotopic methane (14CH4) present in the gaseous                  sample, in any order, and downstream of units (A)                  and (C), and unit (B) for determining the                  combined amount of carbon dioxide (14CO2) present                  in the gaseous sample and carbon dioxide (14CO2)                  converted from the isotopic methane (14CH4)                  present in the gaseous sample by infrared                  absorption spectroscopy. The unit (C) comprises a                  catalyst bed (B2) comprising a catalyst (C2),                  preferably palladium catalyst. The unit (B)                  comprises a cavity down-ring laser spectrometer                  comprising a quantum cascade laser as an infrared                  light source. Preferred Conditions: The catalyst                  is heated to a temperature of at least 300                  degrees C, preferably 350-500 degrees C.

TI Method for operating power plant for generating            electrical energy for delivery to consume by burning            carbon-containing fuel involves separating carbon            dioxide, and separated Carbon dioxide partially            converted into fuel.
AU MOSER P
   WIECHERS G
   SCHMIDT S
   STAHL K
AE RWE POWER AG (RHWL-C)
   RWE POWER AG (RHWL-C)
   RWE POWER AG (RHWL-C)
GA 2020231216
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves separating carbon                dioxidefrom the flue gas of a power plant (1). The                separated Carbon dioxide is at least partially                converted into a fuel. At least temporarily the                fuel is burned in at least one heat engine (4) with                the formation of an exhaust gas. The heat engine                generates electrical energy, which is delivered to                at least one consumer. The thermal energy of the                exhaust gas is used for heating combustion air of a                power plant, for heating a process medium of the                power plant, in a drying of the fuel of the power                plant or in a carbon dioxide separation. The                exhaust gas is fed to the flue gas of the power                plant.
   USE - Method for operating a power plant for                generating electrical energy for delivery to at                least one consumer by burning a carbon-containing                fuel.
   ADVANTAGE - The overall efficiency of a fossil-fired power                plant is improved with subsequent carbon dioxide                separation. The amount of carbon dioxide released                into the atmosphere is reduced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system                for operating a power plant.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation                of the power plant.
   Power plant (1)
   Carbon dioxide separation plant (2)
   Drying plant (3)
   Heat engine (4)
   Turbine (5)

PN CN114982826-A
TI Fresh-keeping process for dried walnut kernel            comprises bagging, vacuumizing and inflating and mixed            gas charged in inflating process comprises oxygen,            carbon dioxide and nitrogen.
AU LIU J
   CUI L
   CAO Y
   QIN S
AE SHIJIAZHUANG WANJING DRIED FRUIT CO LTD (SHIJ-Non-standard)
GA 2022B89465
AB    NOVELTY - Fresh-keeping process for dried walnut kernel                comprises bagging, vacuumizing and inflating, where                the mixed gas charged in the inflating process is a                mixed gas of 2-3 vol.% oxygen, 20-50 vol.% carbon                dioxide and nitrogen (remaining amount).
   USE - The method is useful for fresh-keeping process                for dried walnut kernel.
   ADVANTAGE - The dried walnut kernel has excellent                shelf-life, taste and nutritional value; reduces                loss of nutrient substance kernel and improves the                commodity value of the walnut kernels.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                walnut kernel, which is obtained by the dried                walnut kernel fresh-keeping process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The amount of oxygen                  is 2-3 vol.%, carbon dioxide is 25-35 vol.% and                  nitrogen (remaining amount) based on the mixed                  gas. The preservative comprises 3-5 pts. wt.                  chitosan, 5-10 pts. wt. tea polyphenol, 15-25                  pts. wt. pyroligneous acid and 250-300 pts. wt.                  water.Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises a pre-cooling step, which is set before                  bagging, and pre-cooling for 24-36 hours in an                  environment with an ambient humidity of 80-90%                  and a temperature of 0-2&#8451;. The method                  further comprises a preservative treatment step,                  which is arranged before the pre-cooling step.                  The preservative treatment step comprises soaking                  the walnut kernels in the preservative for 1-2                  minutes, removing and drying. The preparation of                  the preservative comprises dissolving the                  chitosan in the pyroligneous acid, mixing evenly,                  adding with water for dilution, adding tea                  polyphenol and evenly mixing the mixture to                  obtain preservative. The method further comprises                  a drying step, which is set before bagging and                  the dried walnut kernels are dried to a moisture                  content of 3-4% before bagging.

PN WO2021230332-A1
TI Producing cellulose nanofiber capsule used in            medicinal products, by forming pickering emulsion by            irradiating mixture comprising cellulose nanofibers,            water and carbon dioxide fluid, with ultrasonic waves            in closed container and promoting encapsulation of            cellulose nanofibers from emulsion.
AU MISHIMA K
   TOKUNAGA S
   ONO K
AE UNIV FUKUOKA (UFKK-C)
GA 2021D0708F
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing cellulose nanofiber (CNF)                capsule involves forming a Pickering emulsion by                irradiating a mixture, which comprises cellulose                nanofibers, water and carbon dioxide fluid, with                ultrasonic waves within a closed container, and                promoting encapsulation of cellulose nanofibers                from the Pickering emulsion by opening the closed                container.
   USE - The cellulose nanofiber capsule is used in                medicinal products, food and cosmetic                products.
   ADVANTAGE - The cellulose nanofiber capsule is suitable                for a living body while avoiding the use of an                organic solvent and is highly safe.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cellulose                  nanofibers comprises cellulose nanofibers                  physically defibrated from a plant material. The                  mixture further comprises core material. The core                  material comprises hydrophilic substance,                  hydrophobic substance or water-insoluble                  substance, where the mixture does not contain an                  organic solvent. Preferred Method: The ultrasonic                  wave irradiation is performed by directly                  irradiating the mixture with an ultrasonic probe                  arranged in the closed container.
   EXAMPLE - Ultrapure water and coniferous cellulose                nanofibers (CNF) suspended water having purity of                1.03% were charged into a occlutable container                having a volume of 150 ml in the CNF capsule                manufacturing apparatus, 89 g carbon dioxide was                sent from the carbon dioxide cylinder into the                container that can be closed while the temperature                of the constant temperature water tank was set to a                predetermined temperature, and the container was                closed, and the inside of the container was brought                into a high pressure state (6.8 MPa), ultrasonic                waves were irradiated from the ultrasonic probe at                a frequency of 20 kHz and an amplitude of 30% for                125 seconds, and after the CNF pickering emulsion                was formed in the container, a depressurization                operation was performed to open the container, and                CNF microparticles, which were CNF capsules, were                recovered from the container together with the                solution.

PT P
PN KR2018129307-A; KR1973002-B1
TI Preparing copper electrocatalyst for carbon dioxide reduction, involves dissolving copper precursor in organic solvent and subjecting to heat treatment to prepare copper precursor solution, and dispersing carbon support in organic solvent.
AU HWANG Y J
   KOUN M B
   OH H S
   HYEJIN J
AE KOREA INST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (KSCT-C)
GA 2018978741
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing copper electrocatalyst involves dissolving a copper precursor in an organic solvent and then subjecting it to a first heat treatment to prepare a copper precursor solution, dispersing the carbon support in a mixture of a fixing agent and an organic solvent by ultrasonic to prepare a carbon support solution and mixing the copper precursor solution and the carbon support solution to perform a second heat treatment to obtain copper electrocatalyst.
   USE - Method for preparing copper electrocatalyst for carbon dioxide reduction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare copper electrocatalyst which has excellent electrochemical performance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic solvent is selected from the group consisting of ethylene glycol, pentanediol, 1-octadecyne and benzyl ether. The carbon support may be selected from the group consisting of carbon black, acetylene black, carbon nanotubes, graphite, graphene and graphite nanofibers. The fixing agent is cysteamine. Preferred Conditions: The first heat treatment is performed by raising the temperature from room temperature to T1, and T1 is any temperature between 80-140 degrees C. The weight ratio of the fixing agent and the carbon support is 1:10-30. The second heat treatment is performed for 1-10 hours after the temperature is raised from T1 to T2, and T2 is 150-250 degrees C. The first and second heat treatments are performed at a heating rate of 1-5 degrees C/minute. The weight ratio of the fixing agent and the carbon support is from 1:18-22. The first heat treatment is performed by raising the temperature from room temperature to T1, and T1 is any temperature between 110-130 degrees C. The second heat treatment is performed for 5-9 hours after the temperature is raised from T1 to T2, and T2 is 180-220 degrees C.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN US2018002262-A1; WO2018004993-A1; US10189763-B2
TI Reducing greenhouse gas emission, comprises introducing at least one feed stream into a reformer to generate synthesis gas, and converting synthesis gas to dimethyl ether.
AU WRIGHT H A
   ROBERTSON M K
   JIANG W
AE RES USA LLC (REUS-Non-standard)
   RES USA LLC (REUS-Non-standard)
GA 2018009903
AB    NOVELTY - Reducing greenhouse gas emission, comprises: introducing at least one feed stream into a reformer to generate synthesis gas; and converting synthesis gas to dimethyl ether.
   USE - The method is useful for reducing greenhouse gas emission.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: produces carbon dioxide emissions of less than 180 g carbon dioxide/l of dimethyl ether; produces equivalent greenhouse gas emissions of less than 0.3 kg greenhouse gas emissions/mile (claimed); has low natural gas consumption per liter of dimethyl ether produced; does not water consumption, and oxygen consumption; and has low greenhouse gas emissions per liter of dimethyl ether produced.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the method: reformed gas exits the top of the reformer and is separated from spent catalyst; and spent catalyst is routed to a regenerator in which the catalyst is regenerated, where a renewable fuel is used in the regenerator, and comprises landfill gas, bio-digester gas, pyrolysis oils and liquid fuels, spent glycerol, biomass derived syngas or bio-ethanol. The regenerator comprises an air pre-heater and the method utilizes full or partial displacement of natural gas or natural gas derived syngas with a bio-genic gaseous or liquid fuel in the air pre-heater. The method comprises: using full or partial displacement of natural gas or natural gas derived syngas with a bio-genic gaseous or liquid fuel in the regenerator, where the renewable fuel used in the regenerator comprises high molecular weight cellulose degradation products, and contains impurities including arsenic, sulfur, ammonia and/or chlorine; and using full or partial displacement of natural gas feedstock with a bio-genic gaseous feedstock in the reformer. In the method: the feed stream comprises natural gas and renewable feedstocks comprising landfill gas, bio-digester gas, bio-genic feedstocks; and the reformer uses no process water and requires no oxygen.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The reformer: is a fluidized bed dry reforming reactor; and comprises a hydrogen membrane or a hydrogen membrane coated with an erosion resistant layer, where the hydrogen membrane removes hydrogen contained in the synthesis gas and shifts reforming reactions toward completion.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
