PN WO2022173007-A1; JP2022123865-A; JP7169606-B2
TI Production of carbon monoxide or organic compound            e.g. hydrocarbon for environmental application,            involves reducing carbon dioxide in device having            anode, cathode and electrolyte, and selectively            reducing carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide by applying            potential between anode and cathode.
AU GOTO T

GA 2022A6581L
AB    NOVELTY - Production of carbon monoxide or organic                compound involves electrolytically reducing carbon                dioxide in an electrolytic reduction device having                an anode, a cathode, and an electrolyte containing                carbon dioxide to obtain carbon monoxide or an                organic compound, and selectively reducing carbon                dioxide to carbon monoxide or certain organic                compounds by applying potential between the anode                and cathode.
   USE - Production of carbon monoxide or organic                compound e.g. hydrocarbon used for environmental                application. Uses include but are not limited to                methane, ethane, ethylene, propane, propene,                etc.
   ADVANTAGE - The method economically and efficiently                reduces carbon dioxide with low energy, and is                simple method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the                electrolytic reduction device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                experimental apparatus.
   1Electrolytic cell
   2Gas supply pipe
   3Exhaust pipe
   4Lead wire
   5Rubber stopper
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The supporting                  electrolyte is potassium bicarbonate (preferred),                  potassium dihydrogen phosphate, lithium                  tetrafluoroborate, lithium hexafluorophosphate,                  lithium perchlorate, lithium hexafluoroarsenate,                  lithium triflate, lithium                  bis(trifluoromethanesulfonyl)imide, lithium                  bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl) imide                  (Li(CF3SO2)2N), potassium carbonate, lithium                  carbonate, sodium carbonate, or sodium                  bicarbonate. The basic catalyst is an alkali                  metal or alkaline earth metal hydroxide chosen                  from calcium hydroxide Ca(OH)2, lithium                  hydroxide, sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide                  or cesium hydroxide. The electrolytic device                  comprises a reference electrode, which is silver                  (Ag+)/silver electrode. The anode is platinum                  (preferred), metal oxide, glassy carbon or                  boron-doped diamond electrode. The cathode is                  copper, silver, iron or nickel. Preferred                  Property: The cathode has potential of -5 to 1.5                  V.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The electrolyte                  contains ionic liquid, 5 %mass or less water and                  additive, and is an imidazolium-based ionic                  liquid, aromatic-based ionic liquid,                  pyrrolidinium-based ionic liquid, ammonium-based                  ionic liquid, piperidinium-based ionic liquid, or                  quaternary phosphonium-based ionic liquid,                  preferably ionic liquid is                  N,N-diethyl-N-(2-methoxyethyl)ammonium                  tetrafluoroborate,                  N,N-diethyl-N-methyl-N-(2-methoxyethyl)ammonium                  bis(trifluoromethanesulfonyl)imide or                  triethylpentylphosphonium                  bis(trifluoromethanesulfonyl)imide. The additive                  contains supporting electrolyte a basic catalyst.                  The organic compound is 1-10C hydrocarbon or                  organic compound containing carbon, hydrogen and                  oxygen, which is an ether, cyclic ether, alcohol                  or carbonyl compound. Preferred Properties: The                  volume ratio of total amount of ionic liquid and                  water is 1:99-99:1. The temperature of                  electrolyte is 0-100&#176; C. The volume                  ratio of total amount of ionic liquid, water and                  supporting electrolyte is 75:52-25:75.

PN CN114181690-A
TI Carbon dioxide synergistic thick oil viscosity            reducer useful in reducing viscosity of thick oil,            which is composite system of aqueous solution of            quaternary ammonium base and carbon dioxide.
AU ZHANG J
   HUA C
   JING B
AE BEIJING RES CENT CHINA NAT OFFSHORE OIL (CNOO-C)
   CNOOC CHINA CO LTD (CNOO-C)
GA 202243180P
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide synergistic thick oil viscosity                reducer which is composite system of aqueous                solution of quaternary ammonium base and carbon                dioxide, is claimed.
   USE - The thick oil viscosity reducer is useful in                reducing the viscosity of thick oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The thick oil viscosity reducer: can carry                carbon dioxide to interact with the thick oil, has                effect of reducing viscosity; and at the same time,                after heating, is conducive to oil-water                demulsification and phase separation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparing the thick oil viscosity reducer,                comprising feeding the carbon dioxide into the                aqueous solution of the quaternary ammonium base;                and
   (2) reducing the viscosity of thick oil,                comprising mixing the thick oil viscosity reducer                with the thick oil to be treated, and reducing the                viscosity of the thick oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The pH value of the                  thick oil viscosity reducer is 6-8. In the                  aqueous solution of the quaternary ammonium base,                  the content of the quaternary ammonium base is                  0.5-3 wt.%. The quaternary ammonium base is                  dodecyl trimethyl ammonium hydroxide, tetradecyl                  trimethyl ammonium hydroxide,                  cetyltrimethylammonium hydroxide, or                  octadecyltrimethylammonium hydroxide. The                  viscosity of the thick oil is 50-1000 mpa.s. The                  volume ratio of the thick oil viscosity reducer                  and the thick oil is 1:0.1-4.

PN CN113594481-A
TI Carbon dioxide reduction electro-catalyst comprises carbon substrate which is hollow state adhered to each other, and bismuth/bismuth oxide spherical nano particles loaded on substrate.
AU LIU T
   CHEN H
   KUANG Z
   ZHOU X
AE SHANGHAI INST CERAMICS CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAGU-C)
GA 2021C6524K
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide reduction electrocatalyst comprises carbon substrate adhered to each other in hollow state, and bismuth (Bi)/bismuth oxide (Bi2O3) spherical nanoparticles loaded on carbon substrate.
   USE - Carbon dioxide reduction electrocatalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide reduction electrocatalyst has good electrocatalysis reducing CO2 to produce formic acid (root) activity, improves electronic transmission efficiency and performance of electrocatalyst, preparation method is easy to be controlled and raw material cost is low.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing electrocatalyst for carbon dioxide reduction, which involves:
   a. adding ethanol solution of carbon source to ethanol suspension of Bi source and mixing uniformly to obtain mixed suspension;
   b. hydrothermally reacting resulting mixed suspension at 180-200degreesC for 6-8 hours to obtain hydrothermal product;
   c. washing and drying obtained hydrothermal product, and calcining to obtain carbon dioxide reduction electrocatalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon source is tannic acid. The Bi source is bismuth nitrate pentahydrate Bi(NO3)35H2O.Preferred Conditions: The mass ratio of carbon source to Bi source is (1.6-1.8):1. The uniform mixing method is ultrasonic mixing, power of ultrasonic mixing is 500-600 watt, and time of ultrasonic mixing is 20-60 minutes. The first calcination is in nitrogen atmosphere at 600-700degreesC for 1-2 hours, cooled to 100degreesC and room temperature in open open, calcined in air atmosphere at 200-300degreesC for 0.5-2 hours.

PN CN112881325-A; CN112881325-B
TI Zero drift estimation based infrared carbon dioxide sensor concentration detection method, involves updating zero drift quantity delta, and initializing voltage value in corresponding to minimum value of concentration and temperature.
AU LV J
   MAO K
   QIU J
   CHI K
AE HANGZHOU MULTIIR TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HANG-Non-standard)
GA 202164396Y
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves substituting a concentration value of 400 parts per million (ppm) and intracavity temperature into a calibration model to obtain an output voltage value. A zero drift quantity delta is updated to U to be U0-Umin. A minimum value in a carbon dioxide concentration is initialized. A voltage value in corresponding to the minimum value of the concentration and the temperature in a cavity is initialized. The steps are continuously repeated until power is cut off.
   USE - Concentration detection method for an infrared carbon dioxide sensor based on zero drift estimation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method estimates the zero drift of the infrared carbon dioxide sensor so as to correct the specific value of the carbon dioxide concentration estimation value, and improves the accuracy of the carbon dioxide concentration estimation of the sensor.

TI METHOD OF EXTRACTING CARBON DIOXIDE FROM FLUE            GASES AND DEVICE FOR REALIZING SAID METHOD.
AU VOLKOV V A
   AFANASEV S V
   AFANASEV A S
   TURAPIN A N
   PROKHOROV P E
AE DELTA-PROM CO LTD (DELT-Non-standard)
GA 2020A49164
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to production of carbon                dioxide using fume gases produced as a raw material                during combustion of natural gas and liquid                hydrocarbons. Method includes stabilization of                temperature of flue gases, their purification from                nitrogen oxides, absorption and desorption of                carbon dioxide with a triple ethanolamine                composition, cleaning and regeneration of                absorbent, compression of carbon dioxide with its                cooling, drying and conversion into liquefied                state. Device for implementing the method comprises                a flow reactor for catalytic purification of flue                gases from nitrogen oxides, heat exchanger for                liquefied ammonia evaporation and maintenance of                optimum process temperature, gas blower, absorbers,                desorber, carbon dioxide compressor, gas drying                devices, condenser and refrigerating machine. It                includes two absorbers, a boiler-condenser and two                apparatus for cleaning absorbent from resin                compounds and corrosion products.
   USE - Chemistry.
   ADVANTAGE - Technical result is higher efficiency of                removing carbon dioxide from flue gases while                reducing the effect of waste on the environment and                increasing service life of the device.

TI Tundish protection casting of ultra-low carbon steel involves casting ultra-low carbon steel by blowing mixed gas of carbon dioxide and argon using blowing device of tundish cover for preset time.

AE SHOUGANG GROUP CO LTD (SHGG-C)
GA 202030453F
AB    NOVELTY - Tundish protection casting of ultra-low carbon steel involves casting ultra-low carbon steel by blowing mixed gas of carbon dioxide and argon using blowing device of the tundish cover and blowing carbon dioxide gas having flow rate of 1500-2500 Nl/minute when casting is carried out for 0-5 minutes where the flow rate of carbon dioxide changes to 200-1500 Nl/minute after casting. The carbon dioxide gas is used in an amount of 5-15 vol.%.
   USE - Tundish protection casting of ultra-low carbon steel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes carbon dioxide gas which consumes carbon in the steel and produces carbon monoxide gas having low concentration, which is burned when it escapes the tundish and does not cause safety problems.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Composition: The ultra-low carbon steel has carbon content of 0-0.01 %mass.

TI Method for making nanocatalyst nanoparticle for            use in carbon dioxide reduction reactions, involves            providing precursor nanoparticle having precursor            component, where precursor component comprises chemical            bond, and breaking chemical bond.
AU CHEN S
   CHEN G
AE HONDA MOTOR CO LTD (HOND-C)
   HONDA MOTOR CO LTD (HOND-C)
   HONDA MOTOR CO LTD (HOND-C)
GA 201986179H
AB    NOVELTY - A nanocatalyst nanoparticle making method                involves providing a precursor nanoparticle having                a precursor component, and breaking the chemical                bond to obtain the finished product, where the                precursor component comprises chemical bond, and                the nanocatalyst nanoparticle comprises main                component and secondary component doped on a                surface of the nanocatalyst nanoparticle.
   USE - Method for making nanocatalyst nanoparticle                for use in carbon dioxide reduction reactions i.e.                converting carbon dioxide into desired conversion                products (claimed), such as sustainable chemicals                and fuels which is a promising solution for                reducing dependence on fossil fuel sources.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                method for converting carbon dioxide into one or                more products, which involves providing one or more                nanocatalyst nanoparticles, and contacting carbon                dioxide with one or more nanocatalyst nanoparticles                to catalyze conversion of the carbon dioxide into                one or more products.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The chemical bond is                  carbon-nitrogen bond. The second portion is                  nitrogen. The alkylamine comprises                  tetradecylamine, hexadecylamine and                  octadecylamine. The main component comprises                  carbon dioxide conversion catalyst and copper.                  Preferred Process: The method further involves                  ostwald ripening, and providing an ionic solution                  comprising ions of main component and ions of                  precursor component, where the ions of the main                  component is present in the ionic solution at a                  concentration of between 0.03-0.12 mol/L.

PN CN110028576-A
TI Extracting leech using supercritical carbon            dioxide comprises e.g. preliminary preparation of leech            and extractant, ratio of water hydrazine to extractant            and equipment requirements and extraction control of            leech.
AU MA F
   MING M
AE HAIAN HUADA PETROLEUM INSTR CO LTD (HAIA-Non-standard)
GA 201965129X
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting leech using supercritical carbon                dioxide comprises (i) crushing leech with water                content below 5% to 10-100 meshes, extracting agent                using 70%-80% ethanol, extraction device including                carbon dioxide storage tank, ethanol storage tank,                extraction kettle, heating device and separation                kettle, (ii) selecting mashed leeches and ethanol                in mass ratio of 1:4-1:6 in ethanol storage tank                and extraction kettle, (iii) heating extraction                vessel to 55-65 degrees C by heating device, and                heating separation vessel to 45-55 degrees C,                opening carbon dioxide storage tank, and                pressurizing extraction vessel, when the pressure                of the extraction vessel reaches 25 to 32 MPa,                adjusting the carbon dioxide flow rate to 12-24                l/hour, maintaining the temperature and pressure,                and extracting for 1-1.5 hours.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting leech                using supercritical carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the steps (ii) and                  (iii), the mass ratio of the mashed leeches to                  ethanol is 1:6, respectively, placing in ethanol                  storage tank and an extraction kettle, heating                  extraction vessel to 60 degrees C by heating                  device, heating the separation vessel to 50?Oc,                  opening the carbon dioxide storage tank,                  pressurizing the extraction kettle, when the                  pressure of the extraction kettle reaches 30 MPa,                  adjusting the carbon dioxide flow rate to 18                  l/hour, maintaining temperature and pressure,                  performing two extractions and the first                  extraction time was 60 minutes. and the second                  extraction time is 30 minutes. The yield of                  ethanol extract obtained by the method is 15.2%,                  and percentage of nitrogen content of ethanol                  extract yield is 12.1%.

PT P
PN CN114874269-A
TI Nickel-based transition metal complex useful as            catalyst in reduction of carbon dioxide into carbon            monoxide, prepared as mixing solution A containing            bis(2-pyridyl) ketoxime and phenylphosphonic acid and            solution B containing nickel metal salt, and            reacting.
AU LIU P
   YANG S
   SHI N
   HUANG X
AE UNIV FUZHOU (UFZU-C)
GA 2022A4864L
AB    NOVELTY - Nickel-based transition metal complex, is                claimed. The chemical formula of nickel-based                transition metal complex is as given in the                specification.
   USE - The complex is useful as catalyst in reduction                of carbon dioxide into carbon monoxide                (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing the complex, comprising mixing the                solution A containing bis(2-pyridyl) ketoxime and                phenylphosphonic acid and solution B containing                nickel metal salt and reacting, and growing the                single crystal by the gas-liquid diffusion method                in ether vapor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The reaction needs to be                  carried out under stirring conditions, and the                  reaction time is not less than 2 hours. The                  reaction system is alkaline, with a pH of 7.5-11.                  The reaction temperature is room temperature. The                  diffusion time is not less than 48 hours. The                  molar ratio of bis(2-pyridyl)ketoxime to                  phenylphosphonic acid and nickel metal salt is                  1:1:1. The molar concentrations of bis(2-pyridyl)                  ketoxime and phenylphosphonic acid in solution A                  were both 0.02 mol/l, and the molar concentration                  of nickel metal salt in solution B is 0.01 mol/l.                  The electrocatalytic CO reduction to CO requires                  grinding the nickel-based transition metal                  complex crystals, dissolving in                  N,N-dimethylformamide, and electrolyzing the                  homogeneous electrolyte at a constant potential                  in a closed three-electrode system. The voltage                  used for potentiostatic electrolysis is -0.9 V to                  -1.7 V.

PN CN107697942-A; CN107697942-B
TI Preparation of cerium carbonate by precipitating rare earth ion includes taking carbonated ammonia water as cerium ion precipitator, conducting parallel flow precipitation with cerium chloride solution and controlling pH.
AU LU G
   ZHAO Z
   LU Q
AE BAOTOU KERI RARE EARTH MATERIALS CO LTD (BAOT-Non-standard)
   BAOTOU KERI RARE EARTH MATERIALS CO LTD (BAOT-Non-standard)
   BAOTOU RARE EARTHS RES INST (BTSU-C)
   CHINA NORTHERN RARE EARTH GROUP HIGH-TEC (BTSU-C)
GA 201815457V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of cerium carbonate by precipitating rare earth ion comprises taking carbonated ammonia water as cerium ion precipitator, conducting parallel flow precipitation with cerium chloride solution, controlling pH of precipitated mother liquor, centrifuge filtering cerium ions, washing, and drying to obtain cerium carbonate.
   USE - Preparation of cerium carbonate by precipitating rare earth ion.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses carbonated ammonia water to precipitate cerium ions, achieving carbon dioxide utilization rate of 62.5-96.8%, which is much higher than using ammonium bicarbonate as precipitant, which has carbon dioxide utilization rate of only 50%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises simultaneously adding 0.4-1.6 mol/L cerium chloride solution having pH of 1.5-2.5, and carbonated ammonia water solution into a reaction tank, stirring to carry out precipitation reaction, where mol ratio of carbon dioxide and ammonia in carbonated ammonia water solution is 0.47-0.91, preferably 0.47-0.53, the total alkalinity of carbonated ammonia water is 3-13 mol/L and pH is 8.6-10.0, preferably 9.7-10.0, controlling reaction tank solution pH at 6.6-7.4, stopping feeding, continuously stirring to fully react for 30 minutes at 30-55 degrees C, centrifuge filtering, washing, and drying to obtain cerium carbonate. The flow rate of carbonated ammonia water solution is 0.26-2.90 L/minute. The flow rate of cerium chloride solution is 0.80-4.70 L/minute. The temperature of carbonated ammonia water solution is 20-30 degrees C. The temperature of cerium chloride solution is 30-55 degrees C. The addition time of carbonated ammonia water solution is 3.0-10.4 hours. The addition time of cerium chloride solution is 3.0-10.3 hours.

AE UNIV NORTHWEST (UYXB-C)

   USE - Preparation method of dimethyl carbonate using rare earth oxide as catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of dimethyl carbonate with low energy consumption and high yield, by simple, economical and environmentally-friendly process.

Preferred Composition: The loading amount of yttrium oxide is 15 wt.%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The methyl iodide-potassium methoxide cocatalyst is added in a mass ratio of 1:3-3:1.    ZHANG X
   LUAN J

AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC BEIJING RES INST CHEM IND (SNPC-C)
GA 2020366466
AB    NOVELTY - Treating produced water in a carbon dioxide flooding oil field comprises (1) filtering produced water in the oil field to remove oil and solid suspended matter to obtain filtered water, and (2) degassing filtered effluent to remove the gas, and obtaining degassed effluent.
   USE - The method is useful for treating produced water in a carbon dioxide flooding oil field.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: requires less area; solves the problem of carbon dioxide flooding produced water has high carbon dioxide content; and reduces cost.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: The filtration pressure of the filtration treatment is 0.08-0.15 MPa. In the step (1), adopting use of ceramic membrane filter module to filter the produced water in the oil field, where the pore diameter of the filter element of the ceramic membrane filter module is 0.02-0.1 mu m. In the step (2), degassing filtered water using membrane module. The operating temperature of the membrane module is 5-40 degrees C, and the degree of vacuum is -0.06 to -0.1 MPa. The method further comprises (3) sending degassed water to the water injection station for reinjection. Preferred Components: The membrane module includes a degassing membrane, the degassing membrane is a hollow fiber membrane with a resin material and a hydrophobic surface. The outer diameter of the degassing membrane is 0.4-2 mm, and the inner diameter of the membrane is 0.3-1.5 mm. The content of suspended matter in the produced water of the oil field is 5-150 mg/l, the oil content is 2-500 mg/l, the dissolved oxygen content is 0.01-1 mg/l, the aggressive carbon dioxide content is 1.8-50 mg/l, and the average corrosion rate is 0.05-0.64 mm/year. The content of suspended matter in the degassed effluent is less than 1 mg/l, the oil content is less than 0.1 mg/l, dissolved oxygen content less than 0.1 mg/l, and aggressive carbon dioxide content is less than 1 mg/, average corrosion rate is less than 0.076 mm/year.

PN CN114280271-A; US2022146489-A1
TI Test device for testing degradation rate of biodegradable polymer materials in soil, comprises micro-compression air pump, first carbon dioxide absorption bottle, carbon dioxide indicator bottle, hollow leaching device, and carbon dioxide absorption bottles.
AU ZHAO G
   XIANG Y
   CHEN T
   LIU Y
AE SHANXI ZHONGBEI NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY (SHAN-Non-standard)
   UNIV NORTH CHINA (UYZB-C)
   SHANXI ZHONGBEI NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY (SHAN-Non-standard)
   UNIV NORTH CHINA (UYZB-C)
GA 202251974W
AB    NOVELTY - Test device comprises micro-compression air pump (1), a first carbon dioxide absorption bottle (2), a carbon dioxide indicator bottle (3), a hollow leaching device (5), a second carbon dioxide absorption bottle (8) and a third carbon dioxide absorption bottle. The top portion of the hollow-leaching device is provided with an end cap. The upper portion of the end cover is connected with an external water distributor and a mechanical stirring device (6) are arranged above the external water distributor. External water distributor is communicated with the inner cavity of the hollow leaching device through a pipeline, and the mechanical stirring device. The stirring shaft of the mechanical mixing device extends into the inner cavity of the hollow leaching device and the stirring shaft located in the hollow leaching device is provided with a stirring paddle.
   USE - Test device for testing degradation rate of biodegradable polymer materials in soil (claimed), and sampling and testing soil, leaching liquid and gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The test device can make up the defect that the soil culture system only can exchange gas with the outside in the biodegradable performance test standard of biodedegradable polymer material and the test method, can simulate biodegradation process of biocompatible macromolecule material in the natural soil environment, so as to accurately evaluate the bioremediation performance of the polymer material. The test method can conveniently test the soil, leaching liquid and gas sampling test, can solve the problem that the device building is complex in the existing technology, reduces the test cost, simplifies the test process, and improves the test accuracy of the degradation rate of the polymeric material soil.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Test device comprises micro-compression air pump, a first carbon dioxide absorption bottle, a carbon dioxide indicator bottle, a hollow leaching device, a second carbon dioxide absorption bottle and a third carbon dioxide absorption bottle. The top portion of the hollow-leaching device is provided with an end cap. The upper portion of the end cover is connected with an external water distributor and a mechanical stirring device are arranged above the external water distributor. External water distributor is communicated with the inner cavity of the hollow leaching device through a pipeline, and the mechanical stirring device. The stirring shaft of the mechanical mixing device extends into the inner cavity of the hollow leaching device and the stirring shaft located in the hollow leaching device is provided with a stirring paddle. First carbon dioxide absorption bottle is equipped with a drying alkali lime or strong alkali aqueous solution, carbon dioxide indicator bottle is filled with barium hydroxide solution, external water distributor is filled with eluent with set pH value. Upper portion of the inner cavity of the hollow leaching device is provided with a certain height of soil as the leaching area, the bottom is in the shape of an inverted cone, and the inner portion is filled with quartz sand particles as the filtering area, and the bottom of the filtering area is provided with a water outlet, and the lower portion of the water outlet is provided with a filtrate collecting container (7), the second carbon dioxide absorption bottle and the third carbon dioxide absorbing bottle are filled with strong alkali water solution.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for testing degradation rate of biodegradable polymer materials in soil, which involves:
   (a) according to the actual test purpose and experimental conditions, selecting the corresponding soil and eluent, mixing the biodegradable polymer material sample to be tested and the soil of a certain height uniformly and placing it in the hollow eluent in the dissolving device;
   (b) turning on the micro compressed air pump, the liquid inlet control valve (4-1), the mechanical stirring device and the leachate control valve, and setting the inlet flow rate, inlet flow, agitator rotation speed and outflow flow, making biodegradable polymer materials biodegrade in soil under set conditions;
   (c) periodically collecting the filtrate in the filtrate collection container and the strong base solution in the second carbon dioxide absorption bottle and the third carbon dioxide absorption bottle, and utilizing an organic carbon analyzer or acid-base titration to measure carbon content in the filtrate and the strong alkali solution, if the barium hydroxide solution in the carbon dioxide indicator bottle in the intake area is found to become turbid during the degradation process, the first carbon dioxide absorption bottle and the carbon dioxide indicator bottle need to be replaced in time in the strong alkali solution;
   (d) repeating the previous step until the biodegradable polymer material is completely degraded or the degradation rate required for the test purpose is achieved; and
   (e) determining the biodegradation performance and degradation rate of the biodegradable polymer material in the soil according to the measured data.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a test device. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

   Mechanical stirring device (6)
   Filtrate collecting container (7)

PN CN114506866-A
TI Continuously preparing pseudo-boehmite, comprises            e.g. adding first alkaline solution and mixed gas I            containing carbon dioxide continuously to react, adding            second alkaline solution continuously, aging slurry,            filtering, and washing filter cake and drying to obtain            silicon-containing pseudoboehmite.
AU JIN J
   HAN K
   YUAN S
   LIU T
AE SINOPEC DALIAN PETROLEUM & PETROCHEM RES (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 202277922U
AB    NOVELTY - Continuously preparing pseudo-boehmite,                comprises (1) adding the first alkaline solution                and the mixed gas I containing carbon dioxide                continuously into the first reactor to react, and                the pH value of the slurry in the first reactor is                controlled by adjusting the flow rate of the mixed                gas I, (2) adding the second alkaline solution                continuously from the bottom of the second reaction                kettle, and feeding the mixed gas II containing                carbon dioxide continuously into the bottom of the                second reaction kettle for reaction, and                controlling the slurry in the second reaction                kettle by adjusting the flow rate of the mixed gas                II, (3) when the slurries in the first and second                reaction kettles reach or are higher than their                overflow ports, the two slurries flow into the                third reaction kettle for reaction, and the pH                value of the slurry in the third reaction kettle is                controlled, (4) aging the slurry, filtering, and                washing the filter cake and drying to obtain                silicon-containing pseudoboehmite.
   USE - The method for continuously preparing                pseudo-boehmite in preparation of residual oil                hydrogenation catalysts application                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The silicon-containing pseudo-boehmite                prepared has stable properties, high silicon oxide                content, Larger pore size and pore volume can meet                the requirements of hydrogenation catalyst                supports, the method of the present invention                adopts the sodium metaaluminate raw material with                higher alumina concentration, realizes continuous                production, improves the production efficiency, and                the method is simple in process and easy to                operate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Continuously preparing pseudo-boehmite,                comprises (1) adding the first alkaline solution                and the mixed gas I containing carbon dioxide                continuously into the first reactor to react, and                the pH value of the slurry in the first reactor is                controlled by adjusting the flow rate of the mixed                gas I, (2) adding the second alkaline solution                continuously from the bottom of the second reaction                kettle, and feeding the mixed gas II containing                carbon dioxide continuously into the bottom of the                second reaction kettle for reaction, and                controlling the slurry in the second reaction                kettle by adjusting the flow rate of the mixed gas                II, (3) when the slurries in the first and second                reaction kettles reach or are higher than their                overflow ports, the two slurries flow into the                third reaction kettle for reaction, and the pH                value of the slurry in the third reaction kettle is                controlled, (4) when the slurry in the third                reaction kettle reaches or is higher than its                overflow port, the slurry enters the aging tank for                aging, and after the aging is completed, filtering,                and washing the obtained filter cake and drying to                obtain silicon-containing pseudoboehmite, where the                second alkaline solution is a mixed solution of an                aluminum-containing alkaline solution and a                silicon-containing alkaline solution.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                silicon-containing pseudo-boehmite, obtained by the                method as mentioned above, and the                silicon-containing pseudo-boehmite is calcined to                obtain silicon-containing alumina, and the                properties of the resulting silicon-containing                alumina are as follows: based on the weight of                silicon-containing alumina, the content of silica                is 5-85%, the pore volume is &#8805; 1 ml/g,                the specific surface area is 280-360 m2/g; the                amount of infrared acid is &#8805; 0.40 mmol/g,                the ratio of B acid content to L acid content is                &#8805; 0.05.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                device for continuous preparation                silicon-containing pseudo-boehmite.
   1First reactor
   2Second reactor
   3Third reactor
   4Aging tank
   5Comparative example

   7Gas-liquid mixing pump
   8First alkaline solution inlet
   9Mixed gas ii inlet containing carbon                dioxide
   10Stirrer

In step (2), the                  aluminum-containing alkaline solution is sodium                  metaaluminate solution or potassium metaaluminate                  solution, preferably sodium metaaluminate                  solution, and the silicon-containing alkaline                  solution is sodium silicate. In                  step (2), in the second alkaline solution, the                  concentration of sodium metaaluminate and/or                  potassium metaaluminate, calculated as alumina,                  is 1-30 g alumina/l, preferably 5-25g alumina/l;                  In the second alkaline solution, the                  concentration of sodium silicate calculated as                  silica is 5-95 g silica/l, preferably 10-90 g                  silica/l. In step (2), the pH value                  of the slurry in the second reaction kettle is                  controlled to be 2-4.Preferred Method: In step (1), the pH value                  of the slurry in the first reaction kettle after                  the reaction is 9.5-11.5. In step (3), the                  temperature of the reaction is 40-70&#8451;,                  preferably 45-65&#8451;. In step (3), the pH                  value of the slurry in the reaction kettle is                  controlled to be 8.5-9.5 by adjusting the flow                  rate of the slurry in step (1). In step (4), the                  aging conditions are as follows: the temperature                  is 50-95&#8451;, and the time is 30-120                  minutes. The pore distribution of the                  silicon-containing alumina is as follows: the                  pore volume of the pores with a pore diameter of                  less than 6 nm accounts for less than 5% of the                  total pore volume, and the pore volume of the                  pores with a pore diameter of 6-15 nm accounts                  for 70-85% of the total pore volume. The pore                  volume of pores with a pore diameter is more than                  15 nm accounts for 10-25% of the total pore                  volume.

TI Preparation of syngas and olefins for preparing methanol, involves feeding catalytic partial oxidation reactant mixture to reaction zone, feeding cracking zone feed to cracking zone and cooling catalytic partial oxidation reaction zone.
AU CHINTA S
   AL-GHAMDI M
   PANT A
   NARAYANASWAMY R
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   CHINTA S (CHIN-Individual)
   AL-GHAMDI M (ALGH-Individual)
   PANT A (PANT-Individual)
   NARAYANASWAMY R (NARA-Individual)
   ENI SPA (ENIE-C)
   ENI SPA (ENIE-C)
   ENI SPA (ENIE-C)
   ENI SPA (ENIE-C)
GA 2020854850
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of syngas and olefins involves feeding (i) catalytic partial oxidation (CPO) reactant mixture (10) comprising oxygen, hydrocarbons (a), and steam to CPO reaction zone, feeding (ii) cracking zone feed (60) to cracking zone and cooling (iii) catalytic partial oxidation reaction zone by heat transfer between CPO reaction zone and cracking zone (150). At least a portion of the CPO reactant mixture reacts through an exothermic CPO reaction, in the CPO reaction zone to produce syngas. The CPO reaction zone comprises a CPO catalyst. The syngas comprises hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, water, and unreacted first hydrocarbons having molar ratio (M). The molar ratio (M) is ratio of (hydrogen-carbon dioxide) to (sum of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide). The cracking zone feed comprises hydrocarbons (b). The hydrocarbons (a) and the hydrocarbons (b) are the same or different. The cracking zone product stream (63) comprises olefins, hydrogen, and unreacted hydrocarbons (b).
   USE - Preparation of syngas and olefins used for preparing methanol and ethylene (claimed) for lubricant or fuel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces partial oxidation reaction of converting hydrocarbon to carbon monoxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of syngas and olefins involves feeding (i) catalytic partial oxidation (CPO) reactant mixture comprising oxygen, hydrocarbons (a), and steam to CPO reaction zone, feeding (ii) cracking zone feed to cracking zone and cooling (iii) catalytic partial oxidation reaction zone by heat transfer between CPO reaction zone and cracking zone. At least a portion of the CPO reactant mixture reacts through an exothermic CPO reaction, in the CPO reaction zone to produce syngas. The CPO reaction zone comprises a CPO catalyst. The syngas comprises hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, water, and unreacted first hydrocarbons having molar ratio (M). The molar ratio (M) is ratio of (hydrogen-carbon dioxide) to (sum of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide). The cracking zone feed comprises hydrocarbons (b). At least a portion of the hydrocarbons (b) undergoes an endothermic cracking reaction in the cracking zone to produce a cracking zone product stream. The hydrocarbons (a) and the hydrocarbons (b) are the same or different. The cracking zone product stream comprises olefins, hydrogen, and unreacted hydrocarbons (b). INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) production of methanol and ethylene, which involves feeding a CPO reactant mixture to a CPO reaction zone which reacts under near- isothermal conditions at temperature variation of less than 100 degrees C, feeding a cracking zone feed comprising ethane to a cracking zone which undergoes endothermic cracking reaction in the cracking zone to produce a cracking zone product stream containing ethylene, hydrogen, and unreacted ethane, cooling the CPO reaction zone, separating at least a portion of the cracking zone product stream in a separation unit into an ethylene stream, a hydrogen stream, and an unreacted ethane stream, contacting at least a portion of the hydrogen stream with at least a portion of the syngas to produce a hydrogen-enriched syngas, recycling at least a portion of the unreacted ethane stream to the cracking zone and introducing at least a portion of the syngas and/or at least a portion of the hydrogen-enriched syngas to a methanol reactor to produce methanol, in which ratio of hydrogen-enriched syngas is greater than or equal to 1.7; and
   (2) system for producing methanol, which comprises CPO reaction zone to react at least a portion of CPO reactant mixture through an exothermic CPO reaction to produce syngas, cracking zone operable to convert at least a portion of a cracking zone feed, and methanol reactor configured for producing methanol from at least a portion of the syngas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a system for a methanol production process.
   Catalytic partial oxidation reactant mixture (10)
   Cracking feed zone (60)
   Cracking zone product stream (63)
   Cracking zone (150)
   Distillation unit (400)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - MECHANICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The syngas and olefins is prepared using common housing comprises both the CPO reaction zone and the cracking zone. The common housing further comprises a furnace zone and cracking reactor. The fuel stream is combusted in the furnace zone to provide for heat for the endothermic cracking reaction. The cracking reactor comprises cracking reactor outer surface and cracking reactor inner surface is cracking zone and one or more tubes placed within the common housing and CPO catalyst bed.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of carbon to oxygen in the CPO reactant mixture is 0.5:1-3:1, which is total moles of carbon of hydrocarbons in the reactant mixture divided by the total moles of oxygen in the reactant mixture. The molar ratio of steam to carbon in the CPO reactant mixture is 0.01:1-2.4:1, which is total moles of water in the reactant mixture divided by the total moles of carbon of hydrocarbons in the reactant mixture. Preferred Components: The CPO catalyst bed comprises CPO catalyst. The hydrocarbons (b) comprise ethane, propane, butane, naphtha, methane, or their combinations, preferably ethylene. The hydrocarbons (a) and/or hydrocarbons (b) comprise methane, ethane, propane, butanes, naphtha, natural gas, natural gas liquids, associated gas, well head gas, enriched gas, paraffins, shale gas, shale liquids, fluid catalytic cracking, off gas, refinery process gases, stack gases, fuel gas from fuel gas header, or their combinations.
DC A41 (Monomers and Condensants These are also included in Section E.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).)

PT P
PN CN110896805-A
TI Planting selenium-rich strawberry comprises preparing special nutrient substrate for strawberry planting, turning on boiler and water pump, planting strawberry seedlings in planting pots, watering to adjust moisture content and illuminating.
AU DU G
   CHENG L
   XU X
   QIU Y
   WUMING X
AE JIANGSU SHENGHEYUAN RESOURCE ENVIRONMENT (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202024291F
AB    NOVELTY - Planting selenium-rich strawberry comprises (i) preparing special nutrient substrate for strawberry planting, and loading into strawberry planting pot, where the loading amount is 70-80% of the volume of the planting pot, (ii) turning on the boiler and water pump in the temperature control structure of the planting room, passing hot water into hot water pipe, rising temperature of planting room, turning off the water pipe after temperature reaches to 20-25 degrees C, when the temperature is not at the set value, opening return water valve to perform hot water circulation, and adjusting the indoor temperature, (iii) planting strawberry seedlings in strawberry planting pots, watering to adjust the moisture content of the nutrient substrate to 70-80%, (iv) when the strawberry is growing, using an incandescent lamp to illuminate the strawberry for 13-15 hours, placing in dark environment for 9-11 hours, controlling the indoor temperature at 20-25 degrees C under incandescent lamp irradiation period.
   USE - The method is useful for planting selenium-rich strawberry.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: realizes the artificial control of temperature and humidity; maintains growth environment of strawberries in the best state; shortens the growth cycle of strawberries; improves the quality of strawberries, and economic benefits.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Planting selenium-rich strawberry comprises (i) preparing special nutrient substrate for strawberry planting, and loading into strawberry planting pot, where the loading amount is 70-80% of the volume of the planting pot, (ii) turning on the boiler and water pump in the temperature control structure of the planting room, passing hot water into hot water pipe, rising temperature of planting room, turning off the water pipe after temperature reaches to 20-25 degrees C, when the temperature is not at the set value, opening return water valve to perform hot water circulation, and adjusting the indoor temperature, (iii) planting strawberry seedlings in strawberry planting pots, watering to adjust the moisture content of the nutrient substrate to 70-80%, (iv) when the strawberry is growing, using an incandescent lamp, illuminating the strawberry for 13-15 hours, placing in dark environment for 9-11 hours, controlling the indoor temperature at 20-25 degrees C under the incandescent lamp irradiation period and 13-15 degrees C at the dark environment period, where moisture content of nutritional substrate is 60-70%, and indoor carbon dioxide concentration is 0.3-0.4% when illuminated, and carrying out pest management. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for special planting room for strawberry planting, comprising a planting room main body provided with a temperature control structure, a ventilation structure and multiple strawberry planting structures. The temperature control structure comprises a boiler, a hot water pipe and a hot water return pipe. The hot water pipe and hot water return pipe are connected to boiler. The hot water pipe is provided with a water pump. The hot water return pipe is provided with a return valve. The ventilation structure comprises a first carbon dioxide pipe and a second carbon dioxide pipe. Both ends of second carbon dioxide pipe are connected with the first carbon dioxide pipe. The first carbon dioxide pipe is provided with a first air valve, a hot air fan and a second air valve. A first air valve is provided at the inlet end of hot air fan, and a second air valve is provided at the outlet end of the hot air fan. The strawberry planting structure comprises a planting support and an incandescent lamp. A screw rod is provided in the center of the planting support. The bottom end of the screw rod is provided with a drive motor, and the top is provided with a shower support and a shower. Multiple planting basins are arranged on the outer periphery of the planting support, and strawberry planting pots are arranged on the planting basins.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the strawberry planting room structure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Components: The nutrient substrate comprises 45-50 pts. wt. soil, 20-30 pts. wt. urea, 10-15 pts. wt. calcium superphosphate, 5-10 pts. wt. amino acid, 5-10 pts. wt. sodium alginic acid, and 5-10 pts. wt. humic acid. Preferred Method: The method further comprises soaking and sterilizing strawberry seedlings with 80% alcohol and 2-4% sodium hypochlorite before being planted, washing with sterile water and planting. The pest management comprises using 10-20 pts. wt. pepper water, 5-10 pts. wt. thiophanate-methyl, 2-3 pts. wt. chlorantraniliprole, 2-3 pts. wt. chlorfenapyr, and 5-10 pts. wt. plant ash water, uniformly mixing, and carrying out foliar spraying.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: A third carbon dioxide pipe is provided between the air outlet of the hot air fan and the second gas valve. The third carbon dioxide pipe is provided with a third gas valve and an air pump. The open end of the third carbon dioxide pipe extends into the room. The planting support is provided with 2-3 incandescent lamps. The light intensity of each incandescent lamp is 100-300 W. The shower support is made of metal tube. Multiple planting basins are arranged crosswise on the planting support.
DC A88 (Mechanical engineering and tools e.g. valves, gears and conveyor belts.); C03 (Other organic compounds, inorganic compounds and multicomponent mixtures. Polymers and proteins.); C02 (Heterocyclic.); P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).)

PN CN113931605-A; CN113931605-B
TI Capturing and sealing carbon dioxide after            underground gasification of deep coal of coal, involves            excavating gasification injection channel and            gasification output channel from ground to coal layer            of coal deep unit vertically.
AU GUO C
   JIA H
   GONG X
   YANG D
   LI W
   LIU L
   XUE S
   SUN Q
AE UNIV XIAN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYXS-C)
GA 2022171785
AB    NOVELTY - Capturing and sealing carbon dioxide (CO2)                after underground gasification of deep coal of coal                involves excavating the gasification injection                channel and the gasification output channel from                the ground to the coal layer of the coal deep unit                along the vertical direction, and then the                gasification horizontal channel connected with the                gasification injection channel and the gasification                output channel is excavated in the coal bed, the                top unit of the coal layer is covered with an upper                stratum (3) and the bottom is a coal bed subsurface                stratum (5), the pressurizing injection device (7)                is used to inject the gasification agent into the                coal bed, through the gasification injection                channel and ignition is performed, heated, the coal                in the coal layer and gasification agent is made to                carry out gasification reaction, the gasification                product generated by gasification reaction enters                the gasification product separation processing                device for grading treatment.
   USE - Method for capturing and sealing carbon                dioxide after underground gasification of deep coal                of coal, where the coal is used for coal green                development and utilization and carbon dioxide                emission reduction.
   ADVANTAGE - The method combines the coal deep underground                gasification and CO2capture with the sealing                process, at the same time of gasification of the                coal, using the gasified coal layer to carry out                the capture and sealing of the CO2, so that CO2is                solid and liquid, gas three-phase filling and                sealing in the cavity after the gasification                process, thus improving capture and capture speed,                solves the filling treatment problem of the deep                underground coal gasification, and realizes                effective retention treatment of the carbon                dioxide, thus greatly reducing the discharge of                CO2into the atmosphere environment, and solving the                pollution problem of directly discharging carbon                dioxide into the environment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Capturing and sealing carbon dioxide (CO2)                after underground gasification of deep coal of coal                involves excavating the gasification injection                channel and the gasification output channel from                the ground to the coal layer of the coal deep unit                along the vertical direction, and then the                gasification horizontal channel connected with the                gasification injection channel and the gasification                output channel is excavated in the coal bed, the                top unit of the coal layer is covered with an upper                stratum (3) and the bottom is a coal bed subsurface                stratum (5), the pressurizing injection device (7)                is used to inject the gasification agent into the                coal bed, through the gasification injection                channel and ignition is performed, heated, the coal                in the coal layer and gasification agent is made to                carry out gasification reaction, the gasification                product generated by gasification reaction enters                the gasification product separation processing                device for grading treatment along the output of                the gasification output channel, when the coal in                the coal layer is gasified forming a cavity after                the coal layer gasification in the coal layer, and                remaining solid gasification ash, the pressurizing                injection device is used to inject the CO2into the                cavity after the gasification of the coal layer                through the CO2injection passage, at the same time                injecting the waste slurry into the cavity after                the gasification of the coal layer through the                waste slurry injection passage, making the waste                slurry and CO2flow into the cavity, water (H2O)                reaction to generate precipitate realizing capture                of the CO2the waste slurry is formed by mixing                high-salinity waste water and fly ash, when the                CO2is captured using the output channel backfill                rock (9) to block the waste slurry injection                channel, at the same time continuously injecting                CO2to the saturated state in the cavity after the                gasification of the coal bed by the CO2injection                channel, then using the injection channel backfill                rock (8) to block the CO2injection channel to                realize the sealing of the CO2.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - 3Upper stratum
   5Coal bed subsurface stratum
   7Pressurizing and injecting
   8Injection channel backfill rock
   9Output channel backfill rock

PN CN108319820-A; CN108319820-B
TI Method for determining utilization fraction of plant bicarbonate in field habitat, involves selecting second unfolded leaves of plant and measuring photosynthetic parameters, followed by calculating plant bicarbonate utilization fraction.
AU WU Y
   RAO S
   FANG L
   SU Y
   LI H
   WANG R
   WANG S
   LIU C
AE INST GEOCHEMISTRY CHINESE ACAD SCI (GEOC-Non-standard)
GA 201859973R
AB    NOVELTY - A plant bicarbonate utilization fraction determining method involves selecting leaves of the second unfolded leaves on the new branch of the plant, measuring the photosynthetic parameters, obtaining data of extracellular atmospheric carbon dioxide concentration and intercellular carbon dioxide concentration, calculating a ratio of the inter-cell carbon dioxide concentration of the plant leaf, selecting the leaves of the first unfolded leaves on the new branch of the plant, determining the value of the stable carbon isotope composition, calculating a stable carbon isotope fractionation value of the assimilated carbon dioxide of the plant leaves grown in the environment, followed by measuring average value of the stable carbon isotope composition, investigating the roots of the plants, determining the stable carbon isotope fractionation value of the assimilated carbon dioxide and bicarbonate of the plant leaves and calculating plant bicarbonate utilization fraction.
   USE - Method for determining utilization fraction of plant bicarbonate in field habitat.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables determining utilization fraction of plant bicarbonate in convenient, rapid and precise manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A plant bicarbonate utilization fraction determining method involves selecting leaves of the second unfolded leaves on the new branch of the plant grown in the environment under investigation, measuring the photosynthetic parameters of 9:00-10:00 in the morning under a clear weather using a portable photosynthetic apparatus, obtaining data of extracellular atmospheric carbon dioxide concentration and intercellular carbon dioxide concentration from the portable photosynthetic apparatus, calculating a ratio of the inter-cell carbon dioxide concentration of the plant leaf to the extracellular atmospheric carbon dioxide concentration, selecting the leaves of the first unfolded leaves on the new branch of the plant grown in the environment under investigation, determining the value of the stable carbon isotope composition of the plant leaves by a conventional method, obtaining the average value of the stable carbon isotope composition of the atmospheric carbon dioxide in the investigated environment according to a conventional method, determining the value of the stable carbon isotope composition of the rhizosphere soil bicarbonate of the plant grown in the environment under investigation according to a conventional method, calculating a stable carbon isotope fractionation value of the assimilated carbon dioxide of the plant leaves grown in the environment under consideration according to the ratio of the intercellular carbon dioxide concentration of the plant leaf to the atmospheric carbon dioxide concentration, calculating a stable carbon isotope fractionation value of the assimilated bicarbonate of the plant leaf grown in the environment under investigation according to the stable carbon isotope fractionation value of the assimilated carbon dioxide of the plant leaf grown in the environment under investigation, followed by measuring average value of the stable carbon isotope composition of the ambient atmosphere based on the value of the stable carbon isotope composition of the plant leaves grown in the environment under investigation, investigating the roots of the plants grown in the environment, determining the stable carbon isotope fractionation value of the assimilated carbon dioxide of the plant leaves grown under the investigated environment and the stable carbon isotope fractionation value of the assimilated bicarbonate of the plant leaves grown under the investigated environment and calculating plant bicarbonate utilization fraction.

PN WO2018081216-A1; CN109890751-A; DE112017005411-T5; US2020055731-A1
TI Production of ethylene and methanol for e.g. paints, involves reacting stream of gas and hydrocarbons, and combustion product to form ethylene and carbon dioxide, contacting produced carbon dioxide-free stream with solvent, and separating.
AU VARANASI N S R
   GAUTAM P S
   NAIR B
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   VARANASI N S R (VARA-Individual)
   GAUTAM P S (GAUT-Individual)
   NAIR B (NAIR-Individual)
   SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
GA 2018341387
AB    NOVELTY - Production of ethylene and methanol involves producing a combustion product (6), reacting a mixture of a hydrocarbon stream of natural gas and higher hydrocarbons (13) and the combustion product to form a reaction product (14) of hydrocarbons, ethylene and carbon dioxide, producing a carbon dioxide stream and a carbon dioxide-free stream (22), contacting the carbon dioxide-free product stream with an aprotic polar solvent to form an acetylene solution (32), forming a hydrogenation product, separating the resultant into an ethylene stream (61) and a gas stream (63), and reacting the resultants.
   USE - Production of ethylene and methanol. The ethylene is used for producing break-resistant containers and packaging materials. The methanol is used for producing paints, solvents and plastics, and used in energy cells and transportation fuel cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently produces ethylene and methanol, when compared with the existing method. The method that does not integrate hydrocarbon pyrolysis with other process for producing desired products.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of ethylene and methanol involves introducing a fuel gas stream (11) and an oxidant gas to a combustion zone (5) to produce a combustion product, introducing a mixture o a hydrocarbon stream comprising natural gas and/or higher hydrocarbons, and at least a portion of the combustion product which heats the hydrocarbon stream to a temperature required for a pyrolysis reaction, to reaction zone (z1), allowing at least a portion of the reactant mixture to react via the pyrolysis reaction and produce a pyrolysis reaction product comprising unconverted hydrocarbons, acetylene, ethylene, carbon monoxide, hydrogen, water, and carbon dioxide, introducing at least a portion of the pyrolysis reaction product to a carbon dioxide removal unit (20) to produce a carbon dioxide stream comprising carbon dioxide and water. The carbon dioxide-free product stream comprises unconverted hydrocarbons, acetylene, ethylene, carbon monoxide, and hydrogen, and a carbon dioxide-free product stream, contacting a portion (22a) of the carbon dioxide-free product stream with an aprotic polar solvent, in an acetylene absorption unit (30) to produce an acetylene solution and a gas stream (31), contacting at least a portion of the acetylene solution with a portion (22b) of the carbon dioxide-free product stream in a liquid phase hydrogenation reactor to produce a hydrogenation product comprising aprotic polar solvent, unconverted hydrocarbons, ethylene, carbon monoxide and hydrogen, separating at least a portion of the hydrogenation product into an aprotic polar solvent stream (33) and a gas stream (52), separating at least a portion (73a) of the gas stream (52) into an ethylene stream and a gas stream (63), and introducing at least a portion (31a) of the gas stream (31) and/or at least a portion of the gas stream (63) to a reaction zone (z2) to produce methanol and a fuel gas stream (73). The aprotic polar solvent absorbs at least a portion of the acetylene of the portion of the carbon dioxide-free product stream to produce the acetylene solution. The acetylene solution comprises at least a portion of the acetylene of the portion of the carbon dioxide-free product stream. The gas stream (31) comprises unconverted hydrocarbons, ethylene, carbon monoxide and hydrogen. The carbon dioxide-free product stream hydrogenates at least a portion of the acetylene of the acetylene solution to produce ethylene. The aprotic polar solvent stream comprises at least a portion of the aprotic polar solvent of the hydrogenation product. The gas stream (52) comprises unconverted hydrocarbons, ethylene, carbon monoxide and hydrogen. The gas stream (63) comprises unconverted hydrocarbons, carbon monoxide and hydrogen. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for production of ethylene and hydrogen, which involves introducing the another portion (31b) of the gas stream (31) and the gas stream (52) to a pressure swing adsorption (PSA) unit to produce a hydrogen stream and a PSA fuel gas product stream.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic of an ethylene and methanol production system.
   Combustion zone (5)
   Combustion product (6)
   Fuel gas stream (11,73)
   Hydrocarbon stream (13)
   Pyrolysis reaction product (14)
   Carbon dioxide removal unit (20)
   Carbon dioxide-free product stream (22)
   Carbon dioxide-free product stream (22a,22b)
   Acetylene absorption unit (30)
   Gas stream (31,52,63)
   Portions of gas stream s1 (31a,31b)
   Acetylene solution (32)
   Aprotic polar solvent stream (33)
   Ethylene stream (61)
   Portion of gas stream s2 (73a)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The reaction zone (z1) has a residence time of 0.1-100 milliseconds.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method further involves compressing at least a portion of the pyrolysis reaction product to a pressure of 150-300 psig before introducing the pyrolysis reaction product to the carbon dioxide removal unit, compressing at least a portion of the portion (p2) of the carbon dioxide-free product stream to a pressure of 200-350 psig before contacting the acetylene solution with the portion (p2) of the carbon dioxide-free product stream, and compressing at least a portion of the gas stream (31) and/or at least a portion of the gas stream (52) to a pressure of 1000-1300 psig to produce a compressed gas stream, and introducing at least a portion of the compressed gas stream to the reaction zone (z2). At least a portion of the aprotic polar solvent stream is recycled to the acetylene absorption unit. A portion of the gas stream (31), a portion of the gas stream (52) and/or at least a portion of the fuel gas stream (52) is recycled to the combustion zone as the fuel gas stream (11) and/or used as a fuel stream other than the fuel gas stream (11). Preferred Components: The aprotic polar solvent stream comprises N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone, dimethylformamide and/or acetone. The hydrocarbon stream comprises methane, natural gas, natural gas liquid, associated gas, well head gas, enriched gas, higher hydrocarbon, paraffin, olefin, alcohol, oxygenate and/or 1-6C compound. The reaction zone (z2) comprises a catalyst comprising copper, copper/zinc oxide, copper/thorium dioxide, copper/zinc/aluminum oxide, copper/zinc oxide/aluminum oxide and/or copper/zirconium. Preferred Conditions: The reactant mixture has a temperature of 700 degrees C or more. Preferred Composition: The ratio of carbon monoxide and hydrogen in the gas stream (11) is 1.5:1-3.0:1. The ratio of carbon monoxide and hydrogen in the gas stream (52) is 0.5:1-1.5:1. The ratio of difference of hydrogen and carbon dioxide to the total content of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide is 2.0-2.2, in the reaction zone (z2). At least a portion of the compressed gas stream is subjected to a water-gas shift reaction to produce a shifted gas stream. The ratio of difference of hydrogen and carbon dioxide to the total content of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide is 2.0-2.2, in the shifted gas stream. At least a portion of the shifted gas stream is introduced to the reaction zone (z2). The gas stream (31) comprises 40-60 mol% hydrogen. The gas stream (52) comprises 25-40 mol% hydrogen.

TI Regulating gas balance in stem cell culture container comprises e.g. culturing stem cells and placing in stem cell culture container, disposing oxygen sensor and carbon dioxide sensor in the stem cell culture vessel, and culturing.
AU HUA Z
   LIU B
   ZHU T
   ZHANG J
   WANG J
   GAO H
AE LIU B (LIUB-Individual)
GA 201969510S
AB    NOVELTY - Regulating gas balance in stem cell culture container comprises e.g. (i) culturing stem cells and placing in a stem cell culture container, disposing oxygen sensor and carbon dioxide sensor in the stem cell culture vessel, disposing gas exchange membrane at the inlet of the culture solution of the stem cell culture vessel, then culturing the cultured stem cells, controlling the circulation of nutrient solution, real-time monitoring of oxygen and carbon dioxide content in stem cell culture containers, (ii) adjusting the oxygen partial pressure of the gas side of the gas exchange membrane and the partial pressure of carbon dioxide by the air pump to change the content of oxygen and carbon dioxide in the culture liquid, and(iii) correlating gas content change in the stem cell culture vessel with the gas partial pressure corresponding to the gas side of the gas exchange membrane, and when the oxygen consumption of the stem cells is increasing and enhancing the carbon dioxide formation.
   USE - The method is useful for regulating gas balance in stem cell culture container.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: ensures the gas balance in the stem cell roots; regulates the gas balance of the stem cell culture vessel; promotes stem cell growth.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Regulating gas balance in stem cell culture container comprises (i) culturing stem cells and placing in a stem cell culture container, disposing oxygen sensor and carbon dioxide sensor in the stem cell culture vessel, disposing gas exchange membrane at the inlet of the culture solution of the stem cell culture vessel, then culturing the cultured stem cells, controlling the circulation of nutrient solution, real-time monitoring of oxygen and carbon dioxide content in stem cell culture containers, (ii) adjusting the oxygen partial pressure of the gas side of the gas exchange membrane and the partial pressure of carbon dioxide by the air pump to change the content of oxygen and carbon dioxide in the culture liquid, (iii) correlating gas content change in the stem cell culture vessel with the gas partial pressure corresponding to the gas side of the gas exchange membrane, and when the oxygen consumption of the stem cells is increasing and enhancing the carbon dioxide formation, increasing the oxygen content in the input culture solution and reducing the carbon dioxide content and ensuring gas balance in the stem cell growth environment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the gas exchange membrane is gas, which selectively permeates oxygen and carbon dioxide.

PT P
PN RU2699628-C1
TI METHOD OF CLEANING HYDRAULIC SYSTEM PIPELINES FROM OIL AND SERVICE CONTAMINANTS WITH SUPERCRITICAL CARBON DIOXIDE.
AU SOSNINA YU N
   POSPELOVA A V
   KULIKOV K N
AE ONEGA RES & TECHNOLOGICAL BUR STOCK CO (ONEG-Non-standard)
GA 201983187Q
AB    NOVELTY - Disclosed method relates to methods of cleaning using liquid, can be used in various industries for cleaning pipelines from oil and operational contaminants. In the method, carbon dioxide is used as a working medium. Working medium from the supply tank is supplied to the pump, then it is directed along the pressure line to the flushed pipeline; the above flushed pipeline is flushed with the help of the working medium, and then the working medium is returned to the delivery tank through the filter. In the feed tank, carbon dioxide is in a liquid state. Pump creates and maintains working medium pressure higher than critical for carbon dioxide. After the pump, the working medium is supplied to the evaporator of the recuperative temperature maintenance system, which includes an evaporator, a condenser and a coolant compressor circulating in said system, which heats to temperature higher than critical for carbon dioxide, thereby converting carbon dioxide into supercritical fluid state. After cleaning, working medium passes through condenser of recuperative system to maintain temperature mode, cooling it to temperature below critical for carbon dioxide. Filter, through which working medium passes before returning to supply tank, is coalescent. Residual carbon dioxide from flushed pipeline is removed into atmosphere after washing.
   USE - Technological processes.
   ADVANTAGE - Efficient cleaning of pipelines against oil and operational contaminants without formation of oil-containing water solutions requiring further processing and recycling.

AB    NOVELTY - Epichlorohydrin, phthalic anhydride, propylene                oxide and carbon dioxide quaternary block copolymer                (I) is new.
   USE - Used as epichlorohydrin, phthalic anhydride,                propylene oxide and carbon dioxide quaternary block                copolymer.
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation method: adopts commercial                Lewis acid-base pair as catalyst; successfully                introduces epichlorohydrin into the                copolymerization of propylene oxide, phthalic                anhydride and carbon dioxide; produces                polycarbonate-polyester structure containing                epichlorohydrin; greatly improves the thermal                stability of polypropylene carbonate; realizes the                synthesis of pure diblock copolymers; integrates                the excellent properties of the two blocks; and                expands the use range of polypropylene carbonate                materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Epichlorohydrin, phthalic anhydride, propylene                oxide and carbon dioxide quaternary block copolymer                of formula (I) is new.
   x, y, m, a, b, n = greater than or equal to                1.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparation of (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of                  (I), comprises (i) adding propylene oxide,                  catalyst and solvent into a autoclave, adding                  carbon dioxide, and placing it in an oil bath to                  carry out ring-opening polymerization, and (ii)                  adding epichlorohydrin and phthalic anhydride                  into the above reaction solution, placing it in                  an oil bath and carry out second step reaction,                  dissolving the product in dichloromethane after                  the reaction, adding small amount of acid to                  terminate the reaction, and finally precipitating                  the product in ethanol.Preferred Method: The ring-opening                  polymerization reaction is performed at                  50-70degrees Celsius for 12-36 hours; and the                  carbon dioxide is passed into the reaction kettle                  and the internal pressure is 0.5-3 MPa.Preferred Components: The catalyst is Lewis                  acid-alkali pair. The Lewis acid is organic boron                  compound triethylboron, tripropylboron or                  tributylboron, preferably triethylboron. The                  Lewis alkali is organic amines or organic amine                  salts, preferably                  bis(triphenylphosphoranyl)ammonium chloride,                  tetrabutylammonium chloride, tetrabutylammonium                  bromide, tetrabutylphosphonium chloride,                  tetrabutylphosphonium bromide,                  tetraphenylphosphine bromide,                  1,5,7-Triazabicyclo(4.4.0)dec-5-ene or                  4-dimethylaminopyridine. The molar ratio of                  propylene oxide to phthalic anhydride is 1:0.6-1,                  preferably 1:1-1.2. The molar ratio of catalyst                  to propylene oxide is 1:500-1000.

The pores of the silica gel are loaded with active material carboxy groups. The acid solution includes hydrochloric acid, acetic acid or sulfuric acid. When the acid solution is hydrochloric acid or acetic acid, then its concentration is 5.8-6.2 mol/l, preferably 6 mol/l, when the acid solution is sulfuric acid, its concentration is 0.25-2 mol/l, preferably 0.25 mol/l, and when the acid solution is sulfuric acid, then washing the solid product obtained after the completion of the reaction in step (i) using distilled water at least 3 times. The reaction condition includes reacting at room temperature for 8-12 hours; or the washing method comprises washing with distilled water at least 3 times. The conditions of the preliminary drying are: the drying is carried out at 100-130 degrees C for 9-11 hours; or the drying is carried out at 140-160 degrees C under the vacuum condition for 4.5-6 hours.
