PN CN113321312-A
TI Acclimating microalgae tolerant to high ammonia nitrogen in high-concentration carbon dioxide atmosphere, involves culturing microalgae in water, inoculating microalgae liquid with biogas slurry, introducing carbon dioxide mixed gas, inoculating microalgae liquid with biogas liquid and culturing.
AU HUANG Y
   LIAO Q
   XIA A
   ZHU X
   SONG A
AE UNIV CHONGQING (UYCQ-C)
GA 2021A4237V
AB    NOVELTY - Method for acclimating microalgae tolerant to high ammonia nitrogen in a high-concentration carbon dioxide atmosphere, involves (a) collecting and culturing microalgae in water near the farm which needs to process the biogas slurry, (b) inoculating the microalgae liquid with the biogas slurry of the farm in a photobioreactor, placing in a suitable condition for culturing and continuously introducing carbon dioxide mixed gas, (c) inoculating the microalgae liquid with the biogas liquid of the farm in a photobioreactor, placing in a suitable condition for culturing and continuously introducing carbon dioxide mixed gas, and (d) subjecting the microalgae liquid obtained in step (c) to low-speed centrifugation, inoculating the microalgae seed and farm biogas liquid at the bottom of the centrifuge tube into a serum bottle, placing the serum bottle in a constant temperature greenhouse for culturing and feeding the carbon dioxide mixed gas to supply the carbon source during the culturing.
   USE - The method is useful for acclimating microalgae tolerant to high ammonia nitrogen in a high-concentration carbon dioxide atmosphere in aquaculture and environmental protection applications.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for acclimating microalgae tolerant to high ammonia nitrogen in a high-concentration carbon dioxide atmosphere, involves (a) collecting and culturing microalgae in water near the farm which needs to process the biogas slurry, (b) inoculating the microalgae liquid obtained in step (a) with the biogas slurry of the farm in a photobioreactor, placing in a suitable condition for culturing and continuously introducing carbon dioxide mixed gas, (c) inoculating the microalgae liquid obtained in step (b) with the biogas liquid of the farm in a photobioreactor, placing in a suitable condition for culturing and continuously introducing carbon dioxide mixed gas, and (d) subjecting the microalgae liquid obtained in step (c) to low-speed centrifugation, inoculating the microalgae seed and farm biogas liquid at the bottom of the centrifuge tube into a serum bottle, placing the serum bottle in a constant temperature greenhouse for culturing and continuously and quantitatively feeding the carbon dioxide mixed gas to supply the carbon source during the culturing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The biogas slurry and carbon dioxide mixed gas of the farm in steps (b)-(d) are prepared by (i) carrying out anaerobic fermentation of wastewater from the farm to obtain biogas and liquid from the farm and (ii) combusting biogas to obtain electric energy and carbon dioxide mixed gas.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)

PN WO2019236249-A1; IN202017051485-A; BR112020024792-A2; CN112566706-A; EP3801803-A1; US2021163434-A1; IN383224-B; EP3801803-A4
TI Purifying ethylene oxide used in integrated EO/EG plants, involves quenching and washing ethylene oxide reactor effluent with aqueous alkali, and passing gaseous ethylene oxide containing stream to ethylene oxide absorber.
AU OZERO B
AE OZERO B (OZER-Individual)
   OZERO B (OZER-Individual)
   OZERO B (OZER-Individual)
   OZERO B (OZER-Individual)
GA 2019A4771R
AB    NOVELTY - Purifying ethylene oxide (EO) involves quenching and washing ethylene oxide reactor effluent by contacting with aqueous alkali, passing gaseous ethylene oxide containing stream obtained from quench/wash to an ethylene oxide absorber where ethylene oxide is absorbed in water to form a dilute aqueous ethylene oxide and carbon dioxide-containing solution, stripping dilute solution in ethylene oxide stripper to produce a gaseous ethylene oxide and carbon dioxide-containing overhead vapor which is then passed to a reabsorber where ethylene oxide and carbon dioxide are absorbed to form a concentrated aqueous solution, passing reabsorbate to carbon dioxide and lights stripper to release gaseous carbon dioxide and the ethylene oxide-containing solution being recovered for use or further treatment, and passing an impurities-containing liquid stream obtained from quench/wash to a second stripper such as a purge stripper or quench bleed stripper.
   USE - Method for purifying ethylene oxide used in integrated ethylene oxide (EO)/ethylene glycol (EG) plants.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to recover ethylene oxide as a high-purity absorbate stream with minimized concentration of formaldehyde impurity in it in simple and cost effective manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Purifying ethylene oxide (EO) involves quenching and washing ethylene oxide reactor effluent by contacting with aqueous alkali, passing gaseous ethylene oxide containing stream obtained from quench/wash to an ethylene oxide absorber where ethylene oxide is absorbed in water to form a dilute aqueous ethylene oxide and carbon dioxide-containing solution, stripping dilute solution in ethylene oxide stripper to produce a gaseous ethylene oxide and carbon dioxide-containing overhead vapor which is then passed to a reabsorber where ethylene oxide and carbon dioxide are absorbed to form a concentrated aqueous solution, passing reabsorbate to carbon dioxide and lights stripper to release gaseous carbon dioxide and the ethylene oxide-containing solution being recovered for use or further treatment, passing an impurities-containing liquid stream obtained from quench/wash to a second stripper such as a purge stripper or quench bleed stripper, introducing steam and carbon dioxide into second stripper, feeding the gaseous overhead from quench bleed stripper to reabsorber and removing the impurity-containing bottoms from quench bleed stripper.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide fed to the second stripper is recycled carbon dioxide obtained from a point downstream of the residual absorber. The pH in the second stripper after feeding carbon dioxide to it is below 8.0. The carbon dioxide (CO2) vapor is fed into the stripping steam to provide a minimum of 0.07 bar of (CO2) partial pressure Preferred Conditions: The dilute ethylene oxide solution from the absorber is passed directly to the reabsorber without ever passing through the stripper in concentrations of 10-90%, more preferably, 20-80%, most commonly 25-30%. The separate quench column (or the bottom section of an absorber) is provided to thoroughly scrub the EO reactor effluent gas with recirculated, cooled, dilute alkaline solution, normally of 1-30%, preferably 1-15% solution of alkaline hydroxide to neutralize the organic acids and absorb the maximum amount of the formaldehyde, and other heavy (in water) aldehydic impurities. The vaporous reaction stream contains ethylene oxide, CO2, formaldehyde, acetaldehyde, and organic acidic compounds comprising absorption of ethylene oxide and a portion of CO2, formaldehyde, acetaldehyde, and organic acidic compounds in water to form an absorption stream. The vaporous reaction stream from an alkaline quench wash section is fed to a water wash section in which it is washed with fresh process water and demisted before it is fed to the bottom of the EO absorber where it produces a high purity EO-containing absorbate of which 10-90 % can be sent directly to an EO reabsorber to absorb more EO and produce a more concentrated high purity EO-aqueous solution that can then be fed to the glycol reaction section for the production of fiber-grade mono ethylene glycol, or to high-purity ethylene oxide (HPEO) purification column for the production of high-purity EO.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E13 (Heterocyclics.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

TI Preparing methane and ethylene by electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide comprises taking H-type dual electrochemical cell reactor, sealing H-type double electrochemical cell reactor and introducing carbon dioxide gas.
AU FU J
   LI Z
   LV X
   OUYANG P
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 201879482A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing methane and ethylene by electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide comprises taking H-type dual electrochemical cell reactor, sealing the H-type double electrochemical cell reactor, introducing the cathode chamber with carbon dioxide gas before the reaction, taking a three-electrode system with gas diffusion electrode as working electrode, stirring magnetically the cathode chamber, locating the working electrode, applying working voltage, and performing the reaction at room temperature, where the gas diffusion electrode includes a gas diffusion electrode body, and an electrochemical reduction catalyst for carbon dioxide supported on the gas diffusion electrode body, the carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction catalyst is a graphite phase carbon nitride supporting nano copper oxide and the nano copper oxide has two crystal forms of black and red ore.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing methane and ethylene by electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the Faraday capacity of methane, ethylene and other hydrocarbon product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing methane and ethylene by electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide comprises taking H-type dual electrochemical cell reactor, where the H-type double electrochemical cell reactor is separated into a cathode chamber and an anode chamber by a proton exchange membrane, sealing the H-type double electrochemical cell reactor, introducing the cathode chamber with carbon dioxide gas before the reaction, taking a three-electrode system with gas diffusion electrode as working electrode, where the platinum electrode is the auxiliary electrode, and the silver/silver chloride electrode is the reference electrode, the working electrode is opposite to the auxiliary electrode, and the reference electrode is close to the working electrode, adding electrolyte, stirring magnetically the cathode chamber, locating the working electrode, applying working voltage, and performing the reaction at room temperature, where the gas diffusion electrode includes a gas diffusion electrode body, and an electrochemical reduction catalyst for carbon dioxide supported on the gas diffusion electrode body, the carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction catalyst is a graphite phase carbon nitride supporting nano copper oxide and the nano copper oxide has two crystal forms of black and red ore.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction catalyst is loaded in amount of 0.5-10 mg/cm2. The mass ratio of the urea to the copper acetate is 10:1-20:1. The gas diffusion electrode body includes carbon paper, carbon cloth or carbon felt. The carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction catalyst has a concentration of 1-100 g/l in a mixture of isopropyl alcohol and deionized water. The volume ratio of the isopropyl alcohol to the deionized water is 1:10-10:1. The volume ratio of the Nafion solution to the mixed solution is 1:1000-1:100. Preferred Method: The preparation of the carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction catalyst comprises mixing urea and copper acetate to obtain catalyst precursor, and subjecting the catalyst precursor into pyrolysis reaction to obtain graphite phase carbon nitride-supported nano copper oxide. The pyrolysis reaction has reaction temperature of 400-700 degrees C, a heating rate of 2-20 degrees C/minute, and the reaction time is 1-5 hours. The preparation of the gas diffusion electrode comprises dispersing the carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction catalyst in a mixture of isopropyl alcohol and deionized water, adding a Nafion solution to obtain mixed solution, applying a mixed solution to the gas diffusion electrode body, drying, and loading a gas diffusion electrode with carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction catalyst.

PN CN113430541-A; CN113430541-B
TI Preparing gold-nickel alloy nano catalyst with            core-shell structure for electro-catalytic reduction of            carbon dioxid, involves adding gold salt compound,            nickel salt compound and reducing agent into high            boiling point organic solvent.
AU SUN K
   WANG Z
AE HARBIN INST TECHNOLOGY (HAIT-C)
GA 2021B3840K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing gold-nickel alloy nano catalyst with                core-shell structure involves adding the gold salt                compound, nickel salt compound and reducing agent                into the high boiling point organic solvent,                stirring uniformly under the inert gas atmosphere                and the temperature condition of 20-80?oC to obtain                the precursor mixed solution, where the mass of the                gold salt compound, nickel salt compound and                reducing agent and the volume of the high boiling                point organic solvent is (0.001-1)                g:(0.0015-1.5)g:(0.07-70)g:(1-1000)mL, the molar                ratio of the gold element in the gold salt compound                to the nickel element in the nickel salt compound                is 3:1, adding the surface active agent into the                precursor mixed solution, mixing uniformly to                obtain the mixture, where the ratio of the mass of                the surface active agent and the volume of the                precursor mixed solution is (0.005-5)g:(1-1000) mL                under the inert gas atmosphere.
   USE - Method for preparing gold-nickel alloy                nanometre catalyst with core-shell structure used                for electro-catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide                and to carbon dioxide, methane, ethylene, methanol                and ethanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the problem of low catalytic                performance, bad stability and expensive price of                the existing noble metal-based catalyst                electro-catalytic reduction CO2 as CO, and it is                difficult to practical application; and provides                gold-nickel alloy nano catalyst with gold shell                structure of core-shell structure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing gold-nickel alloy nano catalyst with                core-shell structure involves adding the gold salt                compound, nickel salt compound and reducing agent                into the high boiling point organic solvent,                stirring uniformly under the inert gas atmosphere                and the temperature condition of 20-80?oC to obtain                the precursor mixed solution, where the mass of the                gold salt compound, nickel salt compound and                reducing agent and the volume of the high boiling                point organic solvent is (0.001-1)                g:(0.0015-1.5)g:(0.07-70)g:(1-1000)mL, the molar                ratio of the gold element in the gold salt compound                to the nickel element in the nickel salt compound                is 3:1, adding the surface active agent into the                precursor mixed solution, mixing uniformly to                obtain the mixture, where the ratio of the mass of                the surface active agent and the volume of the                precursor mixed solution is (0.005-5)g:(1-1000) mL                under the inert gas atmosphere, raising the                temperature of the mixture to 240-290?oC and                keeping the temperature for 0.5-3 hours at                240-290?oC, then cooling to room temperature to                obtain the product A, adding organic solvent into                the product A for stirring, separating, removing                upper layer liquid collecting the precipitate to                obtain the precipitated product A, purifying,                dispersing product, compounding by using the mixed                cleaning liquid to wash the precipitated product A                for 3-5 times to obtain purified product A,                dispersing the purified product A in organic                solvent to obtain dispersion liquid, adding the                nano-carbon material into the dispersion,                ultrasonically mixing, centrifuging and separating,                removing the supernatant to obtain the composite                solid product, drying to obtain the gold-nickel                alloy nano catalyst, preparing pre-activated                working electrode, mixing gold-nickel alloy nano                catalyst, Nafion solution and solvent to obtain the                catalyst ink, where the ratio of the volume of the                Nafion solution to the volume of the solvent is                (0.1-100) mg:(0.4-400) microLiter:(0.025-25) mL,                uniformly spraying the catalyst ink on the carbon                paper, drying to obtain pre-activated working                electrode. The pre-activated working electrode is                placed in the cathode region of the H-type                electrolytic cell as cathode, the cathode region                and the anode region of the H-type electrolytic                cell are separated by Nafion proton exchange                membrane, the anode region uses graphite or                platinum electrode as anode, the cathode region is                placed with saturated calomel electrode as                reference electrode, added carbonate electrolyte in                the cathode region and the anode region, the                cathode region under the protection of inert gas,                electrified for 10-180 minutes at a voltage of                -1.2-2 V to obtain product.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for using gold-nickel alloy nanometre catalyst with                core-shell structure. which involves using                gold-nickel alloy nanometre catalyst with                core-shell structure as the working electrode and                for electrically catalyzing and reducing carbon                dioxide to prepare carbon monoxide, using the                H-shaped three-electrode electrolytic cell, using                Nafion 117 proton ion exchange membrane to separate                the cathode cell of the H-shaped three-electrode                electrolytic cell and the anode cell, using                potassium hydrogen carbonate aqueous solution with                concentration of 0.5 mole/L as electrolyte, pouring                the electrolyte into the H- shaped three-electrode                electrolytic cell, filling channel between the                anode cell and the cathode cell in the H-shaped                three-electrode electrolytic cell with electrolyte,                the platinum sheet is the counter electrode,                placing the counter electrode in the anode cell,                placing the working electrode and the reference                electrode in the cathode cell, the reference                electrode is silver/silver chloride electrode of                saturated potasium chloride, the cathode cell is                provided with a cathode area air inlet and a                cathode area air outlet, the carbonn dioxide air                inlet pipe extends to below the electrolyte liquid                surface through the cathode area air inlet, the                cathode area air outlet is connected with the gas                collecting device, placing 1 magnetic stirring                rotor in the cathode tank, sealing the cathode tank                by a sealing piece, sealing the working electrode                and the reference electrode and the contact piece                of the sealing piece, obtaining the device for                preparing carbon monoxide by electrically                catalyzing and reducing carbon dioxide, introducing                carbon dioxide gas into the electrolyte of the                cathode tank by the carbon dioxide gas inlet pipe                at a gas flow of 20 mL/minutes after introducing                for 30 minutes, starting the power supply and the                magnetic stirrer, the carbon dioxide gas flow rate                is 1-30 mL/minutes, the magnetic stirring speed is                500-1200 r/minutes and the electric potential of                the working electrode is -1-1.8 V for carbon                dioxide electro-catalytic reduction, the gas                collecting device collects the gas generated by the                reaction in the cathode tank through the cathode                area air outlet to obtain product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The gold salt compound                  is gold acetate or chloroauric acid tetrahydrate,                  the nickel salt compound is nickel                  acetylacetonate or nickel chloride, the reducing                  agent is 1, 2-hexadecane diol glycol or glycerol,                  the high boiling point organic solvent is octyl                  ether or octadecylene and the inert gas is                  nitrogen or argon gas. The surfactant is                  oleylamine, oleic acid and polyethylene                  polypyrrolidone, where the mixing time of the                  surfactant and the precursor mixed solution is                  30-120 minutes. The mixed cleaning liquid is                  ethanol-normal hexane, where the volume ratio of                  the ethanol to normal hexane in the                  ethanol-normal hexane is 1:2. The solvent is                  methanol, ethanol, acetone, propanol or                  isopropanol. Preferred Conditions: The gold salt                  compound is prepared by using nickel salt                  compound and reducing agent into the high boiling                  point organic solvent, under the inert gas                  atmosphere and 20-80?oC temperature condition,                  stirring for 10-60 minutes at a rotating speed of                  300-1000 r/minutes to obtain the precursor mixed                  solution. The temperature of the mixture is                  raised to 240-290?oC with the heating speed of                  3-10?oC/minutes in the argon atmosphere. The                  volume ratio of the product A to the organic                  solvent is 1:5 and the organic solvent is                  ethanol. The nanometre carbon material is added                  to the dispersion liquid in the step three,                  ultrasonically mixed for 20-20 minutes, then                  centrifuged and separated for 1-5 minutes at the                  centrifugal speed of 8000-15000 r/minutes,                  removed the supernatant to obtain the composite                  solid product, placed in the vacuum drying box,                  dried for 8-24 hours at the temperature of                  150-200?oC to obtain the gold-nickel alloy nano                  catalyst, where the ratio of the mass of the                  purified product A to the volume of the organic                  solvent is (0.002-10)g:(5-5000) mL, the organic                  solvent is normal hexane, the ratio of the mass                  of the nano-carbon material to the volume of the                  dispersion liquid is (0.8-800) mg:(1-1000) mL,                  the nano-carbon material is carbon black. The                  ratio of the volume of the Nafion solution to the                  volume of the solvent is 40mg:160L:5mL. The                  volume of the catalyst ink and the area ratio of                  the carbon paper is (0.01-1) mL:1 cm^2, the                  drying temperature after finishing spraying is                  20-80?oC, the drying time is 5-300 minutes, the                  carbonate electrolyte is potassium carbonate,                  sodium carbonate, potassium bicarbonate or sodium                  bicarbonate, the concentration is 0.1-5                  mole/L.

PT P
PN WO2018188711-A1; EP3609842-A1; EP3609842-B1
TI Method for production of precipitated calcium carbonate (PCC), involves removing surface coating precipitated on portion of shaped elements by non-chemical element, when target thickness of coating is obtained.
AU AKTOR H
AE AA-WATER APS (AAWA-Non-standard)
   AA-WATER APS (AAWA-Non-standard)
GA 201881927W
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves introducing aqueous solution through an inlet (5) to a reactor (1) and withdrawing the solution from the reactor from an outlet (6) horizontally separated from an inlet, thus creating a horizontal flow (14) and a corresponding horizontal hydraulic pressure gradient through a bed (2) of shaped elements. A compressed gas (8) is injected into the aqueous solution to effect a displacement of the aqueous solution inside the shafts and corresponding oppositely directed vertical hydraulic pressure gradient through the bed of shaped elements outside the vertical shafts and a gas-water interface to drive off carbon dioxide dissolved in the aqueous solution. The gas including driven off carbon dioxide is removed from a headspace above the bed of shaped elements. The surface coating precipitated on a portion of the shaped elements is removed by non-chemical element, when target thickness of coating is obtained. The coating consists of calcium carbonate.
   USE - Method for production of precipitated calcium carbonate from aqueous solution of calcium, carbon dioxide, bi-carbonate and carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The energy consumption can be simultaneously reduced and dispensed with the use of chemical agents. The generation of wastewater during the production of precipitated calcium carbonate from aqueous solutions can be avoided. The precipitated calcium carbonate can be produced with low water content, high purity and with a broad range of potential agricultural and industrial applications. The hardness and scaling potential of the processed aqueous solution can be lowered.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The aqueous solution of calcium, carbon dioxide, bicarbonate and carbonate is provided. The reactor containing the bed of shaped elements is provided. The elements are prepared from a material showing a lower modulus of elasticity than precipitated calcium carbonate. Number of vertical shafts (3) is extended from the bottom region of reactor to top of bed. The shafts extend from the bottom of the reactor and are perforated in bottom region (4) with a mesh size preventing the shaped elements from entering the shafts. The compressed gas is injected into the aqueous solution at the bottom of the vertical shafts to effect an upward displacement (11) of the aqueous solution inside the shafts and a corresponding downward directed vertical hydraulic pressure gradient through the bed of shaped elements outside the vertical shafts, and a gas-water interface inside shafts (12) to drive off carbon dioxide dissolved in the aqueous solution. The shaped elements are further prevented from entering the vertical shafts by screens provided at the top openings of the shafts. The compressed gas is injected into the aqueous solution above a screen. The screen retaining the bed of shaped elements at a level below a water surface of the aqueous solution, to effect an upward displacement of the aqueous solution above the screen, downward displacement of the aqueous solution inside the shafts, and a corresponding upward directed vertical hydraulic pressure gradient through the bed of shaped elements outside the vertical shafts, and a gas-water interface in the aqueous solution above the screen to drive off carbon dioxide dissolved in the aqueous solution. The ratio between the respective magnitudes of the vertical and the horizontal hydraulic pressure gradients in the bed of shaped elements amounts to 10 to 50. The surface coating on the shaped elements is removed continuously or intermittently to balance the addition of mass from the precipitation. The coating is removed from the surface of shaped elements in the reactor by intermittent injection of compressed air (20) in the bed of shaped elements at one or several points (21). The injection provokes an air scour that detaches coating as separate particles, which due to a high specific density settle at reactor bottom (22), from where they are withdrawn through one or more outlets (23). The coating is removed from the surface of a fraction sample of shaped elements chosen from the bottom of the bed of shaped elements, in a separate vessel or reactor, such as a hydrocyclone, by hydraulic shear, and/or ultrasound. The fraction sample of shaped elements is reintroduced to the top of the bed of shaped elements in the reactor. The compressed gas is atmospheric air. The no extraneous chemicals are added to the aqueous solution of dissolved calcium, carbon dioxide, bicarbonate and carbonate. The aqueous solution is subjected in the reactor to an essentially non-biological treatment only. The aqueous solution is ground water or surface water with an inherent content of dissolved calcium, carbon dioxide, bicarbonate and carbonate. The fraction of the bed of shaped elements is seeded with calcium carbonate before introduction of the solution to the reactor in order to catalyze the precipitation of surface coating on the shaped elements. The specific density of the shaped elements is between 0 0.88-0.98 kg/L. The Young's Modulus of the shaped elements is between 0.4 and 4 GPa. The shaped elements are composed of 80-100 % of polypropylene or polyethylene. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a plant for production of precipitated calcium carbonate has a bed of shaped elements which are prepared from a material showing a lower modulus of elasticity than precipitated calcium carbonate. The vertical shafts are extended from the bottom region of reactor to the top of bed. The inlet and outlet for an aqueous solution are horizontally spaced apart. An inlet is provided for injection of a compressed gas into the aqueous solution. An outlet (10) is provided to remove gas from a headspace above the bed of shaped elements. The inlet is provided for injection of compressed air for removal of a coating from the surface of the shaped elements. The outlet for withdrawal of settled particles of calcium carbonate is provided with a screen, and is operated by a control valve. Vertical baffles (15) are provided in order to bank up any aqueous solution flowing above the bed of shaped elements, thus preventing short-circuiting of the horizontal flow through bed from the inlet to the outlet, and optionally span the top of the bed of shaped elements from a wall of the reactor to the opposite wall. The vertical shafts are cylindrical. The reactor is cylindrical and is shaped in the form of a box. The vertical shafts are formed as rectangular or laminar cases and span the reactor from one wall to the opposite wall. The plant comprises several serially connected reactors.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional view of the reactor.
   Reactor (1)
   Bed of shaped elements (2)
   Vertical shafts (3)
   Bottom region (4)
   Inlet (5)
   Outlet (6)
   Compressed gas (8)
   Outlet (10)
   Upward displacement (11)
   Shafts (12)
   Compressed air (20)
   Points (21)
   Reactor bottom (22)
   Outlets (23)

PN CN108355379-A; CN108355379-B
TI Preparing carbon dioxide-responsive intelligent hydrophilic-hydrophobic conversion copper mesh comprises e.g. placing copper in the acetone, ethanol and ultrapure water for ultrasonically cleaning, etching, washing, and drying.
AU CHU Y
   PAN Q
   QIN L
AE HARBIN INST TECHNOLOGY (HAIT-C)
GA 201862968A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon dioxide-responsive intelligent hydrophilic-hydrophobic conversion copper mesh comprises e.g. (i) placing copper mesh having pore diameter of 50-1000 mu m in the acetone, ethanol and ultrapure water for ultrasonically cleaning for 15-30 minutes, frequency of ultrasound is 25-60 KHz, and drying at 40-80 degrees C for 30 minutes, preparing mixed aqueous solution of sodium hydroxide and potassium persulfate, placing dried copper mesh in a mixed aqueous solution of sodium hydroxide and potassium persulfate, etching at room temperature for 10-120 minutes, washing with ultrapure water, drying at 40-80 degrees C for 30 minutes to obtain copper mesh having a needle-shaped roughness structure, where the concentration of sodium hydroxide in the mixed aqueous solution of sodium hydroxide and potassium persulfate is 8-80 mg/ml, and (ii) placing copper mesh having the needle-shaped roughness structure in an aqueous solution of silver nitrate, and irradiating.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing carbon dioxide-responsive intelligent hydrophilic-hydrophobic conversion copper mesh.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the durability of the surface.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing carbon dioxide-responsive intelligent hydrophilic-hydrophobic conversion copper mesh comprises (i) placing copper mesh having pore diameter of 50-1000 mu m in the acetone, ethanol and ultrapure water for ultrasonically cleaning for 15-30 minutes, frequency of ultrasound is 25-60 KHz, and drying at 40-80 degrees C for 30 minutes, preparing mixed aqueous solution of sodium hydroxide and potassium persulfate, placing dried copper mesh in a mixed aqueous solution of sodium hydroxide and potassium persulfate, etching at room temperature for 10-120 minutes, washing with ultrapure water, drying at 40-80 degrees C for 30 minutes to obtain copper mesh having a needle-shaped roughness structure, where in the step (i), the concentration of sodium hydroxide in the mixed aqueous solution of sodium hydroxide and potassium persulfate is 8-80 mg/ml, and the concentration of potassium persulfate is 2.67-40 mg/ml, (ii) placing copper mesh having the needle-shaped roughness structure in an aqueous solution of silver nitrate, irradiating with UV light of 254 nm or 365 nm for 10-60 minutes, removing the copper mesh and washing with ultrapure water, drying at 40-80 degrees C for 30 minutes to obtain copper mesh surface-assembled with silver nanoparticles, where the concentration of the silver nitrate aqueous solution is 0.5-5 mg/ml, (iii) taking 1-dodecanethiol under inert gas I protection and water bath at 10 degrees C, uniformly mixing the acetone and methyl trioctyl ammonium chloride in a three-necked flask, and stirring for 10-40 minutes to obtain mixed solution I, adding mixture I in a dropwise into sodium hydroxide aqueous solution I, and stirring for 10-40 minutes to obtain mixed solution II, adding chloroform, adding sodium hydroxide aqueous solution II in a dropwise, and stirring for 8-16 hours, adding ultrapure water and concentrated hydrochloric acid, and stirring for 10-40 minutes, stopping the inert gas I, collecting the filter cake I by suction filtration, adding the filter cake I into the isopropanol and stirring for 10-40 minutes, collecting the filter cake II by suction filtration, drying the filter cake II at 40-80 degrees C for 4-10 hours to obtain S-1-dodecyl-S'-( alpha , alpha '-dimethyl- alpha '-acetic acid) trithiocarbonate, under the conditions of inert gas II protection and room temperature, taking S-1-dodecyl-S'-( alpha , alpha '-dimethyl- alpha '-acetic acid) trithiocarbonate, azobisisobutyronitrile, diethylaminoethyl methacrylate and 1,4-dioxane, uniformly mixing, stirring under inert gas protection and temperature of 80 degrees C for 18 hours, stopping introducing inert gas II, placing reaction product in a dialysis bag, dialyzing the dialysis bag in an aqueous solution of hydrochloric acid having pH of 4.5-5 for 70-72 hours, freezing and drying, and removing to obtain polymer with carbon dioxide response, where dialysis bag has molecular weight cut off of 3000 Daltons, the sodium hydroxide aqueous solution I has mass fraction of sodium hydroxide of 50%, and sodium hydroxide aqueous solution II has mass fraction of sodium hydroxide of 50%, where the aqueous sodium hydroxide solution II is equal in volume to the aqueous sodium hydroxide solution I, the mass ratio of the dodecyl mercaptan and the sodium hydroxide in the aqueous sodium hydroxide solution I is (70-100):33.5, the mass ratio of the acetone and the sodium hydroxide in the aqueous sodium hydroxide solution I is (50-25):33.5, and the mass ratio of the methyl trioctyl ammonium chloride and the sodium hydroxide in the sodium hydroxide aqueous solution I is (5-8):33.5, the mass ratio of the chloroform and the sodium hydroxide in the aqueous sodium hydroxide solution I is (60-90):33.5, the mass fraction of concentrated hydrochloric acid is 37%, the mass ratio of the concentrated hydrochloric acid volume and the sodium hydroxide in the aqueous sodium hydroxide solution I is (50-300 ml):33.5 g, the mass ratio of the volume of isopropanol and the sodium hydroxide in the aqueous sodium hydroxide solution I is (100-2000 ml):33.5 g, the volume ratio of the ultrapure water and the aqueous sodium hydroxide solution I is 5:(2-5), the mass ratio of azobisisobutyronitrile and S-1-dodecyl-S'-( alpha , alpha '-dimethyl- alpha '-acetic acid) trithiocarbonate is (50-150):4, the 1,4-dioxane, and S-1-dodecyl-S'-( alpha , alpha '-dimethyl- alpha '-acetic acid) trithiocarbonate is (3-20 ml):4 mg, and the mass ratio of diethylaminoethyl methacrylate and S-1-dodecyl-S'-( alpha , alpha '-dimethyl- alpha '-acetic acid) trithiocarbonate is (250-3750):1, and (iv) mixing PDMS substrate A and curing agent B in a toluene solution, adding the polymer with carbon dioxide response obtained in the step (iii), stirring for 10-40 minutes to obtain mixture III, and placing the surface-assembled silver nanoparticle-containing copper mesh obtained in the step (ii) in the mixture III, soaking for 1-20 minutes, taking out, drying at 40-80 degrees C for 30 minutes to obtain carbon dioxide response intelligent hydrophilic-hydrophobic conversion copper network, where the mass ratio of the PDMS substrate A and the curing agent B is 1:(0.05-0.5), the ratio of the total mass of the PDMS substrate A and the curing agent B to the volume of toluene is 1 g:(10-100 ml), and the ratio of the mass of the polymer having carbon dioxide response to the volume of toluene is 1 g:(10-400 ml).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (iii), the inert gas I is nitrogen, preferably argon gas. In the step (iii), the inert gas II is nitrogen, preferably argon gas.

PN CN112246213-A; CN112246213-B
TI Preparation of calcium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent comprises preparing calcium oxide nano particles, magnesium oxide nano particles and aluminum oxide nano particles, mixing, and grinding.
AU LUO C
   LUO T
   ZHANG L
   LI X
   WU F
AE UNIV HUAZHONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHZ-C)
GA 202111689X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of calcium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent comprises preparing calcium oxide nano particles, magnesium oxide nano particles and aluminum oxide nano particles at mass ratio of (75-85):10:10, mixing, and grinding, where grain diameter of the calcium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent is 30-100 nm.
   USE - The method is used for preparation of calcium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorbent blocks calcium oxide consumption, has small particle size and regular microscopic morphology, and can be accurately control the amount of doped magnesium and aluminum oxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for calcium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The calcium oxide nanoparticles are prepared by mixing calcium nitrate tetrahydrate, citric acid monohydrate and deionized water at molar ratio of 1:1:(32-36) to obtain first mixed solution, stirring at 75-85 degrees C for 3-6 hours to form a first gel, standing at 18-31 degrees C for 18-20 hours, drying for 4-6 hours at 75-85 degrees C and for 11-13 hours at 105-115 degrees C, calcining for 1.5-2 hours at 450-550 degrees C and for 20 minutes at 825-855 degrees C, and cooling to 18-31 degrees C. The magnesium oxide nanoparticle comprises mixing magnesium nitrate hexahydrate, citric acid monohydrate and deionized water at molar ratio of 1:1:(32-36) to obtain second mixed solution, stirring at 75-85 degrees C for 3-6 hours to form gel, standing at 18-31 degrees C for 18-20 hours, drying for 4-6 hours at 75-85 degrees C and for 11-13 hours at 105-115 degrees C, calcining for 1.5-2 hours at 450-550 degrees C and for 20 minutes at 825-855 degrees C, and cooling to 18-31 degrees C. The aluminum oxide nanoparticle is prepared by mixing aluminum nitrate nonahydrate, citric acid monohydrate and deionized water at molar ratio of 1:1:(32-36) to obtain third mixed solution, stirring at 75-85 degrees C for 3-6 hours to form gel, standing at 18-31 degrees C for 18-20 hours, drying for 4-6 hours at 75-85 degrees C and for 11-13 hours at 105-115 degrees C, calcining for 1.5-2 hours at 450-550 degrees C and for 20 minutes at 825-855 degrees C, and cooling to 18-31 degrees C. Preferred Components: The molar ratio of calcium nitrate tetrahydrate, citric acid monohydrate and deionized water is 1:1:(32-36), preferably 1:1:35. The molar ratio of magnesium nitrate hexahydrate, citric acid monohydrate and deionized water is 1:1:(32-36), preferably 1:1:35. The molar ratio of aluminum nitrate nonahydrate, citric acid monohydrate and deionized water is 1:1:(32-36), preferably 1:1:35.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN WO2021146169-A1
TI Autodeposition coating line used e.g. in autodeposition coatings on metallic surfaces of substrate, has autodeposition coating bath having autodeposition composition comprising epoxy-based coating material and catalyst rinse bath.
AU KOZAK W G
   AHMED B M
   DU L
   SKIKUN R M
   HICKEY D A
AE HENKEL AG & CO KGAA (HENK-C)
   KOZAK W G (KOZA-Individual)
GA 2021831045
AB    NOVELTY - Autodeposition coating line comprises: an autodeposition coating bath configured to receive at least one article, where the coating bath contains an autodeposition composition comprising an epoxy-based autodeposition coating material selected to form a layer of uncured autodeposition composition on an active metal surface of the articles upon contact; a catalyst rinse bath configured to receive the articles following the autodeposition coating bath; an ion exchange system comprising an ion exchange column (204) containing a strong base anion exchange resin, many fluid control circuits between the catalyst rinse bath and the ion exchange column and at least one control operatively connected to the fluid control circuits and configured to selectively operate the fluid control circuits; and a cure oven configured to receive the articles following the catalyst rinse bath.
   USE - The autodeposition coating line is useful: in the field of autodeposition coatings on metallic surfaces of a substrate; or for contacting a metal surface of the article e.g. immersion (e.g. dipping), spraying or roll coating.
   ADVANTAGE - The autodeposition coating line: maintains the condition of an active catalyst in a crosslinker bath in autodeposition coating systems; and extends the operative life of catalysts used in the production of articles having autodeposition coatings.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Autodeposition coating line comprises: an autodeposition coating bath configured to receive at least one article, where the coating bath contains an autodeposition composition comprising an epoxy-based autodeposition coating material selected to form a layer of uncured autodeposition composition on an active metal surface of the articles upon contact; a catalyst rinse bath configured to receive the articles following the autodeposition coating bath, where the catalyst rinse bath contains an aqueous catalyst composition comprising a dissolved and/or dispersed crosslinking catalyst that is capable of reacting with and/or absorbing carbon dioxide; an ion exchange system comprising an ion exchange column (204) containing a strong base anion exchange resin, many fluid control circuits between the catalyst rinse bath and the ion exchange column and at least one control operatively connected to the fluid control circuits and configured to selectively operate the fluid control circuits to remove a quantity of the aqueous catalyst composition from the catalyst rinse bath, pass the catalyst composition through the ion exchange column into contact with the strong base anion exchange resin capable of removing a quantity of carbon dioxide from the catalyst composition and return the catalyst composition to the catalyst rinse bath; and a cure oven configured to receive the articles following the catalyst rinse bath. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) an open-to-air crosslinking catalyst rinse bath system comprising catalyst rinse bath containing an aqueous catalyst composition in contact with atmospheric air and comprising a dissolved and/or dispersed crosslinking catalyst that is capable of reacting with and/or absorbing carbon dioxide, an ion exchange column containing a strong base anion exchange resin, a first fluid control circuit connecting an outlet of the catalyst rinse bath to an inlet of the ion exchange column and a second fluid control circuit connecting an outlet of the ion exchange column to an inlet of the catalyst rinse bath, where the first fluid control circuit is configured to convey a quantity of the aqueous catalyst composition from the catalyst rinse bath to the ion exchange column into contact with the strong base anion exchange resin capable of removing a quantity of carbon dioxide from the quantity of aqueous catalyst composition, and the second fluid control circuit is configured to convey the quantity of the aqueous catalyst composition from the ion exchange column to the catalyst rinse bath;
   (2) operating a crosslinking catalyst rinse bath system having a catalyst rinse bath containing an aqueous catalyst composition exposed to at least one sources of carbon dioxide, the aqueous catalyst composition comprising a dissolved and/or dispersed crosslinking catalyst that is capable of reacting with and/or absorbing carbon dioxide and an ion exchange column, where the method (M1) comprising (a) contacting the articles comprising a surface coated with an uncured composition film to the aqueous catalyst composition while at least a portion of the aqueous catalyst composition is deactivated by carbonation of the catalyst with carbon dioxide to a carbonated form, (b) moving a quantity of the aqueous catalyst composition including at least some of the portion of the catalyst in the carbonated form to the ion exchange column, (c) contacting the quantity of aqueous catalyst composition with a strong base anion exchange resin in the ion exchange column thereby converting at least some of the portion in the catalyst in the carbonated form to aqueous catalyst composition in a catalytically active free base form and (d) moving the quantity of aqueous catalyst composition to the catalyst rinse bath; and
   (3) controlling carbonate concentration in an open-to-air catalyst rinse bath, where the method (M2) comprising providing a quantity of an aqueous catalyst composition comprising a dissolved and/or dispersed crosslinking catalyst that is capable of reacting with and/or absorbing carbon dioxide into the catalyst rinse bath, exposing the quantity of aqueous catalyst composition to a source of carbon dioxide to thereby convert a portion of the crosslinking catalyst to a carbonated form, temporarily immersing the articles coated with an autodeposition compound film in the aqueous catalyst composition and upon determining that the amount of crosslinking catalyst in the carbonated form is above a predetermined amount, removing a quantity of carbon dioxide from the aqueous catalyst composition to reduce the amount of crosslinking catalyst in the carbonated form below the predetermined amount without adding water soluble ions or particulates to the catalyst rinse bath.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view of a catalyst bath treatment system.
   Catalyst bath treatment system (200)
   Ion exchange column (204)
   Wash tank (238)
   Fifth fluid control circuit (240)
   Wash system pump (244)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the method (M1): the step (b) is performed after determining optionally by measuring that a total quantity of aqueous catalyst composition in the free base form following step (a) is less than a predetermined threshold quantity; and step (b) is performed a predetermined time after initiating step (a). The method (M1) further comprises: (e) contacting the strong base anion exchange resin in the ion exchange column with a quantity of regenerant, where the regenerant comprises sodium hydroxide or potassium hydroxide; (f) rinsing the ion exchange column with water to remove at least a portion of any remaining regenerant from the ion exchange column, where the step (f) comprises backflushing the strong base anion exchange resin in the ion exchange column; before step (e), rinsing the ion exchange column with water to remove at least a portion of any remaining aqueous catalyst composition from the ion exchange column, where the step (e) is performed after a predetermined number of iterations of steps (a) through (d), optionally measuring that a total quantity of aqueous catalyst composition in the free base form following step (c) is less than a predetermined threshold quantity; and the step (b) further comprises reducing temperature of the quantity of the aqueous catalyst composition including at least some of the portion of the catalyst in the carbonated form prior to entry into the ion exchange column. In the method (M2), removing a quantity of carbon dioxide from the aqueous catalyst composition to reduce the amount of crosslinking catalyst in the carbonated form below the predetermined amount comprises contacting at least a portion of the quantity of aqueous catalyst composition with a strong base anion exchange resin to convert at least some of the portion in the carbonated form to crosslinking catalyst in a free base form. Preferred Components: The aqueous catalyst composition within the catalyst rinse bath is in contact with atmospheric air. The cure oven is operable at a temperature to cure the layer of uncured autodeposition composition on the articles to full cure at less than or equal to 140 degrees C. The aqueous catalyst composition comprises an amine-based catalyst. At least a portion of the amine-based catalyst is deactivated by carbonation of the portion with carbon dioxide. The controls operate manually or automatically. The regenerant comprises at least one of sodium hydroxide and potassium hydroxide. The portion on the amidine is deactivated by carbonation thereof with carbon dioxide. The aqueous catalyst composition consists of at least one amidine and water. The strong base anion exchange resin comprises: quaternary ammonium functional groups in the OH-form substituted thereon; and a crosslinked resin having a vinyl aromatic or acrylic polymeric backbone and the quaternary ammonium groups are bonded to the backbone. The source of carbon dioxide comprises atmospheric air in contact with the aqueous catalyst composition in the catalyst rinse bath.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The autodeposition coating line further comprises: (a) a cleaning rinse bath configured to receive the articles before the autodeposition coating bath, where the cleaning rinse bath comprises a degreasing composition; (b) a water rinse bath configured to receive the articles by following the cleaning rinse bath and before the autodeposition coating bath, where the water rinse bath contains a quantity of water; and (c1) a water rinse bath configured to receive the articles and following the autodeposition coating bath and prior to the catalyst rinse bath, where the water rinse bath contains a quantity of water, or (c2) a post-treatment rinse bath configured to receive the articles and following the autodeposition coating bath and prior to the catalyst rinse bath, where the post-treatment rinse bath comprises a post-treatment reagent comprising at least one soluble zirconium compound, soluble titanium compound and an alkaline earth metal compound. The first fluid control circuit comprises a first valve, the second fluid control circuit comprises a second valve, and at least one of the first fluid control circuit and the second fluid control circuit comprises a first pump. The first fluid control circuit and the second fluid control circuit comprises a heat exchanger or a holding tank. The open-to-air crosslinking catalyst rinse bath system further comprises: a processor operatively connected to the first fluid control circuit and the second fluid control circuit and configured to operate the first fluid control circuit to convey the quantity of the aqueous catalyst composition from the catalyst rinse bath to the ion exchange column and operate the second fluid control circuit to convey the quantity of the aqueous catalyst composition from the ion exchange column to the catalyst rinse bath; a supply of regenerant, a third fluid control circuit extending from an outlet of the supply of regenerant to the inlet of the ion exchange column and a fourth fluid control circuit extending from the outlet of the ion exchange column to a waste receptacle; a processor operatively connected to the third fluid control circuit and the fourth fluid control circuit, and configured to operate the third fluid control circuit to convey a quantity of the regenerant from the supply of regenerant to the ion exchange column and operate the fourth fluid control circuit to convey the quantity of the regenerant from the ion exchange column to the waste receptacle; a water supply and a fifth fluid control circuit (240) extending from an outlet of the water supply to the inlet of the ion exchange column; a processor operatively connected to the fifth fluid control circuit and the fourth fluid control circuit and configured to operate the fifth fluid control circuit to convey a first quantity of water from the water supply to the inlet of the ion exchange column and operate the fourth fluid control circuit to convey the first quantity of water from the ion exchange column to the waste receptacle; a sixth fluid control circuit extending from the outlet of the water supply to the outlet of the ion exchange column and a seventh fluid control circuit extending from the inlet of the ion exchange column to the waste receptacle; and a processor operatively connected to the fourth fluid control circuit, the fifth fluid control circuit, the sixth fluid control circuit and the seventh fluid control circuit, the processor is configured to alternately operate the fifth fluid control circuit to convey a first quantity of water from the water supply to the inlet of the ion exchange column and operate the fourth fluid control circuit to convey the first quantity of water from the ion exchange column to the waste receptacle and operate the sixth fluid control circuit to convey a second quantity of water from the water supply to the outlet of the ion exchange column and operate the seventh fluid control circuit to convey the second quantity of water from the ion exchange column to the waste receptacle. The processor is: operatively connected to the first fluid control circuit and the second fluid control circuit and configured to operate the first fluid control circuit to convey the quantity of the aqueous catalyst composition from the catalyst rinse bath to the ion exchange column and operate the second fluid control circuit to convey the quantity of the aqueous catalyst composition from the ion exchange column to the catalyst rinse bath; and operatively connected to the third fluid control circuit and the fourth fluid control circuit and configured to operate the third fluid control circuit to convey a quantity of the regenerant from the supply of regenerant to the ion exchange column and operate the fourth fluid control circuit to convey the quantity of the regenerant from the ion exchange column to the waste receptacle.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The aqueous catalyst composition comprises cyclic amidines (preferred), tertiary amines, quinuclidine-based catalysts, and triazine-based catalysts, preferably 1,8-diazabicyclo(5.4.0)undece-1-ene, 1,5-diazabicycio(4.3.0)non-5-ene, 1,2-dimethyl-1,4,5,6-tetrahydropyrimidine, N-(3-dimethylaminopropyl)-N,N-diisopropanolamine, diazabicyclo(2.2.2)octane, 1,3,5-tris(3-(dimethylamino)propyl)-hexahydro-s-triazine, imidazole, 1,2-dimethylimidazole and/or dicyandiamide.

PT P
PN CN107697933-A; CN107697933-B
TI Preparation of lithium hexafluorophosphate by reacting lithium fluoride with phosphorus pentafluoride using liquid carbon dioxide or supercritical carbon dioxide fluid as reaction solvent.
AU WANG N
   MAO X
   LI G
AE SHANGHAI LVLINDA NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018154585
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of lithium hexafluorophosphate comprises reacting lithium fluoride with phosphorus pentafluoride using liquid carbon dioxide or supercritical carbon dioxide fluid as reaction solvent.
   USE - Preparation of lithium hexafluorophosphate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides high-purity lithium hexafluorophosphate, which has good quality and does not contain impurities, is green and environment friendly, resource saving, safe and suitable for industrial production, and has low cost.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: When liquid carbon dioxide is used as the reaction solvent, the reaction temperature is - 56.6-31.1 degrees C, preferably - 15-25 degrees C and the reaction pressure is 0.55-50 MPa, preferably 5-15 MPa. The method comprises adding liquid carbon dioxide and lithium fluoride into the reaction vessel while controlling the pressure in the reaction vessel to keep the carbon dioxide in a liquid state, adding phosphorus pentafluoride gas in the reaction vessel and reacting. When supercritical carbon dioxide fluid is used as the reaction solvent, the reaction temperature is 35-100 degrees C, preferably 45-60 degrees C and the reaction pressure is 8-50 MPa, preferably 10-20 MPa. The method comprises adding phosphorus pentafluoride gas and lithium fluoride into the reaction vessel, introducing carbon dioxide, and keeping the temperature and pressure above the critical temperature and critical pressure of carbon dioxide to keep the carbon dioxide in supercritical state. The mol ratio of lithium fluoride and phosphorus pentafluoride is 1:1.01-8, preferably 1:1.2-2. The mass ratio of lithium fluoride and carbon dioxide solvent is 1:5-50. The reaction is carried out in a dry reaction vessel having water content of less than 10 ppm, where inert gas is used to dry the reaction vessel before the reaction, and inert gas is carbon dioxide gas.

TI Porous aggregate for preparing plate, comprises            mixed aqueous solution and mixed dry material, where            mixed aqueous solution includes water reducing agent            and aqueous solution of reinforcing agent, and mixed            dry material includes gelling material, sulfur urea            slag, pigments, carbide slag, and cement.
AU BAI J
   CAI Y
   LI L
   ZHANG Y
   YANG X
   MA J
   CUI D
   ZHANG X
AE SHANDONG HANBO YUZHOU NEW MATERIAL CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022C8895X
AB    NOVELTY - Porous aggregate comprises mixed aqueous                solution and mixed dry material in the ratio of                1:(2-25). The mixed aqueous solution includes a                water reducing agent and an aqueous solution of a                reinforcing agent. The mixed dry material includes                gelling material, sulfur urea slag, pigments,                carbide slag, cement and blowing agent.
   USE - Porous aggregate used for preparing a plate                (claimed) and in concrete, stone and other field,                where the aggregate is granular loose material with                framework or filling function in the concrete,                which is the main raw material in concrete.
   ADVANTAGE - The porous aggregate has wide source of each                component, and contains multiple industrial waste                residue, excellent strength and carbonization                degree, effectively increase the strength of the                stone material, replaces stone in the concrete                industry, stone field and road construction                decoration industry widely used, and solves the                problem of natural stone resource lack and high                cost of the traditional artificial aggregate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   1. a method for preparing a porous aggregate,                which involves:
   a. mixing cementitious material, thiourea                slag, pigment, carbide slag, cement and foaming                agent to obtain mixed dry material; b. mixing the                water reducing agent and the reinforcing agent                aqueous solution to obtain a mixed solution;
   c. mixing the mixed liquid and the mixed dry                material and stirring to form a slurry;
   d. pouring the slurry into shape, and                obtaining an aggregate matrix through curing and                demolding; e. carbonizing the aggregate matrix in a                carbon dioxide environment to obtain product;
   2. a porous aggregate plate, which comprises                160-200 pts. wt. gelling material, 25-40 pts. wt.                gold tailings, 3-8 pts. wt. quartz powder, 22-40                pts. wt. water and 10-30 pts. wt. porous aggregate;                and
   3. a method for preparing a porous aggregate                plate, which involves:
   a. mixing the cementitious material, gold                tailings, quartz powder and water to obtain a wet                material, and mixing the wet material with porous                aggregate to obtain a mixed wet material;
   b. obtaining porous aggregate substrate by                pressing and forming, and then carbonizing in a                carbon dioxide environment to obtain a porous                aggregate plate.

The cementitious material is formed by mixing                  steel slag and furnace slag in a mass ratio of                  (9-5):(1-5). The curing temperature is                  10-100&#8451; for 1-54 hours. The carbon                  dioxide concentration during carbonization is                  3-100%. The carbonization time is 1-72                  hours.

PN WO2022162700-A1; IN202141004385-A
TI Culturing cell for producing fusion protein            composition including sialylated N-glycans involves            e.g. maintaining higher partial pressure of carbon            dioxide levels during production phase than growth            phase, and supplementing medium with sugar.
AU KANDULA R B R
   BANDYOPADHYAY S
   CHANDRAWANSHI V
   VEERAMALLY S K
   POLISHETTY R
   BS M
   MEGHA B
AE REDDYS LAB LTD (REDY-C)
   DR REDDYS LAB LTD (REDY-C)
GA 202299455N
AB    NOVELTY - Culturing (M1) cell for producing a fusion                protein composition comprising sialylated                N-glycans, comprises (a) providing/culturing                mammalian cells expressing the fusion protein, (b)                culturing the cells at a stringent pH value, (c)                maintaining higher partial pressure of carbon                dioxide (pCO2) levels during the production phase                than the pCO2 levels during the growth phase, (d)                culturing the cells at first temperature for a                first period of time, and subjecting the cell                culture to a temperature shift, where the second                temperature is lower than the first temperature,                (e) supplementing the cell culture medium with at                least one sugar, and (f) recovering the fusion                protein composition from the culture, where the                sialylated N-glycan content of the fusion protein                composition is reduced as compared to sialylated                N-glycan content of the fusion protein composition                produced by a similar method devoid of the steps                (b)-(d).
   USE - The method is useful for: culturing cell for                producing a fusion protein composition comprising                sialylated N-glycans (claimed); producing a                glycoprotein composition from mammalian cell                culture, where the composition comprises a target                total sialylated and or di- and tri-sialylated                N-glycan variant; producing fragment                crystallizable-fusion protein compositions                characterized by particular levels of sialylated                glycoforms; and producing a CTLA-4 fusion protein                composition comprising target sialylated                N-glycans.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces sialylated N-glycan content                of the fusion protein composition by 21-38%, di-                and tri-sialylated N-glycans content by 20.83%                and/or total sialylated glycans by 50.82%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                culturing (M2) cell for producing a fusion protein                composition comprising sialylated N-glycans,                comprising (a1) providing/culturing mammalian cells                expressing the fusion protein, (b1) culturing the                cells at a pH of 7 plus minus 0.1, (c1) maintaining                the growth phase pCO2 levels at 20-50 mmHg and                increasing the pCO2 levels to 100-135 mmHg during                the production phase, (d1) subjecting the cell                culture to a temperature shift of 37-34 degrees C                on day 6 of cell culture, (e1) supplementing the                cell culture medium with at least one sugar, and                (f1) recovering the fusion protein composition from                the culture, where the resultant fusion protein                composition comprises 20.83% di-and tri-sialylated                N-glycans content and/or 50.82% total sialylated                glycans.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the method (M1), the                  pCO2 levels is maintained at 20-50 mmHg during                  the growth phase and increased to 100-135 mmHg                  during the production phase. Preferred                  Components: In the method (M1): the stringent pH                  value is 7 plus minus 0.1; and the temperature                  shift is 37-34 degrees C on day 6 of cell                  culture. In the methods (M1)-(M2): the fusion                  protein is a cytotoxic T-lymphocyte antigen 4                  (CTLA-4)-immunoglobulin G fusion protein, or                  abatacept; and the mammalian cells used to                  express the fusion protein is Chinese hamster                  ovary cells.
   EXAMPLE - A cytotoxic T-lymphocyte antigen                4-immunolgobulin G fusion protein, having a primary                protein structure was cloned and expressed in a                Chinese hamster ovary (CHO) cell line techniques.                The cell culture was initiated by seeding the cells                at seeding density of 0.5 million cells/ml in                EXCELL Advanced CHO Fed-Batch Medium (RTM:                Chemically-defined, animal component free, and                contains no hydrolysates or components of unknown                composition). The cell culture medium was                supplemented with glucose, which was made up to a                concentration of 5 g/l every day. A total of 18                vol.% feed medium (BalanCD CHO Feed 4 (RTM: Single                part dry powder feed medium)) was added on day 3                (3%), day 5 (5%), day 7 (4%), day 9 (3%), day 11                (1.5%) and day 12 (1.5%). The cell culture was                initiated at temperature 37 degrees C and a dual                temperature shifts were performed. The first shift                from 37 degrees C to 34 degrees C was done on day 5                and second shift from 34 degrees C to 30 degrees C                was performed on day 9. The cell culture was                maintained at a relaxed pH of 7 plus minus 0.4                using 1 M sodium carbonate and carbon dioxide                sparging. The culture was harvested on day 14 or at                cell viability greater than 80%.

TI Prefabricated underground continuous wall and joint structure, has limiting block located at extruding port, where outer end of limiting block is provided with wet water adhesive layer and limiting groove seat is fixedly connected with film.

   XIE B

   LUO H

AB    NOVELTY - The structure has a detachable steel plate (1) outer end is provided with a preformed steel plate (2). The inner wall of the preformed steel plate fixedly connected with two symmetrical limiting baffle frame (5). The outer end of the preformed steel plate is provided with a horizontal reinforcing steel bar (6). The outer end of the horizontal reinforcing steel bar is provided with a rubber water stopping belt (4). The outer end of the horizontal reinforcing steel bar fixedly connected with a longitudinal reinforcing steel bar (7). A fixing pipe whose outer end is provided with an opening. An inner wall of the fixing pipe is fixedly connected with multiple limiting groove seats. The limiting groove seat is matched with a limiting block. A limiting block is located at an extruding port. An outer end of the limiting block is formed with a wet water adhesive layer. The limiting slot seat and a film are filled with water solution.
   USE - Prefabricated underground continuous wall and sectional joint structure for use in a ground using a digging machine along deep digging engineering of a peripheral axis.
   ADVANTAGE - The structure: can realize pouring concrete, the carbon dioxide solution in the liquid storage ball shaking, causing carbon dioxide to overflow into the straight cylinder, pushing the piston extruding fixing pipe extending into the fixing groove, when the flexible sheet is extruded to the vertical state, driving the two inclined rods away from the extruding reed, and piercing the film, making the wet water adhesive layer generate viscosity, promoting the limiting block and the limiting groove seat tightly adhered and fixed, reinforcing connection stability, and stretching the capsule along with the spring is stretched. The phase change supporting rod is stretched, and the soft becomes hard under the action of pulling force, forming a hard supporting rod, which not only effectively reduces the impact influence of concrete pouring, but also provides supporting firmness.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a whole structure schematic diagram of the prefabricated underground continuous wall and joint structure.

PN CN114001934-A
TI Spacecraft heat balance test method suitable for Martian surface wind field environment, involves verifying influence of forced convection heat exchange between different wind speeds and wind directions of low wind temperature area outside cabin.
AU ZHENG K
   DAI C
   ZHANG B
   MIAO J
   XIANG Y
   JIA Y
   XUE S
AE BEIJING SPACE VEHICLE GEN DESIGN DEPT (BEIP-C)
GA 202225908B
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves determining whether a wind field environment parameter in a space simulator (1) is consistent with an atmosphere environment of a spark surface. A size of a uniform wind field test section (3) is determined in the space simulator according to the wind field environmental parameter. A spacecraft (2) is fixed on a central area of the uniform wind-field test section in front and back directions. A fire surface simulation device (5) is installed under the spacecraft according to an actual rail distance with the spacecraft. An angle between wind field incoming flow and the spacecraft is adjusted by rotating the spacecraft to simulate different wind directions. An influence of forced convection heat exchange between different wind speeds and wind directions of a low wind temperature area outside a cabin is verified.
   USE - Spacecraft heat balance test method suitable for Martian surface wind field environment e.g. low pressure, wide temperature area, high wind speed and carbon dioxide gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables evaluating and analyzing, opening the spark surface, low air pressure, wide temperature area, high wind speed, so that the main component is carbon dioxide atmospheric environment factor into the domestic device capability can effectively simulate the control parameter, thus providing the basis of the wind field heat balance test verification, which is good for realizing spark surface gas, wind field, temperature, air pressure and other environment factors of effective simulation. The setting of the air pressure can accurately simulate the true environment of the maximum pressure of the fire surface. The stroke temperature of the method is controlled for simulating spacecraft low temperature forced convection effect. The method provides guarantee for spacecraft in rail temperature, which can make the spacecraft in the rail low temperature area temperature telemetry value and the pre-set value error is less than or equal to 0.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the spacecraft heat balance test method suitable for Martian surface wind field environment. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Space simulator (1)
   Spacecraft (2)
   Uniform wind field test section (3)
   Temperature adjusting heat sink (4)
   Fire surface simulation device (5)
   Fan system (6)
   Test support (7)
