PN CN113684490-A
TI Solid carbon system used for preparing hydrogen coupling electrolysis of salt-containing waste water includes electrolytic cell, anode chamber and cathode chamber, and membrane for isolating anode and cathode chamber.
AU WANG P
   REN Z
   ZHANG C
   WANG F
   WANG J
   YU Z
AE HUANENG MINGTAI ELECTRIC POWER CO LTD (HUAN-Non-standard)
   SICHUIAN HUANENG FUJIANG HYDROPOWER CO (CHHN-C)
   SICHUAN HUANENG KANGDING HYDROPOWER CO (SICH-Non-standard)
   SICHUAN HUANENG DONGXIGUAN HYDROPOWER CO (SICH-Non-standard)
   SICHUAN HUANENG JIALINGJIANG HYDROPOWER (CHHN-C)
   SICHUAN HUANENG BAOXINGHE HYDROPOWER CO (SICH-Non-standard)
   SICHUAN HUANENG TAIPINGYI HYDROPOWER CO (SICH-Non-standard)
   HUANENG GROUP TECHNOLOGY INNOVATION CENT (CHHN-C)
   SICHUAN HUANENG HYDROGEN ENERGY TECHNOLO (SICH-Non-standard)
   CHINA HUANENG CLEAN ENERGY RES INST (CHHN-C)
GA 2021E4236D
AB    NOVELTY - Solid carbon system comprises electrolytic cell (2), anode chamber and cathode chamber alternately arranged inside, and membrane for isolating anode and cathode chambers. A gas-liquid reaction tower (3) is arranged downstream of electrolytic cell to receive alkali liquid in cathode chamber and externally connect carbon-rich flue gas (5). The alkali liquid and carbon-rich flue gas are arranged inside gas-liquid reaction tower. The filler is provided in filler contact with carbon reaction tower, and filler is used to fix carbon reaction. The evaporating and crystallizing tower (4) is arranged downstream of gas-liquid reaction tower to receive solution after carbon fixation reaction, and low-temperature air (6) is externally connected to concentrate and crystallize solution after carbon fixation reaction.
   USE - Solid carbon system used for preparing hydrogen coupling electrolysis of salt-containing waste water (claimed), which is used in the technical field of wastewater treatment in electric power, coal chemical industry and other industries.
   ADVANTAGE - The salt-containing waste water electrolysis hydrogen-producing coupling solid carbon system has reasonable design, realizes the treatment of waste water, resource recycling and energy material production, and realizes comprehensive utilization of salt-containing waste water by electrolysis process, and simultaneously generates green energy material hydrogen. The electrolyte can be further used for fixing carbon dioxide, pushing carbon to neutralize. The system uses NaOH solution produced in electrolysis process to simultaneously fix carbon dioxide in carbon-rich flue gas to realize carbon capture.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of hydrogen coupling electrolyzing salt-containing waste water comprising:
   (a) passing 2-26.5 wt.% salt-containing wastewater into electrolytic cell, where chlorine is produced in anode compartment and sodium ion is passed through cation exchange membrane into cathode compartment to generate hydrogen, using hydroxide and sodium ions to form a sodium hydroxide (NaOH) solution, and controlling pH of solution to be greater than 13;
   (b) circulating and spraying NaOH solution in gas-liquid reaction tower, and reacting with filler contact layer and carbon-rich flue gas to solidify carbon, and controlling pH to 7-8 to generate sodium bicarbonate (NaHCO3) solution;
   (c) spraying NaHCO3 solution cyclically in evaporation crystallization tower, and contacting with low-temperature air at a temperature of 50-60degrees Celsius, concentration ratio is 2-5 times, and crystallizing NaHCO3; and
   (d) refluxing solution separated by NaHCO3 crystal to the electrolytic cell.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The electrolytic cell is ion exchange membrane electrolytic cell, and diaphragm is cation exchange membrane.Preferred Process: The method further involves passing activated carbon filter through pretreatment system to remove suspended solids and heavy metals, and filtering by ion resin device to remove calcium, magnesium and heavy metal ions before 2-26.5 wt.% salt-containing wastewater is passed into electrolytic cell. The solution after NaHCO3 crystallization is refluxed to the over-pretreatment system.
DC A85 (Electrical applications.); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); X25 (Industrial Electric Equipment)

PT P
PN WO2021186445-A1; US2022263147-A1; KR2022152324-A
TI Recovering valuable metals from spent lithium ion            batteries by carbonizing lithium from spent batteries,            treating with cold water, filtering, heating lithium            carbonate filtrate, and treating residue with flux to            obtain metallic ingots.
AU LUBOMIRSKY I
   KAPLAN V
   VALERY K
AE YEDA RES & DEV CO LTD (YEDA-C)
   YEDA RES & DEV CO LTD (YEDA-C)
GA 2021B5866Q
AB    NOVELTY - Valuable metals are recovered from spent                lithium ion batteries (LIBs) by grinding remaining                spent LIB to obtain particulate spent lithium ion                batteries material; separating plastic and Teflon                (RTM: PTFE) matrix; treating separated particulate                spent lithium ion batteries material with carbon                dioxide (CO2)/carbon monoxide/water vapor mixture,                methane/air, and/or solid carbon for carbonization                of lithium to obtain particulate spent lithium ion                batteries material comprising lithium carbonate;                treating with cold water and optionally with                additional CO2; filtering slurry to obtain lithium                carbonate filtrate and residue comprising cobalt,                iron, other valuable metals, and non-metal                impurities; heating lithium carbonate filtrate to                lithium carbonate precipitate sedimentation and                filtering to obtain lithium carbonate precipitate                and mother liquid; and (h) treating the residue                with a flux to obtain valuable metallic                ingots.
   USE - The method is useful for recovering valuable                metals from spent lithium ion batteries used in                consumer electronics and electric vehicles.
   ADVANTAGE - Recycling of spent LIBs enhances environmental                protection and enhances a circular economy by                separating the valuable metallic constituents into                different products.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Valuable metals are recovered from spent                lithium ion batteries (LIBs) by (a) opening LIB and                removing its cover; (b) grinding remaining spent                LIB at predetermined temperature to obtain a                particulate spent lithium ion batteries material                including particles having a predetermined grain                size; (c) separating from particulate spent lithium                ion batteries material, any plastic and Teflon                (RTM: PTFE) matrix; (d) treating separated                particulate spent lithium ion batteries material                with carbon dioxide/carbon monoxide/water vapor                (CO2/CO/H2O) mixture, methane/air (CH4/air), and/or                solid carbon at predetermined temperature for                carbonization of lithium to obtain particulate                spent lithium ion batteries material comprising                lithium carbonate; (e) treating particulate spent                lithium ion batteries material comprising lithium                carbonate with cold water and optionally with                additional CO2; (f) filtering slurry obtained in                step (e) to obtain a lithium carbonate filtrate and                a residue comprising cobalt, iron, other valuable                metals, and non-metal impurities; (g) heating                lithium carbonate filtrate at predetermined                temperature to lithium carbonate precipitate                sedimentation, followed by filtration to obtain                lithium carbonate precipitate and a mother liquid;                and (h) treating residue comprising cobalt, iron,                other valuable metals, and non-metal impurities of                step (f) with a flux at predetermined temperature                to obtain valuable metallic ingots.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Properties: Particulate spent                  lithium ion batteries material obtained in step                  (b) has a grain size of 2-10 mm, preferably 2-5                  mm. Preferred Conditions: Predetermined                  temperature of grinding of remaining spent LIB is                  20-50 degrees C. Treatment step (d) is carried                  out at 100-900 degrees C. Ratio of partial                  pressure CO2/CO in the CO2/CO/H2O gas mixture is                  0.1-100. Partial pressure H2O in CO2/CO/H2O                  mixture is 0.1-50 vol.% Partial pressure CH4 in                  CH4/air mixture is 0.1-100 vol.%. Heating at                  predetermined temperature of lithium carbonate                  filtrate of step (g) is carried out at a                  temperature of between of 80-120 degrees C. Flux                  treatment step (h) is carried out at 1200-1500                  degrees C. Preferred Processes: Separation of                  plastic and Teflon (RTM: PTFE) matrix from                  particulate spent lithium ion batteries material                  is performed by air separation. Lithium carbonate                  precipitate obtained in step (g) is further dried                  to obtain a dry lithium carbonate powder.                  Preferred Compositions: Weight ratio of carbon to                  separated particulate spent lithium ion batteries                  material is 10:100-200:100, preferably                  50:100-100:100. Carbon for treating separated                  particulate spent lithium ion batteries material                  is selected from coal, coke, charcoal, and/or                  graphite. Flux for smelting other valuable metals                  comprises silica, carbon, calcium oxide, sodium                  oxide, alumina, and/or iron oxide. Metallic                  ingots of step (h) comprise non-ferrous metals                  selected from cobalt, nickel, manganese, copper,                  aluminum, titanium, tin, lead, zinc, sodium,                  and/or calcium, or iron ingots.
DC M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)
MC M25-A01; M25-G04; M25-G11; A04-E08; A11-C03; A12-E06; A12-T04C; X16-B01F1; X16-M

PT P
PN CN113416700-A
TI Expansion of factor secreting natural killer (NK) cells used for anti-inflammatory, by separating mononuclear cells from peripheral blood, sorting mononuclear cells, culturing cluster of differentiation (CD)56 strongly positive cells, and supplementing with serum-free medium to continue culturing.
AU LIU C
   ZHANG Y
   LV X
   LIU Y
AE TIANQING STEM CELL CO LTD (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021B2434A
AB    NOVELTY - Method for expansion of factor secreting natural killer (NK) cells, involves (i) separating mononuclear cells from cord blood or peripheral blood, (ii) using a sorting flow cytometer to sort mononuclear cells at a screening range of 5% of the uppermost part of the Q1 area to obtain cluster of differentiation (CD)56 strongly positive cells, (iii) culturing the CD56 strongly positive cells obtained in step (ii) at 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide saturated humidity by inoculating CD56 strongly positive cells in a T25 flask containing serum-free medium (A) for culture, collecting the cells on third day of culture, supplementing with serum-free medium (A) to continue culturing, on 5th day of culture, transferring the cells to a T75 culture flask and supplementing with serum-free medium (A), supplementing the fluid every 2 days, and culturing to 15th day to complete the expansion of CD56brightNK cells.
   USE - The method is used for expansion of factor secreting NK cells for preparing anti-inflammatory medicine (claimed) for anti-inflammatory.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for expansion of factor secreting natural killer (NK) cells, involves (i) separating mononuclear cells from cord blood or peripheral blood, (ii) using a sorting flow cytometer to sort mononuclear cells at a screening range of 5% of the uppermost part of the Q1 area to obtain cluster of differentiation (CD)56 strongly positive cells, (iii) culturing the CD56 strongly positive cells obtained in step (ii) at 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide saturated humidity by inoculating CD56 strongly positive cells in a T25 flask containing serum-free medium (A) for culture, collecting the cells on third day of culture, supplementing with serum-free medium (A) to continue culturing, on 5th day of culture, transferring the cells to a T75 culture flask and supplementing with serum-free medium (A), supplementing the fluid every 2 days, where the supplement fluid is GT-T551H3 serum-free medium containing interleukin (IL)-2, IL-15 and IL-21, and culturing to 15th day to complete the expansion of CD56brightNK cells, where the serum-free medium (A) in step (iii) is GT-T551 H3 serum-free medium containing IL-2, IL-15, IL-21 and glucose sulfate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method for separating mononuclear cells in cord blood or peripheral blood in step (i) involves taking a portion of cord blood or peripheral blood, centrifuging in a centrifuge tube, removing the upper layer of light yellow plasma, using normal saline to make up the original volume of the plasma, taking a new centrifuge tube and adding a lymphocyte separation solution, adding the diluted blood to the lymphocyte separation solution, layering the diluted blood and the lymphocyte separation solution, centrifuging to obtain a lower layer that is red blood cells and a middle white film layer that is mononuclear cells, removing part of the supernatant, pipetting the white film layer to a new centrifuge tube, adding an equal volume of normal saline, centrifuging to remove the supernatant, and washing. Preferred Conditions: In step (ii), the Q1 region is NK cells. In step (iii), the cell density of the CD56 strongly positive cells inoculated in the serum-free medium is 6x 104 cells/ml, and the cell density (0.8-1)x 106 cells/ml is supplemented every 2 days. Preferred Composition: The serum-free medium (A) in step (iii) comprises 500 IU/ml IL-2, 50 ng/ml IL-15, 100 ng/ml IL-21 and 500 ng/mL GT-T551 H3 serum-free medium with glucose sulfate. The supplemented liquid in step (iii) comprises 500 IU/ml IL-2, 50 ng/ml IL-15 and 100 ng/ml IL-21 GT-T551 H3 serum-free medium.

PN CN112725071-A
TI Extracting high-purity semen Trichosanthis oil comprises e.g. picking mature fruits, cutting, washing seeds, crushing, extracting by supercritical extraction device, filtering, discharging semen Trichosanthis waste and centrifuging.
AU NI Q
   ZHANG Y
   HONG S
   YANG L
   GAO Q
AE ZHEJIANG JIAOZHI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 202148136X
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting high-purity semen Trichosanthis oil comprises e.g. (i) picking mature fruits in batches in autumn, cutting the fruits longitudinally, placing melon flesh and seeds into a pot, and washing, (ii) taking the seeds and washing using the vibration washing device and filtering, and using the high temperature frequency conversion drying device for drying, (iii) adding the dried semen Trichosanthis into the boiling water with a temperature of 80-100 degrees C, soaking for 5-10 minutes, filtering and removing the floating skin by the filtering device, (iv) placing the processed semen Trichosanthis into the crushing device for fully crushing and adding into the supercritical extraction device for supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) extraction, and (v) placing the extracted semen Trichosanthis waste and semen Trichosanthis oil into the oil-solid separating device with filtering device.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting high-purity semen Trichosanthis oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the production efficiency, avoids the external impurity entering to cause purity reduction, and improves the semen Trichosanthis oil purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting high-purity semen Trichosanthis oil comprises (i) picking mature fruits in batches in autumn, cutting the fruits longitudinally, placing melon flesh and seeds into a pot, and washing, (ii) taking the seeds and washing using the vibration washing device and filtering, and using the high temperature frequency conversion drying device for drying, (iii) adding the dried semen Trichosanthis into the boiling water with a temperature of 80-100 degrees C, soaking for 5-10 minutes, filtering and removing the floating skin by the filtering device, (iv) placing the processed semen Trichosanthis into the crushing device for fully crushing and adding into the supercritical extraction device for supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) extraction, (v) placing the extracted semen Trichosanthis waste and semen Trichosanthis oil into the oil-solid separating device with filtering device, (vi) repeatedly circulating and filtering the semen Trichosanthis oil, classifying and discharging semen Trichosanthis waste, and storing the semen Trichosanthis waste as fertilizer, (vii) placing the extracted semen Trichosanthis oil into the high speed freezing centrifuge with the rotating speed of 14000-18000 revolutions/minute for centrifuging for 20-40 minutes to further remove the impurity, and (viii) adding into the high temperature vacuum box for 10-14 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (iii), the boiling water is heated by variable frequency heating device, and repeatedly operating at least 3 times on the filtering and removing floating skin, the boiling water temperature is 100 degrees C, and the heating time is 10 minutes. In the step (iv), the supercritical extraction device adopts the extraction pressure of 10-30 MPa, the separation pressure is 6-10 MPa, the extraction temperature is 60-80 degrees C, the separation temperature is 60-80 degrees C, and the CO2 flowrate is 30-100 l and the extraction time is 3-6 hours. In the step (v), the oil-solid separation device adopts a variable frequency pump to pump in and discharge the semen Trichosanthis waste and semen Trichosanthis oil. In the step (vi), the semen Trichosanthis waste as fertilizer is mixed with the straw and the straw and the straw rod through the mixing device to prepare the fertilizer, or the semen Trichosanthis waste and the straw, the corn stalk and the straw rod are embedded in the fermentation tank to ferment. In the step (vii), the rotating speed is 15000 revolutions/minute, and the temperature is 50 degrees C for 1 hour. In the step (viii), the temperature of the high-temperature vacuum box is 80-100 degrees C, and the high-purity melon seed oil is obtained after placing into the bottle through a filling device for storage.
DC D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).); Q76 (Drying (F26))

PT P
PN WO2021026341-A1; US2022273117-A1
TI Mosquito control net has trap chamber which is            mounted on occupant chamber, and is provided with            openings that are mounted on outer surface through            which mosquitos enter trap chamber.
AU MOUHAMADOU C S
   ROE R M
   MITCHELL R D
   APPERSON C S
   MCCORD M G
   WEST A J
AE UNIV NORTH CAROLINA STATE (UYNC-C)
   UNIV NORTH CAROLINA STATE (UYNC-C)
GA 2021150534
AB    NOVELTY - The mosquito control net has an occupant                chamber (120) and a trap chamber (130) which is                mounted on the occupant chamber. The trap chamber                comprises openings are mounted on an outer surface                (132) through which mosquitos enter the trap                chamber. The openings are configured such that each                is large enough for the mosquitos to enter the trap                chamber, but small enough to inhibit the mosquito                from exiting the trap chamber. A fabric surrounds                the occupant chamber and separating the occupant                chamber from the trap chamber is impermeable to the                mosquitos.
   USE - Mosquito control net.
   ADVANTAGE - The mosquitos are prevented from biting people                while they sleep, thus, reducing the spread of                mosquito-borne illnesses. The construction and                configuration of the net is advantageous in                reducing material costs for construction and                allowing for the head space between the sleeper and                outer surface of the net, so as to effectively                increase the odorant concentration on the top of                the net, as well as adding practical functionality                to the bed net design. The sides and the top or                just the top of the trap chamber is constructed of                a material that does not transmit carbon dioxide,                forcing the carbon dioxide in the exhaled plume to                pass through only the available openings and to                advantageously increase the attraction rate of                mosquitos entering the trap chamber. The mosquitos                are not able to reach the subject in the occupant                chamber, and they are generally prevented from                leaving the trap chamber due to the small diameter                of the mouth of the funnel on the interior of the                trap chamber. The trap chamber is made that have a                color is darker than the color of another region to                increase the attraction of mosquitos and reduces                the visibility of dead mosquitos. The time and                effort needed to wash the nets to effectuate the                removal of mosquito carcasses is reduced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for preventing mosquito bites for                a subject;
   (2) a method of making a mosquito control net;                and
   (3) a kit of portions for converting a                mosquito control net into a trapping mosquito                control net.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic isometric view                of a net for trapping mosquitos to prevent an                occupant within the net from being bitten by                mosquitos.
   Net (100)
   Outer shell (110)
   Occupant chamber (120)
   Trap chamber (130)
   Outer surface (132)

PN CN111686022-A
TI Production of hair care essential oil for nourishing scalp, by taking e.g. Cocos nucifera, Ligusticum striatum, anthocyanin, astaxanthin and vitamin, stirring and mixing, then adding silica hollow microspheres and hypromellose, and stirring.
AU HU J
   CHEN J
   CHEN K
   JIANG J
   HE X
AE SANJIN GROUP HUNAN SANJIN PHARM CO LTD (SANJ-Non-standard)
GA 202095450S
AB    NOVELTY - Production of hair care essential oil involves: (S1) using supercritical carbon dioxide method to extract Cocos nucifera, Pelargonium hortorum, lavender, rosemary, Indian sandalwood, pine, grapefruit, mint and ginger to obtain an extract composition (A); (S2) taking Ligusticum striatum, Lycium barbarum, Salvia miltiorrhiza, Polygonum multiflorum, Astragalus, Angelica sinensis and black sesame, adding a proper amount of water, extracting and separating to obtain an oil phase liquid composition (B) by distillation; (S3) stirring and mixing anthocyanin, astaxanthin and vitamin for 5-10 minutes to obtain a composition (C); (S4) mixing and stirring the compositions (A)-(C) and silica hollow microspheres, again stirring under ultrasonic conditions, then adding hypromellose and stirring.
   USE - The method is useful for producing hair care essential oil, which is useful for nourishing scalp.
   ADVANTAGE - The hair care essential oil has reasonable composition, and long-lasting effect, and is safe.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of hair care essential oil involves: (S1) using supercritical carbon dioxide method to extract 40-50 pts. wt. Cocos nucifera, 20-30 pts. wt. Pelargonium hortorum, 30-40 pts. wt. lavender, 20-28 pts. wt. rosemary, 15-25 pts. wt. Indian sandalwood, 10-18 pts. wt. pine, 12-20 pts. wt. grapefruit, 8-10 pts. wt. mint and/or 16-24 pts. wt. ginger to obtain an extract composition (A); (S2) taking 20-30 pts. wt. Ligusticum striatum, 18-24 pts. wt. Lycium barbarum, 24-30 pts. wt. Salvia miltiorrhiza, 15-25 pts. wt. Polygonum multiflorum, 10-20 pts. wt. Astragalus, 12-18 pts. wt. Angelica sinensis and 10-20 pts. wt. black sesame, adding a proper amount of water, extracting and separating to obtain an oil phase liquid composition (B) by distillation; (S3) stirring and mixing 10-20 pts. wt. anthocyanin, 8-12 pts. wt. astaxanthin and 12-18 pts. wt. vitamin for 5-10 minutes to obtain a composition (C); (S4) mixing and stirring the compositions (A)-(C) and silica hollow microspheres for 10-20 minutes, stirring for 5-10 minutes under ultrasonic conditions, then adding hypromellose, and stirring for 10-20 minutes. The mass ratio of composition (A), oil phase liquid composition (B), composition (C), silica hollow microspheres and hypromellose is 20:10:(0.01-0.05):(2-10):(0.05-0.3).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The particle size of the hollow silica microspheres is 20-100 mu m.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The vitamin is vitamin A1, vitamin B1 and/or vitamin B2.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: In step (1), the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction method involves: (A) drying C.nucifera, P.hortorum, lavender, rosemary, Indian sandalwood, pine, grapefruit, mint and ginger, crushing to 100-200 mesh, then putting the mixture into an extraction kettle, adjusting the temperature in the extraction kettle to 40-60 degrees C and the pressure in the extraction kettle to 25-35 MPa, introducing supercritical state reached carbon dioxide into the extraction kettle, where the flow rate of carbon dioxide is 15-22 l/hour, and maintaining the constant temperature and pressure for 1-3 hours; (B) adding the fluid in the extraction kettle to a separator for separation treatment to get an extracted oil and gaseous carbon dioxide, discharging the gaseous carbon dioxide to obtain the extracted oil to get the composition (A) extract, and controlling the temperature and pressure at 10-15 degrees C and 6-8 MPa during separation. The preparation method of composition (B) in step (2) involves: (i) crushing L.striatum, L.barbarum, S.miltiorrhiza, P.multiflorum, Astragalus, A.sinensis and black sesame, and evenly mixing the crushed components; and (ii) selecting a suitable extraction pot according to the mass of the material, putting the above mixture into the extraction pot, adding 5-10 times water according to the mass of the material, heating to boil, distilling for 2-3 hours, condensing water vapor by a condenser, separating the aqueous phase and discarding by a separator to retain the oil phase to obtain the oil phase liquid composition (B).
   ACTIVITY - Endocrine-Gen.

PN WO2020142191-A1
TI Nanobiocomposite nutrient carrier used e.g. as            water remediation material, comprises first phase            containing water-soluble polymer component having            preselected iron nutrient and preselected nutrient            nourishes predetermined aquatic organism.
AU BUTZLOFF P R
AE BUTZLOFF P R (BUTZ-Individual)
GA 202061921L
AB    NOVELTY - Nanobiocomposite nutrient carrier comprises a                first phase containing a water-soluble polymer                component having at least one preselected iron                nutrient, where the preselected nutrient nourishes                at least one predetermined aquatic organism, where                the water-soluble polymer component includes a                hydrogen-bonded interpenetrating polymer network                entrapping the preselected iron nutrient, where a                particle size of the preselected iron nutrient is                less than or equal to 100 nm, and the nutrient                carrier is buoyant having a density of less than 1                g/cm3.
   USE - The nanobiocomposite nutrient carrier is                useful: as a water remediation material (claimed);                and for treating oceanic near-surface waters to                remediate waste products, and capturing carbon                dioxide from the atmosphere and the ocean.
   ADVANTAGE - The nanobiocomposite nutrient carrier: can                nourish aquatic organisms, remediate oil and                plastics, sequester carbon dioxide, increases                oxygen in an aquatic body, and provides reflective                shading hence leads to cool the aquatic body; and                act as a fertilizer, a thermal barrier material, or                a light reflective material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) a water remediation material, comprising a                nutrient constituent having particles less than or                equal to 100 nm and a density less than or equal to                1 g/cm3 and having a first carbohydrate polymer                network including a bio-polysaccharide, at least                one mineral nutrient coupled to the first                carbohydrate polymer network, a second carbohydrate                polymer network, a crosslinking agent bonding the                first carbohydrate polymer network to the second                carbohydrate polymer network, and a reflective                mineral constituent intermixed with the first and                second carbohydrate polymer networks, where the                biopolysaccharide of the first carbohydrate polymer                network is one of chitin, chitosan, or alginate,                where the second carbohydrate polymer network                includes one of latex rubber, polyvinyl alcohol,                ethyl vinyl alcohol, a polylactide, a                polyhydroxyalkanoate, polyvinyl acetate, or a                cellulose or lignocellulosic composite, where the                reflective mineral includes titanium dioxide, the                crosslinking agent includes a reactive nitrogen                source, and the mineral nutrient includes one of                ferrous oxide, ferric oxide, iron sulfate, or                magnesium sulfate; and
   (2) preparing the nanobiocomposite nutrient                carrier, comprising providing a nutrient                constituent having particle size of less than or                equal to 100 nm and density of less than 1 g/cm3,                providing a soluble silicate constituent having a                particle size of 5-10 microns, and density less                than 1 g/cm3, and hydrogen bonding the nutrient                constituent to the soluble silicate                constituent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The nanobiocomposite                  nutrient carrier further comprises: a second                  dispersed phase at least partially surrounding                  the first phase, where the particle size of the                  second dispersed phase is less than or equal to                  100 nm, and the second dispersed phase includes                  gas inclusion voids to allow the density of the                  second dispersed phase to be less than 1 g/cm3;                  reflective particles, where the reflective                  particles are crystals of titanium dioxide of                  size less than or equal to 0.15 mm; the second                  dispersed phase is soluble silicate-rich                  including one of a soluble silicate salt, fumed                  silica, or silicic acid; and a reflective                  titanium dioxide component including a                  photoactive anatase crystal form, where the                  reflective titanium dioxide component size is                  100-250 mesh (i.e. 0.149-0.053 mm diameter). The                  nanocomposite dispersion morphology of the first                  phase and the second dispersed phase is of a                  shell-and-core type, and either the first or the                  second dispersed phase serve as a shell or a                  core. The biopolysaccharide includes chitin,                  chitosan, alginate, urea, cellulosic fibers, or                  polyvinyl alcohol.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The preselected iron                  nutrient further comprises an iron-rich, nutrient                  phase including one of ferrous oxide, ferric                  oxide, and/or iron sulfate. The iron-rich                  nutrient first phase further comprises a                  preselected water-soluble biopolymer                  hydrogen-bonded to the iron-rich first phase. The                  second dispersion phase comprises a crosslinked                  silica gel. The outer silicate-rich second                  dispersion phase comprises a crosslinked silica                  gel. The iron-rich nutrient first phase and                  silicate-rich second phase are hydrogen bonded                  together as an interpenetrating polymer network                  (IPN). The water remediation material further                  comprises a soluble silicate constituent having a                  particle size of less than or equal to 100 nm,                  and the soluble silicate constituent containing a                  crosslinked soluble silicate includes sodium                  silicate, potassium silicate, silicic acid, or                  fumed silica. The reactive nitrogen source                  includes at least one of urea, saltwater, or                  freshwater. The water remediation material                  further comprises a mixture of the nutrient                  constituent and the soluble silicate constituent,                  where the soluble silicate constituent at least                  partially covers the nutrient constituent, the                  mixture is applied to an aquatic body and a                  predetermined aquatic organism is nourished. The                  reflective mineral constituent includes a                  photoactive anatase crystal form of titanium                  dioxide, having a size of 100-250 mesh (i.e.                  0.149-0.053 mm diameter). The soluble silicate                  constituent further comprises crosslinked                  silicates hydrogen-bonded to the nutrient                  constituent. The nutrient constituent further                  comprises a soluble sulfate salt. The preselected                  nutrient constituent comprises: a soluble iron                  material including ferrous oxide, ferric oxide,                  and/or iron sulfate, and the preselected soluble                  silicate constituent includes water-soluble                  silicates; and a soluble sulfate, and/or an                  anatase crystal form of titanium dioxide. The                  silicate constituent comprises a soluble silicate                  constituent having a density less than 1 g/cm3,                  where the silicate constituent contains a                  crosslinked soluble silicate including sodium                  silicate, potassium silicate, silicic acid, or                  fumed silica, and the soluble silicate                  constituent is applied to an aquatic body, where                  a predetermined aquatic organism is nourished.                  Preferred Method: The step of providing a                  nutrient constituent further comprises providing                  a preselected nutrient constituent containing a                  preselected bioplastics, providing a preselected                  soluble silicate constituent, and combining the                  preselected nutrient constituent with the                  preselected soluble silicate constituent in                  water. The method further comprises providing a                  silicate constituent containing a preselected                  soluble silicate including fumed silica, and/or                  acidified sodium silicate, combining the                  preselected nutrient constituent with the                  preselected soluble silicate constituent for a                  predetermined period forming an interpenetrating                  network, and achieving gelation after the                  predetermined period.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN KR2124354-B1
TI Crash box for vehicle, has first wing flange formed on first wing portion, which is in contact with both sides of upper and lower sides of upper surface of mounting plate to vertically support first panel.
AU LEE D
   LEE S R
   BAE J
AE SUNG WOO HITECH CO LTD (SUWO-C)
GA 202055449C
AB    NOVELTY - The crash box (100) has a mounting plate (20) in which first and second slots of a predetermined length are formed along the vertical direction in parallel to both sides with respect to the center. A first panel (11) and a second panel (12) are press-molded in a cross-sectional shape and constituted by a central portion and a bent portion on both sides. A box portion (10) is coupled to each of the first slot (21) and the second slot of the mounting plate. The first panel includes a first central flange (113) at one end of the first central portion (111), and a first wing portion (115). The second panel includes a second central flange (123) at one end of the second central portion (121) and a second wing portion (125) at both sides of the second central portion. The first wing flange formed on the first wing portion is in contact with both sides of the upper and lower sides of the upper surface of the mounting plate to vertically support the first panel.
   USE - Crash box for vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The thermal deformation by conventional carbon dioxide welding is avoided by eliminating the carbon dioxide welding process at the connection between the box and the mounting plate. The fastening force can be maintained even when the welding is separated since the box part is fitted into the mounting plate and spot-welded in a structure interlocked with each other. The efficient offsetting of stress is possible by applying an inter-reverse force dispersion structure that effectively complements the separation between components. The force can be distributed to complement each other to maintain a strong clamping force even if the force is applied in any direction during towing or shipping.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of assembling a crash box for a vehicle.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view showing the configuration of a crash box for a vehicle.
   Box portion (10)
   First panel (11)
   Second panel (12)
   Mounting plate (20)
   Crash box (100)
   First central portion (111)
   First central flange (113)
   First wing portion (115)
   Second central portion (121)
   Second central flange (123)
   Second wing portion (125)

PN CN110903168-A
TI Subcritical extraction of solanesol from waste tobacco leaves by crushing waste tobacco leaves into coarse powder, and concentrating, separating and purifying using ethanol as solvent and ultrasonic countercurrent method.
AU XIE K
   SUN J
   HOU W
AE HENAN YUANCUI BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HENA-Non-standard)
GA 202026259B
AB    NOVELTY - Subcritical extraction of solanesol from waste tobacco leaves includes crushing waste tobacco leaves into coarse powder, concentrating, separating and purifying using 50% ethanol as solvent at 40-45 degrees C using ultrasonic countercurrent method, obtaining chlorogenic acid, adding the solid tobacco leaf residue with 5-10 times of 5% sodium hydroxide (NaOH) alkaline aqueous solution at 55-60 degrees C, conducting saponification hydrolysis by ultrasonic oscillation for 30-60 minutes, plate-and-frame filter pressing, decompressing and distilling the liquid, extracting to obtain high purity nicotine, directly recycling and reusing the distilled residual leaves, drying the extract, adding propane as solvent, extracting at 40-50 degrees C for 60-90 minutes for 3-6 times, adding 20-30 times methanol into the crude extract, introducing protective carbon dioxide (CO2) gas, high-speed stirring and dissolving, and ultrafiltering the extracting solution.
   USE - The method is for subcritical extraction of solanesol from waste tobacco leaves.
   ADVANTAGE - The method achieves hierarchical and gradual extraction of tobacco target components, has stability, high efficiency, low cost, low solvent consumption, and good environment protection, realizes circulation utilization of solvent in each process section, discharges no waste liquid, and is green and suitable for industrialized production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Subcritical extraction of solanesol from waste tobacco leaves comprises crushing waste tobacco leaves into coarse powder, concentrating, separating and purifying using 50% ethanol as solvent at 40-45 degrees C using ultrasonic countercurrent method, obtaining chlorogenic acid, adding the solid tobacco leaf residue with 5-10 times of 5% sodium hydroxide (NaOH) alkaline aqueous solution at 55-60 degrees C, conducting saponification hydrolysis by ultrasonic oscillation for 30-60 minutes, plate-and-frame filter pressing, decompressing and distilling the liquid, extracting to obtain high purity nicotine, directly recycling and reusing the distilled residual leaves, drying the extract, adding propane as solvent, extracting at 40-50 degrees C for 60-90 minutes for 3-6 times, adding 20-30 times methanol into the crude extract, introducing protective carbon dioxide (CO2) gas, high-speed stirring and dissolving, ultrafiltering the extracting solution by ultrafiltration filter having molecular weight cutoff of 5000, nano-filtering and concentrating to 8-10 times volume, freezing and crystallizing at - 15 degrees C, filtering,obtaining solanesol, adding 8-10 times methanol, introducing protective CO2 gas, high-speed stirring and dissolving, filtering, freezing and crystallizing at - 15 degrees C, filtering to obtain solanesol, and vacuum drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The subcritical extraction uses butane or propane as solvent. The solvent methanol can also be replaced with ethanol. The extracting method comprises dissolving solanesol and using carbon dioxide (CO2) as protecting agent for preventing oxidation of solanesol.

PN CN110724375-A
TI Thermoplastic polyurethane (TPU)/ethylene-vinyl acetate (EVA) supercritical foamed composite material comprises thermoplastic polyurethane elastomer, ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer and maleic anhydride graft modifier.
AU DING Y
   DING X
AE FUJIAN ANDAFU NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (FUJI-Non-standard)
GA 202010543P
AB    NOVELTY - Thermoplastic polyurethane (TPU)/ethylene-vinyl acetate (EVA) supercritical foamed composite material comprises 300-360 pts. wt. thermoplastic polyurethane elastomer, 66-73 pts. wt. ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer, 25-33 pts. wt. maleic anhydride graft modifier, 9-14 pts. wt. peroxide, and 1.2-1.6 pts. wt. nucleating agent. The thermoplastic polyurethane elastomer is a mixture of a polyester polyurethane elastomer and a polyether polyurethane elastomer. The ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer is a mixture of an ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer having a vinyl acetate content of 25-30 wt.% and an ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer having a vinyl acetate content of 35-40 wt.% in a mass ratio of 1:1. The maleic anhydride graft modifier is a polyethylene grafted silicon-containing group prepared by reacting an epoxy-containing polysiloxane with maleic anhydride grafted polyethylene in a mass ratio of 1:(2-3).
   USE - Thermoplastic polyurethane (TPU)/ethylene-vinyl acetate (EVA) supercritical foamed composite material.
   ADVANTAGE - The thermoplastic polyurethane (TPU)/ethylene-vinyl acetate (EVA) supercritical foamed composite material has good foaming effect and high foaming efficiency, cells are uniform, small and dense, with excellent mechanical properties and use properties such as light weight and high strength, high tensile strength, large elongation at break, and good mechanical properties, high tear resistance, good tear resistance and low density.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing a thermoplastic polyurethane (TPU)/ethylene-vinyl acetate (EVA) supercritical foamed composite material, which involves:
   (A) weighing thermoplastic polyurethane elastomer, ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer, maleic anhydride graft modifier, peroxide, and nucleating agent separately by weight;
   (B) mixing the thermoplastic polyurethane elastomer, ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer, and maleic anhydride graft modifier, and sending to the mixer, and the mixer is first mixed at a temperature of 105-110 degrees C and blending for 10-14 minutes, and then adding peroxide and nucleating agent to perform mixing and mixing at a temperature of 125-135 degrees C until the components are uniformly mixed to obtain a dense blend;
   (C) pressing tablet into a sheet by a tablet press and cut;
   (D) putting the sheet produced into an oven, preheating to the foaming temperature, and then placing the preheated foamed sheet into a high-temperature mold; and
   (E) passing in carbon dioxide gas, quickly releasing the carbon dioxide gas in the mold, so that sheet is rapidly foamed to obtain the TPU/EVA supercritical foamed composite material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The peroxide is zinc peroxide. The nucleating agent is mixture of nano zinc oxide and nano aluminum sesquioxide. Preferred Compositions: The TPU/EVA supercritical foamed composite material is made of raw materials includes 330 pts. wt. thermoplastic polyurethane elastomer, 69 pts. wt. ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer, 29 pts. wt. maleic anhydride graft modifier, 11.5 pts. wt. peroxide, and 1.4 pts. wt. nucleating agent. The mass ratio of the polyester-type polyurethane elastomer to the polyether-type polyurethane elastomer in the mixture of the polyester-type polyurethane elastomer and the polyether-type polyurethane elastomer is 1:(2.5-3.2). The ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer is a mixture of an ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer having a vinyl acetate content of 27.5 wt.% and an ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer having a VA content of 37.5 wt.% in a mass ratio of 1:1. The maleic anhydride graft modifier is a polyethylene grafted silicon-containing group prepared by reacting an epoxy-containing polysiloxane with maleic anhydride grafted polyethylene in a mass ratio of 1:2.55. The nucleating agent is a mixture of nano zinc oxide and nano aluminum oxide. The mass ratio of nano zinc oxide and nano aluminum oxide in the mixture of nano zinc oxide and nano aluminum oxide is 1:(0.75-0.80).

PT P
PN CN110540481-A
TI Reducing nitrogen loss during perishable composting process comprises e.g. adding fenton reagent to the original sludge, mixing iron-based biochar, adding fermentation materials to compost bin and deodorizing.
AU YANG J
   XIAO K
   WANG N
   CHEN H
   GUAN W
   XUE Y
   SUN M
   LIANG S
   WANG X
   HU J
   HOU H
   LIU B
AE ANHUI GUOZHEN ENVIRONMENTAL SANITATION (ANHU-Non-standard)
   UNIV HUAZHONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHZ-C)
GA 2019A5038K
AB    NOVELTY - Reducing nitrogen loss during perishable composting process comprises (i) adding fenton reagent to the original sludge for deep dehydration conditioning, stirring, de-watering the plate and frame under a dehydration pressure of 0.8 MPa and a diaphragm pressure of 1.2 MPa to make mud cakes, heating 80-mesh sieve in a tubular or horizontal pyrolysis experimental furnace at 800 degrees C for 2 hours to obtain iron-based biochar; (ii) mixing iron-based biochar with perishable waste and chicken manure to form a mixture, adjusting the moisture content at 40-50% to obtain fermentation raw materials; (iii) adding the fermentation materials to the compost bin, using a stirring shaft during the composting process, passing carbon-dioxide and water generated during the reaction to the deodorization system through the screen on the side of the reaction chamber inside the compost box, fully deodorizing, completing the exhaust holes at the rear of the box after being discharged and the composting process.
   USE - The method is useful for reducing nitrogen loss during perishable composting process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces soil bulk density; increases porosity for increasing the water holding capacity of the soil and soil pH; improves field climate and regulate field water holding capacity and structure of the soil; stabilizes soil triphase ratio .
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Reducing nitrogen loss during perishable composting process comprises (i) adding fenton reagent to the original sludge for deep dehydration conditioning, stirring, de-watering the plate and frame under a dehydration pressure of 0.8 MPa and a diaphragm pressure of 1.2 MPa to make mud cakes, heating 80-mesh sieve in a tubular or horizontal pyrolysis experimental furnace at 800 degrees C for 2 hours to obtain iron-based biochar; (ii) mixing the iron-based biochar with perishable waste and chicken manure to form a mixture, adjusting the moisture content at 40-50% to obtain fermentation raw materials; (iii) adding the fermentation materials to the compost bin, using a stirring shaft during the composting process, passing carbon-dioxide and water generated during the reaction to the deodorization system through the screen on the side of the reaction chamber inside the compost box, fully deodorizing, completing the exhaust holes at the rear of the box after being discharged and the composting process, taking the compost material out at one time from the discharge opening above the compost box.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the Fenton reagent is prepared by 110 mg iron(II) g-1 volatile solid (VS) and 88 mg hydrogen peroxide g-1 VS. The Fenton reagent can be replaced with ferric chloride, fenton-type reagent, potassium persulfate complex salt-ferrous iron / zero-valent iron or persulfate-ferrous iron / zero-valent iron. In the step (ii), the dry weight ratio of chicken manure, perishable waste, and biochar is 3 mg:6-10 mg:1 mg. The perishable waste in the mixture includes kitchen waste and vegetable and fruit waste, bean dregs, carrion, meat and bones, egg shells, and internal organs of livestock and poultry produced by farmers markets and agricultural product wholesale markets, and is not limited to these types of perishable water. The particle size of the iron-based biochar is 0.1-5 mm and the pyrolysis temperature is 300-800 degrees C. In the step (iii), the mixture needs to be inoculated with bacteria, where the inoculated bacteria is chicken manure, pig manure or cow manure or specific composted bacteria before composting the mixture. In the step (iv), the cover of the compost box is provided with a through hole, the moisture content during the composting process is controlled at 40-60%, the pile must be turned once a day for the first two weeks during composting and duration of composting is 1-2 months after every week.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PN CN110386728-A; CN110386728-B
TI Integrated treatment of high-salinity and            high-chemical oxygen demand industrial wastewater            involves introducing wastewater into buffer tank for            sedimentation, and performing            evaporation-crystallization, oxidation and microbial            degradation.
AU ZHOU C
   JIA J
   SUN J
AE YIXING INT ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION ZONE (YIXI-Non-standard)
GA 201993505P
AB    NOVELTY - Integrated treatment of high-salinity and                high-chemical oxygen demand (COD) industrial                wastewater involves wastewater into a wastewater                buffer tank for sedimentation and sand removal, and                separating large particles of sand and other                substances, introducing into an evaporator,                performing evaporation-cooling crystallization to                obtain condensed water, concentrating, cooling the                concentrated solution, crystallizing and separating                soluble salts, and filtering to obtain crystalline                salt compounds and crystallization mother liquor,                adding the crystallization mother liquor to a water                distribution tank, performing wet oxidation,                oxidizing and decomposing macromolecular organic                substances by strong oxidants, oxidizing the                obtained small molecules to carbon dioxide and                water which greatly reduces the COD value,                introducing into a tank for microbial degradation,                filtering through a biological carbon filter bed,                and introducing into a reverse osmosis membrane                treatment device.
   USE - Integrated treatment of high-salinity and                high-chemical oxygen demand industrial                wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical and                energy-efficient integrated treatment of                high-salinity and high-chemical oxygen demand                industrial wastewater.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Integrated treatment of high-salinity and                high-chemical oxygen demand (COD) industrial                wastewater by tubular radical oxidation involves                introducing (i) high-salinity and high-COD                industrial wastewater into a wastewater buffer tank                for sedimentation and sand removal, and separating                large particles of sand and other substances which                causes abrasion of pumps, blockage of pipe                networks, interference, and damage to biochemical                treatment process, introducing (ii) the wastewater                into an evaporator by a sewage pump, performing                evaporation-cooling crystallization to obtain                condensed water, concentrating by evaporation to                obtain a concentrated solution, cooling the                concentrated solution, crystallizing and separating                soluble salts, and filtering to obtain crystalline                salt compounds and crystallization mother liquor,                adding (iii) the crystallization mother liquor to a                water distribution tank, and using the condensed                water in the evaporator to adjust the salinity in                the water distribution tank to 10-15 wt.%,                introducing the wastewater into a wet oxidation                reactor, performing wet oxidation at a certain                temperature, oxidizing and decomposing                macromolecular organic substances by strong                oxidants, oxidizing the obtained small molecules to                carbon dioxide and water which greatly reduces the                COD and biochemical oxygen demand (BOD)/COD value,                discharging (iv) the wastewater from the wet                oxidation reactor and directly introducing into a                microbial degradation tank and performing microbial                degradation, filtering (v) the degradation solution                through a biological carbon filter bed, and                introducing into a reverse osmosis membrane                treatment device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Component: The wet oxidation                  reactor is a free radical generator, and the free                  radical generator loaded with composite catalyst.                  The reverse osmosis membrane treatment device                  comprises a reverse osmosis membrane comprising a                  cellulose acetate, a polyamide and a polyimide,                  and operating pressure of the reverse osmosis                  membrane treatment device is preferably 1.5-2.5                  MPa.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The evaporation                  amount of wastewater in the process (ii) is                  15-35% of the total wastewater. The composite                  catalyst is a lanthanum-manganese-cobalt oxide                  perovskite-type catalyst of formula: LaMnxCo1-xO3                  (I).x = 0.01-0.2.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).)

PN CN110252408-A
TI Metal organic framework-808 zirconium assembled nanometal catalyst used e.g. for N-formylation catalytic reaction of carbon dioxide has coordinatively unsaturated sites metal organic framework-808(zirconium) material and active component.
AU JI S
   MU J
   RAN Z
   GAO M
   LIU J
AE UNIV BEIJING CHEM TECHNOLOGY (UYBC-C)
GA 201991785M
AB    NOVELTY - Metal organic framework (MOF)-808 zirconium (Zr) assembled nanometal catalyst has coordinatively unsaturated sites (CUS) MOF-808(Zr) material and catalytically active component assembled in the MOF-808(Zr) under the action of ultrasonic wave. The catalytically active component is selected from nano-gold (Au), ruthenium (Ru), palladium (Pd), platinum (Pt), Au-Ru, Au-Pd, Au-Pt,Ru-Pd Ru-Pt, or Pd-Pt catalyst active component.
   USE - The MOF-808 Zr assembled nanometal catalyst is used for N-formylation and N-methylation catalytic reaction of CO2 with amine/H2 to obtain e.g. N-formamide, N-methylamine, or N,N-dimethylamine (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has good selectivity in CO2 N-formylation and N-methylation catalytic reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) preparation of the catalyst comprising preparing MOF-808(Zr) material by weighing zirconium oxychloride, dissolving in mixed solution of deionized water and ethanol to prepare zirconium oxychloride solution, weighing trimesic acid (H3BTC), dissolving in mixed solution of N,N-dimethylformamide (DMF) and ethanol to prepare H3BTC solution, dropwise adding zirconium oxychloride solution and H3BTC solution into a reactor, fully stirring, where the molar ratio of metal ions of zirconium oxychloride and H3BTC is 1:1, ultrasonically processing at 150-200 W for 40-60 minutes, crystallizing at room temperature for 48 hours, centrifugally separating, washing with deionized water and ethanol, drying in vacuum at 150 degrees C for 12 hours, and obtaining powdered MOF-808(Zr) material, and preparation of MOF-808(Zr) assembled nanometal catalyst by weighing the MOF-808(Zr)material, dispersing in ethanol to prepare MOF-808(Zr) dispersion as liquid A, weighing precursor chloride of catalytically active component metal in a mixture of ethanol-water, preparing metal precursor solution, under stirring condition, adding the metal precursor solution into liquid A, fully stirring, ultrasonically processing at 50-100 W for 50-60 minutes, during which metal ion can be gently reduced by ethanol, finishing the process of assembling the nanometal in MOF-808(Zr), filtering, washing with deionized water and ethanol and vacuum drying at 150 degrees C; and
   (2) use of the catalyst for N-formylation and N-methylation catalytic reaction of carbon dioxide (CO2) with amine/hydrogen (H2).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The concentration of zirconium oxychloride solution is 10.7-14.3 mass%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Component: The content of Au is 2.95-4.53 wt.%. The content of Ru is 1.92-4.65 wt.%. The content of Pd is 2.98-4.89 wt.%. The content of Pt is 2.73-4.88 wt.%. The content of metal catalytically active component is not less than 4 wt.%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The mass ratio of deionized water and ethanol is 1:1. The mass ratio of DMF and ethanol is 1:1. The concentration of H3BTC solution is 12.6-16.8 mass%. The concentration of MOF-808(Zr) dispersion liquid is 18.6-19.1 mass%. The metal precursor solution is prepared by weighing one or two of chloroauric acid, ruthenium chloride, palladium chloride, and chloroplatinic acid, and dissolving in ethanol and water mixed solution. The method comprises weighing chloroauric acid, dissolving in mixed solution of ethanol and water to prepare metal precursor solution having a concentration of 2-5 mass% as liquid B, weighing ruthenium chloride, palladium chloride, and chloroplatinic acid, dissolving in ethanol-water mixed solution to prepare metal precursor solution having a concentration of 1-5 mass% as liquid C, under stirring condition, dropwise adding liquid B and/or liquid C into liquid A, and fully stirring. The catalytic reaction is carried out in reactor kettle by adding liquid amine and activated MOF-808(Zr) assembled nanometal solid catalyst, introducing CO2 and H2 mixed gas, and reacting at 2.5-3.5 MPa and 80-210 degrees C. In N-formylation reaction, the volume ratio of CO2 and H2 is 1:1. In N-methylation reaction, the volume ratio of CO2 and H2 is 1:3.
DC E16 (Aliphatics - containing N and/or halogen.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN110251634-A
TI Use of white cardamom essential oil in preparing medicine for treating or preventing brain function and disorder e.g. anxiety, depression, paralysis, bipolar disorder and neurodegenerative diseases and improving brain function and disorders.
AU XU M
   HAN J
   CHEN S
   CAO H
   LUO D
   ZHENG S
   LI Y
   GU P
   SONG H
AE DONGGUAN HUAIPU PLANT ESSENTIAL OIL CO (DONG-Non-standard)
GA 201991778D
AB    NOVELTY - Use of white cardamom essential oil in preparing medicine for treating or preventing brain function and disorder and improving brain function or its disorder, is claimed.
   USE - The cardamom essential oil is useful for preparing medicine for treating and preventing brain function and disorder e.g. anxiety, depression, insomnia, epilepsy, mania, schizophrenia, paralysis, bipolar disorder, stroke, brain trauma, spinal injury, and neurodegenerative diseases e.g. Alzheimer's disease, Parkinson's disease, Huntington's disease and amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (Claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The cardamom essential oil is capable of improving activity of brain function and disorders of the experimental animal, regulating neurotransmitters to synergistically act on multiple targets, has good pharmacokinetics, has parameters that easy to pass through the blood-brain barrier and thus have the characteristics of toxicological safety, metabolic stability, longer half-life and less side effects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing white cardamom essential oil comprising (a) taking dried white cardamom root, stalking for 1-2 days and pulverizing to 30-60 mesh coarse particles, (b) immersing the coarse particles obtained in step (a) in hydrochloric acid or sulfuric acid aqueous solution (7-9 times) having pH of 5.0-6.0 and leaching overnight to obtain a leaching mixture, (c) adding cellulase and pectinase to leaching mixture obtained in step (b) in a ratio of 0.5-1:0.1-0.5:100, carrying out enzymatic hydrolysis at 35-37 degrees C for 2-3 hours, (c) extracting by (i) placing the mixed solution obtained in step (c) in a steam distillation extraction apparatus, refluxing at 100-120 degrees C for 5-7 hours, collecting distillate, cooling to room temperature, separating in a water-oil separator, drying the oil layer and separating with anhydrous sodium sulfate, (ii) placing the mixed solution after hydrolysis in step (c) in a carbon dioxide supercritical extraction apparatus at an extraction temperature of 45-48 degrees C, at a pressure of 13.70-17.80 MPa for 50-100 minutes, where the flow rate of carbon dioxide is 25-35 kg/hour, collecting the essential oil and refining in a molecular distillation apparatus at 40-45 degrees C, at an injection speed of 480-530ml/hour, evaporating at 110-115 degrees C, carrying out two-stage evaporation at 60-65 degrees C, collecting two-stage distillation fraction which is the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: The white cardamom essential oil is analyzed by gas chromatography-mass spectrometry, and the amount of components more than 60 wt.% eucalyptus oil, more than 7 wt.% beta -pinene, more than 5 wt.% alpha -terpineol, and more than 2 wt.% alpha -pinene. Preferred Components: The white cardamom is a ginger bean, white cardamom or Javanese white cardamom. Preferred Components: The medicine comprises oral and non-oral administration preparation, where the oral preparation includes tablet, pill, capsule, granule, microcapsule tablet, suspension, dropping pill and oral liquid. The non-oral administration preparation includes inhalant, injection, aerosol, suppository and a subcutaneous administration dosage form. The health product comprises cosmetic, bathing agent and aromatherapy agent.
   ACTIVITY - Tranquilizer; Antidepressant; Hypnotic; Antimanic; Neuroleptic; Muscular-Gen; Neuroprotective; Vasotropic; Anticonvulsant; Nootropic; Vulnerary; Cerebroprotective; Antiparkinsonian.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - Cholinesterase inhibitor.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); C03 (Other organic compounds, inorganic compounds and multicomponent mixtures. Polymers and proteins.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); D21 (Preparations for dental or toilet purposes - including filling alloys, compositions for dentures or dental impressions, anti-caries chewing gum, plaque disclosing compositions, toothpastes, cosmetics, shampoos, topical anti-sunburn compositions and toilet soaps (A61K).); D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).)

PN KR2019055029-A
TI Producing graphene sheet used to synthesize high-quality graphene for ink and paste comprises placing metal foil made of e.g. iron in furnace, injecting reaction gas and doping gas mixture, heating and peeling graphene layer from metal foil.
AU YANG W S
AE KOREA ELECTRONICS TECHNOLOGY INST (KETR-C)
GA 201946155H
AB    NOVELTY - Producing graphene sheet, comprises (a) placing metal foil made of nickel, iron, aluminum, copper, manganese, brass or bronze in a furnace, and injecting mixed gas containing reaction gas including carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, methane, ethane, ethylene, ethanol, acetylene, propane, butane, butadiene, pentane, pentene, cyclopentadiene, hexane, cyclohexane, benzene and/or toluene, and doping gas, which is thermal decomposition gas of ionic liquid in volume ratio of 1:1-10:1, (b) heating at 300-2000 degrees C to grow graphene on the metal foil to form graphene layer, and (c) peeling the graphene layer from the metal foil. The metal foil is reusable. The ionic liquid comprises anions including e.g. acetate, hydrogen sulfate, methyl sulfate, ethyl sulfate, tetrachloroaluminate, carbonate, bicarbonate, nitrite, nitrate, sulfate, phosphate, hydrogen phosphate, dihydrogen phosphate, hydrogen sulfite, copper chloride, chloride, bromide, iodide, tetrafluoroborate and hexafluorophosphate.
   USE - The method is useful for producing graphene sheet (claimed) to synthesize high-quality graphene for ink and paste.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces graphene sheet having high quality and high purity which can be easily dispersed in solvent, and imparts various functionalities to graphene sheet by using doping gas during synthesis.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing graphene sheet, comprises (a) placing metal foil including nickel, iron, aluminum, copper, manganese, brass or bronze in a furnace, and injecting mixed gas containing reaction gas including carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, methane, ethane, ethylene, ethanol, acetylene, propane, butane, butadiene, pentane, pentene, cyclopentadiene, hexane, cyclohexane, benzene and/or toluene, and doping gas, which is thermal decomposition gas of ionic liquid in volume ratio of 1:1-10:1, (b) heating at 300-2000 degrees C to grow graphene on the metal foil to form graphene layer, and (c) peeling the graphene layer from the metal foil. The metal foil is reusable. The ionic liquid comprises anions including acetate, hydrogen sulfate, methyl sulfate, ethyl sulfate, tetrachloroaluminate, carbonate, bicarbonate, nitrite, nitrate, sulfate, phosphate, hydrogen phosphate, dihydrogen phosphate, hydrogen sulfite, copper chloride, chloride, bromide, iodide, tetrafluoroborate, hexafluorophosphate, hexafluoro antimonite, triflate, tetrafluoroethanesulfonate, 1,1,2,3,3,3-hexafluoropropanesulfonate, 2-chloro-1,1,2-trifluoroethanesulfonate, bistrifluoromethylsulfonyl imide, bis(pentafluoroethanesulfonyl)imide, tris((trifluoromethyl)sulfonyl)methanide, trifluoroacetate, 1,1,2-trifluoro-2-(trifluoromethoxy)ethanesulfonate, 1,1,2-trifluoro-2-(perfluoroethoxy)ethanesulfonate and 1,1,2,2-tetrafluoro-2-(1,2,2,2-tetrafluoroethoxy)ethanesulfonate.
TF EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN IN201911004661-A; CN111537018-A
TI Automatic carbon sequestration system useful for monitoring amount of carbon fixing/sequestration in area, comprises carbon sequestration estimation device containing sensors, oxygen sensor, temperature sensor, and humidity sensor.
AU SINGH V P
   HARIBABU P
   BABU S C
   BINDHUMADHAVA B S
   GANGA P G L
   PUM H
   BINHAMADAVA B
   GILL S G P
AE DIT MIN ELECTRONICS & INFORMATION TECHNO (DITM-Non-standard)
   CENT DEV ADVANCED COMPUTING CDAC (ADCO-Non-standard)
   DIT MIN ELECTRONICS & INFORMATION TECHNO (DITM-Non-standard)
   CENT DEV ADVANCED COMPUTING (ADCO-Non-standard)
GA 201934060M
AB    NOVELTY - Automatic carbon sequestration system for monitoring amount of carbon fixing/sequestration taking place in an area, comprises several carbon sequestration estimation (CSE) device, where the CSE device comprises several sensors, an oxygen sensor, a temperature sensor, a humidity sensor, a light intensity sensor, a charging 1C, a solar panel, primary battery, secondary battery, voltage regulator, a data from the CSE device, a user, inputs by the user, where the inputs contains at least one type of soil, at least one type of vegetation type, at least one climatic condition, an anthropogenic activity, a wireless network, a microprocessor, at least one communication protocol, a communication network, an unique data analytics algorithm, a concentration of the gas exchange between tree and atmosphere, a coverage area of the CSE device, and a tree plantation strategy.
   USE - The system is useful for monitoring amount of carbon fixing/sequestration taking place in an area, or evaluating effect of forestation, deforestation, and forest conservation on carbon sequestration.
   ADVANTAGE - The system rapidly monitors the amount of carbon fixing/sequestration in a cost-effective manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Automatic carbon sequestration system for monitoring amount of carbon fixing/sequestration taking place in an area by measuring the environmental physical parameters, comprises several carbon sequestration estimation (CSE) device, where the CSE device comprises several sensors, configured by a carbon dioxide sensor, an oxygen sensor, a temperature sensor, a humidity sensor, a light intensity sensor, a charging 1C, a solar panel, primary battery, secondary batterer, voltage regulator, a data from the CSE device, a user, inputs by the user, where the inputs comprise at least one type of soil, at least one type of vegetation type, at least one climatic condition, an anthropogenic activity, a relatively humidity, at least an intensity of light, a temperature, a carbon dioxide concentration, an oxygen concentration, a rate of respiration by the inputs, a rate of photosynthesis by the inputs, an admin, a server, a wired network, a wireless network, a microprocessor, at least one communication protocol, a communication network, an unique data analytics algorithm, a concentration of the gas exchange between tree and atmosphere, a coverage area of the CSE device, and a tree plantation strategy, where the CSE device measures the concentration of the gas exchange between tree and the atmosphere to monitor the amount of carbon consumption taking place in an area.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Method: The system is operated by collection data using CSE device through sensors from the environmental physical parameters in the area, selecting communication protocol from the wired and wireless network, transferring the data to the microprocessor through the communication protocol, sending the data by CSE device to the admin, storing the data in the server through the unique data analytics algorithms to analysis the concentration of the gas exchange between tree and atmosphere, and analyzing the carbon dioxide sequestration by the unique data analytics algorithms from environmental physical parameters and measured data. The method is configured to correlate between the environmental physical parameters and measured data. The unique data analytics algorithms predict the coverage area of the CSE device. The effects of the anthropogenic activity are quantized by the system. The tree plantation strategy is formulated by the system.
