PN WO2019020376-A1; AR112571-A1; AU2018308586-A1; CA3069240-A1; IN201917052593-A; CN110869314-A; KR2020032671-A; EP3658489-A1; BR112020001487-A2; VN73463-A; US2021198104-A1; EP3658489-B1; US11370658-B2; KR2446845-B1
TI Preparing ammonia synthesis gas for e.g.,            revamping existing authothermal reforming plant, by            autothermal or secondary reforming portion of gaseous            hydrocarbon feed stock with oxygen containing stream            and treating with gas shift reactions.
AU HAN P A
   KROLL JENSEN A E
   KROLL J A E
   KROLLJENSEN A E
   JENSEN A E K
AE HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   TOPSCHE AS (TOPS-C)
GA 201912285M
AB    NOVELTY - Ammonia synthesis gas is prepared by (a)                providing gaseous hydrocarbon feed stock; (b)                separating atmospheric air into oxygen containing                stream and nitrogen containing stream; (c)                preparing hydrogen containing stream and oxygen                containing stream by electrolysis of water; (d)                autothermal or secondary reforming a portion of                gaseous hydrocarbon feed stock with oxygen                containing stream obtained from step (b) and oxygen                containing stream obtained from step (c) to process                gas comprising hydrogen, carbon monoxide, and                carbon dioxide; (e) treating process gas withdrawn                from step (d) in greater than or equal to 1 water                gas shift reactions; (f) removing carbon dioxide                from water gas shift treated process gas; (g)                purifying process gas to obtain purified hydrogen                stream; and (h) introducing nitrogen containing                stream obtained from step (b) into purified                hydrogen stream in an amount to provide molar ratio                of hydrogen to nitrogen of 2.7-3.3 in the mixed                hydrogen and nitrogen gas stream.
   USE - Preparation of ammonia synthesis gas for                revamping and/or increasing production capacity of                an existing autothermal reforming (ATR) or                secondary reforming based ammonia synthesis gas                plant (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Compared to prior art methods using                electrolysis of water for hydrogen production and                air separation for nitrogen production, the oxygen                product from electrolysis of water and from air                separation is advantageously used for partial                oxidation in the autothermal reformer or secondary                reformer, resulting in a reduced size of the air                separation unit (ASU), which is a costly and energy                intensive unit and process. For minimizing energy                loss of the ASU, the size of the ASU can be reduced                to a level where just sufficient amounts of                nitrogen are produced as required in the ammonia                synthesis. When the stoichiometric ratio of                hydrogen and nitrogen for ammonia synthesis is                produced in the autothermal reforming (ATR) or                secondary reforming and water electrolysis, the ASU                size will be at its minimum and will not vent any                excess of nitrogen. Furthermore, energy for                operating the electrolysis unit and ASU can be                renewable energy generated by windmills, solar                cells, hydraulic energy, or other renewables. The                method has increased efficiency of the electrolysis                unit by nearly 50%, compared to the efficiency in                the prior art processes employing solely                electrolysis and air separation, without ATR or                secondary reforming.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: Separation of                  atmospheric air in step (b) and electrolysis of                  water are powered by renewable energy. Purified                  hydrogen stream in step (g) is obtained by a                  liquid nitrogen wash. Separation of atmospheric                  air in step (b) is performed by cryogenic                  separation. At least a portion of hydrogen                  containing stream from step (c) is added to the                  purified hydrogen stream in step (h).
DC E35; H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).)

PN CN108975372-A; CN108975372-B
TI Preparing spindle-shaped precipitated calcium carbonate comprises e.g. digesting active lime in water, adding crystal form control agent having calcium carbonate dry basis mass, carrying out and carbonization reaction and pulverizing.
AU LIU S
   ZHU Y
   MO G
   LIANG M
   LI Y
   MAN Z
AE GUANGXI HUANA NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018A2642T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing spindle-shaped precipitated calcium carbonate comprises e.g. (i) digesting active lime in water, stirring in high-speed mixer to obtain lime slurry, allowing to stand, refining in 100 mesh sieve removal slag, aging to obtain refined lime slurry, (ii) transporting refined lime slurry into reaction vessel, adding crystal form control agent having calcium carbonate dry basis mass, closing water circulation, introducing carbon dioxide-containing mixed gas for carbonation reaction, carrying out calcium carbonate reaction, adding composite organometallic scale inhibitor having calcium carbonate dry basis mass, carrying out carbonization reaction when conductivity of system drops at inflection point, adding carbonic acid-based surfactant with calcium carbonate dry basis mass, stopping aeration to terminate carbonation reaction to obtain calcium carbonate slurry, and (iii) subjecting calcium carbonate slurry to pressure filtration, drying, pulverizing and sieving.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing spindle-shaped precipitated calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The calcium carbonate: has spindle shape, narrow particle size distribution, uniform morphology and excellent dispersibility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing spindle-shaped precipitated calcium carbonate comprises (i) digesting active lime of activity 200-400 in water, stirring in high-speed mixer for 15-30 minutes to obtain lime slurry, allowing to stand, refining in 100 mesh sieve removal slag, adjusting solid content to 8-12%, aging for 24 hours to obtain refined lime slurry, (ii) transporting refined lime slurry into reaction vessel, adding crystal form control agent having calcium carbonate dry basis mass of 0.1-1%, turning on agitation and jacket circulating water for temperature control, controlling temperature of lime slurry at 26-50 degrees C, closing water circulation, introducing carbon dioxide-containing mixed gas for carbonation reaction, carrying out calcium carbonate reaction for 5-10 minutes, adding composite organometallic scale inhibitor having calcium carbonate dry basis mass of 0.1-1%, stirring evenly, carrying out carbonization reaction when conductivity of system drops at inflection point, adding carbonic acid-based surfactant with calcium carbonate dry basis mass of 0.3-3% to continue carbonization, when conductance drops to 0.5 ms/cm, carbonizing for 3-5 minutes, stopping aeration to terminate carbonation reaction to obtain calcium carbonate slurry, and (iii) subjecting calcium carbonate slurry to pressure filtration, drying, pulverization and sieving to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio between digestion reaction lime and water is 1:5-8. The crystal form controlling agent is tartaric acid and/or succinic acid. The composite organometallic scale inhibitor is polyacrylic acid, polymaleic anhydride and/or polyaspartic acid in certain ratio. The cellulose-based surfactant is hydroxymethyl cellulose, hydroxyethyl cellulose, hydroxypropyl cellulose, sodium lignosulfonate and/or calcium lignosulfonate. The concentration of carbon dioxide in mixed gas for 5-10 minutes in early stage of carbonation reaction is 5-20%, gas flow rate is 0.5-1 m3/hours, carbon dioxide concentration of mixed gas is 30-50% and flow rate until end of reaction is 1-4 m3/h. The stirring paddle in carbonation reactor is straight blade turbine type agitating paddle. Preferred Method: The digestion reaction control temperature is 30-60 degrees C and rotation speed is 150-300 revolutions per minute. The agitation rate of carbonation reactor is 500-800 revolutions per minute. The drying temperature is 100-120 degrees C and drying time is 6-8 hours.

PN CN108821318-A
TI Co-producing acid and wood fiber board flame retardant by using phosphogypsum and high-sulfur bauxite comprises e.g. mixing phosphogypsum, high-sulfur bauxite, additives and modifier, grinding, and calcining to obtain clinker.
AU CHEN X
   XUE T
   QUAN B
   WANG L
   HUANG R
AE UNIV GUIZHOU (UYGZ-C)
GA 201891925R
AB    NOVELTY - Co-producing acid and wood fiber board flame retardant by using phosphogypsum and high-sulfur bauxite comprises e.g. mixing phosphogypsum, high-sulfur bauxite, additives and modifier, grinding, calcining to obtain clinker, subjecting to water-grinding, dissolution, and solid-liquid separation, adding calcium oxide to the liquid obtained by solid-liquid separation, heating and stirring, filtering to obtain sodium metaaluminate solution, introducing carbon dioxide gas into the sodium metaaluminate solution to obtain aluminum hydroxide precipitate, washing aluminum hydroxide precipitate with distilled water, drying and washing to obtain aluminum hydroxide, taking the aluminum hydroxide, adding borax and sodium silicate, mixing uniformly to obtain wood fiber board flame retardant, obtaining flotation precipitate, separating the sulfide, calcining, and reacting flue gas generated by the calcination catalytically with vanadium pentoxide, and absorbing.
   USE - The method is useful for co-producing acid and wood fiber board flame retardant by using phosphogypsum and high-sulfur bauxite.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple and environmentally friendly; has high utilization rate; and ensures low production cost of wood fiber board flame retardant.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Co-producing acid and wood fiber board flame retardant by using phosphogypsum and high-sulfur bauxite comprises (A) mixing phosphogypsum, high-sulfur bauxite, additives and modifier, grinding to prepare raw materials, conveying into kiln for calcination to obtain clinker, which is subjected to water-grinding and dissolution, and subjecting to solid-liquid separation, (B) adding calcium oxide to the liquid obtained by solid-liquid separation in step (A), heating and stirring, and filtering to obtain sodium metaaluminate solution, (C) introducing carbon dioxide gas into the sodium metaaluminate solution prepared in the step (B) to obtain aluminum hydroxide precipitate, and washing the aluminum hydroxide precipitate with distilled water, drying and washing the precipitated aluminum hydroxide precipitate to obtain aluminum hydroxide, (D) taking the aluminum hydroxide prepared in step (C), adding borax and sodium silicate, mixing uniformly to obtain wood fiber board flame retardant, and (E) flotation of the precipitate obtained by solid-liquid separation in step (A), separating the sulfide, calcining the separated sulfide, and the flue gas generated by the calcination is catalytically reacted with vanadium pentoxide, and absorbing with concentrated sulfuric acid to produce sulfuric acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (A), the sulfur content in the high sulfur bauxite is higher than 0.7%, the additive is sodium carbonate, sodium sulfate or caustic soda, the modifier is anthracite, carbon or coal gangue, the kiln is an industrial rotary kiln, an industrial tunnel kiln or an industrial vertical kiln. In the step (A), in the raw meal, phosphogypsum and high-sulfur bauxite mixing weight ratio is 1-1.3:1-1.5, the additive addition ratio is 1:1 according to the molecular ratio of the sum of sodium oxide and aluminum oxide+iron (II) oxide contained in the raw meal, the mixing ratio of the modifier is 15-20% of the total weight of the raw meal, the calcination temperature is 1100-1300 degrees C, the time is 1-3 hours, and the ratio of liquid to solid when the water mill is dissolved is 3-7:1. In the step (D), the complex flame retardant comprises 20-28 pts. wt, preferably 24 pts. wt. aluminum hydroxide, 14-18 pts. wt, preferably 16 pts. wt. borax and 4-8 pts. wt, preferably 6 pts. wt. sodium silicate. In the step (D), the borax is passed through 100-150 mesh, and sodium silicate is passed through 80-120 mesh. Preferred Method: In the step (B), the calcium oxide is analytically pure, the amount is 7-12 g/l, the heating temperature is 80-90 degrees C, and the stirring time is 1.5-2 hours. In the step (C), the concentration of carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide gas is greater than 38%, the dust content is less than 20 mg/hm3 , and the drying temperature is 95-115 degrees C. In the step (E), the calcination temperature is 1100-1200 degrees C, the time is 2-5 hours, the condition is that the sulfide is placed in an oxygen-rich environment of 40-45%.

PT P
PN CN108821963-A
TI Synthesizing carboxylic acid with high precision using the Schlenk tube useful e.g. in solar cell field, comprises taking dry Schlenk tube, adding dicyclopentadienyl zirconium dichloride into Schlenk tube, reacting, stirring and filtering.
AU CHENG G
AE CHENG G (CHEN-Individual)
GA 201891912R
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing carboxylic acid with high precision using the Schlenk tube comprises (i) taking a dry Schlenk tube, (ii) adding dicyclopentadienyl zirconium dichloride into the Schlenk tube, (iii) connecting Schlenk tube on double-row tube, (iv) pump vacuumizing, (v) replacing air, (vi) adding hydrogen chloride zircocene, and adding dimethyl terephthalate of strictly removing, (vii) adding the strict dehydration into 1,4-epoxy butane, (viii) cooling liquid nitrogen-acetone bath, (ix) dropping ethyl magnesium chloride and mono-substituted olefin, (x) reacting, removing the liquid nitrogenacetone bath, naturally heating to room temperature and stirring for 24 hours, (xi) drying the long needle for carbon dioxide into the reaction system for bubbling, (xii) water quenching reacting , adjusting the pH value of the aqueous phase to 3-4 after half an hour, (xiii) extracting three times with ethyl acrylate , each time for 10 ml, drying anhydrous magnesium sulfate, filtering in vacuum.
   USE - The carboxylic acid is useful in solar cell field, a catalyst field, fine chemical field and petrochemical field domain (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Synthesizing carboxylic acid with high precision using the Schlenk tube comprises (i) taking a dry 25 ml Schlenk tube, (ii) adding 29.2 mg dicyclopentadienyl zirconium dichloride into the Schlenk tube, (iii) connecting Schlenk tube on the double-row tube, (iv) pump vacuumizing for half an hour at 4:50 degrees C, (v) replacing air for 5 times by nitrogen, (vi) adding 2.1 mg hydrogen chloride zircocene, and adding 2 ml dimethyl terephthalate of strictly removing, (vii) adding 2 ml the strict dehydration into 1,4-epoxy butane, (viii) cooling liquid nitrogen-acetone bath for the reaction system to -78 degrees C, (ix) dropping 1.1 ml of ethyl magnesium chloride and 1 m mol mono-substituted olefin under the protection of nitrogen, (x) reacting at 78 degrees C for 1 hours, removing the liquid nitrogenacetone bath, naturally heating to room temperature and stirring for 24 hours, (xi) drying the long needle for carbon dioxide into the reaction system for bubbling, (xii) water quenching reacting , adjusting the pH value of the aqueous phase to 3-4 after half an hour, (xiii) extracting three times with ethyl acrylate , each time for 10 ml, drying anhydrous magnesium sulfate, filtering in vacuum to obtain the carboxylic acid product.

PN CN108753540-A
TI Fermenting fruit-flavored raspberry wine by crushing raspberry, adding e.g. pectinase, crushing raspberry, adding yeast, and inorganic nitrogen, performing circulation and separating raspberry skin from residue, and clarifying product.
AU LI C
AE LINGHANG WINE IND CO LTD (LING-Non-standard)
GA 201888539K
AB    NOVELTY - Method for fermenting fruit-flavored raspberry wine involves (a) crushing raspberry, adding pectinase and potassium metabisulfite to the crusher, adding compressed oak sawdust to the tank, crushing raspberries, adding into circulation tank, and adding 0.3 g/l tannin during the circulation process, (b) controlling the temperature, increasing the temperature, adjusting the raspberry juice pH value, adding the juice to yeast and yeast nutrient, (c) adjusting the sugar content and the specific gravity drops by 10 points while the fermentation is most vigorous, adding inorganic nitrogen nutrients, and adding tannin when the specific gravity drops by 20 points after the fermentation is most vigorous, and performing circulation, cap glazing and charging carbon dioxide to the raspberry wine, and (d) separating the raspberry skin from the residues, and adding potassium metabisulfite, storing, clarifying, freezing, filtering and filling product into bottle.
   USE - The method is useful for fermenting fruit-flavored raspberry wine.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for fermenting fruit-flavored raspberry wine, involves (a) crushing raspberry, adding 80 mg/l pectinase and 80-100 mg/l potassium metabisulfite to crusher, adding 2 g/l compressed oak sawdust into tank, crushing raspberries, adding into circulating tank, and adding 0.3 g/l tannin during the circulation process, (b) controlling the temperature, increasing the temperature, adjusting the raspberry juice pH value to 3.4-3.7 using sodium bicarbonate, adding the juice to 0.2 g/l yeast and 0.2 g/l yeast nutrient while the temperature reaches 16 degrees C, (c) adjusting the sugar content to 212 g/l after the specific gravity drops by 10 points, where the alcohol content of raspberry wine after fermentation is 12 vol.%, the specific gravity drops by 10 points while the fermentation is most vigorous, adding 0.2 g/l inorganic nitrogen nutrients, and adding 0.2-0.3 g/l tannin when the specific gravity drops by 20 points after the fermentation is most vigorous, where the primary fermentation temperature is not greater than 20 degrees C and secondary fermentation temperature is 22 degrees C, and performing circulation, cap glazing and charging carbon dioxide to the raspberry wine, and (d) separating the raspberry wine skin from the residues, and adding 60 mg/l potassium metabisulfite, storing, clarifying, freezing, filtering and filling product into bottle.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves adding 2 g/l fresh oak while adding compressed oak sawdust. The step (c) involves measuring the specific gravity of product in fermentation process using gravimeter, and using specific gravity to judge the sugar content of the product. In step (d), gluing by using potato protein.

PN CN108706555-A
TI Process of co-producing sulfuric acid using phosphogypsum and low-grade bauxite ceramic tool materials includes mixing phosphogypsum, low-grade bauxite, additive and modifier, crushing, performing solid-liquid separation and drying.
AU CHEN X
   LI J
   LI M
AE UNIV GUIZHOU (UYGZ-C)
GA 201886137X
AB    NOVELTY - Process of co-producing sulfuric acid using phosphogypsum and low-grade bauxite ceramic tool materials includes mixing phosphogypsum, low-grade bauxite, additive and modifier, crushing, dissolving clinker, performing solid-liquid separation, adding carbon dioxide, filtering, washing, drying, burning, crushing to obtain alumina powder, mixing alumina powder, boron oxide, silicon carbide and tungsten carbide, ball milling, sieving to obtain mixture A, mixing waste quartz crucible powder and magnesium oxide, ball milling, sieving to obtain mixture B, mixing material A, mixture B, water reducing agent, calcium fluoride, deionized water and ethylene glycol, ball milling, drying, calcining, crushing, sieving to obtain mixture C, mixing mixture C, polyacrylic acid, polyethylene glycol and wear-resistant additive, adding deionized water, mixing, adding mixed materials, ball milling, drying, sieving, hot pressing, performing flotation, separating sulfide, roasting and performing absorption.
   USE - Method of processing of co-producing sulfuric acid using phosphogypsum and low-grade bauxite ceramic tool materials.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has low cost and high utilization rate and produces good quality materials. The material has high Vickers hardness, strong bending strength, good fracture toughness and simple acid production process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Process of co-producing sulfuric acid using phosphogypsum and low-grade bauxite ceramic tool materials comprises mixing phosphogypsum, low-grade bauxite, additive and modifier, crushing to obtain raw materials, delivering to kiln for roasting to obtain clinker, dissolving clinker, performing solid-liquid separation, adding carbon dioxide, filtering white precipitate, washing, drying, burning, crushing to obtain alumina powder, mixing alumina powder, boron oxide, silicon carbide and tungsten carbide, ball milling, sieving to 250 mesh to obtain mixture A, mixing waste quartz crucible powder and magnesium oxide, ball milling, sieving to 300 mesh to obtain mixture B, mixing material A, mixture B, water reducing agent, calcium fluoride, deionized water and ethylene glycol, ball milling for 2-3 hours, drying, feeding in calciner, calcining at 800-900 degrees C for 1-2 hours, crushing, sieving to 200 mesh to obtain mixture C, mixing mixture C, polyacrylic acid, polyethylene glycol and wear-resistant additive, adding deionized water, mixing, adding mixed materials, ball milling for 2-3 hours, drying at 110-120 degrees C, sieving to 40 mesh, hot pressing, performing flotation, separating sulfide, placing sulfide in oxygen-rich environment, roasting at 800-1200 degrees C for 3-5 hours, catalyzing with vanadium pentoxide and performing absorption.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The additive is sodium carbonate, sodium sulfate or caustic soda. The modifier is anthracite, carbon or coal gangue. The ratio of phosphogypsum and low-grade bauxite is 1:1-2. The kiln is industrial rotary kiln, industrial tunnel kiln or industrial vertical kiln. The ceramic tool material comprises 75-85 pts. wt. alumina powder, 2-3 pts. wt. boron oxide, 6-8 pts. wt. silicon carbide, 3-4 pts. wt. tungsten carbide, 10-12 pts. wt. waste quartz crucible powder, 2-3 pts. wt. magnesium oxide, 0.5-1 pts. wt. water reducing agent, 2-3 pts. wt. calcium fluoride, deionized water, ethylene glycol, 1-2 pts. wt. polyacrylic acid, 0.8-1 pt. wt. polyethylene glycol and 3-4 pts. wt. wear-resistant additive. The wear-resistant additive comprises 0.3-0.4 pts. wt. nano-graphite, 1-1.2 pts. wt. zirconium phosphate, 1-1.2 pts. wt. nano-zirconia, 0.4-0.5 pt. wt. calcium oxide, 0.4-0.5 pts. wt. magnesium hydroxide, 1-2 pts. wt. phosphoric acid, 1-2 pts. wt. molybdenum disulfide, 2-3 pts. wt. alumina, 0.4-0.6 pt. wt. kaolin, 1-2 pts. wt. acrylate, 0.2-0.3 pt. wt. ammonium persulfate, 0.7-0.9 pt. wt. nanocarbon, 10-12 pts. wt. water and 0.4-0.6 pt. wt. polyethylene glycol.

PN CN108645808-A; CN108645808-B
TI Determining early flowering stage of tobacco by collecting tobacco leaves as sample, removing leaf blade, crushing leaves, freezing fine powder, performing infrared scanning, normalizing spectrum and judging normalized spectrum.
AU WANG J
   CHEN J
   CHEN Z
   LI X
   ZZHANG C
   YANG G
   ZHANG T
   ZHANG J
   JIANG J
   ZHANG C
AE CHINA TOBACCO YUNNAN IND CO LTD (CNTA-C)
GA 201881545Q
AB    NOVELTY - Method for determining early flowering stage of tobacco involves (a) collecting sample by selecting tobacco material, raising tobacco seedlings by tissue culture method under sterilized condition, selecting tobacco seedlings measuring 10 cm, transplanting the seedling into hydroponic chamber, cultivating, and collecting tobacco leaves, (b) removing leaf blade from the tobacco leaves, adding tobacco leaves into agate mortar containing liquid nitrogen, crushing the mixture to obtain fine powder, and freezing the fine powder to obtain freeze-dried powder, (c) performing infrared scanning using freeze-dried powder, performing an infrared test to automatically deduct interference of water and carbon dioxide during scanning, subjecting the original spectrum to absorbance for automatic baseline correction and normalizing corresponding standardized spectrum, and (d) determining the tobacco sample material is early flowering by judging the normalized spectrum.
   USE - The method is useful for determining early flowering stage of tobacco.
   ADVANTAGE - The method distinguishes an early flowering seedling stage and normal seedling stage tobacco leaf in advance, reduces production loss, saves manpower and material resources, and improves quality of the tobacco.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for determining early flowering stage of tobacco involves (a) collecting sample by selecting tobacco material, raising tobacco seedlings by tissue culture method under sterilized condition, selecting tobacco seedlings measuring 10 cm with same growth and diameter of mother, transplanting the seedling into hydroponic chamber, cultivating, and collecting 5-9 tobacco leaves, (b) removing leaf blade from the tobacco leaves, adding tobacco leaves into agate mortar containing liquid nitrogen, crushing the mixture to obtain fine powder, and freezing the fine powder to obtain freeze-dried powder, (c) performing infrared scanning using freeze-dried powder, performing an infrared test to automatically deduct interference of water and carbon dioxide during scanning, sequentially subjecting the original spectrum to absorbance for automatic baseline correction, and normalizing corresponding standardized spectrum, and (d) determining the tobacco sample material is early flowering by judging the normalized spectrum, where the judging conditions include (i) absorption peak of amide I band and amide II band appears between 1620-1624 cm and 1527-1532 cm, respectively, (ii) absorption peak of polysaccharide of appears between 1075-1079 cm, (iii) absorbance of the absorption peak of the absorption band of the polysaccharide is compared with the absorbance area of the absorption peak of the amide I band, and (iv) absorption peak of the polysaccharide substance appears between 1025-1030 cm.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The selected tobacco material is Honghua Dajinyuan (HD) tobacco. Preferred Method: The freeze-drying is performed at -80 degrees C for 24 hours using freeze dryer. The step (c) involves adding 100 mg pure potassium bromide to 2 mg sample powder, mixing uniformly, grinding the mixture, tableting the mixture, and holding the tablet at 8t pressure for 2 minutes. The step (c) involves performing infrared scanning for 32 times with resolution of 4 cm, where the scanning range is 400-4000 cm, and the each sample is averaged for thrice to eliminate the error.

TI Preparing medicine in treating rheumatism and rheumatoid arthritis, involves weighing fermented semen trichosanthis, common clubmoss herb, tuckahoe, silkworm excrement, caulis sinomenii and activated yeast.
AU YANG B
AE YANG B (YANG-Individual)
GA 201875927F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing medicine involves weighing 100-110 pts. wt. fermented semen trichosanthis, 30-35 pts. wt. common clubmoss herb, 30-35 pts. wt. tuckahoe, 10-15 pts. wt. silkworm excrement, 3-6 pts. wt. caulis sinomenii and 0.12-0.18 pts. wt. activated yeast, cleaning and draining water on surface, using tissue crusher for crushing into 80 meshes particle size to obtain the mixed material, then adding 5 times amount of clean water into the mixed material, crushing for 10 minutes to obtain slurry, adding activated yeast into the slurry, stirring uniformly, then fermenting at 23 degrees C constant temperature to obtain liquid having into 15% soluble solid content, filtering to obtain wine liquid, and filtering with 4-6 microns microporous film to obtain the finished product.
   USE - Method used for preparing medicine in treating rheumatism and rheumatoid arthritis (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method offers medicine having rich nutrients, excellent taste, functions of dispelling cold and dispersing wind, relieving blood circulation and relieving pain, and satisfying people's drinking habits. It can also treat rheumatism paralysis. It is an effective treatment for rheumatism and rheumatoid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) a method for preparing the fermented semen trichosanthis, which involves weighing the semen trichosanthis, bulrush, Camellia seeds and crushed mosla, drying until 8% water content maintained, crushing, sieving through 100 meshes sieve, mixing uniformly, obtaining mixed powder, putting in the extracting kettle as supercritical carbon dioxide extracting device, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain essential oils and solid extract, putting the obtained solid matter in 3 times amount of clean water and stirring uniformly to obtain the slurry, adding 2.5% Bacillus cereus bacterial liquid, fermenting for 2.5 hours at 30 degrees C temperature, then performing steam sterilization to obtain fermentation liquid, concentrating, drying to adjust 10% water content to obtain the fermented semen trichosanthis;
   (2) a method for preparing the silkworm excrement, which involves pre-treating the silkworm dropping with 0.5% sodium salicylate solution in water bath at 40 degrees C constant temperature for 2 hours, then for evaporating and concentrating, and drying to constant weight to obtain the product; and
   (3) a method for using the medicine, which involves adding with drinking water in 1:5 ratio, or for wiping the affected area with water in 1:2 ratio.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: Preparing medicine involves taking fermented semen trichosanthis containing 80 pts. wt. semen trichosanthis, 15 pts. wt. bulrush, 10 pts. wt. Camellia seeds and 12 pts. wt. crushed mosla. The silkworm excrement is a feces during three sleeps. Preferred Conditions: The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is done at 55 degrees C temperature for 3 hours under 18MPa pressure with 10 kg/hour carbon dioxide flow rate. The Bacillus cereus bacterial liquid has 1.2/asterisk103 cfu/mL viable strains.
   ACTIVITY - Antiarthritic; Antirheumatic; Analgesic; Vasotropic.

AU YAO N
   LAI D
   QIU J
   PANG T
   XU H
   JIANG Y
   LIAO X
AE GUANGXI ZHUANG AUTONOMOUS REGION INST (GUAG-C)
GA 201847013G
AB    NOVELTY - Bovine ecological fermented feed comprises pretreated leaflet silver Acacia, Pennisetum purpureum and lactic acid bacteria fermentation aid. The preparation of bovine ecological fermented feed includes e.g. chopping leaflet silver Acacia into small pieces, and soaking in warm water, taking out, immediately frozen, thawing, placing in a fermentation vessel and the stacking thickness of leaflet silver Acacia is controlled and mixing extracellular enzymes, and introducing nitrogen/carbon dioxide (N2/Co2), performing first fermentation of mixed gas to obtain pretreated leaflet silver Acacia, air-drying the Pennisetum purpureum to a moisture content, cutting into small pieces, pre-treating leaves of silver Acacia and Pennisetum purpureum , and lactic acid fermentation auxiliary bacteria and mixing uniformly in the fermentation vessel.
   USE - Used as bovine ecological fermented feed.
   ADVANTAGE - The fermented feed solves the problems of unbalanced nutrition, high content of neutral detergent fiber and acidic detergent fiber in the feed of a single gramineous herbage forage, preserves the nutrition through a secondary fermentation process, improves the palatability of legumes and biologically active ingredients, reduces the cost of feeding and comprehensive nutritional value, has good palatability, and easy-to-absorb cattle ecological feed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Bovine ecological fermented feed comprises pretreated leaflet silver Acacia, Pennisetum purpureum and lactic acid bacteria fermentation aid. The preparation of bovine ecological fermented feed includes (i) chopping leaflet silver Acacia into small pieces of 1-2 cm, and soaking in warm water at 35-45 degrees C for 12-20 minutes, taking out, immediately frozen at -20 degrees C for 24-36 hours, thawing, placing in a fermentation vessel and the stacking thickness of leaflet silver Acacia is controlled to 4-6 cm and mixing 3-7 cm extracellular enzymes, and introducing nitrogen/carbon dioxide (N2/Co2), performing first fermentation of mixed gas at 23-30 degrees C for 20-35 hours to obtain pretreated leaflet silver Acacia; (ii) air-drying the Pennisetum purpureum to a moisture content of 68-72%, cutting into small pieces of 1-2 cm; (iii) pre-treating 25-30 pts. wt. leaves of silver Acacia and 70-75 pts. wt. Pennisetum purpureum , and 2-5 pts. wt. lactic acid fermentation auxiliary bacteria and mixing uniformly in the fermentation vessel, where the stacking thickness of the control material is 3-6 cm, sealing and fermenting at room temperature for 35-45 days.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the volume ratio of mixed gas of N2/Co2 in N2 and Co2 is 1:20-30. In the step (iii), lactic acid bacteria fermentation aid is Leuconostoc, Lactobacillus, and Pediococcus halophilus at a mixed ratio of 10:1-3:2-5. The concentration of Leuconostoc is 106-8 cfu/ml. The concentration of Lactobacillus is 107-10 cfu/ml. The concentration of Pediococcus halophilus is 106-9 cfu/ml.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PT P
PN CN107720788-A
TI Preparing low-magnesium battery grade lithium carbonate comprises adding excessive sodium hydroxide powder into lithium sulfate solution, stirring, to obtain lithium solution A, centrifugally separating and drying.
AU TAN Y
   TAN Z
AE DAYU XURI MINING TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (DAYU-Non-standard)
GA 201817941N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing low-magnesium battery grade lithium carbonate lithium sulfate solution comprises adding excessive sodium hydroxide powder into lithium sulfate solution, stirring, to obtain lithium solution A, taking e.g. sodium hydroxide solution mixing to obtain ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid and sodium hydroxide solution mixed solution, adding sodium hydroxide solution into lithium solution A to obtain the mixed solution A, adding mixed solution of ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid and sodium hydroxide solution into the mixed solution A, to obtain mixed solution B, mixing and drying.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing low-magnesium battery grade lithium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, has quick reaction, no side reaction and does not produce toxic and harmful substance, and is environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing low-magnesium battery grade lithium carbonate lithium sulfate solution comprises (a) adding excessive sodium hydroxide powder into lithium sulfate solution under room temperature, stirring for 20-30 minutes, centrifugally separating to obtain lithium solution A, (b) taking ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid and sodium hydroxide solution mixing uniformly under the normal temperature, to obtain ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid and sodium hydroxide solution mixed solution, where the mass ratio of ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid and sodium hydroxide solution is 0.5:6, where the concentration of the sodium hydroxide solution is 25 g/l, (c) adding sodium hydroxide solution having concentration 25 g/l into lithium solution A for adjusting the pH value to 10-13 to obtain the mixed solution A, adding the mixed solution of ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid and sodium hydroxide solution into the mixed solution A, where the mass ratio of mixed solution of ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid and sodium hydroxide solution to mixed solution A is 1:1, completely stirring for 10-15 minutes to obtain mixed solution B, (d) placing the mixed solution B into the rotating packed bed reactor, at normal temperature and under normal pressure, continuously introducing carbon dioxide gas, under the action of the centrifugal force the mixed solution B and carbon dioxide gas quickly and completely mixing, reacting to generate lithium carbonate precipitate discharging filling material outlet of the bed by rotating with the mixing liquid, collecting, (e) adding water in the wet lithium carbonate precipitation, agitation at least twice according to the solid-to-liquid ratio of 1:3-4, agitation for 10-20 minutes each time, centrifugally separating and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The flow of carbon dioxide is controlled at 1-4 l/minute. In the step (e) drying is carried out at 100-150 degrees C for 2-4 hours.
DC E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)
MC E11-W; E33-G; X16-B01F1
IP C01D-015/08

PT P
PN EP3964279-A1; US2022072472-A1; IN202021038559-A
TI Biocompatible task specific hybrid solvent            composition for selective capture of carbon dioxide            from gaseous stream, comprises alkanolamine compound,            activator to catalyze reaction, physical solvent, and            carbonate buffer.
AU SAHOO P C
   KUMAR M
   GUPTA R P
   PURI S K
   RAMAKUMAR S S V
AE INDIAN OIL CORP LTD (INOI-C)
   INDIAN OIL CORP LTD (INOI-C)
   INDIAN OIL CORP LTD (INOI-C)
GA 202234343J
AB    NOVELTY - A biocompatible task specific hybrid solvent                composition comprises an organic component and an                inorganic component. The solvent composition                increases carbon capture efficiency, utilizes less                energy for low-temperature carbon dioxide                desorption, improves cyclic capacity, lowers                corrosion and viscosity alteration.
   USE - Biocompatible task specific hybrid solvent                composition is used for selective capturing of                carbon dioxide from gaseous stream e.g. carbon                dioxide-containing flue gas, process gas and carbon                dioxide-containing gas from bio-methanation plant                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The TSHS composition has high CO2 absorption                capacity, high selectivity, high stability, and low                solvent regeneration energy, and synergistically                increases the carbon capture efficiency and                utilizes less energy for CO2 desorption over the                existing solutions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) preparation of the biocompatible task                specific hybrid solvent composition, which involves                preparing a base amine solution by mixing 2-10C                alkanolamine or hindered amine and 8-10C hindered                aromatic amine in a ratio of 5:1, adding at least                one prolinate derivative to the base amine solution                at the rate of 0.2-0.5 ml/minute, adding at least                one intermediate stabilizer to the solution of                prolinate and amine, synthesizing colloidal                covalent metal-organic compound and adding the same                to the solution of intermediate stabilizer,                prolinate and amine at the rate of 2-10 ml/minute,                preparing the surface modified alkaline                nanoparticles and gas-liquid shuttle nanocomposite,                adding the surface modified alkaline nanoparticles                and gas-liquid shuttle nanocomposite at an                intermittent dosing rate of 0.4 ml-0.6 ml/minute to                at least one glycol derivative to obtain a                homogeneous colloidal solution, adding the                colloidal solution to the solution of colloidal                covalent metal-organic compound and other                components, and making up the volume with deionized                water and heat-preserving the whole solution at 55                degrees C for 4 hours to obtain the task specific                hybrid solvent system. The dosage of gas-liquid                shuttle nanocomposite leads to a decrease in                loading of hydrogen sulfide/sulfur oxide/nitrogen                oxide and enhancement in the carbon dioxide                capture; and
   (2) capture or recovery of carbon dioxide,                which involves providing carbon dioxide source                which is chosen from carbon dioxide-containing flue                gas, process gas and gas from bio-methanation plant                having carbon dioxide concentration of 200-900000                ppm in the source gas, passing the carbon                dioxide-containing gas through the solvent                composition in a device to form a fine dispersion                of gas which results in increase in contact area,                sparging the carbon dioxide as microbubbles or                nanobubbles, and carrying out carbon dioxide                desorption by thermal or solar assisted electro                desalination method and amine regeneration by                thermal, ultrasonic processing and microwave                irradiation. The pressure of carbon                dioxide-containing gas is 0.1-10 bar, and                temperature is 20-70 degrees C. The regeneration is                performed at temperature of 85-100 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: Metal is zinc,                  copper, nickel, cadmium or lanthanide. The rough                  surfaced basic nanoparticles include surface                  modified alkaline nanoparticles, calcium                  hydroxide nanoparticles, calcium oxide                  nanoparticles, amine functionalized silica                  nanoparticles, amine-functionalized magnesium                  iron oxide (MgFe2O4), amine-Functionalized                  iron(III) oxide-silica core-shell nanoparticles,                  nitrilotriacetic acid-functionalized                  nanoparticles and/or amine functionalized                  titanium oxide nanoparticles. The alkaline                  nanoparticles include an alkaline core comprising                  a substance comprising calcium hydroxide,                  magnesium hydroxide, magnesium carbonate, calcium                  carbonate, magnesium bis(hexamethyldisilazide),                  calcium bis(hexamethyldisilazide), calcium                  acetylacetonate hydrate and/or calcium                  2,2,6,6-tetramethyl-3,5-heptanedionate,                  optionally hydrophilic cellulose derivative, and                  a stabilizing outer layer containing a stabilizer                  chosen from silylated hydrophobic polysaccharide,                  hydrophobic polysaccharide, terminated                  poly(dimethylsiloxane) diglycidyl ether and                  terminated poly(dimethylsiloxane)aminopropyl                  compound. Surfaces of the core particles are                  coated or covered with a polymer network, such                  that the covered or coated particles are stable                  over the time in obtained dispersion, and the                  nanoparticles have concentration of 100-150 ppm.                  The gas-liquid shuttle nanocomposite is composed                  of nanoparticles comprising silica, aluminum and                  titanium dioxide, alumina, titanium                  dichloride/nana- gamma -alumina, cobalt iron                  oxide (CoFe2O4), magnetic iron(III) oxide,                  gadolinium(III) oxide, functional silica,                  colloidal indium(III) oxide, zinc oxide, cobalt                  oxide, manganese oxide, iron(III) oxide, lead                  sulfide, metal iron oxide (MFe2O4, where M is                  iron, cobalt, manganese or zinc), Lewis acid                  zirconium oxide, silica boron sulfuric acid                  nanoparticles, nickel metal nanoparticles loaded                  on the acid-base bifunctional support (alumina)                  and/or cobalt(III) oxide nanoparticles, and the                  amount of gas-liquid shuttle nanocomposite is                  50-100 ppm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The organic component                  comprises at least one 2-10C alkanolamine or                  hindered amine, at least one 8-10C hindered                  aromatic amine, at least one intermediate                  stabilizer, at least one prolinate derivative and                  at least one glycol derivative. The solvent                  composition has concentration of alkanolamine or                  hindered amine of 10-30 wt.%, and the                  concentration depends on the feed gas carbon                  dioxide concentration. The inorganic component                  comprises at least one colloidal covalent metal                  organic compound, at least one rough surfaced                  basic nanoparticles and at least one gas to                  liquid shuttle nanocomposite. The colloidal                  covalent metal organic compound is zeolitic                  imidazolate framework-8, zinc oxide cluster                  linked with terephthalate (MOF-5                  (Zn4O(terephthalate)3)),                  (zinc(2-methylimidazolate)2) (ZIF-8),                  (copper(III)-(benzene-1,3,5-tricarboxylate)2),                  (copper(II)(1,4-naphthalenedicarboxylate)2(1,4-diazabicyclo-(2.2.2)octane)2)                  hydrophobic ZIF-8, UiO-66 or MOF-177. The                  alkanolamine or hindered amine is isobutyl amine,                  2-amino-2-methyl-ipropanol,                  2-(2-aminoethylamino)ethanol,                  2-amino-2-hydroxymethyl-1,3-propanediol,                  N-methyldiethanolamine, dimethylmonoethanolamine,                  diethylmonoethanolamine, triisopropanolamine,                  triethanolamine, trimethylamine, triethylamine,                  tripropylamine, tributylamine,                  dimethylethylamine, dimethylpropylamine,                  dimethylbutylamine, diethylmethylamine,                  diethylpropylamine, diethylbutylamine, N,N-                  diisopropylmethylamine, N-ethyldiisopropylamine,                  N,N-dimethylethylamine, N,N-diethylbutylamine,                  1,2-dimethylpropylamine, N,N-diethylmethylamine,                  N,N- dimethylisopropylamine,                  1,3-dimethylbutylamine, 3,3- dimethylbutylamine                  and/or N,N-dimethylbutylamine. The hindered                  aromatic amine is 2,4-diaminotoluene,                  2,4-diaminoethylbenzene, 2-naphthylamine,                  1-naphthylamine, N-phenyl-2-naphthylamine,                  N-hydroxy-1-naphthylamine,                  N-hydroxy-2-naphthylamine, 1-amino-2-naphthyl                  sulfate, 1-amino-4-naphthyl sulfate,                  1-amino-2-naphthyl glucuronide,                  1-amino-4-naphthyl glucuronide, 4-aminobiphenyl,                  N-hydroxy-4-aminobiphenyl, methyl (tri-o-acetyl-                  alpha -D-glucopyranosyl bromide),                  N,4-biphenyl-N-hydroxy-B-glucuroniosylamine,                  benzidine, 3,3'-dichlorobenzidine,                  4-amino-2-nitrophenol,                  1,2-diamino-4-nitrobenzene,                  1,4-diamino-2-nitrobenzene,                  4,4'-methylenedianiline,                  4,4'-methylene-bis-(2-chloroaniline),                  2,2'-bis(4-aminophenyl)propane and/or                  4,4'-imidocarbonyl-bis(N,N'-dimethyl)aniline. The                  solvent composition has concentration of hindered                  aromatic amine of 2-10 wt.%, and the ratio of                  2-10C alkanolamine or hindered amine solvent and                  8-16C hindered aromatic amine is 5:1. The total                  concentration of the 2-10C alkanolamine or                  hindered amine solvent and 8-16C hindered                  aromatic amine is at 10-30 wt.%. The prolinate                  derivative is 2-pyrrolidinecarboxylate,                  decyl-5-oxo-L-prolinate, methyl                  (4S)-4-amino-1-methyl-L-prolinate                  dihydrochloride, methyl prolinate,                  2,3-dihydroxypropyl-5-oxo-L-prolinate, ethyl                  L-prolinate, tert-butyl L-prolinate, methyl                  1-benzoyl-5-thioxo-L-prolinate,                  benzhydryl-5-oxo-L-prolinate, methyl                  1-benzyl-L-prolinate and/or                  3-(4-fluorophenyl)propyl-1-(((1R)-1-phenylethyl)carbamoyl)-L-prolinate,                  and the concentration of prolinate derivative is                  100-300 ppm. The reaction intermediate stabilizer                  comprises alkyne-terminated dendron, polyester                  2,2-bis(hydroxymethyl)propionic acid,                  polyethylene glycol linear dendrimer,                  amine-terminated polyethylene glycol-core                  dendrimer, polyethylene glycol linear dendrimer,                  hyperbranched polyethylene glycol dendrimer,                  hyperbranched polyethylene glycol dendrimer,                  hyperbranched polymers, polyamidoamine dendrimer,                  polyethylene glycol-core dendrimer, phosphorous                  dendrimer and/or polypropylene imine dendrimer,                  and the reaction intermediate stabilizer has                  concentration of 20-50 ppm. The glycol derivative                  is tetraethylene glycol monododecyl ether,                  polyethylene glycol monolaurate, polyethylene                  glycol dimethacrylate, methoxypolyethylene                  glycol, polyethylene glycol, polyethylene glycol                  diacrylate, polyethylene glycol dimethyl ether,                  hexaethylene glycol monomethyl ether, diethylene                  glycol monolaurate, octaethylene glycol                  monomethyl ether, tetraethylene glycol monooctyl                  ether, polyethylene glycol divinyl ether,                  allyloxy polyethylene glycol, diethylene glycol                  diglycidyl ether and/or tetraethylene glycol                  monodecyl ether, and the glycol derivative has                  concentration of 1-2 wt.%. The concentration of                  the colloidal covalent metal organic compound is                  300-500 ppm.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. TI Phase transfer solvent composition for enhanced            carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen sulfide capture from            flue gas and biogas, comprises primary and secondary            amines, tertiary amine, hyperthermophilic enzyme, and            thermoregulatory enzyme stabilizer.
AU SAHOO P C
   KUMAR M
   GUPTA R P
   PURI S K
   RAMAKUMAR S S V
AE INDIAN OIL CORP LTD (INOI-C)
   INDIAN OIL CORP LTD (INOI-C)
   INDIAN OIL CORP LTD (INOI-C)
GA 2022B6451D
AB    NOVELTY - A phase transfer solvent composition comprises                (i) a compound comprising minimum one primary                and/or one secondary amine groups, (j) a compound                comprising minimum one tertiary amine, (k) a                hyperthermophilic enzyme, (l) a thermoregulatory                enzyme stabilizer, (m) a phase splitting agent, (n)                a phase stabilizing micellar agent, (o) a                thermo-conductive fluid, and (p) 200-600 ml                demineralized water/litre to make up the volume.                The solvent composition synergistically improves                carbon dioxide/hydrogen sulfide loading and exhibit                a phase separation behaviour with energy-efficient                regeneration for carbon dioxide/hydrogen                sulfide.
   USE - Phase transfer solvent composition used for                enhanced carbon dioxide/hydrogen sulfide capture                from flue gas and biogas.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition provides enhanced carbon                dioxide and/or hydrogen sulfide capture from flue                gas and biogas having various gaseous compositions.                The combination synergistically improves carbon                dioxide/hydrogen sulfide loading and exhibits a                phase separation behavior with energy-efficient                regeneration for carbon dioxide/hydrogen sulfide                compared to conventional solvent systems.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of phase transfer solvent                composition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The hyperthermophilic                  enzyme is carbonic anhydrase (CA) obtained from a                  source chosen from Bacillus thermoleovoransIOC-S3                  (MTCC 25023) and/or Pseudomonas fragiIOC S2 (MTCC                  25025), and/or B.stearothermophilusIOC S1 (MTCC                  25030) and/or Arthrobacter sp.IOC-SC-2 (MTCC                  25028).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The thermo-conductive                  fluid comprises nano-fluids of silica, alumina,                  and titania, alumina, titanium                  chloride/nano-(&#947; )-alumina, cobalt                  ferrite, magnetic iron(II,III) oxide,                  gallium(III) trioxide, functional silica,                  colloidal indium(III) oxide, zinc oxide, cobalt                  oxide, manganese oxide, iron oxide, lead sulfide,                  metal iron oxide of formula: MFe2O4(where M is                  iron, cobalt, manganese, zinc), Lewis acid                  zirconium dioxide, silica boron sulfuric acid                  nanoparticles, nickel metal nanoparticles loaded                  on the acid-base bifunctional support (alumina),                  cobalt oxide nanoparticles, and oxide or metallic                  nano particles. The carbon dioxide sources are                  carbon dioxide-containing flue gas, process gas                  or gas from bio-methanation. Preferred                  Composition: The source gas comprises 0.02-99%,                  preferably 0.02-90% carbon dioxide and 0.001-5%                  hydrogen sulfide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The composition                  comprises 50-70 ppm phase stabilizing micellar                  agent, 20-50 wt.% compound comprising minimum one                  primary and/or one secondary amine groups, 20-30                  wt.% compound comprising minimum one tertiary                  amine, 10-50 ppm hyperthermophilic enzyme, 2-4                  ppm/1000 U/mg enzyme thermoregulatory enzyme                  stabilizer, and 1-5 wt.% phase splitting agent in                  phase transfer solvent. Preferred Properties: The                  thermo-conductive fluid has size of 10-50 nm. The                  thermo-conductive fluid particles has size of 6-8                  ppm. Preferred Components: The phase stabilizing                  micellar agent is chosen from                  N-[2-[(2-aminoethyl) amino]                  ethyl]-9-octadecenamide (AMEO), n-benzalkonium                  chloride (BAC), CnH(2n+1)-COO(CH2CH2O)12CH3,                  polyoxythylene alkyl ether, n-alkyltrimethyl                  ammonium surfactant, potassium alkanoate,                  dodecylpyridinium bromide, octylglucoside, sodium                  dodecyl sulfate, trans-cinnamaldehyde, sodium                  bis-(2-ethylhexyl)-sulfosuccinate,                  cetylpyridinium chloride, primary alcohol                  ethoxylate, polyoxyethylene nonyl phenyl ether,                  polyethylene glycol esters, linoleate, and                  dodecylamine. The compound comprising minimum one                  primary and/or one secondary amine groups are                  chosen from monoethanolamine, diethanolamine,                  triethanolamine, monomethylethanolamine,                  2-(2-aminoethoxy)ethanol, aminoethylethanolamine,                  ethylenediamine (EDA), diethylenetriamine (DETA),                  triethylenetetramine (TETA),                  tetraethylenepentamine (TEPA), 2-amino                  2-methyl-1-proponal (AMP), 2-(ethyamino)-ethanol                  (EAE), 2-(methylamino)-ethanol (MAE),                  2-(diethylamino)-ethanol (DEAE), diethanolamine                  (DEA), diisopropanolamine (DIPA),                  methylaminopropylamine (MAPA), 3-aminopropanol                  (AP), 2,2-dimethyl-1,3-propanediamine (DMPDA),                  3-amino-1-cyclohexylaminopropane (ACHP),                  diglycolamine (DGA), 1-amino-2-propanol (MIPA),                  isobutyl amine, 2-amino-2-methyl-ipropanol,                  2-(2-aminoethylamino)ethanol,                  2-amino-2-hydroxymethyl-1,3-propanediol,                  N-methyldiethanolamine, dimethylmonoethanolamine,                  diethylmonoethanolamine, triisopropanolamine and                  triethanolamine, trimethylamine, triethylamine,                  tripropylamine, tributylamine,                  dimethylethylamine, dimethylpropylamine,                  dimethylbutylamine, diethylmethylamine,                  diethylpropylamine, diethylbutylamine,                  N,N-diisopropylmethylamine,                  N-ethyldiisopropylamine, N,N-dimethylethylamine,                  N,N-diethylbutylamine, 1,2-dimethylpropylamine,                  N,N-diethylmethylamine,                  N,N-dimethylisopropylamine, 1,3                  -dimethylbutylamine, 3,3-dimethylbutylamine,                  N,N-dimethylbutylamine,                  N-methyl-1,3-diaminopropane, piperazine, and                  triethylenetetramine.    LIU G

GA 202165096Q
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves building a high spatial resolution discharge grid database in a certain area. A source grid is selected according to a condition of the filter grid. A selected source grid is searched in accordance with a filter condition. A suitable seal field address is taken in a search range. Multiple source-sink combinations are obtained. Each obtained source-sink combination is evaluated. A filter ordering is performed. An optimal source-sink combination is obtained according to an evaluation result. A matching of a carbon dioxide source-sink combination is finished.

PN CN110482532-A
TI Preparing graphene membrane from graphite liquid carbon dioxide suspension comprises adding liquid carbon dioxide and graphite powder into insulating bucket with stirrer, breaking to suspension, injecting into volatile plate and evaporating.
AU DONG L
   DONG T
AE DONG T (DONG-Individual)
GA 2019A0428S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing graphene membrane from graphite liquid carbon dioxide suspension comprises graphite, liquid carbon dioxide, an insulating bucket, a stirrer and a volatile plate having ultra-low viscosity, super-strong fluidity, super-strong permeability and super-adsorption, weighing liquid carbon dioxide having specific gravity of 1.101 and boiling point of -78.5 and graphite powder having specific gravity of 2.25, adding into the insulating bucket including stirrer, breaking the graphite particles into a suspension having free carbon atoms and mixing uniformly by stirring, injecting the quantitative suspension into a high level volatile plate based on the area of the volatile plate and allowing the liquid carbon dioxide to evaporate to obtain the graphene membrane at the bottom of the volatile plate.
   USE - The method is useful in preparing graphene membrane from graphite liquid carbon dioxide suspension.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple; utilizes rich source of raw materials; is highly efficient in preparing graphene; easily controls the size, shape and arbitrary thickness of the layer; ensures good promotion and application value.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The process comprises subjecting the volatile plate to pre-cooling and heat preservation treatment before using. The process comprises selecting the volatile plate based on the shape or size of the graphene membrane to be made and it is used multiple times to synthesis multi-layer graphene. The preparation device for the graphite liquid carbon dioxide suspension and the production process for obtaining the graphene membrane from the volatile disk has automatic and semi-automatic settings which forms a continuous cycle of industrialization process to produce graphene membrane of various types and specifications.

PN WO2022192501-A1
TI Liquid air contactor for direct-air-capture carbon            dioxide removal system, has hydrophobic membrane with            outer surface in contact with air and inner surface and            liquid handling system for allowing flow of basic            solution from inlet to outlet.
AU NULWALA H
AE ROCO GLOBAL (ROCO-Non-standard)
GA 2022B77144
AB    NOVELTY - The contactor has a hydrophobic membrane                including an outer surface that is in contact with                an air and an inner surface that is in contact with                a basic solution. A liquid handling system is                configured to allow flow of basic solution from an                inlet to an outlet, where a recycled basic solution                is introduced to a liquid handling system through                another inlet. The recycled basic solution is                pumped from an electro-dialysis bipolar membrane                (EDBM) into the liquid handling system through the                latter inlet, where the hydrophobic membrane                comprises a porous polymer chosen from polyvinly                fluoride (PVDF), polytetrafluoroethylene (ePTFE),                and expanded polytetrafluoroethylene (ePTFE).
   USE - Liquid air contactor for use in a DAC system                (claimed) i.e. stand-alone self-powered portable                DAC carbon dioxide removal system that is utilized                to remove carbon from ambient air.
   ADVANTAGE - The liquid air contactor is a stand-alone                self-powered portable direct-air-capture (DAC)                carbon dioxide removal system that can be used to                remove carbon from ambient air. It has a high                permeability membrane that allows ambient air to                permeate the hydrophobic membrane and contact the                basic solution. The air that exits the contactor                has 5-80% less carbon dioxide than the air that                enters the liquid contactor, or 10-70% less CO2                than the water that enters it. The contactor allows                the flow of recycled basic solution from an                electro-dialysis bipolar membrane (EDBM) into the                liquid handling system through the second inlet.                The carbon dioxide stripping unit removes carbon                dioxide from the air.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   a direct-air-capture (DAC) system;
   a process for removing Carbon dioxide from                ambient air ;and

PN CN113430548-A; CN113430548-B
TI Carbon dioxide conversion electrolytic cell useful for e.g. carbon dioxide electrolysis, comprises metal doped cerium oxide/carbonate composite electrolyte, lithium-nickel-copper-zinc-oxide, and lithium-nickel-copper-zinc-oxide-samarium doped ceria-lithium-sodium carbonate.
AU JING Y
   YU L
   FAN L
AE UNIV SHENZHEN (UYSZ-C)
GA 2021B4326N
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide conversion electrolytic cell comprises a solid electrolyte, an anode and a cathode located on both sides of the solid electrolyte. The solid electrolyte is a metal doped cerium oxide/carbonate composite electrolyte. The metal doped cerium oxide is (I). The carbonate is lithium-sodium-potassium-carbonate (II). The anode and cathode are precious metals, rare earths, transition metal oxides, perovskite oxides and/or spinels. The cathode is a composite cathode, and the material of the composite cathode is lithium-nickel-copper-zinc-oxide (LNCZ)-samarium doped ceria-lithium-sodium carbonate (LNSDC). The material of anode is LNCZ.
   USE - The carbon dioxide conversion electrolytic cell is useful for carbon dioxide electrolysis and water/carbon dioxide co-reduction at 400-650degrees Celsius (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide conversion electrolytic cell: utilizes solid electrolyte, which can improve the conductivity of the electrolytic charge carrier, promote the chemical adsorption and conversion of carbon dioxide, and improve the kinetics of carbon dioxide conversion; can operate at low temperature of 400-650degrees Celsius; and has high efficiency, low cost and stable system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon dioxide conversion electrolytic cell comprises a solid electrolyte, an anode and a cathode located on both sides of the solid electrolyte. The solid electrolyte is a metal doped cerium oxide/carbonate composite electrolyte. The doped cerium oxide is of formula (RMXCeYO2-delta) (I). The carbonate is lithium-sodium-potassium-carbonate of formula ((LixNayKz) 2CO3) (II). The anode and cathode are precious metals, rare earths, transition metal oxides, perovskite oxides and/or spinels. The cathode is a composite cathode, and the material of the composite cathode is lithium-nickel-copper-zinc-oxide (LiNiCuZnO) (LNCZ)-samarium doped ceria (SDC)-lithium-sodium carbonate ((Li0.52Na0.48)2CO3) (LNSDC). The material of anode is LNCZ.
   X+Y = 1;
   X = 0-0.25;
   RM = Sm, Gd, Ca, Mg, K and/or Na; and
   x+y+z = 1.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparation of the carbon dioxide conversion electrolytic cell.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the preparation of the carbon dioxide conversion electrolytic cell, the samarium-doped cerium oxide/carbonate composite electrolyte is prepared by adding cerium(III) nitrate hydrate and samarium(III) nitrate hexahydrate to deionized water and stirring to form nitrate solution, pouring ammonium bicarbonate solution into the nitrate solution, mixing, and reacting to obtain samarium-doped cerium oxide precursor, calcining the samarium-doped cerium oxide precursor to obtain samarium-doped cerium oxide; placing lithium carbonate, sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate and anhydrous ethanol in a ball milling tank and then drying them to obtain carbonate precursor, calcining the carbonate precursor to obtain carbonate; and mixing the samarium-doped cerium oxide and the carbonate, ball-milling, and then calcining to obtain final product.

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining phosphorus                gypsum, drying, crushing, grinding, and mixing                silicon-aluminum iron raw material according to the                low KH value of the cement-clinker, where the                obtained low KH-value mixed material is placed into                the desulfurization furnace through the pre-heater.    ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient and stable                preparation of calcium sulfoaluminate modified                silicate cement clinker with reduced carbon dioxide                emission.

   USE - The microbial inoculum is useful for promoting                lawn growth in compacted soil by loosening soil and                improving lawn quality (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The microbial inoculum rapidly improves lawn                quality, since the yeast decomposes glucose to                generate carbon dioxide, hence increases soil                porosity and ability of soil to retain water and                animal fertilizer and promotes absorption of water                and fertilizer by turfgrass. The microbial inoculum                improves quality score up to 7 points (9-point                scale) for the treatment group compared with 4                points (9-point scale) for control group 14 days                after being applied.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of                the microbial inoculum in improving lawn quality                and loosening soil.

Preferred Method: The application                  process involves mixing the yeast powder with                  first portion of water to obtain yeast suspension                  having viable count of 6-2 billion/ml, spraying                  the yeast suspension on the soil at a rate of                  200-800 g/m2, preferably 100-400 g/m2, then                  mixing glucose with second portion of water to                  obtain 8-16 %mass glucose solution, spraying the                  glucose solution on the soil at a rate of 200-800                  g/m2, preferably 100-400 g/m2 and spraying third                  portion of water on lawn grass surface during                  vigorous growing season of the lawn.

PN JP2022102701-A
TI Catalyst contains carrier used for producing            liquid hydrocarbon, containing ferric oxide as main            component and alkali metal supported on carrier.
AU SUGIURA I
   HASHIMOTO T
   MIYAZAWA K
   OGAWA M
AE ENEOS CORP (NIOC-C)
GA 202289366U
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst contains a carrier containing ferric                oxide as a main component and an alkali metal                supported on the carrier, where the carrier has a                specific surface area of 20 m2/g or more.
   USE - The catalys is used for producing a liquid                hydrocarbon from a raw material gas containing at                least one of carbon dioxide or carbon monoxide and                hydrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst produces high yields of liquid                hydrocarbons in the production of                hydrocarbons.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for manufacturing catalyst, involving (a) mixing an                aqueous solution of iron nitrate and a basic                aqueous solution, (b) filtering a precipitate                containing iron (III) hydroxide generated in the                mixing step, (c) cleaning the filtered iron (III)                hydroxide, and (d) producing ferric oxide by firing                the washed iron (III) hydroxide, where the step of                supporting an alkali metal is using ferric oxide as                the carrier.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst system                  has a reactor configured to discharge                  hydrocarbons generated from the raw material gas                  introduced from the upstream inlet from the                  downstream outlet, and is provided with a first                  catalyst that causes a reverse shift reaction,                  arranged on the inlet side of the reactor, and a                  second catalyst arranged on the outlet side of                  the reactor, where the raw material gas contains                  carbon dioxide and hydrogen. The catalyst system                  is further provided with a temperature adjusting                  mechanism for adjusting the temperature of at                  least one of the first catalyst and the second                  catalyst, between the first catalyst and the                  second catalyst. The alkali metal contains at                  least one chosen from sodium and potassium. The                  first catalyst contains copper and zinc oxide.                  The first catalyst contains rhodium. The first                  catalyst contains platinum. The first catalyst                  contains iron-chromium. Preferred Composition:                  The basic aqueous solution is aqueous ammonia.                  The basic aqueous solution is sodium hydroxide.                  The basic aqueous solution is potassium                  hydroxide. Preferred Properties: The carrier has                  a specific surface area of 40 m2/ g or more and a                  pore volume of 0.15 cm3/g or more.
   EXAMPLE - 5 g of gamma -alumina (BK-105) was suspended                in 1 L of pure water by stirring with a homomixer.                An aqueous solution containing 31.7 g of copper                nitrate hydrate and 38.1 g of zinc nitrate hydrate                in the formed suspension. 300 ml was added quickly                at room temperature, then the suspension was                stirred at room temperature for an additional hour.                Then, while continuing stirring with a homomixer,                300 ml of an aqueous solution containing 35 g of                sodium carbonate was added dropwise at room                temperature at a dropping rate of 5 ml/minute using                a roller pump. The suspension containing the                precipitate formed by dropping was aged by leaving                it at 35 degrees C for 24 hours. The supernatant                was removed from the aged suspension by decanting                and the remaining precipitate was diluted again                with water. This decanting and diluting operation                was repeated 4 times. Then, the precipitate was                taken out by suction filtration, suspended in pure                water again, and then taken out by suction                filtration, and the operation was repeated four                times. By this operation, the precipitate was                thoroughly washed with water. The resulting                precipitate was dried by heating at 120 degrees C                for 24 hours. The dried precipitate was subjected                to air flow at 150 degrees C for 1 hour, 200                degrees C for 1 hour, 250 degrees C for 1 hour, 300                degrees C for 1 hour, 350 degrees C for 1 hour, and                400 degrees C, and was fired by heating in the                order of 4 hours. By firing, a black powder, which                was a copper-based catalyst containing a copper                component and zinc oxide, was obtained. This black                powder was pulverized in a mortar, and the fine                powder was molded at a pressure of 40 MPa to obtain                a copper-based catalyst body which was a columnar                molded body having a diameter of 3 mm and a height                of 3 mm.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); E31 (Compounds of V, Nb, Ta, Cr, Mo, W, Mn, Tc, Re, Fe, Ru, Os, Co, Rh, Ir, Ni, Pd, Pt, Pa, U and subsequent actinides.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
