PN WO2022114694-A1; KR2022072145-A
TI Activated carbon for industrial use, comprises carbon aggregate comprising linear carbons and having specific surface area.
AU JEONG Y
   LEE C
   JUNG Y C
AE LG ENERGY SOLUTION LTD (GLDS-C)
GA 2022746993
AB    NOVELTY - Activated carbon comprises a carbon aggregate comprising linear carbons and having a specific surface area of at least 350 m2/g.
   USE - The activated carbon is used for daily life use and industrial use.
   ADVANTAGE - The activated carbon has an improved specific surface area and pore structure by a process with improved stability and efficiency. The method for producing activated carbon has improved stability and efficiency. The activated carbon minimizes the decrease in electrical conductivity inherent to the carbon material while increasing the specific surface area and porosity by the activation process. The method does not use a separate chemical, and performs activation process with improved safety and efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing activated carbon, involving (a) pre-treating a carbon aggregate precursor comprising linear carbons by ball milling, and (b) reacting the pretreated carbon aggregate precursor with carbon dioxide to activate it.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The activated carbon further includes amorphous carbon. Preferred Method: The activation of step (b) is to activate by heat treatment after the temperature is raised at a rate of 1-10 degrees C/minute. Preferred Conditions: The ball milling of step (a) is performed under conditions of room temperature, atmospheric pressure and 30-70% humidity. The ball milling of step (a) is performed under the condition that the weight ratio of the carbon aggregate precursor and the milling ball is (1:1)-(100:1). The heat treatment is carried out at 900-1200 degrees C. Preferred Components: The ball used in the ball milling is a zirconia ball or an alumina ball. Preferred Properties: The ratio (ID/IG) of the maximum peak intensity (ID) of oscillation frequency of 1310-1350 cm-1, to the maximum peak intensity (IG) of vibration frequency of 1560-1600 cm-1, of the activated carbon is 2 or less, where ID and IG represent the maximum peak intensity of activated carbon measured by Raman spectroscopy, respectively. The pore volume of the activated carbon has 0.8-3 cm3/g.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The linear carbon includes at least one chosen from carbon nanotube (CNT) and carbon fiber (carbon fiber). The cross-section of the linear carbon is exposed on the surface of the carbon aggregate.
   EXAMPLE - Under the conditions of 25 degrees C, 1 atm and 50% humidity, a carbon aggregate precursor (CNT1) in which CNTs were entangled was pretreated with a ball milling process. The ball milling process was performed using zirconia balls having a diameter of 3 mm under milling conditions of 300 rpm for 1 hour. In addition, the weight ratio of the carbon aggregate precursor to the ball was set to 50:1. Using a fluidized bed reactor, the pretreated carbon aggregate precursor was activated by reacting it with carbon dioxide. Simultaneously, the activation was heat-treated at a temperature of 950 degrees C, and after raising the temperature for 3 hours at a rate of 5 degrees C/minute, activation was performed at 950 degrees C for 3 hours to prepare activated carbon.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)

PN WO2022026136-A1
TI Forming carbon molecular sieve membrane used for separation of gases, liquids, involves dissolving halogenated precursor polymer in solvent, homogeneously dehydrohalogenating with organic amine base, forming thin film, and pyrolyzing film.
AU LIU J
   MILLAR D M
AE DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
GA 2022185221
AB    NOVELTY - Forming carbon molecular sieve membrane involves dissolving halogenated precursor polymer in solvent, homogeneously dehydrohalogenating dissolved precursor polymer with organic amine base to form partially dehydro-halogenated polymer, forming thin film from partially dehydro-halogenated polymer, and pyrolyzing thin film to form membrane.
   USE - Method for forming carbon molecular sieve membrane used for separation of gases and liquids; separating acidic gases, such as carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide from natural gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to provide carbon molecular sieve membrane with improved processability, where precursor polymers have increased solubility and crosslink upon heating, without triple bonds and inter-polymer chain crosslinking; thin film membranes easily converted to CMS membranes by single thermal pyrolysis step without melting; homogeneously dehydrohalogenated polymers soluble in solvents commonly used to process thin film membrane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The method for forming carbon molecular sieve membrane involves separating partially dehydrohalogenated polymer from solvent, and dissolving partially dehydrohalogenated polymer in second solvent at room temperature; forming thin film from partially dehydrohalogenated polymer comprises applying partially dehydrohalogenated polymer to porous support, where homogeneously dehydrohalogenating results in less than 20.0 wt.% of total halogen content of dissolved halogenated precursor polymer is removed. Preferred Components: The halogenated precursor polymer is selected from polyvinyl chloride (PVC), polyvinylidene dichloride (PVDC) copolymer, poly(vinylidene fluoride) (PVDF) copolymer, chlorinated polyvinyl chloride (CPVC), and mixtures. The solvent is selected from 1,3-dibromopropane, bromobenzene, chlorobenzene, dichlorobenzene, 1-chloronaphthalene, 2-methylnaphthalene, o-dichlorobenzene, hexamethylphosphoramide, tetramethylene sulfoxide, N-acetylpiperidine, N-methylpyrrolidinone, N- formylhexamethyleneimine, trimethylene sulfide, N-n-butylpyrrolidinone, diisopropyl sulfoxide, N-formylpiperidine, N-acetylpyrrolidinone, tetrahydrothiophene, N,N-dimethylacetamide, dimethylformamide, cyclooctanone, cycloheptanone, cyclohexanone, cyclopetanone, 2-butanone, di-n-butyl sulfoxide, tetrahydrofuran, l,4-dioxane, ethyl acetate, and mixtures. The organic amine base comprises protic amines, hydroxides, or both. The organic amine base comprises one or more protic amines selected from mono and dialkyl amines, cyclic amines, aromatic amines, and combinations. The organic amine base comprises one or more hydroxides selected from ammonium hydroxides, tetraalkylammonium hydroxides, alkali metal hydroxides, alkaline earth hydroxides, rare earth hydroxides, and combinations. The halogenated precursor polymer is PVDC, the solvent is tetrahydrofuran (THF), and the organic amine base is N-butylamine. Preferred Conditions: The thin film is pyrolyzed at temperature of from 500-1500 degrees C. The thin film is pyrolyzed at temperature of from 500-650 degrees C. The amount of halogenated precursor polymer is from 0.1 to 50 wt.% based on combined weight of halogenated precursor polymer and solvent. The amount of organic amine base is from 0.1 to 15 mol.% based on total halogen content in halogenated precursor polymer.

TI Preparing oxide load metal useful in heterogeneous            catalytic reaction, and water vapor shift reaction            comprises e.g. grinding, mixing oxide, metal source and            salt uniformly, calcining under inert atmosphere.
AU WANG G
   ZHANG Q
   PAN Z
   ZHANG S
AE QINGDAO INST BIOENERGY & BIOPROCESS TECH (CAQD-C)
GA 2021A8008H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing oxide load metal comprises grinding                and mixing oxide, metal source and salt uniformly,                and calcining under the inert atmosphere.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing oxide load                metal, which is useful in heterogeneous catalytic                reaction, and water vapor shift reaction, carbon                dioxide preferential oxidation reaction, selective                oxidation reaction, selective hydrogenation                reaction and photoelectric catalytic reaction                (claimed) in the field of catalyst                preparation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple preparation, low cost,                and universality; solves the problem that the                existing oxide load metal catalyst synthesis                technology is complex, and difficult to scale                preparation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for an                oxide-supported metal catalysts comprising metal                and multiple metal catalytic active sites among                iron, cobalt, nickel, copper, platinum, palladium,                gold, silver, ruthenium, rhodium, iron and                osmium.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the process,                  evaporating and recrystallizing salt-containing                  washing liquid, and recycling the salt. The                  calcination temperature is 122-1069 degrees C,                  preferably 300-900 degrees C, preferably 500-801                  degrees C. The high-temperature pyrolysis time is                  0.5-10 hours, preferably 2-5 hours. The                  atmosphere of the inert gas is nitrogen, argon,                  helium, and/or hydrogen. Preferred Components:                  The oxide is elements of iron, cobalt, nickel,                  copper, zinc, magnesium, aluminum, zirconium,                  cerium, titanium, and silicon, preferably ferric                  oxide, cobalt oxide, nickel oxide, copper (II)                  oxide, zinc oxide, magnesium oxide, aluminum                  oxide, zirconium dioxide, cerium oxide, titanium                  dioxide and silicon dioxide. The metal source is                  of metal elements containing iron, cobalt,                  nickel, copper, platinum, lead, gold, silver,                  ruthenium, rhodium, iridium, and osmium,                  specifically, they are their corresponding metal                  salts, acids or metal complexes. The salt is an                  alkali metal salt, preferably lithium salt,                  sodium salt, and potassium salt, specifically                  their corresponding halide, nitrate, sulfate, and                  carbonate. The mass ratio of the oxide to the                  metal in the metal source is 10:1 and 1000:1,                  preferably 20:1 and 200:1. PN CN113016934-A
TI Improving rehydration of plant tissue protein comprises e.g. mixing soybean meal and soybean isolated protein into mixed powder, adding water to dissolve sodium bicarbonate, slowly adding to mixed powder, extruding and drying.
AU TAO H
   CUI B
   GUO L
   YU B
   ZHAO H
   LIU G
AE UNIV QILU TECHNOLOGY (UYQQ-C)
GA 2021767888
AB    NOVELTY - Improving rehydration of plant tissue protein comprises (1) mixing 80-90 pts. wt. soybean meal and 10-20 pts. wt. soybean isolated protein into mixed powder, (2) adding water to dissolve 2-4 pts. wt. sodium bicarbonate, slowly adding to mixed powder, mixing and stirring, adjusting water content at 40-50%, and allowing to stand for 18-24 hours, (3) extruding by double-screw extrusion process, after extrusion forming, the water content is in the range of 30-40%, and (4) microwave drying extruded tissue protein.
   USE - The method is useful for improving rehydration of plant tissue protein.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: promotes formation of tissue structure, and water vapor and carbon dioxide in interior to quickly escape, increases porosity, and improves rehydration performance of plant tissue protein.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (4), the microwave frequency is 2450 MHz plus minus 50 Hz, and the drying time is 5-7 minutes. In the step (2), the mass concentration of the sodium bicarbonate solution is 4-6%. In the step (2), the stirring speed is 180-200 revolutions/minutes, and the stirring time is 15 -20 minutes. In the step (3), the conveying section of the twin-screw extrusion process is 30-35 degrees C, the melting section is 110-120 degrees C, and the forming section is 100-110 degrees C. In the step (3), the molding process is a square or circular mold with a side length of 0.5-1 cm in diameter, and the cutting speed is adjusted to control the length at 3-6 cm.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

PN CN112691542-A
TI Preparation of metal composite molecular sieve material used for absorbing-catalyzing and oxidizing volatile organic compounds comprises dissolving molecular sieve in water and dipping or photo-depositing metal precursor on molecular sieve.
AU HU Y
   DU Y
   XIAO G
   LI J
   FU M
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 202144845L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of metal composite molecular sieve material comprises dissolving molecular sieve in water and dipping or photo-depositing metal precursor on the molecular sieve.
   USE - The method is used for preparing metal composite molecular sieve material for absorbing-catalyzing and oxidizing volatile organic compounds (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The material efficiently adsorbs, captures and concentrates volatile organic compounds in exhaust gas, catalytically oxidizes to water and carbon dioxide at a metal active center, realizes integrated adsorption, catalytic oxidation, degrades low-concentration volatile organic compounds in exhaust gas, has high adsorption performance, high activity, and high selectivity, and can be widely used in air pollution control fields, particularly industrial conventional organic waste gas, industrial organic waste gas containing nitrogen oxide, ammonia, sulfur oxide, and organic waste gas in coal-fired flue gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal is transition metal copper or noble metal platinum. The molecular sieve is beta - molecular sieve. The transition metal loading amount is 1-20 wt.% in molecular sieve. The loading amount of the noble metal is 0.1-1.0 wt.%. Preferred Method: The molecular sieve material is prepared by dissolving copper acetate, copper nitrate or copper sulfate in deionized water, adding beta -molecular sieve to dry, and calcining; adding beta -molecular sieve in the water filled with nitrogen, adding chloroplatinic acid, methanol and ethanol, using UV lamp or xenon lamp as light source, continuously stirring and irradiating, centrifuging, and vacuum drying; and dipping honeycomb ceramic in deionized water, copper acetate, beta -molecular sieve, methyl cellulose and glass fiber solution, drying, and calcining. The beta -molecular sieve is prepared by slowly dripping 10-30 mL tetraethyl ammonium hydroxide into 20-25 mL ethyl orthosilicate, heating, stirring for 2-6 hours at 60 degrees C, constant temperature magnetic stirring for 1-3 hours, gradually adding 0.1 g sodium metaaluminate dissolved by 20 mL deionized water, continuously stirring at room temperature, hydrothermal reacting at 120-180 degrees C and 0.1-0.5 MPa, naturally cooling to room temperature, centrifugally washing with deionized water, vacuum freeze-drying at - 10 degrees C to - 50 degrees C and 1.3-13 Pa for 12-24 hours, calcining at 400-550 degrees C for 2-6 hours, and cooling to room temperature.

PN CN112521705-A
TI Preparation of modified aramid pulp/polymethyl methacrylate composite material for tooth base material, initiating polymerization of methyl methacrylate monomer to obtain modified aramid pulp, and compounding with polymethyl methacrylate.
AU KONG H
   LI B
   DU X
   XU Q
   JIANG F
   ZENG J
   HAO Y
   SUN X
   SONG J
   YU M
   YU Z
   ZHANG P
AE UNIV SHANGHAI ENG & TECHNOLOGY (USES-C)
GA 202131639N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of modified aramid pulp/polymethyl methacrylate composite material involves using supercritical carbon dioxide to carry methyl methacrylate monomer and its corresponding initiator to infiltrate the aramid pulp, initiating the polymerization of methyl methacrylate monomer to obtain modified aramid pulp, and compounding the modified aramid pulp and polymethyl methacrylate. The composite material has tensile strength of 47.83-63.52 MPa, elongation at break of 3.5-6.8%, and bending strength of 56.8-62.4 MPa.
   USE - Preparation of modified aramid pulp/polymethyl methacrylate composite material for tooth base material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can achieve good dispersion of aramid pulp in polymethyl methacrylate, increase the content in PMMA, and help improve the strength and wear resistance of polymethyl methacrylate. The method is environmentally-friendly, and does not use toxic solvents.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The preparation of modified aramid pulp/polymethyl methacrylate composite material involves (1) adding dried aramid pulp into reaction device at room temperature, adding methyl methacrylate monomer and its corresponding initiator, closing reaction device, (2) exhausting the air in the reaction device and pipeline by charging carbon dioxide gas at 0.5-0.8 MPa, vacuuming, (3) filling the reactor with carbon dioxide gas at a pressure of 18-25 MPa and temperature of 70-80 degrees C by hg at a rate of 5-15 degrees C/minute for 20-24 hours, increasing the temperature in the reaction device to the set value at a certain rate, making the carbon dioxide in the reaction device reach a supercritical state, warming for a certain period of time, obtaining modified aramid pulp, (4) mixing -20 wt.% modified aramid pulp and polymethyl methacrylate powder, mixing at 10-15 rpm for 5-15 minutes, obtaining mixture, (5) feeding the mixture into a mold and hot pressing at a temperature of 180-200 degrees C and pressure of 10-15 MPa for 15-20 minutes and demolding. Preferred Component: In step (1), aramid pulp is chosen from one of Kevlar 29 (RTM: poly(p-phenylene terephthalamide)), Kevlar 49 (RTM: poly(p-phenylene terephthalamide)), Kevlar129 (RTM: terephthalamide), Twaron (RTM: para-aramid), and domestic 1414 pulp. In step (1), the initiator corresponding to methyl methacrylate monomer is one or more of dibenzoyl peroxide, lauryl peroxide, potassium persulfate and ammonium persulfate. Preferred Composition: In step (1), the mass ratio of methyl methacrylate monomer to aramid pulp is 1:3-3:2. The mass ratio of initiator to methyl methacrylate monomer corresponding to methyl methacrylate monomer is 1:20-100.

   USE - The microalgae carbon fixation enhancer is useful in improving efficiency of microalgae carbon fixation.
   ADVANTAGE - The enhancer improves the carbon fixation efficiency of the microalgae, and has no toxic effect on the microalgae, and does not absorbed and consumed by the microalgae. The cultured algae cells have high carbon fixation efficiency, high carbonic anhydrase activity and high photosynthetic oxygen release rate, which is beneficial to the use of microalgae to treat tail gas containing carbon dioxide, and achieves the effect of reducing emissions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a medium for improving the carbon fixation efficiency of microalgae, comprising microalgae carbon fixation enhancer comprising methanol, polyethylene glycol dimethyl ether, propylene carbonate and/or N-methylpyrrolidone; and
   (2) a method for improving the carbon fixation efficiency of microalgae, involving culturing microalgae using the above-mentioned medium.

Preferred Components: The medium comprises basic medium components, where the basic medium is a medium for culturing photosynthetic microalgae. The basic medium comprises blue-green 11 medium, SE culture or f/2 medium. Preferred Method: The method involves culturing the microalgae, and passing a gas comprising carbon dioxide into the culture solution, where the gas comprising carbon dioxide comprises flue gas, industrial carbon dioxide gas and/or air mixed with carbon dioxide. Preferred Microorganisms: The microalgae comprises green algae, diatoms, cyanobacteria and golden algae.

AU 
AE ANONYMOUS (ANON-C)
GA 2020989844
AB    NOVELTY - The radiation system is formed as a laser-produced plasma source, and comprises a droplet generator that is arranged to produce a stream of fuel droplets, which are tin fuel droplets, along a fuel droplet trajectory. The radiation system comprises a laser system that is arranged to produce a laser beam that intersects the fuel droplet trajectory. The laser system is a carbon dioxide laser system. PT P
PN CN111714465-A; CN111714465-B
TI Nine-taste double-relieving soft capsule comprises capsule shell and content in the capsule shell.
AU ZHANG C
   CHENG L
   QIAO Z
AE UNIV LIAONING TRADITIONAL CHINESE MEDICI (ULNM-C)
   UNIV LIAONING TRADITIONAL CHINESE MEDICI (ULNM-C)
GA 202098316C
AB    NOVELTY - Nine-taste double-relieving soft capsule comprises capsule shell and content in the capsule shell.
   USE - Used as nine-taste double-relieving soft capsule.
   ADVANTAGE - The capsule: is economical, safe, environmentally friendly, easy to swallow, operate and quality control and suitable for large-scale industrial production; has high purity and stable performance; improves the extraction rate and covers the unpleasant taste of medicine.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the nine-taste double-relieving soft capsule comprising (i) taking radix bupleuri, prepared rhubarb, sweet wormwood, honeysuckle, tsaoko amomum fruit, radix scutellariae, dyers woad leaves, dandelion and rhizoma paridis are extracting, concentrating and drying by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction combined with steam distillation, ultrasonic-assisted-reflux extraction and ultrasonic-microwave-assisted reflux extraction, and (ii) taking the gelatin, glycerin and water according to the proportion and using the rotating film method to infusing the suspension into the capsule shell.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The extract powder comprises 200 pts. wt. radix bupleuri, 140 pts. wt. prepared rhubarb, 200 pts. wt. sweet wormwood, 80 pts. wt. honeysuckle, 140 pts. wt. radix scutellariae, 155 pts. wt. dyers woad leaves, 140 pts. wt. dandelion, 80 pts. wt. rhizoma paridis, 60 pts. wt. peeled ginger and tsaoko amomum fruit. The capsule shell is composed of gelatin, glycerine and water according to the weight ratio of 1: 0.45: 1. Preferred Method: The content is a suspension prepared by mixing nine-taste double-dissolving extract powder, a dispersing medium, a suspension and a suspending agent at a ratio of 10:10-15:0.2:0.5. The nine-taste double dissolving extract powder is prepared by (i) taking radix bupleuri, sweet wormwood, honeysuckle and tsaoko amomum fruit in an oven at 40 degrees C to drying to a constant weight, crushing them through a 20-meshes sieve, (ii) placing the resultant powder into the supercritical extraction device, adjusting the carbon dioxide flow rate to 2.5l g/minutes, where the pressure of the extraction vessel is 35MPa, the pressure of the separation vessel I is 20MPa and 40 degrees C and the pressure of the separation vessel II is 12MPa at 25 degrees C, extracting until the volume of volatile oil no longer increases, collecting the extract from the mouth of the separation kettle, dissolving the residue with water and the ratio of material to liquid is 1:50 (g:ml), using steam distillation method, the temperature does not exceed 80 degrees C to extract until the volume of volatile oil no longer increases, combined for use, (iii) taking rhubarb, radix scutellariae and rhizoma paridis in a material-to-liquid ratio of 1:30 (g:ml) and adding 80% ethanol, 55KHz at 60 degrees C with 250W ultrasonic for 35 minutes, refluxing the extraction for 1.5 hours, filtering and recovering the ethanol, (iv) taking the dyers woad leaves and dandelion and heating them in an oven at 60 degrees C to a constant weight, smashing them through a 40-meshes sieve and adding an specific amount of 60% ethanol according to the material-to-liquid ratio of 1:20 (g:ml), placing it into the ultrasonic-microwave synergistic extraction instrument and heating at 65 degrees C, with the fixed microwave power of 100W and ultrasonic power of 200W and refluxing extraction for 1.5 hours, filtering and recovering ethanol, (v) combining the step (iii) solution and step (iv) solution and then concentrating to an extracting with a relative density of 1.10-1.30 (75 degrees C), (vi) mixing the resultant extract with the step (ii) volatile oil and adding soluble starch in a specific weight ratio according to the amount of extract and soluble starch is 25:1, mixing, spray drying and pulverizing into 120 meshes powder. The soft capsule is prepared by (i) mixing the above-mentioned nine-taste double dissolving extract powder with soybean oil, soybean phospholipids and beeswax according to the proportion to make a suspension for later use, (ii) taking gelatin, glycerin and water at a weight ratio of 1:0.45:1, boiling the gelatin, removing air bubbles under reduced pressure, press filtering to the gel storage tank and keeping it at 60 degrees C for use, (iii) pouring the step (i) suspension into the step (ii) capsule shell by the rotary compression method to make a soft capsule and the shape is below 18-25 degrees C and wiping off the surface of the soft capsule with a fiber-free wipe, and drying the oil layer at below 28 degrees C.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.)

AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)

AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of metal organic                framework-titanium dioxide-polydivinylbenzene                photocatalyst involves adding an organic ligand, a                metal source, a titanium dioxide precursor,                divinylbenzene as a crosslinking agent,                azobisisobutyronitrile as a polymerization                initiator and an organic acid to an organic                solvent, stirring, washing, vacuum-drying, and                calcining. The photocatalyst maintains regular                morphology structure, large surface area and                porosity of the metal organic framework, improves                photocatalytic activity of titanium dioxide, and                inherits super-lipophilic hydrophobicity of                polydivinylbenzene.
   USE - Preparation of metal organic                framework-titanium dioxide-polydivinylbenzene                photocatalyst used for volatile organic compound                degradation, water pollution control (all claimed)                and air pollution control.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple and economical                preparation of metal organic framework-titanium                dioxide-polydivinylbenzene photocatalyst having                improved lipophilic pollutant adsorption property,                carbon dioxide deactivation performance and                photocatalytic activity in high-humidity                environment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The acid solution has                  a concentration of 30-40 %mass, and the usage                  amount of the acid solution is 0-0.4 mL.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process involves                  preparing (i) polydivinylbenzene by mixing                  1.5-2.5 g divinylbenzene with 18-22 mL organic                  solvent, preferably tetrahydrofuran,                  dimethylformamide, acetone and/or ethyl acetate                  to obtain solution (A), stirring at 15-25 rpm at                  a temperature of 25-35 degrees C for 10-20                  minutes, adding 0.03-0.07 g                  azobisisobutyronitrile to the solution (A),                  mixing and stirring for 220-260 minutes to obtain                  a solution (B), transferring the solution (B) to                  a polytetrafluoroethylene reactor, placing the                  polytetrafluoroethylene reactor into a                  high-pressure reactor, hydrothermally-reacting at                  a pressure of 0.05-0.25 MPa and a temperature of                  80-100 degrees C for 10-24 hours, naturally                  cooling, volatilizing and removing the organic                  solvent at room temperature to obtain a                  super-hydrophobic polydivinylbenzene material,                  preparing (ii) metal organic framework by mixing                  1-2.2 g metal source comprising chromium nitrate,                  chromium trichloride, iron trichloride or                  aluminum trichloride and 3-4.2 g organic ligand                  comprising terephthalic acid, 2-aminoterephthalic                  acid or trimellitic acid to 40-70 mL deionized                  water to obtain a solution (C), stirring at 15-25                  rpm at a temperature of 25-35 degrees C for 10-30                  minutes, adding acid solution comprising                  hydrofluoric acid or nitric acid, mixing and                  stirring for 10-30 minutes to obtain a solution                  (D), transferring the solution (D) to a                  polytetrafluoroethylene reactor, placing the                  polytetrafluoroethylene reactor into a                  high-pressure reactor, hydrothermally-reacting at                  a pressure of 0.1-0.3 MPa and a temperature of                  180-240 degrees C for 8-24 hours, naturally                  cooling, washing the obtained precipitate with                  deionized water, methanol and dimethylformamide,                  and vacuum-drying at 100-150 degrees C for 10-12                  hours, preparing (iii) metal organic                  framework-titanium dioxide by adding 0.2-0.8 g                  metal organic framework to 20-50 mL ethanol to                  obtain a mixed solution (A1),                  ultrasonically-processing for 10-30 minutes,                  stirring at room temperature for 10-30 minutes,                  adding 0.8-1.5 mL titanium dioxide precursor                  comprising butyl titanate, titanium tetrachloride                  or isopropyl titanate, stirring at 15-25 rpm at a                  temperature of 25-35 degrees C for 10-30 minutes,                  mixing and stirring for 10-30 minutes, adding                  0.5-1 mL alkali solution, preferably ammonia or                  sodium hydroxide solution, mixing and stirring                  for 10-30 minutes to obtain a mixed solution                  (B1), transferring the solution (B1) to a                  polytetrafluoroethylene reactor, placing the                  polytetrafluoroethylene reactor into a                  high-pressure reactor, hydrothermally-reacting at                  a pressure of 0.05-0.25 MPa and a temperature of                  100-150 degrees C for 10-48 hours, naturally                  cooling, washing the obtained precipitate with                  deionized water, and vacuum-drying at 100-150                  degrees C for 10-12 hours, and preparing (iv)                  metal organic framework-titanium                  dioxide-polydivinylbenzene by drying                  polydivinylbenzene in a vacuum-drying oven,                  drying metal organic framework-titanium dioxide                  at 100-150 degrees C for 10-12 hours in a                  vacuum-drying oven, adding 0.1-1 g                  polydivinylbenzene and 1-1.5 g metal organic                  framework-titanium dioxide, grinding for 20-40                  minutes, calcining at 220-270 degrees C at a                  constant heating rate of 0.5-2 degrees C/minute                  for 1-4 hours under an inert gas atmosphere,                  preferably argon or nitrogen atmosphere and                  naturally cooling to room temperature.

PN RU2724252-C1
TI METHOD OF PRODUCING CARBON-CONTAINING ADSORBENT FOR REMOVING AROMATIC COMPOUNDS (VERSIONS).
AU TSODIKOV M V
   CHISTYAKOV A V
   NIKOLAEV S A
   BUKHTENKO O V
AE AS RUSSIA TOPCHIEV PETROCHEMICAL SYNTHES (ASTO-Non-standard)
GA 202063387L
AB    NOVELTY - Versions of the invention relate to a method of producing carbon-containing adsorbent based on carbon residues of lignine reforming. Adsorbent is proposed for adsorption of aromatic compounds from waste water. Carbon-containing adsorbent is obtained from the residue of carbon dioxide reforming of lignin into synthesis gas obtained in microwave radiation in the presence of iron-containing catalysts, by further treatment with microwave radiation with frequency of 2.402.50 GHz and current density of 100150 mA for 2040 minutes in CO2 medium at a radiation-induced temperature of 9501,000C. According to another version, the obtained porous carbon adsorbent is further treated with aqueous solution of ammonium hydroxide and then subjected to thermal shock in a muffle furnace at 400C in an Ar medium for 30 minutes.
   USE - Technological processes.
   ADVANTAGE - Increased specific surface of adsorbent and its adsorption capacity in relation to aromatic compounds.
4 cl, 3 dwg, 3 tbl, 6 ex

PN CN111229027-A
TI Processing of organic waste gas involves adding organic waste gas to combustion chamber for combustion, cooling, adding resultant to dust removal device, reacting under action of UV radiation and photocatalyst, and obtaining purified gas.
AU LI C
   WANG T
AE UNIV HOHAI (UYHO-C)
GA 202052002T
AB    NOVELTY - Processing of organic waste gas involves adding organic waste gas to a combustion chamber for combustion, cooling to 30-40 degrees C, adding resultant to a dust removal device for dust removal treatment, reacting under the action of UV radiation and photocatalyst, adding obtained product to purification particles for purification treatment, and obtaining purified gas. The purification particles comprise a carrier and negative ion powder supported on the carrier.
   USE - Processing of organic waste gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables effective processing of organic waste gas with high dust removal effect by converting most of organic material in organic waste gas into carbon dioxide, and adsorbing harmful substance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for organic waste gas processing device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The purification particles are prepared by mixing alumina, sodium carboxymethyl cellulose and water in a mass ratio of 1:3:6, obtaining glue solution, spraying the glue solution on outside of carrier in a mass ratio of 1:15, spraying anion powder on the carrier, and calcining at 200 degrees C for 45 minutes. The processing of organic waste gas further involves adding dust-removed organic waste gas to purification slurry comprising iron oxide and magnesium oxide, and processing. The purification slurry is prepared by grinding iron oxide and magnesium oxide into particles with particle diameter of 250-500 mu m and 400-600 mu m, respectively, and mixing with water. Preferred Composition: The carrier is spherical theta -alumina with particle diameter of 1 mm. The mesh number of anion powder is 300 mesh. The usage amount of anion powder is 10 %mass based on amount of the carrier. The mass ratio of iron oxide, magnesium oxide and water is (2-3):(3-4):(14-15).

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); A88 (Mechanical engineering and tools e.g. valves, gears and conveyor belts.); Q73 (Combustion equipment/processes (F23))
